description: A touch of Mirabelle plum joins a fresh Conference pear note on the nose. The same fresh, clean flavors are abundant on the refreshing palate, which is toned, dry and has lovely concentration and poise.
title: Domaine Pfister 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Juicy white grapefruit and lime notes are accented by hints of smoke, spice and crushed earth in this easy drinking yet exhilarating kabinett. It's light footed and approachably fruity yet lingers on a refreshing mineral finish.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Blue Slate Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Designation: Bernkasteler alte Badstube am Doctorberg Spätlese
description: While initially closed and earthy, time and aeration coax prettier notes of blossom, melon and peach. It's still a deeply mineral spätlese, full of crushed stone and earth, but also a bounty of ripe stone fruit and tangerine. The finish is moderately long, ending on a slightly astringent crushed-vitamin note.
title: Dr. Pauly Bergweiler 2016 Bernkasteler alte Badstube am Doctorberg Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of dried herb, dark cherry, black raspberry and barrel spice are at the fore on this 100% varietal wine. They lead to sleek cherry and cranberry flavors that provide intrigue.
title: Efeste 2014 Upright Klipsun Vineyards Merlot (Red Mountain)

description: Somewhat shy and reserved at first, with time this comes around to reveal pleasantly nuanced notes of white peach, pear and pineapple core, as well as hints of white florals. There's a vibrant verve to the palate, with nervy notes of green plum and lime rind that carry through to the clean, mouthwatering finish. Drink now–2020.
title: Eikendal 2015 Chardonnay (Western Cape)

description: This likable white is made in a light vibrant style, utilizing only neutral oak. Apricot fuzz and mellow toast ride along a thick acid-driven road of green apple skin, melon and fig.
title: Etude 2015 Grace Benoist Ranch Estate Grown Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: This is a 100% varietal wine from William Hill Estate grapes. It is a softly textured effort that offers the perfect amount of underlying tannic grip. Cinnamon, red currant and plum flavors complement cured meat, black olive, soy and black licorice.
title: Gallo Signature Series 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The winery's Dundee Hills bottling mixes fruit from three vineyards, two dating back to the 1970s. Black-cherry fruit fills out with nuances of black olive, black tea and roasted coffee. Everything is handled with balance and clarity, and the finishing tannins are light and supple.
title: Holloran 2014 Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: High-toned, vivid notes of red, tart cherry rise from the glass. The palate adds more dimension and richness to the fruit, but this remains slender and wonderfully tart throughout. The tannins are fine but could do with a little more focus. Nonetheless, a rustic charm plays about this.
title: Hugel 2014 Classic Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Forest floor, truffle, leather, spiced blueberry and scorched-earth aromas all take shape in the glass. Round and juicy, the chewy palate doles out fleshy black cherry, blackberry and licorice alongside polished, seasoned tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Icario 2011  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: The Applegate Valley is looking more and more like the perfect spot for Syrah in southern Oregon. Here are pretty mulberry and blueberry fruit flavors, with medium concentration and snappy tangy acids. The flavors are persistent and balanced, and hints of bacon and cured meats put a varietal signature on the finish.
title: Iris Vineyards 2016 Steelhead Run Vineyard Syrah (Applegate Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The aim here, according to the winery, is to show both floral and earthy components. The floral, sweetly fruity aspect is front and center, and you can find lighter, more subtle suggestions of earth and dried leaves as well. It's a working blend, and a wine best consumed over the next five years.
title: J. Christopher 2015 Sandra Adele Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This wine offers a bouquet of cherry, raspberry and coffee bean. Flavors of black cherry, raspberry and coffee bean wrapped in subtle tannins are followed by a vein of eucalyptus that continues into the cool, minty finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2014 4990 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Judean Hills)

description: Plummy blueberry, black cherry and black currant wrap around a softly layered core of toasted oak in this light and delicate take on the oft-hearty variety. Smatterings of vanilla and dark chocolate tease on the finish.
title: Judge Palmer 2014 Blau Vineyard Malbec (Knights Valley)

description: Forward and nicely structured, with lime, grapefruit and green-apple flavors, this strikes a perfect balance from start to finish. It's clean, true to variety and annotated with suggestions of wet stone. A great choice for your every-day Chardonnay.
title: King Estate 2016 Chardonnay (Oregon)

description: The merest touch of honeysuckle plays on the fresh apple nose. The slender palate offers lemon freshness and a pithy texture. The finish is clean and combines ripe pear with vivid lemon.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 White (Alsace)

description: Aromas of waxy melon, orange rind, pear flesh and white flowers set the stage on the nose of this attractive white. The palate is full yet harmonious, the ripe fruit tones balanced by ample acidity and a lingering note of citrus pith on the close. Drink now–2020.
title: Leeuwenkuil 2015 Marsanne (Paardeberg)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of baked plum, coconut and vanilla carry over to the medium-bodied palate along with dried black cherry, dark baking spice and star anise. Close-grained tannins leave a firm finish. Drink through 2023.
title: Lunadoro 2013 Quercione Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: This opens with aromas of raspberry, violet and baking spice that carry over to the bright easygoing palate along with a touch of white pepper. Fresh acidity and velvety tannins provide supple support.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2015 Santa Rita  (Valtellina)

description: Made entirely with Syrah, this opens with aromas of cedar, black currant and a hint of thyme. The juicy palate delivers blackberry jam, tobacco and a touch of truffle alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Marchesi de' Frescobaldi 2013 Ammiraglia  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Warm spices and rich fruits fill up this plush, flavorful and mouth-filling wine that is 90% Zinfandel. It smells like balsam and cedar and tastes like plums and cardamom while soft tannins make it cushy on the palate yet give just a little bite to keep it appetizing.
title: Markus Wine Co 2015 Blue Red (Lodi)

description: The nose offers slight hints of lemon and yeast, with a herbal note reminiscent of fern. The slender palate fills out with a savory note and adds white pepper to accentuate a green tinge.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Peau Rouge Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: An earthy hint plays on the nose before disappearing on the palate. The body is very slender, showing a high-toned freshness of lemon that pervades from start to finish. This ends wonderfully refreshing, dry and clean.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Terroir B Sylvaner (Alsace)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The 2014 was designated as Nysa vineyard; this is simply labeled Dundee Hills. Very young, almost like a barrel sample, this offers raspberry and cherry fruit backed with ample acidity and showing a touch of heat. The finish turns tangy, with a hint of interesting funk.
title: Megan Anne 2015 Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This blend of 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Sangiovese, 20% Merlot, 10% Syrah and 10% Teroldego presents aromas of red-skinned berry, Mediterranean scrub and green peppercorn. The aromas follow through to the bright savory palate along with a hint of cinnamon while velvety tannins and fresh acidity provide support. Drink through 2019 to capture the fruit and freshness.
title: Michele Satta 2015 Rosso  (Bolgheri)

description: Intense, ripe and textured, this wine is strongly mineral in character. It is balanced with vibrant apple fruitiness as well as concentration from the old vines in the blend. This wine could do with another few months aging, so drink from 2019.
title: Niepoort 2016 Redoma Branco White (Douro)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Delicate scents suggesting dried aromatic herb, wild berry, purple flower, leather and underbrush slowly emerge. Mirroring the nose, the firm, structured palate offers Marasca cherry, pomegranate, star anise and an earthy hint of game alongside vibrant acidity and rather grainy tannins. It has a rustic charm but it's also savory and full of personality.
title: Palazzo Vecchio 2013 Terrarossa  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: The forward and fruity Pommard clone shines brightly here, cloaked in flavors of ginger cookies and plum cake. It's spicy and front loaded, and it seems to be peaking about now. Hints of stem and astringency come up in the aftertaste. The advice from here is drink it now through 2019.
title: Phelps Creek 2014 Regina Pommard Clone Selection Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: This was barrel fermented in French oak, presumably some of it new or once-used, as there is a light and pleasingly toasty frame around baked-apple flavors. It drinks like an apple pastry, with enough acid to prop it up and keep flavors lively.
title: Phelps Creek 2015 Chardonnay (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: A lovely coppery hue, this is a full-bodied wine with plentiful flavors of citrus, strawberries and cherries. It broadens out in the mouth, without losing focus, and brings in a streak of sassafras. As with most rosés, it should be consumed with two years of the vintage.
title: Phelps Creek 2016 Fleur de Roy Rosé of Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Fragrant blue flower, wild herb, pipe tobacco and black-plum aromas waft out of the glass. Elegant and concentrated, the smooth delicious palate delivers juicy black cherry, raspberry preserve and licorice offset by well-knit, velvety tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Boscarelli 2013 Sotto Casa  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Made entirely with Pinot Nero, this well-structured sparkler boasts enticing aromas of red berry, crushed herb and brioche. The bright tangy palate delivers Marasca cherry, pomegranate, juicy nectarine and bread crust alongside an elegant perlage. It's well balanced, with bright acidity.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2008 Annamaria Clementi Rosé Riserva  (Franciacorta)

description: This quinta, situated in the heart of the Port vineyards, always forms the backbone of Dow's Ports. It makes a densely textured wine, powerful and with immense dusty tannins. These are backed by ripe black fruits, a good balance between sweet and dry, with a good shot of acidity. This complex Port needs to age for many years. Drink from 2028.
title: Dow's 2015 Quinta do Bomfim Vintage  (Port)

description: This is winemaker Dianna Novy Lee's debut sparkling wine, blending 56% Chardonnay and 44% Pinot Noir and then aging the wine on the lees for three years before release. It impresses from first sip, showing acid-driven raciness and a core of green apple, lime zest and biscuit flavors that linger. It'll serve well as a delicious aperitif or fitting companion for the table.
title: Flaunt NV Brut Sparkling (Sonoma County)

description: Flowery, fragrant aromas of spring blossoms and pink grapefruit belie the power and intensity found on the palate of this bold, steel-touched Riesling. While dry and medium bodied in style, ripe citrus and crisp stone-fruit flavors penetrate deeply. The finish is long, marked by cool strikes of steel and lime acidity.
title: Fritz Haag 2016 Brauneberger Juffer Trocken GG Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep and hearty aromas of black currant, caramel, lilac and a touch of roasted oak show on the dense nose of this wine. It is massive on the palate, but with appropriate balancing factors of entrenched acidity and chalky, sticky tannins that hold up the rich flavors of vanilla, black-plum skin and blackberry sauce.
title: Justin 2014 Focus Syrah (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Owned by the Wrights, this beautifully proportioned wine is supple and tasty, with berry, cherry and blue plum. The barrel aging adds a dusting of cocoa, and the tannins, ripe and still a bit stiff, add notes of ground coffee.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Canary Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: Rich tannins and concentration are driven both by ripe black fruits and a strongly mineral texture in this wine. The tannins have been smoothed by wood aging, but that's deceptive. Although the silkiness suggests early drinking, the firmer core suggests aging. Go for aging, because this will be an even more impressive wine from 2021.
title: Niepoort 2015 Charme Red (Douro)

description: Forward, pristine fruit and mineral intensity align in this penetrating, complex dry Riesling. It offers luminous yellow peach, nectarine and grapefruit flavors offset by dazzling lemon-lime acidity and a firm strike of steely minerality. The palate is creamy and expansive yet finishes with laser-edged precision. Beautiful already but should continue to improve through 2035 and hold further.
title: Schloss Johannisberger 2015 Silberlack Trocken GG Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Dense tannins and rich black fruits mark out this ripe wine. Its tannins are cushioned by the concentrated but generous black fruits that were organically grown. The wine has great purity as well as structure. Drink from 2024.
title: Château la Vieille Cure 2015  Fronsac

description: While pristine pink grapefruit, pineapple and peach flavors are crisp and concentrated, this lacy, textured auslese is more a showcase of intensely layered minerality. Invigorating and fresh, it offers deeply embedded veins of salt, dusty slate and smoke that reverberate through a long, honey-kissed finish. Drinks beautifully already but should improve from 2021.
title: Dr. H. Thanisch (Erben Müller-Burggraef) 2016 Berncasteler Doctor Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Tight-grained tannins lie beneath a sleek, sophisticated structure of ageworthy complexity in this old-vine wine made from grapes planted in 1959. Intensely layered, it shows wisps of currant, pencil shavings and anise—seasonings for a lush backdrop of brambly blackberry. Enjoy 2023–2033.
title: Grgich Hills 2013 Old Vine Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Yountville)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
Designation: Cuvée Extra Brut Methode Champenoise Estate Grown
description: Estate-grown fruit—principally Pinot Noir with 8% Chardonnay—goes into this impressively complex sparkling wine from Trisaetum. It's rich and deep, showing both power and finesse. A fine bead and light details of cocoa, coconut and cream bring exceptional complexity.
title: Pashey 2014 Cuvée Extra Brut Methode Champenoise Estate Grown Sparkling (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Wild red berry, tilled soil, violet, new leather and a whiff of sandalwood slowly unfold on this fragrant red. The structured savory palate delivers dried black cherry, pomegranate, white pepper and clove framed in polished, fine-grained tannins. A hint of coffee lingers on the close. Drink 2020–2025.
title: Poliziano 2015 Asinone  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: A selection made both of grapes in the vineyard and wines in the winery, this rich wine has power and density as well as superb fruits. The well-judged oak aging melds well with the dark fruits and acidity, while the tannins give strength and aging potential. Drink from 2021
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2015 La Rosa Reserva Red (Douro)

description: This is a well-made, small-production wine from the producer's home estate. Inviting in perfumed oak, pencil and cedar, it has brawny power that remains elegant in the glass, with persistent acidity and layers of cassis and cinnamon.
title: Tierra Roja 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: The style of Warre's Ports tends to dryness, and this fine quinta vintage is no exception. It's dry, but the rich fruit forms a great counterbalance. Dense, still young and bursting with perfumed blueberry fruits, the wine has great promise. Drink from 2027.
title: Warre's 2015 Quinta da Cavadinha Vintage  (Port)

description: This is a lovely light-bodied wine with serious intent and sophistication. Pear puree and apple skin mingle effortlessly along a plane of gravelly rocky mineral complexity that will show well with a variety of meals.
title: Blue Rock 2015 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: Linear and elegant, this vibrancy of this sparkler belies its age. It opens with enticing scents of wild flower, orchard fruit, a hint of hazelnut and a whiff of pastry. The palate shows complexity, depth as well as freshness, delivering Golden Delicious apple, apricot, toasted bread crust and a hint of tangerine zest. Tangy acidity lifts the rich flavors while an elegant mousse lends finesse.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2007 Cuvée Annamaria Clementi  (Franciacorta)

description: This wine is dense, complex and with great tannins. It is powerful and rich, packed with black fruits. Good acidity gives the wine its freshness, its black-currant fruit and its potential longevity. Rich and with great aging potential, this is becoming a major wine in its own right rather than just a second wine of Cos d'Estournel. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Cos d'Estournel 2015 Pagodes de Cos  (Saint-Estèphe)

description: Deep, brooding scents of black plum, cherry and cassis form the soul of this beautiful, richly concentrated wine. Plenty of licorice, cigar-box spice and bittersweet cocoa ride atop the fruit core, while notes of green peppercorn, leather and tobacco flesh out the midpalate and flourish on the finish. The crushed-velvet texture offers ample tannic grip and balancing acidity to keep everything in harmony. While it's hard to resist now, this should shine from 2023–2028.
title: De Toren 2015 Fusion V Red (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of mature black-skinned berry, baked plum, mint and violet mix with whiffs of dark culinary spice. The ripe chewy palate doles out raspberry jam, fleshy black cherry and licorice set against firm, polished tannins. The juicy fruit flavors deftly support the hefty alcohol. Drink through 2022.
title: Dei 2012 Bossona  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Musk and earthen complexities of smoke and struck flint meld into sun-kissed yellow cherry and tangerine in this seductive, complex kabinett. It's ethereal and sheer yet boldly penetrating, with a piercing lemon-lime finish. Delicious already but sure to improve through 2022 and hold well beyond.
title: Egon Müller 2016 Scharzhofberger Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: This gorgeous, radiant sparkler opens with enticing aromas of ripe orchard fruit, pressed wild flowers and cream-filled brioche. Elegantly sparkling, the bright palate delivers Golden Delicious apple, tangy lemon drop, Alpine herbs and minerals alongside tiny continuous bubbles. Fresh acidity brightens the rich flavors and lends impeccable balance.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2010 Casa delle Colonne Brut Riserva  (Franciacorta)

description: Just a shade off dry in style, this feather-light dynamo offers bold, concentrated stone fruit and blossom tones, enveloped in rich, penetrating swathes of smoke and earth. It's a gorgeously complex wine, with spine tingling acidity and a delicate, lilting mouthfeel that extends endlessly on the finish. Drinks beautifully already but concentrated and structured enough to improve well through 2030.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Wintricher Geierslay GW Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Bright red fruits, sappy acids and citrus highlights combine here. The supporting tannins are ripe and toasty, with a slightly grainy mouthfeel. Suggestions of coffee grounds add further interest to the finish, which expands gracefully as it breathes. This is a wine still pulling itself together and clearly built to expand and improve with further bottle age. Drink now through 2030.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Bryce Vineyard Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Lovely black-cherry fruit is the highlight here, with aromatics that elevate the fruit with high-toned intensity. The wine is cloaked with chocolate from barrel aging, and detailed with coffee grounds, graphite and loam.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Freedom Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Hints of honey, caramelized sugar and ripe tangerine on the nose contrast a palate that's lean and bracingly mineral here. While light bodied and lean, zesty white grapefruit and tart pineapple flavors are piercing and persistent. Tasted in 2017, it's still a taut, nervous wine that needs time to meld. Hold till 2020.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Kaseler Nies'chen GG Trocken Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is an excellent example why so many go crazy for Pinot Noir. The wine exudes a nourishing warmth that transports you to a cozy country kitchen, with bouquets of drying flowers and currants hanging from the rafters, szechuan beef sizzling in the stir fry, earthy sumac in the spice bowl and smoke from the dying embers of a campfire wafting in through the windows. It's silky and open enough to drink now, but should continue to age well through 2025.
title: Shaw and Smith 2014 Pinot Noir (Adelaide Hills)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The terrific texture and wealth of detail makes this truly exceptional. Citrus, mineral, candied pineapple and a hint of honey all combine in a long, dense and bright-tasting wine. In the spirit of Oregon's finest Rieslings, it's both sweet and tart, with a lively inviting mouthfeel.
title: Alloro 2016 Riesling (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Aromas reminiscent of bread crust, nut and orchard fruit carry over to the vibrant linear palate along with pomegranate and citrus zest notes. Bright acidity and a lively elegant perlage provide support and energy.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2010 Casa delle Colonne Zero  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Woodland berry, dark culinary spice, tilled earth and new leather aromas combine with whiffs of aromatic herb on this fragrant graceful red. The vibrant palate is all about balance and finesse, offering strawberry, red cherry, star anise and a hint of white pepper balanced by bright acidity and polished tannins. If you love extreme elegance, this is for you. Drink through 2024.
title: Gracciano della Seta 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Inky red-violet in color, this wine offers aromas of black currant and blueberry. It fills the palate with flavors of cassis, blackberry, juniper and anise. Mildly stiff tannins recede into a bright finish, laced with touches of iodine and smoke.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Special Edition Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

description: Deep garnet to the eye, this wine has a nose of lingonberry, raspberry and a touch of rosemary. Soft tannins provide a framework for flavors of tart cherry, strawberry, clove and chocolate, with a touch of smoked meat. The pleasing finish provides a lingering sense of brightness on the palate.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Virtuoso Merlot (Galilee)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The 40% new French oak seems just right, putting a toasty frame around robust black-cherry fruit. There's a spicy zip to the back-half flavors, and a clean lingering finish with highlights of mocha and smoke.
title: Iota Cellars 2014 Pelos Sandberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: A robust and flavorful Chardonnay, this exhibits superb balance between the ripe fruit, woodspice and vibrant acidity. Notes of baked apple, clove-spiked orange, toasted hazelnut and peach pit abound on the nose and palate, with added hits of citrus pith, honeysuckle and lemon verbena that add grace and elegance. It's medium in weight, with a lightly textured grip and lively acidity that lifts the sweet-spiced finish. It's hard to resist now, but should mature well through 2023.
title: Jardin 2015 Nine Yards Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: This wine has a classic nose of pencil shavings, cedar and ripe black plum, following through on the palate in similar fashion. Full bodied and highly concentrated, it layers in flavors of cherry compote and dark chocolate.
title: Joseph Carr 2014 Revelist Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Moon Mountain District Sonoma County, Sonoma, California, US
description: From the winery's Mountain Terrace Vineyard 1,700-feet up, this is a floral, subdued and brightly woven red wine. Vanilla and milk chocolate flavors provide a richness and likability, with a pronounced bite of oak and tannin on the finish.
title: Ledson 2014 Estate Vineyard Merlot (Moon Mountain District Sonoma County)

description: This wine has inviting caramel-apple aromatics, which follow along a subdued palate of ripe texture and oak. A brightness of orange blossom and tangerine add freshness and vibrancy to the lush fig-filled surroundings, dotted in nutmeg.
title: Martinelli 2015 Bella Vigna Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: Heady aromas of sun-kissed mango, peach and melon are cut by blades of steel and stone in this captivating kabinett. It's delightfully spry in texture, offering loads of crisp-tart yellow peach and nectarine flavors highlighted against streaks of lime acidity. The finish is fresh and mineral-laden.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Abtsberg Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This reserve-level best-barrel blend spent 16 months in 40% new oak. It's dark, chewy and toasty, with black cherry, black coffee and dark chocolate all in the mix of flavors. A solid and substantial wine, it drinks well young and may best be consumed over the next five years.
title: Megan Anne 2015 Black Love Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Ripe berry aromas with a touch of root beer are lush and soft. That is not the case on the palate, which is tight in the middle and scratchy along the edges. Blueberry and black-currant flavors are a touch minty, while this wine displays chocolate and lemon-peel notes on a tough, pulling finish. Drink through 2030.
title: Monteabellón 2012 Finca Matambres  (Ribera del Duero)

description: This is a stainless steel bottling done right. Citrusy aromas of lime peel, orange pith, just-ripe Asian pear and a flash of sweet butter show on the nose. The texture is phenomenally grippy on the sip, where flavors of crushed chalk meet with yuzu zest, nectarine and white peach, remain brisk throughout. Pink-grapefruit zest carries the finish.
title: Morgan 2015 Metallico Unoaked Chardonnay (Monterey)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is based upon a single Dijon 115 clone, and delivers potent ripe black-cherry flavors. It's a full-throttle bottle, textural and lightly touched with dried seaweed. The barrel aging adds a welcome touch of chocolate to the powerful tannic finish.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Durant Vineyard Madrone Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This bright, big and mouthfilling wine was fermented with wild yeast, which seems to infuse it with a dollop of mystery. Ultraripe lemon and grapefruit come with a hint of marshmallow and plenty of toasted-peanut flavors. It finds a smooth runway and coasts gently to a gliding stop, with those toasty flavors still resonating.
title: Ponzi 2015 Avellana Vineyard Chardonnay (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Based at Quinta de Roriz in the Douro, this wine is produced by a joint venture between the Prats family of Bordeaux and the Symington family of the Douro. Made as a second wine to Chryseia, it is soft and rich, with great tannins and ripe fruit. Black currants and acidity go together with a young, dry core. Drink from 2020.
title: Prats & Symington LDA 2015 Post Scriptum de Chryseia Red (Douro)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: Based on old Tinta Roriz vines, this elegant wine is ripe and densely structured, with good balance between red fruits and juicy acidity. It is a wine to age for several more years. Drink from 2021.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2015 Referência Grande Reserva Red (Douro)

description: Exhibiting all that one could hope from a coastal site, this wine offers earthy rose petal aromas around a tightly wound core of forest and tea. Structured tannin provide a framework for secondary characteristics to develop in the glass, from cranberry and orange peel to Asian five spice.
title: Talisman 2014 Wildcat Mountain Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: This is a light, ethereal and hugely satisfying wine, delicately layered and soft in texture. Huckleberry, Asian five spice and forest floor figure prominently along a succulent persistence of acidity and salty freshness.
title: Whistler 2014 Estate Grown Estate Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: This single-vineyard wine is rock solid, with punchy black-cherry fruit, a streak of mineral and a sprinkling of coffee grounds. Clean earth and graphite add more complexity, and the tannins—ripe and sturdy—provide a perfect foundation. The wine was aged for 17 months in one-third new French oak, and should drink well into 2028.
title: WildAire 2015 Fairsing Vineyard Pinot Noir

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: This is an inaugural blend of 75% Merlot and 25% Cabernet Franc made by Dan Petroski of Larkmead for the new producer. The grapes come from vineyards located near the winery in Oak Knoll. Dried herb de Provence aromas invite one in to mingle with aspects of cedar, pencil and red currant, the style subdued and soft. Layers of quiet power and oak lie beneath the fruit.
title: Ashes & Diamonds 2014 No. 1 Grand Vin Merlot-Cabernet Franc (Oak Knoll District)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Candied black cherry, rosebuds, violets and a minty tobacco quality all show on the nose of this countywide blend by Bruno D'Alfonso. It commands attention on the palate, showing black currant, blueberry, black-pepper-crusted beef and candied violet flavors, balanced by strong acidity.
title: Badge 2014 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Roasted cocoa, espresso cream and caramel provide richness to the nose of this Cabernet Sauvignon-led blend, but there's also bright black raspberry fruit and hints of pepper and fennel to give complexity. The palate starts with a zip, which dissipates into flavors of lilac, blueberry and anise seed.
title: Bishop's Peak 2014 Elevation Red (Paso Robles)

description: The 70% of Merlot in the blend of this wine gives it richness and ripe red-berry fruits. The tannins, dusty and spicy, are a blend of the wood aging and the structure of the Cabernet Sauvignon. Ripe and juicy, the wine is packed with fruit and likely to age well. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Anthonic 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Ripe, and densely textured, this wine shows an impressive, rich balance between fruit and tannins, with a dry core and broad black fruits that have great intensity. This wine, with 12 months in wood, conveys spice, concentration and excellent potential. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Biston-Brillette 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Run by the Nony family, this 17-acre estate has produced a generous, rounded wine dominated by black fruits. The barrel aging has enhanced the black-currant flavors and the acidity. All these elements are coming together in a full wine that will age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Labat 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This rich wine comes from a historic estate in Listrac, dating to the 18th century. It offers tannins and a dry core as well as dark black fruits and acidity, giving the wine its structure and potential longevity. The wine will develop slowly and will be ready to drink from 2025.
title: Château Saransot-Dupré 2015  Listrac-Médoc

description: Old vines on the estate, close to the ancient city walls of Saint-Émilion, produce this smoothly ripe wine. It is broad, generously rich with black fruits. The toast from wood aging will integrate with time. Wait until 2024 for this impressive wine to be ready to drink.
title: Clos des Menuts 2015 L'Excellence  (Saint-Émilion)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: All Dijon clones from the Alloro vineyard, this tightly wound effort mixes tree-fruit flesh and skin flavors in a compact balanced wine with some good years of life ahead. There's a pleasing nuttiness resonating through the finish, from a small percentage that was fermented in new French oak. Give it an extra year or two in bottle and it should open up nicely.
title: Alloro 2015 Estate Chardonnay (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This lovely wine shows its dark side throughout, mixing black fruits with strong flavors of black coffee. Barrel aging, just under a year in one-quarter new French oak, brings up flavors of dark chocolate and chicory. It's delicious and substantial, ready for drinking over the next half decade or longer.
title: Ayoub 2015 Memoirs Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Ballard Canyon, Central Coast, California, US
description: Clean aromas of cinnamon, dried rose petals and tight raspberry fruit make for a very pretty nose on this lighter delicate style of Grenache. The palate is quite fresh, with cherry, cranberry, orange rind, dried ginger, cracker pepper and sumac flavorings combining for a very refreshing, elegant bottling.
title: Crawford Family 2015 Kimsey Vineyard Grenache (Ballard Canyon)

description: This wine exhibits a beautifully pristine, precise and elegant character. It opens with delicate scents of apple flesh, orange blossom, saline and just a hint of toasted, earthy spice. That briny, mineral seam continues to shine on the palate, dancing through the fresh fruit flavors and leaving a lip-smacking kiss on the finish. A stunner now, this will continue to evolve and mature well through 2022.
title: Crystallum 2015 The Agnes Chardonnay (Western Cape)

description: This wine offers aromas of black, cherry, raspberry and violet. The tannins are barely noticeable at first, slowly building as flavors of cherry, cassis, violet and chocolate-covered espresso bean make their way across the palate. Hints of spice and cherry are evident throughout the prolonged finish.
title: Dalton 2014 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This wine sees 40% French oak and is a reserve-level barrel selection. Soft and smooth, the pretty berry and plum fruit has a touch of mocha, but not too much. It's had ample bottle time to be ready to enjoy immediately.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2013 Estate Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from one of the state's premier Syrah vineyards, the aromas pop, with notes of boysenberry, bacon fat, orange peel, herb and spice. The palate is dense and rich, packed with ultraripe blue- and black-fruit flavors. It delivers a lot of hedonistic appeal. For those looking for a big, bold, warm vintage Syrah, this one's for you.
title: Efeste 2015 Jolie Bouche Boushey Vineyard Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: This wine is delightfully fruit-forward in cherry cola and strawberry jam, balancing the strong well-defined flavors against a backdrop of powerful tannin and oak.
title: Eric Kent 2015 Sascha Marie Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Savory and vibrant, this bright polished sparkler delivers aromas and flavors of red cherry, crushed raspberry, Rennet apple, tangy nectarine and bread crust. Refreshing acidity and a lively, refined perlage lift the finish.
title: Ferghettina 2013 Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Focused and elegantly structured, this sparkling blend has enticing scents of orchard fruit, citrus and brioche. The vibrant, precise palate evokes white cherry, peach and tangerine zest framed in bright acidity and a refined lively mousse. A mineral note closes the dry finish.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2013 Freccianera Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas of cherry, raspberry and coffee bean set the stage for flavors of black cherry, blackberry, apple peel, white chocolate and baking spice. Opulent tannins coat palate, making way for a juicy, blueberry pie flavored finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2013 Gilgal Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The Latchkey vineyard is shared with Cody Wright (of Purple Hands). Spicy cranberry, strawberry and raspberry fruit shines here, young and fresh and vibrant. It's snappy and sappy, with good grip and supporting acidity. It should drink best in the early 2020s.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Latchkey Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: There's a dusty character to the tannins, which complement the tart pomegranate and raspberry fruit. It's already quite smooth, lightly toasty and finished with a dollop of cinnamon.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Savoya Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: This shows power and richness with a bit of finesse as well. Accompanying a deep, dark hue are equally dense aromas of black cherry and blackberry, cola, cocoa and a hint of dried herbs. The palate comes with chewy tannins and more plush fruit, but this well-structured wine should come into its own with great dignity soon enough. Drink 2020–2032.
title: Kilikanoon 2013 Oracle Shiraz (Clare Valley)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is a terrific wine from a classic estate which sits at 2,700 in the Sierra Foothills. It offers rich vanilla and cream aromas, with abundant pear and nutmeg flavor balanced by subtle acidity.
title: Lava Cap 2016 Reserve Chardonnay (El Dorado)

description: A fifty-fifty blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Nero, this opens with enticing scents of brioche, white stone fruit, citrus and red berry. Mirroring the nose, the vibrant palate offers tart sour cherry, pomegranate, bread dough and tangerine zest alongside a refined perlage and tangy acidity. A bitter almond note marks the close.
title: Le Marchesine 2012 Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Leeuwin's characteristic elegance and approachability, combined with ageability, are in full force here. Trademark pencil lead, eucalyptus and red currant characters mingle with dried flowers, herbs and furniture polish. The tannins are dusty and tight-grained, with an herbaceous currant flavor continuing throughout. Drink now–2032.
title: Leeuwin Estate 2014 Prelude Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon (Margaret River)

Appellation: Dunnigan Hills, Central Valley, California, US
description: This wine from Matchbook has been improving steadily over recent vintages and now it's taken a big step toward more finesse and less flash. A very soothing mouthfeel and generous, but not overt, flavors make a great combination in this creamy, yummy and full-bodied wine. It has everything from toasty oak and lemon curd to mild marzipan nuances that interlace with good acidity for a balanced palate and a lingering finish.
title: Matchbook 2016 Estate Bottled Old Head Chardonnay (Dunnigan Hills)

description: Tinta Negra is a hybrid grape that takes on the characteristics of other Madeira grapes. To taste, this oak-aged wine could almost be a Bual. Smooth and rich, it has a sweet toffee and caramel character to go alongside the intense acidity. Drink now.
title: Miles 1996 Tinta Negra Mole (Madeira)

description: Delicately wood aged, this wine is beautifully structured and balanced. Ripe apple and quince fruits are cut with acidity and a clean, clear minerality. The wine needs to age for a while and will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Niepoort 2016 Redoma Branco Reserva White (Douro)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Black fruits dominate this full-bodied block selection. The alcohol is pulled back a half step from the previous vintage, and there's a pleasing minerality to the foundation. A hint of caraway comes up as it winds down through the chocolatey finish.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Balcombe Vineyard Block 1B Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Blue flower, Asian spice and wild berry aromas mingle with a whiff of espresso. The rounded generous palate offers baked plum, fleshy blackberry, licorice and a hint of mocha alongside supple tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Poggio alla Sala 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Seriously wood aged, this reserva is meant to be matured over several years. Firm tannins, rich black fruits and concentration provide the aging potential. It's a rich, dense wine, still with its dry core. Give this ripe wine until 2020.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2015 Quinta dos Aciprestes Reserva Red (Douro)

description: While the nose is intensely honeyed and floral, there's a steely mineral intensity on the palate of this elegant, dry Riesling. Pristine white grapefruit, lime and gooseberry flavors are invigorating and tart but fall on the palate in creamy, expansive layers. It's an impeccably balanced wine with a long, honed finish.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Graach Josephshöfer Trocken GG Riesling (Mosel)

description: Hints of smoke and pencil shavings accent fragrant cherry blossom and pink grapefruit notes in this feather-light kabinett. It's a juicy, unctuously fruity wine but structured keenly by laser-light acidity and earthy nuances that linger long on the finish.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Scharzhofberger Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Named after the “Son of Apulia,” Frederick II of Swabia—the 13th century Emperor of the Holy Roman Empire who made his summer home the Castel del Monte, for which the DOCG is now named—this is a pure expression of the Nero di Troia variety. Aromas of dark cherries, spiced bramble berries, old leather and anise permeate the nose and carry through to a tightly woven, grippy palate. There's wonderful tension, depth and complexity to this medium-bodied wine which needs a few years to soften its tannic edge. Drink from 2020.
title: Rivera 2012 Puer Apuliae Nero di Troia (Castel del Monte)

description: Plumes of ripe peach, apricot and mango are exuberant on the nose, while the fruit profile on the palate is markedly demure, offering crisp but penetrating hits of gooseberry, green plum and lime. It's taut and nervous on the palate, with a firm mineral undertone. While elegant already, this should meld its fruit and mineral intensities after a few years in the cellar. Hold till 2019.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Kiedrich Turmberg Trocken Erste Lage Riesling (Rheingau)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: It's at least the third excellent vintage in a row from Ruby Hill, which is a historic name in Livermore Valley. Rich, concentrated and oaky, this is a no-holds-barred wine that layers toasted, smoky, spicy aromas overripe pear and nutmeg flavors. The body is full and the texture is mouth-filling.
title: Ruby Hill Winery 2015 Jewel Collection Series Estate Reserve Chardonnay (Livermore Valley)

description: Deeply concentrated lemon and pineapple meld seamlessly into earthier complexities of crushed earth and flint in this vivacious spätlese. It's deft and dancing on the palate, with a piercing honey-struck finish. Drinks well already but should improve further through 2025.
title: St. Urbans-Hof 2016 Nik Weis Goldtröpfchen Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: A rich style of Paso Cab, this bottling starts with aromas of rich black-cherry cream, vanilla, nutmeg, gravel and cinnamon. The palate is full of caramel, French toast and kirsch flavors at first, but then the chalky, still somewhat stiff tannins add a mouth-coating texture.
title: TW Ferm Co. 2014 Straight Cab Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Berry, apple and pastry aromas delicately swirl in the glass while the vibrant palate offers white peach, tangy tangerine zest and white cherry. An elegant, persistent perlage and bright acidity provide elegance and energy while a ginger note wraps around the finish.
title: Vigna Dorata NV Rosé  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Toasty dark-fruit aromas are compact. In the mouth, this wine is more screechy, tannic and taut. Oaky and berry flavors are balanced and integrated on a long finish with driving acidity. Drink this strong-boned Tempranillo from a lesser vintage through 2028.
title: Viña Vilano 2014 Legón Premium  (Ribera del Duero)

description: This offers delicate scents of spring blossom, white stone fruit and a whiff of bread crust. On the vivacious palate, vigorous bubbles and bright acidity frame yellow apple, chopped herb and tangerine zest.
title: Monogram NV Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: Earthy, herbal aromas are a bit murky and reflect a marginal Toro vintage in 2014. A grabby, overextracted palate delivers rock-hard tannins along with gummy flavors of blackberry and blueberry. On the finish, creamy oak takes on a clove-like flavor.
title: Muruve 2014 Elite  (Toro)

description: This Merlot brings a lot of aromas and flavors to the table, with tones of baked red and black fruits, nutmeg, clove and balsamic. The tannins are silky and the acidity gives a fresh lift to the feel.
title: Narmada 2012 Merlot (Virginia)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: This clean, grassy wine is a pure and pleasing example of varietal character, from a cool-climate region on the northern Oregon border. Lemon and lime rind and grapefruit flesh combine with plenty of lip-tingling acidity. It's not a big wine, but well done, lively and refreshing.
title: Phelps Creek 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Designation: Val de la Loba Paraje Singular
description: A pinched, rubbery nose hints at volatility. In the mouth, this single-vineyard Mencía is wiry, tough and high in tartaric acidity. Savory, saucy plum and berry flavors are salty on a dense finish.
title: Pittacum 2014 Val de la Loba Paraje Singular Mencía (Bierzo)

description: A blend of Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon, this easygoing red has aromas of iris, underbrush band and Mediterranean scrub. The juicy palate delivers Marasca cherry, white pepper and tobacco alongside velvety tannins. It's made to be drunk young so enjoy soon.
title: Poggio al Tufo 2015 Rompicollo  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Light aromas of cherry, currant and wild herbs carry the nose. Medium in weight, with vibrant acidity countering sandy tannins, crunchy red fruit flavors dominate the palate, yet are restrained by a core of granite and crushed herbs.
title: Polvanera 2013 Aglianico (Puglia)

description: Lightly spiced plum aromas are minerally and come with a hint of nutmeg. A flush, bouncy palate is made tougher by rubbery tannins, while spicy plum and blueberry flavors are solid through the back end. The blend offers Grenache, Syrah, Carignan and Cabernet Sauvignon.
title: Portal del Priorat 2014 Luno Red (Montsant)

description: This second wine from Quinta de Roriz is ready to drink young, with warm red fruits, soft tannins and juicy acidity. It has structure as well, which will allow it to develop further although it is already delicious and juicy. Drink now or wait until 2019.
title: Prats & Symington LDA 2016 Prazo de Roriz Red (Douro)

description: The second wine of this great Douro quinta is generous and warm, made to be drunk young. Its rich tannins are equalled by the licorice and ripe black-fruit flavors, smooth structure and good acidity. Drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Quinta da Romaneira 2015 Sino da Romaneira Red (Douro)

description: The vineyards on the south bank of the Douro face north, giving respite from the sun and producing an elegant style of wine. This is perfumed, fruity and concentrated from the old vines used in the blend. Drink this juicy wine from 2019.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2015 Quinta dos Aciprestes Red (Douro)

description: Delicate aromas of baking spice and red plum come together on this. Made entirely with Sangiovese, the palate delivers wild cherry, vanilla and a touch of coconut alongside round tannins.
title: Ricci Curbastro NV Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Dense dark fruit aromas blend with sweet tobacco leaf and anise on the nose. Solidly built with grippy tannins and structured acidity, the palate offers a good concentration of more dark fruit, invigorated by accents of spice and crushed herbs.
title: Rivera 2012 Cappellaccio Aglianico (Castel del Monte)

description: Aromas of white flower, beeswax and stone fruit mingle in the glass. The lively palate delivers Bartlett pear, white peach and a touch of ginger alongside fresh acidity.
title: Rocca di Montemassi 2016 Calasole White (Maremma Toscana)

description: This is principally Syrah and Merlot, with smaller amounts of other Bordeaux grapes in the mix. It's fruity, fresh and flavorful, with berries and cherries in abundance, along with scents and flavors of smoke and roasted coffee. Call it a fine, gulpable, everyday red.
title: Rock Point NV River Red (Oregon)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a decent serving of thyme and sagebrush on the nose of this affordable bottling alongside the piquant raspberry and cranberry fruit, with a touch of wet earth in the background. The decently weighted palate shows cranberry and more dried herbs, gaining complexity toward the finish.
title: Rock View 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Monterey County)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Pinot Noir is most often done in a rounder fruitier style in Southern Oregon, and this pins the tail on that characteristic. It's a sturdy, blocky and tasty wine—a bit tannic, with flavors of black cherry, dried herbs and a lick of espresso.
title: Schmidt 2014 Pinot Noir (Applegate Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The price and special designation suggest that this is considered a reserve-level cuvée. It seems quite dry, even astringent, with tea-flavored tannins around thin berry fruit. There are hints of mint, wintergreen and chocolate, but the wine doesn't show much depth. Perhaps more bottle age is needed. Two bottles were tasted with comparable notes.
title: Sokol Blosser 2015 Twelve Row Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Red cherry and blackberry aromas meld with snippets of baking spice and menthol on the nose. The medium-bodied palate is firm, with structured tannins and crisp acidity, housing juicy berry flavors dotted with red floral tones. Dark plum rounds out the finish.
title: Tormaresca 2015 Torcicoda Primitivo (Salento)

description: A blend of 45% Sangiovese, 45% Cabernet Sauvignon and 10% Petit Verdot, this has delicate aromas of tilled soil and black-skinned berry. The firm palate offers black-currant jam, tobacco and leather alongside closed-grained tannins.
title: Villa Pinciana 2012 Terraria  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Just a touch of red cherry fruit show on the nose. The palate is light and slender but fresh in feel, with smooth, unobtrusive tannins. This will work well chilled. Drink soon.
title: Willm 2016 Réserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This blend of three local grapes including Albariño opens with briny core aromas surrounded by white-fruit notes. The palate hits with a piercing shot of acidity but then turns thicker and more syrupy. Flavors of passion fruit and nectarine are slightly briny and finish plump.
title: Altos de Torona 2016 Rosal Caiño Albariño-Loureiro White (Rías Baixas)

description: The nose isn't very expressive but the palate offers mature yellow apple, bread crust and citrus alongside a note of honeyed nut. Bright acidity and small bubbles refresh the finish.
title: Bèlon du Bèlon 2009 Pas Dosé Riserva  (Franciacorta)

description: In the village of Arcins, in the heart of the Haut-Médoc, this estate belongs to the Castel family, one of the largest drinks companies in France. This wine, with its generous tannins and ripe blackberry fruit, is easy, generous and likely to age well and quickly. Its tannic structure is fully integrated into the fruitiness. Drink this wine from 2023.
title: Château Barreyres 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine from an 11-acre property is soft and ripe, with plenty of ripe berry fruits. Hints of the 18 months in wood are still there although they are not at all obtrusive. It is an easy wine just touched by spice, and will be ready to drink by 2018.
title: Château Carreyre 2015  Margaux

description: Vineyards in the Press family's 450-acre estate surround one of the Médoc's grand chateaus owned by Bordeaux elite over centuries. The wine from 155 planted acres has been spiced with 12 months in wood, giving a toasty character to the juicy black-currant fruit. It is a structured wine, ripe and with good potential. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Castera 2015  Médoc

description: The 39-acre estate is under the same ownership as Château les Trois Manoirs. This wine has a generous use of wood that has nicely stopped short of smothering the fruit. While the wine offers plenty of spice and toast, it also offers juicy black currants and acidity. The two elements are coming together and the wine will be ready from 2023.
title: Château Chantemerle 2015  Médoc

description: This estate in the northern vineyards of Bordeaux is planted to a blend of 75% Merlot and the two Cabernets. The wine has structure as well as rich berry fruits. Its tannins mark it out as still young, and this potentially ripe, warm wine should be drunk from 2019.
title: Château Dallau 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: This spicy, toasty has the potential for bright berry fruits. With acidity as well as a dry structure, it will need aging. This family estate dates back to the 19th century. Drink from 2023.
title: Château du Raux 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This light, fruity wine already has a good balance between bright fruits and the wood-aging. With black-currant flavors from the 60% Cabernet Sauvignon and spice from the 12 months wood aging, this wine is still structured and will be best from 2021.
title: Château Landat 2015  Haut-Médoc

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: It's no surprise that this appropriately named blend of 76% Petite Sirah, 20% Tannat and 4% Petit Verdot is dense and dark, starting with aromas of crushed asphalt, elderberry jam, white pepper and baked goods. The palate is equally unctuous, showing concentrated purple flowers, tar, more elderberry and a judicious hint of wild mints.
title: MCV 2015 Black Red (Paso Robles)

description: This wine exhibits superb interplay between just-ripe fruit character and earthy, spicy tones. Notes of tree bark, fresh turned soil, cocoa nib and olive mingle harmoniously with fruity tones of black cherry and plum. A sanguine accent dances across the silky, medium-bodied palate, while a peppery kick unfolds on the lingering close. Drink now–2023.
title: Mullineux 2015 Syrah (Swartland)

description: Dirk Niepoort's venture into the Bairrada region at Quinta de Baixo blossoms in this wine. Rich fruit gives the wine both firm tannins and a ripe, crisply textured character. It is balanced with acidity, full of juicy fruit and perfumed with mint. Drink this wine from 2019.
title: Niepoort 2015 Lagar de Baixo Baga (Bairrada)

description: Toast, underbrush, tobacco leaf and cassis aromas lead the nose. Reflecting the cool wet vintage, the palate is austere and lighter bodied than usual but still elegant, offering raw cherry, pomegranate, licorice and ground pepper alongside tight close-grained tannins. Give the tannins several more years to unwind.
title: Ornellaia 2014  Bolgheri Superiore

description: Two clones and two vineyards combine here, with 18 months in French oak pulling it all together. It's a tasty mix of fruit, spice and barrel, with pomegranate and red-plum flavors at the core. Baking spices suggest buttered cinnamon toast, with a touch of cocoa powder adding the coup de grâce.
title: Quintet 2015 Yamhill Duet Pinot Noir

description: Aromas of black plum and coffee bean set the scene for flavors of blackberry and black cherry that are quickly joined by stronger flavors of smoked meat and espresso bean. Rich tannins coat the mouth, receding into a smoke-filled finish.
title: Recanati 2016 Shiraz (Upper Galilee)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Drenched with melted butter and vanilla flavors, but not blatantly oaky, this full-bodied Chardonnay is so lush that it's almost sweet. It seems to expand on the palate, brimming with delicious Fuji apple and Bosc pear notes that don't quit but last through a lingering finish.
title: Ruby Hill Winery 2015 Reserve Collection Chardonnay (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Clear varietal character comes out in this wine, with solid black-cherry fruit and highlights of licorice, espresso, vanilla and black tea. The concentration and balance are impressive, and it lingers with a late show of power. This winery does far better with its red wines than its whites.
title: Schmidt 2014 Syrah (Applegate Valley)

description: A solid effort, this brings plum and blueberry, black cherry and baking spices together in a substantial, even muscular style. Earthy tannins form a strong base, and the overall sturdy character, along with the balance and texture, suggest that this will age nicely over the next decade. Drink now through the late 2020s.
title: WildAire 2015 Timothy Pinot Noir

Appellation: Templeton Gap District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fresh pressed boysenberry, fennel bulb, white pepper dust and Italian herbs show on the nose of this blend of 40% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Syrah and 25% Zinfandel. The leathery tannins grab hold of the palate rather quickly, and still need some time to smooth out, yet provide solid structure from which to hang the black plum and mulberry flavors.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2015 Fire Sign Red (Templeton Gap District)

description: This vineyard emphasizes rather steely mineral-driven flavors. The fruit is ripened in an austere mode, firm and sculpted, with flavors of wild mountain berry. A whiff of tobacco follows through, with a similar vein in the finish. It's a dense wine that will reward aggressive decanting.
title: Ayoub 2015 Brittan Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)

description: Savory tones of smoke and crushed earth permeate from nose to finish, suggesting a complex auslese that's more mineral than fruit. Still, vibrant lemon-lime acidity and silken streaks of honey penetrate the midpalate and extend through a long, invigorating finish. It drinks well now but should show even better from 2021.
title: Bollig-Lehnert 2016 Dhroner Hofberger Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Formerly the property of one of Napoleon's generals, this estate is now performing impressively after renovations. This wine is richly structured and packed with black fruits. Dark tannins vie with black currants and ripe spice in this wine that will age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Bernadotte 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This is a fruity wine with lovely acidity and a firm background of tannins. The black-currant and berry flavors are up front, giving delicious acidity and a crisp aftertaste. Drink this fruity wine from 2023.
title: Château Couhins 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: Earthy berry aromas are sweetened by notes of raisin, creamy oak and root beer. This gran reserva is more easygoing, plump and chunky than what you'd expect from a great vintage like 2010, with flavors of tobacco, woodspice, vanilla, cherry and berry fruit. Cocoa is a prime flavor on the finish. Drink now through 2023.
title: Covila 2010 II Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Veteran vintners Bruno D'Alfonso and Kris Curran manage to tease out uniquely fresh aromas and flavors from this Syrah, starting with the nose, which is full of cola, root beer, beet juice and Mission fig scents. The medium-bodied palate shows lots of flowery purple fruit and candied purple-fruit flavors, with a hint of licorice spice.
title: D'Alfonso-Curran 2014 Black Oak Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: An abundance of spicy smoky oak aromas meets equally abundant flavors of dark chocolate, cocoa and raspberry in this full-bodied and heavily tannic wine. The color is about as dark as it gets, the mouthfeel is massive and yet the flavors are so big they balance out the huge structure. That it was aged for 30 months in 80% new French barrels explains the alluring spiciness.
title: Dante Robere 2014 Petite Sirah (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: From one of the state's top Syrah vineyards, this offers aromas of dried and fresh herb, violet and black cherry that lead to full-bodied, extremely pure dark-fruit flavors. Aged in three- to five-year old-oak, it has a purity and mouthfeel that add to the appeal. It should show well on the dinner table.
title: Descendant 2014 Les Collines Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Plump yellow cherry, peach and apricot abound in this juicy, deeply concentrated kabinett. It's an intensely sun-kissed, almost tropical wine but light as a feather on the palate and deftly balanced by spine tingling acidity.
title: Dr. Heidemanns-Bergweiler 2016 Graacher Himmelreich Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: While smoky and earthen on the nose, the palate of this light-footed auslese is plush, almost fleshy, with unctuous yellow-peach, mango and tangerine flavors. Racy lime acidity revitalizes the midpalate, leading to a long, clean finish. Drink now through 2025.
title: Dr. Hermann 2016 Erdener Prälat Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is an expressive yet well-balanced wine, with wonderful interplay between the dense black-fruit characteristics. Brambly boysenberry, blackberry and cherry meld seamlessly with notes of sweet spice, purple violets, game and white pepper. The palate's slightly creamy texture is countered by framing tannins that lend grip and structure through the finish. Drink now–2023.
title: Fram 2015 Shiraz (Swartland)

description: Named after the latin name of the Ionian sea (Jonio), this single-variety Negroamaro has a dense, permeating nose of jammy red and black berries, tobacco leaf, crushed cocoa nibs and graphite. Firmly medium in weight, the palate shows a vibrant force of tart berries and game that turns richer and riper come the midpalate, translating as a lingering red cherry note on the finish. Sandy tannins offer plenty of grip, yet the bright acidity and pervasive fruit tones keep everything in check.
title: Gianfranco Fino 2015 Jo Negroamaro (Salento)

description: Made primarily with Vermentino, this opens with delicate scents of white spring flower, yellow stone fruit, citrus and a whiff of honey. Mirroring the nose, the fresh enveloping palate doles out dried apricot, juicy nectarine drop and a honeyed mineral note alongside tangy acidity. Hold for even more complexity. Drink through 2021.
title: Guado al Melo 2016 Criseo White (Bolgheri)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Plum, sweet cinnamon and cardamom are gracefully threaded through the core of brambly berry fruit. Aged for 11 months in barrel, it finishes with a streak of vanilla.
title: Gypsy Dancer 2014 Legacy Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Firm tannins are well integrated with the black-currant fruits in this dense, ripe and very fruity wine. It is juicy and perfumed with a generous texture and good freshness. The Merlot sings in this wine, bright and structured. Drink from 2021.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Château Laforge  (Saint-Émilion)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's strong woody spice on the nose of this appellation blend with sandalwood and lacquer aromas meeting with stewed plums, mulberries and candied flowers. Those same fruit flavors show on the sip, alongside a pine-needle lift. It is a quite rich expression.
title: Kaena 2015 Grenache (Santa Ynez Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This 100% varietal wine is a blend of fruit from top sites Klipsun (60%) and Dioynsus. Baking spice, herb, cocoa, tobacco, espresso and cherry aromas lead to suave, silky cherry and cocoa flavors. Like the vintage it came from, it holds nothing back. The oak (65% new French) is a bit up front at present but should integrate with additional time in bottle. Best after 2020.
title: Kerloo 2015 Calling Club Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The vanilla and wood scent of new oak is evident on the nose. On the palate this is more integrated, the fine wood notes intertwining with the ripe cherry fruit. The tannin structure is fine and firm, supported by vivid freshness. This is lovely now but will mellow over the coming years. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2015 Trois Châteaux Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Plump aromas of poached pear, Meyer lemon pith, honeysuckle and an underlying slate-like minerality hit the nose on this bottling. There's a roundness to the palate of Asian pear, but the sip is all about grippy texture and sizzling citrus energy that carries deep into the finish.
title: Longoria 2016 Clover Creek Vineyard Albariño (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: This is a well-made version of the variety, 100% in this case. Earthy tobacco, clove and dusty oak ride along seamless tannin structure and plenty of length and breadth. Balanced within a full-bodied sense of powerful weight and presence, it softens on the finish to reveal lighter touches of pencil shavings and clove.
title: Mira 2013 Petit Verdot (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara, Central Coast, California, US
description: A very reductive take on the grape, this bottling shows lawn cuttings, guava skin, tangy green orange pith on the nose, but also hints of an oily underlying structure. Those aromas come together on the palate, with dried grass and citrus skins proving rather pungent.
title: Montemar Wines 2014 Vogelzang Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)

description: Pear and melon aromas are good but basic. Citric acidity is grabbing, while citrus and peach flavors show a hint of green pepper on the backside. On the finish, the fruit character doesn't last long. Drink immediately.
title: Nessa 2016 Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: Aromas of charred wood and black pepper meet concentrated flavors of black cherry and blackberry in this full-bodied wine. A hint of sweetness rounds its edges on the palate and gives a jammy note to the finish.
title: Old Soul 2016 Petite Sirah (Lodi)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Barrel fermented in 20% new French oak, using indigenous yeasts, this is soft and supple, with a sense of impending oxidation. Bruised apples and pears constitute the fruit, and it should be consumed over the next year or two.
title: Omero 2016 Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)

description: Medicinal, rubbery aromas suggest earthy berry, compost and latex. This Grenache-Carignan-Syrah blend feels baked and chunky but not fully defined. Roasted dark-berry flavors finish heavy and without finesse.
title: Portal del Priorat 2015 Negre de Negres Red (Priorat)

description: Bright aromas of strawberry, lavender, violets and rooibos are met with cedar and ginger. These carry through to the palate where an element of charred wood is introduced. Ample acidity and a mid-weight body offer a nice mouthfeel and a long, woody finish.
title: Rosemont NV Virginia Red (Virginia)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: A wealth of oak tones—wood, vanilla and cocoa—ride over a core of cherry jam. The full-bodied palate follows suit, with pillowy tannins and mild acidity giving just enough support. The oak sticks out a bit, but beneath that layer is a rich fruity core.
title: San Marzano 2014 Annivesario 62 Riserva  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: Solid plum and berry aromas lead to a fruity palate that's bouncy but not deep or layered. Candied berry flavors are satisfactory, but kind of basic. A flush finish closes out this varietal Monastrell.
title: Sierra Norte 2016 Porta Regia Colección Privada 5 Meses Monastrell (Jumilla)

description: Made with 60% Sangiovese and 40% Alicante, this opens with aromas of leather, baking spice and a hint of violet. The easygoing palate suggests red cherry, raspberry jam and star anise alongside smooth tannins.
title: Tenuta L'impostino 2014 Ottava Rima  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Pleasing aromas of spiced bramble, anise and dark flowers give a good start to this wine. Medium in weight, with a mix of ripe and tart berries, this wine is well-balanced and well-structured but simplistic in its profile.
title: Tormaresca 2015 Trentangeli Rosso Red (Castel del Monte)

description: Attractive in underripe peach and spry lemon aromas, the medium-bodied palate shows juicy melon and stone fruit flavors balanced by lemon-lime acidity. This is a crisp, refreshing Chardonnay, perfect for simple enjoyment.
title: Tormaresca 2016 Chardonnay (Puglia)

description: This wine is straightforward and rich in ripe tropical fruit aromas and flavors. Well-balanced acidity helps maintain freshness on the medium-bodied palate, leading to a crisp, clean finish.
title: Tormaresca 2017 Chardonnay (Puglia)

description: This has subdued aroma suggesting forest floor, leather and a whiff of menthol. The light-bodied palate offers tart sour cherry, cranberry and a note of dark culinary spice alongside racy acidity.
title: Triacca 2014 Casa La Gatta  (Valtellina)

description: Woody berry aromas are hot but also creamy and show vanilla notes. A bold, grabby palate delivers oaky flavors of prune, spice and chocolate. Some heat and aggressiveness must be dealt with on a firm finish.
title: Vega Sauco 2016 La Sonrisa del Nómada  (Toro)

description: This opens with aromas of toasted oak, ripe apricot and butterscotch while the textured palate offers cedar, vanilla and lemon drop. It closes on a note of bitter almond.
title: Villa Pinciana 2015 Viognier (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Whiff of wild berry, moist earth and chopped herb aromas merge with a blue flower note. Suggestions of the nose carry over to the light-bodied delicate palate along with a hint of ground pepper. Bright acidity and dusty, not very persistent tannins provide gauzy support. Drink soon.
title: Villa S. Anna 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Intriguing notes of curing spice, game, green pepper, coriander and cedar grace the black-fruit core of this Cab. The tannins are soft and approachable, while seductive notes of campfire and charred fruit skin unfold on the finish. Drink now.
title: Warwick 2015 The First Lady Cabernet Sauvignon (Western Cape)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This is fresh, fruity and off dry, with a buttery smooth mouthfeel. There are mixed tree fruits and just enough acidity to offset the sweetness adequately. It's a perfect springtime sipping wine, low in alcohol, ripe with fruit flavor, and should be consumed over the next year or two.
title: Whitetail Ridge 2015 Müller-Thurgau (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Outer Coastal Plain, New Jersey, US
description: Fresh lemon juice freshens the richer aromas of butter cookie and caramelized sugar. The creamy mouthfeel gives way to bright, juicy pineapple, lemon curd and vanilla flavors, finishing crisp and fruity.
title: William Heritage 2016 Unoaked Chardonnay (Outer Coastal Plain)

description: This rare native grape creates a wine that is enjoyable in primary fruit expression. Floral and spicy, with a wealth of red cherry and black berry flavors, this is light bodied but crisp, clean and perfect with a slight chill.
title: A Mano 2016 Imprint Susumaniello (Salento)

description: Ripe red fruits dominate this soft wine. The fruitiness of Trincadeira is boosted by the firmer character of Alicante Bouschet and Touriga Nacional, yielding a rich wine that is smooth and juicy, with spice and dark-coffee flavors. Drink now.
title: Adega Cooperativa de Vidgueira 2016 Bilontra Red (Alentejo)

Appellation: Contra Costa County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This medium-bodied, dry and spicy-tasting wine has a relatively light color, aromas like cedar and cinnamon and lean cherry flavors. A definite touch of tannin adds grip to the mouthfeel.
title: Campos Family Vineyards 2015 Gigi's Blend Estate Red (Contra Costa County)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is mostly Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah. Aromas of blueberry pie and huckleberry pop, showing grace notes of barrel spice and herb. The flavors are lighter in style, losing concentration in the middle and seeming watery, and never come back together.
title: Canoe Ridge 2016 The Expedition Red (Horse Heaven Hills)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: In terms of sheer hedonism, this is a successful wine, full of oaky aromas of vanilla cake, caramel and plum pudding, loaded with crème brûlée and ripe fruit flavors. But the oak winds up feeling single-noted by the finish, and the grape seems lost in the seasonings.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Reserve Barbera (Paso Robles)

description: Berry and spice aromas that suggest clove open this medium-bodied Garnacha. Spiced berry flavors hint at cinnamon, while chocolate notes are welcome on a plump finish.
title: Cellers Unió 2015 Perlat Garnacha (Montsant)

description: Sauvignon Gris may be only 10% of the blend but it gives a delicious perfumed character to this wine. Grapefruit and apricot flavors combine with the mineral texture to give a rich while still taut wine. Drink from late 2018.
title: Château les Clauzots 2016  Graves

description: This small 12-acre property has a high proportion of Merlot in its blends. Still packed with high-toast wood flavors, this wine has a future, with plenty of ripe red-berry fruits and a generous texture. Wait for the wood to dissipate and for the fruit to come forward.
title: Château Moulin de Canhaut 2015  Médoc

description: This wine is rich with sweetness although it shows only a light botrytis character. It has a touch of spice from the wood aging as well as honey and lemon to balance the sweetness and acidity. Even though young, it is ready to drink.
title: Château Villefranche 2015  Sauternes

description: Aromas of juicy black plums and raspberries meld with vanilla and sage on the nose. Loaded with flavors of vanilla and baked plums, the palate is plush in feel, with hints of raspberry, white pepper and oregano. Mellow acidity fades into the medium-length finish, showing soft notes of blackberry speckled with pepper.
title: Creekbend 2015 Estate Bottled Crimson Cabernet (Indiana Uplands)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Bright, pleasant aromas of yellow apple, pear and papaya lead to a palate chock full of tropical-fruit flavors. It finishes a little tangy.
title: Drink Washington State 2015 Welcome to Columbia Valley Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Derived from an assortment of Dundee Hills vineyards, this pays tribute to the Jory soils of the AVA. Strong mineral accents keep a tight focus on brambly cherry flavors. The barrel aging, which was minimal given the wine's early release, adds just a touch of caramel.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Volcanic Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Across the river from the small town of Pinhão and some of the finest Port vineyards, this estate is well situated. This wine comes from 100-year-old vines and boasts great concentration and richness. It is dense and full of black fruits, ripe from a fine year in the Douro. It will age well; drink after 2021.
title: Quinta das Carvalhas 2015 Carvalhas Vinhas Velhas Red (Douro)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: This wine comes from an estate owned by the Amorim cork family. Powerful, perfumed tannins and dense texture from the old-vine Touriga Nacional give a rich character to the wine. Big, bold red fruits are laced with acidity and packed with concentration. Drink this wine from 2021.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2015 Grande Reserva Red (Douro)

description: This quinta, named after its cypress trees, has produced a firm and structured wine in this vintage. Tannins and a dry core give a great possibility for aging. It is rich and the black fruits will come through the structure. Drink this wine from 2021.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2013 Quinta dos Aciprestes Grande Reserva Red (Douro)

description: While lithe and lean in style, this dry Riesling builds in mineral intensity on the palate. Lime, lemon and green apple flavors are vivaciously fresh, fringed by hints of candlewax and blossom. The finish is long, steely and linear.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Scharzhofberger Trocken GG Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
