description: Roasted aromas of wet campfire, black pepper and roasted lamb crust meet with a pickled boysenberry scent on the nose of this bottling, the first time that this winery has made two estate Syrahs. (Hillside is the other.) The palate shows a strong lift from tarragon and mint, but the gamy quality is powerful as well as is the spice, with Szechuan peppercorn and kung pao beef flavors.
title: Stolo 2015 Creekside Estate Syrah (San Luis Obispo County)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Mossy forest floor, pine needle, juniper and black pepper aromas show against a backdrop of elderberry on the extremely compelling nose of this bottling by Ernst Storm from a vineyard to the northwest of the Sta. Rita Hills. The palate shows much more of that foresty spice, as well as tapenade, wild berries, bay leaf and thyme.
title: Storm 2015 Duvarita Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)

description: In most years, Alabaster blows you away with its power, concentration and depth of flavors. In 2014, a mediocre year in much of Northern Spain, this is still excellent. Blackberry, cassis and creosote aromas are toasty, while this is flush in feel and severely tannic. Blackberry, mocha and coffee flavors finish with creamy oak and chocolate notes along with meaty tannins. Only 100 cases produced; drink through 2030.
title: Teso La Monja 2014 Alabaster  (Toro)

description: Very pure notes of red cherry fill the nose. The same pure fruit is framed by bright, luminous freshness on the palate, which echoes in flavors of cherry, pepper and licorice. An aromatic, fresh and vivid Pinot made with a light touch.
title: Trimbach 2015 Personnelle Réserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Light in color, this wine offers beguiling aromas with notes of fire pit, flower, stem, potpourri, black raspberry and smoked meat. Ethereally light and enchanting, it's texturally rich and flavorful, with a warm finish—a delicious example of this site and variety.
title: Two Vintners 2015 Claire Boushey Vineyard Grenache (Yakima Valley)

description: Beautifully inviting in aromas of caramelized biscuit, vanilla wafer and baked apple, this is a creamy and lush white wine, full bodied and highly ripe in style. A lovely hit of tangerine-laced acidity keeps it fresh and fleshy.
title: V. Sattui 2015 Chardonnay (Los Carneros)

Appellation: Ballard Canyon, Central Coast, California, US
description: Drake Whitcraft is becoming a master at weaving a wide array of sensory experiences into very light wines. This starts with candied pomegranate, fennel bulb and cracked white pepper on the nose, then hits the palate with bright snappy red fruits, heavy peppery spice and a penetratingly bright acid profile. It goes down easy, but with complexity.
title: Whitcraft 2016 Stolpman Vineyard Grenache (Ballard Canyon)

description: This is a blockbuster wine, full bodied and ripe, yet able to hold its considerable power in check. Its well-integrated oak and sizable structure give it weight and breadth, as well as age-worthy intrigue. Red currant, cedar and cigar box mark the flavor profile. Enjoy best 2024–2034.
title: Whitehall Lane 2014 Millennium MM Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Winemaker Sabrine Rodems manages to tame the richness of this vineyard while retaining its hedonistic joy. Aromas of rose, pomegranate, hibiscus, rhubarb and dark spice lead into pillowy flavors of strawberry, raspberry and clove. This wine feels light and racy yet full of body and flavor.
title: Wrath 2015 Boekenoogen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Paso Robles winemaker Tyler Russell applies that region's rich style to Sta. Rita Hills fruit for massive effect, great for lovers of ripeness and density. Aromas of baked vanilla cake, black cherry, loamy soil and star anise leap from the glass. Then the palate delivers loads of blackberry compote, licorice and clove, letting it all hang out yet bolstered by bright acidity and firm lavish tannins.
title: Cordant 2015 Radian Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pomegranate, carnation, dried cranberry, potpourri, baked earth and vanilla cookie all come to the fore on the nose of this light but expressive wine from the historic vineyard. Stewed red cherry and cranberry arise on the palate alongside thyme, peppercorn and the slightest hint of game.
title: Dolin 2013 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: This deep violet-red colored wine has a nose of cassis, lavender and anise. It is surprisingly soft on first sip, with tannins that gradually increase in power while showcasing flavors of black cherry, dark plum, cranberry, fennel bulb and a touch of mint. The slightly grippy finish offers pronounced notes of cherry and orange zest.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Winemaker's Choice Virtuoso Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This flagship bottling is still a whopper of a wine. This vintage, a blend of 73% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Cabernet Franc and 12% Merlot, offers barely ripe black cherry, green peppercorn and charred fennel on the nose. The palate is loaded with both savory and ripe flavors, from roasted black plums and charred beef to oregano and dill. Very complex and chalky in texture.
title: Justin 2014 Isosceles Red (Paso Robles)

description: Firm and focused, this young wine has miles to go. It opens with bright raspberry and cherry fruit, then puts on bulk in the midpalate, adding lovely orange-peel highlights. Drinking well this young, it has the structure and depth to add many more layers over the next decade or longer. Drink now through 2030 and beyond.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Abbott Claim Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: There is a certain high-toned intensity to many of Ken Wright's 2015s, which amps up the young fruit. Here the dominant flavors are ripe strawberry, vivid and layered against firm ripe tannins. There's a touch of coffee and dark chocolate running through the finish. For reasons unknown, this new cuvée bears the old Ken Wright label design.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Hirschy Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: This radiant savory mountain Nebbiolo opens with enticing scents of wild berry, flint, smoke and a whiff of alpine herb. It's elegant and focused, delivering crushed raspberry, Marasca cherry, tobacco and star anise alongside taut refined tannins and bright acidity. It's already tempting but will develop even more complexity over the next several years or more. Drink 2020–2032.
title: La Perla Marco Triacca 2012 La Mossa  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Enticing scents of yellow stone fruit, acacia and brioche show this wine's Chardonnay pedigree. A refined beading and tangy acidity lend elegance and balance to flavors of creamy yellow pear, apple and lemon. A hint of fennel graces the finish.
title: Le Marchesine 2009 Secolo Novo Riserva Dosage Zero  (Franciacorta)

description: Concentrated tangerine and candied lemon-peel flavors are juxtaposed by complexities of savory earth and smoked nuts. While off dry and sprightly, there's a lusciousness that builds on the midpalate that extends into a long, lavishly fruity finish.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Abtsberg Superior Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep and dark in the glass, this Petite Sirah-dominant blend offers aromas of pure blackberry jam on the nose with hints of dried wild fennel and charred plum. It's rich and dense on the palate, but vivacious with fresh black-fruit energy and lifted by clove and purple-flower flavors. The acidity is bright, and tannins are firm but set in the background.
title: MCV 2015 1105 Red (Paso Robles)

description: A stunning wine, this opens with scents of golden straw, honeydew rind, firm nectarine and fresh honeycomb that all carry through to the medium-weight mouth. The palate boasts a flinty, mineral texture and bright acidity that lends verve and precision to the notes of toasted apple, spiced orange rind and pressed yellow flowers. Hints of fresh herbs and minty fynbos grace the enduring finish. It sure is delicious now, but try this stunner from 2020–2028.
title: Mullineux 2015 Granite Chenin Blanc (Swartland)

description: Aromas suggesting steeped prune, dark culinary spice, new leather, tobacco and balsamic notes lead the nose. The aromas follow over to the firmly structured palate along with raisin and licorice, while tightly knit fine-grained tannins and fresh acidity provide the austere framework. A smoky mineral note lingers on the finish. Drink 2019–2026.
title: Nera 2011  Sforzato di Valtellina

description: This new release, a limited production wine from this estate, is named after Tim Bergqvist, the patriarch of the family that owns this property. The wood aging has given richness, almost creamy sweetness to the ripe fruits. The attractive slight oxidation adds complexity to what is already a fine wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2015 Tim Grande Reserva Red (Douro)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: Almost black in color, this rich, unctuous wine at first seems smooth. Ripe black-plum and jammy berry fruits are ripe and dense. Behind that generous exterior, firm tannins and a dense structure will allow the wine to age well. Drink after 2026.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2014 Vintage  (Port)

description: This wine is richly layered, offering a density of brick, toasty char and dusty rock flavors and aromas—a muscular showing from a warm vintage. Black pepper, leather and dried herbs provide additional complexity around a full-bodied core.
title: Trinchero 2014 BRV Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Lots of herbs, dried red currants and cranberry compote aromas show on the nose of this bottling, which has an earthy, gamy undercurrent. The palate tastes like it has more age on it, which is a good thing for now, with flavors of charred wood, damp forest floor, dried fruit and allspice, all riding a squeaky texture.
title: Wrath 2015 115/667 Pinot Noir (Monterey)

description: A rich and toasty wine, this opens with abundant aromas of baked apple, honey-roasted nuts, lemon oil and ginger-clove orange peel. The medium-weight palate is round and mouthfilling, but with superb acidity to balance, lifting the ripe fruit flavors and lending a saline lift to the finish. Drink this well-balanced beauty now–2024.
title: Paul Cluver 2015 Seven Flags Chardonnay (Elgin)

description: This opens with aromas of woodland berry, alpine herb, menthol and whiffs of oak-driven spice. The elegant precise palate delivers sour cherry, cranberry, star anise and a hint of salty mineral set against a backbone of fine-grained tannins and bright acidity. It's still youthfully austere and needs time to fully develop. Drink 2021–2031.
title: Plozza 2013 RedEdition  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: This dark garnet colored wine has aromas of black cherry and a faint touch of green bell pepper. Sleek tannins coat the palate as flavors of black cherry, raspberry, smoke, black pepper and eucalyptus cavort towards a lasting, smoky finish.
title: Recanati 2014 Special Reserve Red (Galilee)

description: Brioche, toasted nut, acacia honey and dried apricot aromas follow through to the dry vibrant palate along with yellow apple, nectarine and hint of exotic spice. Bright acidity and a refined perlage provide the backbone while a bitter-almond note marks the close.
title: Ricci Curbastro 2009 Gualberto Dosaggio Zero  (Franciacorta)

description: Orange blossoms and crushed mineral tones perfume this delicately off-dry Riesling. Light bodied and spry, the palate is taut, with deeply penetrating high-toned acidity and crunchy peach, tangerine and lemon flavors. Finishes long on notes of lemon zest, fennel and crushed earth.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Tradition Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Perfumed lavender opens the way to a softly layered, structured wine that's medium bodied and restrained in tone. Cassis and red cherry are matched by bright, lingering acidity that provides a welcome succulence throughout.
title: Smoking Gun 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A typical yet classy example of Margaret River, this wine dishes up notes of dark cherry, currant, tobacco, mocha, eucalyptus and dried green herbs. The medium-bodied palate has soft but chewy tannins, with some apparent oak components and a dark-cherry and green-tinged finish. It's elegant and soft enough to drink now but will age well through 2028.
title: Snake and Herring 2014 Dirty Boots Cabernet Sauvignon (Margaret River)

description: Quite pronounced in tropical fruit and floral aromas, this opens with attractive scents of pressed buttercup, honeysuckle and lime lead that frame the guava, pineapple rind and red apple fruit core. Those ripe-fruit tones carry through to the medium-weight palate, with bright, orange-laced acidity to lift. A final note of delicate sweet spice on the way back of the close. Drink now–2022.
title: Spier 2015 Vintage Collection Chenin Blanc (Swartland)

description: This red embraces its main variety's ancestral home, blending in 12% Syrah, 10% Grenache and a pinch of other Rhône reds. Peppery and tannic, it shines in blueberry, cedar, dried herb and cinnamon, begging for more time in the bottle. Enjoy best 2019 through 2029.
title: Stags' Leap Winery 2014 Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Lemon butter and plump pineapple aromas are cut by crushed gravel and an edgy citrus peel character on the nose of this blend of 55% Roussanne, 28% Grenache Blanc and 17% Picpoul Blanc. The Roussanne provides a rich midpalate, full of fleshy but not overripe baked apple flavors, dusted with baking spice and leveled by buoyant acidity.
title: Tablas Creek 2015 Esprit Blanc de Tablas White (Adelaida District)

description: Hits of exotic spice and salt accent rich, ripe streaks of peach, yellow cherry and tangerine in this decadent spätlese. Despite its lavish ripeness, it's a sprightly, revitalizing wine, with zesty acidity and a cool, mineral finish. Drink now through 2025.
title: Thanisch 2016 Lieserer Niederberg Helden Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This small-production wine (just 22 cases) is a blend of 53% Malbec, 42% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Merlot that spent 32 months in 35% new French oak. Aromas of caramel, chocolate cake and black cherry are cut by a minty lift. The palate shows similar weight and richness, with flavors of baked strawberry, cinnamon and caramel sauce. It's soft on the palate and chalky in texture, boasting a  butterscotch finish.
title: TW Ferm Co. 2014 Cultivator Red (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Red and purple flowers meet with crushed slate, turned soil and plump cherry fruits on the nose of this bottling. It's a solid, down-the-middle expression of the grape, with red and black plums, clove and tarragon lifts to the palate.
title: Villa Creek 2015 Garnacha (Paso Robles)

Designation: Zeltinger Sonnenuhr Josefberg Old Vines Spätlese
description: Delicate hints of clay and crushed earth lend savory complexity to this otherwise wildly fruity spätlese. It's unctuously ripe offering loads of summery peach and nectarine flavors but balanced by a spine tingling lemon-lime finish. Drink now through 2025.
title: Weingut Albert Gessinger 2016 Zeltinger Sonnenuhr Josefberg Old Vines Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Rounded and ripe aromas of baked strawberry, red plum, dried rose petal and anise show on the nose of this bottling by theater tech-turned-winemaker Sabrine Rodems. The complex texture is still a bit stiff and sticky on the palate, and the flavors are strong and straightforward, with cranberry and sandalwood. It's clean and refreshing overall, but it could use more time.
title: Wrath 2015 Alta Loma Vineyard Grenache (Monterey)

description: This Chenin Blanc-based wine, with 14% Sémillon, is complex, layered and evolving, expressing different waves of flavor and texture with each sip. Initial scents of lanolin and waxy pink apple transition into earthy notes of dried yellow flowers and grass, while the medium-weight palate evokes tart, refreshing flavors of lime leaf, lemon peel and white melon rind. It's zippy and bright in feel, with lively, briny acidity, and the finish shows fantastic evolution that ends on a earthy, forest-foliage note. Drink now–2025.
title: Alheit 2015 Cartology White (Western Cape)

description: Violets and black cherries perfume this lavish, intensely fruity Pinot Noir. Concentrated in lush black plum and berry flavors, it's a full-bodied and fleshy wine but fringed by soft tannins and a long granite finish. Drink now through 2030.
title: August Kesseler 2015 N Pinot Noir (Pfalz)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: A blend of three vineyards from across the Central Coast, this bottling shows lively aromas of violet and lavender along with black-plum skins and lamb meat. The palate has rosewater, candied rose petals, red plum and a slight maraschino cherry quality.
title: Bonny Doon 2013 Le Posseur Syrah (Central Coast)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Wild berry, dark culinary spice, purple flower and a whiff of aromatic herb are some of the aromas you'll find on this vibrant red. The elegant medium-bodied palate shows pomegranate, tart cherry, clove and star anise alongside bright acidity and polished tannins. Drink over the next several years.
title: Boscarelli 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Orchard fruit, bread crust and a whiff of wild herb lead the nose. On the racy linear palate, firm acidity and a lively perlage underscore yellow apple, Bartlett pear and lemon zest before the crisp dry finish.
title: Castel Faglia 2013 Dosage Zero  (Franciacorta)

description: Inviting aromas of black-skinned berry, underbrush and a whiff of ginger lead the nose on this savory blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc and Merlot. The structured, juicy palate offers ripe black currant, licorice and black pepper alongside polished tannins. Enjoy through 2024.
title: Castello di Bolgheri 2015  Bolgheri Superiore

Appellation: Red Hills Lake County, California, US
description: Sweet and spicy sensations complement the deep dark-berry and chocolate flavors of this full-bodied, dry and firmly mouth-gripping wine. Deep and dark describes the color, too, while the texture is drenched with fine-grained tannins that will add to the wine's longevity. Best after 2020.
title: Chacewater 2014 Astel Petite Sirah (Red Hills Lake County)

description: Densely tannic and full of rich blackberry and black-plum flavors, this is a structured wine. It already balances the fruit and the dry core of tannins, indicating a wine of great potential and richness. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Bellegrave du Poujeau 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Continuing the sequence of fine vintages from this estate, this wine is rich, dense and structured. It has plenty of tannins as well as generous black-plum and spice flavors. Wood aging added light toastiness and is smoothing out the fruit. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Cap Léon Veyrin 2015  Listrac-Médoc

description: This seven-acre property is located to the west of the city of Saint-Émilion on the chalk plateau. This gives the texture to the wine and also the good exposure has added to its richness. Structured with solid tannins and rich with black damson fruits, the wine is ready to age. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Clos Junet 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: Plenty of attention seems to have been lavished on this wine produced from the best parcels on the estate. It offers dense tannins, new-wood mint flavors and an edge of extraction in the bitter-chocolate flavors. With time this will be a rich wine, but one balanced and based around fruit and tannins. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Haut-Piquat 2015 La Fleur de Château Haut-Piquat  (Lussac Saint-Émilion)

description: One of the properties that forms the Chanfreau family's Listrac holdings, this 103-acre estate has produced a powerful, rich wine. With tannins still evident, it will obviously age. But ripe blackberry fruit gives the wine a juicy future. It will be ready to drink from 2025.
title: Château Lestage 2015  Listrac-Médoc

description: This vast, 360-acre estate has produced a rich, generous wine in 2015. Its berry fruits are bold, ripe, full bodied and lifted by acidity. The wood aging (12 months) is very present but not at all excessive. Since it needs some time, drink from 2023.
title: Château Tour Prignac 2015  Médoc

description: Equally divided between Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with a seasoning of Cabernet Franc, this richly perfumed wine is juicy and full of blackberry flavors. From the same ownership as Cambon la Pelouse and in the southern Haut-Médoc, the wine will age well. Drink from 2021.
title: Château Trois Moulins 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: With its 60% Touriga Nacional, this wine has rich, wood-aged perfumed fruit. Black plums and berries come through the tannins and spice to give a ripe character to this big-hearted wine. It still has a dry core and needs to be aged. Drink from 2020.
title: Christie's 2015 Quinta dona Mafalda Vinhas Velhas Red (Douro)

description: This single-vineyard blend of 60% Greco and 40% Malvasia shows spiced aromas of orchard fruit on the nose. Edgy flavors of apple and pear rinds meet almond skin on the medium-bodied palate, balanced by a persistent vein of acidity and a dusting of earthy stone.
title: Botromagno 2015 Poggio al Bosco White (Gravina)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of chopped herb, wild berry and a whiff of cake spice float out of the glass. The taut bright palate offers red cherry, raspberry and hints of star anise set against vibrant acidity and polished tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Cantina del Giusto 2014 San Claudio II  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas suggesting underbrush, oak, new leather and chopped herb come together in the glass. The austere palate offers dried cherry, dried sage and roasted coffee bean set against close-grained tannins that grip the finish.
title: Carpineto 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Made entirely with Syrah vinified and aged in terracotta amphora, this opens with aromas of black currant, black pepper and a whiff of pine forest. The firm but easy-drinking palate offers mature blackberry, cooking spice and clove alongside velvety tannins.
title: Casteani 2013 Marujo  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Sporting a smooth, almost satiny mouthfeel, this high-octane Pinot brings a mouthful of brambly berries, with highlights of lightly peppery herbs. The tannins turn astringent, with a green-tea character. Best consumed over the next four or five years.
title: Catman 2015 Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Heady rose petal notes are framed by lots of earthiness on the nose. The off-dry palate has the same contrast of heady perfume and earthy core on a very fluid, almost oily palate. The finish has a pleasantly bitter freshness.
title: Cave de Ribeauvillé 2013 Clos du Zahnacker White (Alsace)

description: Lovely, herb-edged notes of pear make for a refreshing and engaging nose. The fresh palate is juicy in notes of pear and lemon. This is easy, fruity and dry.
title: Cave de Ribeauvillé 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Red cherry with just a lick of black cherry dominate the nose. The palate tones this exuberant fruit down a little but pairs it with bright freshness. The body remains light throughought and has enticing edges of conifer.
title: Cave de Turckheim 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This opens with delicate aromas of red-skinned berry, underbrush and a whiff of mint. The straightforward palate offers raspberry jam, tobacco and a touch of licorice alongside polished tannins.
title: Caven 2010 Giupa Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: This wine, dominated by ripe Cabernet Sauvignon, is well structured, richly tannic and likely to age well. A firm underlay of dry tannins supports the rich fruit and acidity. It has a crisp final texture. Drink from 2021.
title: Château Bel Air 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: One of a number of estates owned by the Pagès family in the northern Médoc, this property has produced a wood-laden wine, yet one that is also full of ripe fruit. Young at the moment and with the toasty character still prominent, this wine will take its time to bring out the potential of the blackberries and acidity. They are there and will be show best from 2023.
title: Château la Roque de By 2015  Médoc

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: There's a pleasing richness to this wine in its aromas of ripe dark cherry, blackberry and tobacco leaf. The palate brings oak tones that are well-integrated with the juicy dark fruit flavors, the medium-grained, grippy tannins and vibrant acidity offer solid support.
title: Concura 2013 Riserva  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: With a nose of melon and almond blossom, this wine offers flavors of green apple, pear and lemon, with soft floral notes. It is bright on the palate yet soft in the mouth, with a citrus finish that is marked by a touch of bitter almond.
title: Dalton 2016 Estate Unoaked Pinot Gris (Galilee)

description: A ripe notion of yellow pear joins hints of pithy citrus on the nose. The palate represies the notion of very ripe, juicy, yellow pear and the pithy, textural element of citrus. The palate is light and dry but full of fruit. The finish is clean and fresh.
title: Domaine Charles Frey 2016 Blettig Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Very gentle hints of ripe orchard fruit lay on the nose while the palate has the richness of juicy, ripe pear. Easy and harmonious, fresh and lively. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 L'aplomb Ad Quadratum White (Alsace)

description: Tropical fruit and orange blossom aromas lead the nose. The round palate doles out white peach, citrus and honeydew melon alongside fresh acidity. A hint of bitter almond marks the finish.
title: Feliciana 2016 Felugan  (Lugana)

description: One sniff announces that this Viura-Malvasia-Garnacha Blanca blend is fully oaked, while good acidity cuts some of the wine's resiny weight. While barrel-based flavors are firmly in charge, this offers apple and stone-fruit notes prior to a finish that tastes slightly buttery.
title: Fernández Gómez 2016 Selección Familia White (Rioja)

description: While delicate in concentration, this medium-sweet Riesling offers loads of pink grapefruit and honeysuckle tones calibrated by zesty lime and orange peel. It's a fresh, frisky wine, with a lingering honey finish.
title: Friedrich-Wilhelm-Gymnasium 2016 Fritz Willi Feinherb Riesling (Mosel)

description: Aromas of the wood and anise lead to flavors of raspberry, strawberry, cured meat, smoke and eucalyptus. The tart cherry finish has notes of flint and smoke. Slightly grippy tannins and smoky flavors may be a little strong on their own, but this wine will work well alongside fatty cuts of meat.
title: Galil Mountain 2016 Syrah (Upper Galilee)

description: This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Sangiovese opens with aromas of black currant, Mediterranean scrub and cedar. The juicy palate offers dried black cherry, mocha and black pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Grattamacco 2016  Bolgheri

description: Inky red-violet in the glass, this wine has aromas of black plum, cassis and juniper. It is bright on the palate, with smooth tannins that support flavors of cherry, cranberry, chocolate and violet. A quick splash of pomegranate arrives at the finish.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Genesis Shiraz (Galilee)

description: An appealing aroma of ripe pear fills the nose. The palate is similarly abundant in fruit yet ultimately feels clean, dry and slender.
title: Hugel 2016 Cuvée Les Amours Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: This reserve Chardonnay is slightly buttery and yeasty on the fore, brightened by aromas of green apple, fresh melon and a hint of tropical fruit. The palate is tightly structured, with crunchy fruit, a chalky textural weight and a long, green-apple finish.
title: Jacob's Creek 2016 Reserve Chardonnay (Adelaide Hills)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a barrel selection, seemingly chosen for its muscular aspect. The estate's characteristic sweet baking spices are amply in evidence, with ginger-cookie highlights. Fermented with native yeast and aged for 18 months in one-quarter new French oak, it should continue to drink well into the mid-2020s.
title: Johan 2014 Nils Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Made entirely with organically grown Ciliegiolo grapes, this opens with aromas of violet, raspberry and baking spice. On the vibrant palate, velvety tannins support red cherry, cinnamon and vanilla.
title: La Selva 2014  Maremma Toscana

description: Subtle aromas of vanilla, cedar and steeped prune slowly take shape on this. The dense palate offers dried black cherry, mocha, licorice and star anise alongside close-grained tannins.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2013 Corte di Cama  (Sforzato di Valtellina)

description: Crushed aromatic herb, underbrush and balsamic aromas lead the nose. The firm palate offers dried black cherry, mocha and tobacco alongside close-grained, austere tannins. Drink after 2022.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2015 Sassella Sommarovina  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: This wine is decadent without going over the top, offering attractive aromas and flavors of mocha, black forest cake, brambly raspberry and cola spice. The medium-weight palate boasts slight creaminess and velvety tannins, lifted by ripe fruit flavors and ample acidity. Drink now.
title: MAN Family Wines 2015 Skaapveld Shiraz (Coastal Region)

description: A blend of 70% Vermentino and 30% Sauvignon, this opens with aromas of white peach, acacia flower and apricot. The aromas follow through to the ripe palate along with an almond note.
title: Michele Satta 2016 Costa di Giulia White (Bolgheri)

description: This wine is fruit-forward and layered in powerful swathes of grape and cherry flavor, with aspects of earth, forest pine and cardamom seasoning throughout. The structure is moderate and the texture fleshy, with oak pronounced.
title: Napa Cellars 2015 V Collection Pinot Noir (Carneros)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Elton is a new label under the Willamette Valley Vineyards umbrella, though the vineyard has been a favored designate for the past decade. This latest release falls a little short. The barrel flavors of buttered nuts have a slightly oily mouthfeel, and the apple and orange fruit is only moderately ripened. It seems to promise more than it delivers, given the packaging and price.
title: Elton 2015 Chardonnay (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Underbrush, violet, aromatic herb and wild berry aromas appear at the forefront. The light-bodied palate is less expressive, showing dried blackberry and clove alongside dusty tannins.
title: Fattoria Del Cerro 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Exotically floral, this wine is extreme in spicy acidity and robust earthiness, a mix of rose petal, citrus and black tea that struggles somewhat to find balance and fruit.
title: Flowers 2015 Seaview Ridge Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: A nose of honeydew melon and vanilla bean set the stage for flavors of apple, nectarine and hazelnut. This wine is full on the palate, with closing notes of citrus fruit and a hint of toasted nuts.
title: Gva'ot 2016 Dances In White White (Shomron)

description: This blend of Riesling, Vidal Blanc and Seyval Blanc has soft aromas of elderflower, lemon, wet stone and dried apple. Zippy acidity tingles on the palate and lends itself to a long finish full of minerality.
title: James Charles 2016 Cuvée de la Reine White (Virginia)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This wine is made from three barrels were chosen for their particular aroma and flavor characteristics. It carries aggressive, spicy cranberry flavors and searing acidity that cuts the palate short. Drink now.
title: Johan 2014 Three Barrel Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Exuberantly fruity and forward, this light-footed kabinett offers loads of sweet, juicy tangerine, grapefruit and berry flavors. It's not a complex wine but approachable and easy drinking, with a clean mineral finish.
title: Karl Kaspar 2016 Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: This playful, easy-drinking blend of 46% Shiraz, 31% Cinsault, 20% Mourvèdre and 3% Grenache is ripe and fruit-forward, offering oodles of black plum, cherry and currant tones that are hit by accents of blackcurrant leaf and bramble. The juicy palate boasts soft tannins and bright acidity, resulting in a clean, mouthwatering finish. Drink now–2019.
title: Lubanzi 2015 Red (Coastal Region)

description: From a chateau owned by the Bouey Bordeaux negociant family, this wine is ripe, rich and soft. Its tannic structure gives just-enough support to the attractive red-currant and berry fruits. Drink this forward, fruity wine from 2020.
title: Maison Bouey 2015 Château Maison Blanche  (Médoc)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of mint, woodspice, raspberry and coffee are followed by ripe red-currant flavors. Soft, lightly grainy tannins provide support. It has good things going on and is varietally correct but seems a bit woody.
title: Maryhill 2014 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Dry and astringent, this is remarkably similar to a hard cider. Half was fermented in stainless steel, half in concrete, and it is the concrete that comes out in the aromatics, much more than fruit. For food pairing, look to the sorts of things that would accompany a good cider.
title: Maysara 2016 Anahita Riesling (McMinnville)

description: From Dijon clones, this shows dried cranberries and a touch of leather. The finish seems a bit attenuated also. Two bottles were tasted; the second a little less leathery, but with comparable fruit.
title: Merriman 2014 Cummins Road Pinot Noir

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The least expensive of the winery's Pinots, this has a light and fruity style that makes it instantly approachable. Simple red-berry flavors, crisp acids and a little crunch to the tannins give it some extra depth.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 Jill Marie Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: An earthy overtone sits on the nose. The palate has a similarly earthy ring but adds gentle pear and lemon notes to a very light, dry body.
title: Meyer-Fonné 2016 Vieilles Vignes Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Light rose buds, dried potpourri and tart cranberry aromas show on the nose of this appellation blend. The palate is a touch thin but with decent grip, and delivers easily likable flavors of cranberry and cinnamon.
title: Mignanelli 2015 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: This wine is very fruity in flavor and almost sleek in texture, but still has the varietal's typical firm tannins and hints of black pepper in the generous black-cherry flavors. It's medium bodied, not too heavy or too astringent, so it will be more versatile than many.
title: Oak Grove 2016 Family Reserve Petite Sirah (California)

description: Aromas of toasted nut, bread crust and a whiff of grilled rosemary lead the nose. The aromas carry over to the palate along with yellow apple skin and a hint of coffee while brisk acidity gives it a rather lean finish.
title: Olivini 2011 Brut Metodo Classico  (Lugana)

description: Earthy forest and mushroom belie a hearty, dense and velvety expression of rich dark cherry and huckleberry in this full-bodied powerful wine.
title: Pali 2016 Riviera Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Aromas of orange peel, melon and vanilla come together fairly well. This feels plump but solid, with modest acidity. Melony flavors turn yeasty on a lightly oxidized finish that lacks length and kick. Drink now.
title: Perelada 2016 Només Garnacha Blanca (Empordà)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Despite the extra years in bottle, this wine has not really come together. Somewhat awkward and astringent, it pushes stiff berry fruits against drying tannins. Sometimes blending is a better approach than focusing too specifically on a single block and clone.
title: Phelps Creek 2014 Upper East Slope Dijon 115 Clone Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: This entry-level, simple yet well-made wine offers straightforward cranberry and cherry fruit. It's fresh and bright, with juicy youthful appeal. Drink now while it's at its freshest.
title: Phelps Creek 2016 Le Petit Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: Made from the regional grape of unknown origins, Minutolo, this wine is not bashful with its heady floral and fruity nature. Ripe tropical fruit, grapefruit and white flower tones carry the nose and palate. This is medium in weight with a delightfully astringent texture that helps maintain balance.
title: Polvanera 2016 Bianco Minutolo (Puglia)

description: A touch of cream joins the pear note on the nose. The palate, however, has a main theme of fresh lemon on a slender, dry body. Drink soon.
title: Preiss-Zimmer 2016 Réserve Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: The ocean is never far away in all wines from the Lisboa region. The crispness and the bright red fruits of this open wine testify to that fact. This is a light wine, full of fruit, delicious now and ready to drink.
title: Quinta de Chocapalha 2015 Mar de Lisboa Red (Lisboa)

description: A tightly wound framework of bittersweet tannin and oak dominate in this mountainous red, which exhibits pencil shavings, cedar and sage around ripe full-bodied black cherry and plum.
title: Raymond 2014 District Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Howell Mountain)

description: Grown in the comparatively cool conditions of the plateau of Alijó, this wine has a crisp, poised character. Its citrus fruits and touch of minerality give freshness and a good texture. Drink this bright wine now.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2016 Quinta do Síbio Arinto (Douro)

description: Black fruit, dark lilacs and wet earth show on the nose of this blend of 54% Cabernet Sauvignon, 22% Merlot and 24% Petit Verdot. Elderflower and elderberry flavors show on the sip, which is tight and sticky in texture and could use a little more mellowing.
title: Reyes 2013 Ryan's Blend Red (California)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Restrained and earthy, this Sauvignon displays citrus peel and crushed stone aromas. The palate offers a bit more intensity and complexity, with grapefruit zest creating a focused line of acidity from start to finish.
title: Rivera 2016 Terre Al Monte Sauvignon (Castel del Monte)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: There's a density to the nose and palate that can't be ignored, which translates to a syrupy aroma and thick palate. There are some uplifting tones of herbs and savory cherry that offer levity, with brisk acidity and bolstering tannins giving support.
title: San Marzano 2013 Sessantanni Old Vines  (Primitivo di Manduria)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Pure varietal and estate grown, this opens with concentrated, sweet berry preserves of strawberry and raspberry jam. It stiffens up in the middle as it moves into rather astringent, lightly earthy tannins.
title: Schmidt 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Applegate Valley)

Appellation: Stags Leap District, Napa, California, US
description: Among the Napa Valley's most crowning achievements in Cabernet, this vintage of Hillside Select is stunning, bright and beguiling in every way. Intensely layered, its interwoven flavors burst in black licorice, dark chocolate, pipe tobacco, gun smoke and garrigue. Spicy oak adds to the power and weight. Drinkable now, it will show even greater finesse in time; enjoy best 2023–2033 and possibly beyond.
title: Shafer 2013 Hillside Select Cabernet Sauvignon (Stags Leap District)

description: Intoxicating scents of smoke, flint, dried mountain herb and woodland berry are just some of the multifaceted aromas you'll find on this dazzling Nebbiolo. It's extremely elegant, with a weightless intensity, delivering Marasca cherry, crushed strawberry, white pepper and tobacco framed in fine tannins. Vibrant acidity gives it impeccable balance while a flinty mineral vein energizes the finish. Some may find this almost too ethereal while lovers of finesse, complexity and balance will be enchanted. Drink through 2027.
title: Arpepe 2007 Sassella Riserva Rocce Rosse  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Since the first vintage in 1991, this wine and its vineyard have travelled far. Now close to the top of the classification tree in Saint-Émilion, this vintage shows the impressive nature of this powerful wine. With a good balance between acidity and black fruits backed up by tannins, the wine is powerful and stylish. Drink from 2027.
title: Château Valandraud 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: Almost entirely Cabernet Franc, this single-vineyard wine has a wonderful perfumed character. Firm tannins are fully integrated into the fruit, giving a structured yet balanced feel. Ripe and elegant, it will age well. Drink from 2027.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Le Dôme  (Saint-Émilion)

description: Direct and concentrated, this wine is powered by tannins and a firm texture. Dense, it has the potential for plenty of fruit as it matures. That will take time, because this impressive wine is powered by its structure. Wait until 2028.
title: Château Haut-Brion 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: Always one of the great white wines of the world, this vintage continues that reputation. With its richness, density and layers of fruit and acidity, this wine is powerful and beautifully ripe. The wood aging is just a hint in background. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Haut-Brion 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: It's always a cliché to compare wine to the nectar of the gods but there's little that comes closer than this. It's decadently sweet, offering layer upon layer of unctuous honey and stone-fruit nectar, yet exhilarating, fine and filigreed. The finish is endless and breathtaking. Delicious now but will hold for decades to come.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Kiedrich Gräfenberg Trockenbeerenauslese Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Starting with the 2008 vintage, the firm's flagship bottling is now made without added dosage. It opens with aromas of yellow stone fruit, brioche and crushed stone that carry over to the elegantly structured palate, along with tangy tangerine and bright acidity. A refined perlage gives it a weightless concentration while a savory mineral note lingers on the bone-dry finish. In a word, it's gorgeous.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2008 Annamaria Clementi Dosage Zero Riserva  (Franciacorta)

description: This wine is packed with ripe black fruit and structured by a dense, mineral texture. Generous fruit tones and plenty of tannic grip translate to a wine worthy of the cellar. Drink from 2025.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Les Astéries  (Saint-Émilion)

description: This wine, rich and opulent, defines the terroir of this estate. It has a rich, dense character, powerful and concentrated. The juicy black fruits enhance the impression of generosity as well as concentration. Drink from 2027.
title: Château La Mission Haut-Brion 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: This wine is dense in expressive, dark fruit and solid tannins which give it a rich, enveloping feel. With its spicy score and crisp acidity, there is great potential for aging. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Smith Haut Lafitte 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: If any wine from the Douro has a Bordeaux influence, it is this wine. Produced as part of a joint venture between the Prats family of Bordeaux and the Symington family of the Douro, this wine shows great attention to tannins and structure while allowing the black fruits to shine. It is a stylish wine, powerful while wearing that power lightly. Drink this wine, from a very good year, from 2022.
title: Prats & Symington LDA 2015 Chryseia Red (Douro)

description: While this wine is rich, it has a classical demeanor, with fine tannins that support but never dominate the ripe black fruits. Full of juicy acidity, it offers black currants and some fine, firm tannins at the end. This will take some time to mature, and the wine will not be ready to drink before 2027.
title: Château Cos d'Estournel 2015  Saint-Estèphe

description: Full of black-currant flavor, this firm wine has structure and a rich texture. Its bolstering tannins and vibrant acidity mark out an age-worthy selection. Spice tones and a dry core set the stage for a slow development into an even more complex and fulfilling wine. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Malartic-Lagravière 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: This huge, dense and firmly structured bottling absolutely begs for time in the cellar to allow the primary characteristics to mellow and greater elegance and nuance to unfold. Currently, it's an absolute bruiser, with boatloads of plush, ripe black berry and plum characteristics that are coupled with seductive notes of campfire, vanilla bean, Thin Mint, woodspice and licorice root. The assertive tannins produce a bold, extremely firm texture that is in equal measure to the superbly rich and concentrated fruit. Secondary notes of pencil shavings, violets, pipe tobacco and char unfold beneath the fruit, and suggest even further evolution and grace to come with time. Drink 2026–2038.
title: De Toren 2015 Book 17 XVII Red (Stellenbosch)

Designation: Scharzhofberger Beerenauslese #25 Grosse Lage Gold Kap
description: Sweet tangerine, pineapple and mango pulsate through this piercing, feather-light wine. It's an exhilarating sweet sip accented by savory nuances of musk and white truffles. Expansive yet thrilling and spry, the finish meanders endlessly. Drinks beautifully now but should hold gorgeously for decades to come.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2011 Scharzhofberger Beerenauslese #25 Grosse Lage Gold Kap Riesling (Mosel)

description: While exceptionally sheer, almost diaphanous in texture, this sprightly, sumptuous Riesling offers penetrating layers of honey, melon and pineapple nuanced by spice, hay and crushed earth. It's a spine tingling, concentrated wine, with an endless, rippling finish. Delightful already but sure to hold for decades to come.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Kiedrich Gräfenberg Beerenauslese Riesling (Rheingau)

description: This beautifully golden-colored, oak-aged wine is bone dry, with the intense acidity working with an orange-marmalade character. Together they give a mature wine that is ready to drink.
title: Blandy's 1968 Sercial (Madeira)

description: Concentrated and with tight, mineral fruitiness, this wine is potentially ripe and rich, with citrus and orange peel crispness. The wine will be rich, rounded and packed with fruit, but it will take time. Drink from 2023.
title: Château La Mission Haut-Brion 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: Spice and ripe fruit aromas lead into a wine that has both richness and a crisp, fruity herbal character. Acidity shines through, giving the wine a tangy, mineral edginess. It will be a delicious fruity wine. Drink from 2023.
title: Domaine de Chevalier 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: This dazzling sparkler hits all the right marks. It opens with heady aromas of citrus, bread crust and lemon pastry while the vibrant palate delivers juicy tangerine, Rennet apple and an energizing mineral note alongside an elegant, continuous perlage. Made from 60% Chardonnay and 40% Pinot Nero with no added dosage, it boasts a pristine quality and a pure, crisp finish.
title: Barone Pizzini 2013 Naturae  (Franciacorta)

description: Bottled in 2017, this wine aged in old oak for 60 years. It still has all the sweetness you would expect from a Bual Madeira while also having that old wood, almost golden character. Acidity balances the opulent fruit. Drink this wine now.
title: Blandy's 1957 Bual (Madeira)

description: This is a rich style of white, in keeping with the rich vintage. Its acidity and tight texture are paired off with the apricot and ripe pear fruitiness. Spice from wood aging adds an attractive background. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Couhins 2015  Pessac-Léognan

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Sturdy and structured, this wine offers beautiful tones of ripe blackberry and black cherry, with a dark streak of chocolate. It's primed and brightened by snappy acids, and flows gracefully into a pleasingly creamy finish highlighted by smoke and toast.
title: Domaine Serene 2014 Mark Bradford Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Demure notes of lemon and crushed mineral on the nose contrast an exuberant palate full of pink grapefruit and hibiscus nectar. It's a gorgeously silky, concentrated dry Riesling, with a firm mineral backbone and electric acidity that need time to meld. Hold until 2020 as it's likely to improve well through 2030.
title: Fritz Haag 2016 Brauneberg Juffer Sonnenuhr Trocken GG Riesling (Mosel)

description: First produced in 1991, this wine was a pioneer in the new generation of red wines from the Douro. It is firm with tannins and the strong minerality typical of the Douro. It has weight despite its low alcohol and tense fruitiness. Drink this complex wine with great aging potential from 2020.
title: Niepoort 2015 Redoma Red (Douro)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The best bottle in a stellar lineup of the winery's first 2016 releases, this marries old-vine elegance to lush aromatics, with entwined notes of walnuts, raspberries, thyme, rosewater and citrus. It's a long-lasting, beautifully integrated wine, made with grapes from some of the oldest blocks in the vineyard.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Medici Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Balanced and fragrant, this impressive sparkler offers alluring scents of yellow stone fruit and an inviting whiff of brioche. A blend of 80% Chardonnay and 20% Pinot Nero, it's elegantly structured and focused, delivering flavors of yellow apple, white peach, bread crust and crushed stone framed in bright acidity and elegant perlage. A vanilla note lingers on the bone-dry finish. It's extremely well balanced and refined.
title: Berlucchi 2010 '61 Nature  (Franciacorta)

description: This stunning sparkler is all about precision, finesse and purity. It opens with enticing scents of white spring flower, crushed stone, lemon pastry and hazelnut. The aromas carry over to the chiseled palate together with creamy yellow apple, tangerine zest, brioche and mineral notes alongside an elegant perlage. Vibrant acidity gives it balance and structure.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2013 Vintage Collection Dosage Zero  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Yorkville Highlands, North Coast, California, US
description: This excellent wine from a steep terraced vineyard offers beautiful aromas of lavender and violet that segue to a palate replete with black fruit, black pepper and anise tones. Each flavor seems especially well defined and they layer together harmoniously on a velvety texture that provides good support for all that concentration. Best after 2020.
title: Halcón 2015 Tierra Petite Sirah (Yorkville Highlands)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This 100% varietal wine is a blend of top sites: Boushey (55%) and Red Willow, with just a kiss (5%) of new French oak. The aromas are intriguing, with notes of white grapefruit, nectarine, lemon pith, straw, mineral and herb. Flavors are sleek and puckering, with a steely spine of acidity that keeps the interest high. It only gets better with time open and should sing at the dinner table.
title: Avennia 2016 Oliane Sauvignon Blanc (Yakima Valley)

description: This offers nuanced aromas of black cherry, red plum, rosewater, crisp red-delicious apple, burnt orange and Christmas spices. These aromas unfold onto the palate where the spices really sing. While this wine is in balance, boasting bright and zippy acidity and ample weight, the woody and spiced tannins stand out and steal the show.
title: Barboursville Vineyards 2014 Reserve Nebbiolo (Virginia)

description: There's a pleasant earthy, spicy character right off the bat on the nose of this wine, opening with pronounced scents of Dr. Pepper, cherry cola, red plum, chicory root and church incense. The medium-weight palate offers structured tannins that lend surprising grip, yet bright acidity keeps the mouthfeel lifted. The stalky, tight red-berry flavors on the palate return to spicy abundance on the medium-length finish. Drink 2020–2025.
title: A.A. Badenhorst Family Wines 2015 Raaigras Grenache (Swartland)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This juicy bottle bursts open with pomegranate, cherry and orange zest. Hints of Dr. Pepper streak through, but overall it's an elegant and detailed wine, showing more finesse than sheer power.
title: Alloro 2015 Estate Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A shift from the 2005, this is one-third Pinot Noir and two-thirds Chardonnay, disgorged in July of 2017. It's tasty and surprisingly young, with tart apple and peach flavors dominant, along with light custard and some lovely toast. This may be enjoyed now or cellared for up to five years.
title: Argyle 2007 Extended Tirage Brut Sparkling (Willamette Valley)

description: A blend of 80% Pinot Noir and 20% Chardonnay, this vibrant sparkler offers fresh aromas of red berry and citrus. On the palate, a lively perlage underscores pomegranate, red grapefruit and a hint of baking spice. It's well balanced, with crisp acidity.
title: Barone Pizzini 2013 Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Exuberantly blossomy, this perfumed kabinett offers loads of pristine white peach and pineapple flavors off set by layers of cool minerals. It's a juicy yet revitalizing wine, with a long crystalline finish.
title: Bastgen 2016 Kestener Paulinshofberg Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Initially this wine is charred and smoky-smelling, with accents of graphite and cola. A tight, juicy palate is bolstered by bold core acidity, while ripe blackberry and currant flavors capture both the black and red sides of the flavor spectrum. A dry, tannic finish with peppery flavors is intense. Drink through 2026.
title: Bodegas Fuentespina 2014 Selección Old Vines Crianza  (Ribera del Duero)

Winery: Caves São João Quinta do Poço do Lobo
description: It's rare to find a Portuguese white wine this old still available. While it does taste mature, it has wonderful nutty flavors and acidity, giving an old gold, yeasty character. This is a food wine, with its structure and mineral aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Caves São João Quinta do Poço do Lobo 1995 Poço de Lobo Colheita Arinto (Bairrada)

description: Spicy, structured and with a smoky character, this rich wine will develop power. It has a dense texture, layers of tannins that are integrating with the blackberry fruits. This will be a good wine as it matures. Drink from 2024.
title: Château La Cardonne 2015  Médoc

description: This rich, juicy wine has 90% Merlot in the blend giving it vibrancy, rich tannins and great black-plum and spice flavors. It is a ripe wine, powerful and dense with intense youthful acidity that will soften. Drink this balanced, firm wine from 2022.
title: Château Reysson 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Made with 100% Pinot Nero, this offers inviting aromas of cherry, acacia, bread dough and a whiff of hazelnut. On the bright structured palate, fresh acidity lifts toasted nut, brioche and pomegranate alongside a lively refined perlage.
title: Contadi Castaldi 2011 Pinònero Natura  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: The nose on this ultimately zesty wine starts off on the richer side, with baked apple, yellow melon, cherimoya and orange peel. On the palate, the acidity is immediate and tremendously persistent, with flavors of apple blossom, pear syrup and tangerine spray riding the chalky texture.
title: Crawford Family 2016 Albariño (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Enticing, fruity aromas of red cherry and brambly berry set the stage for layers of cola, baking spice and cocoa. The medium-weight palate offers a texture like crushed satin, smooth yet defined, with ample acidity to keep the structure and ripe-fruit flavors from feeling overly dense. The spice returns on the finish and continues alongside a hint of peppery warmth. Drink now–2023.
title: David & Nadia 2015 David Grenache (Swartland)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Sourced from a vineyard on the western edge of the valley, this sports lovely aromatics introducing matching flavors of black cherry and a dash of pepper. There's a pleasing minerality, like a lick of granite, and a whiff of dried thyme also.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2013 Lonesome Rock Ranch Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: A blend of 29% Roussanne, 23% Chardonnay, 22% Chenin Blanc, 18% Grenache Blanc and 8% Viognier, this opens with delicate aromas of pink apple, orange blossom, strawberry hull and white melon. The medium-weight palate offers just enough fruity weight to ground the precise cut of the vibrant acidity. Harmonious and well-balanced, this is one to enjoy now–2022.
title: DeMorgenzon 2015 Maestro White (Stellenbosch)

description: There is a slight hint of lime zest and fern on the nose. The palate is more expressive but in a slender, subtle way, offering a lime zest flavor countered by white pepper and a streak of green fern. Citrus freshness tingles everywhere and even has a pithy, textural element to it.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2016 Les Vieilles Vignes Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Dusty mineral and zesty lime aromas are contrasted by plump, summer-sweet peach and tangerine on the palate. It's a spry, frisky kabinett, with lip-smacking sweetness balanced neatly by streaks of spine tingling acidity.
title: Dr. Heidemanns-Bergweiler 2016 Bernkasteler Badstube Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: There's a buzz of electric acidity that hits the midpalate here, softened by tones of luscious peach, pineapple and guava. It's delicate and thrilling on the palate but lavishly floral and fruity through a long, reverberating finish.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Bernkasteler Lay Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit for this wine comes from the state's original Syrah vineyard. Expressive aromas of dried herb, dark raspberry, huckleberry, spice and licorice lead to ripe, full fruit flavors that glide on the finish. It's not shy, bringing a lot of richness and appeal.
title: Efeste 2015 Eléni Red Willow Vineyard Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: Intensely earthen tones of smoke and slate abound in this profoundly mineral spätlese. The midpalate brings forth sweeter, sunnier tones of fresh lemon and lime, along with a pleasantly bracing astringency that lends structure to the finish. It's a savory but complex spätlese that needs time to meld. Hold till at least 2020 but its likely to improve for at least a decade beyond that.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Kestener Paulinsberg Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Just a handful of Oregon wineries produce this varietal wine, and one hopes more will follow. Fresh, leesy and rich, with mineral-soaked flavors, this broadens out in the mouth with apple-flavored fruit. There's an appealing note of toasted cracker, peanut shells and a whiff of sea breeze. It's a fascinating wine.
title: Helioterra 2016 Redman Vineyard Arneis (Ribbon Ridge)

description: A delicious bottle of Pinot Gris from Iris is as certain as a sunrise. This lightly tawny wine brings scents of lemon oil, white flowers and lemon verbena. The lovely fruit pulls in apple, pear, kiwi and a touch of banana, all in perfect balance. In short, this is a thoroughly engaging and versatile white wine.
title: Iris Vineyards 2016 Pinot Gris (Oregon)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This exceptional site was planted back in the 1970s, and winemaker Jay Somers pulls some of that old-vine depth and nuance out of his first release from Medici. Bursting with ripe cherry fruit, it's got a base of clean earth, with highlights of coffee grinds and dark chocolate.
title: J. Christopher 2015 Medici Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: This is tight and reserved at first, needing time to open and reveal notes of green apple, lemon-lime citrus, yellow plum and underripe mango. Given those tight, bright aromas, it's surprising to encounter the medium-weight, round mouthfeel, with low acidity and ripe tones of guava and white grapefruit that carry through to the close.
title: Ken Forrester 2015 Dirty Little Secret One Chenin Blanc (Western Cape)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This remains tightly wound, but the deep flavors of black cherry and cassis suggest that once it unwraps it will add muscle and mass. Highlights of coffee and charred wood lead into an astringent finish with drying tannins.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Guadalupe Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of oak, coconut, forest floor and plum cake that carry over to the chewy palate along with juicy black cherry, mocha and vanilla. Soft rounded tannins provide supple support.
title: La Braccesca 2013 Vigneto Santa Pia Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Winery: La Cave des Vignerons de Pfaffenheim
description: Pure and very appetizing notes of baked pear and fresh, yellow apple play on the nose. The palate reprises these flavors with juicy, fruity abundance and balances them with vibrant freshness. There is concentration and depth of pear and apple flavors. The finish is just off dry but full of that glorious fruit.
title: La Cave des Vignerons de Pfaffenheim 2016 Black Tie White (Alsace)

description: There's a lot to like about this refreshing, savory sparkler. It opens with inviting aromas of baked bread, baking spice, white stone fruit and a whiff of lemon pastry cream. The vibrant elegant palate delivers ripe Gala apple, white peach, candied tangerine drop and hazelnut alongside tangy acidity. A lively perlage provides the backdrop.
title: Lantieri 2013 Arcadia Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This tightly wound, still very young bottling from Rick Longoria's estate vineyard needs a little time to open up, but when it does, there are grapefruit pith, candied lemon rind and freeze-dried honeydew aromas to enjoy. The texture is tremendously chalky, delivering flavors of lime skin, apple rind and orange candies.
title: Longoria 2015 Fe Ciega Vineyard Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Zippy and fresh, this dry, medium-bodied Weissburgunder, or Pinot Blanc, offers loads of pristine apple and tangerine-peel flavors. It's concentrated yet sprightly in mouthfeel and finishes with lingering complexities of sea salt and smoky nuts. Drink now through 2022.
title: Pflüger 2016 Quarzit Trocken Weissburgunder (Pfalz)

description: Made with 60% Sangiovese, 20% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Sauvignon, this offers aromas of black currant, underbrush and green peppercorn. The juicy palate offers dried black cherry, tobacco and ground black pepper alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Rocca di Frassinello 2015  Maremma Toscana

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This cuvée has a bit less new oak than its companions and seems a tad less ripe, though certainly ripe enough. There's a tart streak of pineapple and orange under firm red-currant fruit. A light hint of char and chocolate wraps up the finish.
title: Saffron Fields Vineyard 2015 Spectrum Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Southern Oregon, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: A real success, in part because the alcohol has been kept in check, this palate-pleaser brings flavors of cranberry sauce, dried orange peel, clove and dark chocolate. It's balanced and bright, forward and chockful of pretty spices.
title: Schmidt 2014 Touriga Nacional (Southern Oregon)

description: This is currently a bit shy and reserved, begging for a bit more time to better express itself. Notes of black cherry and currant are hit by accents of espresso powder and baking spice, white the palate offers a slightly jammier impression that lingers through the medium-length finish. The tannins are structured and youthful, yet resolve themselves somewhat easily alongside the ripe fruit tones. Give this a couple of years; try after 2020.
title: Trizanne 2015 Reserve Syrah (Elim)

description: Hints of honey and orange blossom soften cutting lime and lemon flavors in this medium-bodied Riesling. Dry in style, it's a zesty, intensely thirst quenching wine, with a long finish fringed by bitter, waxy lime-pith complexities. Drink now through 2020.
title: Wittmann 2016 100 Hills Dry Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Dry Portuguese red wines are all the rage, and yet virtually unknown (and ungrown) in the U.S. This pure varietal example offers a mix of purple berries, a somewhat chalky mouthfeel, and tannins with the astringency of tea leaves.
title: Abacela 2014 Barrel Select Red (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This barrel selection from the estate's Revana vineyard offers medium concentration and tart fruit flavors of wild berries and Bing cherries. The acids add a note of citrus, but overall this lacks a bit of the oomph expected in a reserve.
title: Alexana 2015 Signature Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Though at first it feels a bit thin in the mouth, this block selection has pleasant strawberry fruit, with hints of compost in the bouquet and the finish. With ample aeration it expands a bit, then fades gently through a clean finish.
title: Alexana 2015 West Blocks Sedimentary Soils Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, tilled soil and cedar lead the way on this Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Petit Verdot blend. The dense palate offers dried black currant, licorice and star anise alongside polished tannins.
title: Argentiera 2015 Villa Donoratico  (Bolgheri)

description: Smoky char and bacon give this wine a lot of personality and depth—big-time flavors that highlight a soft velvety texture of concentration. Brawny and broad, it's a powerhouse of ripeness and richness.
title: August Briggs 2014 Petite Sirah (Calistoga)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Hot, minty aromas of burnt oak, graphite and road tar introduce a fiery palate. Burnt-oak flavors turn resiny and towards mocha and blackberry as the wine breathes. Keeping with a theme set from the start, this is fiery on the finish, with toasty, blackened flavors.
title: Avante 2014  Ribera del Duero

description: Aromas of bread crust, walnut and citrus carry over to the linear, slightly lean palate along with a saline-like mineral note. It's vibrant and refined, with small, continuous bubbles.
title: Barone Pizzini 2013 Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: Subtle aromas of pressed chamomile, baked bread and dried yellow apple slice follow over to the palate along with white peach, toasted walnut and a hint of medicinal herb. Small persistent bubbles lend elegance while a citrus note refreshes the finish.
title: Bonfadini NV Brut Nature  (Franciacorta)

description: Orchard fruit pressed wildflower aromas fill the glass along with a whiff of bread crust.The aromas follow over to the tangy palate along with dried herb and a hint of bitter walnut.
title: Bonfadini NV Opera Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Dark cherry and blackberry aromas meld with tobacco leaf and eucalyptus on the nose. Round and juicy in feel, there's a nice expression of ripe dark fruit struck against a taut line of acidity and medium-grained tannins.
title: Cantele 2014 Teresamanara Negroamaro (Salento)

description: Aromas of raisin, prune, spice cake, clove and cinnamon are oaky. This selection of the high part of the grape bunch known as the shoulder feels grabby, with clamping tannins. Woodsy, earthy berry flavors are accompanied by licorice and clove notes, while chocolaty oak helps to overcome a light green character on the finish.
title: Casar de Burbia 2013 Hombros Mencía (Bierzo)

description: Fresh, ripe notes of Meyer lemon are joined by hints of rose. It is on the slender palate that the rose aromas become stronger, countered by lovely lemon freshness. The finish is dry, perfumed and refreshing.
title: Cave de Turckheim 2016 Réserve Muscat (Alsace)

description: The nose is still under a cloud of reduction yet a vivid cherry aroma shines through. The palate offers the freshness of conifer, with fine tannins and a pleasant, youthful crunch. It is savorines that is in the foreground here, bewitching with spice and pervasive freshness. An easy, fresh but authentic Pinot.
title: Cave du Roi Dagobert 2016 Tradition Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Dark-skinned berry, forest floor, blue flower, toast and a whiff of culinary spice take the lead. The bright straightforward palate shows wild cherry, espresso and a hint of pipe tobacco framed in lithe tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Cecchi 2013  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Generous aromas of lime leaf, white peach, flower and slate lead to off-dry green-apple flavors backed by zippy acidity. The lovely sense of balance keeps you coming back for more.
title: Charles & Charles 2016 Riesling (Washington)

description: The vines of this 50-acre estate, close to Listrac, have produced a rich wine in this vintage. With its spice and toast character and ripe berry fruits coming together nicely, the wine will develop well. Its potential is for medium-term aging, meaning it will be ready to drink from 2023.
title: Château Bibian 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Ripe Merlot makes its generous mark in this rich, fruity wine. It has structure and dense tannins as well as juicy black-currant fruits. The richness is not excessive, accompanying this core of tannins. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Haut-Piquat 2015  Lussac Saint-Émilion

description: Bramble and spice characteristics grace the just-ripe plum and cherry fruit on the nose and mouth of this easy-drinking wine. It boasts a fine structure, with framing tannins and complex accents of fruit skin, smoked game and just a dash of peppery warmth on the close. Drink now–2020.
title: DeMorgenzon 2015 DMZ Syrah (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas intrigue, with notes of dark raspberry, red and black licorice, herb and cherry gummy. Aged in neutral oak, it has a palate that emphasizes purity of expression. Put it on the dinner table to see it at its best. Drink now.
title: Descendant 2013 Mourvèdre (Red Mountain)

description: This is bright and attractive, with easy-pleasing notes of fresh apple flesh, honeydew, citrus pith and yellow flowers on the nose and mouth. A lightly pithy texture and bright acidity shape the finish, with final notes of tart apple and plum. Drink now.
title: Eikendal 2015 Janina Unwooded Chardonnay (Western Cape)

description: Inky red violet to the eye, this wine offers a bouquet of black cherry and anise. It is smooth on the palate, with flavors of bramble fruit, black plum, fennel bulb and black pepper. The tannins are soft, dropping off before the cherry and anisette finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2014 Yarden Malbec (Galilee)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot and Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, mocha and cedar. The dense palate delivers blackberry jam, licorice and tobacco alongside austere tannins. Enjoy through 2021.
title: Grattamacco 2014  Bolgheri Superiore

description: Pale and slightly bricked in hue, this delicately concentrated Pinot Noir offers a rather ghostly expression of the grape. Its floral perfume suggests dried violets and lavender and its brandied cherry and berry notes are faded and demure. Dusty tannins are soft but pleasantly bitter on the finish. It's showing signs of maturity already so enjoy now through 2021.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2015 Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Mosel)

description: Pure Conference pear notes dominate the nose and palate. This shows some concentration that is balanced by fresh acidity. The finish is dry and fruity.
title: Joseph Cattin 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, underbrush and a hint of green olive. The straightforward palate offers vanilla, black-currant jam and a touch of caramel alongside smooth tannins.
title: Orma 2015 Passi di Orma  (Bolgheri)

description: his crisp wine is full of red fruits and attractive acidity. It has some spice as well as a core of tannins that support the fruitiness. It is ready to drink.
title: Parras Wines 2016 Quinta do Gradil Red (Lisboa)

description: Made with a selection of grapes from Quinta do Gradil, this oak-aged wine is rich, generous and smooth. Ripe black fruits give it a warm character, with spice and rich tannins that support the fruit. The wine will age a little further before it is ready to drink. Wait until late 2018.
title: Parras Wines 2016 Quinta do Gradil Ruelas Reserva Red (Lisboa)

description: This 100% Sangiovese opens with iris, raspberry and Mediterranean scrub aromas. The easygoing palate offers red cherry, raspberry jam and star anise alongside velvety tannins and fresh acidity. Enjoy soon.
title: Pepi Lignana 2014 Cerida  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Complexities of leather and bitter chocolate lend a rustic note to lush blackberry and plum flavors. It's a soft, plump wine, with delicate acidity and fine, feathery tannins fringing the finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Pflüger 2015 Cuvée Noir Red (Pfalz)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Made in a solid, chunky style, this emphasizes fruit over floral characteristics. Flavors of white melon, sweet herb and lychee come with just a hint of talcum powder. It's finished dry and ready to drink while young and fresh.
title: Phelps Creek 2016 Gorge Crest Gewürztraminer (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: Gamy, savory plum and berry aromas recall asphalt, tree bark and wildness. This blend offering Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc and Monastrell is clampy and tannic. Oaky savory berry flavors finish choppy, with a hint of clove.
title: Rafael Cambra 2015 El Bon Homme Red (Valencia)

description: Crisp, cool Síria gives this wine a bright and refreshing character. Minerality and apple and citrus flavors are delicious and very direct. Lightly textured and fruity, the wine is ready to drink.
title: Ribafreixo 2016 Barrancôa White (Alentejano)

description: This is a chunky wine with fruit leaning to the green (but still ripe) side of flavors. Apple, lime and grapefruit are in play, with a touch of bitter skin flavors also. It's balanced and persistent, with the structure to accompany poultry seasoned with fresh herbs.
title: Rock Point 2016 Pinot Gris (Oregon)

description: This single-variety Malvasia Nera is dense in purple-fruit aromas, with accents of violets, anise and menthol. The fruity, soft palate offers plenty of ripe dark-fruit flavors, balanced by fresh acidity and black plum-skin tannins.
title: San Marzano 2015 Talò Malvasia Nera (Salento)

description: Spicy, dense aromas of tobacco leaf, briar berries and tilled earth make for an engaging nose. The palate is a bit more primary in ripe dark fruit flavors, yet solidly balanced by fresh acidity and savory, medium-grained tannins.
title: San Marzano 2015 Talò Negroamaro (Salento)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This remains stiffly compact, showing blue and purple fruits with a seam of chocolate mint. It was barrel aged entirely in neutral oak, and the tannins are smooth and polished. A bit of aeration would help to open it up.
title: Sokol Blosser 2015 Estate Whole Cluster Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This wine is surprisingly dense and dark in aromatic character, with brooding notes of black cherry, cassis and blackberry that set the stage for an equally concentrated palate. The astringent tannins lend good grip, framing the plush, ripe fruit. Final hints of tree bark, chicory and char spice up the close.
title: The Three Foxes 2015 Carignan (Swartland)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Syrah makes up half of this wine, with the rest equal parts Grenache and Mourvèdre. Aromas of marionberry, herb, smoked meat and spice lead to a well-balanced palate that glides on the baking-spice-filled finish. The oak plays a bit prominently at times but overall it works.
title: Three of Cups 2014 Le Jugement Lonesomes Springs Vineyard Red (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Edna Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is an unctuous tale on Chardonnay, very much in line with the classic Californian style. Aromas of baked Asian pear, oak, butterscotch and caramel show on the nose, leading into a broad, buttery and viscous palate of lemon balm and coconut cream.
title: Tolosa 2016 Poletti Edna Ranch Chardonnay (Edna Valley)

Appellation: Rattlesnake Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Inviting aromas of cherry cordial, dried herb and chalk lead to generous, well-fashioned fruit flavors. Light tannins provide support.
title: Twin Cedars 2014 Merlot (Rattlesnake Hills)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: The barrel influence is primarily aromatical with notes of nougat, vanilla and sweet spices out in front of cherry and red apple.
