The fruit vies with the oak for attention on the palate as well, although the latter shows a pleasing supple feel as well as excellent concentration. There's impressive fruit here, but the oak unfortunately gets in the way of it.
title: Watermill 2014 Anna Marie Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

description: Confident saline and citrus aromas are a good entry to this clean, fairly lean brut. Lime and tangerine flavors are racy and not too sweet, while this is fresh and well made on the finish.
title: 1+1=3 NV Mètode Tradicional Brut Sparkling (Cava)

description: There's a surprising intensity to the aromas of fresh blackberries, turned earth and violets. The palate mirrors the nose in a package of grippy tannins and crisp acidity. While enjoyably straightforward in nature, the concentration is rather light, so enjoy for its immediate appeal.
title: A Mano 2015 Imprint Primitivo (Puglia)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The best of Airlie's current white wine releases, this is bright and fruity, with cucumber and green apple in the mix. The mouthfeel is lively, and the flavors show a bit of residual sugar, resulting in an impression not entirely unlike vinous Gatorade.
title: Airlie 2016 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
Designation: Queen of Quite a Lot Rosé of
description: As the low alcohol suggests, this is a light and just barely ripe rosé, a salmon shade with a blush of cherry red. Tart flavors of strawberry and rhubarb are dusted with a dash of Thai spices.
title: Angel Vine 2016 Queen of Quite a Lot Rosé of Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: A gold color and resiny aromas of nutty oak and oxidized white fruits capture the essence of aged white Rioja. A round, oily palate is dominated by oak, tasting of spiced apple, almond skins and woodspice. Flavors of peanut, dried apricot and stone-fruit pits provide for a good finish.
title: Bodegas Vínícola Real 2007 200 Monges Selección Especial Reserva Blanco White (Rioja)

description: Intense aromas of smoke, crushed earth and pollen permeate through sweet-tart lemon and lime flavors. It's savory yet invigorating, offering penetrating streaks of acidity that linger through a long finish.
title: Bollig-Lehnert 2016 Piesporter Goldtröpfchen Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of three vineyards, including one in Santa Barbara County, offers intriguing sweet-and-sour aromas of dried sage, bay leaf, forest floor and olive tapenade. Dried berry fruit shows on the sip alongside chaparral, and it tastes much older than its young age.
title: Bonny Doon 2016 Grenache (Monterey County)

description: Produced by Niepoort for the importer, this dense wine is rich in prunes and ripe black plums. Poised between sweet and dry, it has tannins as well as generous fruitiness and a liquorous, smooth aftertaste.
title: Broadbent NV Auction Reserva  (Port)

description: This is a solid-quality wine that tastes good and fruity, has a nice blend of acidity and richness and a rather full body that coats the palate. It's enjoyable to drink and seems to have everything in good proportions.
title: Cambridge & Sunset 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: Aromas of cedar, dark-skinned fruit and baking spice lead the nose on this 70% Sangiovese and 30% Merlot blend. The firm palate offers black plum, clove and toast with tight assertive tannins. Drink 2019–2023.
title: Casteani 2012 Terra di Casteani  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Earthy, buttery aromas join good fruity ones in this medium-bodied and mineral-tinged wine. It has firm acidity and a slightly astringent texture that give it a lean, bracing quality.
title: Chacewater 2016 Chardonnay (Lake County)

description: This is the first vintage of this estate under the ownership of the Bouey family, which has a Bordeaux negociant business. The wine is dry, anchored in tannins at this stage although the juicy black fruits will come through over time. Wait for this wine until 2022.
title: Château la Ribaud 2015  Médoc

description: This is one of the most northerly of the Médoc estates, only six miles from the ocean. That gives a cool feel to the wine, emphasizing fruit rather than structure. Plenty of juicy acidity gives the wine a lift. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Lacombe Noaillac 2015  Médoc

description: You'll find enticing aromas of black-skinned berry, graphite and Mediterranean brush on this blend of 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Syrah and 10% Teroldego. On the intense palate, notes of tobacco, black pepper and a touch of cocoa add depth to a core of black currant. Velvety tannins provide elegant support. Enjoy through 2026.
title: Michele Satta 2013 I Castagni  (Bolgheri Superiore)

description: Aromas of red berry, dark culinary spice, wild mint and a whiff of new leather are textbook mountain Nebbiolo. The savory racy palate is loaded with finesse, delivering crushed raspberry, Marasca cherry, star anise and tobacco set against taut ultrafine tannins. A mineral note closes the finish while vibrant acidity gives it energy and balance.
title: Nera 2010 Sassella Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's impressive aromatic depth to the nose of this winery's top-tier bottling, with black cherry, olive, wisteria, wet wood, juniper and bay leaf all present. These elements converge on the sip, where black plum, light licorice and loamy earth flavors are enhanced by hints of truffle, nutmeg and vanilla. The tannic texture is upright yet not sticky or in the way.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Made from organically grown grapes, this wine comes from the plateau north of the Douro river itself. It is a limited-edition wine, rich and tannic at this stage. Alongside all this structure, the wine also has ripe black fruits that are about to burst out, juicy, rich and with final acidity. Drink this wine from 2019.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2014 Síbio Red (Douro)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This thoroughly delicious wine offers a lush mix of raspberry compote, cherry pie and a touch of lemon curd. The tannins are supple and generous, and the wine glides effortlessly through a yummy midpalate and on into a satisfying finish with a lick of buttery caramel.
title: Styring 2014 Signature Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Winemaker Dominic O'Reilly worked for years under Ojai Vineyard Syrah master Adam Tolmach, so it makes sense that his take on this historic property would be so excellent. Aromas of deep black plum fruit are cut by game, tar and wild herbs. The palate shows fresh purple fruit and violets alongside cracked pepper, held up by a strong tannic backbone.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2014 Bien Nacido Vineyard Reserve Syrah (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Made with Chardonnay and Pinot Nero, this offers delicate scents of wildflowers, orchard fruit and a whiff of baking spice. Racy, linear and refreshing, the crisp dry palate delivers Rennet apple, nectarine and a hint of ginger, while a graphite note adds depth. It's elegant, with small, continuous bubbles.
title: Villa Crespia NV Brolese Extra Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Distinct tones of cherry pit and cinnamon-clove define the aromatics of this wine. It's a relatively full-bodied style of the variety from the east side of the appellation. Succulent and fleshy, it offers an array of salty, savory notes and is appealing in its velvety texture and roundness. Cola, warm baking spices and orange peel add to the complexity.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Lewis MacGregor Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This wine explores the gamy, cool-climate side of Syrah, sourced from the winery's estate vineyard just outside of the Santa Lucia Highlands. Roasted coffee, burnt toast, charred lamb, powdered cocoa and elderberry jam show on the nose, while a gamy flavor comes through even stronger on the firmly structured palate.
title: Wrath 2015 San Saba Vineyard Syrah (Monterey)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This sappy, sensational effort is bursting with an ultraripe flavor of crushed blueberry. Dusty tannins and a sprinkling of coffee grounds amplify the finish, with excellent texture and length, ending on a flourish of butterfat.
title: Abacela 2014 Reserve East Hill Block Malbec (Umpqua Valley)

description: Pristine aromas of white melon, green plum, apple blossom and lemon leaf open the nose of this gorgeous white, with touches of fresh fynbos and almond skin for added depth. The palate offers a pithy, lightly astringent texture that supports the soft fruit flavors. This is more about structure, balance and feel than overt fruit tones, with the overall harmony and complexity to continue to evolve well through 2030.    The palate is very well balanced with fine acidity, more a textural delight than overt flavors with sublime orange pith, walnut and saline notes on the ethereal finish.
title: Alheit 2015 Radio Lazarus Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: This is a complex and delicious wine that's completely inviting now, but should continue to hold well for another 5–10 years. Earthy, herbal notes of tobacco leaf, leather, cigar box and crushed violets open the bouquet, grounded by a core of lush, ripe blackberry, currant, raspberry sauce and blueberry crumble. The mouthfeel is full and robust, yet structured by fine but present tannins and bold spicy tones that carry through to the enduring finish. Drink now–2022.
title: Anthonij Rupert 2011 Vinified and Bottled on Lormarins Cabernet Franc (Western Cape)

description: Wild berry, rose petal, smoke, new leather and alpine-herb aromas make for a delicate bouquet. Focused and savory, the bright, elegant palate delivers cranberry, pomegranate, white pepper and black tea framed in taut refined tannins and bright acidity. A tangy saline note lingers on the close. While some may find this almost too austere, if you love finesse you'll be captivated. Drink through 2027.
title: Arpepe 2012 Inferno Fiamme Antiche  (Valtellina)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This complex and engaging wine threads together equal portions of red fruits, leather, coffee, chocolate and toasted coconut. The tannins retain some palate-clinging astringency, and the aging in one-quarter new French oak puts a wreath of light smoke around the finish.
title: Ayoub 2015 Overlook Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Enticing scents of white spring blossom, orchard fruit and a whiff of pastry take center stage on this elegant sparkler. Made entirely with Chardonnay, the graceful palate doles out white peach, yellow apple, lemon and a hint of brioche alongside bright acidity and a silky perlage.
title: Berlucchi NV '61 Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: Toasted aromas of brioche and hazelnut meld with whiffs of yellow stone fruit. On the structured palate, a lively elegant perlage underscores Golden Delicious apple, bread crust, white peach and nectarine drop. Bright acidity lifts the rich flavors.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2013 Vintage Collection Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This is dense and concentrated, reflecting the character of Haut-Brion itself. It has weight, firm tannins and power alongside juicy black fruits and acidity that will give great pleasure as they develop. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Haut-Brion 2015 Le Clarence  (Pessac-Léognan)

description: This is the Pauillac-appellation portion of a larger vineyard, Château Peyrabon, which is in the Haut-Médoc. The wine is rich, perfumed and likely to age very well. Juicy acidity and firm texture go with the black-currant fruit in a wine that will become balanced and elegant. Drink from 2024.
title: Château La Fleur Peyrabon 2015  Pauillac

description: The small vineyard in Soussans in the northern part of the Margaux appellation has produced this richly structured wine. Dark tannins and layers of wood aging are accompanied by black-currant fruits as well as dark-chocolate flavor. This wine needs to age, and will be best from 2023.
title: Château la Fortune 2015  Margaux

description: With Michel Rolland as consultant and the Cuvelier family, also owners of Léoville Poyferré in Saint-Julien, in charge, no wonder this is an impressive wine. It has put on weight since it was tasted at en primeur. Packed with tannins, dark fruits and potential, it is a wine for aging. Solid tannins march along with the black fruit, acidity and spice from wood aging. The wine will take its time and will not be ready before 2025.
title: Château Le Crock 2015  Saint-Estèphe

description: This is the luxury cuvée from vineyards in the Seze family since 1918. With its perfumed fruits and firm tannins it is serious as well as sumptuous. It has weight and a dry texture that will soften into the blackberry fruits and generous structure. This wine, with its still-firm texture, needs to age, so drink from 2022.
title: Château Mayne Vieil 2015 Cuvée Aliénor  (Fronsac)

description: Those who like their Pinot on the savory and earthy side will enjoy this wine. Leading with the aroma of charred, salted meat, notes of bright red fruits and herbal bitters quickly follow along. The light-bodied palate is beautifully composed, offering a wealth of fruit and floral flavors, and a long, savory finish. Drink now–2025.
title: Dalrymple 2014 Pipers River Cottage Block Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

description: From a consistently great producer, the 2015 Pipers River offers a wealth of aromas: sunbaked earth, dried leaves, sea spray, cocoa nibs and just-picked forest fruit. The palate is light and elegant, with soft but structured tannins and a cinnamon spice tone. It's totally approachable now but could age another five to seven years if you can bear to wait that long. Drink 2023–2025.
title: Dalrymple 2015 Pipers River Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

description: Lifted, succulent tones of mango, peach and melon abound in this lip-smacking yet complex auslese. It's boldly concentrated in fruit but exceptionally fine and filigreed on the palate and shaded by nuances of crushed slate, blossom and salt. The finish is piercing and long marked by spine tingling lime acidity.
title: Dr. H. Thanisch (Erben Müller-Burggraef) 2016 Berncasteler Lay Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: A blend of 85% Chardonnay and 15% Pinot Nero, this offers enticing scents of brioche and yellow stone fruit. The elegant, focused palate delivers golden apple, bread crust, pomegranate and a mineral note alongside a refined perlage and vibrant acidity.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2013 Freccianera Nature  (Franciacorta)

description: Plumes of smoke and dark earth intensify tonic, lime and salty mineral notes in this nimble-footed auslese. It's a bracing, revitalizing wine, balanced by sweet-tart yellow peach and tangerine flavors that pierce through a long, nervous finish.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Kestener Paulinsberg Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Vanilla, sea salt, red berries, florals, smoke and orange zest all swirl together in this silky smooth Pinot. The palate is simultaneously salty and tangy, with flavors of sea spray and dried cranberries and raspberries.
title: Holm Oak 2015 Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This wine is a riot of strawberry preserves on the nose, while the palate is full bodied and detailed. It comes with a touch of lemon custard, hints of barrel toast, and an elegant texture.
title: J. Christopher 2015 Abbey Ridge Vineyard Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Pretty cherry candy flavors hold your attention here, balanced with perfectly ripened tannins. There's a light wash of milk chocolate and it finishes with cinnamon spice. In a stellar lineup of single-vineyard selections, the Shea is always surefire.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Shea Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: Layers of sweet, sun-kissed honeydew, grapefruit and tangerine flood the palate of this forward yet nuanced wine. There's a delightful cling of honey and nectar on the midpalate but it's counterbalanced neatly by strikes of lemon-lime acidity and an earthen minerality that lingers long on the finish. Drinks beautifully already but should improve further through 2035.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Herrenberg Spätlese Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Packed with red fruits, this wine is bright and crisp, while also having a firm, tannic background. The dense, dusty texture promises a ripe, juicy wine as it ages. Drink this generous wine from 2019.
title: Château Martinat 2014  Côtes de Bourg

description: This wine is ripe, juicy and packed with black-currant fruits. Its tannins and wood aging are playing a good supporting role. Generous and full, this juicy wine, from a 61-acre estate, will age well. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Poitevin 2015  Médoc

description: Fleshy red and black cherry aromas set the tone on the nose. The palate adds lots of freshness that is toned down by strucutre. Fine tannins add grip but this is all about hedonistic fruit with a clean, grippy finish. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2016 Les Jardins Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: A touch of honey joins the fresh lemon notes on the nose. The fruity, easy and light palate also has that honey-lemon combo. The finish is lip-smacking, easy and fresh.
title: Domaine Pfister 2016 Cuvée 8 White (Alsace)

description: A very shy, muted nose leads onto a very fresh, easy palate that offers a gentle hint of red apple. The body is light and refreshing.
title: Domaine Sipp-Mack 2016 Rosé (Alsace)

description: Fresh, vivid notes of juicy, ripe pear make for an inviting nose. The palate has the same easy, fruit-driven freshness: plenty of pear edged by lemon freshness. This is light, dry, easy and moreish. Drink soon.
title: Dopff & Irion 2016 Crustacés White (Alsace)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Pretty fruit flavors of raspberry and cherry come together, balanced against juicy acids. It was 30% whole cluster fermented and aged in 15% new oak, which remains well in the background. Another year or two of bottle age might well improve this wine a point or two.
title: Duck Pond 2016 Dundee Hills Reserve Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This 100% Sangiovese has aromas of red-skinned berry, Mediterranean scrub and white pepper. It's bright and fresh, with crushed raspberry, Marasca cherry and clove. Polished tannins provide lithe support.
title: Fattoria di Magliano 2014 Sinarra  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This light and elegant wine displays inviting scents of bread, strawberry, raspberry and wet stone, with flavors to match. It's immaculately clean, well-defined and persistent. Drink over the next five years.
title: Helioterra 2014 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: After barrel fermentation (40% new) and extended barrique aging, this wine is bottled with some of the lees left, extending autolysis and intentionally diminishing fruit. It's oaky and textural, with a creamy, yeasty flavor more than any particular fruit. It's unusual and compelling, though not made in any conventional style.
title: Johan 2014 Visdom Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)

Winery: La Cave des Vignerons de Pfaffenheim
description: A hint of bruised apple plays on the nose but the palate shows ample freshness in flavors of ripe pear and apple, with a tart lemon balance. This is fruity and easy but also rather refreshing. Drink soon.
title: La Cave des Vignerons de Pfaffenheim 2016 Tradition Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux, Bordeaux, France
description: A lieu-dit in French is a place historically recognized for what was there, a hazelnut (coudrat) tree. This wine comes from a single vineyard named after the trees grew there. It is a rich wine, packed with the bright fruit of the vintage as well as some firm tannins. Aged in wood, it has spice and toast as well as the black-currant fruits. Drink this wine from 2021.
title: Les Vignerons de Tutiac 2014 Lieu-Dit Coudrat  (Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Made from 100% of the variety, this is expectedly dark in color and big and weighty in tannin and oak. Inviting aromas of cinnamon, mushroom, graphite and cedar give it more of a savory profile, searching for fruit, which appears in the form of black currant. The finish is bitter in leather and black pepper.
title: Luna 2015 Petit Verdot (Napa Valley)

description: This opens with aromas evoking red-skinned berry, alpine herb and vanilla. The linear palate offers Marasca cherry, tobacco and clove alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2015 Grumello Garof  (Valtellina Superiore)

Appellation: Terrazze Retiche di Sondrio, Lombardy, Italy
description: Aromas suggesting wild strawberry, rose petal and a whiff of mint take shape in the glass. The lively palate delivers crushed raspberry, Marasca cherry and a hint of cinnamon alongside lithe tannins.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2016 Botonero Nebbiolo (Terrazze Retiche di Sondrio)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Spicy oak combines with ripe black grape and black-cherry flavors in this bold and nicely tannic wine. Paired with a beefy dish it will be just right. Best after 2020.
title: Occasio 2014 Telos Red (Livermore Valley)

description: Pristine lemon and yellow apple notes are marked by saline minerality and whiffs of smoke and nuts in this briskly composed Pinot Blanc. Dry and medium bodied in style, it's a revitalizing, cutting sip, with a moderately long lime-zest finish. Drink now through 2021.
title: Schloss Saarstein 2016 Pinot Blanc (Mosel)

description: The style of this LBV changes little. It is always a fruity, ready-to-drink Port, rich in black fruits and juicy acidity. It is perfumed, ripe, warming and lightly structured. It is ready to drink.
title: Taylor Fladgate 2013 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

Appellation: Gioia del Colle, Southern Italy, Italy
description: This wine is brooding in dense cherry and red plum aromas, peppered with notes of herbs and violets. The palate is broad, bright, juicy red and black fruit flavors and lively acidity are balanced by the bolstering tannins.
title: Tenute Chiaromonte 2014 Muro Sant'Angelo Contrada Barbatto Primitivo (Gioia del Colle)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Tight at first, this wine opens to aromas of ripe red berries, tar and coconut shavings. The palate is rich in feel with dark cocoa bitterness, yet juicy acidity builds on the midpalate to reveal fresh berry tones. This needs time in the cellar to fully integrate. Drink from 2022.
title: Tormaresca 2013 Bocca Di Lupo Aglianico (Castel del Monte)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Roasted blackberry and caramelized fennel bulb show on the nose of this blend of 53% Cabernet Sauvignon, 42% Syrah and 5% Petite Sirah. Prominent licorice spice shows on the blueberry-laced palate, where tannins stick to your teeth and the acidity holds everything up.
title: Trenza 2013 Obispan Red (San Luis Obispo County)

description: Blue flower, baked plum, vanilla and oak aromas segue over to the bright palate along with black cherry and star anise. Fresh acidity and polished tannins are there to provide support.
title: Triacca 2012 Sassella Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: The spice from aging in American oak barrels is evident in this perfumed wine. It also has ripe berry fruits and acidity as a promise for the future. The wine could do with some aging, so wait to drink until 2019.
title: Vicente Faria Vinhos 2016 Gloria Reserva Red (Douro)

description: A hint of pear drop plays amidst the lemon scent. It is the lemon freshness that takes over on the palate which is supremely light and fresh. This freshness rings right through to the dry finish.
title: Cave du Roi Dagobert 2016 Tradition Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: This is a rich wine, generous with ripe berry fruit. Produced in the northern Médoc, it holds equal quantities of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, giving both juicy fruits and a firm structure that will allow the wine to age. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Amour 2015  Médoc

description: This family-owned estate has produced a dark and dense wine. With plenty of ripe berry fruits and good acidity, it is well proportioned, juicy and firm. Perfumed black currants and a sense of balance are hallmarks of this wine. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Balac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This is a structured northern-Médoc wine with good aging potential from Jean-Jacques Baudoux. With its tannins well in play, it has a dry core that is emphasized by the spice and toast from wood aging. The wine is likely to need some time, so drink from 2022.
title: Château Cangruey 2015  Médoc

description: With a blast of Petit Verdot in the blend (15%, not typical Médoc), this structured, toasty wine revels in its ripe tannins. It is packed with fruit, with a dry core that will allow it to age. Drink the wine from 2024.
title: Château l'Argenteyre 2015  Médoc

description: Ripe earthy aromas of berry fruits, compost and cheesy oak are disparate but good. A raw, grabby palate is bumpy as it deals flavors of berry fruits, cassis and aged cheese. While the finish lacks total harmony, this wine has enough power and quality of flavors to continue on.
title: Clos Berenguer del Molar 2015 Clos de Tafall Red (Priorat)

description: Hints of ripe red cherry and tart Morello combine on the nose. The palate is light but has a frame of fine tannins. Freshness and lightness are the chief attractions here, with conifer in the background. Overall a light, traditional, savory Pinot.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2016 Rouge des 2 Cerfs Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Demeter-certified biodynamic grapes go into the making of this light and delicate wine—a fine example of coastal cooling. Layered in earthy rose petal and cardamom, it shows length and breadth around a quiet core of tart rhubarb and pomegranate.
title: Benziger 2015 Signaterra La Reyna Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: A blend of 90% Chardonnay and 10% Pinot Nero, this refined sparkler opens with heady aromas of white wild flower, citrus and a whiff of bread crust. On the lively palate, fresh acidity brightens creamy white peach, Golden Delicious apple and lemon drop. A brioche note wraps up the finish.
title: Berlucchi NV '61 Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Bright and savory, this refreshing, polished sparkler boasts alluring aromas of juicy raspberry, wild cherry, baking spice and a whiff of bread crust. The inviting aromas follow over to the vibrant palate along with a continuous refined perlage. A tangy candied nectarine note closes the finish.
title: Berlucchi NV '61 Rosé  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from one of the state's top Syrah vineyards, this offers high-toned aromas of potpourri, red and blue fruit, sage, bacon fat, iron and spice. These lead to supple, restrained smoked meat and red-fruit flavors, stretching out on the finish. It brings a sense of elegance, taking some time to get going before strutting its stuff.
title: Betz Family 2015 La Serenne Boushey Vineyard Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: Bright and delicately scented, this elegant sparkler evokes aromas of spring wild flower, white stone fruit and crushed herb. The bright palate offers Rennet apple, Anjou pear, pastry cream and tangy lemon drop. It's balanced with fresh acidity while a silky mousse lends finesse.
title: Bonafidi NV Nobilium Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Exotic spice, pressed violet and woodland-berry aromas create an inviting bouquet. The linear elegant palate delivers tart cherry, strawberry, licorice and white pepper while bright acidity and firm refined tannins provide balance and support. A note of orange zest closes the finish. It's still young so give it time to fully blossom. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Boscarelli 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: In the opulent, concentrated school of thought this wonderful luxurious-tasting wine is shaped by very ripe fruit flavors and generous helpings of suave oak. Flavors run from marzipan to honeysuckle and poached pears, and a nicely viscous texture fills the mouth and helps the finish linger.
title: Cedarville 2016 Estate Viognier (El Dorado)

description: Part of Domaines Martin, which also owns Château Gloria in Saint-Julien, this wine benefits from a classed-growth winery's attention to detail. The wine is rich, dense and structured. Its black-plum and blackberry fruits are rich, sustained by fine tannins and acidity. It's a wine to age, so drink from 2024.
title: Château Bel Air Gloria 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Close to some of the top Margaux vineyards, this estate regularly produces some fine wines. This bottling is beautifully structured, firm with tannins and also generous with black fruits. A high percentage of Merlot gives a particularly ripe and juicy wine that will age over the medium-term. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Cambon la Pelouse 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: From vineyards surrounding the medieval moated castle, this wine is well balanced, rich and yet structured. It has great blackberry fruit and dry tannins for aging and it's coming together well. Drink from 2022.
title: Château d'Agassac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Ripe and densely textured, this is a plush, full-bodied wine. With swathes of rich black fruits and some firm tannins, it has good potential. The richness contrasts with the black-currant acidity that gives this otherwise dense wine a bright, crisp aftertaste. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Lestage Simon 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Purchased by Olivier Decelle, who also owns property in Saint-Émilion and a negociant business in Burgundy, this estate is producing impressive wines. This bottling is richly structured while preserving all the ripe blackberry fruits and intense acidity. Drink this wine from 2023.
title: Château Maurac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine, full of dusty dry tannins as well as ripe black-currant fruit, blends equal proportions of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Its richness comes from the full fruitiness as well as the dry core that will allow the wine to age. It needs time and should not be drunk before 2024.
title: Château Pontoise Cabarrus 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Dark and dense, this wine has good aging potential, with black fruits as well as a dry core of tannins. Minerality shows through in the texture of the wine. It is rich and generous, reflecting the vintage. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Quintus 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: Made with organically grown Pinot Nero, this vibrant sparkler has subtle aromas of wild berry, crushed herb and an apple orchard in bloom. Structured and elegant, the savory palate delivers tart red cherry, raspberry compote, baking spice and a hint of brioche set against bright acidity. Small continuous bubbles provide the refined perlage.
title: Corte Bianca 2011 Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Showing its age well, this pure expression of Negroamaro immediately engages the nose with aromas of concentrated berries, wild herbs and a hint of hoisin sauce. Round in feel, with structured acidity and supportive, fine-grained tannins, the palate offers a wealth of vitality in fresh red cherry, cranberry and streaks of granite. Savory herbs and earth tones linger on the finish. This is in a sweet spot now, so drink soon.
title: Cosimo Taurino 2011 Patriglione Negroamaro (Salento)

description: The nose is very shy but gives the slightest promise of ripe, red fruit. Red cherry appears in full splendor on the palate, edged by racy, exciting freshness and superfine but crunchy tannins. The body remains slender but there is a sensuous, warm core of ripe fruit. Lovely now but will develop over the coming five years. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Domaine Barmès-Buecher 2016 Réserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This is fresh, aromatic and primary in cherry aromas. The same luscious, generous fruit also dominates the juicy palate. There is freshness to balance this abundance but this is hedonistic and fruit driven to the core, with a cinnamon undertone. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 Strangenberg Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: The nose offers lovely touches of stone, fern and white pepper, with the freshness of green apple. There is something pristine about this wine, something light, clear and utterly moreish. The finish is clean and dry.
title: Domaine Roland Schmitt 2016 Grand A du Petit Léon Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: While feather light and ethereal in texture, there's an abundance of plump, juicy yellow cherry, guava and even strawberry fruitiness here. It's unabashedly forward and fruity yet racy and fresh. The finish lingers on a lovely floral tone.
title: Dr. H. Thanisch (Erben Müller-Burggraef) 2016 Berncasteler Doctor Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Hints of caramelized sugar, pollen and orange peel glaze this succulent, lip-smacking Riesling. Its delicate sweetness is bolstered by sunny squirts of orange and yellow peach, but balanced neatly against a solid backbone of lime acidity. A deeply penetrating spätlese with a long burnt-sugar finish.
title: Dr. Hermann 2016 Erdener Herzlei Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: A cool, steely undertone intensifies crisp, yellow stone fruit and lime on the nose and palate. With electric, sweet-tart darts of honey and nectar, this is a piercing, concentrated wine that has a long lime-pith finish.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Erdener Treppchen Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Intoxicating notes of exotic spice, smoke and crushed earth intensify flavors of pristine white grapefruit and peach in this tantalizing kabinett. Deeply penetrating in fruit and vibrantly balanced in acidity, it finishes with a long, crystalline finish.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Ürziger Würzgarten Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Dusty earth and smoke tones are predominant on first whiff but open gradually, offering a succession of ripening fruits from fresh lime and gooseberry to crunchy peach and pink grapefruit. It's a spry, taut wine, with scintillating acidity and a long crystalline finish.
title: Fritz Haag 2016 Brauneberger Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of 49% Syrah, 38% Grenache and a whopping 13% Viognier, with the latter expressing itself on both the aromas and palate. The wine pops, with notes of freshly peeled orange, stargazer lilly, gravel, olive, smoked meat, blueberry and boysenberry. Soft and sumptuous, the palate shows a lot of elegance and expression along with an extended finish.
title: Gård 2015 Reverence Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Pronounced aromas of struck match and flint belie the intensity of fresh grapefruit and lemon at the core of this delicately off-dry wine. It's more earth and herbs than forward or fruity but there's a savory intensity here that's beguiling. It's a wine that should improve with some cellaring, try after 2020.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Piesporter Treppchen von den Terrassen Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is the top cuvée from the winery, and just a single barrel was produced. It's packed with baking spices, vanilla, black tea and cardamom. These are more than highlights: they are a substantial element, along with ripe black fruits. This was all whole-cluster fermented, and aged for 18 months in barrel.
title: Gypsy Dancer 2014 Cuvée Romy Pinot Noir (Oregon)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This typifies the best wines from this AVA, with well-matched components. Ripe sweet red berries are at the core, with accents of dried herbs and a whiff of cannabis. Everything is smoothly integrated and works well as a seamless whole.
title: J. Christopher 2015 Bieze Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Arroyo Grande Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: There are the typical rich aromas of blackberry pastry and black plum on the nose of this Zinfandel, but also a flowery elegance and savory hints of char. The palate is quite interesting, with powerful cracked pepper, dried herb and charred wood decorating the pure black plum backdrop and a long caramelized finish. A compelling take on Zin.
title: Labyrinth 2015 Zinfandel (Arroyo Grande Valley)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine is super dark in hue and packed full of blackberry, wood smoke and black pepper flavors. It brings a relatively light (for the varietal) mouthfeel considering that it does have a wealth of fine-grained tannins and elevated alcohol. Drink now with a steak or chop, or cellar until 2025 for more maturity and nuance.
title: Lava Cap 2014 Petite Sirah (El Dorado)

description: Lovely, bright notes of green citrus, pear, greengage and fig set the stage for this crisp, refreshing white, with secondary hints of fresh straw, lanolin and strawberry hull add additional layers of interest. The palate boasts a fresh, chalky mineral texture, with a final hint of just-toasted almond on the evolving close. Drink now–2023.
title: Quinta Essentia 2016 Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: Earthy tones of fresh tilled soil, licorice root and sweet char lead the bouquet of this wine, grounded by the ripe black-fruit core. Velvety in texture, yet somewhat firm in structure, the palate boasts flavors of ripe black cherry and berry that carry through to the finish alongside hints of curing spice and hickory smoke. Drink now–2021.
title: Raats Family 2015 Dolomite Cabernet Franc (Stellenbosch)

description: This is made from two estate vineyards in partnership with a restaurant, Poggio, in Sausalito, where the winery owner lives. Combining 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 31% Merlot, 6% Petit Verdot and 3% Malbec into its own unique cuvée, and then aged in less oak than many of the winery's other wines to make it pairable with Poggio's cuisine, it offers mountain notes of sage, leather, cedar and graphite within a dense, structured frame of chocolate-covered blackberry.
title: Robert Craig 2015 Poggio Howell Mountain Cuvée Red (Howell Mountain)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Quite light in the glass, this bottling from three famous vineyards in the appellation shows intriguing aromas of candied raspberry with hints of sage, thyme, star anise and pickled rose petals. It's light and easy on the palate, with flavors of hibiscus and cranberry. Goes down fast.
title: Scar of the Sea 2015 Seven Leagues Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: There's a leathery, tobacco-like seam throughout this dark and masculine Cab, adding intrigue to the black plum and cassis core. Hints of char and roasted coffee give a rich impression to the palate, which feels plush and velvety in texture yet well-structured and defined. The finish is long and evolving, with an enduring grip and final notes of cigar box and black-fruit skin. Drink 2021–2025.
title: Stark-Condé 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Though disconcertingly packaged in a Burgundy bottle (hence the whimsy?) this is another delicious Riesling from Styring. Rolling fruit flavors of apple, pear, peach and tangerine support pink-grapefruit acids. It's tangy and tasty, not too tart, and persists through a clean, crisp finish.
title: Styring 2016 Whimsy Riesling (Ribbon Ridge)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of Zinfandel from Bailey Ranch, Will's Hills and Osgood Farms offers aromas of dark strawberry, black cherry and a dusty baked-earth minerality. The dark black fruit is prominent on the sip, where richness is central to the experience, with hints of dried marjoram giving depth and a rising acidity on the finish.
title: Thacher 2014 Triumvirate Reserve Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: Unusual and delicate aromas include dried herb, graphite and balsamic notes that carry over to the refined palate along with Golden Delicious, lemon zest and toasted bread crust. A walnut note closes the finish.
title: Villa Crespia 2008 Francesco Iacono Riserva Dosaggio Zero  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: A chocolaty, well-oaked nose with minerality and core cassis aromas is contradicted by a juicy palate with bright acidity and tension. Dry, salty flavors of berry and cassis are mildly toasted, while this wine is peppery and snappy on an acid-driven finish. The acid level on this wine suggests more aging; drink through 2032.
title: Viña Vilano 2014 Terra Incógnita  (Ribera del Duero)

Designation: Caldo Infernale Zeltinger Sonnenuhr Alte Reben Auslese
description: Sun-kissed yellow cherry and mango notes are juxtaposed by struck flint, salt and slate in this spry yet powerfully fruity Riesling. The midpalate is sweet and spicy yet intensely mineral, reverberating through a long, stony finish. Drinks well already but likely to improve from 2021.
title: Weingut Albert Gessinger 2016 Caldo Infernale Zeltinger Sonnenuhr Alte Reben Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Fleshy and fully tannic, this is a big bold 100% varietal red from one estate. Dried herb, cedar and black current give it a classic character of savory seasoning and appropriate herbaceousness.
title: Whitehall Lane 2014 Leonardini Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (St. Helena)

description: This is a 100% varietal wine, made in a dark concentrated style that's perfumed in cherry blossom. Taut sinewy layers of cassis and ever-present tannin combine effortlessly into a seamless whole, with accents of cedar and graphite.
title: Whitehall Lane 2014 Mancino Lanciatore Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Quite young and powerful at the moment, this broody Margaret River Cabernet offers up ripe cherry, mocha, florals and dusty, herbal oak notes. The palate is rich and plush, with more oak driven herbacousness, chewy tannins and a savory finish. Set this in the cellar for a few years and try it again with some more age on it. Drink 2019–2032.
title: Xanadu 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Margaret River)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Malbec shines in Southern Oregon. Here it's given 16 months in new French oak, emerging supple with tangy berry and pie cherry fruit. Suggestions of citrus juice come with balancing acidity. Tannins are ripe, substantial and lightly earthy, with a hint of coffee.
title: 2Hawk 2015 Malbec (Rogue Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Blue flower, plum cake and baking spice aromas come to the forefront while a whiff of tilled earth lingers in the background. The elegant firmly structured palate shows raspberry preserves, star anise and espresso set against taut, polished tannins. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Antico Colle 2013 Il Saggio Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Enticing scents of white spring blossoms and yellow stone fruit waft out of the glass. The bright creamy palate delivers Golden Delicious apple, juicy citrus zest, white almond and white peach flavors alongside an elegant perlage.
title: Bellavista 2013 Satèn  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from an up-and-coming vineyard, this wine is 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Syrah. It's aromatically expressive, with notes of ripe black cherry, plum, licorice and high-toned dried herbs. The palate is dense and layered, showing breadth and richness while staying in balance. Drink in the near term.
title: Buty 2014 Columbia Rediviva Phinny Hill Vineyard Red (Horse Heaven Hills)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
Designation: Rediviva of the Stones Rockgarden Estate
description: This wine is a unique blend of 77% Syrah, 13% Cabernet Sauvignon and 10% Mourvèdre. Hailing from the Rocks District, it offers generous aromas of black olive, plum, wet gravel, crushed flower, green olive, smoked meat, parsley and dried herb. These lead to pure, ample fruit and savory flavors. A taste of smoked meat lingers on the finish.
title: Buty 2014 Rediviva of the Stones Rockgarden Estate Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Chardonnay, this elegant sparkler offers aromas of yellow stone fruit and white spring flower. The creamy palate delivers ripe yellow apple, crushed herb and tangerine zest  alongside a soft perlage.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2013 Vintage Collection Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: Notes of rosehip, clove, nutmeg, red currant, rhubarb and wild strawberry all abound on the nose and mouth in this fresh, vibrant wine. Fine tannins lend a soft structure to the medium-weight palate, with bright acidity providing freshness and levity. Hints of Dr. Pepper and tart red currant ride out the mouthwatering close. Drink now–2021.
title: A.A. Badenhorst Family Wines 2015 Ramnasgras Cinsault (Swartland)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Seven clones contribute to this blend, an aromatic and elegant wine with wild berries, a touch of rhubarb, and some peppery green highlights. It's light but not at all thin, nor does it short the finish. In fact, it's deceptively long, and that is its real strength.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 Dijon Clones Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is three-quarters Pommard, the rest a field blend from low-yielding reserve-quality vines, and treated to 50% new French oak. Lightly peppery and herbal, its berry fruit comes with suggestions of chicken stock and a dash of sea salt. Not quite as concentrated as previous vintages, but balanced and drinking nicely.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Mark IX Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: A reserved earthy nose smells a bit leafy and stalky. A wide palate with hard tannins delivers textbook Rioja flavors of dried cherry and spiced berry fruits. An earthy note alongside chocolate on the finish ensures that is very much in the vein of Rioja gran reserva. Drink through 2027.
title: Bodegas Franco-Españolas 2008 Gran Reserva Excelso  (Rioja)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of underbrush, pipe tobacco, toasted oak and a whiff of baked plum appear in the forefront. The tightly wound palate offers dried black cherry, licorice and grilled herb framed in austere close-grained tannins. Give the tannins a few more years to relax then drink to catch the fruit richness.
title: Carpineto 2010 Vigneto Poggio Sant'Enrico  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: A ripe, balanced wine, this is full of generous fruit, rich tannins and blackberry flavors. Acidity comes through this concentrated wine to give a crisp edge. This wine, with a blend dominated by 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, will age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château La Lauzette 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: The old vines of the Laride estate go into this wine. Serious wood aging has given a dark-toast and bitter-chocolate character that currently masks the rich blackberry and black-plum fruits. The wine needs time. Drink from 2021.
title: Château Laride 2014 L'Iris de Laride  (Haut-Médoc)

description: From a blend dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon, this is a structured wine. Its tannins and black-currant fruit are balancing well, giving both a dry side and a rich, juicy aftertaste. This wine has good potential, so drink from 2022.
title: Château Leboscq 2015  Médoc

description: Pear, citrus zest, brioche and toast aromas flow over to the vibrant palate along with tangerine and yellow apple skin. Bright acidity and small continuous bubbles lend freshness and finesse. A bitter-almond note closes the finish.
title: Contadi Castaldi 2013 Zero  (Franciacorta)

description: Delightful aromas of red plum and cherry tomato float above bread dough and basil on the nose. The juicy palate brings tart cherry and more plum into the mix, with white pepper and green herb accents. This offers a light and easy mouthfeel full of vibrant acidity and structured by velvety tannins. The juicy cherry finish is utterly charming.
title: Dablon 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Lake Michigan Shore)

description: A blend of 35% Chenin Blanc, 23% Roussanne, 15% Clairette, 19% Viognier and 8% Sémillon, this is dense and softly toasty upfront, with seductive notes of baked apple, pressed yellow flowers, dried orange peel, straw and lanolin. The palate is similarly lush and decadent, though with an ample seam of acidity to keep it all in check. Drink now–2022.
title: David & Nadia 2015 David Aristargos White (Swartland)

description: Reduced, with a spritzy underbelly of acidity, this wine lands fresh and vibrant in dried herb and green apple, with a medium-bodied ripeness and use of oak.
title: Benziger 2015 Signaterra West Rows Sangiacomo Vineyard Chardonnay (Los Carneros)

description: This is not your average red blend, because it has outstanding concentration, multiple layers of dark-fruit flavors and cedary, spicy nuances as well as a richly tannic texture. It will pair well with a grilled lamb chop, and should improve with a little age. Best after 2020.
title: Bogle 2014 Phantom Red (California)

Appellation: Los Olivos District, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a growing trend of making red wines fresh, fun and ready to glug, and this bottling, which spent just six months in French oak, fits the movement perfectly. Bright aromas of rose, lilac, smashed black raspberry, candied plum skin and pepper dust dive into a zippy palate of cherry fruit, baking spice and dried ginger. Chill and drink on a hot day.
title: Brander 2016 Vino Joven Cabernet Sauvignon (Los Olivos District)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: From vines planted in 1983, this delivers old-vine complexity at a budget price. It's big, broad and fruity, with a range of candied strawberry, raspberry, cherry flavors. Those old vines ramp up the texture and add length as well.
title: Cardwell Hill 2014 Old Vines Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Fruity, herbal and with crisp acidity, this bright wine is full of grapefruit, apples and green plums. The mineral tang will give this wine shape as it matures. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Cos d'Estournel 2015 Cos d'Estournel Blanc  (Bordeaux Blanc)

Designation: La Grande Dame de Château Lavagnac
description: This oak-aged wine is the top cuvée from this estate. The wood is prominent at this young stage, although there is also plenty of black fruit. The wood, as it integrates, will enrich this ripe wine, bringing out the firm structure. The wine needs to age. Drink from 2020.
title: Château de Lavagnac 2015 La Grande Dame de Château Lavagnac  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: Set in the heart of Haut-Médoc and close to the 18th-century fortress designed to stop the British attacking through the Gironde estuary (they never tried), this estate has made a firm, tannic wine that has great black fruits and a rich potential. Drink from 2022.
title: Château du Moulin Rouge 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Coming from up to 60 different parcels spread across 100 acres, this wine is impressive in its richness and fine, dusty tannins. It is dense, concentrated and sure to age well. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Grivière 2015  Médoc

description: As befits a wine that is part of this Pomerol satellite appellation, this is almost entirely Merlot. New-wood aging adds a minty character and dark-coffee flavor. Firm tannins, bold black fruits and balanced acidity all make their mark in this potentially opulent wine. Drink from 2021.
title: Château le Manoir 2015 Cuvée Prestige  (Lalande de Pomerol)

description: Merlot gives this wine its richness and density. Partial oak aging adds to the ripe, smooth texture. Tannins, layers of spice and bold red fruits add concentration as well as juicy acidity. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Mayne Vieil 2015  Fronsac

description: This wine comes from a seven-acre property in the south of the Haut-Médoc. Well textured, ripe with black fruits and firm with tannins, this is quite an enjoyable wine. It will develop into a beautiful, rich wine. Drink from 2024.
title: Clos la Bohème 2015  Haut-Médoc

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A dark potent wine, this blends smoky black-cherry fruit with a streak of cola and a generous helping of brown spices. The chewy finish has a nice lick of minerality which keeps it aloft.
title: Alexana 2015 East Blocks Volcanic Soils Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Pommard clone wines are generally fruit-forward, as shown here, with a pleasing array of red and blue berries and fruit. Tangy acids prop up the focused core and lead into a tight finish dotted with baking spices.
title: Alexana 2015 Revana Vineyard Single Clone Pommard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The ripest of the winery's 2013 Pinots, this rounds out the fruit with pretty raspberry and cherry flavors. Light touches of oranges and chocolate are also evident, and the overall balance, length and elegance are fine. Just two barrels were produced, one new and one neutral.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 Pommard Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This cuvée aims, the winery explains, for the most elegant expression of the estate vineyard. It succeeds, with minimal barrel influence (50% once-filled) around pretty flavors of raspberries and citrus. Finishing details bring up lemon rind, pineapple and a touch of cola.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Heather's Reserve Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: High acid yet tilting toward off dry with 13 g/l residual sugar, this shows the spicy side of the grape, rather than dousing the drinker in perfume. It plays out broadly peachy across the palate, dotted with hints of powdered sugar, ginger and melon.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Nicholas Vineyard Dry Gewürztraminer (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Production continues to climb on this widely available well-priced Oregon sparkling wine. A tawny straw color, it's vibrant and fresh, with yeasty dough accents around pear fruit. The balance and concentration suggest it may be cellared for up to a decade, but it's drinking quite well right now.
title: Argyle 2014 Grower Series Vintage Brut Sparkling (Willamette Valley)

description: Not one of the biggest or boldest versions, this earthy wine is medium to full bodied and firmly tannic, offering untamed aromas and flavors of earth, barnyard, beef and wild blackberry. It's funky in a good way, very interesting to taste and has good balance, too.
title: Barra of Mendocino 2015 Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Mendocino)

description: Pressed wild flower, ripe yellow stone fruit and brioche aromas abound on this lively sparkler. The vibrant palate delivers Golden Delicious apple, tangerine zest and bitter almond alongside tangy acidity. A vivacious perlage provides the backdrop.
title: Bellavista 2012 Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Bracing lemon zest and orange blossom perfume brightens this robustly concentrated spätlese. Fresh tangerine and apricot flavors are bright and sweet, accented by spine tingling acidity and a reverberating orange-pith finish.
title: Bischöfliche Weingüter Trier 2016 Dom Off-Dry Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is a stunning concoction, a mix of mostly Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, with smaller additions of Syrah, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc and Malbec. Together they combine to offer a classically structured elegance of dried herb and juicy red currant that finds companionship in gamy meat and white pepper.
title: Blue Rock 2015 Baby Blue Red (Sonoma County)

description: Notions of ripe cherry fruit are evident from the start. This is fruity and fresh, full of tart cherry tones and a refreshing conifer edge. The body is slender and the light tannins offer a little crunch. A lovely, easy, authentic and fruit-driven wine.
title: Boeckel 2016 Midelberg Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Bramble, licorice root and fresh fynbos lead the nose of this attractive wine, grounded by bold black-cherry and boysenberry fruit at its core. Firmly structured tannins lend serious grip and texture, while fruity flavors transition into waves of olive tapenade, cured meat and peppery spice on the enduring finish. It's drinkable now, but should be best with a bit more time; drink 2020–2025.
title: Boschkloof 2015 Sons of Sugarland Syrah (Stellenbosch)

description: This blend of 60% Aglianico and 40% Montepulciano boasts tobacco, methol and crushed herbs over a core of spicy bramble berries on the palate. There's solid structure, weight and intensity which echos the tones of the nose and carries those flavors on a tight mineral vein.
title: Botromagno 2014 Pier delle Vigne Red (Murgia)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine leans into the savory, with aromas of sliced green pepper along with assorted fresh herbs, flowers and macerated cherry. The palate is soft in style, filled with sleek, minerally cherry flavors. It brings a sense of freshness, purity and elegance.
title: Buty 2015 Conner Lee Vineyard Merlot-Cabernet Franc (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Syrah and Petit Verdot, this has aromas that recall black-skinned berry, violet, leather and tilled soil. The concentrated, balanced palate doles out black currant, tobacco, licorice and a touch of tar framed in polished tannins. Enjoy through 2022.
title: Castello di Bolgheri 2015 Varvàra  (Bolgheri)

description: A wonderfully subtle nose hints at rose and honeysuckle blossom. The palate is completely dry, light in nature and offers floral flavors brightened by lemon freshness. This combines wonderful, dry lightness with subtlety.
title: Cave du Roi Dagobert 2016 Sélection Muscat (Alsace)

description: Structured and still firm, this is a dense wine from the Donze family estate. It has firm tannins over the rich, juicy black fruits that are generous and reflect the vintage. This wine is still young, so wait and drink from 2020.
title: Château Martinat 2015  Côtes de Bourg

description: The 18 months in wood has enhanced the already succulent, exuberant black fruits of this wine. Black-coffee flavors combine with dense tannins and black-plum flavors to produce a superrich, big-hearted wine. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Tour de Pressac 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: The 30% new wood in this wine from Lionel Poitou (5th generation) shows in the minty flavors. They balance with the ripe black-currant fruits to produce a wine that will benefit from aging. Juicy, dense and smoky, it's a ripe wine that will be ready from 2023.
title: Château Tour du Haut-Moulin 2015  Haut-Médoc

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Enticing scents of fragrant blue flower, perfumed berry, baking spice and a whiff of tilled earth float to the forefront. Savory and medium-bodied, the smooth palate reveals juicy wild cherry, raspberry preserve, licorice and cinnamon alongside polished tannins. Bright acidity lends balance. Drink through 2023.
title: Dei 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Vanilla and toasted-coconut-laced black-fruit abound on the nose of this seductive Bordeaux-style blend, with a solid concentration of black cherry, boysenberry and blackberry flavors. Medium-plus in weight, the tannins are bold but integrated into the plush fruit profile, and become well-resolved on the char-spiced finish. Drink 2021–2025.
title: Delaire Graff 2015 Botmaskop Red (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is 58% Grenache, 26% Syrah and 16% Mourvèdre. Aromas are a little funky out of the gate but open to reveal notes of savory herb, brown stem, smoked meat, black licorice, raspberry and blackberry. The cherry-gummy flavors are pure and fresh, with a lovely mouthfeel. Black-raspberry flavors linger on the finish. All about purity, it impresses.
title: Descendant 2013 Belle G-S-M (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The nose offers a hint of pear and a touch of lemon. These tones are reflected on the slender, dry palate, with concentration and ample fruit at the core. A lovely, light-bodied and elegant wine with a lasting finish.
title: Domaine Weinbach 2016 Réserve Clos des Capucins Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Designation: Bernkasteler alte Badstube am Doctorberg Spätlese
description: Upon first whiff, there's more earth than fruit here, but aeration reveals a surprising bounty of tropical fruit on the palate. Light footed and medium sweet in style, its chock full of honeyed mango, pineapple and citrus flavors juxtaposed by zesty lime acidity. The finish is moderately long, tinged by struck slate and smoke.
title: Dr. Heidemanns-Bergweiler 2016 Bernkasteler alte Badstube am Doctorberg Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Intensely chalky, smoky mineral tones penetrate through bracing white grapefruit and tangerine in this delicately sweet kabinett. Spine tingling lemon-lime acidity captivates the midpalate and leads a long, thrilling citrus finish. It's delightful now but should continue to improve through at least 2021 and hold far longer.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Erdener Treppchen Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Ripe, rich tangerine, apricot and peach pulsate through this light-bodied yet powerfully fruity spätlese. It's unctuously concentrated yet balanced by spine tingling acidity and a murmur of lime zest on the finish. Delightful already but likely to improve further through at least 2030.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Ürziger Würzgarten Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Exuberantly fresh white peach and grapefruit flavors are cut by streaks of lime in this concentrated, juicy kabinett. Lip smackingly sweet and tart, it offsets its forward, fruity facade with a core of bracing minerality. The finish is marked by lingering slate and smoke tones.
title: Dr. Loosen 2016 Wehlener Sonnenuhr Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This is the winery's first red release from this site, located next to famed Boushey Vineyard. Aromas of iron, bacon fat, brown spice and herb rise up from the glass. The palate bursts with red-fruit flavors that show a pleasing sense of intensity, depth and length. It's a promising start for this site.
title: Efeste 2014 Oldfield Estate Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: A superbly balanced selection, this opens with tight, slightly reserved notes of blackberry, plum and boysenberry that are graced with earthy hints of tobacco leaf, licorice and sweet-cured meat. The palate is rich and tasty, yet kept in check by ample, bright acidity and structured tannins. The evolving finish leaves you wanting more. Drink now–2023.
title: Eikendal 2015 Pinotage (Stellenbosch)

description: A hint of underripe cherry is all the nose gives away right now, but the palate opens into pure Morello notes. The body is poised and tart, fresh and vivid, with a lovely little crunch. This is light and crisp—ideal for those who love Pinot Noirs with an aromatic, light touch.
title: Emile Beyer 2015 Tradition Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This brand, which employs an army of winemakers from around the world to make dozens of wines under the Handpicked label, has succeeded in crafting a delicious, delicate Chard at an affordable price. Attractive notes of red apple peels and blossoms are backed by a hint of baking spice and a copper penny tang. The palate is light and fresh, with a tight, chalky texture and a long ginger and apple-peel finish. One to sip with salads and seafood.
title: Handpicked 2016 Version Chardonnay (Victoria)

description: Deep garnet in the glass, this wine has a nose of black cherry, blackberry and violet. Tannins are soft at first and then build slowly, enveloped in flavors of black currant, cherry, vanilla, clove and white pepper. The lush mouthfeel diminishes just as the bright finish draws to a close.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Genesis Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

description: This is a lean style and still a baby with its sappy red strawberry and raspberry notes, with cinnamon, tar, violets, warm stones and furniture polish. The palate is tightly wound, ending on a long, tart cranberry and raspberry finish. Drink now if you like your Pinot al dente, otherwise cellar through 2025.
title: Henschke 2015 Giles Pinot Noir (Adelaide Hills)

description: This wine is dusty and a little austere but is unmistakably Margaret River. Aromas of cedar, pencil lead, blackberry, baking spices and cocoa powder are followed by a drying palate wrapped in savory tannins. The fruit is slightly lost here, and things are still on the lean and mean side, but this should behave a little more generously with time in the cellar. Drink 2020–2027.
title: Howard Park 2014 Miamup Cabernet Sauvignon (Margaret River)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Pressed iris, plum cake and camphor aromas take center stage on this approachable red. The round chewy palate offers fleshy black cherry, blackberry jam and cinnamon alongside relaxed smooth tannins. Drink sooner rather than later to enjoy the juicy fruit.
title: Il Macchione 2011 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Inky garnet in color, this wine has a bouquet of black cherry, cranberry and toffee. It is bright on entry, followed by chewy tannins and flavors of cherry, blackberry, espresso bean and chocolate. A quick splash of pomegranate brightens the enduring finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2016 3400 Premium Cabernet Sauvignon (Judean Hills)

description: Succulent yellow-peach, grapefruit and melon aromas on the nose are contrasted by bracing hits of lime and lemon on the palate of this dry Riesling. While dancingly light on its feet, it's penetrating through a long, mineral-focused finish.
title: Johannishof 2016 Rüdesheimer Ramstein Trocken Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Delicate scents of white melon, orange rind, pressed wild flowers, quinine and barely-toasted apple skin dance in the bouquet of this attractive wine. Clean, precise flavors of apple flesh, Anjou pear and lime unfold on the medium-weight palate, while vibrant acidity enlivens the mouthfeel and results in a bright, mouthwatering finish. Drink now–2023.
title: Keermont 2015 Riverside Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: Lushly ripe pear and butter flavors are backed by vibrant, lemony acidity in this wine. It has good depth, medium body and a creamy texture, but stays balanced and fresh on the palate, leading to a lingering finish. Another outstanding vintage for a classic California wine.
title: Kendall-Jackson 2016 Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay (California)

Appellation: Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux, Bordeaux, France
description: This wine is a selection from three parcels fermented and aged in barrel. Despite this generous use of wood, the wine is just as much about the bright, ripe fruit of the vintage. Spice, black-currant flavor and concentration give a wine that deserves to age. Drink from 2020.
title: Les Vignerons de Tutiac 2014 L'Un dans l'Autre  (Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Prune and spice-cake aromas suggest a richly roasted wine. A massive palate with saturated weight and density delivers blackberry, chocolaty oak and pepper flavors in front of a finish that's a bit fiery. Drink this power-packed Valencian Cabernet through 2022.
title: Los Frailes 2014 G-11 Cabernet Sauvignon (Valencia)

description: A lovely note of mint chocolate rides atop the core of ripe wild berries and cherry in the nose and mouth of this attractive wine. It boasts a medium body and satiny texture, with ample structure and acidity for overall balance. The fruit-forward palate is accented by herbal, earthy hints, finishing with a flourish of baking spice. Drink now–2023.
title: Momento 2015 Grenache (Swartland)

description: Produced from the Quinta da Lomba vineyard, this blend of Bical, Encruzado and Malvasia is ripe, creamy with an oxidative character from the wood aging that adds an extra dimension to the crisp fruit. It is spicy, mineral and will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Niepoort 2015 Conciso Branco White (Dão)

description: This fruit-driven, ripe wine is also balanced. Full of black fruits and with elegance and poise, it is a wine for drinking early. Fruitiness and the touch of minerality and concentration from the old vines in the blend give both texture and crispness. Drink now.
title: Niepoort 2015 Vertente Red (Douro)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The nose brings suggestions of rum, hazelnuts and cocoa; in the mouth the wine is deep and delicious. Brambly berries are mixed with well-integrated barrel flavors of toasted nuts. The wine was aged in half new French oak for 20 months.
title: Ponzi 2015 Aurora Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: This wine, a field blend from old vines, is beautifully textured. It is made in a sweet, perfumed style, with dried fruits and prune flavors. Acidity does appear and gives the wine a crisp edge, although the main thrust of this wine is the opulent character. Drink now.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2013 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: Scents of honeysuckle, beeswax, baked apple and whole spice lead the nose of this attractive white. The palate showcases a goregeous balance between ripe fruit tones, well-integrated spice and ample acidity, with a gravely texture that lends length to the close. Final notes of woodspice and citrus pith leave you ready for more. Drink now–2024.
title: Radford Dale 2015 The Renaissance of Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: A fleshy fruity wine with tangy cranberry and red plum, and carrying a lush vein of pretty milk chocolate. The tasty barrel flavors are beautifully proportioned, with a finishing lick of cinnamon spice.
title: Saffron Fields Vineyard 2015 Dijon Clones Pinot Noir

description: This Cinsault-based blend, with 25% Grenache and 17% Syrah, opens with a beautiful bouquet of bright red cherry, raspberry and fig, all dusted by hints of sweet spice and fresh, minty fynbos. The medium-weight palate boasts fine tannins and a silky texture, with nuanced layers of earthy spice, tilled soil and tea that dance deftly on the finish. Drink now–2023.
title: Savage 2015 Follow The Line Red (Western Cape)

description: Lots of herbs, dried red currants and cranberry compote aromas show on the nose of this bottling, which has an earthy, gamy undercurrent. The palate tastes like it has more age on it, which is a good thing for now, with flavors of charred wood, damp forest floor, dried fruit and allspice, all riding a squeaky texture.
title: Wrath 2015 115/667 Pinot Noir (Monterey)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The ??? Pinots from Ayoub are made with fruit sourced from such excellent sources as Arcus, Renegade Ridge and Winderlea. Here, as elsewhere, the barrel management is as important as the fruit, putting a concise frame of toast around tight well-defined red-berry and black-cherry flavors. Streaks of cola and coffee add further interest through a lingering finish. Drink now to 2030.
title: Ayoub 2015 ??? Vineyards Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: A very intriguing hint of nettle plays along with the orange and mandarine zest aromas, creating a perfumed whiff of utter freshness. On the palate it is apple and orange ripeness that create a sort of dry, juicy richness and concentration. This is an unusual but utterly seductive Muscat.
title: Boeckel 2016 Midelberg Muscat (Alsace)

description: This rich, dense vintage Port is produced by Niepoort. It has immense tannins as well as layers of ripe fruit. The concentrated texture, powerful dry core and the richness of the wine promise good aging. Drink from 2028.
title: Broadbent 2015 Vintage  (Port)

description: One of the properties owned by the Chanfreau family of nearby Fonréaud, this 14-acre estate has produced a structured, toasty wine. Its high proportion of Cabernet Sauvignon gives it plenty of juicy black currants as well as fruit tannins. The wine needs to age and should be drunk from 2025.
title: Château Caroline 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Biodynamically grown, the grapes from this estate offer structure, concentration and a delicious balance between fruit and tannins. The wine has a serious side, with its dense texture and a straight line of pure black fruits. Drink from 2025.
title: Château la Marzelle 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: This is a rich wine with generous tannins and, more importantly, generous black fruits. It has a soft texture, the tannins fine and well integrated into the juiciness. It will be a delicious, fruity wine. Drink from 2022.
title: Château La Mission Haut-Brion 2015 La Chapelle  (Pessac-Léognan)

description: Produced from a Domaines Fabre parcel of old vines, this wine is rich, firm and concentrated. Wood aging has left its mark of toast and spice without losing the generous fruits. It is an elegant wine, full in the mouth and with great balance between all its elements. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Laride 2015 L'Iris de Laride  (Haut-Médoc)

description: From the southern part of the Haut-Médoc, this Tessandier family wine is a richly structured blend with Cabernet Sauvignon dominant. It has a dry, firm and densely tannic structure along with ripe and potentially generous blackberry fruit. The combination will allow this dark, firm wine to age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Maucamps 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: From a grand estate, whose owners also have vines in Margaux and Moulis, this wine is well proportioned and dense. Rich tannins and concentration are just part of what will become a generous, full wine. With great black-currant fruit and acidity to match, this structured wine will need to age. Drink from 2024,
title: Château Paloumey 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This deep garnet colored wine has aromas of strawberry, black cherry and fennel. Opulent tannins offer a rich mouthfeel, providing a backdrop for flavors of black cherry, black currant, mocha, pencil lead and lavender that wrap up in a cranberry finish.
title: Covenant Israel 2015 Syrah (Israel)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aged in neutral French oak, this has aromas that start out brooding but intriguing, with notes of raw meat, mineral, dried herb, crushed flower and huckleberry. The palate is earth-shaking in its richness and intensity, coating the palate with waves of fruit flavors. It hits the gong hard but maintains a lovely sense of balance.
title: Efeste 2015 Emmy Stone Tree Vineyard Mourvèdre (Wahluke Slope)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine erupts from the glass, with aromas of brown sugar, candied pineapple, dried orange rind and flower. Flavors are viscous, intense and rich, with a mango-filled finish that sails. It's the perfect end to any evening.
title: Gård 2014 Ice Wine Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Dark violet in the glass, this wine has aromas of blackberry, black cherry and vanilla. Flavors of black plum, blueberry, vanilla, clove and pipe tobacco are set into a framework of silky tannins that reed into a smooth cherry finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2014 Yarden Petit Verdot (Galilee)

description: Thrilling lime acidity and smoky, savory mineral tones meld beautifully in this delicately off-dry feinherb kabinett. It's finely textured and nimble but crisp yellow peach and apricot flavors penetrate firmly on the palate. The finish is lingering and silky, marked by hints of spice, struck flint and pollen. A fascinating wine that's likely to improve. Drink from 2020.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Brauneberger Juffer Kabinett Feinherb Riesling (Mosel)

description: The faintest touch of mocha and vanilla play enticingly on the dark-cherry nose. The palate is full of ripe red cherry, has a glossy, luscious sheen of oak, but handles it very well. A more international style but still with all the hallmark freshness and brightness of Alsace. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Henri Schoenheitz 2015 Herrenreben Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This is a densely packed, powerful and ripe wine from a tremendous site in the heart of the appellation. Black cherry, dark chocolate and coconut shavings make for a rich impression on the velvety palate, with plenty of toasted oak for support. This wine will do well in the cellar. Enjoy from 2024–2034.
title: Heritance 2014 Beckstoffer Georges III Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Grenache (69%) takes the lead on this wine, followed by Mourvèdre (26%) and a pinch of Syrah. Aged in neutral French oak, it offers aromas that aren't yet ready to reveal their charms on first pour. Notes of spice, herb and black pepper evolve into smoked meat and raspberry accents over time. The palate is swoon-worthy, with soft, supple fruit and savory flavors that lead to a persistent finish. The sense of purity dazzles.
title: Kerloo 2015 Majestic Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The merest hint of mandarin peel plays on the nose. This is heightened on the slender, dry palate which seems to have a pithy depth of citrus peel and the airy lightness of a cloud of mandarin. There is ample freshness here as well as elegance and persistence. The finish is off-dry and long.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2015 Trois Châteaux Muscat Ottonel (Alsace)

description: At first, this wine smells a bit burnt. But airing allows this gran reserva to show mature aromas of grilled beef, dry leaves and spice. Hard tannins make for a rubbery mouthfeel, but savory flavors of leafy plum and berry fruits bring it around. A finish with baked fruit and chocolate flavors concludes this classical Rioja. Drink through 2025.
title: Larchago 2008 Pagos de Tahola Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Blackberry aromas are deep and ripe, with woodsy side notes. A powerful palate is sinewy but not overly rugged or tannic. Blackberry and toasty flavors of chocolate and brown sugar smolder across a strident finish; drink through 2022.
