title: Los Frailes 2014 Sinergia Cabernet Sauvignon (Valencia)

description: Tangs of lime and salt penetrate through delicately sweet layers of grapefruit and tangerine in this pristine, laser-edged Riesling. It's spine tingling yet honeyed by a long, intensely mineral finish.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Herrenberg Superior Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, cedar and underbrush lead the nose on this savory blend of Merlot, Syrah, Cabernet and Sangiovese. The chewy palate delivers black-currant jam, mocha and star anise alongside polished, velvety tannins. Drink through 2027.
title: Michele Satta 2014 Piastraia  (Bolgheri Superiore)

description: Rounded black currants and a hint of prune mix with chocolate and black pepper on the nose of this very rich and powerful bottling from Paso Robles. The palate is thick and gooey with pepper spice cutting through the black-plum-cake flavors. Firm tannins and enduring acidity hold everything in check.
title: Nelle 2015 Syrah (California)

description: Blackberry and prune aromas are smoky and suggest hoisin or barbecue sauce. This Monastrell-Garnacha blend is a bit drawing and wiry on the palate, while flavors of toasty oak, blueberry and savory plum are bold. Oak character dominates a clampy, grating finish. Drink through 2023.
title: Pago Casa Gran 2015 Casa Benasal Crux Red (Valencia)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Full body and lots of toasted oak character give this reserve-style wine plenty of impact in the aroma, flavor and finish. Tempting scents of toasted almonds, vanilla and grilled baguette lead to concentrated pear, butter and cream on the palate, while the texture is powerful and velvety.
title: Rubino Estates Winery 2015 Landmark Collection Chardonnay (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Santa Clara Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Red cherry, rust, cinnamon, vanilla and wet herbs make for a crisp and compelling nose on this bottling by winemaker Tim Slater, who manages to elicit cooler climate properties from a warmer region. A wide array of herbs, including thyme, oregano, marjoram and sage, decorate the firmly structured hibiscus, red currant and cranberry backdrop. The acid goes deep into the finish.
title: Sarah's Vineyard 2015 Estate Pinot Noir (Santa Clara Valley)

description: This is a mouthwatering, succulent kabinett chock full of sweet-tart guava and white grapefruit flavors. It's silken and sweet yet elegantly spry and mineral. Delightful now but likely to hold well through 2026.
title: Schloss Saarstein 2016 Schloss Saarsteiner Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Chewy and intensely herbal, this bold, powerfully built wine is bittersweet in tobacco and cigar box elements. The fruit leans into dark cherry and cassis, with a dusting of cocoa powder seasoned seductively throughout.
title: Trinchero 2014 Mario's Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Made entirely with Chardonnay, this opens with aromas of yellow stone fruit, Spanish broom, toasted nut and a whiff of sage. The aromas follow over to the palate along with mature golden apple, bread crust, ginger and dried citrus zest alongside an elegant, persistent perlage. Fresh acidity lifts the dry finish.
title: Villa Crespia NV Dosaggio Zero  (Franciacorta)

description: Only the slightest hints of lemon and bruised apple reach the nose. The palate has the same combination of very lithe lemon overtones on an earthy, light palate with notes of bruised apple flesh. This is dry, fresh and light and very moreish in an unusual, earthy but authentic way.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 Trois Châteaux Auxerrois (Alsace)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Syrah continues to be a core strength among this winery's reds. Firm varietal flavors combine in a well-integrated wine, bursting with brambly blackberry, vanilla, coffee and black pepper highlights. The tannins are chewy yet ripe, showing a decent amount of power.
title: Ledger David 2014 Syrah (Rogue Valley)

description: This is a dense, dark and well-concentrated wine, with abundant notes of blackberry, boysenberry and cherry on the nose and mouth. Hints of pepper, licorice and char add depth and interest, featuring most prominently on the finish alongside a hint of fudgy brownie. The mouthfeel is bold, gripping and firmly textured. Drink now–2021.
title: Leeuwenkuil 2015 Grenache (Swartland)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: This is proving to be one of the more distinctive vineyards in the region, set in the De Luz hills to the west of the valley floor. Aromas of charred hickory and cherry wood meet with black cherry juice, roasted meats and campfire aromas on the nose. The palate shows licorice, hoisin, star anise and a slight gamy quality.
title: Leoness 2014 Alessandro Vineyard Syrah (Temecula Valley)

description: Medium-intense black cherry and brambly berry aromas set the stage for this lushly fruited red, with supporting tones of cocoa, tea leaf and tree bark. Clean and well-balanced, the medium-weight palate offers ample acidity and soft, accessible tannins. Drink now–2021.
title: Lismore 2015 Syrah (Cape South Coast)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Fleshy strawberry and cherry fruit carries some phenolic bite. The tannins have a slightly granular feel. The fruit is dappled with dried herbs and the finish threaded with smoke. This young winery is the project of second generation Beaux Frères founders Jared Etzel and Marc-André Roy.
title: Maison Roy 2015 Petite Incline Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: There is an intriguing floral streak on the otherwise subdued nose. The palate is dry and has a pithy, zesty and slightly bitter edge that gives structure and weight to this concentrated wine.
title: Maurice Schoech & Fils 2016 Vieilles Vigne Pinot Auxerrois (Alsace)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Dark gold in color, this is from a fantastic, famous vineyard site in the heart of the appellation. Confectionery aromas give it a welcoming richness and lush character that's hard to resist. Green olive, vanilla and pineapple shine brightly alongside caramel crème brûlée and toasted oak.
title: Merry Edwards 2014 Olivet Lane Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Rose petals and ripe, yellow Mirabelle plums create an inviting, appetizing nose. The palate is light, fruity and fresh, with just a dash of gentle, fruit-enhancing sweetness. This is a gentle, easy but aromatic wine, with an off-dry finish.
title: Meyer-Fonné 2016 Gentil White (Alsace)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Oodles of fruit flavor and a sense of sweetness give this bold and tannic wine a softer edge than some others. It overflows with blueberry and blackberry aromas, rich fruit flavors and touches of milk chocolate. It will be nice by a campfire or with a chocolate dessert.
title: Naggiar 2015 Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Sierra Foothills)

description: Aromas suggesting fruitcake, blue flower and dark spice lead the nose. It's taut and medium-bodied, offering dried cherry, cranberry, sage and clove set against tight, close-grained tannins. Drink 2020–2025.
title: Nino Negri 2015 Vigneto Fracia  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: While the nose on this well-aged Mencía is damp, earthy and almost nutty smelling, the palate is healthy, with biting tannins. Familiar black-fruit flavors are oaky but not overdone. It tastes saucy and slightly herbal on a racy, intense finish.
title: Pittacum 2011 Mencía (Bierzo)

description: This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc and Ciliegiolo opens with aromas of ripe raspberry, baking spice and a hint of violet. The juicy balanced palate doles out red currant, dried black cherry and star anise alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Podere Conca 2015 Agapanto  (Bolgheri)

description: Rather ripe notions of yellow Mirabelle plum join lemon and green pear notes on the nose. On the palate, a lemon note cuts through the slender, lithe but expressive body. The finish is whistle-clean, dry and very refreshing.
title: Preiss-Zimmer 2016 Réserve Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Dark colored and rich, this smooth wine is poised between sweet and dry. It offers good acidity at the same time as really ripe fruit and a generous texture. This wine, although it is ready to drink, could well age. Drink now or keep until 2020.
title: Quinta das Carvalhas 2013 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: This is an attractive, easy-going Pinot at an affordable price from winemaker Tim Knappstein. Ripe—very nearly confected—red cherry and strawberry aromas are made more appealing by whispers of tree bark, dried herbs and savory, meaty and smoky components. The medium-bodied palate offers even more bark and medicinal herb characters, finishing on a sappy, black-fruited note.
title: Riposte 2016 The Dagger Pinot Noir (Adelaide Hills)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Vibrant in aromas of spicy cherry, blackberry and wild mint, this Primitivo sees no time in oak, opting for aging in cement tanks. This translates to pure fruit expression on the palate, showing juicy red cherry and strawberry, with flecks of spice, earth and crushed stone adding nuance to the medium-length finish. Lithe in feel, but with proper tannic grip, this is a savory and enticing expression of the variety.
title: Rivera 2015 Triusco  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: Hints of crunchy apricot, apple and pear are juxtaposed by racy streaks of lemon-lime acidity. The palate is more mineral and earth than fruit but elegant and composed, finishing with reverberating lime-zest astringency.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Trocken Gutswein Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Aromas of toast and yellow apple lead the nose on this lively sparkler while the vibrant palate doles out ripe tangerine zest, mature yellow pear and a hint of bitter almond. Fresh acidity and a vivacious perlage buoy up the rich flavors.
title: Ronco Calino NV Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Brioche, bread crust and white stone-fruit aromas follow through to the bright palate along with yellow apple and citrus. Fresh acidity and a creamy perlage provide the backdrop while a white almond note signals the close.
title: Ronco Calino NV Satèn  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Subdued aromas of stewed prune, sunbaked earth and a whiff of dark baking spice follow over to the dense fleshy palate, together with hints of licorice and nutmeg. Velvety tannins give it a smooth mouthfeel. Drink sooner rather than later to enjoy the brooding fruit.
title: Salcheto 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: This 100% varietal wine is juicy and dusty in concentrated flavors of cinnamon stick and baked plum. Firm tannins support without distraction and give way to a soft lengthy texture of fruit-forward approachability.
title: Summers 2015 Estate Villa Andriana Charbono (Calistoga)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Quite impressive for the entry level Zin, this bottling by brewer-turned-winemaker Sherman Thacher shows black currant, loamy soil, vanilla and licorice on the nose. The palate is rounded with black-cherry flavors and minty herbs, proving a cohesive experience.
title: Thacher 2015 Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: Softly textured tannins surround a hefty full-bodied plushness of plummy cobbler, blueberry and dark chocolate in this well-made, robust and rich wine. Caramel and mocha appear on the lengthy finish.
title: Trentadue 2015 La Storia Petite Sirah (Alexander Valley)

description: Tomato leaf, dried herb and cedar are offered on the nose and palate of this wine, which is well-integrated in oak. Full-bodied and grippy, it's youthful in structure, with final notes of pencil shavings and focused acidity.
title: Whitehall Lane 2014 Leonardini Estate Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This Shiraz-based blend, with 21% Cinsault and 8% Grenache, offers earthy notes of fresh mint, white pepper and herbal fynbos, all spicing up the red cherry and raspberry fruit core. A delicate whiff of sweet smoke is seductive, while the palate offers more earthy notes of tilled earth and currant leaf that carry through to the finish. Fine but structured tannins lend a velvety texture. Drink now–2020.
title: A.A. Badenhorst Family Wines 2015 Secateurs Red (Swartland)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This bursts forth with spicy cranberry and cherry fruit. It's a bright vivacious wine, fruit-driven and lightly peppery. Thoughts of roast turkey inevitably come to mind—save a bottle for next Thanksgiving.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Nicholas Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very light in the glass, this blend of 43% Grenache, 36% Syrah and 21% Mourvèdre offers fairly simple yet likable berry aromas. There's a surprising amount of textural grip to the palate and loads of earthy minerality, with hibiscus, red plum and gravel flavors proving more complex than the nose implied.
title: Archium 2015 Dissident G-S-M (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Dark-skinned berry, forest floor, new oak and exotic spice aromas are front and center. The dense palate offers dried black cherry, cranberry, oak, vanilla and coconut framed in tightly knit, fine-grained tannins. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Avignonesi 2013 Grandi Annate  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit for this wine comes from Block Two of Gamache Vineyard. Aged in a mixture of stainless steel and French oak (30% new), the aromas offer notes of hay, corn silk, lees and cut yellow apple along with peach. Flavors are broad and leesy, gliding on the finish.
title: Barons 2016 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This northern Médoc estate has produced a wine that has both structure and fruit. The tannins are certainly present, giving a burnt character from the wood aging. However, black currant flavors are there as well. Put the two together as they integrate, and the wine will be juicy and attractive. Drink from 2020.
title: Château Noaillac 2015  Médoc

description: Pure, rich ripe Touriga Nacional gives a perfumed wine, rich in tannins and with a stylish character from the bright red fruits. The wine is juicy, balanced, and certain to age well. Drink from 2019.
title: Christie's 2015 Quinta dona Mafalda Touriga Nacional (Douro)

description: Dense soil aromas, leather, olive, charred beef, roasted wood and crushed asphalt make for a very dry and savory nose on this bottling. There's a tarry character to the palate that shadows the black fruit, allowing the black olive and black pepper to take the lead.
title: Donati Family Vineyard 2014 Ezio Cabernet Sauvignon (Paicines)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas seem unsettled, with notes of dust, herb and cherry. The flavors are plush and ripe, delivering plenty of enjoyment.
title: Eternal 2015 Eternal Gratitude Gamache Vineyard Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This full, fleshy and affordable wine gets treated to small-lot ferments in neutral oak barrels. The fruit seems young and fresh—Gamay-like, with bright and spicy berry flavors. Drink this over the next couple of years while that freshness remains in full force.
title: Eugene Wine Cellars 2016 b2 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Breezy and fresh, this light-footed Riesling offers a crush of pristine white grapefruit, apple and nectarine flavors. Delicately sweet in style, it's refreshing and quaffable, with a delicately mineral finish. Drink now.
title: Familie Steffen 2016 Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Made with organically farmed Vermentino grapes, this offers aromas of nectarine, yellow peach and a whiff of Mediterranean scrub. The aromas carry over to the crisp palate alongside firm acidity.
title: Fattoria di Magliano 2016 Pagliatura Vermentino (Maremma Toscana)

description: This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Syrah opens with aromas of blackberry, ginger and sandalwood. The medium-bodied palate has black-currant jam, cedar and vanilla alongside smooth tannins.
title: Guicciardini Strozzi 2015 Campo al Capriolo  (Bolgheri)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Though it's a bit astringent, the medium-bodied fruit is clean and juicy, with raspberry and cherry flavors. It seems to spread out across the palate, then winds down gracefully as it flattens into a drying astringent finish.
title: Hawkins Cellars 2015 Enigma Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This tasty little Pinot introduces a well-made lineup from this producer. The pretty cherry fruit might seem delicate, but it's nonetheless persuasive. There's no thinness here; it's spot-on varietal and balanced throughout.
title: Holloran 2015 Stafford Hill Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This wine exhibits hearty oak and ripe black cherry, cranberry and plum. Blended with 15% Cabernet Sauvignon, its grapes from Coombsville primarily, it's high-strung in supportive acidity and dried herb and tobacco.
title: Ilaria 2014 Malbec (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This three-acre site, planted to Dijon 114 Pinot, comes up with strong aromatics loaded with baking spices. Those sweet flavors continue around standard medium-ripe berry fruit. The acids finish a bit chalky, and the effect is to dry out the flavors a bit too quickly.
title: J. Christopher 2015 En Gedi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: This Cabernet Sauvignon expresses classic aromas and flavors of blackberry leaf, cassis, bramble, black pepper and eucalyptus. There is lovely oak integration that adds notes of clove and cedar. The tannic structure soft which make the finish feel shorter and more fruit forward.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2015 Estate Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Monticello)

description: This wine carries pronounced tones of black currant, cassis, bramble, juniper and oak spice. The tannins are full but well integrated and hold up to the weight of this wine. The finish is long and woody.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2015 Estate Reserve Heritage Red (Monticello)

description: Notes of very ripe pear dominate the nose. The palate shows more freshness to aid the spicy pear fruit on the midpalate. This is dry and fresh, with a slightly lemony finish. Drink soon.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Vibrant and fun, this opens with zesty aromas of tangerine zest, white wildflower, white cherry and apple. The crisp savory palate offers Gala apple, crushed herb and orange slice. Tangy acidity and a vivacious perlage provide the backdrop.
title: Lantieri NV Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: A blend of Negroamaro and Malvasia Nera, this is red-fruit driven on the nose, with shades of tobacco and rubber. The palate brings a bit more depth in flavors of blackberry, cranberry and leather, yet ultimately this is light in feel from racy acidity and soft, sandy tannins. A slightly bitter dusting of cocoa powder marks the close.
title: Leone de Castris 2014 Donna Lisa Red (Salice Salentino)

description: A rather neutral nose offers glimpses of pear and lemon. The palate is also reticent but the pear fruit it shows is fresh, clean and edged with zesty lemon. This is light, dry and fresh.
title: Lucien Albrecht 2016 Cuvée Balthazar Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Tropical fruit and apple aromas are ripe and nice. This wine is fruity but imprecise on the tongue, with bold orange and apple flavors that are juicy, bright and fairly potent. Drink now.
title: Mar de Viñas 2016 Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: This biodynamic grower and producer makes distinctive wines that require a bit of a palate adjustment for some tasters. Here, there are some funky earthy aromas suggesting compost and barnyard, with flavors following. There is little of the fresh bright fruit flavors one might associate more commonly with Oregon Pinots, but plenty of complexity, length and a light touch of new French oak.
title: Maysara 2014 Jamsheed Pinot Noir (McMinnville)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Lovely spicy fruit stands out here and sets this apart from many of the winery's offerings. Concentrated ripe cranberry and plum flavors come with some bite to them, in a good way. The supporting acids have a citrus character, and the clean fruit continues on through a satisfying finish.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2015 Gamay Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of truffle, crushed herb, celery and wild berry slowly take shape on this. The medium-bodied elegant palate offers red cherry and baking spice notes alongside polished tannins and fresh acidity. Drink through 2019.
title: Metinella di Stefano Sorlini 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: The Baga grape, a specialty of the Bairrada region, is traditionally known for its tannins. Here, in the hands of Dirk Niepoort from the Douro, it reveals a rounded, smoother character. The tannins are there although in a supporting role for the black fruits. Crisp, light and juicy, it's ready to drink from 2020
title: Niepoort 2015 Poeirinho Baga (Bairrada)

description: A rather closed nose hints at lemon. The palate has lemon freshness but lacks a little focus. Nonetheless this is fresh and clean, with an appetizing citrus note on the dry finish.
title: Paul Blanck 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and 15% Petit Verdot, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, green olive and a whiff of pencil shaving. The straightforward palate offers black-currant jam, vanilla and a whiff of tobacco alongside round tannins.
title: Pepi Lignana 2013 Il Cucchetto  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Orange and other citrus aromas are a touch creamy and yeasty. A medium-bodied palate is average in feel, while touches of spice and smoke accent mild orange flavors. The finish is simple.
title: Perelada NV Torre Galatea Brut Rosé Sparkling (Cava)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot and Cabernet Franc, this has delicate aromas of black plum and baking spice. The dense palate offers prune, espresso and cedar alongside close-grained, rather astringent tannins.
title: Podere Prospero 2015  Bolgheri

description: Made entirely with Alicante, this presents aromas suggesting red-skinned berry, white pepper and leather. The easygoing palate offers cranberry, clove and exotic spice alongside smooth tannins. Enjoy soon.
title: Poggio al Tufo 2015  Maremma Toscana

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is Riesling for acid lovers; it screams out for freshly shucked oysters. Bone dry, quite tart, it's right on the edge of sour, with freshly squeezed lemon flavors in abundance. When matched to the right food, it should be a home run.
title: Ram 2016 Riesling (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
Designation: Lone Madrone Vineyard Three Wives Giulio
description: There's a strong flavor of grilled fruits, especially peaches, and the red fruits are tangy and concentrated, like raspberry compote. A charred smoky streak runs through the tannins. All in all, though a bit heavy handed, this brings lots of potent flavors. Drink now through 2020.
title: Remy 2015 Lone Madrone Vineyard Three Wives Giulio Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Gentle notes of red apple appear on the nose. The palate has the same apple fruit that combines tart freshness with ripe fruit. This is easy and fresh, with a slight floral streak on its slender, dry body.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2016 Maru White (Alsace)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A fine value, this widely available Pinot doesn't shy away from its natural herbaceousness, but compensates with plenty of bright berry-cherry fruit. The compact tannins support a firm blackberry and black cherry core, and flavors persist on through a clean crisp finish.
title: Duck Pond 2016 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This appears to be the top cuvée, at least by price, among the winery's 2015s. Cherry fruit comes through in a solid full-flavored core, leading into tannins that are still stiff and astringent. Give this extra breathing time, or a couple more years in the cellar.
title: Erath 2015 Willakia Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: Made entirely with Syrah, this opens with aromas suggesting black-skinned berry, underbrush and cooking spice. The firm palate offers blackberry, licorice and bay leaf alongside close-grained tannins.
title: Fattoria di Magliano 2013 Perenzo  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This is a spicy aromatic wine, with potent aromas of vanilla and black tea. The red fruits are compressed, and there is a big streak of coffee liqueur running across the palate. It's got plenty of flavor, though what is grape and what is barrel remains a bit of a puzzle.
title: Grizzly Peak 2014 Tempranillo (Rogue Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The blend is principally Pommard, focusing on ripe berry-fruit flavors, along with streaks of citrus. The barrel aging adds a light vein of dark chocolate.
title: Gypsy Dancer 2014 Christine's Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Fresh, ripe apple, lemon and pear are on full display on the open nose. The palate balances this abundance with a pleasantly bitter, tangy note. This is dry, slender and refreshing, with a layered, stony core.
title: Hugel 2016 Gentil White (Alsace)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This full and fruity wine offers generous flavors of tangy apple and peach. Though technically dry at 5 g/l of residual sugar, it hints at a light dusting of sweet powder, which helps to cut down on the acidity. In any event, it drinks quite well and is ready for near-term enjoyment.
title: I/G 2016 Riesling (Rogue Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This cuvée gets off to a good start with rich scents and flavors of strawberry jam, along with ripe plum and cherry highlights. There's a fairly strong herbal component, with a stemmy note that comes out more strongly in the finish as it fades away.
title: J. Christopher 2015 Charlie's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This wine offers a bouquet of black currant, cherry and black olive. The tannins come on strong but never overpower the rich flavors of black cherry, blackberry, thyme and anise. Strawberry and eucalyptus tones show on finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2014 4990 Reserve Petite Sirah (Judean Hills)

description: Aromas of dark plum and pomegranate lead into flavors of wood, blueberry, vanilla and anise. This wine is full in the mouth, with softly lingering tannins and a bright finish of baking spices.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2016 2900 Cabernet Sauvignon (Judean Hills)

description: Smoke and crushed slate juxtapose punchy yellow peach and nectarine in this crisp, cutting spätlese. Delicately honeyed yet invigoratingly fresh, it finishes on a lip-smacking tangerine tone. Drink now through 2020.
title: Karl Josef 2016 Piesporter Michelsberg Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Toast, oak, bread crust and graphite aromas lead the way. It's vibrant but rather lean, offering roasted nut, coffee, dried apple slice and a note of lemon rind before a dry finish. Fresh acidity and small beading lend elegance.
title: La Montina 2007 Baiana Pas Dosé Riserva  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Blackberry jam, tangy plum skins, pepper and sagebrush show on the nose of this blend of 60% Grenache and 40% Syrah. The palate reveals tart hibiscus, cranberry and the slightest dusting of dried herbs, all presented with decent acidic energy.
title: Lion Ranch 2015 Two Lions Red (Central Coast)

description: Soft, toasty notes of apple skin, creamed melon and orange peel greet the nose and carry through to the medium-weight palate, where they're met with additional hints of melon rind and baking spice. A pithy texture lends length to the close. Drink now.
title: Lismore 2015 Viognier (Greyton)

description: Bread crust, acacia honey and dried herb aromas lead the nose. The vibrant palate offers up dried apricot, mature red apple, candied citrus slice and a hint of toasted nut alongside lively beading.
title: Majolini NV Blanc de Noir Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This is a hearty version of the variety, blended with 12% Cabernet Sauvignon and 2% Petite Sirah. Black licorice, Dr. Pepper and densely rich, extracted black cherry and blueberry accent a full-bodied softly textured palate of approachability.
title: Markham 2015 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: Aromas suggesting white stone fruit, citrus blossom and bread crust waft out of the glass. On the lively palate, a vigorous perlage and bright acidity underscore yellow apple, citrus zest and chopped herb. A note of bitter almond marks the close.
title: Monogram NV Blanc de Blancs Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: A savory touch of undergrowth blends in with the tart, fresh cherry scent on the nose. The same savory and fruity combination dominates the palate. This offers tart cherry fruit, a fine crunch tannins and ample, bright acidity on the light body.
title: Preiss-Zimmer 2016 Réserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This Cabernet Franc-led blend, with 36% Malbec, 9% Cabernet Sauvignon, 5% Merlot and 2% Petit Verdot, opens with seductive notes of dried cherry, cocoa-dusted raspberry, vanilla bean and sweet oak. There's ample tannic structure, though they are quite fleeting, allowing the vibrant acidity and bright red-fruit flavors to shine through. Drink now–2021.
title: Raats Family 2015 Red Jasper Red (Stellenbosch)

description: Here's a 100% Sangiovese that offers aromas of blueberry, violet and a hint of thyme. The lively palate evokes red cherry, cinnamon and a balsamic note alongside smooth tannins. It's made to be enjoyed young so drink soon.
title: Rocca di Montemassi 2016 Le Focaie  (Maremma Toscana)

description: This is a light, appetizing style of a sometimes heavy varietal. Unique citrus and pine aromas lead to juicy, fresh and tangy flavors that range from lemon to honeysuckle. On the palate it's crisp and clean, lightly fruity and refreshing.
title: Steele 2016 Viognier (Lake County)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pineapple, candied mango and melon show on the plush, nearly tropical nose of this bottling. That ripeness dissipates a bit on the sip, where poached apple and pear flesh flavors rule.
title: Tablas Creek 2016 Grenache Blanc (Adelaida District)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Succulent and fresh, this puts out clean crisp flavors of lemon-lime, grapefruit and a touch of white peach. It's nicely supported with ample juicy acidity. Put a slight chill on it and drink now through 2020.
title: The Four Graces 2016 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Aromas of citrus, sea spray and toast ride over a riper core of melon on the nose. This is expressive in fruit intensity and weight, offering a silken palate of juicy melon and citrus flavors, balanced by an integrated vein of acidity. A lightly charred lemon note lingers on the finish.
title: Tormaresca 2016 Pietrabianca Chardonnay (Castel del Monte)

description: A blend of 90% Vermentino and 10% Viognier, this opens with aromas of beeswax, acacia flower and citrus. The juicy palate offers up apricot, yellow peach and dried herb alongside bright acidity.
title: Villa Pinciana 2016 Airali White (Maremma Toscana)

description: Only slight hints of ripe pear reach the nose. The palate offers more fruit and the textural element of pear peel. This is fresh clean and has a lovely mouthfeel. The finish is pristine.
title: Wolfberger 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Melony aromas are leesy and a touch rich and flat. That's also how the palate comes across. Inexact nectarine flavors are plump and turn slightly bitter and yeasty on the finish. It's best to drink this right away.
title: Adegas Gran Vinum 2016 Selección Especial Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: The nose offers a sense of freshness and the palate follows suit, with clean outlines of Conference pear and lemon. There is moderate concentration and a juicy, refreshing finish that is dry and lasting.
title: Boeckel 2016 Midelberg Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: This is currently closed and shy, offering subtle scents of yellow apple, musk melon, white peach and toasted spice. The round, mouthfilling palate boasts more concentration and decadence, with rich flavors of melon, clove-spiked orange and crusty brioche. Try 2019–2023.
title: Boekenhoutskloof 2014 Semillon (Franschhoek)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This winemaker's selection is from a warm ripe vintage, yet it remains rather light. The fruit touches on pomegranate and raspberry, and a whiff of ground coffee suggests some time in a percentage of new or once-used oak. It's light on the palate and stiffly herbal through the finish.
title: Winter's Hill 2014 Cuvée du Vigneron Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The winery makes a number of block and clone cuvées, along with regular and reserve Pinots. It would perhaps differentiate them as well as showcase them more distinctively to focus on blending fewer, more complete wines. This reserve has the same high acid, herbal, earth and stem character as the regular bottling.
title: Winter's Hill 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Just a touch of fresh apple appears on the almost mute nose. The palate follows with easy, clean apple and ripe pear notes on a slender, light body. The finish is dry and refreshing. Drink soon.
title: Wolfberger 2016 Edelzwicker White (Alsace)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This very sweet, 210g/l dessert wine was produced cryogenically. It's peachy and syrupy, with candied apricots and a touch of maple syrup. There's just enough acidity to cut some of the treacle, but the sweetness has a sugary granularity rather than a smooth, integrated mix of flavors.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Nicholas Vineyard Gewürztraminer (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: Baked licorice leads the nose on this blend of 74% Merlot and 26% Cabernet Franc, along with thyme and stewed elderberries. The mouthfeel is lighter bodied, carrying flavors of exotic marjoram and red currant.
title: Avensole 2013 Sole Serenade Red (Temecula Valley)

description: The nose is closed and does not want to give too much away. The palate, however, has lovely lemon and fresh pear fruit on a rather light, dry body. This is fresh, easy and fairly neutral, but clean and fresh. Drink soon.
title: Bestheim 2016 Anne de K Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit-forward aromas of black currant and plum lead to plump, easy-drinking fruit flavors. It's straightforward, though it seems to lack concentration, especially in the middle.
title: Boomtown 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A neutral nose offers a quiet, lemony streak. The palate is equally neutral, almost dilute, but fresh and clean. Some apple and pear notes appear on the refreshing finish. Drink soon.
title: Bott Frères 2016 Edelzwicker White (Alsace)

description: Nothing but the slightest hint of lemon appears on the closed nose. The palate is clean and fresh, just hinting at green pear. Drink soon.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: You'll find aromas of toast, nut and a whiff of adhesive bandage on this. The aromas carry over the rather lean, somewhat muddled palate along with dried cherry, a hint of lead pencil, powdered sage and a confectionary note alongside steely bubbles.
title: Bèlon du Bèlon 2013 Instinct Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Toasted nut, medicinal herb and bread crust aromas follow through to the lean palate along with yellow-apple skin, sage and bitter almond. Fresh acidity and a vigorous perlage provide the backdrop.
title: Bèlon du Bèlon 2013 Satèn Party  (Franciacorta)

description: Raisin and prune aromas announce a chunky palate that's solid but not agile or elegant. Flavors of prune and cassis come with a sense of grape skins. It finishes well.
title: Bodegas Valdemar 2013 Conde Valdemar Crianza  (Rioja)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of red currant are bright and fresh, with little apparent new-oak influence. Raspberry and currant flavors bring more of the same. It's a straightforward, easy drinker.
title: Boomtown 2015 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is a good, solidly built table wine with a robust texture and bold fruit and dark-spice flavors. It barrels straight ahead in style and will easily wash down some Korean barbecue or steak fajitas.
title: C.G. di Arie 2014 Mixed Breed Estate Grown Red (Sierra Foothills)

description: Lettuce and white-fruit aromas lead to a plump palate with modest acidity. Yeasty melon and powdery apple flavors dry out on the finish, leaving a note of tropical papaya.
title: Casa de Valor NV Brut Sparkling (Cava)

description: Slightly stalky aromas are not the cleanest. In the mouth, this has lime-like acidity and yeasty white-fruit flavors that include mild green notes. A chalky finish closes out this basic Cava.
title: Castellví de la Marca NV Santa Monica Brut Sparkling (Cava)

description: Hints of fresh, green pear and a touch of lemon appear on the nose. The palate offers more of the same on a dry, light body which lacks a little focus. Drink soon.
title: Cave de Ribeauvillé 2016 Collection Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Rich, probably too rich, this is a smooth, concentrated wine. Its tannins are already embedded in the cushion of superripe fruit offering flavors of black plums and dark chocolate. The wine will age quickly; drink from 2019.
title: Château Bel Air Gallier 2015  Graves

description: Subdued notes of lemon and herb on the nose extend to the palate of this off-dry, slightly earthen Riesling. Pressed apple and pear flavors are moderately concentrated but pleasantly clingy on the finish. Drink now.
title: Dachshund 2016 Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: Ripe, sweet yellow peach and tangerine flavors are shaded by darker tones of smoke and sea water in this slightly rustic, savory kabinett. It's well balanced by acidity and the finish is refreshing and crisp.
title: Dr. Pauly Bergweiler 2016 Wehlener Sonnenuhr Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: This wine is ripe, soft, rich and smooth. Its tannins are totally integrated into the generous damson fruit. Lightly textured and with balanced acidity, the wine is ready to drink.
title: José Maria da Fonseca 2016 Ripanço Private Selection Red (Alentejano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Underbrush, dried herb and toasted aromas lift out of the glass while the no-frills palate evokes wild cherry, ground clove and a hint of coffee bean. Fleeting tannins give loose support. Drink soon.
title: La Ciarliana 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Fleshy and simple, this is packed with flavors of strawberries. It's fresh and forward, with a ripe core of fruit. Don't look for complexity here, just enjoy it for its lush strawberry goodness.
title: Melrose 2015 Baco Noir (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Intriguing aromas of dark pomegranate, white pepper, crushed gravel and herb meet with a touch of tar on the nose of this appellation blend. The mouthfeel is heavy and the density impresses, but the flavors of rich cranberry and tart orange rind are a bit muddled and sour.
title: Mignanelli 2014 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: Aromas of toasted walnut, yellow stone fruit and candied citrus zest follow through to the vigorous palate along with bitter yellow-apple skin and a hint of medicinal herb. Steely bubbles provide a rather aggressive perlage.
title: Monte Rossa 2012 Cabuchon Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: A hint of smoked nuts lends a savory tone to flavors of pressed apple and pear. While delicate in concentration, it's dry and approachable, with refreshing lemon-lime acidity. Drink now.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Pinot Blanc (Rheinhessen)

description: Slighty muted tones of strawberry and red apple are glazed with caramel in this slightly off-dry blanc de noir, a Pinot Noir vinfied like a white wine. Light bodied and soft in acidity, it's an easy, approachable wine to drink now.
title: Paul Anheuser 2016 Blanc de Noir Pinot Noir (Nahe)

description: A ripe, rich, perfumed nose of butter, citrus, melon and yeast lead into a palate that feels a little disjointed, with a creamy, weighty texture yet biting acidity and very little fruit.
title: Penley Estate 2016 Aradia Chardonnay (Coonawarra)

description: Made with 70% Sangiovese and 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, this has subdued aromas of red plum and baking spice. The aromas carry over to the simple palate together with a hint of vanilla. Round tannins lend easy support.
title: Poggio d'Elsa 2016  Maremma Toscana

description: This wine, with its earthy aroma and light oak aging, is made for early drinking. Its red fruits and acidity are generous, rounded with the warmth of the Alentejo in its texture. The wine can be drunk now.
title: Ribafreixo 2014 Pato Frio Red Edition Red (Alentejano)

description: Made with 100% Nebbiolo this has fresh aromas of wild red berry, violet and a whiff of star anise. The racy linear palate delivers pomegranate, sour cherry and dried alpine herb alongside vibrant acidity and taut, polished tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Nera 2013 Inferno Efesto  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Vibrant and linear, this opens with aromas of French oak, exotic spice and a whiff of wild rose. The palate has a nervous tension, offering oak-driven spice, sage and cranberry set against racy acidity and fine-grained tannins.
title: Nino Negri 2014 Le Tense  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Equal parts of peach pit, petrol and oak aromas drive this Viura towards a round, full palate. Nutty flavors of popcorn and dry fruits are modest but elevated, while this tastes similarly of stone fruits, salt and oak on the finish.
title: Palacios Remondo 2014 Placet Viura (Rioja)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Planted in 1997, this is the oldest estate vineyard. In this vintage it crafts an almost delicate wine, with a mix of dried herbs, mountain berry and earthy tannins. There's an overall sense of austerity, though it remains well balanced throughout.
title: Patton Valley 2014 10 Acre Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This block is singled out in order to present a wine that has received full western exposure throughout the growing season. Using a basket press and native yeast fermentation, it's a well-defined effort with ripe cherry fruit and a streak of cola running down the spine. Medium bodied and full in the midpalate, it fades gently, though just a bit sooner than you'd like.
title: Patton Valley 2014 West Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Black cherry, tarragon, toasted coconut and anise show on the nose of this blend of 26% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Cabernet Franc, 24% Merlot, 16% Petit Verdot and 9% Malbec. The dark palate offers black currant, black plum, cocoa, light mint and crushed gravel on the finish. It's well structured.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Distraction Red (Paso Robles)

description: Just a hint of rose petal blends in with lemon on the nose. The palate shows the same flavors but lacks a little direction. Nonetheless, there is freshness in the rose and lemon flavors. Drink soon.
title: Pierre Sparr 2016 One White (Alsace)

description: Made with 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Merlot and 15% Petit Verdot, this opens with aromas of black-skinned fruit, tobacco and a whiff of green peppercorn. It's concentrated, offering black-currant jam, mocha and vanilla flavors alongside assertive tannins.
title: Podere Sapaio 2015 Volpolo  (Bolgheri)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: As ode to his family's butchering past, this red blend would go very well with one of the fatty steaks that winemaker Adam Comartin's relatives once cut. Aromas of licorice, sandalwood, lilac and jammy fruits lead into a palate loaded with clove, nutmeg, thyme and pepper spice, all set against a backdrop of boysenberry fruit.
title: Porterhouse Winery 2014 Williams' Reserve Red (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Made with 80% Sangiovese, 10% Merlot and 10% Syrah, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, leather and tobacco. The smooth, savory palate offers Morello cherry, star anise and licorice alongside polished tannins.
title: Rocca di Montemassi 2015 Sassabruna  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Spicy, briary berry aromas are subtle and reserved, but sound. This wine is firm and a bit drawing on the palate, with flavors of plum, berry fruits and cocoa. Mild berry flavors on the finish are tasty and not pushy or overdone.
title: Valdelana 2014 Agnus Crianza  (Rioja)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The winery makes a trio of Pinot Gris, this one fermented entirely in stainless steel. It's clean and fresh, mountain spring over rock, with green apple and jicama fruit. There's a tension to it, and it's well-defined and taut through the finish.
title: Winter's Hill 2016 Pinot Gris (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: The Tempranillos from this pioneering estate winery bring deep dense purple fruits, along with very potent flavors of vanilla, black tea and coffee liqueur. Tannic and earthy, this has a somewhat grainy mouthfeel. It's packed with flavor, but with less emphasis on pure fruit and more on the vanilla-laced tannins.
title: Abacela 2014 Barrel Select Tempranillo (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Despite its remarkably low alcohol, this well-made wine broadly displays flavors of nectarine, pie cherry and chocolate. As expected, there is ample acidity, but not too much to turn the whole thing sour. Just two barrels were produced, one new and one neutral.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 Wadenswil Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Inviting aromas of wild dark-skinned berry, chopped mint and forest floor unfold in the glass. The enjoyable palate offers juicy Marasca cherry, white pepper and a hint of salty mineral alongside polished tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Antico Colle 2015  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Made from Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, this evokes aromas of spiced blueberry, baking spice and a hint of mocha. The savory palate offers black-currant jam, licorice and a touch of cocoa alongside polished tannins.
title: Banfi 2014 Aska  (Bolgheri)

description: Aromas of toast and dried apricot carry over to the linear palate along with candied tangerine zest and a note of bitter walnut. Lively bubbles provide the backdrop.
title: Bellavista 2013 Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Savory aromas of medicinal oak combined with spicy, minty berry and cherry scents make for a good opening. A fairly full palate is balanced by healthy acidity, while this wine tastes of plum, berry fruits, vanilla and tobacco. A solid finish with nothing extreme is the final seal.
title: Bodega Classica 2014 Hacienda Lopez de Haro Crianza  (Rioja)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Rounded red-cherry fruit meets with aromas of potpourri and caramelized oak on the nose of this fairly priced bottling. The oak is very prominent on the palate, lending vanilla and hickory flavors to the red currant and clove base. Acidity washes into the finish.
title: Cambiata 2016 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Here's a Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend with aromas of ripe red currant, cedar and Mediterranean herb. On the round palate, pliant tannins softly support blueberry jam, tobacco and vanilla.
title: Campo alla Sughera 2015 Adeo  (Bolgheri)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Oak, cassis, prune and forest-floor aromas follow through to the palate along with dried cherry, powdered sage and espresso. Tightly knit astringent tannins clench the finish. Give the tannins several years to unwind then drink sooner rather than later to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Carpineto 2010 Vigneto Sant'Ercolano  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Gentle but clean notes of green and yellow pear create an appealing nose. The palate presents the same tones, assisted by lemon brighteness. This is a vivid, fresh and easy wine, with a dry, slender body.
title: Cave de Cleebourg 2016 Prestige Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A rather heady nose of red cherry rises from the glass alongside a note of oak. The palate is fluid and slender, with lovely freshness that frames the heightened tones. The tannins are firm and the finish is refreshing. This needs some time to knit together. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Charles Sparr 2016 Collection Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Tart red cherry freshness and a hint of conifer play gently on the nose. The palate brings both these tones to the fore. This is a light-bodied, elegant Pinot Noir for those who like it on the lighter side.
title: Charles Sparr 2016 Tradition Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This ripe wine with red-berry aromas and firm structure is dominated by its 75% Merlot. It is ripe with jammy fruits and a core of tannins. Wood aging has added another attractive dimension to this rich wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Château de Lavagnac 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: From one of the properties owned by the Ducourt family, this wine is structured and firm. It also has juicy fruit, rich tannins and plenty of crisp acidity, with a touch of spice from wood aging. With its firm tannins, it needs to age, so drink from 2021.
title: Château Jacques Noir 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: Situated in the north of the Haut-Médoc, close to Saint-Estèphe, this vineyard has been owned by the Fabre family since 1982. This vintage is rich, with an interesting proportion of Petit Verdot in the blend to give color and structure. It's ripe with black-currant and berry fruits, with a firm aftertaste. Drink from 2022.
title: Château la Tonnelle 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine, with its exuberant black fruits, is very young. But it is already deliciously fruity, with a backdrop of tannins. The brightness, rich texture, poised acidity and black-currant flavors promise a good future. Drink from 2020.
title: Château le Chêne 2016  Haut-Médoc

description: This property, now owned by the Antoine Moeuix negociant firm, has made a rich wine with ripe tannins that support the generous black-currant fruits. The structure is dense although there is clear light through the tannins to the fruit. It needs to age further, so drink from 2021.
title: Château Liversan 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine is firm, with a dry core that will take time to soften. But that will happen, because of the ripe black-currant fruit that lies underneath the tannic structure, giving a juicy potential to this rich wine. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Lousteauneuf 2015  Médoc

description: This wine shows a heady, toasty nose of black-fruit aromas, along with maple, spice and heat notes. Spiced blackberry and cassis flavors are typical of this Tinta de Toro, however a palate that's rough as sandpaper makes this a tough go. Drink through 2025.
title: Maurodos 2014 San Román  (Toro)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Smooth and toasty, this cuvée pushes pretty barrel flavors front and center, well-defined and framing crisp fruit flavors of raspberry and cherry. There's a dash of orange marmalade that seals the finish. The wine spent 16 months in barrel, 40% new and 60% once-used.
title: Megan Anne 2015 Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: This medium-bodied Pinot offers aromas of tree bark, vanilla, dried leaves, cherry cola and a hint of licorice. The palate is supersmooth, with silky tannins and a tart cherry finish.
title: Ninth Island 2015 Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

description: A blend of 51% Petit Verdot, 42% Cabernet Sauvignon and 7% Carmenère, this opens with floral tones of crushed violets and carnation alongside brambly berry, chocolate-covered cherry and sweet spice. The plush, velvety palate showcases a solid tannic structure and a fruity berry-preserve flavor, while final notes of tree bark, wood spice and cocoa powder linger long on the close. Drink now–2023.
title: Paserene 2015 Marathon Red (Western Cape)

description: Lightly wood aged, this ripe wine was fermented initially in open tanks, giving the wine its rich black-fruit character and open texture. Spice from wood aging mixes with generous fruits to make a richly textured wine that will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Quinta de Chocapalha 2013 Red (Lisboa)

description: This is a ripe wine full of broad black fruits. Smoothed by the wood aging and with plenty of acidity to balance, it is rich and structured. The tannins in this wine from the de la Rosa estate are already enmeshed in the fruit. This wine could be drunk now for its fruit, although it will be better from 2019.
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2016 Red (Douro)

description: This is a well-balanced and attractive pour, with scents of orange blossom, lemon verbena, quinine and lime that frame the just-ripe peach and melon fruit core. A pithy astringency provides a subtle texture to the palate and lends some lingering grip to the finish, coupled with a vibrant acidity that leaves you read for more. Drink now–2022.
title: Quinta Essentia 2015 Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: While subdued on the nose, there's a deep concentration of sweet-tart tangerine and grapefruit flavors on the palate of this lushly textured Riesling. It's bold in mouthfeel for a kabinett but zesty acidity and a flinty mineral undertone keeps it invigorating.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Graach Josephshöfer Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Subdued red apple and lemon on the nose ripen into sweeter, honeyed tones of mango, peach and nectarine on the palate. Surprisingly lavish and deeply concentrated, it's an unctuously fruity yet elegantly balanced wine, with a vivacious lemon-lime finish.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Graach Josephshöfer Spätlese Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Scintillating acidity highlights crisp yellow peach and apple in this racy, light-footed kabinett. It's a delicately honeyed but penetrating wine that's approachable now but concentrated enough to hold well through 2025.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Ockfener Bockstein Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Reflecting a warm, dry vintage, this wine smells ripe and balsamic, with notes of cheese blending with raw wood, cowhide, coconut and cassis aromas. A bipolar palate features creamy oak and lemony acidity. Deep berry-fruit flavors are pushed by perky acidity towards a finish with toast and chocolate notes. Drink from 2019–2030.
title: Resalte 2012 Expresion  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: A blend of 70% Nero di Troia and 30% Montepulciano, this is an enticingly savory and earthy wine with lots of appeal. Aromas of spiced red cherry, cured meat, peppery tobacco and anise meld seamlessly on the nose. The aromas are echoed on the mouthwatering and engaging palate of medium weight, lingering on a sanguine note. Currently grippy and structured with grainy tannins, this will soften with time in bottle, giving way to its wealth of complexities. Enjoy from 2020.
title: Rivera 2012 Il Falcone Riserva Red (Castel del Monte)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Here's a savory red with surprising depth for the cool wet vintage. Dark wild cherry, licorice, violet, juicy plum, tilled earth and truffle aromas all follow through to the buffed palate. Fresh acidity and supple polished tannins lend brightness and give it a smooth, rounded texture.
title: Romeo 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Red berry, bread dough and toasted nut aromas emerge on this elegant, structured sparkler. On the palate, bright acidity underscores Rennet apple, pomegranate and nectarine alongside a refined perlage.
title: Ronco Calino NV Radijan Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: A gorgeous bouquet of apple skin, grilled pineapple, beeswax and a touch of baking spice sets the stage in this easy-to-like wine. The medium-weight body offers a superb balance of richness and freshness, with ripe, spicy fruit tones that harmonize with bright, lingering acidity, resulting in a fine overall texture. Drink now–2021.
title: Rustenberg 2015 Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: Pommard and Wädenswil are the clones, and the wine spent 18 months in roughly two-thirds new French oak. It's luscious and loaded with blueberry, cherry and chocolate flavors, gliding into a finish with moderate tannins, a whiff of coffee and a streak of caramel.
title: Saffron Fields Vineyard 2015 Heritage Clones Pinot Noir

description: Earthy plum and berry aromas open this firm gran reserva from a cold vintage. This wine is chunky on the palate, with scraping tannins. Rooty, meaty berry and cinnamon flavors are slightly stewed, while this is long but hollow on the finish. Drink through 2026.
title: Sierra Cantabria 2008 Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

Designation: Nik Weis Goldtröpfchen Kabinett Grosse Lage
description: While sunny tangerine and yellow peach flavors abound, deeply embedded layers of smoke, struck flint, fennel and pollen lend savory complexities to this kabinett. It's luscious yet spry, with racy streaks of acidity and a lingering, spicy finish. Drinks well already but should improve through 2025.
title: St. Urbans-Hof 2016 Nik Weis Goldtröpfchen Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: The debut of Napa native winemaker and entrepreneur Josh Phelps' own label, this is a blend of 73% Cabernet Sauvignon, 8% Malbec, 7% Cabernet Franc, 6% Merlot and 6% Petit Verdot. It delivers tremendous value for the quality, a mix of chocolate-covered cherry, blueberry and vanilla cola wrapped in smooth, seductive layers of robust elegance. Tobacco, pencil shavings and cigar box add alluring seasoning.
title: Steady State 2015 Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Gioia del Colle, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Spicy bramble berry aromas are lifted and pure on the nose of this wine, riding over an earthy core of pressed violets and Mediterranean scrub. There's energy to the moderately full palate, with vibrant acidity cutting through juicy red and black berry flavors. Orange rind and tense granite tones provide further intrigue, with soft, integrated tannins giving structure.
title: Tenute Chiaromonte 2012 Riserva Primitivo (Gioia del Colle)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This unique blend of 44% Mourvèdre, 33% Zinfandel, 20% Grenache and 3% Counoise starts with dried alpine strawberry, pomegranate, wild chaparral spice, crushed pepper and bay leaf on the elegantly woven nose. A strong sagebrush character ensues on the palate, giving nuance to the saucy plum fruit. The acidity and tannins are well primed and ready to drink.
title: Thacher 2014 Controlled Chaos Red (Paso Robles)

description: This blend of 42% Carignan, 29% Cinsault and 29% Grenache is full and concentrated, but not overly done. There's a mentholated and slightly liquorous edge to the ripe black plum, cherry and boysenberry fruit, as well as subtle hints of cocoa powder and pressed violets. Medium weight, the texture is like crushed velvet, with structure and finesse to frame the fruity palate. Final notes of charred sweet spice, black pepper and tea grace the lingering finish. Drink now–2022.
title: The Blacksmith 2015 Vin Noir Red (Coastal Region)

description: Toasty lemon peel and spiced orange rind set up a spicy, citrusy welcome on the nose of this wine. At the core are rich notes of lemon custard, pear flesh and melon, all cut by superb acidity and a fresh, steely minerality. It's driven and focused, with clear precision on the palate and a medium-length finish that evolves from fruit flesh to rind and finally a kiss of earthy spice. Drink now–2022.
title: The Foundry 2015 Grenache Blanc (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This wine offers aromas of scorched earth, pipe tobacco, mature black-skinned berry and a hint of leather. The rounded, generous palate doles out juicy black cherry, fleshy raspberry, star anise and a hint of cinnamon alongside chewy tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Trerose 2015 Santa Caterina  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: A tiny production of lightly oaked wine from primarily Pommard clones, this wine presents as thick and tannic at first, with a touch of reduction. Its intensity and density mellow in the glass to show a greater sense of elegance and fresh acid-driven flavor, built around pomegranate, strawberry and wild lavender.
title: Whistler 2014 Corvus Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Bright and focused raspberry fruit comes buttressed by a firm streak of Mediterranean herbs. Hints of iron thread through the finish. This is a taut substantial wine that will require some breathing time to show its best.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2013 Dundee Hills Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This lush and lovely wine, a New World version of Muscadet, is tangy and loaded with bright apple and pear flavors. Amplified by hints of citrus rind, it opens up even further once it warms in the glass, showing unexpected and quite impressive depth and detail.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2015 D.F.B. Melon de Bourgogne (Dundee Hills)

description: A very gentle touch of summer blossom is joined by fresh, bright lemon on the nose. The palate has a creamy element that enriches the bright lemon palate without adding heaviness. This is sprightly and light, but also creamy and mellow. A dry, fresh and totally convincing wine with a clean, long finish.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 L'éclat Ad Quadratum White (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of baking spice, blackberry and cherry draw you into the glass. The palate is chock full of cherry and dark-chocolate flavors, with the tannins giving a light squeeze.
title: Efeste 2014 Final-Final Cabernet Sauvignon-Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Ancient Lakes, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas pop, with notes of clarified butter, pineapple, candlewax, corn silk, apricot and mango. The palate is full of stone and tropical fruit flavors that bring a big yum factor. Aromas and flavors are ripe, like the vintage this wine comes from.
title: Efeste 2015 Lola Evergreen Vineyard Chardonnay (Ancient Lakes)

description: Produced from a field blend of 50-year-old vines, this wine is complex and densely textured. It comes from a vineyard near the mountains of the Serra da Estrela, giving a crisp character to this fruit- and spice-laden wine. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2014 Cav #14 Reserva Especial White (Dão)

Appellation: Moon Mountain District Sonoma County, Sonoma, California, US
description: From a section of the producer's vineyard, this wine is juicy and herbal, with a full-bodied robustness of ripe red currant and cherry. The fleshy fruity character is buoyed by a richness of blueberry and vanilla biscuit, as the finish offers black pepper and clove.
title: Ty Caton 2015 Upper Bench Merlot (Moon Mountain District Sonoma County)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Crisp and light aromas of squeezed lemon, Pink Lady apple and Rainier cherry prove soft and supple on the nose. The palate is almost effervescent in acidity and crispness, showing lime peel flavors and a chalky backbone.
title: Whitcraft 2016 Zotovich Vineyard Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Templeton Gap District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Roasted blueberry, allspice, nutmeg, loam and oregano show on the nose of this blend of 55% Syrah, 40% Zinfandel and 5% Viognier. The palate is rich and ripe with roasted black plum and mace spice. Very drinkable yet complex.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2015 Zephyr Red (Templeton Gap District)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Malbec shines in Southern Oregon. Here it's given 16 months in new French oak, emerging supple with tangy berry and pie cherry fruit. Suggestions of citrus juice come with balancing acidity. Tannins are ripe, substantial and lightly earthy, with a hint of coffee.
title: 2Hawk 2015 Malbec (Rogue Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Blue flower, plum cake and baking spice aromas come to the forefront while a whiff of tilled earth lingers in the background. The elegant firmly structured palate shows raspberry preserves, star anise and espresso set against taut, polished tannins. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Antico Colle 2013 Il Saggio Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Enticing scents of white spring blossoms and yellow stone fruit waft out of the glass. The bright creamy palate delivers Golden Delicious apple, juicy citrus zest, white almond and white peach flavors alongside an elegant perlage.
title: Bellavista 2013 Satèn  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from an up-and-coming vineyard, this wine is 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Syrah. It's aromatically expressive, with notes of ripe black cherry, plum, licorice and high-toned dried herbs. The palate is dense and layered, showing breadth and richness while staying in balance. Drink in the near term.
title: Buty 2014 Columbia Rediviva Phinny Hill Vineyard Red (Horse Heaven Hills)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
Designation: Rediviva of the Stones Rockgarden Estate
description: This wine is a unique blend of 77% Syrah, 13% Cabernet Sauvignon and 10% Mourvèdre. Hailing from the Rocks District, it offers generous aromas of black olive, plum, wet gravel, crushed flower, green olive, smoked meat, parsley and dried herb. These lead to pure, ample fruit and savory flavors. A taste of smoked meat lingers on the finish.
title: Buty 2014 Rediviva of the Stones Rockgarden Estate Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Chardonnay, this elegant sparkler offers aromas of yellow stone fruit and white spring flower. The creamy palate delivers ripe yellow apple, crushed herb and tangerine zest  alongside a soft perlage.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2013 Vintage Collection Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas of baked plum, underbrush and balsamic notes lead the nose. The firm palate offers dried black cherry, tobacco and licorice, with fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Caven 2011 Messere  (Sforzato di Valtellina)

Appellation: Fair Play, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine has a compelling, exuberant black-fruit character that makes it taste alive and buoyant despite a thick layer of fine-grained tannin and very full body. Blackberry jam, black cherry compote and dark chocolate power the flavors and linger on the finish.
title: Charles B. Mitchell 2014 Old Vine Petite Sirah (Fair Play)

description: A firmly structured wine, this is powerful and dense. It comes from a 14-acre property dominated by Merlot that gives the wine weight, rich fruit and juicy acidity. The result is a wood-aged wine full of spice and with a dry core that is packed with fruit. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Chemin-Royal 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Owned by the Rivière family, which also owns a Saint-Émilion-based negociant firm, this estate has produced a generous, ripe wine. The 85% Merlot in the blend gives a jammy, rich character that will soften and broaden as the wine matures. A firm structure within the rich fruitiness will allow the wine to develop. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Cheval Brun 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: The Chanfreau family continues its skilled course with this ripe, full and rich wine. Tannins from both the fruit and wood are prominent, giving a dry character to this youthful wine. However, the rich black-currant fruit is developing well. This wine has a long-term future. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Fonréaud 2015  Listrac-Médoc

description: The luxury cuvée from this estate, this blend includes 70% Merlot and 30% Malbec. It is still wood laden, with strongly toasty flavors that are balanced by opulent black fruits and firm tannins that promise a richly succulent wine. It will need plenty of time, so don't drink before 2022.
title: Château Martinat 2015 Cuvée Epicurea  (Côtes de Bourg)

description: A half-and-half Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon blend, this wine is richly structured and packed with fruit. It has a perfumed character, juicy with blackberry flavors and acidity. Drink this opulent Patrick Meynard wine from 2024.
title: Château Pomeys 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This is the winery's first release off its Taylor Mag Vineyard. Aromas don't reveal their charms on first pour, with notes of café au lait, cherry, raspberry, woodspice and herb. The palate brings firm, brawny tannins along with plentiful cherry flavors. Cocoa notes linger on the finish. It's everything you expect from a Cabernet, at a price below many wines from this appellation.
title: Efeste 2014 Taylor Mag Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This winery is under new ownership and off to a good start with this widely available, succulent and flat-out delicious Pinot. Spicy cranberry, raspberry and cherry flavors fill out a big midpalate. It boasts tannins that are ripe, supple and proportionate. This lovely wine should be enjoyed over the next couple of years.
title: Firesteed 2015 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Here's a vibrant sparkler with delicate but alluring scents of white stone fruit, citrus blossom, hay and bread dough. The elegantly sparkling palate presents golden apple, lemon zest, toasted almond and dried mountain herb set against bright acidity.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2013 Freccianera Satèn  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The best of an outstanding white wine lineup, this enters the palate with the clarity of a mountain lake. The clean and crisp fruit flavors of melon and white peach gently pull in accents of butter and caramel, without any single component dominating. It's very fine winemaking.
title: Helioterra 2016 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)

description: A fleshy hint of ripe cherry creates an inviting nose. The palate is similarly fruity but also firm with fine tannins. There is something rustic, rugged and earthy about this, but in a very engaging way. Slightly rustic but quite alluring. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Hugel 2015 Classic Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Camphor, sandalwood, new leather and a whiff of wild berry aromas lead the way. The bright palate mirrors the nose, offering red cherry, pomegranate and star anise framed in fresh acidity and taut polished tannins.
title: Il Macchione 2013  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Telltale cinnamon and vanilla headiness hints at new some oak on the nose. The palate offers red cherry flavors that are juicy enough to counterbalance the oak. For now, fine tannins coat the mouth but give structure for fruit and oak to express their well-matched tones. A modern take on Alsace Pinot but with ample freshness.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2014 Trois Châteaux Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: The honeysuckle scent on the nose has an alluring edge of candied lemon peel. The palate, despite being slender and fresh, shows a similarly decadent streak. This lovely fruitiness is joined by more orange peel freshness and a concentrated texture. This is joyous and rich, with a dry, enduring finish.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 Trois Châteaux Muscat Ottonel (Alsace)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: There's a telltale licorice-like spice that comes from many Temecula Valley wines, and this blend, which throws Syrah in with four Bordeaux varieties, offers a hint of that, but in a well-measured way. Add to that candied blueberry fruit and baking spice on the nose, and black currant, gingerbread and polished tannins on the palate, and this is a winning wine.
title: Lorenzi Estate 2013 Alpha Red (Temecula Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This offers classic Sangiovese aromas of wild cherry, tilled soil, blue flower, pipe tobacco and a whiff of truffle. The aromas flow over seamlessly to the polished palate along with a hint of licorice. Reflecting the cool, wet vintage, it's more delicately structured than usual but is a fantastic effort. Drink through 2020.
title: Lunadoro 2014 Pagliareto  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Almost overripe aromas of prune and raisin are at the fore on the nose. A full, chewy palate shows big tannins and roasted, earthy flavors of olive, spice cake and berry fruits. Assertive tannins linger through the finish, with flavors of licorice, herbs and chocolate. Drink through 2022.
title: Marqués de Cáceres 2010 Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Classic is a pretty good description of this wine. It's meaty, firm, muscular and loaded with ripe blackberry and black-cherry fruit. The ripe ample tannins support that compact strength, and it has the balance and structure to cellar for the next decade. Drink 2020–2030.
title: MonksGate 2015 Classic Pinot Noir

description: This dense wine has great tannins as well as rich fruit. Juicy crispness gives the wine balance and aging potential. Wood aging for eight months has smoothed the wine and given it extra richness. Drink from 2019.
title: Parras Wines 2015 Quinta do Gradil Reserva Red (Lisboa)

description: A bouquet of black currant and flint sets the stage for flavors of black plum, cassis, dark chocolate and smoked meat. This wine is bright on entry, followed by deep spicy flavors set into a web of strong but not overbearing tannins. Brightness returns with a splash of acidity on the finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2016 3400 Premium Shiraz (Judean Hills)

description: Strikes of zesty tangerine and lime are juxtaposed by swirls of mineral intensity in this light-bodied and austere wine. It's dry in style, with a piercing lemon-lime finish. Drink now through 2025.
title: Johannishof 2016 Charta Riesling (Rheingau)

description: A blend of Castelão, Touriga Nacional and Touriga Francesa, this wine, with its wood aging still apparent, needs to age. It will turn into a rich wine, full of black fruits with a structure of generous tannins. Give this ripe wine until late 2018.
title: José Maria da Fonseca 2015 Periquita Reserva Red (Península de Setúbal)

description: Peach blossom, orchard fruit and bread crust aromas waft out of the glass. On the lively palate, fresh acidity provides the backdrop for yellow apple, fennel and lemon zest while a refined perlage lends finesse.
title: Le Marchesine NV Extra Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Made entirely with Pinot Nero, this lively sparkler opens with aromas of brioche, wild red berry and crisp red apple. The aromas segue over to the vibrant palate along with a tangerine note. Tangy acidity lifts the finish while a hint of hazelnut signals the close.
title: Majolini NV Extra Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Charred beef, pepper dust, oregano, thyme and sage make for a savory entry to the nose of this bottling from the Malibu area, but there is aromatic lift from dried lilac aromas as well. The palate is classical, with firm tannins carrying more herb and pepper as well as tar, blueberry and a slightly gamy quality.
title: Montage Vineyards 2012 Seven Sons Vineyard Syrah

description: Enticingly delicate scents of wild berry, mountain herb, blue flower and a whiff of culinary spice come together on this fragrant Nebbiolo. The elegant vibrant palate offers tart cherry, crushed strawberry and a hint of star anise framed in tight, refined tannins. Bright acidity lends balance. Drink through 2022.
title: Nera 2010 Inferno Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Named after the Gonçalves Faria family who own the vineyards, this is a wine produced with input from Dirk Niepoort of the Douro. Made entirely from Baga, it has plenty of tannins in an otherwise light wine. There is a sense of a traditional wine vinified in a traditional way. Drink from 2019.
title: Niepoort 2015 Gonçalves Faria Baga (Bairrada)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Wild berry, tilled soil, violet and cake spice aromas lift out of the glass along with an earthy whiff of new leather. The fresh, youthfully austere palate delivers red cherry, tangy cranberry, ground clove and crushed herb framed in fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Nottola 2013 Origine Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Fragrant purple flower, exotic spice, forest floor and baked plum aromas mingle with a whiff of new leather. The round juicy palate offers morello cherry, licorice and a salty note alongside supple tannins.
