title: Palazzo Vecchio 2012 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Electric acidity lends vitality to fresh yellow-apple and nectarine flavors in this dry, medium-bodied white. Despite its fresh, cutting style, the mouthfeel is expansive and rich, lingering through a long, spice-tinged finish.
title: Pflüger 2016 Bunstandstein Trocken Riesling (Pfalz)

description: This is a richly smooth LBV. Superripe black fruits, dried fruits, sultanas and spice are brought together in a generous, full-throated Port. Its tannins are just enough to suggest it could age further, although it is enjoyable now.
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2013 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: Heavily roasted aromas of gamy berry fruits, herbs and coconutty oak settle on charred. This wine is bulky and a touch stewed and soupy in feel. Oaky flavors of chocolate, baked berry and cassis end with chocolaty oak on a finish that's heavy and lacking in verve. Drink through 2024.
title: Ramirez de la Piscina 2010 Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Set on the plateau above the northern bank of the Douro, this quinta's elevation makes it ideal for white wines. This wine is a field blend of old vines, with creamy rich fruit, baked-apple flavors as well as touches of vanilla and spice. The wine is ripe, full bodied and ready to drink.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2015 Quinta do Síbio Field Blend White (Douro)

description: Made entirely with Merlot, this has aromas of roasted coffee bean, coconut and black-skinned berry. The concentrated palate offers black-currant jam, mocha and bay leaf alongside assertive tannins that grip the finish.
title: Rocca di Frassinello 2015 Baffonero  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Aromas of cassis, leather and cedar abound on this Syrah, Petit Verdot and Cabernet Sauvignon blend. The savory juicy palate delivers blackberry jam, tobacco and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Rocca di Montemassi 2015  Maremma Toscana

description: Ripe yellow stone fruit, bread crust and aromatic herb aromas come together on this dry sparkler. The ripe palate delivers yellow apple, peach and a tangerine zest alongside bitter almond. An elegant perlage lends finesse.
title: Ronco Calino 2011 Brut Nature  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: A good blast of black cherry and cedar in the aroma is followed by rich grapey flavors on a moderately firm texture that's softened by a hint of sweetness on the finish.
title: Rubino Estates Winery 2015 Ferrario Famiglia Collection Red (Livermore Valley)

description: It's rare to find a beerenauslese at prices like these and luckily, this bottling offers surprising bang for your buck. Dusty and earthen yet chock full of fragrant orange blossom and peach, it's a creamy swirl of honey, fruit and mineral, with a lingering finish. Enjoy now through 2021.
title: Schmitt Söhne 2015 Beerenauslese Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: Made by proprietor Anna Monticelli, who also makes the Pina Napa Valley wines, this is a robust hearty vineyard-designate made in tiny numbers. With 5% Cabernet Sauvignon blended in, it offers earthy tannin and a soft muscular texture of lightly spiced oak and dark chocolate cherry.
title: Sergio 2014 Dr. Reid Vineyard Malbec (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Mature plum, chopped herb and a whiff of truffle lead the nose. The aromas carry over to the ripe palate along with black cherry and a hint of star anise encased in supple tannins.
title: Valdipiatta 2015  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: This 100% Chardonnay opens with aromas of yellow flower, golden apple and a whiff of bread crust. The fresh creamy palate has yellow Bartlett pear, lemon rind and toasted hazelnut alongside an elegant mousse.
title: Villa Crespia 2009 Millè Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The steely, crisp fruit has been softened up a bit by barrel fermentation, which also adds a light toasty note. The core remains firm, with freshly sliced apple and pear fruit and a smooth lingering finish.
title: Winter's Hill 2016 Reserve Pinot Blanc (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Layers of roasted beef, blackberry compote, pepper, tarragon and vanilla show on the nose of this bottling from the longtime Paso Robles producer. The palate combines New World richness with Old World elegance, offering dried plums, spice cake and pecan flavors.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Reserve Syrah (Paso Robles)

description: You'll find red berry, rose petal and thyme aromas on this fresh mountain Nebbiolo. The savory palate offers crushed raspberry, cinnamon and white pepper alongside polished tannins.
title: Caven 2010 Sassella La Priora Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: The 30% new barrels really show in this toasty, spicy wine. This intense wood aging is saved by the rich underlying fruit that delivers great concentration and blackberry flavors. Given time these will balance with the wood, produce a ripe, fruity wine. Drink from 2021.
title: Château Fleur la Mothe 2015  Médoc

description: The 16th-century Scottish baronial-style chateau is surrounded by a 45-acre vineyard that gives structured wines like this. It has the richness of the year although it has not lost the ripe tannins and dark fruits. It needs to age, so drink this firm wine from 2024.
title: Château la Haye 2015  Saint-Estèphe

description: This elegant wine is rich, with lightly structured black fruits that reveal the generosity of the vintage and also the aging potential. Powered by the juicy black-currant fruit of the Cabernet Sauvignon, this is a wine to age. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Peyredon Lagravette 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This rich and dense wine shines with ripe Merlot, a product of the warm vintage. Dusty tannins, licorice and black-fruit flavors and plenty of spice from wood aging all contribute to the generally ripe effect of this generous wine. Drink from 2023.
title: Clos des Menuts 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: This Coonawarra Chard is less about the fruit and more about oak and malo characters like melted butter, toasted nuts and copper penny, with prickly acidity wrapped in an oily texture. It's a versatile food wine, ready to drink now or to cellar through 2023.
title: Di Giorgio Family Wines 2016 Chardonnay (Coonawarra)

description: A blend holding 55% Cabernet Sauvignon gives plenty of black currant fruit and provides the tannins that form the background to this richly juicy wine. Merlot adds weight to this perfumed, ripe wine that will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Château le Chêne 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine is generous, with red-berry and red-currant fruitiness. Its tannins still show some firmness although they are integrating well with the fruit and acidity. The wine will be ready to drink by mid-2018.
title: Château L'Oiseau 2015 Vieilles Vignes  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: This estate lies in the far western reaches of the Médoc close to the forest, giving a cool character to the wine. It obviously been wood-aged, although with room left for the blackberry fruits. Spice and toast flavors are already integrating well, indicating the wine's potential. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Méric 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This structured wine comes from an estate that has records going back to the 13th century. A blend of half-and-half Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, it is firm and tannic at this young stage. The juicy black currant fruit is beginning to develop, bringing with it plenty of crisp acidity. Drink this wine from 2023.
title: Château Ramafort 2015  Médoc

description: Yellow wildflower, bread crust and chamomile aromas lead the way. The bright foaming palate offers mature yellow apple, orange rind and toasted walnut alongside a fun lively mousse.
title: Contadi Castaldi 2013 Satèn Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: A perfumed cloud of rose petals hovers over the glass and beneath that is a touch of earthiness. The palate is surprisingly light and translucent but utterly rose-scented. This is off-dry but finishes a little short.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 L'arabesque Ad Quadratum White (Alsace)

description: A rather neutral nose merely hints at some citrus and pear. The palate is more forthcoming with fresh, lively lemon that frames ripe pear. This is juicy, balanced and refreshing, with a dry and fruity finish. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Zinck 2016 Portrait Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A hint of ripe pear can be discerned on the closed nose. The palate is clean and fresh, with just a little texture. This is an easy, light and dry wine. Enjoy soon.
title: Domaine Zind-Humbrecht 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot, this opens with subtle aromas of black-skinned fruit and underbrush. The firm palate offers black plum, vanilla and cocoa alongside velvety tannins.
title: Donne Fittipaldi 2015  Bolgheri

description: Smoke and caramel accent fresh peach, lime and lemon in this sweet-tart kabinett. Light in body and offering a delicate concentration, it's a spry, racy wine with a refreshing finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Dr. Hermann 2016 Erdener Treppchen Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Warmly fruity, this is among the most immediately satisfying of the winery's 2015 Pinots. The berry and cherry flavors carry a light streak of cola, and the tight acids bring a hint of orange juice. Drink now through the early 2020s.
title: Erath 2015 Prince Hill Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: An opulent nose of cabbage, ginger and buttery oak aromas sets up for a palate that is slightly less in your face. It boasts bright acidity but is nevertheless rich and creamy, with a rather large smudge of oak. One for the full-bodied Chard lovers.
title: Fowles Wine 2016 Farm to Table Chardonnay (Victoria)

description: Sanguine and bitter in dried herb, this is a thick dense wine, explosive on the palate in juicy black cherry and berry. The finish is a tad harsh in tannin.
title: Heffernan 2012 VV Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: All of Helioterra's white wines are immaculate, fresh and framed with crisp, clean acids. This brings tightly-focused flavors of lemon and star fruit, with a sprinkling of caraway through the persistent finish.
title: Helioterra 2016 Melon de Bourgogne (Willamette Valley)

description: A rather neutral nose just hints at freshness. The dry palate is light in feel, with lemon and pear flavors. Overall this is light, off-dry and fruity.
title: Henri Schoenheitz 2016 Val Saint Gregoire Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Red Hills Lake County, California, US
description: This is a giant of a wine, from the heady, dried-fruit aromas to the flavors soaked in espresso and blackberry syrup to the thick blanket of tannin that wraps it all up. It is very full bodied, feels warm on the palate and leaves an astringent aftertaste.
title: IG 2015 Petite Sirah (Red Hills Lake County)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Johan grows biodynamically farmed grapes, and generally employs such techniques as native yeast and minimal intervention. The wines, as is the case here, tend to be quite low in alcohol. This offers precise flavors of wild berry, accented with allspice and a toasty note from aging in 30% new French oak.
title: Johan 2015 Estate Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: A touch of ripe, red cherry on the nose becomes fully expressed on the palate where it is met with freshness and a conifer tone. The tannins are fine and have the slightest crunch, while the body is light and translucent. This is fresh-faced, honest and clean.
title: Joseph Cattin 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (WA), Washington Other, Washington, US
description: Aged in neutral French oak, this offers intriguing, slightly funky aromas of lemon verbena, green-apple skin and mineral that lead to sleek green- and red-apple flavors backed by racy acidity.
title: Kerloo 2016 Limited Release Celilo Vineyard Chardonnay (Columbia Gorge (WA))

description: The nose offers intense aromas of orange peel, dried apricot, marmalade and ginger, which carries to the palate along with fresh brioche. It feels creamy and round, with a supple body lending itself to a long, opulent finish.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2015 Signature Series Viognier (Monticello)

description: A tart but aromatic tone of cranberry hovers on the nose and then continues to fill the palate with its tart, appetizing purity. This is a very light, slender wine that offers ample freshness. Drink soon.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Oily, briny, waxy aromas reflect aging and extended time on lees, both of which this wine has been through. Piercing acidity keeps things in balance, while oxidized flavors of corn and salted peach finish strong and slightly bitter. This unique style of Albariño is not for everyone.
title: La Val 2011 La Val Sobre Lías Albariño (Rías Baixas)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of French oak, coconut and baked plum lead the nose. The chewy palate offers raspberry compote, prune, licorice, grilled sage and oak-driven flavors alongside close-grained tannins that leave a tongue-drying finish. You'll also notice the warmth of evident alcohol.
title: Le Bèrne 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of moist soil, underbrush, leather and a whiff of eucalyptus take shape on this. Taut and racy, the linear palate offers sour cherry, cranberry, powdered sage and a hint of clove set against vibrant acidity. Tight, rather grainy tannins grip the close.
title: Le Bèrne 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Grassy and herbaceous, as befits this variety, you'll want to drink this zesty, lively wine while it's at its freshest. Peppery and pungent, it's a good stand-in for an inexpensive Loire Valley Sauv Blanc.
title: Ledger David 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Rogue Valley)

description: From a property owned by the Bouey family since 1998, this wine is generous, ripe and offers attractive black-currant fruitiness. A blend led by 70% Merlot, it is soft and rounded, with a core of tannins for a bit of aging. Drink from 2021.
title: Maison Bouey 2015 Château Lestruelle  (Médoc)

description: Raspberry and pomegranate rise above aromas of white pepper and herbs. Tart cranberry, cedar and vanilla flavors are supported by chalky tannins and a vibrant vein of acidity, finishing on notes of cedar and raspberry.
title: Markko 2013 Select Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Ohio)

description: Perfumed in black cherry and smoky oak, this is a robust, richly ripe red, meaty and brawny in leather and blackberry jam.
title: Peju 2014 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: This is a value wine, thick in resolved tannin and a density of tart cassis and red and black currant. Dried herb and earthy graphite elements give it varietal character and a brightness against the weight.
title: Philippe-Lorraine 2014 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This wine is soft, gently textured and ripely fruity. Citrus and apple flavors are lifted by attractive acidity and the wine ready to drink young. The aftertaste has spice and fresh acidity. Drink now.
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2016 La Rosa Branco White (Douro)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Despite the high alcohol, this is relatively restrained, with flavors mixing rosewater and raspberries. It's a simple, lightly fruity wine, with stiff tannins that introduce suggestions of black tea and a whiff of tobacco.
title: Schmidt 2014 Tempranillo (Applegate Valley)

description: This ripe wine, with its rich tannins and smooth texture, is delicious. Balanced and soft, it's a ripe blend of Tinta Roriz, Touriga Nacional and Jaen. With blackberry and black-currant flavors, it is fruity. It has no wood aging, although there's some final spice from the fruit. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2014 Casa Américo Red (Dão)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This young wine is quite tight and tannic, with hard fruit flavors of red currant. Stemmy scents and flavors are out in full force, and this might well want another year or two in bottle before it's all pulled together. There's a finishing lick of ultra dark chocolate threaded through those chewy tannins.
title: The Four Graces 2015 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This is a straightforward yet not overly expressive Shiraz, with subtle notes of currant and plum skin on the nose and mouth. The structure is easy and approachable, with soft tannins and fruit-skin flavors that lend subtle grip through the licorice-laced finish. Drink now.
title: Topiary 2015 Shiraz (Franschhoek)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: The oak is far out front, with aromas of vanilla, shaved coconut, toast and freshly chopped wood, making it hard to get at the notes of dark fruit and herb. The flavors show fruit mixed with a heavy barrel influence, with the latter winning out on the finish.
title: Watermill 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

description: This affordable blend combines Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah and Zinfandel. Elusive and thick in concentrated black fruit and leather, it offers massive, assertive tannins and a lasting imprint of dried herb.
title: Whitehall Lane 2015 Tre Leoni Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This is a straight-ahead, electric red, slightly off-dry rosé, made from Pinot Noir grapes. Red fruits with a spicy kick have plenty of fruit sweetness, though not necessarily residual sugar. Given that it's a 2015, it should be consumed as soon as possible.
title: Whitetail Ridge 2015 Rosé (Umpqua Valley)

description: The nose wants to give almost nothing away. The palate, however, presents subtle green pear and yellow plum on a very slender, dry body.
title: Willm 2016 Réserve Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Fruity aromas of wild red berry and a whiff of moist soil lift out of the glass. On the restrained light-bodied palate, bright acidity and dusty, not very persistent tannins give sheer support to red cherry and a hint of white pepper. Drink soon.
title: Fattoria Del Cerro 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Fresh and leesy, this all-stainless wine has cucumber, apple and green-melon flavors, done in a light tart refreshing style. Drink up.
title: Airlie 2015 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Another barrel selection, this seems quite thin in comparison with the rest of the winery's 2013s. It's tart and acidic, without the supporting detail and overall elegance of the rest. There are simple fruit flavors of citrus and sour berries.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 Estate Selection Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Pleasing aromas of almond butter, lemon zest and kiwi lead to papaya, pineapple and other tropical-fruit flavors. It delivers immediate appeal.
title: Boomtown 2016 Pinot Gris (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Lightly toasted apple, melon and guava form the core of this ripe and fruit-forward wine. It's light in body and style, with clean yet delicate acidity and a brisk close. Drink now.
title: Bradgate 2015 Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: Citrus blossom, white stone fruit and medicinal herb aromas mingle with a whiff of bread crust. The invigorating palate shows Golden Delicious apple and nectarine rind balanced by lively acidity and racy bubbles. It closes on a walnut note.
title: Castel Faglia NV Satén  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Lightly floral, this delicate but quite drinkable wine shows flavors of peavine, lime and pineapple. The fruit is just ripe enough, balanced and clean, with refreshing acidity.
title: Cedar + Salmon 2016 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

description: Superripe Merlot, 60% in this blend, gives this wine power and density, with full-bodied black fruit and firm tannins. Wood aging, partially in new wood, has brought in spice as well as a dry core that indicates the potential of this wine as it matures. Drink from 2023.
title: Château de Callac 2015 Cuvée Prestige  (Graves)

description: This soft, juicy wine has ripe black-currant fruits and some smoky tannins. It is warm, generous and likely to mature quickly. Drink from 2020.
title: Château Tourteyron 2015  Médoc

description: Fresh red- and black-berry aromas are underscored by tilled earth on the nose. The palate is similar in character, boasting a ripe and juicy feel, with medium weight, light tannins and soft acidity. Drink now.
title: Conti Zecca 2016 Donna Marzia Primitivo (Puglia)

description: Murky, gamy aromas indicate that 2014's wet, humid conditions affected this wine. In the mouth, this is heavy and tough, with grating tannins. Black-fruit flavors combined with lemony oak are chunky, massive and acidic on the finish. This wine is good but has balance issues.
title: Cyan 2014 Valdefinjas Crianza  (Toro)

description: Fresh apple, orange and green melon are at the fore of this straightforward yet refreshing and tasty wine. It is light in weight and concentration, offering fruity flavors of apple and pear that finish bright and clean. Drink now.
title: Deetlefs 2015 Chenin Blanc (Breedekloof)

description: Mirabelle plum and very ripe pear create a fruity expression on the nose. That same fruit is abundant on the palate. This is a fruity wine with a medium-dry finish.
title: Domaine Gresser 2015 Kritt Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Subtle notes of yellow plum have an edge of freshness on the nose. The plum notes also pervade on the juicy, fruit-driven palate. This is light easy, dry and refreshing.
title: Domaine Schoffit 2016 Vieilles Vignes Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Aromas of yellow peach, citrus and a whiff of almond carry over to the medium-bodied palate. Fresh acidity gives it a bright finish.
title: Fattoria San Felo 2016 Viognier (Maremma Toscana)

description: Aromas of pear and lemon rise from the rather neutral nose. The palate comes in with far more fruit in notes of ripe apple and pear, with freshness and a creamy aspect. This is a fruity, easy, dry wine. Drink soon.
title: Gustave Lorentz 2015 Evidence Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Subdued notes of ripe, yellow pear play on the nose and palate. Freshness balances this beautifully, giving it a dry, crisp finish. Drink soon.
title: Jean-Baptiste Adam 2016 Les Natures Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Just a hint of cherry appears on the shy nose. On the fresh, light and translucent palate the fruit tone is pure but tart. This is fresh, honest and still has the slightest tannic grip. A very balanced, fresh-faced wine. Drink soon.
title: Jean Biecher & Fils 2016 Reserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: The nose offers a touch of ripe pear. The palate shows less fruit and majors on a neutral but clean and balanced, dry body. This is simple but refreshing. Drink soon.
title: Koenig Vins Alsace 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Sweet herb, lime twist and peach aromas are followed by sleek, narrow citrus and stone-fruit flavors that drink off dry.
title: Maryhill 2016 Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Offering classic, varietal fruit flavors of apple and pear, this pleasant wine is lightly spiced and buoyed with supporting acids. It was finished in 5% new French oak.
title: Melrose 2016 Pinot Gris (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This seems to be quickly drying out, with fruit-leather flavors more than fresh berries and cherries. There's ample acidity and stiff tannins. Best to consume this as soon as possible.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 Stinger Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: One of the finer Oregon Gamays, this deeply-colored wine is spicy and rich, from first sniff through the last sip. Intense aromas of clove and cranberry come up first and continue into the core flavors, along with fruit leather and orange peel.
title: Duck Pond 2016 Gamay Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Cabernet Sauvignon (59%) and Syrah (33%) make up the majority of this wine, which is finished off with Merlot. Aromas of woodspice, herb, licorice and cherry lead to full-bodied cherry and cranberry flavors. It shows focus and intensity.
title: Estrin 2014 Big Tree Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This opens with enticing aromas of Bartlett pear, chamomile and Spanish broom. The structured savory palate offers white peach, nectarine and a touch of ginger alongside fresh acidity.
title: Fattoria San Felo 2016 Le Stoppie Vermentino (Maremma Toscana)

Designation: Ladies who Shoot their Lunch Wild Ferment
description: Unapologetically rich, this full-bodied wine offers heady aromas of baking spices, butterscotch and toast. The mouthfeel is round and creamy, with plenty of spicy, oak notes but also a bounty of peach and melon fruit to keep things balanced.
title: Fowles Wine 2016 Ladies who Shoot their Lunch Wild Ferment Chardonnay (Victoria)

description: Whiffs of brandy and black tea leaves accent delicate black cherry and violet in this lithe Pinot Noir. It's a frail beauty rather than a powerhouse, but invigorating in acidity. Delicate spice and soft but pleasantly astringent tannins mark a moderately long finish.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2014 Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Mosel)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is intentionally made in a lighter style for near-term consumption, and it's showing salmonberry and raspberry fruit, with a sliver of toasted cracker. Tannins are nicely structured and it finishes with a touch of dark chocolate. Drink now through 2020.
title: Hamacher 2015 H Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This bottling takes a step up in concentration from the Stafford Hill cuvée, while retaining a comparable style. Pretty black raspberry and cherry fruit comes in a well-balanced elegant frame, with medium length and body.
title: Holloran 2014 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Earthy aromas of scorched earth, black-skinned berry and a whiff of black olive lead the nose. A blend of 40% Sangiovese and eleven other organically farmed grapes, the savory juicy palate offers Marasca cherry, licorice and vanilla alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Il Paradiso di Frassina 2013 12 Uve  (Maremma Toscana)

description: This is rather light, given that it is the winery's top-tier Pinot Noir. It's a pleasant, fruity blend of ripe watermelon, strawberry and light cherry fruit. Though quite well balanced, it feels slightly dilute, and should be consumed over the next couple of years.
title: King Estate 2014 Backbone Pinot Noir (Oregon)

description: Aromas of apple skin, beesway, musk melon and white flowers dance in the bouquet of this pretty white. There's a pithy, astringent texture to the palate that frames the ripe melon and white strawberry flavors, while an earthy spice unfolds on the finish. Drink now–2020.
title: Leeuwenkuil 2015 Grenache Blanc (Paardeberg)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep black plum, vanilla, star anise and a touch of caramel show on the nose of this blend of 71% Grenache, 15% Mourvèdre and 14% Syrah. The palate is fairly rich and nearly sweet, with sugar plums and dark-brown-spice flavors, all balanced by a compelling texture and bright acidity.
title: Leverage 2015 Acceleration Red (Paso Robles)

description: An enticing interplay between lightly grilled apple, spiced orange and honeydew form the bouquet of this ripe white. It's quite plush in feel, the weighty midpalate just lifted by the acidity. It finishes with dense flavors of orange creamsicle and wood-grilled peach. Drink now–2019.
title: Lourensford 2015 The Dome Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: This opens with delicate aromas of toasted oak, baked plum and leather. The concentrated palate delivers vanilla, espresso, black-cherry jam and licorice alongside assertive tannins.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2013 Albareda  (Sforzato di Valtellina)

description: This is a Syrah-led blend, with 23% Cinsault, 13% Carignan, 7% Tinta Barocca and 6% Grenache. It's well defined and loaded with attractive spicy tones of Frankincense, baking spice and cherry pie. The medium-weight palate offers surprising grip that frames the lush red-fruit flavors, with more of that sweet spice character prevailing on the close. Drink now–2020.
title: Mullineux 2015 Kloof Street Red (Swartland)

description: A blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, green peppercorn and bay leaf. The firm palate delivers ripe black currant, vanilla and a touch of cedar alongside lithe tannins.
title: Orma 2016 Passi di Orma  (Bolgheri)

description: Aromas of cassis, tobacco and ground pepper follow over to the austere palate along with anise and prune. Tightly woven fine-grained tannins offer taut support. Drink sooner rather than later to catch the fruit and freshness.
title: Ornellaia 2015 Le Serra Nuove  (Bolgheri)

description: This wine is packed with black cherry, dark chocolate and espresso flavors that coat the palate with a nicely thick texture. It's not overtly fruity, though, and has lots of fine tannins to shape the mouthfeel.
title: Parducci 2014 True Grit Reserve Red (Mendocino County)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Full bodied and flavorful, this still shows its tannic backbone a bit aggressively, but that should soften with another year or two in bottle. The core of fruit suggests berry preserves, complemented with a lick of chocolate.
title: Phelps Creek 2015 Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Gioia del Colle, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Sourced from the clay and limestone soils of the Marchesana Vineyard, this Primitivo is earthy in aromas of tilled earth, pressed violets and Mediterranean scrub. With no time in oak, the palate is transparent in its juicy bramble fruit intensity, with additional floral and herb notes imparting interest. A rich feel, balanced by bright acidity and integrated tannins make this immediately appealing.
title: Polvanera 2013 14 Vigneto Marchesana Primitivo (Gioia del Colle)

description: A very subtle nose hints at roses and summer blossom. The dry palate puts its concentrated core against this aromatic richness and creates a subtle, understated balance. The finish is dry and refreshing.
title: Preiss-Zimmer 2016 Réserve Muscat (Alsace)

description: Lemon, stone fruit and guava aromas meet white pepper and wet stone minerality in this classic example of cool-climate Chardonnay. The palate offers crisp acidity balanced by an oily texture, making this rich enough for the dinner table but refreshing enough to sip on its own. Drink now–2021.
title: Sidewood 2016 Chardonnay (Adelaide Hills)

description: This opens with aromas reminiscent of star anise, tobacco, woodland berry and a whiff of oak-driven spice. On the vibrant austere palate, bright acidity and lithe taut tannins provide tense support for cranberry, sour cherry and a hint of clove. Drink after 2020.
title: Triacca 2013 La Gatta Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Fresh, vibrant aromas of red berries and dark cherries are highlighted by graphite accents on the nose of this 60% Primitivo and 40% Syrah blend. The medium-bodied palate offers an engaging balance between juicy fruit and tense mineral and spice tones. Medium-grained tannins offer just enough grip and help extend the fruity finish.
title: Villa Schinosa 2016 Laghiglione Red (Puglia)

description: This broad appellation Chardonnay is all about rinds—those from melons, peaches and lemons. The palate is tightly wound in bright acidity and balanced by a chalky texture. The finish brings notes of oak and bitter rinds.
title: Wolf Blass 2016 Reserve Release Chardonnay (South Australia)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a fine introduction to the winery's low alcohol, elegant stable of Pinots. Rhubarb and strawberry fruit comes with a lightly toasty note, and a dash of cocoa. Whole cluster fermentation and indigenous yeast ar the playbook at this winery.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 The Uncarved Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Essentially two-thirds Pinot Noir (65%), the blend also includes 15% Pinot Meunier and 20% Chardonnay. It's a light-salmon-pink, and the flavors bring a touch of rosewater along with diluted cherry. The acids suggest lemon and lime, flesh and rind, with a finishing lick of grapefruit.
title: Argyle 2013 Artisan Series Brut Rosé Sparkling (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This opens with subtle aromas of red-skinned berry, Mediterranean herb, dark culinary spice and a whiff of violet. On the sleek light-bodied palate, polished tannins and fresh acidity underscore Marasca cherry, raspberry and clove. Drink through 2021.
title: Avignonesi 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from a hot vintage, the aromas bring notes of blackberry jam, coffee, fruit rollup, flower and herb. The tannins are quite firm, showing a bit of astringency out of the gate but settling down with some time open. Despite the warmth of the vintage, it brings a pleasing sense of acidity.
title: Bledsoe 2015 Flying B Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is 60% Marsanne and 40% Viognier. Aromas are light, with notes of lime leaf, marzipan and white flower. It drinks dry, with stone fruit flavors throughout, and finishes a bit short.
title: 51 Weeks 2016 Lonesome Springs Cuvée White (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Banana and pear aromas are lightly briny. A minerally mouthfeel is driven by citric acidity, while tangerine flavors are backed by sea-based briny notes. This offers real value; drink immediately.
title: Altos de Torona 2016 Sobre Lías Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: This is in its infancy now, but it's remarkably refined and nuanced, showing great promise for evolution and development. Notes of melon rind, orange peel, lemon verbena and fresh fynbos fill the bouquet, while the palate offers more pithy citrus and green melon characters. It's well-balanced, with vibrant acidity and a fresh wet-stone minerality that endures through the long finish. It's hard to resist now, but it will continue to mature and hold well through 2024.
title: L'Avenir 2015 Single Block 30 Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

description: While initially offering more earth than fruit, this sprightly kabinett amasses bold white peach, grapefruit and gooseberry flavors as it opens and melds. Vivacious acidity and lingering undertones of crushed earth and clay lend structure and depth. Drinks well already but should improve through 2030.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Herrenberg Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: This barrel selection is still tight and tannic, with compact, yet substantial cranberry, raspberry and pie-cherry fruit. It's got muscle and depth, and opens up nicely with aeration, displaying ripe and polished tannins through a long finish.
title: MonksGate 2015 Trinity Pinot Noir

description: There's strong oak, vanilla and spice cake on the nose of this rich bottling from Paso Robles vineyards as well as sugar plums and wet soil aromas. There's an earthy minerality of turned loam to the palate, with hints of forest moss, pine oil and cracked pepper.
title: Nelle 2015 Grenache (California)

description: New leather, baked plum, truffle and fruitcake aromas rise up from the glass. It's rounded and full bodied, with velvety tannins that underscore fig, steeped prune, cinnamon, graphite and chewing tobacco. Fresh acidity lifts the rich flavors. Drink through 2023.
title: Nino Negri 2013 Sfursat Carlo Negri  (Sforzato di Valtellina)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Utilizing Pommard clones planted in 1990, this exceptional wine has a nose-tickling funky note, providing a rich composty accent to juice loaded with raspberry and cherry fruit. It's detailed and textural, a truly engaging wine which goes well beyond the fruit to add the sort of complexity not often found in this price range.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Balcombe Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Thick, firm tannin is pronounced in this reserve-level wine. Coconut and vanilla-seasoned oak rides along a roadway of dried herb, clove and blackberry as the texture softens and teases in complexity.
title: Pride Mountain 2014 Vintner Select Merlot (Sonoma County)

description: This impressive single-quinta wine is packed with fruit and plenty of the firm tannins. Ripe, perfumed and with touches of sweetness, it will age well. Drink from 2027.
title: Quinta das Carvalhas 2015 Vintage  (Port)

description: With its north-facing aspect, this vineyard is just right for red wines rather than Port. A cool character comes through even in ageworthy wines such as this. Firm tannins mix in with the juicy red fruits and crisp aftertaste. Wood aging adds spice and rounds out the wine. Drink from 2021.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2014 Quinta dos Aciprestes Grande Reserva Red (Douro)

description: Cool, intensely mineral tones of slate and river rock permeate from nose to finish. It's dry on the palate but elegantly fruity, offering notes of crisp stone fruit, tangerine and lime. Piercing acidity leads a long, thrilling finish. It's a lovely wine already but likely to show even better from 2019.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Kiedricher Trocken Ortswein Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Yellow flower, bread crust, ripe orchard fruit and graphite scents mingle together in the glass. It's loaded with finesse, offering Golden Delicious apple, peach and tangerine zest balanced by bright acidity and a refined perlage.
title: Ronco Calino 2010 Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Tasty, spicy oak nuances give a great lift to this frankly heavy, massively built wine that is very full bodied and has a thick layer of chewy tannins. Hints of toasted walnuts, cinnamon and nutmeg weave through dark chocolate, blackberry and espresso flavors that are concentrated and deep.
title: Rubino Estates Winery 2015 Riserva Collection Petite Sirah (Livermore Valley)

description: Bright and forward on the nose, this leads with attractive scents of lemon custard, verbena and grilled pineapple. The palate is plush and round, yet not overdone, boasting lush flavors of toasted apple and citrus oil that linger on the close. Bright acidity keeps the weight in check and lends a mouthwatering sensation on the finish. Time will encourage added depth and complexity, so consider drinking 2020–2024.
title: Rustenberg 2015 Five Soldiers Chardonnay (Simonsberg-Stellenbosch)

description: Again in 2015 this offers reserve-level density and extra complexity, with the added bonus that it's the winery's biggest production Pinot. Winemaker Tony Rynders loads it with appealing cinnamon, caramel and milk chocolate components from aging in half new French oak. This is all nicely integrated into a base of fresh raspberry fruit. Tasty and ready for near-term drinking.
title: Saffron Fields Vineyard 2015 Pinot Noir

description: Dusty mineral tones juxtapose pristine lime and lemon in this zesty, steely wine. While dry and light footed, it has an expansive, juicy richness that's satisfying through a long finish.
title: Schloss Saarstein 2016 Alte Reben Riesling (Mosel)

description: A little reserved at first, with time this opens up to reveal notes of pressed fynbos, dandelion, fresh straw, apple blossom and crushed mineral that grace the just-ripe apple and green-melon core. Surprisingly round and mouth-filling, that weight is countered by a stunningly vibrant seam of citrusy acidity and spiced tones of allspice and nutmeg. Final notes of grilled apple skin and pie crust dance on the lingering finish. Drink now–2026.
title: The Blacksmith 2015 Vin Blanc Chenin Blanc (Swartland)

description: This is an intensely youthful blend of 48% Cabernet Sauvignon, 21% Syrah, 21% Cabernet Franc and 10% Malbec. Concentrated flavors of black cherry, blackberry, cured meat and black licorice are well wrapped within a cocoon of toasted oak and firm tannins. Enjoy 2025–2030.
title: Viader 2015 Estate Limited Edition Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Z'IVO's Pinot Blanc is an obscure clone called Gouges, after the Burgundy winery that first isolated it. It's exceptionally dense, textural and racy, with scents and mouthfeel close to Chablis, with the same underlying minerality. Oyster shells and sea-breeze accents come with tart melon fruit and a touch of lime zest.
title: Z'IVO 2013 Pinot Blanc (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Blackberry, leather and plum aromas are inviting. This wine is full and lush on the palate, with grippy tannins. Oaky berry flavors are accented by spice and chocolate notes, while the finish brings pronounced coffee and mocha flavors. Drink through 2025.
title: 12 Linajes 2014 Crianza  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This reserve has the same detail and overall finesse as the regular estate bottling, but here there's an extra dollop of black fruits. Overall, the 12-barrel select is a bit bigger and fruitier, but much in the same style.
title: Alloro 2015 Estate Riservata Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Rich, ripe and downright succulent, this offers pinpoint fruit flavors of grapefruit, apple and melon, with a broad streak of vanilla cream. The length is a bit surprising, sneaking up as the wine breathes open. Though just 10% was aged in new barrels, there is a pleasing toasty frame around the finish.
title: Ayoub 2016 Chardonnay (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: A must-get for those attracted to obscure grapes, this cool vintage bottling of a northern Italian variety by Marisa Beverly (assistant winemaker at Au Bon Climat, and Jim Clendenen's niece) shows cranberry, strawberry, dried plum, baked earth and sumac-cinnamon spice on the nose. The palate offers strong loamy minerality right away, then settles into dried sage, peppery spice and tangy but dense dark fruits.
title: Bevela 2011 Matela Le Bon Climat Vineyard Teroldego (Santa Barbara County)

description: This wine, under the same ownership as Margaux estate Mongravey, is a well-structured blend with 70% Cabernet Sauvignon. The 100% new-wood aging gives the wine an intensely toasty, dry character at this young stage. But the great black-currant and berry fruit is developing as well. Drink this rich wine from 2024.
title: Château de Braude 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Packed with black fruits and dense tannins, this is a rich wine with a fine future. This is full bodied and structured in feel, with a dry, spicy core of tannins. With its deep concentration, this wine will do well with time in the cellar. Drink from 2025.
title: Château du Cartillon 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This is an impressively structured and balanced wine from Patrick Meynard. Rich black fruits are nourished by the spice and toast of wood aging for 14 to 18 months.Dense and juicy, this ripe wine likely will age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Lalaudey 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Produced under the same management as Château Larose Trintaudon, this wine is rich, smoky and packed with blackberry fruits. Its warm, ripe character contrasts with the juicy fruitiness and firm backdrop of tannins, giving a wine with potential. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Larose Perganson 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This estate, based in the Gironde port village of Lamarque, has produced a richly textured, chocolate and bitter-cocoa-flavored wine. That is only a problem at the moment, because ripe black-currant fruits are developing under the structure to give density, concentration and a serious wine as it ages. Drink from 2021.
title: Château Malescasse 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Big, ripe and packed with tannins, this is a dense wine. The concentration is palpable, rich with black plum and berry fruits. This powerful wine with its great tannins and fruit needs to age. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Sansonnet 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: Generous in its ripe Merlot, this wine is sumptuous and full bodied. Although there are plenty of tannins, they give just the right structure to this wine. From a 74-acre property that is named after the small village of Pez, the wine is rich and will age well. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Tour de Pez 2015  Saint-Estèphe

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: A classic blend of 80% Sangiovese, 10% Canaiolo and 10% Colorino, this fragrant, almost ethereal wine is all about finesse. It opens with earthy aromas of woodland berry, tilled soil, truffle, violet and new leather while the elegant palate delivers succulent Marasca cherry, crushed strawberry and baking spice. Bright acidity and polished tannins provide balance and refined support. Drink through 2028.
title: Contucci 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The name identifies this as the winery's Internet-only cuvée, and it has much the same character as the Uncarved Block Pinot. Rhubarb and tart strawberry fruit, low alcohol, and an overall balance emulate the lighter style of Pinot.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 iPinot Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Pago de Arínzano, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Gamy, roasted aromas of berry fruits and black olive are less than stellar for a great year like 2010. A stewed, tomatoey palate is drawing in feel, while this Tempranillo from a single estate in Navarra tastes of olive and herbal black fruits. A severely tannic finish is rough and drying.
title: Arínzano 2010 La Casona Tempranillo (Pago de Arínzano)

description: While the nose on this dry Riesling is lavish, marked by plumes of white blossom and grapefruit zest, the palate is surprisingly restrained. Taut with lemon-lime acidity and a firm, steely backbone, it's a refreshing, spry wine with a moderately long finish.
title: Binz 2016 Nackenheimer Kabinett Trocken Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Los Olivos District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Broad black cherry aromas meet with soft touches of rosewater, star anise and hibiscus on this rather light bottling. It's very fresh on the palate, with lively pomegranate and berry flavors, though not much of the dark spice or tannic depth expected of the grape.
title: Brander 2015 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Los Olivos District)

description: A touch of pear peel is joined by a fresh lemon aroma on the nose. The palate offers a crisp lemon flavor that brightens the light, dry and easy-drinking body. Drink soon.
title: Cave de Turckheim 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: The high proportion of Cabernet Sauvignon in this wine brings out both tannins and black-currant fruit. It is firm and structured with layers of acidity and judicious wood-aging flavors. It is likely to age well, and should be ready to drink from 2023.
title: Château Haut Beyzac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: From vines in the Entre-deux-Mers region, this ripe wine, with its 80% Merlot, is young with firm tannins that need to soften. It has plenty of ripe berry fruits laced with acidity and a dry core of tannins. Drink from 2019.
title: Château L'Oiseau 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: Tannic and oaky, this is a big, powerful wine with a lot of extraction and density to it. Intense notions of blackberry, black licorice and cedar meet accents of cigar box and pencil, finishing with a robust richness.
title: Concannon 2014 Clone 7 Cabernet Sauvignon (Chalk Hill)

Appellation: Spring Mountain District, Napa, California, US
description: Leathery oak dominates in this medium-bodied wine from the mountain appellation. Green, dried herb emphasizes its varietal character, as thick tannins wrap tightly on the palate.
title: Concannon 2014 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Spring Mountain District)

description: Made with organically grown Chardonnay, this opens with scents of dried stone fruit, acacia honey, chamomile and toast. The aromas follow through to the foaming palate along with candied lemon slice, vanilla and dried apricot alongside a soft mousse. A bitter walnut note marks the close.
title: Corte Bianca 2011 Satèn Brut  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aged in older French oak, with a third whole-cluster, this offers aromas of peeled apple skin, black raspberry and black licorice that lead to palate-coating black-fruit flavors that drop off on the finish. There's no mistaking the warm vintage and warm region it comes from.
title: Descendant 2014 Syrah (Red Mountain)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is an unusual red that is light in color and extract and has a good grippy texture to carry the light spice, raspberry and earth flavors. It's nicely balanced and makes a refreshing change of pace from bigger bolder wines.
title: Donkey & Goat 2015 The Bear Red (El Dorado)

description: An aromatic hint of Williams pear plays on the nose. The palate is fresh, clean and vivid, with the pear fruit highlighted by fresh lemon. This is a dry, appetizing and refreshing wine with a slender body.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2016 The Furst Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Subtle hints of yellow pear appear on the nose and lead to a very fruity palate where ripe and juicy pear flavors are boosted by lemon freshness. An easy-drinking, fruity number to drink soon.
title: Dopff & Irion 2016 Cuvée René Dopff Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fresh aromas of dark strawberry, red plums, dark chocolate and brown spices show on the nose of this bottling by the legendary Gary Eberle. Crisp and buoyant purple fruits pop on the palate, set against a backdrop of dried lilacs and chalky tannins.
title: Eberle 2015 Vineyard Selection Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Elton is a new label under the Willamette Valley Vineyards umbrella, though the vineyard has been a favored designate for the past decade. This latest release offers pretty scents of red fruits. Along with those flavors come some bitter stem components, and a whiff of compost. The tannins at this age remain stiff and hard.
title: Elton 2015 Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The scents are simple and grapey, like grape candy, and the fruit flavors leave a little bit of a hole in the middle. But it fills in quickly with tart strawberry, ample acids, good balance and some stiff, drying tannins.
title: Erath 2015 Leland Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This wine offers a bouquet of cassis, black plum and licorice. It is smooth and bright on the palate, with flavors of blackberry, pomegranate, vanilla, black pepper and clove that lead into a prolonged fruit-filled finish.
title: Hayotzer 2016 Vintner's Select Cabernet Sauvignon (Judean Hills)

description: Aromas of black currant, vanilla and violet pave the way for flavors of tart cherry, strawberry, lavender and vanilla. Soft tannins lay a groundwork for bright fruit flavors that work their way across the entire palate, leading into a zippy cranberry finish.
title: Hayotzer 2016 Vintner's Select Merlot (Judean Hills)

description: Made entirely from Verdeca, a white grape of unknown origins which finds its home in parts of Southern Italy, this wine is delicately floral on the nose in notes of orchard blossoms and sweet dried hay. Pithy green apple and pear take center stage on the palate, which shows some textural weight and vibrant acidity.
title: I Pastini 2016 Faraone Verdeca (Valle d'Itria)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas lean into the green, with notes of savory garden herb along with dark raspberry and plum. The plum flavors are plump with a lightly scratchy feel to the tannins. There's intrigue but it seems a bit underripe and leafy.
title: J & J 2015 Summit View/Les Collines Vineyard Malbec (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: This wine has aromas and flavors of black currant, cassis, fennel, cedar, dark chocolate and forest floor. A welcome barnyard note goves it a hint of rusticity. Decent acidity and velvety tannins make way for a long and woody finish.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2014 Estate Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Monticello)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
Designation: Barrel Select Field Blend Candy Ridge Vineyard
description: This wine blends Cabernet Sauvignon (41%), Cabernet Franc, (28%), Malbec (28%) and Petit Verdot. Smoke, woodspice and cedar aromas are in front of fresh-chopped herb, green pepper and black raspberry, with green-olive notes lurking in the background. The palate is light but juicy, with coffee and cherry flavors that bring a slight sense of astringency. Citric acidity lingers on the finish.
title: Kitzke 2015 Barrel Select Field Blend Candy Ridge Vineyard Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Lightly scented, this has subtle aromas evoking bread crust, wild flower and white stone fruit. On the palate, a lively mousse and fresh acidity offset yellow apple, white peach and a drop of vanilla.
title: La Montina NV Satèn Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas of ripe strawberry, iris and baking spice lead the nose on this Sangiovese and Alicante blend. Made with organically farmed grapes and without added sulfites, the lively palate doles out red cherry, raspberry jam and a touch of star anise alongside smooth tannins.
title: La Selva 2016  Maremma Toscana

description: Abundant aromas of sweet spice—think cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice—set the stage in this bright, vivacious wine, grounded by notes of red currant and brambly berry. Medium in weight, it offers fine tannins and ample acidity to produce a clean and well-balanced package. Refreshing and so easy to like, drink now–2020.
title: Leeuwenkuil 2015 Cinsault (Swartland)

Appellation: Santa Clara Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep and dark black currant, black plum, prune, dried sage and licorice spice show on the nose of this bottling. The dark dried fruit meets with a minty lift on the palate, proving relatively light in texture but with a strong, somewhat leathery tannic backbone.
title: Martin Ranch 2014 Therese Vineyards Dos Ninas Vineyard Syrah (Santa Clara Valley)

description: With its cherry notes on the nose a little muddled, the palate nonetheless is bright fresh and vivid in a crunchy red-cherry note. This is simple and a little rustic, and would also work as a lightly chilled red.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Cuvée Mathieu Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: A rather earthy nose leads onto the fresh, slender and balanced palate. The body has a kind of rustic simplicity, with honest, clean red-cherry fruit. This is easy and fresh and would be ideal for a country-style lunch. Drink soon.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Fully 70% was fermented whole cluster in neutral oak, yielding a wine with bright cranberry and raspberry fruit, juicy acids, and a touch of citrus. The wine avoids excessive stemmy flavors, though a bit of cardboard comes out in the tannins.
title: Minimus 2016 Dijon Free Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Named for a parcel of vines in the Síbio vineyard, this wine is perfumed, with aromatic fruit and a zesty texture. Lychees and spice are an important part of this ripe, rounded wine. Drink now.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2015 Quinta do Síbio Ananico White (Douro)

description: A saturated, grapy nose with jammy black-fruit aromas is massive but not exact. A blocky, clumsy palate is weighty and a touch stewed. Blackened berry flavors show every bit of this Garnacha-based wine's 15% abv. Notes of fig and chocolate are up front on a heavy finish.
title: Scala Dei 2015 Prior Red (Priorat)

description: Correctly labeled medium sweet, this low alcohol, fruit-driven wine shows honeysuckle and apricot flavors principally, with a touch of tea. The sugar is offset with ample acidity, maintaining balance and precision, and keeping it in the ballpark to pair with spicy noodle dishes.
title: Sweet Cheeks 2016 Riesling (Oregon)

description: Vibrant in aromas of peach, guava and watermelon, these notes carry to a creamy palate that boasts lively acidity to balance. A crushed mineral note offers further levity on the finish.
title: Tormaresca 2017 Calafuria Negroamaro Rosato (Salento)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Light in color, this offers faint aromas that give up almost nothing on first pour, except notes of spice. Still young, the palate is more revealing, showing elegant flavors of raspberry and plum. If the aromas open up it could move up a notch.
title: Upsidedown 2016 The Gold Drop Collection Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Bold and oaky, this full-bodied wine layers wood smoke and butter aromas over baked pear and almond flavors, while hints of new oak run through it. The wine has a mouth-coating texture and tastes warm and spicy on the finish.
title: Van Ruiten 2016 Chardonnay (Lodi)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This opens with earthy aromas suggesting forest floor, toast, scorched soil and a whiff of game. The palate shows dried black cherry, licorice and powdered sage alongside assertive, grainy tannins that grip the finish.
title: Vecchia Cantina di Montepulciano 2012 Briareo Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: There's a nice vitality to the nose of this wine, with aromas of lemon and grapefruit rinds alongside ripe tones of pear and apple. The palate shows equalthe same verve in ripe yellow apple flavors struck against a core of tangerine and chalk.
title: Villa Schinosa 2016 Fiano (Puglia)

description: While quiet on the nose, the palate of this juicy, easy drinking spätlese offers loads of ripe tangerine, pineapple and yellow-apple flavors. It's not a complex wine but revitalizing and crisp, with a pleasant hint of astringency lending structure to the finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Weingut Georg Albrecht Schneider 2016 Niersteiner Hipping Spätlese Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: This blend of three local grapes including Albariño opens with briny core aromas surrounded by white-fruit notes. The palate hits with a piercing shot of acidity but then turns thicker and more syrupy. Flavors of passion fruit and nectarine are slightly briny and finish plump.
title: Altos de Torona 2016 Rosal Caiño Albariño-Loureiro White (Rías Baixas)

description: The nose isn't very expressive but the palate offers mature yellow apple, bread crust and citrus alongside a note of honeyed nut. Bright acidity and small bubbles refresh the finish.
title: Bèlon du Bèlon 2009 Pas Dosé Riserva  (Franciacorta)

description: Fresh and floral on the nose, the palate is ripe in red berry flavors, balanced by streaks of granite. Vibrant acidity helps cut through some of the fruit weight. This is simplistic but easy to glug.
title: A Mano 2016 Primitivo (Puglia)

description: Oaky, rubbery aromas are stewy and include notes of clove and cinnamon. A raw, pulling palate is defined by rubbery tannins, while this wine is toasty on top of tart plum and black-currant flavors. A firm, tough finish holds leftover clove, toast and licorice flavors.
title: Abad Dom Bueno 2014 Mencía (Bierzo)

description: Berry and tomato aromas are basic. Typical of Garnacha, this is grabby and chewy on the palate. Flavors of grape skins, black plum and herbal berry fruits are peppery and show a hint of oak-based clove on the finish.
title: Acentor 2016 Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: Aromas of toasted bread crust and walnut follow through to the palate along with mature yellow apple skin and a hint of ripe lemon zest. An almond note gives it a bitter finish.
title: Bèlon du Bèlon 2011 Pas Dosé Blanc 48 mesi  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of creamed corn, spice and baked apple lead to light, pleasant fruit flavors in this straightforward wine.
title: Boomtown 2016 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: On first take, this smells oaky, with notes of animal fur and earthy berry fruits. A tight palate shows tomatoey acidity, while a narrow field of flavors includes plum and herbs. Drawing tannins and a minty flavor drive the finish on this basic but positive Rioja.
title: Carlos Serres 2014 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: Pure and fresh notes of freshly cut Conference pear are immediately apparent. The same light, pure, fresh fruit plays on the slender, dry palate. This is an crowd-pleaser of a wine, with lovely balance.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: The nose is ripe in aromas of red cherry and berry tones, while the palate is less expressive but presents a juicy, easy freshness on a light body. This will also work chilled. Drink soon.
title: Emile Beyer 2015 Eguisheim Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This wine features a bouquet of white peach and pink grapefruit. It offers a nice balance of fruit and floral flavors, with grapefruit, lemon and jasmine.
title: Galil Mountain 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Upper Galilee)

description: This opens with aromas of black-skinned fruit, scorched soil and a hint of game. The firm palate offers black-currant jam, licorice and star anise alongside polished tannins.
title: Guicciardini Strozzi 2015 Momi  (Maremma Toscana)

description: A blend of 60% Verdeca, 35% Bianco d'Alessano and 5% Minutolo, this is a snappy, enjoyable wine, with aromas of ripe apple, pear and dried hay that echo on the palate. This shows a waxy feel that is cut by lively acidity on the midpalate. Enjoy with white fish or ceviche.
title: I Pastini 2016 Antico White (Locorotondo)

description: This wine shows a perfumed nose of lemon, rose water and orange zest. On the palate pithy citrus counters the rose petal softness. There is just the slightest edge of bitterness on the light and resolutely dry palate.
title: Joseph Cattin 2016 Muscat (Alsace)

description: This Cabernet Franc is abundant in aromas—vacillating from greener notes of tomato leaf and spearmint to jammy red currant, black plum and violets. The racy acidity is much needed to match the full body. Smooth tannins are well integrated and the finish is long and mouthwatering.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2016 Estate Reserve Cabernet Franc (Monticello)

description: This blend of 45% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Shiraz, 11% Merlot and 9% Petit Verdot offers massive black-plum character throughout the nose and mouth, as well as accents of black cherry, cassis, tobacco and woodspice. The medium tannins lend good grip to the palate, but resolve fairly quickly on the close. Notes of black currant leaf and fruit skin ride out the close. Enjoy now and through 2020.
title: KWV 2015 Roodeberg Red (Western Cape)

description: Underripe strawberry and watermelon tones carry the nose and palate, with Mediterranean shrubs in the background. Medium in weight, with a nice balance of acidity, cranberry-skin tannins offer support from start to finish.
title: Leone de Castris 2016 Five Roses Rosato (Salento)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Jammy red fruit aromas mix with floral and sweet spice undertones on the nose. Medium in weight, with gentle acidity, the palate bursts with red berry flavors and notes of anise and mint.
title: Leone de Castris 2016 Villa Santera  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: A touch shy upon first sniff, this wine opens up more with time to reveal bright aromas of brambly berry, raspberry and cherry, all hit by a decent dusting of cinnamon. The medium-weight palate is straightforward and approachable, with moderate tannins and a mouthwatering finish that leaves the palate refreshed. Drink now.
title: MAN Family Wines 2015 Bosstok Pinotage (Coastal Region)

description: Mango, cantaloupe and lychee shine on the nose, with subtler hints of grass and peach blossom. This wine is simple and pleasant in its flavors of melon, orange zest and fresh cut grass, ending on a juicy orange note.
title: Messina Hof 2016 Riesling Father and Son Cuvée Riesling (Texas)

description: Cantaloupe and stone-fruit aromas of this Trepat-based Cava are clean but basic. That also describes the palate, which is good but a bit soft and low on acidity, with some dry orange flavors.
title: Mont Marçal 2016 Brut Rosado Sparkling (Cava)

description: Principally Riesling, with 8% Muscat blended in, this touches the borders of off dry, but has enough acid to keep it food-friendly. Ripe lemon and pink grapefruit, peaches and apples come through most pleasantly. It's well made and well balanced.
title: Rock Point NV River Riesling-Muscat (Oregon)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Toast, underbrush, licorice and charred-earth aromas carry over to the palate along with prune, blackberry extract, coconut and a confectionary note. Dusty tannins give it a rather grainy finish.
title: Salcheto 2012 Salco  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: A mix of 60% Chardonnay, 15% Moscatello Selvatico, 15% Minutolo and 10% Passulara, this unusual white blend is perfumed in grapefruit, tangerine zest, papaya and lemon balm aromas. Medium in weight, with a pleasing roundness, rich melon and exuberant floral flavors are lifted by clementine acidity and a light mineral dusting.
title: San Marzano 2016 Edda Lei White (Salento)

description: The 1,500-foot elevation of the vineyards close to mountains gives this wine its crisp, bright character, allowing the acidity, the citrus and the zingy texture to shine. The wine is all tight fruit balanced by a creamy texture. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2016 Casa Américo White (Dão)

description: Notes of wet stone, Pilsner and yeast meet with fresher elements of lemon, white pepper and honeydew in this Chardonnay. Sprightly acidity lends itself well to the long, energetic finish.
title: Sunset Hills 2016 Estate Vineyard One Chardonnay (Middleburg)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Though well made and easy drinking, this has an overall lightness that seems almost too thin. Flavors conjure up snippets of lemon rind, pineapple and honeydew melon—fruity but lacking concentration. It's balanced with mineral acidity, and should be enjoyed while at its most fresh.
title: The Four Graces 2016 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Vibrant aromas of blue fruit, dried herb and potpourri lead to light, soft, tart fruit and barrel flavors. Blue-fruit flavors linger on the finish. It seems compact right now but good things are going on.
title: Three of Cups 2015 L'Astre Les Collines Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Outer Coastal Plain, New Jersey, US
description: This Chardonnay opens with alluring aromas of vanilla, lemon curd, dried apricot and pie crust. Lemon-lime acidity pulls together flavors of ripe peach and vanilla, with a hint of wood spice. Lime peel and spice linger on the fruity finish.
title: William Heritage 2016 Pumpkin Hill Vineyard Chardonnay (Outer Coastal Plain)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This pretty wine is lightly peppery, with excellent concentration. The strawberry fruit is bolstered by bright lemon acidity. Although made from 100% Grenache, a small portion (5%) of finished red wine was added to enhance color and tannin.
title: 2Hawk 2016 Grenache Rosé (Rogue Valley)

description: This Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Merlot, Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc blend offers aromas of ripe blackberry and baking spice. The round, soft palate evokes black cherry jam, vanilla and star anise alongside lithe tannins.
title: Argentiera 2016 Ai Ginepri  (Bolgheri)

description: This is a soft, juicy and approachable wine, in full grasp of grapey cherry flavors that veer overtly fruity. Medium bodied, it's a nice choice for the table, indoors or out.
title: August Briggs 2014 Charbono (Calistoga)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This is the winery's first release of Chardonnay, an extreme rarity in this appellation. It comes from the upper Mill Creek area. Delicate, intriguing aromas of cut pear, yellow apple, straw, melon, candle wax and flower lead to full-feeling, lightly creamy banana and pineapple flavors.
title: Bledsoe 2016 Elizabeth Chardonnay (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Aromas of red plum, ground pepper and a whiff of Mediterranean scrub lead the nose. The savory straightforward palate offers ripe black cherry, fig and licorice alongside firm tannins.
title: Bruni 2015 Oltreconfine  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Made with 80% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Sauvignon, 5% Cabernet Franc and 5% Petit Verdot, this has aromas of ripe black plum, vanilla and a touch of caramel. The dense palate offers black-currant jam, sweet exotic spice and a hint of licorice alongside round tannins.
title: Campo al Mare 2016  Bolgheri

description: Cranberry and wild strawberry aromas lead on the nose of this Rosato. Creamy in feel, with a healthy vein of acidity, the palate brings flavors of herbs and watermelon. Soft tannins offer support and help extend the finish.
title: Cantele 2016 Negroamaro Rosato (Salento)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a clonal mix—Pommard, Wädenswil, Dijon 115 and 777—with leathery aromas around candied-fruit flavors. Ginger cookies and intense baking spices dominate, although the leathery character somewhat diminishes the fruit and dampens down the finish.
title: Cardwell Hill 2014 Monet Blocks Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Sticking closely with the winery's style of forward fruity spicy wines, this has a burst of strawberry and raspberry flavors, backed with juicy acids and baking spices. There's a slightly chalky quality to the finish.
title: Cardwell Hill 2014 Pommard Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: The closed nose has an earthy tint. On the palate this gives way to a pithy note of lemon and a bright, sunny freshness. This is light, fresh and has a superclean, dry finish.
title: Cave de Turckheim 2016 Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: This rich wine is juicy with black-currant fruit and crisp with acidity. The wood aging is just on the right side of too much, but shows in the wine's spicy, dry character. The fresh fruit should come through strongly as the wine matures. Drink from 2023.
title: Château d'Aurilhac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This wine comes from a sector of the Entre-deux-Mers that is particularly suited to white wines. It shows in this ripe, lightly mineral-textured wine with apple, citrus and touches of spice. Drink this attractive wine from mid-2018.
title: Château la Rose de Vitrac 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: There are dramatic views from this estate's vineyards over the Garonne river. The setting gives the chance to produce a fine wine from a privileged location. Rich and juicy, this black-fruit-flavored wine is made from organically grown grapes. Drink this ripe wine from late 2018.
title: Château Lauretan 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: This fruity wine is soft, rounded and already shows a good balance between fruit and tannins. It has a lightly dry core but the red-berry flavors and acidity are up front and give this wine its immediate attraction. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Les Trois Manoirs 2015  Médoc

description: This wine comes from the lower vineyards of Château Quintus. It is fruity, soft and with balanced black fruits and tannins. Spice and hints of wood aging add extra flavor to the wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Quintus 2015 Saint-Émilion de Quintus  (Saint-Émilion)

description: You'll find aromas of yellow stone fruit, bread crust and dried chamomile on this vibrant sparkler. The rich linear palate offers Golden Delicious apple and fennel set against bright acidity and a lively perlage. Bitter walnut signals the close.
title: Corte Bianca NV Extra Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This crisp, savory riserva offers light aromas of racy cherry and spiced game. The palate is lithe in feel, with soft tannins wrapped around a tart red-plum flavor. A sanguine note marks the close.
title: Cosimo Taurino 2010 Riserva Red (Salice Salentino)

description: From vines close to Fronsac, this wine comes from Thunevin family vines. It is soft, with attractive black-currant fruit and spicy wood and tannins. It will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Virginie Thunevin 2015  Bordeaux

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This unusual wine is a bright strawberry shade and offers intense aromatics and flavors of strawberries and ripe cherries. It was fermented on the skins for 14 days, and one-quarter of the total was whole cluster. This is a tasty wine with some citrus snap to the finish.
title: Duck Pond 2016 St. Jory Vineyard Skin Contact Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dark strawberry, black plum, fennel pollen, tarragon and a hint of caramel show on the nose of the solid second vintage for this memorably labeled bottle. Ripe purple fruit meets with minty spices on the palate, where the richness is cut by directed acidity.
title: Fatty Pope 2015 Red (Paso Robles)

description: While the nose is ripe and borderline over-the-top in aromas of candied melon, tropical fruit and butter, the palate is unexpectedly fresher, with bright acidity ensconced in a cloak of grainy, textural weight and oak flavors throughout.
title: Fowles Wine 2016 Are You Game? Chardonnay (Victoria)

description: This is a very good, moderate rendering of the varietal, blending good blackberry aromas with lightly smoky peppery flavors and medium to full body. It's firmly tannic and will taste great with juicy meats and indulgent cheesy pastas.
title: French Bar 2015 Petite Sirah (California)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Hints of mint and cedar in the aroma lead to full ripe fruit flavors like black cherry and blueberry in this Merlot-based Bordeaux-style blend. It has full body and moderate tannins that give just enough heft and grip on the palate.
title: Darcie Kent Vineyards 2013 Crown Block Red (Livermore Valley)

description: A traditional Bordeaux-style blend of 42% Cabernet Sauvignon, 29% Merlot, 15% Malbec, 10% Petit Verdot and 4% Cabernet Franc, this is jammy and spicy upfront, boasting assertive fruit notes of boysenberry, mulberry and black plum that are hit by spicy tones of cigar box, leather and allspice. Medium in weight, it offers firm tannins that are partnered with ample acidity, lending lift to the close. Drink now–2021.
title: DeMorgenzon 2015 Maestro Red (Stellenbosch)

description: A perfumed nose of honeysuckle and lemon has just a touch of earthiness about it. The palate is translucent, rounded but light, carrying intense notes of lemon edged by floral perfume. The finish is wonderfully dry.
title: Domaine Bott-Geyl 2015 Les Élements Muscat (Alsace)

description: Lemon, honeysuckle, lime and rose petal aromas unite into a perfumed, appealing nose. The light palate adds a fruity dollop of ripe, yellow plum to these flavors and boosts them with creamy richness. This is a rounded but still dry and fresh Muscat with a wonderfully lasting echo.
title: Domaine Charles Frey 2016 Fruité Croquant Muscat (Alsace)

description: A touch of earthiness and lemon pith play on the quiet nose. The palate is fresh in feel, with an intriguing touch of herbal bitterness. Concentrated and intense, this remains taut nonetheless.
title: Domaine Schoffit 2016 Vieilles Vignes Chasselas (Alsace)

description: Lemon and earth aromas on the nose lead onto a dry and gently pear-fruited palate. There is an element of softness, a creamy touch radiating from a ripe, concentrated core of subdued Mirabelle and pear fruit. The finish is rich, resonant and dry.
title: Domaine Zind-Humbrecht 2015 Zind White (Alsace)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming mostly from esteemed Riverbend Vineyard, this wine saw just 10% new French oak, putting the focus on the fruit. High-toned aromas of dried flower, herb, cherry, vanilla and dried cranberry lead to a full, flavorful palate packed to the brim with dark fruit. Cranberry flavors linger on the finish.
title: Estrin 2013 Cabernet Franc (Wahluke Slope)

description: With a backbone of well-defined acidity, this coastal white is creamy in texture, with highlights of fig, pear and toasted oak. Juicy and likable, it lingers in a vanilla wafer tone.
title: Flowers 2015 Camp Meeting Ridge Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: A bouquet of raspberry, strawberry and Mediterranean herbs preps the palate for flavors of blackberry, cassis, vanilla, lavender and black pepper. It is well integrated, with velvety tannins and spicy finish.
title: Galil Mountain 2016 Merlot (Upper Galilee)

description: This is a well-blended red, combining 73% Merlot, 22% Cabernet Sauvignon, 3% Malbec and 2% Cabernet Franc. Pungent earth and tobacco highlight a soft round palate of dried herb, clove and red and black currant, finishing in cherry-vanilla.
title: Gundlach Bundschu 2016 Mountain Cuvée Red (Sonoma County)
