description: Delicate tangerine and yellow peach flavors are layered in smoke and dusty slate in this sprightly off-dry Riesling. It's a light-footed, piercing wine anchored by nervous lemon-lime acidity. The finish is long and lean, accented by a touch of dried herb.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Steinmetz Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is a friendly, attractive and easy to like bottling. It boasts solid fruit concentration, expressed in waves of raspberry sauce, brambly boysenberry and just a touch of dark chocolate. The medium-weight palate shows a bit more restraint, with herbal tones of raspberry leaf and cherry stem. Drink now–2020.
title: Kasteelberg 2015 Shiraz (Swartland)

description: Crisp and refreshing, this opens with enticing scents of white spring blossom, white stone fruit and a whiff of dried aromatic herb. The bright palate delivers creamy golden apple and juicy nectarine alongside tangy acidity and a lively mousse. A ginger note backs up the finish.
title: Le Marchesine NV Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Classic Rioja aromas of vanilla, tobacco and dried red fruits are on display. In the mouth, this wine has bracing natural acidity that supports ripe flavors of baked berry fruits and brown sugar. Raisiny hints on the finish suggest drinking this sooner rather than later.
title: Marqués de la Concordia 2009 Lagunilla Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: This wine is delightfully smooth as it delivers moderate fruit, butter and almond flavors on a rich-feeling yet light texture. It coats the palate from the first sip through to the mild, lingering finish.
title: Moniker 2016 La Ribera Vineyard Single Vineyard Chardonnay (Mendocino)

description: This opens with delicate aromas suggesting small red berry, pipe tobacco and a whiff of chopped mint. The smooth bright palate delivers crushed strawberry, orange zest, a hint of clove and a smoky mineral note. Buffed tannins and vibrant acidity provide support. Drink over the next few years.
title: Nera 2010 Grumello Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Plump and fleshy yet crisply balanced, this fruity, full-bodied red offers an abundance of ripe blackberry and cherry accented by dark char, bitter chocolate and spice. It's rich and silken on the palate, finishing with ripe, pleasantly drying tannins.
title: Pflüger 2015 Friedelsheimer Trocken St. Laurent (Pfalz)

Appellation: Gioia del Colle, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Sourced from the clay soils of the Montevella Vineyard, this Primitivo sees no time in oak, yet brims with complexities. Bergamot, cedar oil and moist earth aromas ride over a core of dense blackberry. These notes echo on the full-bodied palate, balanced by vibrant acidity and an energizing mineral vein that lingers on and on.
title: Polvanera 2013 17 Vigneto Montevella Primitivo (Gioia del Colle)

description: This ripe, lightly wood-aged wine is made from a blend of Chardonnay and Arinto. Rich while also crisp, the wine is warm, generous and packed with both fruit and minerality. It is still developing, so drink this wine from 2019.
title: Quinta de Chocapalha 2016 Reserva Branco White (Lisboa)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: With its eight months in wood, this wine is opulent, spicy and ripe. It has great fruit as well as plenty of judicious wood-aging flavors. The yellow fruits such as pear are cut with citrus to give a rounded, rich wine that will age further. Drink from late 2018.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2016 Mirabilis Grande Reserva White (Douro)

description: A dark, gritty, spicy nose with aromas of black peppercorn, coffee and black fruits sets up a high-acid palate with lemony oak. Flavors of vanilla and creamy oak dominate, while the finish echoes what came before: oak, creamy notes and lemony acid. Drink now through 2027.
title: Ramón Bilbao 2010 Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Meaty, sanguine flavors greet the straightforward entry to this appellation wine, a showcase of the area's warm climate. Substantial concentration builds along the midpalate, dusted in clove, black cherry and berry.
title: Raymond 2014 District Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Calistoga)

description: Deep violet in the glass, this wine offers aromas of black cherry, strawberry and black pepper. It is bright on the palate, with soft tannins and flavors of tart cherry, cranberry, juniper berry and black pepper that round off in a zippy finish.
title: Recanati 2016 Merlot (Upper Galilee)

description: The nose is very shy and wants to give little away. The palate slowly opens into a taut, elegant and fresh body adorned with flavors of cranberry and red cherry. All is toned down but comes with disarming purity and freshness, resulting in honesty and utter charm.
title: René Muré 2016 Signature Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: This bottling was first produced in 1985 and was the first commercial Chardonnay made in Puglia. Over 30 years later, this is a solid representation of the variety, grown on the limestone hills of the Castel del Monte DOC. Fermented in stainless steel and aged in cement vats, this wine exudes ripeness struck against tense mineral sensations. Juicy peach and honeydew melon flavors are wrapped around a core of crushed rocks. This is silken in feel, yet balanced by driving acidity.
title: Rivera 2016 Preludio N.1 Chardonnay (Castel del Monte)

description: This punchy, fruity kabinett offers loads of ripe pink grapefruit, guava and caramelized sugar notes. Electric acidity refreshes the midpalate leading a long, lean finish. Delicious already but should hold well through 2020.
title: Single Post 2015 Ockfener Bockstein Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a bright fresh take on the grape, lightly spicy and showing a gentle dusting of cinnamon. The freshly cut pear flavors are perfectly ripe and densely packed.
title: Sokol Blosser 2016 Pinot Gris (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Plummy fruits meet with rosewater and tart strawberry on the nose of this bottling by vintner Jason Haas. Against a tannic backbone and chalky texture on the sip, there are punchy flavors of red and purple fruit, bolstered by solid acidity. It is quite easy to quaff.
title: Tablas Creek 2015 Mourvèdre (Adelaida District)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bright, crisp black raspberry and sharp hibiscus tones make for a very pungent, nose-tickling aromatic start to this appellation blend. The palate is also sharp, with mace and allspice, while searing acidity cuts through the strawberry and raspberry flavors.
title: Velvet Bee 2014 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Racy and linear, this lively sparker has delicate aromas of bread crust, citrus blossom and white stone fruit. On the palate, vibrant acidity and a racy perlage underscore candied lemon drop, yellow apple and white peach. A bitter almond note announces the close.
title: Villa Crespia NV Novalia Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas of ripe pear, yellow peach and medicinal herb follow over to the vibrant palate along with yellow apple and tangerine zest. Bright acidity gives it a crisp clean finish.
title: Monte del Frà 2016  Lugana

description: Restrained aromas of crushed mineral, apple and lemon turn ripe and sunny on the palate. Delicately sweet, it's an approachable, briskly balanced wine that finishes on a vibrant tangerine note. Drink now through 2019.
title: Nebel 2016 Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is stylish and youthfully fresh and tasty. Fermented in cement and aged for 10 months in neutral oak, it's got ripe spicy berry and plum flavors, just a touch high toned. Drink now through 2019.
title: Omero 2016 Gamay Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Produced by the winemaking team of Château Giscours in Margaux, this crisply herbal wine has great citrus and ripe yellow fruits and a rich, creamy texture. It is ready to drink.
title: Petite Sirène 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: All the winery's wines are 100% varietal, and this is a solid effort with substantial flavors of mulberry and blackberry fruit. There's a streak of cola and lightly earthy tannins. Flavors are broad and accessible, and the wine finishes with a streak of vanilla.
title: Schmidt 2014 Merlot (Applegate Valley)

description: This is a rich red-fruit-dominated wine. Berries and acidity combine with light tannins to give a wine that bursts from the glass. Enjoy this attractively fruity wine soon.
title: Seacampo 2015 Monte Serra Red (Dão)

description: From the new venture from major producer Sogrape, this wine is soft and juicy, with attractive red fruits. Its acidity and gentle tannins are balanced with the refreshing fruitiness. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Sogrape 2016 Herdade do Peso Trinca Bolotas Red (Alentejo)

description: Aromas of pollen, wildflower honey and peach are enticing on the nose. This off-dry wine is ripe yet balanced, with flavors of orange, peach and juicy mango. The ripe melon finish is charming and clean.
title: St. Julian 2016 Reserve Late Harvest Riesling (Lake Michigan Shore)

description: There's an intriguing levity to the nose of this this wine in its pressed violet, mint and wild strawberry aromas. Medium in weight, with solid tannic grip, the palate offers more of the same, yet ends on a chalky, mineral finish.
title: Tenute Chiaromonte 2015 Elè Primitivo (Puglia)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Rock manager turned winemaker Sheri Hood honors some old-school rockers (the band MC5) with this affordable Pinot. Boysenberry and plum fruits come along with stiff earthy tannins. For the modest price it has plenty of flavor and some muscle as well.
title: The Pressing Plant 2014 Kick Out The Jams Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Barrel aromas are at the fore, with notes of vanilla, dill, toast and baking spice, along with a light meaty note. The flavors show a luscious, velvety feel, with the oak influence running quite high.
title: Watermill 2014 Cabernet Franc (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: This variety is unheard of in the appellation. Quite pale in color with an orange tinge, the aromas bring notes of macerated cranberry and cherry, along with a vanilla note that gives the impression of confection. The palate brings lighter-styled fruit flavors, with the oak taking over on the back half. The tannins are quite light for the variety. It's intriguing but doesn't seem varietal.
title: Watermill 2014 Dugger Creek Vineyard Nebbiolo (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

Appellation: Outer Coastal Plain, New Jersey, US
description: Oaky vanilla and sweet spice overpower apricot and lemon aromas on the nose. A rich vanilla tone leads flavors of mango, apricot, and mandarin on the palate. The mouthfeel is soft, with balancing acidity and a clean vanilla and apricot finish.
title: William Heritage 2016 Estate Reserve Chardonnay (Outer Coastal Plain)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: An austere wine, this does not shy away from flavors of stem, beet root and rhubarb. The fruit touches on strawberry, but does not display the ripe flavors you might anticipate from the listed alcohol. It's high acid, herbal and earthy.
title: Winter's Hill 2015 Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This is a nice, juicy wine that has interesting fruit and earth aromas, good ripe berry flavors and an easy-going texture. It feels medium bodied and tastes not too strong and not too weak.
title: 5 Wine Co. NV Red (California)

Appellation: Yolo County, Central Valley, California, US
description: This full-bodied wine is loaded with aromas of pear nectar and grilled baguette, and very ripe fruit flavors. It has a warmly oaky personality, showing vanilla and coconut in addition to generous pear notes.
title: Black's Station 2016 Chardonnay (Yolo County)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Interesting bay leaf and clove aromas are followed by focused and bright black-cherry flavors and a vivid texture shaped by firm acidity and moderate tannins. It has a lean and lively feel and no oakiness.
title: Boeger 2015 Milagro Red (El Dorado)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of dark fruit, smoked meat and herb lead to plentiful plum flavors that miss a bit in the middle. It comes up short on the finish but otherwise provides appeal.
title: Boomtown 2015 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This opens with aromas of red berry, underbrush, mint and leather. The palate is more straightforward, offering blackberry, powdered sage and hints of baking spice set against bright acidity and dusty, not very persistent tannins.
title: Cantina del Giusto 2014 Baradiero  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Effusive aromas of strawberry and raspberry lead to rich, equally fruity flavors and a velvety texture that balances some tannin with a touch of ripeness. It's a full-bodied, mouth-coating and smooth blend.
title: Caricature 2015 Red (California)

description: Dusty aromas suggest apple. A full, round palate is soda-like and not precise, while this tastes of lightly spiced apple with a dusting of powdered sugar. A short, mellow finish is nondescript.
title: Castellroig NV Brut Sparkling (Cava)

description: The 67% of Cabernet Sauvignon in this wine gives it both richness and a firm structure. It is a solid wine, still showing its oak aging but with juicy fruit and acidity. It will develop as it softens, so drink from 2022.
title: Château Haut Grignon 2015  Médoc

description: Medium straw in color, this wine features aromas of apple and honeysuckle. It is tart at first sip, with flavors of lemon-lime and Gala apple that are slowly overtaken by strong notes of eucalyptus. Fruit flavors return on the finish, offering a closing note of pear.
title: Dalton 2016 Estate Oak Aged Chenin Blanc (Galilee)

description: This wine is tannic and dusty, a big, dense expression of a formidable grape that leers into smoky, dark and brooding territory. Blackberry and cherry drip from an herbal backdrop of intensity.
title: Eagle Eye 2013 Estate Reserve AlphaWolf Vineyard Petit Verdot (Napa Valley)

description: A number of wineries draw upon grapes from this excellent site. Here, the flavors are of cranberries and raspberries, and remain tart and rather acidic. The tannins are stiff and leave a slightly bitter aftertaste.
title: Erath 2015 Bishop Creek Pinot Noir

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Solid, at first seeming to be a bit on the chunky side, this thins out in the mouth, offering tart berry fruit. Along with that come some stemmy components and tannins that are just hitting not-quite-ripe flavors. It's a sound, but not distinguished, estate selection.
title: Erath 2015 Estate Selection Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Unusual aromas of watermelon Jolly Rancher, lime zest and white flower are followed by light flower and peach flavors that finish tart. It doesn't all come together.
title: Eternal 2016 Eternal Sunshine Morrison Lane Vineyard Viognier (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Red berry, toast and underbrush aromas mingle with a whiff of eucalyptus. The tightly wound, austere palate offers sour cherry, espresso and a hint of star anise set against grainy tannins that leave an astringent finish. Drink after 2021.
title: Fattoria Del Cerro 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Deep pink in the glass, this wine has a nose of raspberry and orange zest. It is full on the palate, with flavors of strawberry, tart cherry and Red Delicious apple that culminate in a finish marked by lingering acidity.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2016 Gilgal Rosé (Galilee)

description: A rather neutral nose does not give much away. The palate is in a similarly neutral vein, yet it is clean, fresh and balanced. Drink soon.
title: Gustave Lorentz 2016 Réserve Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A hint of ripe peach joins the rose and honeysuckle notes on the nose. The palate seems to have a smidgeon of sweetness, boosting the rose and peach notes. There also is an earthy, pithy textural grip to the rich palate. This is concentrated fresh but intense with an off-dry finish.
title: Domaine Schoffit 2016 Tradition Muscat (Alsace)

description: Four Bordeaux varieties work as a team in this bottling to deliver aromas of blackberry jam, caramel, roasted cocoa, peppercorn and dark soil. There's great cohesion to the palate, where black cherry, hints of root beer and purple flowers ride buoyant acidity and chalky tannins.
title: Donati Family Vineyard 2014 Claret (Paicines)

description: Pronounced aromas of smoke and struck flint dissipate into zesty tangerine, grapefruit and lime on the palate of this juicy, lip smacking kabinett. Delicately sweet but impeccably balanced in acidity, it's a spry, revitalizing wine to enjoy now though 2021.
title: Dr. Fischer 2016 Bockstein Ockfen Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Waxy apple skins, green-apple and lime tones meld gorgeously in this medium-bodied but texturally rich Riesling. Dry in style, it's refreshingly brisk, with a lingering lime-pith finish. Drink now through 2025.
title: Dr. Nägler 2016 Steinkaut Rüdesheimer Trocken Erste Lage Riesling (Rheingau)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas are pitch black, with notes of blackberry, black cherry, vanilla and anise, showing the warmth of the vintage. The palate is rich and ripe, packing a big punch of dark-fruit flavors.
title: Dusted Valley 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This is mostly Riverbend Vineyard fruit topped off with Red Mountain Vineyard Merlot (7%). Herb, cranberry and cherry aromas are followed by rich, full-bodied black-fruit flavors. It brings richness and intensity but stays in balance.
title: Estrin 2012 Cabernet Franc (Wahluke Slope)

description: Hints of smoke and struck flint lend a deep mineral undertone to crisp green apple, gooseberry and lime in this dry, full-bodied Riesling. It's satisfyingly rich in mouthfeel yet vivaciously spry. The lemon-lime finish is lean and lingering.
title: Fitz-Ritter 2016 Dürkheimer Abtsfronhof Trocken Riesling (Pfalz)

Appellation: Primitivo del Salento, Southern Italy, Italy
description: This iconic Primitivo from winemaker Gianfranco Fino delivers in its dense aromas of spiced cranberry, blackberry, violets and tilled earth. There's weight and intensity to the palate in all aspects, from the punchy, juicy berry and dark plum flavors to the racing acidity and bracing tannins. Time in the cellar to soften the sharp edges will reward with the already developing complexities. Drink from 2022.
title: Gianfranco Fino 2015 Es  (Primitivo del Salento)

description: A blend of 80% Cabernet Sauvignon, 10% Merlot and 10% Cabernet Franc, this wine opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, bell pepper and cedar. The concentrated palate offers blackberry jam, clove and licorice alongside solid, fine-grained tannins. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Guado al Melo 2015 Atis  (Bolgheri Superiore)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The opening scents bring traces of citrus, leather and vanilla, and such flavors are subtly conveyed in the mouth. Compact red berries with dried Italian herbs distinguish the midpalate, and the tannins are substantial and firm up the finish.
title: Hamacher 2013 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Aromas of blackberry, apple peel and juniper berry set the stage for slightly tart flavors of cherry, pomegranate and eucalyptus. Soft tannins and bright fruit glide across the palate, making way for a cranberry and mint finish.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Lyrica Meritage (Galilee)

description: Perfumed Muscadelle gives a great lift to this wine with its smooth texture and crisp lemon and apple flavors. Touches of toast add spice. It is a wine with great fruitiness just hinting at minerality. Drink from late 2018.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Le Nardian  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: A wonderfully subtle but pure lemon note rises from the glass, accompanied by a hint of jasmine. The light palate that follows is also bathed in lemon and summer blossom notes, but in an understated, refreshing way. The finish is whistle-clean and dry.
title: Jean-Baptiste Adam 2016 Réserve Muscat (Alsace)

description: This Pinotage-based blend, with 34% Cabernet Sauvignon and 12% Merlot, is fruit-loaded right from the start. A smoky overlay sits atop the core of ripe blackberry, plum and boysenberry fruit. The fruity richness dominates the medium-weight palate as well, with secondary notes of earth, sweet smoke, licorice and cocoa that unfold on the finish. Drink now–2020.
title: Kanonkop 2015 Kadette Red (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Tasted a few months ahead of its official release, this is a bright, fresh mix of cranberry, raspberry and cherry fruit. The flavors are crisp and clear, and the mouthfeel is already supple, with citrus highlights. It should be drinking quite nicely by the back half of this year.
title: Laelaps 2016 Nemarniki Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Bread dough, orange zest, pressed wildflower and white stone-fruit aromas mingle in the glass together with a whiff of ginger. The fresh palate reflects the nose, offering yellow apple, Bartlett pear, citrus and toasted hazelnut set against bright acidity and a lively perlage.
title: Lantieri NV Extra Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas of medicinal herb, bread crust and wild cherry follow over to the lively palate along with pink grapefruit, thyme and mineral. Bright acidity and an invigorating perlage accompany the bright flavors while a note of bitter walnut marks the close.
title: Le Marchesine 2013 Blanc de Noir Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: An earthy, mellow and ripe notion of pear is foremost on the nose, joined by pleasantly spiky lemon zestiness. It is the lemon freshness that takes the lead on the slender, dry palate. This is clean, vivid and moreish.
title: Maurice Schoech & Fils 2016 Côtes d'Ammerschwihr White (Alsace)

description: From Pommard and Wädenswil clones, this tart and tasty wine brings cranberry and raspberry fruit, lively and acid-driven. Highlights of lemon curd and orange marmalade add further interest, and it's a clean and refreshing wine all the way through the finish.
title: Merriman 2014 Estate Pinot Noir

description: Bright, clean aromas of apple flesh, green melon, quinine, lanolin and straw lead the charge on the nose of this precise wine. Medium in weight, vibrant acidity and a slightly pithy texture frame the ripe citrus and apple tones, while the finish returns to a pleasantly herbal, grassy impression. Drink now–2022.
title: Momento 2015 White (Western Cape)

description: Zippy and fresh, this dry, medium-bodied Weissburgunder, or Pinot Blanc, offers loads of pristine apple and tangerine-peel flavors. It's concentrated yet sprightly in mouthfeel and finishes with lingering complexities of sea salt and smoky nuts. Drink now through 2022.
title: Pflüger 2016 Quarzit Trocken Weissburgunder (Pfalz)

description: Made with 60% Sangiovese, 20% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Sauvignon, this offers aromas of black currant, underbrush and green peppercorn. The juicy palate offers dried black cherry, tobacco and ground black pepper alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Rocca di Frassinello 2015  Maremma Toscana

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This cuvée has a bit less new oak than its companions and seems a tad less ripe, though certainly ripe enough. There's a tart streak of pineapple and orange under firm red-currant fruit. A light hint of char and chocolate wraps up the finish.
title: Saffron Fields Vineyard 2015 Spectrum Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Southern Oregon, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: A real success, in part because the alcohol has been kept in check, this palate-pleaser brings flavors of cranberry sauce, dried orange peel, clove and dark chocolate. It's balanced and bright, forward and chockful of pretty spices.
title: Schmidt 2014 Touriga Nacional (Southern Oregon)

description: This is currently a bit shy and reserved, begging for a bit more time to better express itself. Notes of black cherry and currant are hit by accents of espresso powder and baking spice, white the palate offers a slightly jammier impression that lingers through the medium-length finish. The tannins are structured and youthful, yet resolve themselves somewhat easily alongside the ripe fruit tones. Give this a couple of years; try after 2020.
title: Trizanne 2015 Reserve Syrah (Elim)

description: Hints of honey and orange blossom soften cutting lime and lemon flavors in this medium-bodied Riesling. Dry in style, it's a zesty, intensely thirst quenching wine, with a long finish fringed by bitter, waxy lime-pith complexities. Drink now through 2020.
title: Wittmann 2016 100 Hills Dry Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Lush aromatics lead into a complex wine with a compelling and rich play of pear, peach, nectarine, guava and papaya. A touch beyond bone dry at 7 g/L residual sugar, this blend of Viognier, Marsanne and Roussanne is a lovely break from the typical white wines of the region.
title: 2Hawk NV Plume Blanc v.1 White (Rogue Valley)

description: This Touriga Nacional is sourced from vineyards close to the Atlantic Ocean and as a result it gets a touch of saltiness to its rich tannins. The wine has power and density, with rich black fruits and violet tones. It is still young and will be best from 2019.
title: Adega Mãe 2014 Touriga Nacional (Lisboa)

description: Minerally aromas are balanced by light, elegant fruit scents akin to applesauce and pear. This feels refined on a palate that's only slightly pulpy. A fruit-bowl's worth of stylish citrus and apple flavors are lasting on the finish. Drink now.
title: Adegas Gran Vinum 2016 Esencia Diviña Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: A subtle note of ripe, red cherry plays on the nose, along with the merest hint of conifer that becomes more vivid on the palate. The body is slender, light and unforced. This is a very honest, easy and traditional Pinot, made with an admirably light touch.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A young vineyard has been planted with this old-school clone, which has a broad open mouthfeel, though it's not as concentrated as the Dijon clones. Strawberry and cherry fruit dominates, touched with orange peel and finishing with a lick of almond paste.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Nicholas Vineyard Wädenswil Clone Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Chunky black-fruit aromas are in the overripe zone, with a smoky offset. A raw palate is bolstered by citrusy acidity that creates a screechy mouthfeel. Flavors of plum skins and orange peel are good.
title: Bodegas Vínícola Real 2009 200 Monges Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Voluptuous, slightly overripe notes of yellow peach, pineapple and red berry are accented by caramel and smoke in this off-dry kabinett. High-toned acidity lends balance to the midpalate leading to a refreshingly mineral finish.
title: Bollig-Lehnert 2016 Trittenheimer Apotheke Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Lovely freshness of cut Conference pear rises from the glass. The rounded palate offers more fruit yet is maintained by a fresh vein of acidity.
title: Bott Frères 2016 Tradition Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese and 10% Alicante, this presents aromas of red-skinned berry, new leather and baking spice. The juicy easydrinking palate offers raspberry jam, tobacco and cinnamon alongside polished tannins.
title: Casteani 2013 Sessanta  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Slightly sauced red berry aromas meld with earth and floral tones on the nose. There's a smooth feel to the medium-bodied palate, with sandy, fine-grained tannins and zesty acidity housing flavors of tilled earth and racy red fruit. A lean graphite edge runs from start to finish, keeping this focused and perfectly food friendly.
title: Castello Monaci 2014 Aiace Riserva Red (Salice Salentino)

description: Beautifully aromatic hints of Williams pear play on the nose. The palate stays in the same vein, yet ultimately this is easy, friendly and very appetizing. Drink soon.
title: Cave du Roi Dagobert 2016 Tradition Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Very appetizing notes of ripe, juicy and aromatic pear are immediately apparent. The juicy palate frames these lively fruit notes with great freshness. The finish is a little warm but dry and refreshing.
title: Charles Sparr 2016 Tradition Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Ripe and fruity, this is becoming a delicious wine. The black-currant fruits from the Cabernet Sauvignon give both richness and crispness. With good acidity and a touch of wood, the wine will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Château Laride 2014  Haut-Médoc

description: This well-balanced wine offers a great mix of tannins and juicy fruit, It is rich and likely to be delicious as it matures, conveying weight as well as blackberry flavors and acidity in abundance. The wine needs to age over the medium term. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Lassus 2015  Médoc

description: Produced from organically grown grapes, still a rare concept in the Médoc, this wine from vineyards close to Margaux is stylish, fruity and elegant. Dry tannins form part of a juicy wine with blackberry fruits. Likely to age well over the next few years, it will be ready to drink from 2023.
title: Château Meyre 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This 60-acre estate has produced a spicy, wood-laden wine. Rich fruit saves the wine from being overwhelmed by the toastiness and gives it the necessary weight. It will need time, so drink this wine from 2023.
title: Château Pierre de Montignac 2015  Médoc

description: This is a structured, dense wine produced in the southern Haut-Médoc, close to Bordeaux. It is packed with dry tannins that give an austere character. The weight hints at the black-currant fruit to come. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Saint Ahon 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Pale straw to the eye, this wine has aromas of pear and vanilla. It is bright from first sip through the grapefruit finish, while also offering a sense of creaminess on the palate among flavors of green apple and peach, with a touch of vanilla custard.
title: Dalton 2016 Alma Ivory White (Galilee)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Tangy fruits of pomegranate, strawberry and orange rind meet with dried ginger and Christmas spice on the nose of this bottling. It's light and easy on the palate, sailing through with bright red fruits and hints of herbs.
title: Di Bruno 2013 Sangiovese (Santa Barbara County)

description: An earthy, almost savory hint of soy plays amidst the lemon freshness on the nose. The light, dry palate has a soft, creamy aspect and adds a notion of pear fruit. There is some alcoholic heat on the finish.
title: Domaine Bott-Geyl 2015 Points Cardinaux Métiss White (Alsace)

description: Just a touch of Conference pear hits the nose which appears in the same, subtle way on the dry palate. The body is slender and fresh while the finish is clean and moreish.
title: Domaine Sipp-Mack 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Tones of cereal and brioche ride over layers of lemon curd, overripe yellow apples and nuts. Mouthwatering acidity offers a lift to the weight, while the finish is long and racy.
title: Early Mountain 2015 Quaker Run Vineyard Chardonnay (Virginia)

description: This familiar brand exhibits the lush Fonseca style. Tannins are encased in generous red fruits, giving a wine that is smooth with just a touch of structure. This perfumed wine is ready to drink.
title: Fonseca NV Bin 27 Finest Reserve  (Port)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Tart and racy, this brings juicy lip-licking berry flavors front and center. The high acidity may be over the top for some tasters, but set it up with a buttery poultry dish or a creamy sauce over pork or lamb and you're in the comfort zone.
title: Foris 2014 Maple Ranch Pinot Noir (Rogue Valley)

description: This is a well-made approachable wine, aromatic in black cherry, cola and plum. Thick ripe flavors are highlighted in brick, black pepper and toasted oak.
title: Franciscan 2014 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: A ripe, fully oaked nose includes charred aromas along with herbal plum and currant scents. This wine has so much pep on the palate that it feels a touch raw, with a resiny oak offset. Woody plum and raspberry flavors finish with a final blast of oak.
title: Izadi 2014 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: The nose wants to give little away apart from a lemony hint of freshness. The palate is slender and dry, displaying a touch of apple and pear notes. This is easy, friendly and light. Drink soon.
title: Jean-Baptiste Adam 2016 Vieilles Vignes Auxerrois (Alsace)

description: Spicy aromas of anise seed, licorice and black pepper are set against a blackberry backdrop. The mellow palate offers vanilla, blackberry and pepper, with crushed velvet tannins balanced by a fresh vein of acidity. Notes of white pepper and berries mark the finish.
title: Laurentia 2015 Stoltz Block Cabernet Sauvignon (Grand River Valley)

description: Broad apple, lemon and tangerine flavors meld on the palate of this rounded, richly textured spätlese. It's juicy and honeyed but briskly balanced by a delicate fringe of astringency on the finish. Drink now through 2021.
title: Louis Guntrum 2015 Nierstein Rehbach Spätlese Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Savory herb and game aromas ride over a core of spiced cranberry and cherry in this wine. There's nice weight and tension, offering a balance of juicy red fruit flavors, earthy tones and lively acidity. Medium-grained tannins lend plenty of support.
title: Masseria Altemura 2014 Altemura  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: There is a distinctly sweet edge to this wine, suggesting that some residual sugar remains. Flavors and scents show honey and peach, with a hint of apple tart as well. Though made entirely in stainless steel, there's a light streak of caramel in the finish.
title: Maysara 2016 Autees Pinot Blanc (McMinnville)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Here is a well-balanced, lightly off dry style, with an emphasis on lemon-drop flavors. The residual sugar, listed at 20 g/l, shows as a bit of cotton-candy character. The wine is nicely concentrated and finishes with a streak of breakfast tea.
title: Melrose 2016 Riesling (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Fair Play, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine oozes fresh fruity, floral aromas and has light bright flavors to match. Touches of honey and orange come out on the palate, where the texture feels just right between a touch of richness and liveliness.
title: Mount Aukum 2015 Viognier (Fair Play)

description: Dark baking spice, wild berry and a whiff of pressed rose form a delicate bouquet. The light-bodied easygoing palate offers strawberry, pomegranate and a hint of tobacco alongside vibrant acidity and lithe tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: Nino Negri 2014 Quadrio  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: From one of the premium vineyards in the Lisboa region, this wine is made from some of the oldest vines in the estate. Its rich character and spice from wood aging are still young although they show ripe promise. Textured and mineral, it will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Quinta de Chocapalha 2016 CH by Chocapalha White (Lisboa)

description: From a blend that includes several local white grape varieties, this wood-aged wine has attractive soft fruit and a smooth texture. Touches of spice from the wood aging are well integrated with the orange and lemon-zest flavors and crisp apples. Drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Quinta de la Rosa 2016 La Rosa Reserva Branco White (Douro)

description: Pretty and fruit-forward, this leads with attractive aromas of ripe dark berries and bramble that are hit by a good dose of tobacco leaf and purple flowers. The palate is juicy and lush, but not overdone, with tasty plum and berry flavors that are supported by fine tannins and a spicy, oaky overlay. Drink now–2020.
title: Rainbow's End 2015 Estate Wine Cabernet Franc (Stellenbosch)

description: White stone fruit, toast and bread crust aromas follow over to the vibrant palate along with yellow apple, fennel and bitter walnut. Bright acidity and a lively perlage provide the backdrop.
title: Ricci Curbastro 2013 Extra Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: White wild flower, orchard fruit and a whiff of nut lead the nose. Reflecting the aromas, the bright palate offers yellow apple, lemon zest, graphite and toasted nut alongside vibrant acidity and a lively refined mousse.
title: Ricci Curbastro 2013 Satèn  (Franciacorta)

description: This Pinot is forward and fruity, bursting with lush flavors of ripe plum and maraschino cherry. A thin vein of dark chocolate threads through the tannins. The length and structure suggest that this can be aged another half decade or longer.
title: Rock Point 2016 Pinot Noir (Oregon)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Pipe tobacco, wild berry and star anise aromas set the tone on this light-bodied but savory red. The bright, almost ethereal palate reflects the nose, offering Marasca cherry, licorice and a hint of graphite framed in polished tannins.
title: Salcheto 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Also known as Mencia in Spain, this grape has established itself in the Dão region in Portugal. It makes a soft wine, low in acidity while rich in black fruits. It has plenty of almost-sweet fruit, ripe tannins and a rounded, smooth aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2014 Casa Américo Jaen (Dão)

Appellation: North Coast, North Coast, California, US
description: Deliciously dry and mouthfilling, this full-bodied red packs in black cherry, boysenberry and mint flavors and wraps them in a velvety, softly tannic texture. Made from Merlot, Petite Sirah, Zinfandel and Petit Verdot, it has wonderful ripeness and richness.
title: Sean Minor 2015 Nicole Marie Red (North Coast)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a pretty, even sexy style, that matches its crisp green apple core to lightly buttery barrel toast. It's a classic combination, well executed, not too ripe, but fresh, clean and quite tasty.
title: Sokol Blosser 2016 Chardonnay (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is thick in youthful tannin and earthy intrigue—an explosive, spicy and exotic expression of the variety that's all in. Wild strawberry, pine, clove and black tea intermingle with purpose and distinct power.
title: Talisman 2014 Starscape Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Underbrush, chopped herb, baked plum and a whiff of new leather aromas take shape in the glass. The taut dense palate offers prune, blackberry extract and licorice framed in tightly knit close-grained tannins that give the finish a grip.
title: Talosa 2013 Filai Lunghi  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of ripe plum, cake spice and menthol make their way to the forefront. On the full-bodied palate, taut fine-grained tannins support dried black cherry, prune and mocha. Fresh acidity lifts the brooding flavors.
title: Talosa 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: A nicely oaked bouquet includes aromas of tropical fruits, melon and green banana. This has a full, creamy palate, with oak resin and acidity balancing each other out. Salty melon and banana flavors are oaky, while this is texturally pleasing on the finish.
title: Valserrano 2016 Blanco White (Rioja)

description: Aromas of white stone fruit, bread crust and a whiff of aromatic herb float from the glass in this soft sparkler. The creamy palate doles out ripe yellow apple, white peach, toatsted hazelnut and a hint of ginger alongside a silky mousse.
title: Vigna Dorata 2012 Satèn Millesimato  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Spicy berry aromas come with a bell-pepper note. In the mouth, this wine is raw and somewhat abrasive. Plum and raspberry flavors are lightly herbal, while this is distinctly tannic, raw and tough on the finish. This quality wine begs for food.
title: Viña Vilano 2014 Legón Crianza  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a block selection and the highest level effort of the winery's three Pinot Gris. Fermentation was carried out in both barrels and tanks, and that adds some welcome roundness and flesh to the midpalate. Citrus, cucumber and apple flavors come lightly touched with barrel toast. Drink now through 2021.
title: Winter's Hill 2016 Reserve Pinot Gris (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The winery has re-released this wine, providing a close look at how a properly-stored Pinot can age. In this instance, quite gracefully, and now drinking at a pleasing balance between youthful fruit and the autumnal flavors of older wines. Spicy cranberries and flavors of mellow baked berries and cherries come along with a streak of cola. It should drink well for another four or five years.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2010 Dundee Hills Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: An extremely pale, orange-tinged color belies an aromatically expressive wine, with appealing notes of white pepper, wild raspberry and herb. The palate is sumptuous yet light in feel, with achingly pure fruit flavors. This wine is all about elegance—perhaps too much for some tastes. But for those seeking the variety's delicate side, look no further. Drink now.
title: Descendant 2013 Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The nose is a subtle combination of the merest touch of cinnamon and red cherry. The palate is light and firm, with ample freshness and a lovely impression of tart fruit. This is understated and fresh in a savory style.
title: Domaine Bott-Geyl 2013 Galets Oligocène Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Plumes of saffron, spice and crushed earth accent ripe yellow-cherry and honeysuckle notes in this deeply honeyed spätlese. Equal parts sun-kissed fruit and earth offset by dazzling lemon-lime acidity, it's a deftly balanced wine that offers a pleasantly astringent finish.
title: Dr. Fischer 2016 Bockstein Ockfen Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Forward, fruity tones of peach candy and tangerine stand in contrast to this producer's leaner, earthier Abtsfronhof bottling. They share the same deeply embedded minerality that lends elegance and structure to this dry but approachable wine. While frisky and spry on the midpalate, the finish is long and lean.
title: Fitz-Ritter 2016 Dürkheimer Riesling (Pfalz)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is a well-made vineyard-designate, showing a full embrace of integrated oak and full-bodied richness. Red fruit, leather, meat and black licorice all figure into the mix, as the thickness in the glass suggests decanting or time to air.
title: Francis Coppola 2014 Bevil-Wolcott Vineyard Reserve Petite Sirah (Dry Creek Valley)

description: This crisp lively sparkler delivers aromas and flavors of yellow apple, juicy citrus zest, bread crust and savory mineral. It's balanced, with bright acidity while a refined continuous perlage lends finesse.
title: Fratelli Berlucchi 2013 Fraccianera Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: There are inviting aromas of dark-skinned berry, bay leaf and a hint of ginger in this 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Merlot blend. The savory, balanced palate delivers ripe blackberry, star anise and a touch of espresso alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Guado al Melo 2016 Rute  (Bolgheri)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Rich and fruity, this is a lovely, satisfying wine that's medium bodied and salty in oak. Asian five spice and cola dominate alongside black pepper and cinnamon, the dark cherry and strawberry dense and balanced.
title: Hawley 2015 Oehlman Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Thick and dusty in leathery tannins, this is a robust, well-built wine, exhibiting a wealth of varietal character, from cedar and pencil to black currant and cranberry. It finishes on lingering impressions of bright acidity and spice.
title: Heffernan 2013 VV Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A limited (two barrel) vineyard selection, this offers mixed citrus and berry fruit, principally oranges and raspberries. It's set up by refreshing natural acidity, and finished with a broad streak of milk chocolate. All in all it's ready for near-term enjoyment and medium-term aging.
title: Helioterra 2015 Björnson Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Not to be confused with the winery's Areté reserve version of this same wine, this offers considerable pleasure at a lower cost. The steely core of ripe tree fruits is cloaked in pretty toast. A classic combination for many domestic Chardonnays, here supported with Oregon's exceptional natural acidity. In the resonant finish you'll note a hint of honeycomb.
title: Iris Vineyards 2015 Chalice Estate Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)

description: Ripe, soft and fruity, this is a delicious, accessible wine. It has red-berry fruits, soft integrated tannins and ripe fruitiness. This wine will develop quickly and easily. Drink from 2019.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Château Teyssier  (Saint-Émilion)

Designation: Hacienda Lopez de Haro Gran Reserva
description: Earthy plum and cherry aromas are typical of Rioja gran reserva. A full, grabby palate is not silky-smooth, while this wine tastes of fig and chocolaty berry fruits that are on the cusp of cooked. It loses steam on a finish with raisiny notes. Drink through 2022.
title: Bodega Classica 2009 Hacienda Lopez de Haro Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Heady aromas suggesting grilled herb, sea breeze, wild berry and eucalyptus lead the nose. Made with Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah and Petit Verdot, the tightly knit palate offers dried black cherry, red currant, white pepper and star anise framed in taut, fine-grained tannins.
title: Campo alle Comete 2015 Rosso Stupore  (Bolgheri)

description: Under the same management as Château la Dominique in Saint-Émilion, this wine, dominated by ripe Merlot, is full, open and developing quickly. There's a rich edge to the black fruits as well as some powerful tannins that will need to soften. It is an open, generous wine, full of fruit and richly structured. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Clément-Pichon 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Dating to before the French Revolution, this estate produces some rich, generous wines. This vintage is in that mold, with its rounded black fruits, crisp acidity and solid core of dry tannins. The structure will allow the wine to age, giving great potential. Drink from 2024.
title: Château de Panigon 2015  Médoc

description: Under the same ownership as Château Cissac, this ancient estate, dating to the 6th century, is producing some fine wines. This rich vintage is well structured, dense and with classic black-currant Cabernet Sauvignon flavors. Ripe Merlot adds its rich jamminess to a wine that is still young. Drink from 2024.
title: Château du Breuil 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: With over 80% Cabernet Sauvignon in the this Kopp family blend, this wine is packed with black currants, ripe tannins and a smoky backdrop from the wood aging. It is structured, dry and juicy at the same time. With this firm core, the wine will need aging, so drink from 2024.
title: Château du Retout 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Aged for a year in wood, this wine has a toasty character. At the same time, it also has good fruit, plenty of black-currant flavors and acidity. A well-wrought wine, it melds the tannins and fruit into a balanced whole. Drink from 2024.
title: Château La Branne 2015  Médoc

description: Owned by the Pagès family, this rich wine is dense and structured. It has layers of dusty tannins and full black-currant fruit from the 60% Cabernet Sauvignon in the blend. Drink this firm wine with great fruit from 2023.
title: Château la Valière 2015  Médoc

description: This ripe, impressively textured wine is going to be rich and generous. Not just yet, because the tannins need to soften and integrate. That said, the blackberry fruits are juicy, with balanced acidity and a full texture. Drink this wine from 2022.
title: Château Paveil de Luze 2015  Margaux

description: Just to the north of Saint-Estèphe, this 44-acre estate regularly produces good, structured wines. In this vintage, the structure is right there along with layers of rich blackberry fruits. This is a solid, dense wine, ripe and ready to age well. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Tour Saint-Joseph 2015  Haut-Médoc

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Rich black cherry, clove, gingerbread and nose-tickling Christmas spices show on the rich nose of this appellation blend. It's also rich on the tip of the sip, with dried black cherry and more clove, but there is a black plum skin tang that holds it in check.
title: Cordant 2015 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Bright and focused raspberry fruit comes buttressed by a firm streak of Mediterranean herbs. Hints of iron thread through the finish. This is a taut substantial wine that will require some breathing time to show its best.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2013 Dundee Hills Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This lush and lovely wine, a New World version of Muscadet, is tangy and loaded with bright apple and pear flavors. Amplified by hints of citrus rind, it opens up even further once it warms in the glass, showing unexpected and quite impressive depth and detail.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2015 D.F.B. Melon de Bourgogne (Dundee Hills)

description: A very gentle touch of summer blossom is joined by fresh, bright lemon on the nose. The palate has a creamy element that enriches the bright lemon palate without adding heaviness. This is sprightly and light, but also creamy and mellow. A dry, fresh and totally convincing wine with a clean, long finish.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 L'éclat Ad Quadratum White (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of baking spice, blackberry and cherry draw you into the glass. The palate is chock full of cherry and dark-chocolate flavors, with the tannins giving a light squeeze.
title: Efeste 2014 Final-Final Cabernet Sauvignon-Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Ancient Lakes, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas pop, with notes of clarified butter, pineapple, candlewax, corn silk, apricot and mango. The palate is full of stone and tropical fruit flavors that bring a big yum factor. Aromas and flavors are ripe, like the vintage this wine comes from.
title: Efeste 2015 Lola Evergreen Vineyard Chardonnay (Ancient Lakes)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is a velvety thick, delightfully lush and seductive wine, savory in pine forest and clove, with layered highlights of orange peel, cinnamon and black tea. Dark cherry cobbler provides rich flavor that remains balanced in the glass.
title: Eric Kent 2014 Stiling Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Plump with ripe black-plum and cherry flavors, this single-vineyard wine offers a greater density of fruit than the producer's entry-level Pinot Noir. Still, it shares a ghostly elegance marked by faded whiffs of dried violet and lavender, dusty spice-box and earth notes. Vibrant acidity lends finesse to the palate along with a meandering finish of fine grained, bitter tannins.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2014 Kestener Paulinsberg Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Mosel)

description: This wine features a bouquet of raspberry, black cherry and violet. It is smooth on the palate, with flavors of blackberry, cherry, pomegranate, vanilla and almond blossom. Cool minty notes come into play towards the finish, followed by lingering brightness.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Genesis Merlot (Galilee)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Excellent concentration and pinpoint focus are hallmarks at this property. Tart cranberry and raspberry fruit comes with a streak of dark chocolate and polished tannins. The focus and detail are exceptional.
title: Helioterra 2014 Vintner's Select Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Shades of subtle oak, fig and lemongrass are framed by tightly wound acidity on the palate. Light in feel, this is a bright, exotic Chardonnay with loads of energy.
title: Hendry 2015 Barrel Fermented Chardonnay (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A gentle mix of red berry, red plum and cherry carries some of the lightly earthy tones of the AVA. Soft and accessible, there's a hint of Dr. Pepper running through the flavors also. It's drinking right about at its prime and should hold through the early 2020s.
title: Holloran 2013 La Chenaie Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
Designation: Not One iOTA Pelos Sandberg Vineyard
description: Here is a tasty chewy full-flavored effort, loaded with black-cherry fruit. There are notes of cola and brown sugar, along with a finishing hint of coffee. It was aged for 11 months in just 7% new French oak.
title: Iota Cellars 2014 Not One iOTA Pelos Sandberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: This showcases a nice balance between ripe-fruit notes, toasty warmth and ample acidity. Aromas of lemon custard, creamed pear and baked apple set the stage, framed by scents of toasted walnuts and clover honey. The palate offers a bit more restraint and verve, with notes of orange and lime peel that refresh the sip. Drink now–2023.
title: Ken Forrester 2015 The FMC Chenin Blanc (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Young, big and pleasantly chunky, this flavorful wine is loaded with green apple, tart melon and enough acidity to conjure up gooseberries. Enjoy it now for its exceptional fruit and freshness.
title: King Estate 2016 Backbone Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Fruity aromas of red berry unite with dark culinary spice and fragrant blue flower. On the soft juicy palate, supple tannins lithely support fleshy Marasca cherry, crushed blackberry and ground pepper. Drink through 2021.
title: La Braccesca 2015  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: White flower, yellow orchard fruit and brioche aromas carry through to the elegantly sparkling palate together with yellow apple, white peach and tangerine zest. Tangy acidity lifts the rich flavors while a creamy mousse gives it a silky texture.
title: Majolini 2009 Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This opens with aromas of dark-skinned berry, violet and leather. The savory balanced palate doles out raspberry jam, black cherry, tobacco and black pepper alongside fine-grained tannins and bright acidity. Enjoy through 2027.
title: Mamete Prevostini 2013 Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: Savory, mossy plum and currant aromas are mature and woodsy. High acidity is the lifeblood of this gran reserva that tastes of reedy, leafy currant and herbs. The finish is dry, with a note of tobacco. Drink through 2026.
title: Marqués de la Concordia 2008 Viña Alarde Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Earthy black-fruit aromas open this fully built crianza. A tannic palate with cutting lemony acidity gives this a ton of energy. Berry and cassis flavors are equaled by oaky vanilla and café-mocha notes, while a coconut flavor and heat come up on the finish. Drink through 2026.
title: Martín Berdugo 2014 Crianza  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of cherry cough syrup and herb bring a light volatility. The black-cherry and licorice flavors are light and appealing but can't make up the difference.
title: J & J 2014 Middleton Petty Vineyard Merlot-Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This vineyard sits high on the ridge crest of the SeVein project in the southern end of the valley. Aromas of herb, green tea and purple fruit are followed by tart, plump fruit flavors backed by tacky-feeling tannins.
title: J & J 2015 Summit View Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Overtly fruity aromas of ripe pear and lemon have an almost tropical element about them. These fruit tones remain abundant on a light, easy body. Drink soon while aromas are at their peak.
title: Jean Biecher & Fils 2016 Reserve Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A touch of ripe pear appears on the nose. The palate adds lemon freshness to the pear flavor and comes together in a balanced, fruity and dry body. Drink soon.
title: Joseph Fritsch 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Aromas of honeysuckle and peach blossom lead the nose. Made with organically farmed Vermentino grapes, the lively palate offers yellow peach and lemon alongside bright acidity.
title: La Selva 2016 Vermentino (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This is a beefy wine with interesting components that suggest dried blood, compost, black cherry and cola. It's front loaded and flavorful, but turns a bit stemmy and tannic through the finish.
title: Ledger David 2014 Sangiovese (Rogue Valley)

description: This blend of 90% Negroamaro and 10% Malvasia Nera is bright in red currant and red cherry aromas, with dustings of saw dust and anise. Medium in weight and displaying lively acidity, the palate is racy and streamlined in crisp red-fruit flavors under a layer of vanilla.
title: Leone de Castris 2015 50th Vintage Riserva Red (Salice Salentino)

description: Apple peel, grapefruit and riper tropical fruit notions meld with a floral background in this light-bodied, crisp wine. The concentration is light, yet what's there is quite enjoyable.
title: Masseria Altemura 2016 Falanghina (Salento)

description: Delicate hints of yellow cherry and apple sweeten this uncomplicated but fresh wine. Off dry in style, it's pleasantly sweet and tart on a zesty lemonade finish. Drink now.
title: Nein Lives 2016 Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Ripe red fruits combine into a pleasant, though generic style. There's a touch of earth underlying the tannins, and a high-toned edge to the aromatics.
title: Omero 2016 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: From the more experimental side of the Craft Wine Company, this rosé blends St. Laurent, Zweigelt, Blaufränkisch and Grüner Veltliner. The result is a pretty but somewhat dilute rosé, with thinnish raspberry fruit, rosewater and a touch of neutral wood. It's fully mature and should be consumed in the near term.
title: Origin 2016 Omero Vineyard Rosé (Ribbon Ridge)

description: Crisp raspberry and red cherry flavors are invigorating and fresh on the palate of this easy-drinking, juicy Pinot Noir. It's delicate in concentration and light in body but balanced by soft but pleasantly bitter tannins. A hint of vanilla lingers on the finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Undone Pinot Noir (Rheinhessen)

description: This blend of Syrah, Touriga Nacional and Petit Verdot is smooth, ripe and full of black-cherry and plum fruits. With its acidity, crisp texture and soft tannins, it will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Parras Wines 2016 Castelo do Sulco Colheita Red (Lisboa)

description: This is a good but basic rosé Cava. Aromas of mild citrus fruits and vanilla lead to a good but basic palate with just enough energy and acidity. Cantaloupe and citrus-peel flavors are steady on the finish.
title: Perelada NV Brut Rosé Sparkling (Cava)

description: Except for a hint of lime, this basic brut Cava smells neutral. A simple palate is citrusy and in solid shape. Generic white-fruit flavors come with mild green notes of celery and scallion. It's lively but lacks flavor on the finish.
title: Poema NV Brut Sparkling (Cava)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This wine is fresh and forward, with simple but appealing flavors of raspberry and cherry candies. The flavors don't go any further than that, but for a fruity quaffable wine, it's quite well done.
title: RHEA 2016 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: This has a funky nose of truffle, game, scorched soil and just a whiff of barnyard. The fleshy palate has a rustic allure, showing baked plum, blackberry extract, vanilla and the same truffle and gamy notes of the nose. Grainy dusty tannins provide the soft framework.
title: Romeo 2012 Riserva dei Mandorli  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Aromas of ripe red berries and violets are fresh and light on the nose. A juicy, medium-bodied palate of blackberry and racy cranberry flavors shows good concentration, with floral accents that dot the finish.
title: San Marzano 2016 Il Pumo Primitivo (Salento)

description: This ripe wine is full of black fruit as well as a serious tannin structure. Rich, concentrated, juicy with berry flavors and with layers of acidity, it strikes a balance between firm tannins and black fruit.
title: Senhora do Convento 2012 Red (Douro)

description: This is a velvety thick and oaky red, aged some time in bottle. Somewhat herbal in clove, cedar and graphite notes, its texture is defined more by leather.
title: Summit Lake 2012 Sophia Lynn Petite Syrah (Howell Mountain)

description: This Bordeaux-style blend is comprised of mainly Cabernet Franc and rounded out with Merlot and Petit Verdot. This wine offers black currant, black plum and pepper, with additions of toasty clove, nutmeg and charred wood. Barnyard and fig bring depth of character to the mix. The acidity is bright and the tannins are present but soft. The finish is long and full of oak spice.
title: Sunset Hills 2013 Mosaic Red (Virginia)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Sourced from both Volcano Ridge and The Pines vineyards, this is a smooth, supple wine, 100% varietal, with a mix of plum and blueberry fruit. Somewhat high toned and bright, it is ready to drink over the next couple of years.
title: The Pines 1852 2015 Merlot (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: There's a warm, ripe character to this wine, which boasts generous waves of macerated berries and cherries throughout. Accents of licorice, star anise and curing spice lend interest to the round, creamy palate. It's an easy, fruity pour that's best enjoyed now.
title: Tormentoso 2015 Old Vine Cinsault (Paarl)

description: There's the slightest pink tint to the color of this bottling, which shows baked apple and poached pear aromas on the ripe but pleasant nose. The palate is also quite ripe, showing more cooked pome fruit and squeezed lime, with a decent texture.
title: Vinum 2016 Chardonnay (Monterey)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This bottling is very mellow and shy on many fronts, though it emerges from its shell with patience. Light aromas of red fruit and dark spice lead into a palate with a full mouthfeel, carrying flavors of raspberry, rose petal, loam and eventually sharp baking spice.
title: Windrun 2014 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This very sweet, 210g/l dessert wine was produced cryogenically. It's peachy and syrupy, with candied apricots and a touch of maple syrup. There's just enough acidity to cut some of the treacle, but the sweetness has a sugary granularity rather than a smooth, integrated mix of flavors.
title: Anam Cara 2015 Nicholas Vineyard Gewürztraminer (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: Baked licorice leads the nose on this blend of 74% Merlot and 26% Cabernet Franc, along with thyme and stewed elderberries. The mouthfeel is lighter bodied, carrying flavors of exotic marjoram and red currant.
title: Avensole 2013 Sole Serenade Red (Temecula Valley)

description: The nose is closed and does not want to give too much away. The palate, however, has lovely lemon and fresh pear fruit on a rather light, dry body. This is fresh, easy and fairly neutral, but clean and fresh. Drink soon.
title: Bestheim 2016 Anne de K Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit-forward aromas of black currant and plum lead to plump, easy-drinking fruit flavors. It's straightforward, though it seems to lack concentration, especially in the middle.
title: Boomtown 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A neutral nose offers a quiet, lemony streak. The palate is equally neutral, almost dilute, but fresh and clean. Some apple and pear notes appear on the refreshing finish. Drink soon.
title: Bott Frères 2016 Edelzwicker White (Alsace)

description: This blend of Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, Malbec, Merlot and Petit Verdot is dense and heady, with bold scents of black-fruit preserves, blackberry, licorice, mocha and balsamic. The crushed-velvet texture is supported by fine yet gripping tannins, lending ample structure to the dark black plum and cherry fruit flavors. Final notes of tree bark, leather and olive tapenade grace the close. Drink now–2022.
title: KWV 2015 The Mentors Orchestra Red (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: Licorice spice, black cherry cordial, sweet sage and rustic earthy aromas show on the nose of this bottling, which also includes 14% Cabernet Franc, 8% Merlot and 3% Petit Verdot. Blackberry, pepper and dark cocoa are evident on the sip, along with more of that tangy licorice spice and bay leaf flavor.
title: Leoness 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Temecula Valley)

description: Earthy berry aromas come with notes of beef stock and dried leaves. A clampy palate with rubbery tannins is the opposite of smooth, while this wine tastes of raw plum, berry fruits and grape skins. It feels pinchy on the finish.
title: Maetierra Dominum 2009 QP Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Light and crisp on the nose, this bottling shows passion fruit, lemon, chilled Anjou pear slices and a hint of grassy scents, proving broadly appealing. There is great zip to the sip and a bit of viscosity as well, with zesty flavors of green apple rinds and chopped lemongrass.
title: Morgan 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Abundant Bing cherry and black-plum flavors fill out this deeply colored, full-bodied and rather soft-textured wine. It tastes nicely concentrated and feels velvety on the palate due to fine-grained tannins.
title: Naggiar 2015 Zio Luciano Estate Grown Red (Sierra Foothills)

description: Produced at the Quinta de Baixo in Bairrada, this is a delicious, juicy wine. Quince and kiwi flavors are laced with great acidity and with a texture so light that the wine dances on the palate. Drink this delicious wine now.
title: Niepoort 2015 VV Vinhas Velhas Bical-Maria Gomes White (Bairrada)

description: A blend of Sangiovese, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah, this has aromas suggesting black-skinned berry, leather and underbrush. The firm palate delivers black currant, cedar and clove alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Rocca di Frassinello 2015 Ornello  (Maremma Toscana)

description: This wine offers aromas of baked rhubarb, red plum, lavender, violets and rooibos. There is a lovely addition of cedar from the oak that is also present on the palate, along with red plum. Lively acidity and well-integrated tannins make for an attractive mouthfeel. The finish is long, evolving and full of spice.
title: Rosemont 2015 Kilvavock Red (Virginia)

description: An overt ripeness dominates the nose and mouth of this plush, decadent wine, as notes of black cherry and forest berry feel sauced and muddled together. The palate offers a more animal character, with cured meat and game notes that ride through to the spicy, coffee-laced finish. The velvety tannins and ample acidity are nicely balanced on the palate. Drink now.
title: Rudi Schultz 2015 Syrah (Stellenbosch)

description: While light bodied and spry, there's a pleasantly silky mouthfeel to this easy going, elegant, dry Riesling. Lime-zest and green apple flavors are accented by hints of white florals and an invigorating lemon-juice finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Schäfer 2016 Made With Organic Grapes Dry-Style Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: Just a touch of oak aging has given this wine a spicy character that enhances its rich, creamy texture and cool, clear character. With its apple and pear fruits and good acidity at the end, this wine still needs more time. Drink from late 2018.
title: Seacampo 2015 Casa Américo Encruzado (Dão)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This vintage sparkler is refined, crisp and clean, following the Méthode Champenoise. There's a hint of strawberry fruit and plenty of lemony acids. The bubbles are fine and the presentation classy. It should age nicely for up to a decade.
title: Sokol Blosser 2014 Sparkling Rosé of Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Exuberantly ripe yellow-peach and mango flavors are offset by a streak of crushed slate in this light-as-a-feather wine. It's not a deeply concentrated wine but fresh and pretty, with a lingering tangerine finish. Drink now.
title: Twisted River 2016 Bin 568 Late Harvest Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This solid, even chunky style brings brambly black-cherry fruit, dotted with scents and hints of fresh thyme. Cask fermented and barrel aged, it adds a light touch of chocolate to the finish.
title: Verve 2015 Croft Vineyards Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of French oak, espresso, violet and dark spice slowly take shape. The austere palate shows raw cherry, dried sage and star anise set against a backbone of close-grained tannins.
title: Villa S. Anna 2013 Poldo  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Plump, peachy fruit tones are elegantly offset by fresh acidity and a lean mineral undertone. Light bodied and dry in style, it's a refreshing value wine with wide appeal. Drink now through 2019.
title: Zen Garden 2016 Riesling (Pfalz)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Done in a ripe, rather chunky style, this has a fruit-forward mix of lemon, grapefruit and kiwi. It was half stainless and half barrel fermented, and finishes with a firm grip.
title: 2Hawk 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Rogue Valley)

description: Honeysuckle, rosewater and fresh lime peel unite to create a very perfumed but fresh nose. The palate is intensely aromatic but remains light and fresh, there is a textural, pithy side to the fluid body.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2016 Muscat (Alsace)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is essentially a barrel select winemaker's blend. Mixing oranges, raspberries and cherries in the fruit bowl, it has more palate weight than you might expect given the low alcohol. The tannins have a little extra grip, and leave a light trail of cocoa powder in the aftertaste.
title: Amalie Robert 2013 Amalie's Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Ripe, earthy plum and cassis aromas are slightly smoky, with a graphite note. An oaky palate is spread a bit thin, while flavors of red currant and plum are backed by dry oak that extends onto the finish. Drink now.
title: Baron De Ley 2014 Reserva  (Rioja)

description: A gold color and wheaty aromas of straw and haystack are devoid of Verdejo's patented high-toned aromas. A soft palate is low on acidity, while this tastes woody, with hard-to-find white-fruit flavors. A peachy, leesy finish shows residual oak. Enjoy soon.
title: Belondrade 2015 Belondrade y Lurton Verdejo (Rueda)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of red and black-skinned berry align with whiffs of dark culinary spice. Reflecting the nose, the light-bodied, almost ethereal palate offers red cherry, strawberry, orange zest and star anise set against bright acidity and slightly dusty tannins.
title: Bindella 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is a fruity mix of maraschino cherries, candied citrus, chocolate and cinnamon spice. It's a dry wine, yet tastes a bit like dessert in a bottle. Full, round and forward, it showcases the house style, which is immediate, delicious, forward, spicy and loaded with candied fruit.
title: Cardwell Hill 2014 Estate Bottled Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dark berry fruit, loamy soil and dense purple flowers make for a pure straightforward nose on this bottling from the longtime producer. The palate shows pleasant flavors of black currant and black cherry with hints of vanilla and anise-laced biscotti. The tannins are chalky, tough nearly chunky, and will smooth with time.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: This Bouey estate, with its historic 18th-century tower, is named for an ancestor. This wine is richly endowed, structured and with a solid core of tannins. The black-currant fruit around the tannins is balanced, ripe and likely to age along with the wood and tannic structure. Drink from 2021.
title: Château la France Delhomme 2015  Médoc

description: With a high percentage of Merlot, this is a ripe, juicy and red-fruit-flavored wine. It is soft, with the tannins already well integrated into the plush fruit. This rich, generous wine will be ready to drink from 2022.
title: Château Labadie 2015  Médoc

description: This well-priced, aged wine offers aromas of brandied cherries, spiced game and herbs on the nose. Its round feel and soft tannins make it entirely approachable to drink on its own, while its savory palate and graphite finish make it a perfect accompaniment to braised lamb or duck. Drink now.
title: Cosimo Taurino 2010 Notarpanaro Negroamaro (Salento)

description: Both lemon and ripe pear meet on the nose. It is the lemon notes that are foremost on the slender, dry palate. The finish is clean and moreish.
title: Domaine Barmès-Buecher 2016 Rosenberg Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: An earthy touch hangs over pear and lemon aromas on the nose. The palate offers bright lemon freshness, with a slightly savory streak on the light, refreshing palate. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2016 Les Jardins Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Fleshy red cherry notes are expansive on the nose. These tones are equally expressive on the palate where they have a counterpoint of freshness. The body is textured with fine tannins, keeping the feel light and fluid. A lovely, easy and fruit-driven wine.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Santa Cruz Mountains, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a purity to the black fruit, cocoa, espresso and wisteria aromas on the nose of this bottling from a small dry-farmed vineyard 2,100-feet up in the mountains. The zesty palate throws potpourri and white pepper together with fresh boysenberry and elderberry, making for a uniquely flavored wine, redolent with dried purple flowers.
title: Scar of the Sea 2015 House Vineyard Syrah (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: This is half barrel fermented and half fermented in stainless steel. It's gritty and expansive on the luxurious palate and well-made, offering layers of apple, pear and melon with a tease of anise on the lengthy full-bodied finish.
title: Stag's Leap Wine Cellars 2015 Arcadia Vineyard Chardonnay (Coombsville)

description: This is a sexy, elegant, light-on-its-feet beauty from one of Tasmania's most highly respected producers. The nose is a heady blend of cinnamon bark, bright red berries, salt and garrigue, while the palate is crunchy and sappy. A picture of drinkability combined with complexity and superb winemaking.
title: Tolpuddle 2015 Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

description: Wakefield's top wine offers enchanting aromas of mocha, milky coffee, vanilla, licorice and sun-ripened raspberries and blackberries. It's texturally plush, with a fair amount of richness, silky tannins and well integrated oak. This should age with exceptional grace. Drink 2019–2027.
title: Wakefield 2013 The Pioneer Exceptional Parcel Release Shiraz (Clare Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A whiff of white flowers, then a crush of ripe fruit, and the wine opens with tangy lemon, grapefruit and peach flavors. Notes of citrus rind add depth and the acids strike just the right chord.
title: Amalie Robert 2016 Our Muse Viognier (Willamette Valley)

description: This 11-acre property, centered on an ultramodern winery, is the most northerly estate in Margaux. The wine, with its blend of dark tannins and juicy black-currant fruit, is rich, with impressive concentration and minerality. With weight and a firm core, the wine will age well. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Bellevue de Tayac 2015  Margaux
