description: This is a structured wine, beautifully perfumed with richly tannic black fruits. It comes from a large 111-acre estate in the village of the same name. Made to age with firm tannins and layers of sumptuous fruit over firm wood aging, the wine will mature slowly. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Cissac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This dense and impressively ripe wine comes from a Sorge family estate in the northern part of the Margaux appellation. With black fruits and generous tannins, it is a big wine with considerable richness. This complex wine will age. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Deyrem Valentin 2015  Margaux

description: This wine comes from a 50-acre crus bourgeois estate in the middle of some of the greatest crus classés estates. The terroir is impressive, giving a rich, structured wine with good concentration, ripe tannins and a sumptuous bed of black fruits. The wine is likely to age well; drink from 2025.
title: Château Fonbadet 2015  Pauillac

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This wine is deeply striated with tones of licorice, coffee, tobacco and black tea. The palate offers the blackest of black fruits, sanguine tones, cured meat and coffee, taking Southern Oregon Syrah to the next level.
title: Abacela 2014 Reserve South Face Block Syrah (Umpqua Valley)

description: This wine is currently a baby, wanting more time to completely form and express itself with full potential. The nose is shy at first, needing some airing to coax out notes of singed fynbos, greengage and white melon rind. The palate offers a bit more fruit expression, as waves of citrus peel, Asian pear and dried apple skin are framed by a chalky minerality and just a hint of nutty stone-fruit pit. A sea-breeze salinity graces the driven, precise finish. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Alheit 2015 Magnetic North Mountain Makstok Chenin Blanc (Olifants River)

description: This is a lovely wine from a fine appellation, on the outskirts of the town of Napa. Rose, cassis and cherry-strawberry accent a chiseled structure of finely honed acidity and a succulent core that will equally please a crowd and connoisseurs.
title: Ancien 2015 Mink Vineyard Pinot Noir (Coombsville)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a very prominent gamy quality throughout this wine, which starts with aromas of roasted lamb, charcoal, creosote and animal pelt. The palate takes that feral quality even deeper, with hints of Worcestershire sauce, dried lamb and peppery spice. Great for Northern Rhône lovers.
title: Archium 2015 Faction Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: Rose petal, wild berry, graphite and crushed aromatic herb lead the way. Reflecting the nose, the fresh linear palate offers strawberry, sour cherry, white pepper and a savory saline note. Bright acidity and polished tannins provide graceful support. Drink through 2027.
title: Arpepe 2012 Grumello Rocca De Piro  (Valtellina Superiore)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Initial cola and root-beer aromas precede floral berry scents. A strict palate is firm but avoids crushing weight. Narrow plum, currant and berry flavors are focused and framed by roaring tannins. This is still young and tough, but with great potential; drink from 2019–2035.
title: Bodegas Alejandro Fernandez 2012 Pesquera Reserva Tinto  (Ribera del Duero)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah and Petit Verdot, this offers enticing scents of red and black-skinned berry, fragrant purple flower and a whiff of Mediterranean scrub. On the fresh, elegant palate, polished tannins support cassis, plum and raspberry alongside culinary spice notes.
title: Campo alle Comete 2016 Rosso Stupore  (Bolgheri)

description: This wine comes from a 10-acre estate that is run by the same team as the much larger Château Larose Trintaudon, helping explain its quality and the richness. It has broad tannins, ripe blackberry fruit and a generous, succulent character. It should be aged further so drink from 2024.
title: Château Arnauld 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Owner Philippe Raoux has created both a major sculpture park and a series of impressive vintages at this estate at the western end of Margaux. This vintage is one of the best yet, rich and vibrant with great fruits and solid tannins. It needs serious aging, so drink from 2025.
title: Château d'Arsac 2015  Margaux

description: This wine is light and fruity, with attractive acidity and a crisp texture. Black-currant flavors and balanced tannins keep it elegant and delicious. It is crisp, fresh and likely to develop over the medium term. Drink from 2023.
title: Château Soutard-Cadet 2015  Saint-Émilion

description: While each of Dalrymple's Pinots are distinctly different, they're all equally delicious. The Coal River will appeal to those funk lovers, with notes of earth, horse saddle, wet leaves, dried currants, orange rind and bitters. The medium-bodied palate is less funky, offering soft tannins and no harsh edges.
title: Dalrymple 2014 Coal River Pinot Noir (Tasmania)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is equal parts Grenache and Mourvèdre (co-fermented), finished off with Syrah and aged in neutral French-oak puncheons. The aromas are fascinating, with notes of fresh savory herbs, capers, sea salt, white pepper, bacon fat and mixed fruit. The flavors are plump and appealing, mixing fruity and savory tones that lead to a pepper and olive finish. It's a standout interpretation of this vineyard.
title: Damsel 2015 The Fates Boushey Vineyard Red (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The top wine from De Ponte, this gets 60% new French oak and a full 15 months in barrel prior to bottling. Those flavors come up quickly, putting a lush vein of butterscotch right down the center. The tangy marionberry fruit is a perfect complement, keeping it fresh and luscious, and suggesting that it will drink optimally for the next decade.
title: De Ponte Cellars 2014 Baldwin Family Reserve Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Charred hickory and cured bacon waft from the glass, overriding but not completely overpowering the lush, fruity center. Waves of velvety tannins brush across the palate, lending structure to the ripe black cherry, blackberry and mashed plum-flesh flavors. Final hints of tree bark, licorice root and black tea unfold on the medium-length finish. It's delicious now, but should evolve well from 2021–2028.
title: De Toren 2015 Z Red (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This starts out dense and tight, with compact seams of smoke, char, espresso and cacao, sprinkled with dashes of sea salt. It moves into a thick core of mulberry, red currant and black cherry fruit, set in a frame of polished tannins, hitting a faintly green note as it fades.
title: Domaine Serene 2014 Fleur de Lis Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: This black wine is rich, earthy and chocolaty on the nose, with fully toasted aromas of blackberry and cassis. A massive palate with tannins as heavy as cinderblocks features saturated flavors of blackberry, black coffee and chocolate. A malty, smoky, woody finish is anything but shy. Drink from 2020–2030.
title: Emina 2012 Atio  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a stunning example of a wine that's fresh and light yet loaded with flavor. Buoyant aromas of black raspberry juice, dried fennel and wet slate lead onto a palate of snappy, slightly sour pomegranate, white pepper and sumac. The texture is equally complex, while the finish brings additional flavors of candied cherry and strawberry.
title: Gary Farrell 2015 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: With a slight nose of noble reduction, this seductive wine offers a wealth of balanced flavor and body, shining in tones of nuts, fennel, vanilla and mango. The oak is measured and supportive, never getting in the way, allowing the acidity to pierce through the fruit.
title: Grgich Hills 2014 Estate Grown Chardonnay (Napa Valley)

description: Deep ruby to the eye, this wine has a nose of dark plum, bramble fruit and thyme. A sense of brightness is ever-present on the palate, with flavors of cherry, cassis, butterscotch, fennel and white pepper. Smooth tannins play on the tongue and then linger just beyond the black-cherry finish, with a touch of caramel.
title: Hayotzer 2014 Lyrica G-S-M (Galilee)

description: This young wine comes up quickly with strawberry fruit, ripe and dotted with white pepper. There are hints of parsley, and impressive midpalate concentration. With all of Ken Wright's wines, which are released quite young, the odds strongly favor additional development and improved complexity with another few years of bottle age.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Bonnie Jean Vineyard Pinot Noir

Appellation: Snipes Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Vineyard-designated wines from this appellation are rare, and this wine shows the promise of this site. There's no mistaking the variety, with its enchanting aromas of fragrant flower, smoked meat, chopped parsley, green olive and purple fruit. Flavors are supremely fresh and bright, showing a mixture of fruit and savory, with assorted herb notes intertwined throughout. An extended finish, filled with notes of olive, caps it off. It's beautiful on its own but should sing at the dinner table. Give it time to open up.
title: Kerloo 2015 Limited Release Upland Vineyard Syrah (Snipes Mountain)

description: Sourced from clay-rich vineyards outside the coastal town of Trani, this blend of 50% Uva di Troia, 35% Merlot and 15% Syrah is slightly jammy on the nose in aromas of stewed red and black berries, with a dusting of coffee and crushed herbs. There's a pleasant roundness and plushness to the palate, where the fruit profile seems fresher in nature, accented by streaks of spiced game and cocoa nibs. Soft and approachable, there is just enough tannic and acidic cut, enjoy this wine for its immediate appeal.
title: Villa Schinosa 2012 San Valentino Red (Puglia)

description: Pristine notes of lime zest, grapefruit and gooseberry persist from nose to finish in this vibrant, dry Riesling. Light bodied and bracing, it's a crisply textured wine with a refreshing, long finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Wittmann 2016 Trocken Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is an appealing, substantive wine that combines ample fruit and spice aromas with well-concentrated black cherry flavors and a moderately tannic texture that will help it pair well with rich dishes. It's a classic Bordeaux-style blend made from 65% Cabernet Sauvignon plus the four other red Bordeaux varietals.
title: Wood Family Vineyards 2015 El Loco Rojo Red (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this lively medium-bodied red opens with aromas of new leather, red plum and a whiff of peppercorn. The bright palate offers tart red cherry, white pepper and a hint of pomegranate alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Ambrogio e Giovanni Folonari 2014 Torcalvano  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Delicate scents of wild berry, star anise and alpine herb float out of the glass. On the polished palate, supple tannins lightly support juicy Marasca cherry, strawberry preserve and clove. A mineral note wraps up the finish.
title: Arpepe 2015 Rosso  (Valtellina)

description: This tight, sharply defined wine shows sculpted raspberry fruit, focused and amplified with light suggestions of orange peel and chocolate. A mix of Dijon and Pommard clones, it spent 11 months in 40% new French oak, finishing with a touch of peppery herbs.
title: Atticus 2014 Atticus Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: This is a dense and seductive blend, with brooding yet enticing aromas of mocha, cocoa-dusted cherry, brambly berry and black forest cake. The medium-weight palate is plush and smooth, but not overdone, framed by ample acidity and velvety tannins that lend structure to the rich dark-fruit flavors. Final notes of black pepper, olive and toasted oak unfold on the enduring finish. Drink now–2021.
title: Black Pearl 2015 Oro Cabernet Sauvignon-Shiraz (Coastal Region)

description: A rather neutral nose nonetheless has hints of lemon zest that suggests freshness and lightness. But the palate shows tinges of lemon, fern and the merest hint of pepper. A little green pear fruit comes in, too. A very light, very subtle wine, with a long, dry finish.
title: Boeckel 2016 Midelberg Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Aromas of violet, red-skinned berry and a whiff of alpine herb lift out of the glass. The juicy palate offers red cherry, tobacco and a touch of white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Caven 2010 Inferno Al Carmine Riserva  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: This second wine comes from both La Mission Haut-Brion and Haut-Brion vineyards. It has great fruit, crisp acidity and a lemon and grapefruit fruitiness. A tang to the texture lifts the wine into crispness as well as acidity. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Haut-Brion 2015 Le Clarté  (Pessac-Léognan)

description: This wine comes from a six-acre property under the same ownership as Château Paloumey. It has 20% Petit Verdot in the blend, giving it both the tannins and the dark color. It is a structured wine, rich in black plums and densely textured. The wine will be ready to drink from 2024.
title: Château La Garricq 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: Under the same ownership as Château la Tonnelle, this wine comes the small village of Cissac next door to Pauillac. Dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon, it is richly structured, spicy from wood aging and juicy with black-currant fruitiness. Drink this still-firm, young wine from 2022.
title: Château Lamothe-Cissac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Merlot dominates in this second wine of Château Quintus. Ripe, juicy and with delicious red fruits, it is smooth and lightly tannic. This wine will develop over the medium term. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Quintus 2015 Le Dragon  (Saint-Émilion)

description: Produced from a small parcel of vines on the Valandraud estate, this wine is crisp and fruity, with attractive acidity. Ripe lime and lemon flavors are given extra richness by the tangy mineral texture. Drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Château Valandraud 2015 Virginie  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: A wonderful hint of Mandarin peel on the nose translates onto zesty, juicy freshness on the palate. This shows ripe pear and juicy citrus, with intense echoes of tangerine peel. A highly appetizing, fruity but light and dry wine. This will get any party going.
title: Domaine Bott-Geyl 2015 Métiss White (Alsace)

description: A touch of reduction clouds juicy, ripe cherry notes on the nose but they appear edged by freshness on the light, taut palate. There is a pleasant grip of fine tannin and a slight crunch. A fresh-faced but very authentic and pure wine.
title: Domaine Pfister 2016 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: An earthy and very ripe notion of yellow plum and pear on the nose carry a hint of reduction. The palate follows this with zesty lemon notes on a dry but concentrated body. Honeysuckle carries on the clean, long finish.
title: Domaine Zind-Humbrecht 2016 Turckheim Muscat (Alsace)

description: The subdued scent of red and black cherry plays on the nose. On the palate this hints at fruit, with freshness and a fine frame of softly crunching tannins. A light-bodied but balanced wine of understatement and elegance.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2016 The Furst Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Cherry fruit shines here, balanced against well-managed acids and tannins. Some unspecified barrel aging adds hints of chocolate wafer. Despite its youth, this is already drinking quite well and should be consumed over the next half decade.
title: Duck Pond 2016 Willamette Valley Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Given the size of the winery, there unfortunately is very little of this single-vineyard offering, which is the best of the 2015s. Black-cherry fruit conjures up the classic flavors of the AVA, with good flesh and light notes of toast and mocha from the barrel aging.
title: Erath 2015 Knight's Gambit Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Syrah (53%) takes the lead on this blend, followed by Merlot (27%) and Cabernet Sauvignon. Aromas of toast, anise, vanillin and cherry lead to rich, full-bodied cherry and cranberry flavors that coat the palate.
title: Estrin 2014 Tre Aris Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A blend of 40% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Cabenet Franc and 5% Petit Verdot, this structured red opens with aromas of black plum, mocha and tobacco. The savory palate offers black currant, licorice and black pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Fattoria di Magliano 2013 Poggio Bestiale  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This soft, approachable red impresses in a mix of earthy mushroom, soy and cedar, its body thickly constructed around a core of ripe tannin and charred oak. Well structured, it finishes in bursts of black pepper and leather.
title: Frei Brothers 2015 Reserve Merlot (Dry Creek Valley)

description: This leads with a powerful toasted-oak overlay to the ripe fruit tones of yellow melon, grilled apple and peach preserves. The mouthfeel is round and full, with a slightly pithy texture that lends some structure to the robust palate. Medium acidity keeps the richness in check, while a pleasant minty afterthought dances through the close. Drink now–2020.
title: Glenelly 2015 Estate Reserve Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: A bouquet of black plum, cassis and licorice leads into flavors of black cherry, raspberry, anise, white chocolate and violet. It is rich on the palate, with velvety tannins and touches of floral and spice notes on the finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2014 Yarden Odem Vineyard Merlot (Galilee)

description: Shadowy notes of autumn leaves, dried lavender and earth accent delicate red cherry and raspberry in this pretty but rather diaphanous red. It's invigorating and fresh, offering crisp cranberry acidity and fine grained but persistent tannins that extend the finish.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2014 Kestener Herrenberg Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Mosel)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: One of the better Syrahs from the Willamette Valley, this smooth and brambly effort is bursting with tart red and blue berries. There's a nice sprinking of cinnamon spice as well, and as with all of Helioterra's wines, the precision and focus are exceptional.
title: Helioterra 2014 Dion Vineyard Syrah (Willamette Valley)

description: This unusual blend mixes Riesling with rarely-seen Huxelrebe, a high-yielding early-ripening grape with scents similar to Muscat. The Starthistle is off dry, with honeyed fruits mixing peach, apricot and papaya. More palate weight than Riesling alone, yet it maintains Riesling's precision and elegance.
title: Helioterra 2015 Starthistle Cuvée White (Oregon)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A single block of Dijon 114 is the source here. This shows medium concentration in mixed berry flavors and peppery herbs. The balancing acidity brings a pleasing minerality. Drink now through 2020.
title: Holloran 2013 La Colina Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Truffle, new leather, violet and moist-earth aromas delicately lift out of the glass. The ethereal palate shows sour cherry, pomegranate and a hint of dark culinary spice alongside bright acidity. Lithe tannins offer light, polished support. Drink through 2020.
title: Il Conventino 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: This is a pretty concentrated fruit-driven wine that shows focused red and black-cherry flavors and an elegant texture. It sips very smoothly, feels light to medium bodied and has a fresh fruity finish.
title: It Is What It Is! NV Red (Lodi)

description: Deep red-violet to the eye, this wine has aromas of dark plum, cherry and juniper. It might be a little too restrained for some, with flavors of black cherry and Red Delicious apple, joined by strong notes of eucalyptus, spearmint and anise. Smooth tannins fill the palate right into a cool mint finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2016 2900 Merlot (Judean Hills)

description: Invigorating freshness abounds in this dry, medium-bodied Grauburgunder, or Pinot Gris. Pristine lemon, apple and pear flavors are cutting and bright, marked by fresh acidity and a delicately textured mouthfeel. It's an easy going quaffer to enjoy now.
title: Juwel 2016 Grauburgunder (Rheinhessen)

description: A touch of bruised apple peel fills the nose. The very light palate counters this with bright notions of fresh lemon on a dry, slender palate. This is clean-cut and refreshing, with a lovely lemon finish.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2015 Muscat (Alsace)

description: A fresh, clean nose of peeled Conference pear leads onto a palate where a yeasty, rounded tone joins this fruity notion. The palate is light and has the merest hint of savoriness, akin to soy. The finish is yeasty, fresh and dry.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2016 Trois Châteaux Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Aromas of ripe red berry and dried Alpine herb follow through to the racy, rather slender palate together with a note of tangy nectarine zest. Vibrant acidity and a lively perlage give it an invigorating finish.
title: La Montina 2010 Extra Brut Rosé  (Franciacorta)

description: Pristine tones of peach, melon and grapefruit are tinged by earth and crushed stone in this light-bodied dry Riesling. Silken on the palate but boasting cutting lemon-lime acidity, it finishes moderately long and citrusy.
title: Lucashof 2016 Riesling (Pfalz)

description: Appetizing, fruity notes of pear drop dominate the nose. The palate shows fresh notes of green and yellow pear on a lemony, fresh body. The finish is dry and superclean. Drink soon.
title: Marcel Hugg 2016 Réserve Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: This easygoing blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Syrah offers aromas of black-skinned berry and cooking spice. The savory palate evokes black currant, clove and white pepper alongside polished tannins.
title: Marchesi de' Frescobaldi 2015 Terre More Ammiraglia  (Maremma Toscana)

description: A luscious bouquet of rose petal and ripe peach on the nose is very convincing and not overblown. The fluid palate shows and edge of pleasant bitterness, countered by an off-dry finish. A lovely aperitif style. Drink soon.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Trio White (Alsace)

description: Red cherry notes on the nose hint at ripeness. The palate presents this ripe fruit with simplicity and freshness. There could be more focus but this is juicy and easy.
title: Meyer-Fonné 2016 Réserve Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Made almost entirely with Chardonnay, this opens with aromas of chamomile, yellow stone fruit and a whiff of alpine herb. The lively palate mirrors the nose, offering yellow Bartlett pear, Golden Delicious apple and a hint of pastry dough. A vibrant perlage provide the sparkle.
title: Monte Rossa NV Brut Nature  (Franciacorta)

description: Aromas suggesting toast, bread crust and whiff of nut meld together. The aromas carry on to the soft palate together with ripe yellow apple and a hint of citrus alongside a light mousse. Bitter walnut is there at the end.
title: Monte Rossa NV P.R. Brut Blanc de Blanc  (Franciacorta)

description: This dramatic, hefty wine has strong oaky, spicy aromas, a rich and honeyed fruit flavor and a texture that's almost thick, making it cling to the sides of the mouth. A touch of sweetness registers on the finish.
title: Mt. Konocti Winery 2016 Barrel Select Chardonnay (Lake County)

description: Raspberry, violet and new leather aromas lead the nose. The lithe, rather light-bodied palate offers sour cherry, strawberry and a hint of white pepper. It's easygoing, with supple tannins and firm acidity. Enjoy soon.
title: Nera 2012 Sassella Alisio  (Valtellina Superiore)

description: The delicate nose suggests wild berry and a whiff of culinary spice that carry over to the fresh easygoing palate along with crushed strawberry and white pepper. It's already approachable, with bright acidity and lithe tannins. Drink soon.
title: Nino Negri 2014  Valtellina Superiore

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Sharp, racy and precise, this particular clone of Pinot Blanc is a rarity anywhere, especially in Oregon. Brisk apple and white-peach fruit comes with a scent of lemon verbena. The finishing tannins have a grainy expression. This would tuck in well with foods usually accompanied by a Loire Valley dry white wine.
title: Origin 2016 Z'ivo Vineyard Gouges Pinot Blanc (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This youthful wine pulls from several vineyards across the appellation, offering strong floral aromas of rose, geranium and lavender. Sizable tannins add to a feeling of full-bodied richness, highlighted in black tea, forest and clove.
title: Pali 2016 Bluffs Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Aromas of cocoa and maple syrup lead to lush, lightly sweet fruit flavors that have plenty of oak spice to dress them up. This wine tastes very ripe and full bodied, oozing with jammy flavors but it also has enough of a tannic backbone to keep it fresh on the palate.
title: Peltier 2015 Triomphe Estate Grown Red (Lodi)

description: Crisp tangerine, yellow apple and grapefruit abound in this juicy, delicately sweet Riesling. Easy going yet elegantly balanced, it's a dependable everyday wine with wide appeal.
title: Peter Mertes 2016 Platinum Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: For this white Pinot Noir, the grapes were quickly pressed and barrel fermented, yielding a wine whose scent, color and flavor are virtually indistinguishable from a young Pino Blanc. It's tart and light, with Meyer lemon, grapefruit and a dot of butter.
title: Phelps Creek 2014 Corduroy Blanc Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: Taking its name from the Bordeaux estate owned by this negociant firm, this wine is soft, rich and ready to drink. Blackberries and damsons are laced with acidity and hint of spice from wood aging. Drink now.
title: Pierre Rivière 2015 Menuts Merlot-Cabernet  (Bordeaux)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of underbrush, toast, oak and espresso take the forefront. On the approachable palate, close-grained tannins give flexible support to fleshy black cherry, mature plum, coffee and nutmeg. Enjoy soon to capture the remaining freshness and fruit.
title: Poggio alla Sala 2013 Parceto  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This racy wine was pressed whole cluster and aged sur lie in neutral oak for nine months. It's textural and fresh, with flavors of bright apple set against a mineral base.
title: Sokol Blosser 2016 Pinot Blanc (Dundee Hills)

description: Ripe blackberry, raisin and cassis aromas border on syrupy. This extracted wine is soupy in feel, with hard tannins. Toasty black-fruit flavors end with burnt oak notes on a saturated finish.
title: Vall Llach 2015 Embruix Red (Priorat)

description: Rich, ripe yellow-apple, pear and grapefruit flavors are delicate in concentration but bright and invigorating in this dry, full-bodied Riesling. It's a vibrantly composed wine, with fresh lemon-lime acidity and a moderately long finish accented by hints of honey and pine nut.
title: Weingut Georg Albrecht Schneider 2016 Niersteiner vom Kalk Kabinett Trocken Riesling (Rheinhessen)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is all Chardonnay. Bright aromas of candied apple, crayon and melon lead to broad, dry fruit flavors that show a touch of bitterness on the finish. It's a pleasing, very cleanly made offering of this variety.
title: Wind Rose 2016 Bravo Bianco White Table Wine Chardonnay (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This young, tart and focused wine mixes fresh green herbs, citrus rind, celery and apple components. It's balanced and refreshing, and not notably different, other than slightly cheaper, from the winery's regular bottling of Pinot Gris.
title: Winter's Hill 2016 Watershed Pinot Gris (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A pretty shade of strawberry pink, this light tasty undemanding wine has strawberry fruit and a touch of white pepper. It was briefly barreled in a mix of stainless and neutral wood.
title: Z'IVO 2016 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: The nose offers lemon zest and green pear, two tones that also dominate the light, fresh and dry body which has a pithy textural side. Wonderfully clean and refreshing.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2016 Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Produced in the heart of the Entre-deux-Mers region, this wine is rich, young and fruity. And that's the way it should be, because this is not a wine for aging but for enjoying with its black fruits and ripe texture. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château la Rose de Vitrac 2015  Bordeaux

description: Named after the stagecoach that used to stop here on its way up the Médoc, this wine is light, with soft blackberry fruits and acidity. An easy wine that's likely to mature quickly, it should be ready from 2019.
title: Château Patache d'Aux 2015  Médoc

description: A mix of black and red cherries carry the nose, with tobacco leaf and herbal spice tones in a more moderate concentration. There's levity to the mouthfeel from the crisp acidity and light, sandy tannins, yet the palate offers a good deal of racy fruit flavors that end clean and refreshing.
title: Conti Zecca 2014 Donna Marzia Negroamaro (Puglia)

description: A blend of Merlot, Sangiovese, Cabernet Franc and Syrah, this has a shy nose that eventually reveals aromas of baking spice and black-skinned fruit. The straightforward palate offers sweet vanilla and cherry jam alongside round tannins.
title: Corte Medicea 2015 Zotikos  (Bolgheri)

description: Ripe and fruity notes of yellow Mirabelle plums make for an appetizing nose. The palate has a similarly rich impression but lacks a little direction and focus. There is plenty of fruit and lemon brightness. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Here is a light, tart, all-stainless style of wine, with interesting phenolic flavors of pear skin and green apples. Juicy acids bring a citrus component into it as well. It's lively and fresh: a good all-purpose crisp white wine.
title: Eugene Wine Cellars 2016 b2 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: High-toned cherry aromas meld with saw dust, anise and game on the nose of this pure expression of Nero di Troia. Light- to medium-bodied in style, flavors of savory bramble berries offer plenty of zip to the palate, with soft tannins giving just enough support. An herb-dusted cherry note marks the finish.
title: Giancarlo Ceci 2014 Parco Marano Nero di Troia (Castel del Monte)

description: With aromas of white peach and honeysuckle, this wine has flavors of ripe peach, marzipan and orange blossom. It is sweet but not cloying, with a burst of refreshing brightness on the finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2016 Hermon Moscato (Galilee)

description: This wine is rich, with superripe tannins and a barnyard character. It has structure but it mainly focuses on fruit and acidity. Made by the team at Cos d'Estournel, the wine has a rich structure and will be ready to drink soon, from 2019.
title: Goulée 2015  Médoc

description: This wine offers a bouquet of grapefruit and lime zest followed by flavors of chirimoya and guava. Grapefruit flavors return to the palate just in time for a zippy finish.
title: Jerusalem Wineries 2016 3400 Premium Dry White (Samson)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This estate features biodynamically grown fruit and artisanal winemaking practices. This wine is tart and focused in snappy lemon, orange and pineapple flavors.
title: Johan 2015 Grüner Veltliner (Willamette Valley)

description: This wine has a nice oomph of blackberry flavor and full enough body to carry it well. It feels rich and velvety on the palate and a generous layer of fine tannins doesn't get heavy.
title: LangeTwins 2013 Midnight Reserve Red (Lodi)

description: Lemon balm, herbs and citrus blossom aromas show a nice, clean intensity on the nose. Bright on the palate, with a bit of weight and roundness, citrus pith and fleshy yellow apple flavors meld with more balmy, herbal notes.
title: Leone de Castris 2016 Messapia Verdeca (Salento)

description: Mild white-fruit aromas suggest peach juice. This Godello is both plump and citrusy in feel. Generic citrus flavors are closest to orange. This is a touch bitter and slightly oxidized on the finish, so drink now.
title: Luna Beberide 2016 Godello (Bierzo)

description: Lemon and hay aromas carry delicate pollen and floral tones on the nose. Medium in weight and acidity, the palate is yellow-fruit driven, with a grapefruit zest vein of acidity.
title: Masseria Altemura 2016 Fiano (Salento)

description: This is a big bold wine that lays on the oak flavor without restraint. A strong aroma of cedar and baking spices is followed by very concentrated dark-fruit flavors and spicy charred oak flavors. It is quite tannic and almost thick in texture, giving plenty of grip on the palate.
title: McManis 2016 Petite Sirah (California)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Compared to the 2015 vintage which retained its liveliness, this new vintage seems woody and oxidized. It's a natural wine, and those can sometimes thrill tasters willing to stretch out a bit. Here, it's soft and slightly flat, with flavors of mashed fruits. Drink up.
title: Minimus 2016 Vitae Springs Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Willamette Valley)

description: Light floral and peach aromas lead to a lively palate full of fresh fruit, lychees, and honey while a subtle fizz helps balance out considerable sweetness. It's a charming, light wine that's easy to swallow.
title: Mirassou 2016 Moscato (California)

description: Very much the local white grape, Arinto benefits from the relatively cool climate of the region to give a delicious, crisp wine. It is aromatic, full of citrus flavors with a zesty, mineral edge. Drink this wine now.
title: Quinta de Chocapalha 2016 Arinto (Lisboa)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: The Delfino vineyard was the source for these grapes, a mix of two clones. It captures a bit of the varietal character, with spicy, toasty notes around pale cherry fruit. In the finish, some chalky acids dominate.
title: Ram 2015 Tempranillo (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Baked, raisiny aromas are dull and soft. Following that lead, this wine is jammy and lacking in verve. Flavors of prune, raisin and spice cake finish smooth and easy, with almost no acidity.
title: Rento 2014  Ribera del Duero

description: This wine is fresh with raspberries, rooibos, red plum and cedar. Rooibos is the star of the show in terms of flavors. While not compex, it has great acidity and silky tannins that translate to a long, lush finish.
title: Rosemont 2015 Syrah (Virginia)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: There's plenty to enjoy about this lighter-than-average Primitivo. It's ripe and spicy, with a strong showing of bramble berries and anise. A soft, pliable feel make it immediately appealing.
title: San Marzano 2013 Talò  (Primitivo di Manduria)

description: While closed on the nose, the palate of this easy-drinking Riesling is plump in tones of juicy yellow peach and citrus. It's a lively, mouthwatering sip offering just a touch of bitter herb on the finish. Drink now through 2019.
title: Schmitt Söhne 2016 Crisp and Fruity Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: Vineyards close to Portugal's highest mountain range give this wine a crisp, cool character. A blend of Touriga Nacional and Alfrocheiro, it is fruity with red currants and plenty of acidity. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Seacampo 2016 Casa Américo Rosé (Dão)

description: Smoky, earthy aromas are cloaked by red- and black-fruit intensity on the nose. The palate brings ripe berry flavors boosted by leather, cedar and allspice, while the tannins are grippy and spicy but cut through by a vein of acidity. Tangy currant and allspice tones carry through the finish.
title: Shelburne 2014 Reserve Marquette (Vermont)

description: Aromas of orange-blossom honey, peach and lemon zest waft from the glass. The palate shows clementine, ripe white peach and lemon juice, with a crushed rock minerality. The sweetness is balanced by a bright backbone of acidity leading to a clean lemony finish.
title: Shelburne 2015 Riesling (Vermont)

description: The elegant Minho stone manor house is the heart of this fine vineyard. The wine, still with a light prickle on the tongue, is a classic from the Vinho Verde region, crisp, incisive and packed with fruit. Drink this tangy wine now.
title: Sogrape 2016 Quinta de Azevedo White (Vinho Verde)

description: Ripe tangerines are accented by notes of caramel, crushed earth and fennel in this earthy, slightly savory kabinett. Bristling lemon acidity and honey tones brighten the midpalate, leading a finish edged by spice and a hint of tannins.
title: Studert-Prüm 2016 Wehlener Sonnenuhr Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: This wine offers aromas and flavors of baked red currant, red plum, eucalyptus, black pepper and rare meat. Its zippy acidity is a great match for the full body and depth of flavors.
title: Veramar 2013 Estate Club Merlot (Virginia)

description: The name of this release goes back to the first red wine made by Niepoort in 1990. The wine was made with long old-barrel aging and was not bottled until 2017. Tannins are important to this rich wine; so is the powerful black fruit. They offer an mineral- and fruit-driven wine that still needs to age. Drink from 2020.
title: Niepoort 2013 Robustus Red (Douro)

description: Alluring aromas of bread crust, nectarine zest, berry and orchard fruit take center stage on this compelling sparkler. Made entirely with Pinot Nero, it's all about elegance, offering wild cherry, brioche, citrus and mineral alongside fresh acidity and tangy acidity. An ultrarefined perlage carries through to the dry succulent finish.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2008 Vintage Collection Dosaggio Zéro Noir  (Franciacorta)

description: This wine is ripe, rich and generous. It brings out the almost-sweet character of the fruit in this vintage. At the same time, the tannins are firm and support all this fruitiness. The wine will develop well. Drink from 2024.
title: Domaine de Chevalier 2015  Pessac-Léognan

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine offers a compelling nose of pretty florals and spicy forest-floor aromas. Dense on entry, it lingers in flavors of orange, cherry and rhubarb, with textured tannins and an undercurrent of a stony, mineral character.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Calegari Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Justina is the top wine for this estate, a mellifluous melding of berries and cherries, with a hint of peach. The acids are ample but never intrusive, and bring a rather delicate flavor of lemon oil. Exemplary in every way, this is drinking beautifully but may be cellared for up to a decade.
title: Alloro 2015 Justina Estate Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Enticing scents of scorched earth, cassis, new leather, vanilla, truffle and Mediterranean brush abound on this classy red. The full-bodied youthfully austere palate is still tightly wound but already shows mature black cherry, raspberry preserve, licorice and grilled herb set against a backbone of firm fine-grained tannins. Give it time to fully develop. Drink 2022–2030.
title: Campo alle Comete 2015  Bolgheri Superiore

description: Smoothly textured, this fruity wine offers acidity and ripe apricot flavors. The zesty, mineral texture will allow it to develop well, promising of great fruit and texture in the future. Wait until 2024.
title: Château Malartic-Lagravière 2015  Pessac-Léognan

description: This powerful wine has great structure, fine and elegant tannins, and ripe black fruits. It wears its heart on its sleeve. The concentration doesn't take away from the juicy fruit and wealth of complex flavors. A wine with a great future, it won't be ready to drink before 2026.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Vieux Château Mazerat  (Saint-Émilion)

description: Perhaps the best vintage of Compostella to date, the 2015 vintage is a blend of 40% Cabernet Franc, 32% Cabernet Sauvignon, 13% Merlot, 11% Petit Verdot and 4% Malbec. It showcases a beautiful elegance and herbal edge in waves of cigar tobacco and blackcurrant leaf, with a grounded fruit core of blackberry, plum and cassis. Additional notes of leather and German chocolate cake lend depth and intrigue, while the finish exhibits a masterful evolution between all these components with each sip. Well-structured and superbly balanced, it boasts gripping tannins and ample acidity to frame the ripe fruit. A beauty that should mature masterfully, drink 2023–2033.
title: Mvemve Raats 2015 MR de Compostella Red (Stellenbosch)

description: Already with a wonderful old-gold color, this aromatic wine with its fruit, acidity and touch of spirit is bone dry, but presents a rich texture. The typical intense acidity is right there as well as lemon peel, wood, spice and concentration. This wine, aged in wood and bottled in 2017, is ready to drink.
title: Blandy's 2000 Colheita Single Harvest Verdelho (Madeira)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this full-bodied red opens with aromas of wild berry, exotic spice, aromatic herb and a whiff  of fragrant blue flower. It's elegant and structured, with a youthful tension that underscores juicy wild cherry, raspberry compote, cinnamon and a hint of pipe tobacco. Fresh acidity lends balance while fine-grained tannins provide polished support. It's already tempting but will develop even more complexity with cellaring. Drink 2018–2033.
title: Boscarelli 2013 Il Nocio  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: This wine is dense with superripe tannins. It has rich black-plum and berry fruits along with an opulent texture typical of this wine in good vintages. Along with its power, the wine has great acidity to keep the aftertaste crisp and fruity. Drink from 2024.
title: JCP Maltus 2015 Le Carré  (Saint-Émilion)

description: Jim Barry's top wine and one of Clare Valley's most renowned, the 2012 doesn't disappoint. Enchanting, complex aromas of purple beets, mocha, strawberry leaf, raspberries, figs and dates continue on the palate, all cloaked in savory, powdery tannins and a structure that sets it up for many years of aging. Drink 2019–2037.
title: Jim Barry 2012 The Armagh Shiraz (Clare Valley)

description: This is one of the top vineyard selections in this vintage, among a select group, notable for its outstanding focus and concentration. Still compact, its purple fruits are lightly spiced and deftly balanced; leaving little doubt that further improvement is just around the corner. Drink now through 2035.
title: Ken Wright 2015 McCrone Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: The nose is intense and heady, combining unlikely but harmonized elements of white currant, fresh white mushroom, honey, pear and lemon zest. The palate then majors on fresh, pure and fluid pear juiciness. There is a textural element and overtones of beeswax reinforced by more lemon zestiness. A very complex, very alive and facinating wine. This is mouthfilling, long and intense, utterly full-flavored but resolutely dry.
title: Maurice Schoech & Fils 2013 Harmonie R White (Alsace)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This particular block selection is focused upon old-vine Pommard grapes, and the result is a layered and deeply focused wine that continually evolves once poured. It's a delicate explosion of textures and flavors, berry and cherry, wet stone, cola and caramel. Though it will surely age beautifully over a decade or longer, if you give it ample aeration and attention, it drinks quite nicely already.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Durant Vineyard Bishop Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The vineyard cannot be disclosed, but there's nothing mysterious about the flavor, which pins the meter on the yum factor. Juicy huckleberry flavors come wrapped in mineral-drenched acids. Made from 100% Pommard grapes, this marries Old World complexity to pretty purple fruits. As with many Patricia Green wines, the oak aging is a minor player, while the ripe fruits and individual terroirs are expressed in riveting fashion.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Mysterious Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Wet river rocks and mineral oil juxtapose zesty tangerine, pomelo and grapefruit in this lavish yet composed dry Riesling. It's clingy and plush in mouthfeel yet vibrantly balanced in acidity, with an unctuous and rippling finish. It's stunning already for its youthful fruit and electric acidity, but sure to improve further through 2035.
title: Robert Weil 2016 Kiedrich Gräfenberg Trocken GG Riesling (Rheingau)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dried sagebursh, wild cherries, rose petals and baked soil aromas make for a compelling blend of savory and sweet on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard on the eastern side of the appellation. A chalky texture and front-to-back acidity presents flavors of ripe boysenberry and black raspberry as well as clove, black pepper and other intriguingly dark spices.
title: Storm 2015 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Long associated with the Graham's name, this quinta makes impressive Ports. This hugely tannic and equally hugely fruity Port has a dry backbone as well as potentially rich black fruits. Acidity and a dry edge give structure to this dense wine. Drink from 2028.
title: W. & J. Graham's 2015 Quinta dos Malvedos Vintage  (Port)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Instantly gratifying, this wine starts off ripe, focused and compact, with raspberries and pie cherries galore on the nose. Let it breathe and it offers complex acid-driven tones of pineapple and orange peel. Barrel aging shows from start to finish, with rich, toasty aromas and streaks of butter and vanilla cookie. It's delicious from first sniff to last sip.
title: Domaine Serene 2014 Winery Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Made from the original 150 year-old vines—of which just 800 remain—this wine is made only in the best years. The 2012 is a heady mélange of blue and purple fruits, raspberry leaf, beets, coffee and wet leaves. The medium-bodied palate is approachable now with its silky, sexy tannins, plush fruit and a complex, superlong finish, but this will age beautifully for many years to come. Drink now–2027.
title: Kilikanoon 2012 Attunga 1865 Shiraz (Clare Valley)

description: Pretty blossom, guava and pink grapefruit notes abound in this gorgeously perfumed, dry Riesling. It's lively and fresh, but maintains a creamy lusciousness on the palate. Concentrated and zingy, it lingers through a long finish marked by murmurs of astringency. Drinks beautifully already but should improve through 2030 and hold further.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Abtsberg GG Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Demure notes of white blossom, pineapple and lemon amass power and ripeness on the palate of this dry, medium-bodied Riesling. Silken and finely textured, it offers penetrating pink-grapefruit and yellow-peach flavors highlighted by zippy streaks of lemon-lime acidity and a cool, crystalline finish.
title: Maximin Grünhäuser 2016 Herrenberg GG Riesling (Mosel)

description: Superrich and undeniably flavorful, this stand-out wine achieves fine balance despite the huge tannins. It has an utterly dark color that stains the glass, wonderful aromas of crushed raspberries and black cherries, and deep fruit flavors that weave through tones of dark chocolate and mint. The mouthfeel is notably dense but somehow gentle. Best from 2020–2030.
title: Portalupi 2015 L. Venturi Vineyards Petite Sirah (Mendocino County)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fresh Asian pear, lemon blossoms and honeysuckle make for an enjoyable nose on this bottling from a very coastally influenced vineyard just east of Santa Maria. The acidity on the palate is super racy and vibrant, carrying flavors of chalk and lime pith that sizzle long and deep.
title: Whitcraft 2016 Presqu'ile Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Truffle, flint and wild berry aromas mingle with pipe tobacco and a whiff of new leather on this radiant red. On the savory precise palate, licorice and white pepper underscore a juicy core of red cherry and cranberry alongside a flinty mineral vein. Polished tannins and fresh acidity provide impeccable balance. Drink through 2032.
title: Arpepe 2012 Sassella Stella Retica  (Valtellina)

description: Tight and firm, this young wine has great potential. The richness, good acidity and minerality promise a great future. Densely textured, it is powerful and very rich. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Smith Haut Lafitte 2015  Pessac-Léognan

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Toasty blackberry and cassis aromas are classic for Ribera, while the firm, compact palate is structured but not fiercely tannic. Flavors of spiced blackberry, plum and fig finish with the right interplay between fruit and oak. Drink through 2028.
title: 12 Linajes 2012 Reserva  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Big, potent and deeply concentrated, this holds nothing back with its rich, ripe black-cherry-compote flavor. Lavender overtones lead into more expressive layers of vanilla, black tea and Kahlúa. It is intense and lingering.
title: Abacela 2014 Reserve South East Block Tempranillo (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a whopper of a Pinot Noir, seen through the eyes of a Paso Robles winemaker. Dark and thick in the glass, it shows black cherry, clove and allspice on the nose. The massive palate offers blackberry and boysenberry flavors, with spicy gingersnap notes giving balance.
title: Cordant 2015 Kessler Haak Vineyards Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This Grenache is a little older than the typical release, and that gives it some extra depth. Aromas of cranberry sauce, baking spice, rose petals, hibiscus and carnations are nose-tickling. The palate is loaded with spice cake, gingerbread and rounded, slightly sour red-cherry flavors, altogether soft and pleasing.
title: Curran 2012 Grenache (Santa Barbara County)

description: Gentle ripe, red cherry makes for an appetizing opening on the nose. The same fruit is vivid on the smooth and fluid palate where freshness gives wonderful direction. The tannins exercise their fine grip with elegance. This has lovely depth, elegance and resonance.
title: Domaine Charles Frey 2015 F Pinot Noir (Alsace)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of Merlot (61%) and Cabernet Franc. High-toned aromas of black raspberry, plum, black licorice, herb and barrel spice lead to plush, plentiful red- and black-fruit flavors. It brings a very pretty sense of styling along with a lengthy finish. Give it some time to open up.
title: Efeste 2014 Nana Willow Vineyard Red (Yakima Valley)

description: Intense aromas of dusty slate and flint show prominently but there's also plenty of ripe, penetrating yellow-peach and nectarine flavors waiting to burst through. High toned acidity and a fine, filigreed texture mark the midpalate. It's a complex, faceted wine that should meld further from 2021–2030.
title: Fritz Haag 2016 Brauneberger Juffer Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: A dark color and charred, lemony black-fruit aromas announce a wine that's loaded with hard-pounding oak and tannins. This wine tastes super-toasty, blackened and slightly burnt, with black-fruit flavors at the center. This will work best if quality oak applied in abundance is your thing; drink through 2028.
title: García Figuero 2014 Viña Viejas  (Ribera del Duero)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Truffle, new leather, menthol, star anise and red berry are just some of the aromas you'll find on this perfumed red. Made with 95% Sangiovese and 5% Canaiolo, the medium-bodied palate offers juicy Marasca cherry, raspberry compote and licorice set against fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Gracciano della Seta 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Notes of white grapefruit, apple and pear are obscured in layers of smoke, dark herb and mineral, but with a few years in the cellar, these intense earth and mineral complexities should soften and meld. This is a spine tingling, concentrated and complex wine that's worth waiting till 2021 to uncork.
title: Günther Steinmetz 2016 Brauneberger Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Own-rooted Pommard clone Pinot Noir, planted in 1972, is the focal point for a wine rich in texture and detail. The mix of black fruits carries highlights of coffee and char, from aging for 20 months in a small percentage of new French oak. The finish sneaks up on you and lingers gracefully. As with all the Holloran wines, clarity and balance are first and foremost.
title: Holloran 2014 Le Pavillon Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Designation: Johannisberger Auf der Höll Trocken Erste Lage
description: Pristine lime and grapefruit tones are flourished by spring blossoms in this invigorating dry Riesling. Spry and racy, it offers a crush of fresh citrus flavors that dart and skip like laser beams on the palate. The finish is lean but meandering. Drink now through 2035.
title: Johannishof 2016 Johannisberger Auf der Höll Trocken Erste Lage Riesling (Rheingau)

description: This is round and pretty, and seems, at least for now, to be a bit more accessible than some of the more compact and ageworthy 2015 single-vineyard selections from Ken Wright. Drink it up soon, and enjoy the light touches of orange peel and cinnamon highlights.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Tanager Vineyard Pinot Noir

description: This six-acre Malbec vineyard is one of a handful planted in Bourg and Blaye where the grape now ripens more easily than in the past. This Bordeaux view of the grape is rich, with warm tannins and black fruit along with acidity and spice. This powerful, ripe wine from Tutiac's single-variety, single-parcel range should age, so drink from 2020.
title: Les Vignerons de Tutiac 2015 Lieu-Dit Ter Pointe  (Côtes de Bourg)

description: This is the third release of a wine from a new venture by Niepoort into the Dâo region. Made from 100-year-old vines, it celebrates two local grapes, Baga and Jaen, which dominate the blend. It is structured, firm and has intense fruit as well as tannins. The wine is young, fruity, elegant and has a good future. Drink from 2021.
title: Niepoort 2015 Conciso Red (Dão)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Still young, with sharp barrel flavors, this will need another year or two of bottle age to fully smooth out. Punchy, tart apple and peach fruit is bright and toasty, with a touch of brioche. Give it that extra bottle age, and drink after 2020.
title: Phelps Creek 2015 Lynette Chardonnay (Columbia Gorge (OR))

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Fragrant woodland berry, blue flower and cake spice aromas mingle with a balsamic menthol note. The concentrated but almost weightless palate offers juicy raspberry, fleshy black cherry, espresso and star anise alongside polished supple tannins.
title: Poliziano 2015  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Smooth and showing primary bready flavors, this has a concentrated core of apple and quince, with plenty of barrel flavors. Roasted peanuts, lemon zest and a touch of salted caramel come up, but this young wine really needs another year or two in bottle to show its best.
title: Ponzi 2015 Aurora Vineyard Chardonnay (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Zesty, aromatic notes of lemon and tangerine carry the nose. The palate then bursts forth with ripe citrus: tangerine, orange and mandarin. This off-dry version of Sylvaner may be unusual but it is a triumph of balance. The concentrated, zesty freshness of citrus is countered by the ripeness of the fruit on a very light, fresh, concentrated and compelling palate. This is intriguing and irresistible.
title: René Muré 2016 Cuvée Oscar Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Dominated by dark-colored, dense Alicante Bouschet, this is a rich wine. Its tannins are still up front, although watch for the rich black fruits as they develop. Aged in wood, it has vanilla and spice flavors that enhance the ripe fruit of the wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Ribafreixo 2014 Gáudio Reserva Red (Alentejano)

description: This is a small-production wine from a former French soccer player. It is soft, with great fruit and a rounded texture. Generous while also dense, it's powered by firm tannins. Drink this ripe wine from 2025.
title: Sol Béni 2015  Saint-Émilion

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is all Cinsault from Olsen Vineyard. Aged in neutral French oak, it's resplendent with aromas of raspberry jam, potpourri, plum, flower and garrigue. The palate is transparent in style with elegant but rich red-fruit and plum flavors that lead to a lengthy, warm finish.
title: Two Vintners 2015 Make Haste Cinsault (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dense aromas of strawberry, mace and vanilla combine for an alluringly rich experience on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Notes of clove, allspice and gingerbread rise on the palate, where the background tones of mulberry and elderberry jam prove elegant and interesting.
title: Velvet Bee 2013 Bentrock Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: This wine is spicy, savory and dense—a full-bodied wine from the sparkling wine producer which farms throughout the appellation. Buzzing acidity lies beneath a hearty helping of black cherry, strawberry and cola.
title: WindVane 2015 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Carneros)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Plush and candied aromas of red cherry compote, rum butter, vanilla and mulling spice show on the nose of this bottling. Opulent cherry and rhubarb flavors mix with dark spice, bay leaf and tarragon on the palate, all riding a compelling texture with sizzling acidity.
title: Wrath 2015 KW Ranch Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

Appellation: Chehalem Mountains, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Dry as labeled, redolent with the inviting crispness of mountain air, this brings a lovely mix of green apple, pear, melon and cucumber flavors. Bright citrus highlights the zippy acids, leading to a lip-smacking, tart but oh-so-satisfying finish.
title: Anam Cara 2014 Nicholas Estate Dry Riesling (Chehalem Mountains)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This 100% varietal wine is a blend of top sites: Boushey (55%) and Red Willow, with just a kiss (5%) of new French oak. The aromas are intriguing, with notes of white grapefruit, nectarine, lemon pith, straw, mineral and herb. Flavors are sleek and puckering, with a steely spine of acidity that keeps the interest high. It only gets better with time open and should sing at the dinner table.
title: Avennia 2016 Oliane Sauvignon Blanc (Yakima Valley)

description: This offers nuanced aromas of black cherry, red plum, rosewater, crisp red-delicious apple, burnt orange and Christmas spices. These aromas unfold onto the palate where the spices really sing. While this wine is in balance, boasting bright and zippy acidity and ample weight, the woody and spiced tannins stand out and steal the show.
title: Barboursville Vineyards 2014 Reserve Nebbiolo (Virginia)

description: This is a dense wine, packed with black fruits and rich tannins. The dominance of Cabernet Sauvignon gives the wine its ripe, juicy black-currant flavors and good acidity. This 56-acre estate has made a wine that will age well. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Clauzet 2015  Saint-Estèphe

description: This wine comes from an historic 700-acre estate in the southern Médoc that's home to vines, forests and stallions. The wine is ripe and full of black currants from its majority of Cabernet Sauvignon. Rich and packed with generous fruit, the wine shows the progress the property has made in recent years. It still holds plenty of tannins, so age further and drink from 2023.
title: Château de Malleret 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: The estate recalls the Pontac family, once one of the largest landowners in Bordeaux, who built this grand 16th-century hunting lodge. Today's wine is well balanced with ripe tannins, juicy red fruits and acidity. The 60% Merlot gives a full, generous character, although the tannins need time to soften. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Pontac Monplaisir 2015  Pessac-Léognan

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a supple and sexy version of a notoriously tannic wine. It has a deep dark color, enticing dark chocolate and blackberry aromas and a thick layer of berries and black cherries on the palate. Tightly meshed tannins give it plenty of pucker power, and mean that this wine can age for quite a while. Best from 2020 to 2025.
title: Concannon 2014 Reserve Polo Field Petite Sirah (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Ribera del Duero, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Lusty black-fruit aromas are earthy, oaky and smooth, exemplifying an excellent Ribera del Duero. A palate with pulsing acidity tempers rich flavors of blackberry, chocolate and vanilla. Creamy oak and some fire on the finish guarantee that this will evolve nicely through 2028.
title: Astrales 2014  Ribera del Duero

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The estate vineyard, now about 15-years-old, delivers spicy black-cherry fruit deftly set against dark toasty barrel flavors. Just one-quarter new French oak was used, but winemaker Mo Ayoub is a barrel master who mixes and matches the oak like a painter mixes his or her colors. There's a strong streak of coffee, and it finishes with light accents of forest mushrooms.
title: Ayoub 2015 Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Produced close to Saint-Estèphe, this Merlot-dominated wine is powerful and dense. Great black fruits and juicy acidity contrast with firm tannins and the ripe texture from wood aging. This rich wine will age well. Drink from 2024.
title: Château d'Hanteillan 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: The rich wood and fruit of this wine already show good balance. The estate, owned by Bordeaux negociant Dourthe, is now on excellent form with this structured wine offering concentration and long-term aging. The tannins and the dry core from roughly equal proportions of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot and a pinch of Petit Verdot promise great potential. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Le Boscq 2015  Saint-Estèphe

description: The fifth vintage from this estate since its purchase by the Barton family of Léoville Barton in Saint-Julien, this is a generous, structured wine. It has ripe black fruits in profusion that are held together by the serious layer of tannins. Rich and concentrated, the wine will age for many years. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Mauvesin Barton 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

description: The second wine of Château Valandraud, this is rich, spicy with great fruit and firm tannins. The wood aging is still showing but it will soon integrate better with the ripeness of the rich fruit. This wine has weight, plenty of black fruit and acidity. Drink from 2022.
title: Château Valandraud 2015 Virginie  (Saint-Émilion)

description: A closed nose offers a hint of cherry. The palate follows with a balance of pure cherry, firm tannin and bright freshness graced with a touch of dark peony. This is understated, light-bodied but wonderfully elegant and has a habit of growing on you slowly and stealthily. This has a long and lovely resonance.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2016 Fronholz Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This higher-end, elegantly packaged bottling delivers depth, starting with a well-woven, unique nose of baked strawberry, cinnamon, juniper, peppercorns and tobacco. Those elements converge on the palate alongside sandalwood, Montmorency cherry and vanilla, bolstered by a soft but steady acidity.
title: Donati Family Vineyard 2014 Family Reserve Grenache (Paicines)

description: There's a lifted, vibrant impression to the nose of this impressive, well-crafted wine. It's a pleasant surprise, as it lends levity and definition to the notes of black cherry, crushed purple flowers, licorice root and earth. The mouthfeel is plush and mouthfilling, yet not oppressively so, with ample acidity and structured but velvety tannins. The rich, ripe and robust palate leads into a long finish. It promises a long life of beautiful evolution ahead, maturing to reveal greater nuance and elegance, so cellar through 2030.
title: Kanonkop 2014 Black Label Estate Wine Pinotage (Simonsberg-Stellenbosch)

description: The 2013 Attunga expresses aromas of blueberries, baking spices, coffee and tarragon. It's currently very youthful, with rich, plush fruit and big fine-grained tannins, but structurally it's set up for a long and beautiful life. Drink 2020–2037.
title: Kilikanoon 2013 Attunga 1865 Shiraz (Clare Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bright and fresh aromas of pomelo pith, grapefruit flesh, guava skins and tangerine souffle show on the nose of this top-tier Chardonnay by veteran Rick Longoria. Clean and crisp flavors of mandarin pith and a crushed marble minerality give backbone to the richer touches of macadamia nut, cashew and the slightest kiss of buttercream.
title: Longoria 2015 Cuvée Diana Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Batuta, named after the baton used by an orchestral conductor to blend various musical instruments together, has splendid structure and fruit. It is richly textured with dense tannins as well as layers of black fruits. It holds fruit from three north-facing vineyards on the south bank of the Douro, giving the wine elegance and potential. Drink from 2020.
title: Niepoort 2015 Batuta Red (Douro)

description: This monumental wine is one for the ages—or at least for 15 years. A deep color is followed by gorgeous aromas of rose, dark chocolate and blackberry syrup. The mouthfilling, ripe fruit flavors are concentrated, bolstered by thick, powerful tannins that give it a mouth-puckering astringency. The firmness sets this wine up for a long life. Best from 2022–2032.
title: Parducci 2015 True Grit Reserve Petite Sirah (Mendocino)

Appellation: Ribbon Ridge, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
Designation: Blanc de Blancs Extra Brut Methode Champenoise Estate Grown
description: The new sparkling wine project from Trisaetum is off to a spectacular start. This 100% Chardonnay sparkler is refined and detailed. It keeps a lid on the excessive fruitiness and brings in the minerality, toast and overall finesse of the finest Old World examples.
title: Pashey 2014 Blanc de Blancs Extra Brut Methode Champenoise Estate Grown Sparkling (Ribbon Ridge)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The Weber vineyard, originally planted in 1978, shows well with bold bright black-cherry fruit, amplified with lemony acids and flavors of lemon meringue. The lingering midpalate leads gracefully into a smooth, lusciously fruity finish.
title: Patricia Green Cellars 2016 Weber Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Aromas of struck matches and smoke are pronounced initially but dissipate into elegant mineral complexities on the palate of this intensely fruity kabinett. Juicy tangerine and grapefruit flavors are sweet and zesty, edged by a bracing fringe of astringency on the finish.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2016 Piesporter Goldtröpfchen Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: A beautifully pure topnote of black cherry sings on the nose, followed by peony and cinnamon. The palate is luminous in a dark cherry flavor. This wine is profound and concentrated but nothing weighs it down. Drink 2018–2028.
title: René Muré 2016 Clos St Landelin Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This shows beatutifully aromatic notes of fresh Cox Orange Pippin apple, jasmine and rose. The palate brims with these same notions, made bolder by pure, lively lemon freshness. The streamlined palate has a pithy, zesty edge that makes you want to drink more. This is a lovely, concentrated, grown-up Muscat of unusual power. The finish is bone-dry, a little salty and lingering.
title: René Muré 2016 Steinstuck Muscat (Alsace)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Soft and alluring, this wine opens in bursts of rose petal and black tea, with plenty of baking spices accenting its core of cherry, blueberry and rhubarb. As the wine mellows on the finish, it show a velvety texture of round, supple tannins.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Foss Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A whiff of white flowers, then a crush of ripe fruit, and the wine opens with tangy lemon, grapefruit and peach flavors. Notes of citrus rind add depth and the acids strike just the right chord.
title: Amalie Robert 2016 Our Muse Viognier (Willamette Valley)

description: This 11-acre property, centered on an ultramodern winery, is the most northerly estate in Margaux. The wine, with its blend of dark tannins and juicy black-currant fruit, is rich, with impressive concentration and minerality. With weight and a firm core, the wine will age well. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Bellevue de Tayac 2015  Margaux

description: This is a structured wine, beautifully perfumed with richly tannic black fruits. It comes from a large 111-acre estate in the village of the same name. Made to age with firm tannins and layers of sumptuous fruit over firm wood aging, the wine will mature slowly. Drink from 2024.
title: Château Cissac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: This dense and impressively ripe wine comes from a Sorge family estate in the northern part of the Margaux appellation. With black fruits and generous tannins, it is a big wine with considerable richness. This complex wine will age. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Deyrem Valentin 2015  Margaux

description: This wine comes from a 50-acre crus bourgeois estate in the middle of some of the greatest crus classés estates. The terroir is impressive, giving a rich, structured wine with good concentration, ripe tannins and a sumptuous bed of black fruits. The wine is likely to age well; drink from 2025.
title: Château Fonbadet 2015  Pauillac

description: A touch of orange zest, honeysuckle and rose petals make for a gently aromatic opening. On the palate it is the sheer concentration of flavor that hits you, there seems to be a force of life straining to break free, which swishes across the dry palate in rich flavors of orange zest and florals. This is a vivid, arresting wine which is lovely now but sure has mileage. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2016 Fronholz Muscat (Alsace)

description: Stark, electric acidity bolsters this intensely steely yet deeply fruity off-dry Riesling. It's a cutting, spine tingling wine pulsating with staccato hits of white grapefruit, stone fruit and citrus flavors. The finish is gossamer and sheer yet endless.
title: Egon Müller 2016 Scharzhof Riesling (Mosel)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Made with the traditional blend of Sangiovese, Colorino and Canaiolo, this opens with aromas of pressed violet, woodland berry, menthol and dark culinary spice. The supple full-bodied palate delivers juicy morello cherry, black raspberry and star anise accompanied by fresh acidity and polished tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Il Conventino 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The Carter is a complex, even rugged wine, replete with layered flavors of herb, black cherry, cola and a touch of molasses. The tannins are as smooth as river rock, with appealing mineral and rock flavors following right behind.
title: Ken Wright 2015 Carter Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Far from simply fruity, this is a complex and sophisticated wine that shows how distinctive the grape variety can be. It relies on savory, minerally flavors that have wonderful subtlety and reserve. With each sip it settles a little deeper on the palate, lingering for a long finish buoyed by underlying acidity.
title: Lava Cap 2016 Viognier (El Dorado)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This new offering from John Kinney's tiny, consistently high-performing winery is big and muscular—very ripe and full bodied, generously laced with butter, toasted oak and almond flavors on a particularly creamy texture but not going over the top. Only about six barrels were made so it's rare, but worth a search.
title: Occasio 2016 Del Arroyo Vineyard Chardonnay (Livermore Valley)

description: Leafy, herbaceous tones are juxtaposed by robust, ripe black-plum and raspberry flavors here. Full bodied and quite silky on the palate, this wine is framed by fine-grained, persistent tannins. Drink now through 2021.
title: Domaine Chamfort 2014 Red (Vacqueyras)

description: This wine is still young, with its fruit tied up with the tannic structure and dry core. However, its juicy, red-berry character will emerge as it develops into a richer, less taut wine. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de Bel-Air 2016 Granits Roses  (Fleurie)

Winery: Domaine de la Combe au Loup
description: Produced from 50-year-old vines, this is cool, crisp wine with a fine layer of tannins as well as plenty of acidity. It comes from a small parcel in the high-altitude vineyards of Chiroubles. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de la Combe au Loup 2016  Chiroubles

description: This wine is traditional in style and fruity with light tannins and spice. Aromatic and crisp at the end, the wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine de Roche Guillon 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: Dark shades of earth, sage and just a hint of rubber lend a rustic tone to restrained black plum and berry in this wine. It's savory yet elegant, offsetting a supple, mouthcoating palate with fresh acidity and hints of curry and cardamom spices. Baby-smooth tannins accentuate a soft finish.
title: Domaine Guy Mousset 2015 Les Garrigues Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are bright and focused, with notes of lemon pith, candle wax and spice. The palate brings a mixture of peach and orange flavors. It provides a lot of appeal, especially at this price.
title: Domaine Ste. Michelle NV Brut Méthode Champenoise Sparkling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Ripe aromas of peach, yellow apple and orchard blossoms offer some concentration on the nose. Medium sweet in style, there's a weighty feel to the stone-fruit flavors, with a mild vein of lime maintaining balance.
title: Dr. Konstantin Frank 2016 Reserve Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Delicate aromas of baking spice and red plum come together on this. Made entirely with Sangiovese, the palate delivers wild cherry, vanilla and a touch of coconut alongside round tannins.
title: Fattoria Le Fonti 2014 Le Fonti  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with aromas suggesting prune and star anise. The aromas carry over to palate along with a hint of wild raspberry, chopped herb and bright acidity. It's already approachable, with dusty tannins that provide loose support. Drink through 2019.
title: Fattoria Poggerino 2014 Bugialla Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Jammy black-fruit aromas include notes of raisin. This is wild, inexact and saucy in feel, with dense but slightly mushy flavors of plum and raspberry. A chunky, saturated finish is steady but plodding.
title: Garcia + Schwaderer 2014 Vigno Old Vines Dry Farmed Carignan (Maule Valley)

description: This wine has some structure and tannic character, although the fruit dominates. Blackberry flavors are shot through with acidity supported by a dry core of tannins. The wine is still developing and will be better from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This is a very pleasant, smooth and ripe-tasting wine that glides across the palate and spreads a jammy blackberry flavor to the corners of the mouth. It's soft in terms of acidity and tannins and has a sensation of sweetness from its ripeness.
title: Identified 2015 Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: From one of the top climats or areas of Morgon, this wine is richly structured but has plenty of berry fruits laced with cherry acidity. It is ripe and certain to age further with the tannins that are still prominent. Drink from late 2018.
title: Jean-François Coquard 2016 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: This wine is fresh, crisp and offers blackberry fruit. It is light, softly tannic and juicy. Drink now.
title: Jean-Michel Dupré 2016 Haute Ronze Vieilles Vignes  (Régnié)
