Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Pale lemon in color, this is quince-driven on the nose, with ripe, pithy citron and lime notes that carry to the dry palate. Medium in weight, yet boasting a soft, pillowy texture, vibrant acidity leads mineral-forward flavors, with a delicate marshmallowy finish.
title: Kelby James Russell 2014 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: In the northern reaches of the country, just above Auckland, this winery is best known for its Burgundian style Chardonnays. Their 2015 Pinot certainly walks to its own beat. A mélange of orange peel, cumin, florals, meats, dried leaves and wet earth all swirl together. The palate is light to medium bodied, with flavors of earth, stones, and dusting polish. There's a slightly off-putting bitterness on the finish, but this is nevertheless charming in an old school kind of way.
title: Kumeu River 2015 Hunting Hill Pinot Noir (Kumeu)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This dry wine has a vivid gold color and smells earthy at first, but it soon opens up to rich peanut and honey aromas, soothing almond and marzipan flavors and a mouth-clinging texture.
title: La Folia Winery 2014 Fiano (Sierra Foothills)

description: A nose of ripe, almost confected tropical fruits, apples and waxy lemon lead into a fleshy, chalky-textured palate full of apple skins and citrus. This is a food friendly wine to be enjoyed with a wide variety of dishes.
title: Lexicon 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: A small amount of oak aging in old barrels has had a smoothing effect on this crisp wine, balancing the acidity and fresh red-currant flavors. It is fruity and ready to drink now.
title: Manoir du Carra 2016 Non-Filtré  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Hints of smoked nuts, crushed mineral and blossom accent muted apple and pear in this delicately structured white blend. While restrained in concentration, it's a dry, refreshing wine with a soft lemon-peel note that lingers on the finish.
title: Marrenon 2016 Grande Toque White (Luberon)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Made from a blend of Saperavi and Sereksiya Charni, grapes indigenous to eastern Europe, this red is dense in aromas of coconut cream, sweet spice and brambly fruit. Medium-bodied, with a structured grip of tannins, the palate boasts plenty of crunchy red fruit flavors and sweet oak inflections. Searing acidity currently propels this along, yet with time this should mellow and integrate. Drink from 2020.
title: McGregor 2013 Black Russian Red (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: Pomegranate and plum are complemented by dried herb in this brushy, moderately structured red wine. Thick dense layers of ripeness and oak give it a concentrated power highlighted in black licorice and black pepper.
title: Monticello Vineyards 2014 Estate Grown Syrah (Oak Knoll District)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This 100% varietal Cab has reserved aromas of blackberry Fruit Roll-Ups and barrel spice that lead to medium-bodied fruit flavors. It shows a fair amount of acidity considering the warmth of this vintage.
title: Mustard Seed 2013 Moving Mountains Winesap VIneyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: North Coast, North Coast, California, US
description: Very ripe and saturated with dark fruit flavors, this full-bodied wine combines a jammy character with wisps of smoke and grilled herbs. It gets points for good fruit concentration and feels smooth and full on the palate.
title: Nine North Wine Company 2015 Parcel 41 Merlot (North Coast)

Appellation: Snipes Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: There's no mistaking the variety, with its perfumed aromas of rose petal, lychee and spice. It drinks a hair off dry and is broad in feel, gliding on the finish.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 Ramos Vineyard Gewürztraminer (Snipes Mountain)

description: From 60-year-old vines, this wine has some concentration even though its main thrust is fruity. Tannins underneath the red-cherry fruit and crisp acidity give a wine that is lightly structured and ready to drink.
title: Pardon et Fils 2016 Cuvée Tim  (Régnié)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Combining both citrus pith and more tropical components, this bottling shows fresh melon, Key lime, light guava and a touch of passion fruit on the nose. It tightens up on the palate, with sour orange and lime-sprayed popcorn flavors.
title: Pear Valley 2016 Pear Valley Vineyard Albariño (Paso Robles)

description: Big oak-based aromas are cheesy and feral. This has a full flush mouthfeel with tannic rub. Heavy spicy plum and berry flavors finish with raw scraping tannins. If an elegant, smooth Pinot is what you're after, keep looking.
title: Rafael Tirado Wines 2014 Laberinto Pinot Noir (Maule Valley)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Very ripe fruit flavors like raisins and dried plums give this full-bodied wine a high concentration factor, while moderate tannins add more heft to the texture. It has a mouth-filling richness, high alcohol and a jammy character that lingers on the finish.
title: Russian Ridge 2014 Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: This offers aromas of black-skinned berry, tobacco and a hint of violet while the straightforward palate evokes mature black cherry, vanilla and a note of ground pepper. It's easygoing, with supple tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Savignola Paolina 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from Rosebud Vineyard, the aromas show a bit of oxidation along with a touch of volatility. It's a shame as the palate is full of luscious rich fruit flavors that provide a lot of enjoyment. Vanilla notes linger on the finish.
title: Rio Vista 2015 Tempranillo (Wahluke Slope)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: High-toned cherry and cranberry aromas meld with shades of sweet spice on the nose. Round and juicy in feel, the palate brings a mild intensity of tart cherry and cranberry, with shots of cinnamon and vanilla. This is an easy-sipping, enjoyable red.
title: Sannino 2014 Spotlight Petit Verdot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Lemon, guava, ginger and baking spices lead into a zippy, citrusy and refreshing palate. This is an enjoyable, straightforward wine, perfect for easy sipping.
title: Sea Pearl 2017 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Spry underripe peach, citrus spray and slightly bruised apple mark the nose of this new endeavor from Constellation. Off dry in style, the palate brings a bit more ripeness to the fruit, with a mild vein of acidity offering some lift.
title: 240 Days 2016 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This wine is packed with ripe fruit with a texture giving just the right amount of tannin. The wine has weight, the result of the clay soil in which the vines grow. Tank aging has preserved the cherry flavors and crisp acidity. Drink now.
title: Albert Bichot 2016 Château de Jarnioux  (Beaujolais)

description: This frothy wine is very fruity and lightly sweet, with a strong effervescence and pleasant apple and peach flavors. It tastes bright and lively, very fresh and straightforward.
title: Ca' Momi NV Heartcraft Sparkling White Sparkling (California)

description: This medium-bodied wine has lots of fruitiness in the aromas and the flavors, showing golden apple and Bosc pear in the nose, and richer, almost-sweet yellow-peach flavors on the palate. It's on the soft side in terms of acidity, and has a plush texture.
title: Carson Scott 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: Powdery, yeasty white-fruit aromas are generic but pleasant. A fair palate weighs in on the plump side, while flavors of peach and melon are mild prior to a quick finish with surprisingly low acidity and not much kick.
title: Casa Silva 2017 1912 Vines Sauvignon Gris (Colchagua Valley)

description: There's a green edge to the red berry and plum fruit core of this wine, with touches of fruitcake spice, violet and milk-chocolate brownie to sweeten the deal. It's medium in body, with fine and accessible tannins and a quick finish that ends clean.
title: David Frost 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Western Cape)

description: This smooth, rounded wine offers attractive black cherries and red plums. The wine is full, pure fruit, produced from organically grown grapes. It is soft and ready to drink.
title: Domaine Chasselay 2016 Quatre Saisons  (Beaujolais)

description: Aged for three months in barrel, this smooth, rounded rosé is both fruity and rich. It has good acidity from the attractive red-currant fruit and an orange tang of zest at the end. Drink now.
title: Domaine Dupeuble Père et Fils 2016 Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

description: Elderberry, wood smoke, charred beef and baked soil show on the nose of this bottling from a region south of Hollister. There is a lot of earthy flavor on the sip, with wet soil adding to the tangy black plum skin and smoke qualities.
title: Donati 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paicines)

description: Slightly sour cranberry fruit meets with dust and uncrushed peppercorn on the nose of this mostly Merlot bottling. The tangy red-plum-skin fruit shows on the sip too against a backbone laced in dried garden herbs.
title: Donati 2014 The Immigrant Red (Paicines)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: A very pale yellow in color, this bottling shows aromatic pear and lime juice on the broad yet sharp nose. The texture is a bit flat on the sip, and the peachy flavors are quite strong, with yellow pear in the midpalate and a slightly vinous touch on the finish.
title: Donati 2016 Pinot Blanc (Central Coast)

description: While this wine is all about fruit, it also has some concentration that gives it a full-bodied character that contrasts with the berry flavors and acidity. It is ready to drink.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Coeur de Presse  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Petrol and wild herb notes mask cleaner aromas of citrus and apple on the nose. There's some nice weight to the palate, with bright acidity to match, yet the kerosene note continues to overpower ripe peach and apricot.
title: Heron Hill 2016 Ingle Vineyard Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Designation: Front Row Earth Zin and Fire
description: This is a well-behaved wine that should please a crowd with its easygoing plummy flavors, smooth texture and soft acidity. It's medium-bodied and fresh-tasting.
title: Jessie's Grove 2013 Front Row Earth Zin and Fire Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: This wine has some structure, with an edge of dryness that highlights the ripe red fruits and acidity. A fruity wine from one of the top Burgundy producers, it is ready to drink now.
title: Louis Jadot 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: Aromas of moist soil, new leather and a whiff of dark spice lead the way. The wiry palate is on the lean side, offering hints of sour cherry and dark spice alongside firm acidity and dusty, rather fleeting tannins. Racy acidity keeps it light on its feet. Drink now.
title: Ormanni 2014  Chianti Classico

description: This is basically Sauvignon Blanc, with 10% Carmenère added in for body. As a result, the nose is chunky and smells lightly of pumpkin and squash. A tangy palate is oily in feel, with green-fruit flavors. Popping acidity pushes a salty tasting finish.
title: Oveja Negra 2016 Reserva Sauvignon Blanc-Carmenère White (Maule Valley)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Wilting flowers—green stems and all—meld with soft citrus on the nose. The rounded palate boasts plenty of ripe peach and melon, with loads of more florals dragging along. Mild acidity tries to keep up but ultimately dissipates on a green, geranium finish.
title: Pellegrini Vineyards 2015 Estate Grown Gewürztraminer (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: A rich, slight boozy, nose of brandied cherry, cinnamon and bubble gum spice leads to a primary, ripe palate. Medium in weight, with soft tannins, its showy fruit lingers on the finish.
title: Sannino 2014 Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Citrus and grassy aromas are just lively enough. The same can be said for the palate on this basic SB: it's a touch flabby and not blazing with acidity. Melon, citrus and generic green flavors finish quietly and without edginess. Drink immediately.
title: Santa Ema 2017 Select Terroir Sauvignon Blanc (Maipo Valley)

description: Stewed berry aromas are a touch murky, while this feels dense, tannic and sticky. Stewed earthy plum and berry flavors come with a note of coffee grounds, while this is herbal and beefy on the finish.
title: Siegel 2015 Crucero Collection Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Ripe notes of peach and apricot meld with white flowers and freshly cut grass in this Riesling. There's a nice roundness to the palate, yet the melon and peach flavors lack intensity. Mild acidity leads to a short, yet clean, finish.
title: Silver Thread 2015 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: A green, herbal streak weaves throughout the ripe plum and boysenberry character on the nose and mouth of this solid, straightforward wine. It's well balanced and easy to like, with soft tannins that frame the vibrant plum and cherry flavors, followed by a short yet clean finish.
title: Spier 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Predominately Cabernet Franc, this rosé shows a good intensity of ripe strawberry and raspberry on the nose, with tart pomegranate kicking in on the palate. A well-placed balance of acidity keeps the fruit-filled palate in check, making this a perfect easy-sipping glass.
title: Swedish Hill 2016 Blue Waters Dry Rosé (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: A yellowish shade of pink in the glass, this bottling shows aromas of yellow peach and orange rind. Seared lemon rinds appears on the sip, alongside a touch of walnut in what is almost an oxidative style.
title: Velvet Bee 2016 Rosé (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: País is known for rustic meaty aromas, and this wine, which also contains Moscatel, smells a lot like hot dogs before cooking. A mildly thick palate with floral cherry and raspberry flavors finishes with drying tannins and the taste of rose water.
title: Ventisquero 2015 Reserva País-Moscotel Red (Maule Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Stewed cherries, baking spice and cranberry-laced fruit-punch aromas show on the nose of this bottling. Cooked watermelon, plum jam and pomegranate reveal themselves on the plush palate, which lacks a bit of zip.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2016 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 50% Grenache, 29% Syrah, 14% Carignane and 7% Cinsault is a versatile and well-priced table wine for all occasions. Aromas of hibiscus, berry, cola, rose petal and pine smoke lead into a palate where bay leaf and pepper hold up the red fruit.
title: Tower 15 2015 The Jetty Red (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Aromas of coffee grounds and murky earth cast a shadow over the nose of this Carmenère-Syrah blend. In the mouth, this is wiry and tough, and arguably a bit raw and thin. Herbal plum and currant flavors finish edgy and with abrasive tannins.
title: Ventisquero 2013 Vertice Apalta Vineyard Carmenère-Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

Designation: Gran Reserva Made with Organically Grown Grapes
description: Spicy plum and red-berry aromas are floral. This organic blend is quite hard and tannic, which makes plum and currant flavors seem rough. A scratchy rubbing finish is intense, with an herbaceous note.
title: Viña Tarapacá 2014 Gran Reserva Made with Organically Grown Grapes Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Good depth and concentration lift this medium-bodied and well-balanced wine well above the average. It has complex fruit flavors, light touches of vanilla and butter, and a velvety smooth texture supported by just enough acidity to keep it lively.
title: 786 Wines 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: This is a silky Pinot cloaked in fine, savory tannins and fresh acidity with stalky, cherry cola, red fruit and floral notes. There's a lovely tension in this wine that keeps you going back for more.
title: Ana 2016 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Spicy berry, dry oak and herbal aromas are typical of Chilean Carmenère. This is a lively offering with kicking acidity that's on the high side. Spiced cherry and plum flavors are herbal and juicy due to the wine's acid level, while this tastes oaky and chocolaty on the finish.
title: Arboleda 2015 Carmenère (Aconcagua Valley)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: There's an intriguing blend of candied cherry and rosewater reduction on the nose of this bottling, which includes 5% Syrah, along with sweet bay leaf and fennel. Red currants and cranberries show on the sip, lifted by caramelized sage and pepper dust.
title: Avensole 2013 Aventura Zinfandel (Temecula Valley)

Appellation: Edna Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Grapefruit zest, freshly squeezed lime and a steely minerality show on the nose of this widely available wine made by the Talley family. The palate is tightly wound with more lime skins, but, with time, opens up into flavors of buttered sourdough, marmalade and brioche.
title: Bishop's Peak 2016 Chardonnay (Edna Valley)

description: Baking spices and ripe fruit come together to give this medium-bodied wine a big, friendly personality. It feels broad and smooth on the palate and the vanilla, nutmeg and butter notes are as generous in the flavors as in the aromas.
title: Bonterra 2016 Made with Organic Grapes Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: This opens with aromas of French oak, vanilla, coconut and black-skinned fruit. The aromas carry over to the racy palate along with dried black cherry, blackberry and star anise. Assertive, close-grained leave an astringent finish.
title: Casa Emma 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with aromas of forest floor, oak, leather, exotic spice, coconut and a whiff of baked plum. The firm, extracted palate offers dried cherry, nutmeg and licorice framed in chewy tannins. You'll also detect the warmth of alcohol on the finish. Drink through 2024.
title: Cascina Bruciata 2014  Barbaresco

description: Scorched earth, Asian spice and stewed prune aromas combine with a whiff of camphor. The tight palate offers dried cherry, star anise and espresso alongside grainy tannins that leave an astringent finish. Drink sooner rather than later to capture the remaining fruit richness.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2012 Clemente VII Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: While subdued on the nose, bright yellow peach, apple and lemon flavors come alive on the palate of this wine. While soft in acidity and delicate in concentration, it's a silken sip with a fresh mineral finish.
title: Cave de Tain 2015 Grand Classique  (Saint-Joseph)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Savory notes of freshly tilled soil, dried oregano and sage penetrate into brighter tones of lemon and white peach. This is a soft, rounded wine with medium body and delicate acidity, but the finish is refreshingly mineral. Drink now through 2020.
title: Château de Nages 2015 Vieilles Vignes White (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Pressed apples and pears are layered in honey and caramel in this dry white blend. While medium bodied in style, it has a soft, rippling mouthfeel accentuated by delicate acidity. A waxy honeycomb note lingers on the finish.
title: Château d'Or et de Gueules 2015 Les Cimels White (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Black plum and cherry flavors are plush and round on the nose and palate of this full-bodied wine. It's juicy and supple yet briskly balanced by fresh acidity and a frame of ripe, fine-grained tannins.
title: Château Saint-Louis la Perdrix 2015 La Dernière Croisade Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: This wine is soft, with ripe red fruit and the lightest touch of tannins. It's smooth, open and generous. A succulent, juicy character marks the red-cherry fruit and balanced acidity. Drink now.
title: Domaine de Bel-Air 2016 Briante  (Brouilly)

description: A dense planting of old vines has yielding small quantities of concentrated fruit. This wine is dense, with a firm tannic character as well as bold blackberry fruits. It is rich and will age well. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de Colette 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Régnié)

description: Swathes of ripe black fruits mark out this rich wine. Coming from the village of Lantignié, it is a cut above simple Beaujolais-Villages and that shows in the berry fruits, the wood aging and the balanced acidity. Drink this wine now.
title: Domaine des Capréoles 2016 L'Amourgandise Terroir de Lantignié  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: Luscious black-cherry and raspberry flavors are straightforward and juicy in this voluptuous yet elegantly balanced red. It's a creamy, mouthfilling sip nuanced with hints of nuts and sage. The finish is moderately long and framed by ripe, sinewy tannins.
title: Domaine des Pasquiers 2015 Sablet Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: This ripe, smooth wine is rich and full of black-currant and black-cherry fruits. It is perfumed with just the right amount acidity to balance the richness. A blend from different parcels, the wine is delicious now and ready to drink.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016 Domaine De La Chanaise  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Blended with 10% Cabernet Franc and 10% Cabernet Sauvignon from Riverbend, this wine principally comes from Red Mountain Vineyard. The aromas are all high toned at present, with notes of vanilla, herb and exotic spice, not quite ready to reveal their charms. The red-fruit flavors are plump in feel, lingering on the finish.
title: Estrin 2013 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this offers aromas of tobacco leaf, black-skinned berry and an earthy whiff of truffle. The structured palate delivers black plum, clove and a touch of cinnamon alongside pliant tannins.
title: Fietri 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This perfumed wine is rich in black-cherry fruit and has a generous, rounded texture and crisp acidity. Delicious already, it is smooth and ripe, ready to drink now.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée J  (Juliénas)

description: New leather, grilled porcini, fig and charred soil aromas meld with balsamic notes. The forward, structured palate offers stewed prune, dried cherry and clove set against fine-grained tannins. Give the tannins a few more years to soften then drink to capture the remaining fruit richness.
title: Franco Conterno 2011 Pietrin  (Barolo)

description: Fresh yellow peach, apple and pear notes are accented by bitter walnut skin and bramble in this bold, richly textured wine. While dry in style, hints of honey and blossom lend a perception of sweetness that lingers on the finish.
title: François Grenier 2016 Fragrance  (Saint-Joseph)

description: This wine is full of juicy fruit and a balanced structure of tannins. It is ripe yet shows a crisp edge of acidity. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Château de Saint Amour  (Saint-Amour)

description: Named after the small chapel that tops the hill behind the village of Fleurie, this wine is structured and firm. It has some fine tannins as well as plenty of juicy red-cherry fruits. It will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 La Madone  (Fleurie)

description: This wine comes from a five-acre parcel planted on schist. The 50-year-old vines have produced a solid wine, perfumed with red cherries and flavored with rich berry and black-cherry fruit. The tannins are integrating well to give a wine that will be ready from late 2018.
title: Henry Fessy 2016  Juliénas

description: Oaky, oily and robust, this white is moderate in acidity, offering a focused line of stone fruit that's fleshy and ripe.
title: Hoopes 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Coombsville)

description: Opaque in color, this dense, rich wine feels nearly impenetrable. Hidden beneath the dusty oak and ripe blackberry flavors lie savory, herbal undertones. There's juicy black fruit on the palate and the tannins are drying but the fruit keeps things just supple enough to be approachable.
title: Villa Maria 2015 Cellar Selection Merlot-Cabernet Sauvignon (Hawke's Bay)

description: Rubbery roasted aromas of burnt leaves and generic berry fruits are fine but not compelling. This Cab has a big jammy outlook. Candied flavors of berry fruits, oak and brown sugar convey ripeness, but the finish is low on acidity and weak on tannins, and thus this ends quietly.
title: Viña Aquitania 2015 Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Currant, tomato and grassy scents bring ripeness into question. This feels solid but grabby, while slightly green and oaky flavors of medicinal red fruits carry over to a steady finish.
title: Viña Chocalan 2015 Gran Reserva Origen Pinot Noir (Maipo Valley)

description: A gritty nose smells of asphalt, which brings overall ripeness into question. A flat wide palate is resiny and oaky, while this tastes a bit burnt and green, with tarry berry flavors.
title: Viña Tarapacá 2015 Gran Reserva Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

Appellation: New York, New York Other, New York, US
description: Sweet aromas of coconut and vanilla dance over light fruit tones of cherry and strawberry on the nose. This Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant blend shows a playful palate of spice, cherry and anise, with enough tension to keep you coming back for more.
title: Whitecliff Vineyard 2014 Sky Island Red (New York)

description: This is a very smooth, gentle wine, offering plenty of lush strawberry and cherry in the aroma and flavor while not going overboard in body. It feels supple and lightly sweet on the palate, and has very mild tannins.
title: Zinsane 2015 Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: Grassy aromas are mildly citric and show cool, underripe notes of canned peas and beans. A citric bolt adds life to an otherwise fleshy palate, while this tastes of green grape skins, lime and asparagus prior to a slightly bitter finish.
title: Amayna 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

description: Cola spice, cherry stems and red plum lead the entry of this pleasant Syrah. The lightweight palate is easy and accessible, with juicy red-fruit tones that fill the palate. Hints of baking spice grace the close. Drink up.
title: Balance 2015 Shiraz (Western Cape)

description: Generous oak and tannin dominate around a sizable ripeness of cherry and vanilla in this youthful wine. Tart acidity accents the baked character of the fruit.
title: C R Graybehl 2015 Mathis Vineyard Grenache (Sonoma Valley)

description: New leather, camphor and dark spice aromas take shape on this. The wispy, light-bodied palate isn't as expressive, offering suggestions of tart cherry and ground brown spice alongside loose tannins and racy bright acidity. Drink now.
title: Castello di Monterinaldi 2014  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Crushed asphalt and wet gravel meet with baked black cherry and a slight hint of herbs on the nose of this bottling. Though the acidity is balanced, the tannins are a bit clunky on the palate, where boysenberry, tar, cocoa and coffee flavors arise.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Merlot (Paso Robles)

description: After a few swirls of the glass, you'll detect aromas of fleshy black cherry, leather and culinary spice. On the lean, linear palate, you'll find sour cherry, orange zest and chopped sage set against brisk acidity and bracing, grainy tannins.
title: Collina San Ponzio 2013  Barolo

description: A five-acre parcel of Chardonnay is the source of this soft, fruity wine. Wood aging has added extra richness as well as spice to the crisp apple and pear flavors. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine Dupeuble Père et Fils 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: Brawny and rustic, this wine is sizable in tannin and oak, making for a big presence on the palate. Spicy clove is met by leathery tobacco and varietal aspects of cedar and dried herb.
title: Eagle Eye 2013 Estate AlphaWolf Vineyard Reserve Cabernet Franc (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: This single-vineyard Cabernet Franc has an interesting nose of dried beef and mesquite over a ripe cherry core. The soft, pillowy palate gives way to sweet vanilla and cherry flavors, balanced by a mild current of cranberry acidity. Oak dust marks the close.
title: Heron Hill 2014 Ingle Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Rustic cherry, rhubarb and turned loam, along with a touch of fresh thyme, show on the nose of this fancifully labeled twist-top bottling. It's a bit thin on the palate, with sour red fruit, bay leaf and anise flavors.
title: It's a Headsnapper 2015 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)

description: A one-acre parcel of young vines has given this bright, crisp wine. With its flavors of pear and touches of apricot it is both fruity and brightly textured. Drink this wine now.
title: Jean-Marc Burgaud 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: This wine pulls from all over Sonoma County to create a tannic, oaky and fruity combination of lush black cherry and cassis wrapped around vanilla and dried herb. Bold and brawny, it holds its ripeness in check.
title: Kendall-Jackson 2014 Vintner's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma County)

description: Standard aromas of celery, lime, gooseberry and minerals set up a palate that's not flat but is definitely low on snap and acidity. Lightly vegetal citrus flavors finish soft. Drink immediately.
title: Montes 2017 Classic Series Sauvignon Blanc (Aconcagua Costa)

description: Wood smoke aromas and flavors give a spicy edge to this full-bodied, moderately tannic and perhaps slightly sweet wine. A toasted oak element is behind the smokiness, and it provides good counterpoint against the almost syrupy fruit flavors.
title: Nine North Wine Company 2014 Hullabaloo Zinfandel (California)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Cut grass, wet cement and lemon wedge show on the slightly reductive nose of this bottling. The palate is sour and almost bitter with more cut grass and strong grapefruit-peel flavors.
title: Owl Ridge 2015 Sauvignon Blanc (Central Coast)

description: Mild fruit flavors and subtle spices give this medium-bodied wine a low profile. The aromas are vaguely appley and the flavors bring hints of vanilla, while soft acidity and a light buttery finish complete the picture.
title: Paris Valley Road 2015 Chardonnay (California)

description: Quite aromatic and equally flavorful, this medium-bodied wine has a sense of sweetness from the strawberry and sandalwood aromas to the marinated cherry and maple syrup flavors. The texture is rounded and soft.
title: Pra Vinera 2016 Reserve Pinot Noir (California)

description: Aromas reminiscent of weedy underbrush, overripe berry, roasted herb and a whiff of salted game lead the nose. The gamy note follows over to the evolved, rustic palate along with dried cherry, grilled sage, and clove alongside while close-grained fleeting tannins give it a loose framework. A salty note marks the close.
title: Principiano 2010  Barolo

description: Rustic leafy aromas are gritty and lean. This light-colored, lighter-bodied Pinot is racy up front, with a sticky note of animal fat on the back palate. Earthy, lightly oxidized flavors finish without elegance.
title: Prisma 2015 Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Vegetables are in the fore of this wine, leading with notes of buttered green beans and mushrooms—grapefruit and lemon tones bring up the rear. The palate is rich and heavy but with searing acidity.
title: Prophecy 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Subdued aromas of underbrush, celery, truffle and a whiff of used leather set the tone. The aromas carry over to the dense, evolved palate along with dried blackberry, prune and evident alcohol. Raspy, fleeting tannins dry out the finish.
title: Rattalino 2014 Quarantatre 43  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Hailing from Antoine Creek Vineyard, the aromas of herb, cherry and spice seem to show some oxidation. Meanwhile, the medium-bodied cherry and raspberry flavors are straight down the fairway, providing enjoyment if not a lot of complexity.
title: Rio Vista 2015 Malbec (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This opens with subdued aromas suggesting underbrush, camphor, oak and a whiff of cured meat. The palate is a bit muddled and rather diluted, offering hints of sour cherry, salt and espresso alongside dusty, somewhat fleeting tannins that leave a mouth-drying finish.
title: San Matteo 2014  Barbaresco

description: A tropical fruit bowl of pineapple, mango and papaya carries intensity on the nose, with a little grass and white pepper in the background. The palate is less exuberant but it makes for an enjoyable sipper nonetheless.
title: Satellite 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Rose, scorched earth, dark spice and balsamic aromas merge together on this. The straightforward, medium-bodied palate shows dried cherry, tart cranberry and a star anise note. It's savory but straightforward for Barolo, with loose knit, rather dusty tannins.
title: Ricossa 2012  Barolo

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are fruit forward, with notes of cherry, plum, herb, raspberry and wood spice. The flavors are rich, sappy and full, backed by soft tannins. Barrel notes linger on the finish.
title: Rio Vista 2015 Estate Grown and Bottled Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of Marsanne, Chardonnay and Roussanne is hard it pin down. Aromas of salty nuts, honey mustard, dried apples, hay and orange rind are fairly shy. Unique flavors of popcorn, fermented tropical fruits, citrus peel and sandalwood show on the sip.
title: Sanguis 2014 The Hat-trick White (Central Coast)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A dense core of dark fruit is nuanced by shades of cocoa and coffee on the nose. The palate shows a pleasant grip of tannin, but overall the feel is soft with rich black fruit and integrated toasty oak. Cocoa-dusted cranberry skins mark the finish.
title: Shalestone 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

description: This is a combination of 85% Merlot and 15% Petite Sirah. Shy on the nose, it slowly reveals aromas of pencil, bittersweet tobacco and clove, unfurling a juicy core of plum and dark cherry on the lengthy full-bodied palate. A strong presence of tannin and oak is felt.
title: Smith Devereux 2015 Ibex Reserve Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas of licorice, black fruit and dried herb bring a slightly baked quality. The flavors are full and up front, showing pleasing concentration while retaining elegance. There are a lot of good things going on here but I can't see them evolving much. Drink it in the near term.
title: Substance 2014 Me Vineyard Collection Northridge Vineyard Merlot (Wahluke Slope)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Soft, feathery tannins and plump black-cherry and berry flavors give this Syrah-Viognier blend an easy, widely pleasing demeanor. It's a full-bodied but balanced wine with subtle notes of earth, dried lavender and thyme. Fresh acidity lingers on the finish.
title: Tenet 2015 Le Fervent Red (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Richly developed flavors of dark chocolate, pretzel, leather and cedar with dried herb mark this big darkly concentrated wine, that is substantial in tannin.
title: Tin Barn 2014 Gilsson Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very plummy, hibiscus-laced aromas show strongly on this blend of 50% Grenache, 30% Syrah and 20% Mourvèdre, which is enhanced by a pinch of dried herbs and some cola spice. There is chalky depth to the tannins on the palate, which is easygoing overall, with more red plum and light herb flavors. It is a solid everyday sipper.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2015 Blend 150 Red (Santa Barbara County)

description: Made with 95% Sangiovese and 5% Colorino, this has aromas of cedar, vanilla and a touch of violet. The dense palate delivers mocha, black cherry jam and star anise while firm, somewhat grainy tannins provide support. Drink through 2022.
title: Villa Cerna 2015 Primocolle  (Chianti Classico)

description: The nose is limey and floral, with stone fruit and pea shoots at the core. It's the palate that keeps you coming back for more, with acidity that is in check rather than enamel-stripping, held up by a juicy, limey core.
title: Villa Maria 2017 Private Bin Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: This unusual blend of Riesling, Chardonnay and Torontel smells much like a Moscato or Vermentino, with aromas of cured ham and lychee. Low acidity creates a fleshy palate, while flavors of lychee, honeydew melon and lime finish with green banana notes. Drink now; this probably will not age well.
title: Vistalago 2017 Mezcla Blanca White (Maule Valley)

description: Fresh melon aromas lead to more buttery, pear-like flavors in this fruity, well-rounded and rather soft-textured wine from a well-established Napa Valley brand. It's medium bodied and easy to sip.
title: ZD 2015 Chardonnay (California)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Equal parts fruit and earth, this juicy, nuanced red balances bold black-plum and cherry flavors against savory mushroom, clay and game notes. It's easy drinking and fruity yet elegantly balanced.
title: Abbeycourt 2015 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dried lime skin aromas meet with rounded red apple, nectarine and lemongrass aromas on the nose of this bottling, one of the first single-varietal Picpouls coming to market. The palate is extremely racy with tangy lime skin flavors, but rounded out by the midpalate with stone fruit and lemon wedge and the slightest hints of sweet oak and butter.
title: Adelaida 2016 Anna's Estate Vineyard Picpoul (Adelaida District)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This puts 38% Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Merlot and 30% Malbec to good work, offering a sizable sense of tannin and integrated oak. Leather, black pepper and pencil shavings provide contrast to the fruitier components of baked plum and dark cherry.
title: Arbor Bench Vineyards 2014 Estate Meritage (Dry Creek Valley)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: This bottling, which includes 12% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Franc and 3% Cabernet Sauvignon, shows bright raspberry and pomegranate fruit as well as crushed slate and dried earth. The palate is fresh and light with cranberry and boysenberry flavors.
title: Avensole 2014 Malbec (Temecula Valley)

description: Bright aromas of lime, pear and crushed chalk rise to the nose atop a slight bit of effervescence in this latest release from the actress Drew Barrymore. The sip is bright and playful with lots of fun energy and a compelling texture, proving easy to quaff if simple overall.
title: Barrymore 2016 Pinot Grigio (California)

Appellation: Paso Robles Willow Creek District, Central Coast, California, US
description: A golden shade of yellow, this slightly richer take on the grape shows lemon rinds, honeysuckle oil and lightly roasted nuts on the nose. The palate is lively and textural, with an immense grip at the tip of the grip leading into flavors of yellow apple and citrus peel.
title: Barton 2015 Tiny Dancer Grenache Blanc (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)

description: Blue flower, black plum, espresso and a whiff of dark culinary spice lead the nose. The aromas carry on to the austere palate along with dried cherry, coconut and star anise. Hints of powdered sage and tightly drawn, close-grained tannins give it an astringent close.
title: Bibbiano 2014 Vigne di Montornello Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This light-salmon colored wine has a good deal of red-wine flavor carried nicely by fine-beaded effervescence and good acidity. Plum and peach fill the aromas, while the flavor is plummy and just off dry.
title: Campos Family Vineyards NV Sparkling Rosé (Lodi)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, ripe plum and underbrush lead the nose. The straightforward palate offers dried Morello cherry, tobacco and star anise alongside chewy tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2015 Clemente VII  (Chianti Classico)

description: Somewhat funky aromas of truffle, scorched soil, toast and game slowly take shape on this. The earthy flavors carry over to the ripe, chewy palate along with dried black cherry, fleshy blackberry and clove while close-grained tannins provide support.
title: Castello di Verrazzano 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep and earthy aromas of red and black cherries meet with loamy soil, crushed rocks and rusty earth on the nose of this bottling. Meaty flavors show on the palate alongside gravel, tangy black plum and a hint of anise, with a decent tannic structure carrying roasted coffee bean flavors into the finish.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Crisp acidity offsets plump black cherry and plum in this elegant but approachable red blend. Polished tannins and hints of dark chocolate mark a moderately long finish.
title: Château Beauchêne 2016 Grande Réserve Red (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: Piquant hints of spice, vanilla and toasted coconut accent sun-kissed black cherry and plum in this juicy red wine. Full bodied and fleshy on the palate, it's an easy-drinking wine with ripe, yielding tannins and a smooth finish.
title: Château Maucoil 2015 Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: Red-fruit aromas with a touch of herbs carry the nose on this drawn-in Pinot. Medicinal raspberry and creamy oak flavors are missing some harmony, while this tastes woody and like cherry cough drop on the finish.
title: Concha y Toro 2016 Marques de Casa Concha Pinot Noir (Limarí Valley)

description: Scorched earth, underbrush, dark spice and grilled sage aromas are front and center along with a balsamic note. The firm palate reflects the heat of the vintage, showing dried cherry, clove and a hint of licorice alongside assertive, close-grained tannins that grip the finish. Give the tannins a few years to unwind then drink to catch the remaining fruit.
title: Costa di Bussia 2011 Bussia Riserva  (Barolo)

Appellation: San Joaquin County, Central Valley, California, US
description: Wonderfully expressive aromas of rose petals, litchis and yellow peach meet vivid, slightly sweet fruit-basket flavors in this rather full-bodied mouth-filling wine. It has great concentration and keeps the fruit notes humming through the finish.
title: Deaver 2016 Malvasia Bianca (San Joaquin County)

description: A small parcel of Chardonnay has produced this attractively soft and creamy wine. With its apple-purée and citrus flavors easily coming together, this perfumed wine is light, bright and crisp at the end. Drink now.
title: Domaine Bertrand 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Burnished gold in hue, this is already showing some development on the nose, with ginger, saffron and furniture polish, with lingering rich peach and red apple. Incredibly juicy on the round palate, more stone fruit carries the flavors, with melon showing midway and orange spice on the finish.
title: Sannino 2016 Gewürztraminer (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a honeyed character to the nose of this bottling, along with lemon cream, orange cake and a slight nuttiness. The palate shows the slightest hint of sweetness, but it slips to the background quickly, allowing the pecan, seared orange and baking spice elements to shine, with a fresh spray of lemon wedge and a creeping sense of petrol toward the finish.
title: Scratch 2014 San Saba Vineyard Riesling (Arroyo Seco)

description: From one of New Zealand's historic producers, this is a solid, well-made wine, with notes of pressed yellow flowers, peas, stone fruit and waxy lemon. It's prickly on the tongue from zippy acidity, with a bit of weight and a long waxy lemon finish.
title: Seifried 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Nelson)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Green apple, lemon and peach aromas show bright and clean on the nose. There's a bit of roundness to this off-dry Riesling, which boasts spritzy acidity to balance the full flavors of crunchy apple, stone fruit and blossoms.
title: Sheldrake Point 2016 Archival Estate Grown Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This full-bodied wine is frosted with a chocolaty character from oak, and has ripe plush fruit flavors underneath. The spicy cocoa-like oakiness also has a smoky tinge.
title: Sobon Estate 2014 ReZerve Primitivo (Amador County)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine is very fruity, concentrated and saturated in terms of flavor and rich and broad in texture. The tannins are quite soft and acidity low so It seems mouth-filling but not heavy on the palate.
title: Sobon Estate 2015 Rocky Top Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: This is an unusual comingling of 40% Blaufränkisch, 30% Syrah, 10% Cabernet Franc, 10% Malbec and 10% Petit Verdot. It compels in earthy tobacco and mushroom flavor, adorned in violet aroma. Soft and velvety, the fruit is compressed and tightly structured.
title: Thirty-Seven Wines 2014 Tolay Red (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a richer-leaning blend of 29% Viognier, 29% Marsanne, 29% Roussanne and 13% Grenache Blanc, with aromas of banana split, cognac-poached pears and a hint of caramel. Creamy flavors of baked pear, clove and vanilla are cut by a sharp Meyer lemon acidity.
title: Tower 15 2016 The White Cap White (Santa Barbara County)

description: Mocha, cedar and blackberry aromas lift out of the glass. The firm palate offers licorice, tobacco and black cherry jam alongside assertive tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Villa Calcinaia 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A very pretty pale peach color, the aromas display intriguing notes of tangerine peel, herb, flint, strawberry, flower and mineral. It drinks dry and is elegant in style, with plentiful tangerine flavors on the finish. It's shows some sneaky complexity.
title: Vino 2016 Rosé Sangiovese (Washington)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Blended with 21% Cabernet Sauvignon, the aromas bring pleasing notes of plum, green herb and baking spice. The flavors show a sense of elegance, painting around the edges, with the middle a bit lacking. It finishes with a tart kick.
title: Wind Rose 2015 Lonesome Springs Ranch Malbec (Yakima Valley)

description: Lifted oak spices fuse with red cherries, florals, dark chocolate, pepper and campfire smoke. The palate is tough and leathery at the moment, with slightly clunky oak, biting tannins and sour red fruit. This is a flashy style for those who like oaky reds.
title: Wither Hills 2012 Taylor River Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Aromas suggesting grilled sage, French oak, ground clove and a whiff of camphor come together on this. The tightly wound palate evokes raw sour cherry, coffee bean and anisette but not much fruit richness. Assertive, close-grained tannins leave a grippy finish.
title: Agostino Bosco 2013 Neirane  (Barolo)

description: This value-driven wine is successful in its smooth texture and floral aromas, with minimal if any oak perceptible on the palate. Peach, apricot and melon mix for a fruity profile dipped in lingering acidity.
title: Angeline 2016 Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)

description: Hard-capped aromas of gritty black fruits, oaky char and asphalt are fierce. On the palate, this feels abrupt and clipped, with high acidity. Oaky racy plum and black-currant flavors finish with a lot of residual oak, hard tannins and juicy acidity.
title: Apaltagua 2015 Grial Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Made from 41% Cabernet Sauvignon, 34% Merlot and 25% Malbec, this wine works in its presentation of dusty, soft and rounded red fruit, with touches of vanilla and cinnamon. Full bodied and ripe, it embraces a juicy, approachable style.
title: Arbor Bench Vineyards 2012 Estate Meritage (Dry Creek Valley)

description: This blend of 90% Sangiovese, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Petit Verdot opens with aromas of toasted oak, roasted coffee and a whiff of red plum. The firm palate offers cocoa, licorice and black-currant jam alongside assertive tannins that lead to a drying finish. Drink through 2023.
title: Barone Ricasoli 2013 Castello di Brolio Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are intriguing but not yet ready to reveal their charms, with notes of cherry, red apple, ground herb and spice. The cranberry and cherry flavors seem a bit bunched up but still bring a sense of elegance. It doesn't all seem to come together at present.
title: Barons 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Toast, charred earth, underbrush, pressed violet and plum aromas slowly take shape on this. It's taut and light on its feet, offering strawberry compote and a hint of star anise framed in tightly drawn, close-grained tannins.
title: Borgo Salcetino 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Humboldt County, North Coast, California, US
description: Complexity is the hallmark of this medium-bodied, organically grown wine that smells like cedar and new leather and tastes like tamarind and raspberries. It has more vivid acidity than usual, and a lively gripping texture.
title: Briceland 2014 Ishi Pishi Ranch Zinfandel (Humboldt County)

description: A hit of pungent oak spice, a mix of nutmeg and vanilla, permeates the nose and palate of this full-bodied wine, moderately structured with fruit-forward flavors of green apple and Meyer lemon. It offers heat on the finish.
title: Buena Vista 2015 Private Reserve Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: Made with 100% Sangiovese, this fragrant red has alluring aromas of wild berry, crushed herb, blue flower and cake spice. It's straightforward but savory, with juicy red cherry, cinnamon and star anise framed in bright acidity and easygoing tannins. Enjoy through 2019.
title: Cafaggio 2016 Single Estate  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of melon, peach and citrus grace the nose, along with vanilla, baking spices and cut grass. There's some weight to the palate amidst a good deal of acidity, which is carried by stoney minerality and a chalky texture.
title: Chasing Venus 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Black plum and cherry flavors are juicy and fresh, accented by bright acidity and just a hint of granite minerality. Framed neatly with fine-grained tannins, this is a straightforward, fruity wine with wide appeal and an easy price tag.
title: Château de Campuget 2015 Tradition Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Produced from organically grown grapes, this ripe, smoothly fruity wine has a light toast flavor from the wood aging. With its balance between citrus acidity and ripe yellow fruits, the wine is both rich and crisply textured. Drink now.
title: Château Thivin 2016 Clos de Rochebonne  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: The heat that dominated the 2012 Chilean vintage is evident in this blend led by Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah. While the palate is lush, obtuse and dense, it also rings of tartaric acid, and thus the mouthfeel is not in full sync. Heavy oak along with brandied plum flavors are baked through the finish.
title: Chilensis 2012 Lazuli Red (Maule Valley)

description: Inky black in color, this is a rich beast of a wine destined for the cellar. At the moment, however, it's tight and not offering much, mostly just oak spice in the form of cola, pepper and dusting polish, along with some ripe red and black fruit. Drink 2019–2027.
title: Craggy Range 2015 Sophia Gimblett Gravels Red (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Quite reserved on the nose, this bottling from a vineyard seven miles behind Cambria, shows light berries and sweet wood on the nose. The palate combines dried berries with fennel fronds, but is still quite wound up and needs more time to emerge.
title: Cutruzzola 2014 Riven Rock Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Broad aromas of honeydew, tuberose and guava prove quite floral and tropical, and there is a chalky backbone providing aromatic structure. Crisp, clean flavors of Juicy Fruit gum show on the palate.
title: D'Alfonso-Curran 2016 Viognier (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: With a giant fleur-de-lis on the bottle, the nature of this wine is matched in intensity and outward fruit-forwardness. It's a mix of noble reduction, salty oak and baked apple and pineapple richness.
title: De Loach 2015 Estate Grown Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Leafy berry and cassis aromas are basic. This feels hard and choppy, with no sense of flow to the palate. Generic berry flavors finish chocolaty and a bit oaky.
title: Agustinos 2016 Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: This straightforward and fruity wine is relatively light bodied, offering juicy black cherry and strawberry flavors and a hint of sweetness. It's soft and easy to sip.
title: Beringer 2015 Founders' Estate Merlot (California)

description: Pleasant notes of baked plum and black-cherry compote present an enticing bouquet to this wine. The palate is sharper and leaner in feel, with tart and slightly bitter fruit-skin flavors that finish clean but quick. Drink now.
title: Eikeboom 2015 Pinotage (Western Cape)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very shy on the nose, this bottling offers dark red fruit and baking spice aromas with patience. The palate is flat and dull yet inoffensive, with flavors of sweet red fruit, herbs and char on the finish.
title: Firefly Ridge 2015 Merlot (Central Coast)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Assertive oak wraps around substantial richness and body weight in this highly ripe wine that lacks somewhat in structure. Tropical vanilla and coconut linger longest.
title: Gracianna 2016 Suzanne's Blend Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: From vines that surround the Fessy winery, this juicy, fruity wine also has a tang of tannins. Its acidity and crisp texture are immediately attractive and the wine is drinkable now.
title: Henry Fessy 2016  Beaujolais

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A note of kerosene dominates on the nose of this wine, which carries to a ripe, juicy palate of stone fruit. Medium dry in style, this is straightforward and easy drinking. Drink now.
title: Knapp 2016 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Soft and almost sweet, this medium-bodied wine has a rose perfume, stewed plum, cherry syrup and nutmeg flavors. It is smooth on the palate, showing little acidity or tannin.
title: LangeTwins 2016 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Clarksburg)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas display an uncomfortable mix of vanilla, dill and smoke, with the barrel influence taking over the show. The wood rides high on the palate as well, making it near impossible to get at the fruit.
title: Maryhill 2014 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Other than that this is made from Pedro Ximénez and comes from a high-elevation vineyard in remote Elqui, there's almost nothing to this basic white table wine. Mild white-peach and yeasty aromas are followed by a bland palate. Lightly oxidized flavors of wheat and stone fruits are salty, while low acidity means the finish is soft.
title: Mayu 2017 Pedro Ximénez (Elqui Valley)

description: Wood-heavy aromas of bacon and clove don't allow for much fruit expression. This is tannic and weighty on the palate, with flavors of baked berry fruits, clove and burnt wood. Bacon and clove notes remain out front on a finish that's all about oak.
title: MontGras 2013 Reserva Merlot (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas bring bright notes of mandarin orange peel, honeysuckle and lime zest. It drinks off dry, with a lightly spritzy palate packed full of ripe peach flavors.
title: Rio Vista 2016 Estate Grown and Bottled Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Soupy berry and oak aromas lead to a thick dense palate. Highly herbal plum flavors are drenched in milk-chocolaty oak, while this is sluggish on the finish.
title: Santa Carolina 2016 Reserva Carmenère (Cachapoal Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Citrus and green apple take center stage in this medium-bodied Chardonnay. The palate has a mild intensity of these flavors, with soft acidity offering some structure.
title: Silver Thread 2015 STV Estate Vineyard Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

description: Aromas of toasted oak, pressed violet, roasted coffee bean and ground pepper carry over to the wiry palate along with raisin and powdered sage. It lacks fruit richness while grainy tannins leave a raspy finish.
title: Tenuta di Arceno 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Toasted oak and a huge concentration of tannin dominate in this burly, full-bodied and highly ripe wine, which finishes in a bittersweet and sour taste of leather.
title: The Vice 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: There's a chemical quality to this wine that doesn't seem to blow away. Dusting polish, smoked meats and berries lead into a tightly wound palate of tart red fruit and more polish, with chunky tannins and apparent oak.
title: Wither Hills 2012 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Reticent aromas of citrus, green apple and crushed stone carry to a light-bodied, quince-driven palate, with a metallic edge. Spritzy in feel, this is simplistic but enjoyable.
title: Atwater 2015 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Funky aromas of scorched soil, withered rose, used leather, stewed prune and a hint of barnyard follow over to the overripe palate along with licorice and a gamy note. Dusty, evolved tannins offset the earthy flavors.
title: Batasiolo 2011 Riserva  (Barolo)

description: This has a shy nose that eventually reveals moist underbrush, oak and a whiff of salted meat. The palate is somewhat diluted, presenting suggestions of dried black cherry, fennel and toasted oak alongside fleeting but drying tannins.
title: Canonica a Cerreto 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Jammy berry aromas are herbal like desert brush. This is a pulpy wine with a jammy palate. Earthy stewy berry flavors are green and oaky on the finish.
title: Casa Silva 2015 Cuvée Colchagua Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: Crisp and fruity, this light, bright wine has strawberry and red-currant flavors as well as a lively background of acidity. It is totally ready to drink.
title: Château de Corcelles 2016 Rosé d'une Nuit Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

description: Fermented with natural yeast, this light wine is crisp with red fruits and acidity. It is a delicate wine, bright and ready to drink.
title: Château de Javernand 2016 Rosé de Gamay Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

description: Aromas of stewed prune and wet underbrush follow over to the evolved palate together with a hint of dried cranberry and clove. Dusty, fleeting tannins give it an abrupt finish.
title: Coli 2013 Pratale  (Chianti Classico)

description: This is a light wine, just juicy red cherries and bright acidity. The structure is also light, leaving fruitiness and immediate drinkability as the wine's main characteristics. Drink now.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée B  (Beaujolais)

description: Medium-bodied and pleasant to sip, this unassuming wine has subtle plum flavors and a smooth texture. The mouthfeel is soft and the finish is short.
title: Geyser Peak 2015 Pinot Noir (California)

description: Coconut, French oak, vanilla and dried sage aromas follow through to the austere, assertive palate alongside firm acidity. Unyielding, close-grained tannins give it an astringent, lean finish. It lacks the fruit richness needed to stand up to the oak.
title: Querciabella 2014  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of fruit from Antoine Creek and Gingko Forest vineyards. Quite light in color, the aromas show a light volatility, along with notes of raspberry, strawberry and maraschino cherry. The fruit flavors are light, simple and sweet, finishing with peppermint notes.
title: Rio Vista 2015 Pinot Noir (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Neutral aromas lack pungency and instead are yeasty and suggestive of green pepper and mild citrus fruits. A round palate lacks the pop and zest that Chilean SB often shows, while this tastes of grassy melon and white pepper prior to a flat finish.
title: Santa Rita 2017 120 Reserva Especial Sauvignon Blanc (Central Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: From a new urban winery located in the heart of Geneva, NY, this Riesling shows toast, smoke and slate notes over a core of citrus on the nose. Round in feel, with a good balance of acidity, the palate shows more fruit—peach and lemon curd—that carries to a crisp, clean finish.
title: Wicked Water 2016 Sawmill Creek Vineyards Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Slightly gritty aromas of latex, rubber and foxy berries start off this blend of Barbera, Garnacha and a bit of Carignan. In the mouth, this is pulling and a bit raw. Punchy red-fruit flavors finish bright, with a candied, medicinal-ending flavor.
title: Erasmo 2016 Barbera-Garnacha Red (Maule Valley)

description: Lemon, smoke, butter and white pepper aromas lead into a light-to-medium bodied palate that offers a little creaminess to go with toasty oak, citrus and more white pepper notes. A perfectly enjoyable Chard with mild complexity.
title: Esk Valley 2015 Chardonnay (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit for this wine comes from a mixture of Riverbend, Conner Lee and Red Mountain vineyards. Aromas of vanilla, wood spice, herb, cranberry, apple and red and black cherry are followed by plentiful dark-fruit flavors. Vanilla and cranberry notes intertwine on the finish, with the fruit seeming slightly grapey.
title: Estrin 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine, which includes 17% Merlot and 8% Syrah, all comes from Riverbend Vineyard. The aromas show pleasing complexities, with notes of graphite, herb, raspberry, barrel spice, dark cherry and vanilla. The flavors show depth, with a backbone of firm tannins providing support.
title: Estrin 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This domaine, one of several in Morgon followed by Duboeuf, has made a rich wine, structured and dense. It has plenty of concentration as well as bright fruits and a jammy, ripe texture. It will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine de Javernière  (Morgon)

description: A subdued nose opens the door to a thickness of cranberry and cassis-tinged tartness in this densely tannic wine. There's a hint of brightness from underlying acidity that peaks on the midpalate before a modest finish.
title: Hagafen 2014 Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (Coombsville)

description: This blend is Merlot-driven, with 87% of it represented, the remainder 7% Malbec, 4% Petit Verdot and 2% Cabernet Sauvignon. It's fun and fruit-driven, heartily well-made for an attractive price. Cinnamon, blackberry pie and charred oak mix well on the palate within a soft herbaceous backbone.
title: Hoopla 2014 The Mutt Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Cayuga Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Delicate aromas of peach blossom and green apple lead to a light- to medium-bodied palate filled with juicy stone fruit and pineapple flavors. Semidry in style, this carries just enough lime-zest acidity to balance out the ripe fruit.
title: Hosmer 2016 Limited Release Riesling (Cayuga Lake)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: This Bordeaux-style red blend boasts a smoky nose of charred cherry wood, mesquite and coffee over a core of red cherry and currant. The palate is plush and pillowy, with fine-grained tannins for support. A blend of juicy red fruit and tart berries carry the flavors to a wispy, smoky finish.
title: Hound's Tree 2015 Cornus Reserve Red (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: San Diego County, South Coast, California, US
description: Bright cranberry, earthy herbs of oregano, thyme and dried sage and toasted wood show on the nose of this blend of 78% Sangiovese, 14% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon and 3% Lagrein. Herbs and smoke play with red plum and strawberry on the sip, which is framed by squeaky tannins.
title: Hungry Hawk 2015 Super Tuscan Red (San Diego County)

description: This wine is rich and structured while also being ripe and full of black fruits. The tannins and concentration of this fruity wine will allow it to age. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Les Saprolites  (Fleurie)

Appellation: North Coast, North Coast, California, US
description: This wine is straightforward, refreshing and non-oaky in style, offering citrus aromas, rather lean and savory flavors and a nice underpinning of acidity to keep it crisp and appetizing.
title: La Follette 2015 Chardonnay (North Coast)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Pristine blackberry and blueberry flavors are rich and mouthfilling in this ripe yet freshly composed wine. It's straightforward and fruity but hints of bramble and earth lend nuance. The moderately long finish is framed by fine, delicate tannins.
title: Lavau 2015 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: Rattlesnake Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is 42% Syrah, 32% Grenache and 26% Mourvèdre. Aromas of sagebrush, toasty barrel spice and jammy cherry are at the fore, with a medicinal note that causes some distraction. Despite the high alcohol, the flavors show an elegant styling, tapering toward the finish.
title: Maryhill 2014 Marvell G-S-M (Rattlesnake Hills)

Appellation: Dunnigan Hills, Central Valley, California, US
description: This is a substantial wine with a spicy, lightly herbal aroma and lots of fruit flavors that spread across the palate. It is medium to full-bodied, has a smooth texture and a good dose of raspberry-like acidity that keeps it fresh.
title: Matchbook 2013 Tempranillo (Dunnigan Hills)

Appellation: Malibu Coast, South Coast, California, US
description: This wine, which is a beautiful shade of pink, shows buoyant aromas of strawberry, Key lime pith and baking soda, making for quite a classic nose. Watermelon and melon rind flavors show on the sip, which has compelling texture and a cotton-candy finish. It's a strong showing for the new region.
title: Montage Vineyards 2016 Rosé (Malibu Coast)

description: This is a nicely balanced Chard with waxy apple skins, lemon rind, toasty oak notes and a slight honey and copper-penny quality. While the oak influence is more apparent on the palate, the oily texture and acidity are in harmony.
title: Mt. Beautiful 2015 Chardonnay (Canterbury)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Single-vineyard Malbec is a rarity from this appellation. This one, which is 100% varietal, offers aromas of fresh and dried green herb along with clove, as the fruit lurks in the background. The palate brings ripe but in-balance purple-fruit flavors that show a sense of elegance. It's a pretty interpretation of this variety.
title: Mustard Seed 2014 Cockburn Vineyard Malbec (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Lime pith and sharp apple aromas show on the nose of this bottling from the extremely coastal Spanish Springs Vineyard, and there is a touch of a vinous quality as well. Ripe pear flavors show on the tip of the sip, where acidic energy cuts through the somewhat sweet characteristics of the midpalate.
title: Oceano 2016 Chardonnay (Central Coast)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are a bit light for the variety, with notes of lime leaf and white peach. The palate is broad in feel, full of off-dry mandarin orange and peach flavors that bring a lot of appeal. It keeps you coming back for more.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 J Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of sliced ripe red apple and pear are followed by a quite sleek linear bone-dry palate that shows a pleasing sense of balance. It's an intriguing wine, best suited for the dinner table.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 Spring Creek Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Yakima Valley)

description: This full-bodied wine has toasty, peppery aromas and rather smoky flavors, for a personality that's more oaky than fruity. But the wine pulls it off well and is flavorful, mouth-filling and yet well-balanced.
title: Parducci 2016 Small Lot Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: A full woodsy nose is as ripe as they come but still integrated and foresty. The fully ripe theme is taken over to the palate, which is saturated, clampy and reduced. Flavors of cherry cough drop, pepper and chocolate turn hot on a medicinal finish.
title: Pargua 2012 Anka Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Smooth, rounded and easygoing on the palate, this is an approachable wine that's well-made, a study in tobacco, black currant and cherry-vanilla swirls.
title: Peju 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Forest floor, incense, exotic spice, stewed prune and grilled herb aromas slowly take shape. It's forward, offering dried black cherry, cake spice and raisin flavors alongside buffed, not very persistent tannins and tangy acidity.
title: Principiano 2010 Boscareto  (Barolo)

Appellation: Sierra Pelona Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: A bronze-orange in color, this “orange” wine is quite interesting, with burnt orange peels, light maraschino cherry and liquor-like aromas on the nose, which is reminiscent of an old-fashioned cocktail. Rusty, sherry-like flavors show on the sip alongside more burnt orange and toasted barley. It's unique but worth trying.
title: Reyes 2015 Amber Chardonnay (Sierra Pelona Valley)

description: Dusty red in color, the nose is a medley of red currant, cranberry, raw meat and cola spice. On the palate, cranberry and raspberry combine with appealing savory, smokey characters but as a whole it's a little lean and mean and showing prominent oak on the finish.
title: Rubus 2013 The Terraces Collection Pinot Noir (Waipara Valley)

description: Aromas of French oak, menthol, dark spice and ripe berry carry over to the firm palate along with prune, toasted nut, licorice and orange zest. Assertive, close-grained tannins clamp down on the finish.
title: San Biagio 2014 Montersino  (Barbaresco)

description: This is crisp, fresh and exemplary for value-priced Cabernet. Lightly herbal cherry and cassis aromas are clean, while this feels right. A touch of toast along with dark spice and cassis flavors is about all you can ask for.
title: Santa Carolina 2016 Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: Apricot and peach aromas include notes of wood polish and lemon peel. A plump, fleshy palate is braced by acidity, while this tastes of tropical fruits, raw oak and butter, all of which are common for Chilean Chardonnay. Woody leftovers push the finish.
title: Santa Ema 2016 Reserva Chardonnay (Leyda Valley)

description: For those who like their Chards bursting with fruit, this wine delivers all the ripe peach and pineapple aromas you could ever want. The palate, however, is weighty with an odd tang to it, but the fruit and acid get lost somewhere along the line.
title: Giesen 2016 Chardonnay (Hawke's Bay)

description: Aromas of vanilla and buttered popcorn sit atop the plush berry preserve and raspberry sauce tones. The palate is similarly ripe and jammy, though easy to like, with a smooth texture and ample acidity to keep the lushness in check. Drink now.
title: Guardian Peak 2015 Shiraz (Western Cape)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Bristling aromas of cherry Jell-O and herbal spice carry to the palate that is almost sanguine in flavor. Taut in feel, with tart red-fruit flavors, sandy tannins and brisk acidity leading to a savory spice finish.
title: Heron Hill 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: Ripe black-skinned berry, dark culinary spice and purple flower aromas come to the forefront on this straightforward red. The round, savory palate offers juicy Maresca cherry, crushed raspberry and a hint of star anise alongside supple tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: La Castellina 2015 Squarcialupi  (Chianti Classico)

description: Big and brawny, this full-bodied wine has tight flavors of dark chocolate and wood smoke, very firm tannins and a finish that echoes maple syrup. Bring on the meats when you open this one.
title: LangeTwins 2014 Diablo Vineyard Petite Sirah (Clarksburg)

Designation: Single Vineyard Coastal Cool Climate Wine
description: Cool-climate aromas of herbs and latex contain raspberry and mint notes. A full yet abrasive palate displays coastal rawness, while this tastes slightly green as well as minty and oaky. A plump finish remains oaky and herbal.
title: Lomas del Valle 2016 Single Vineyard Coastal Cool Climate Wine Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Delicate aromas of prune, scorched earth and underbrush lead the nose on this blend of 90% Sangiovese, 5% Colorino and 5% Canaiolo. The tight palate delivers mature black plum, mocha and clove alongside closed-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Piemaggio 2010  Chianti Classico

description: Aromas of French oak, roasted coffee bean and baked blackberry lead the nose. The bracing palate offers dried sour cherry, espresso and clove framed in tightly wound, grainy tannins that leave a rather raspy finish.
title: Poggio ai Mandorli 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Truffle, underbrush, toast and charred earth aromas emerge in the glass. The palate shows the heat of the vintage, offering prune, dried black cherry and roasted coffee bean alongside close-grained tannins that leave a rather raspy finish. Drink sooner rather than later before the remaining fruit dries out.
title: Poggio Scalette 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Spunky aromas of citrus and green notes are juicy and classic for Chilean SB. This is fresh at first, but turns flat fairly quickly. Flavors of tangerine and orange peel are grassy, with short-lasting edge and zest on the finish.
title: Santa Rita 2017 Secret Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: There's dense power behind this Georgian variety, with dark cherry meeting loads of sweet vanilla and other baking spices. The palate shows a nice grip and weight, yet is shot through with flavors of tart berries and granite.
title: Shalestone 2014 Saperavi (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Candied lemon and ripe apple aromas carry to the round palate that offers juicy flavors of stone fruit, more apple and blossoms. There's ampleness to the weight, with just enough acidity to maintain balance in this semidry Riesling.
title: Silver Thread 2015 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Shy aromas of citrus and green apple meld with slightly richer tones of apricot in the background. Light bodied in feel and slightly off-dry, the palate yields a pleasing mix of stone fruit balanced by tart green apple and textured apple skins. A powdery texture lingers on the finish.
title: Silver Thread 2016 STV Estate Vineyard Riesling (Seneca Lake)

description: Made with organically cultivated Sangiovese grapes, this has subdued aromas suggesting baking spice and black plum. The concentrated palate offers vanilla, espresso, prune and clove alongside assertive tannins that lead to a mouth-drying finish. Give the tannins a few more years to unwind then drink to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Terra di Seta 2012 Assai Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Subtle aromas of baking spice and black currant lift out of the glass. The lively, straightforward palate evokes clove, white pepper and a hint of raspberry alongside grainy, rather astringent tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Terra di Seta 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Produced from organically grown grapes, this simple, fruity wine has a direct character that is immediately attractive. Crisp acidity and juicy cherry flavors make it ready to drink.
title: Trenel Fils 2016 Cuvée Bio  (Beaujolais)

description: Spicy aromas of clove and allspice announce a fully oaked wine. A plump palate with tomatoey acidity and a resiny feel is home to woody savory flavors of smoked plum and currant. A woody smoky finish maintains consistency.
title: Ventisquero 2015 Herú Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
