description: While this Cabernet Franc is showing its age in hoisin sauce and stewed-berry aromas and flavors, there's still a glimmer of freshness on the palate, with the tart cranberry keeping things lively. Round in feel and structured by integrated, soft tannins, this wine should be enjoyed soon to savor its lingering chocolate-laden finish.
title: Ventosa 2010 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: Light in color, this blend of 70% Sangiovese and 30% Cabernet Sauvignon shows berry fruit, baked brick minerality, rose petals and bay leaf on the nose. It's easygoing and unique on the palate, with tangy juniper, balsam oil and pine needle flavors spicing up the berry fruits. Butterscotch flashes on the finish.
title: Vienza 2014 Vendetta Super Tuscan Red (Temecula Valley)

description: Warm woody aromas of oak, apple and barrel spice lead to a palate with low acidity and a resiny feel. A resiny oak flavor is up front, while on the finish this tastes of clove but not showing a lot of fruit or verve.
title: Villard 2016 Grand Vin Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Delightfully perfumed in ripe cherry and cranberry, the red fruit palate brings more of the same, with geranium and tilled earth adding to the mix. Light in frame, but not without proper tannic grip, there's a plumpness to the wine, making this easy and perfectly enjoyable.
title: Bellangelo 2015 Cabernet Franc (Seneca Lake)

description: Dried cherry is complemented by brawny leather and tobacco flavors in this full-bodied extracted wine that drinks heavy. The tannins are firm and a tad harsh.
title: Buena Vista 2014 Private Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma Valley)

description: This opens with subtle aromas reminiscent of black currant, mocha and baking spice. The brooding palate offers dried black cherry, licorice and a hint of French oak alongside chewy tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Castello di Monastero 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This wine has ripe fruit and a fine line of tannins to add to the acidity and berry flavors. It conveys richness as well as a crisp texture, full of fruit and ready to drink. The 220-acre estate is one of the largest in Beaujolais.
title: Château de Corcelles 2016  Brouilly

description: This wine is all fruit, with black cherries dominant. The tannins are light, the fruit crisp and the structure makes this delicious wine ready to drink.
title: Château Thivin 2016 Reverdon  (Brouilly)

Winery: Domaine de la Combe au Loup
description: A two-acre vineyard of the estate planted with Chardonnay gives this fruity, crisp wine. Offering flavors of citrus and green apple, it is bright, light and ready to drink.
title: Domaine de la Combe au Loup 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: This wine is rich, full of black-cherry flavors while also having a crisp texture. With its light tannins and intense acidity, it is juicy, ripe and ready to drink.
title: Domaine de la Prébende 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais)

description: The 60-year-old vines that give the grapes for this wine are planted on the typical clay and chalk soil of southern Beaujolais. The result is a ripe wine with a dense character and plenty of cherry fruit. With light tannins and crisp acidity, the wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas bring notes of lemon, baked bread and freshly peeled orange. The palate is noticeably sweet but with enough acidity to keep it all moving along.
title: Domaine Ste. Michelle NV Extra Dry Méthode Champenoise Sparkling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Named after the wife of Willy Frank (second generation owner), this dry-style Riesling carries a pleasant aroma of yellow apple, citrus and white flowers. The light-bodied palate is straightforward in its pithy and apple notes, with a soft vein of citrus acidity keeping it in balance.
title: Dr. Konstantin Frank 2015 Margrit Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Lime zest, peach and floral notes precede a clean, zippy, slippery-textured palate. There's a nice weight to this wine, along with a long, peachy finish.
title: Villa Maria 2017 Cellar Selection Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: This wine has plenty of tannins and a firm structure. The black fruits seem reticent and slow to come forward through the tannins. There is potential, but it will take time to release that juicy, fruity character. Drink from 2019.
title: Yohan Lardy 2016 Les Michelons  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Merlot and Syrah both make up 39% of this blend, with the balance Cabernet Sauvignon (13%), Cabernet Franc (7%) and Petit Verdot. Aromas of vanilla, coconut, herb, cedar and cherry lead to lighter-styled fruit flavors, with blueberry notes on the finish. The tannins bright a slight grittiness, and the oak seems a little intense.
title: Airfield Estates 2015 Bombshell Red Vineyard Salute Red (Yakima Valley)

description: A value-driven proposition that's been left unoaked, this wine is fully creamy and robust despite its lack of wood, shining in lemon and grapefruit flavor and a depth of texture. It finishes clean and crisp.
title: Bennett Valley Cellars 2016 Bin 5757 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: This opens with aromas suggesting new leather, moist underbrush, blue flower and chopped herb. The straightforward, rather diluted palate offers sour cherry and a hint of star anise framed in loose knit, somewhat fleeting tannins.
title: Bersano 2014 Mantico  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Notes of freshly roasted coffee and charred red berries pervade on the nose. Light in frame yet with a pleasing roundness, there's just enough support from tannins. Ripe red currant and tart cherry flavors are speckled by anise, with a green stem note on the finish.
title: Buttonwood Grove 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: Aromas of road tar and black cherry are medicinal. This has a raw grabby mouthfeel backed by generic flavors of roasted berry fruits. A benign finish is lightly herbal and shows a hint of brown-sugar sweetness.
title: Calcu 2014 Reserva Especial Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Sonoma County-Lake County, North Coast, California, US
description: Stainless-steel fermented, with a small percentage of grapes from Lake County, this light-bodied white is bristling in acidity, highlighted in quince, lemon, melon and a shock of grass.
title: Captûre 2016 Tradition Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County-Lake County)

description: This rich, medium-bodied wine offers notes of green beans and asparagus in a chili sauce. A buttery, nutty core leads to a slightly bitter, toasty finish.
title: Carrick 2015 Bannockburn Pinot Gris (Central Otago)

description: Off the bat, this cool-coast SB leans towards green via flat aromas of bell pepper and jalapeño. A chunky palate shows only moderate lift, while this tastes green and mildly bitter, like Casa Marin's Sauvignon Blancs often do. From a cloudy and rainy year with widespread ripeness issues, this is decent but incomplete.
title: Casa Marin 2016 Cipreses Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (San Antonio)

description: Typical of Casa Marin, this shows aromas that are more vegetal than fruity. On the palate, this offers good weight and balance, although the flavors run heavy on pyrazines, and thus taste distinctly green and bitter on a chunky finish.
title: Casa Marin 2017 Cipreses Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (San Antonio)

description: This is a very affordable, gentle, medium-bodied wine with good ripe-fruit flavors, hints of spice and a soft, plush texture. It's smooth to sip and has a welcome sense of ripeness along with low acidity.
title: Castle Rock 2015 Cuvée Pinot Noir (California)

description: Aromas suggesting prune, vanilla and a hint of bell pepper lead the nose. The ripe palate offers raisin, black currant and licorice alongside dusty tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Coli 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Winery: Domaine de la Combe au Loup
description: From a 5.5-acre vineyard on granite soil, this juicy, red-cherry-flavored wine is crisp and perfumed. It is all about delicious, forward fruit, with a light texture and ready to drink.
title: Domaine de la Combe au Loup 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: This light and fruity wine has a crisp texture as well as plenty of acidity. These contrast with the red-currant and berry fruits that are juicy and succulent. Drink now.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: A pale, almost Provençal-colored wine, this is light and smoothly fruity. With a soft texture and caramel flavor as well as red fruits, this rounded, attractive wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Nugues 2016 Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

description: This has muted aromas of tilled soil, stewed prune and a whiff of game. The light-bodied palate is slightly more expressive, showing sour cherry and a hint of dark culinary spice set against loose tannins. Drink through 2018.
title: Fattoria di Petroio 2014  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Bright vibrant acidity persists in this vineyard-specific wine, made in a fleshy full-bodied style with plenty of toasted oak and gravelly, tannic texture.
title: Gracianna 2015 Bacigalupi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This is a very fruity wine in its ripe peach and apple flesh tones. The grainy texture isn't quite in balance with the searing acidity, but the appealing, ripe apple characters will please many drinkers.
title: Kirkland Signature 2016 Ti Point Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Oily apple and melon aromas are the opening, while this Chardonnay feels lean, lemony and a bit hollow. Citrus flavors are yeasty, soft and a touch bland, while this is solid on the finish but doesn't taste like much.
title: Lapostolle 2016 Grand Selection Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very ripe pear and peach aromas meet with coconut and white lily on the nose of this bottling. Lime, peach and yellow-melon flavors show on the slightly vinous sip.
title: Layer Cake 2016 Chardonnay (Central Coast)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Bright, fruity tones of red cherry resound on the nose, leading to a softly structured palate that carries more bright red fruit alongside shots of granite and vanilla. Aged 10 months in Hungarian oak, this is a straightforward, pleasing Merlot.
title: Lieb 2015 Reserve Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Lean, herbal currant aromas are lightly briny and prickly. A palate with moderate acidity and some creaminess feels good, while this tastes of spiced cherry and currant. A short finish doesn't expand or evolve.
title: Loica 2015 Pacific Series Pinot Noir (Rapel Valley)

description: Made from young Chardonnay vines, this perfumed, elegant wine is crisp with acidity and ripe with red apple and spice. It is a soft wine, fruity and ready to drink.
title: Mommessin 2016 Les Grandes Mises  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: This offers aromas of leafy underbrush, berry, vanilla and a whiff of espresso while the straightforward palate shows black cherry extract, coffee and clove. Grainy tannins give it a rather abrupt, firm finish.
title: Natale Verga 2013  Barbaresco

description: Wild berry, new leather and dark spice aromas blend together with a whiff of blue flower. The austere, lean palate shows dried cherry, orange slice and a hint of ground clove. Assertive, grainy tannins create a bracing, mouth-drying finish.
title: Orsumella 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This bright wine is full of black-cherry flavors and crisp acidity, with a light touch of tannins. It has great fruitiness and freshness, and is completely ready to drink.
title: Pardon et Fils 2016 Cuvée P  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Aromas of toast, oak, cured meats and grilled herb follow over to the austere, astringent palate along with a hint of coconut and oak-driven spice. Lacking fruit richness, it can't stand up to the dominant oak sensations and grainy tannins.
title: Parusso 2013 Mosconi  (Barolo)

description: Aromas of toasted oak, menthol and scorched earth lead the nose while a whiff of plum stays in the background. The palate shows oak sensations including roasted coffee bean, toast as well as powdered sage and prune. Astringent, grainy tannins clamp down on the finish.
title: Rignana 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: A deep shade of mauve in the glass, this rosé conveys much of its Syrah parentage, starting with aromas of rust, wet earth, dark plum and dewy herbs. Thyme and dark cherry fruit show on the palate, which is texturally sound although a somewhat odd expression overall. Might be better billed as simply a light Syrah.
title: San Luis Bay Winery 2014 Rosé of Syrah (Santa Maria Valley)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: While oak-driven on the nose, boasting swathes of espresso bean and cocoa nibs, there's a core of ripe cherry and plum that carries the palate. A judicious balance of acid keeps this wine in check, while mild-grained tannins offer further support. A slight note of green stems arises on the midpalate through the astringent finish.
title: T'Jara 2013 Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Aromas of underbrush, prune, toast, used leather and a whiff of blue flower emerge in the glass. The short, simple palate offers black cherry and vanilla alongside dusty, somewhat fleeting tannins that leave a drying finish.
title: Torlasco 2013  Barbaresco

description: Old vines with concentration and light tannins give this wine its intensely fruity character. It has a juicy, crisp edge along with good acidity and a bright aftertaste. Drink the wine now.
title: Trenel Fils 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: Cherry and plum aromas include a note of green. This blend of Grenache, Carignan and Mourvèdre is lean and choppy on the palate, with more acidity than body and fruit. Raw plum and cherry flavors finish gritty and underdeveloped.
title: Ventisquero 2016 Grey Glacier Single  Block Garnacha-Cariñena-Mataro Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Slightly raisiny aromas form the opening. In the mouth, this is chewy and full, with abrasive tannins. Saucy flavors of plum, tomato and currant finish dry and woody, with a lasting note of clove that comes straight from the barrel.
title: Xavier Flouret 2014 Oscar Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Candied, slightly broken raspberry aromas are mildly grassy. This Cab has rubbery tannins and a by-the-glass mouthfeel. Plum and tomato flavors are a bit salty, while this burns with spice notes on the finish.
title: Bodegas Manutara 2016 Cabernet Sauvignon (Maule Valley)

description: Whiffs of raspberry and cherry follow over to the extremely simple, light-bodied palate along with a hint of clove. Loose tannins offer relaxed support. Drink soon.
title: Castello Vicchiomaggio 2016 San Jacopo  (Chianti Classico)

description: Subtle stone fruit and delicate floral tones lead into a heavier-than-expected palate. There's a touch of residual sugar here amidst the fleshy stone fruit characters, with a brisk, short finish.
title: Cupcake 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Gamay makes an attractive rosé, crisp and fruity. With red-currant and berry flavors, the wine is light, bright and lively. It demands to be drunk immediately.
title: Domaine de la Prébende 2016 Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Extremely light on the nose, this wine eventually shows pleasant aromas of nectarine and orange peel. The palate is also inoffensive but mild, with poached apple and pear flavors, a touch of seared orange and the taste of a lemon juice on sweet squash.
title: Firefly Ridge 2016 Pinot Gris (Central Coast)

description: This attractively structured wine offers ripe cherry fruit and an edge of firm tannins. It's rich, full of fruit, smooth and ready to drink.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Special Selection  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This is a simple, fruity wine, crisp and bright with red cherries. Acidity and light texture make it immediately drinkable. It is a well-made, delicious wine.
title: Louis Jadot 2016  Beaujolais

description: Classic Kiwi Sauvignon Blanc aromas of passionfruit, grapefruit and blossoms set the stage for a crisp, easy-going palate.
title: Overstone 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Withered violet, dried sage and toast aromas lead the nose. The firm, fleeting palate offers dried cherry, orange zest and roasted coffee bean alongside dusty, astringent tannins.
title: Poggio Morino 2015  Chianti Classico

description: A pumped-up oak and spice character dominates this medium-bodied wine in terms of both aroma and flavor, so it sings a two-note song of nutmeg and vanilla. The texture is smooth and the finish seems sweet from the baking spice tones.
title: Pra Vinera 2016 Reserve Chardonnay (California)

Appellation: Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
Designation: Amulet De Marco Vineyards Estate Bottled
description: An aroma of coffee puts a blanket over herbal bramble berry tones. There's a savory and almost sour note of tart fruit on the palate, with a thin feel and sharp acidity.
title: Seneca Shore 2012 Amulet De Marco Vineyards Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (Seneca Lake)

description: This SB from the southerly outpost of Malleco smells briny and green, which given its cool origins is not a shock. A chunky palate holds vegetal flavors of peas, green onion, fennel and bell pepper, while a lean finish suggests nettle and milkweed.
title: William Fèvre 2016 La Misión Reserva Especial Sauvignon Blanc (Malleco)

description: Aromas of coconut, toast, oak-driven spice and a whiff of prune lead the nose. The aromas carry over to the rather diluted, lean palate along with sour cherry and roasted coffee bean. Astringent, fleeting tannins give it an abrupt close.
title: Cantalici L'Antica Fornace di Ridolfo 2013  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of plum and high-toned dried herb lead to cherry and berry flavors. The tannins bring a lightly gritty feel. The tartness of the acidity adds to the enjoyment, but you'd never peg it for Cabernet.
title: Catman 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

Designation: Serie Riberas Gran Reserva Ribera del Tinguiririca
description: Rough aromas of road tar and forced oak are most prominent on this Cabernet. A fully extracted palate is tough to process, while roasted blackberry flavors run heavy on the oak and clove. On the finish, a leftover clove flavor dominates.
title: Concha y Toro 2016 Serie Riberas Gran Reserva Ribera del Tinguiririca Cabernet Sauvignon (Marchigue)

description: Scents of vanilla and sweet spice greet the nose upon entry, while raspberry sauce and brambly berry provide a solid grounding for the medium-weight palate. The tannins are soft and the acidity low, with a lingering mocha accent on the rich finish. Drink now.
title: David Frost 2015 Shiraz (Western Cape)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Black cherry and strawberry notes are a tad muted in this demure, full-bodied red. Soft acidity and tannins lend an approachable appeal but it's best to drink this wine young.
title: Domaine Guy Mousset 2016 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This wine is simple and fruity. Crisp black-cherry fruit is lifted by refreshing acidity while the light texture gives the wine an attractive tangy character. Drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This bright and crisp wine has red fruits, a touch of vanilla and lively acidity. It is a simple, fruity wine that is ready to drink.
title: Laurent Gauthier 2016 Vintage Rosé (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Subtle notes of tomato leaf, passionfruit and tropical fruit undertones are followed by a boney palate of tongue tingling acidity. Drink now.
title: Lobster Reef 2017 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very shy aromas of red berries and wet mud make for an earthy muddled nose. There is a flash of acid on the sip, followed by dust, more red fruit and a slightly woody spice.
title: Martin Ranch 2015 J.D. Hurley R.E.D. Vineyards Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: Sweet-seeming oak aromas and flavors grab the attention in this medium-bodied wine, while moderate tannins and rather soft acidity make it easy to sip.
title: Sand Point 2015 Merlot (Clarksburg)

description: This wine tastes quite mature and savory and feels soft, showing some complexity of sun-baked fruit and earthy undertones.
title: Shooting Star 2015 Pinot Noir (Lake County)

description: This soft, strawberry-jam-flavored wine is made from organically grown grapes. It has a pure fruity character just gently touched by tannins, presenting an almost sweet, ripe character. Drink now.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2016 Cuvée Classic  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This is one of the more odd-smelling Pinots you'll come across. Aromas of milk chocolate, cherry and mint are candied as a whole. In the mouth, however, this smacks with cool-climate acidity. Flavors of cherry, plum and oak-based chocolate finish with raw tannins.
title: Viña Aquitania 2014 Sol de Sol Pinot Noir (Malleco)

description: Made with 80% Sangiovese and 20% Merlot, this opens with aromas of black plum, coconut and incense. The firm palate delivers black currant, star anise, espresso and a touch of orange peel alongside austere tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Setriolo 2012 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Loads of fresh apple aromas lead the nose of this Reserve Riesling, with a bit of pollen-dusted ripe stone fruit in the background. The off-dry palate is clear in its Gala apple expression—ripe with a mild, crisp balance. A savory, yeasty note melds into the powdery texture of the finish.
title: Sheldrake Point 2016 Reserve Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Seductive cinnamon aromas lead to ripe red-cherry flavors in this attractively oaky wine. It adds nutmeg and clove to a pleasant and almost sweet fruitiness, while the texture is smooth and just lightly tannic.
title: Tangley Oaks 2015 Lot #15 Merlot (Mendocino)

description: The extra seriousness that the slopes of Mount Brouilly give to a wine are very evident here. It is dense and structured, with flavors of mint as well as black fruits. The wine should age further, so drink from mid-2018.
title: Trenel Fils 2016  Côte de Brouilly

description: Light green-fruit aromas are tight and show a hint of dusty yeast. This has a lean, citric mouthfeel with acidic tang. Oily citrus flavors come with a sprinkle of salt, while this shows both body and length on the finish.
title: Undurraga 2017 Sibaris Gran Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Barbera (61%) takes the lead on this blend, followed by Cabernet Sauvignon (18%), Primitivo (16%), Dolcetto (4%) and Sangiovese. The aromas offer notes of herb, freshly hewn wood, cherry and vanilla. The cranberry tart and juicy fruit flavors bring appeal. Pair it with pizza or pasta.
title: Wind Rose 2014 Bravo Rosso Red (Yakima Valley)

description: Made from half Merlot and nearly equal amounts Petite Sirah and Zinfandel, this is a brawny broad-shouldered blend rich in black fruit, oak and sturdy tannin. Full bodied, it shows secondary tones of graphite and tobacco.
title: 29 & Oak Wines 2015 Proprietary Red (Napa Valley)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese, 5% Merlot and 5% Colorino, this opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, cooking spice and a hint of orange peel. The bright palate delivers red cherry, clove and white pepper alongside smooth tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Aiola 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of cocoa, coffee, herb and cherry are followed by somewhat lightly styled plum and dark-fruit flavors. It pulls up a bit short on the finish and the tannins are a touch dry.
title: Airfield Estates 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: From the producer's estate vineyard, the fruit certified biodynamic and organic, this is a rustic, daring wine, ethereal on the one hand in body and spirit, while biting in tannin. Ash and earthy strawberry provide the core of flavor.
title: Ampelos 2014 Delta Grenache (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of fresh and dried herb, cedar box, cranberry, Red Vines, smoke and dark fruit. The palate reveals tightly wound, slightly dried-out seeming red and black-fruit flavors that carry through the finish.
title: Barons 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Templeton Gap District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bright aromas of melon, strawberry guava, lemon blossom, tuberose and ripe apples show on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines citrusy energy and decent tension with ripe pear and guava-cream flavors.
title: Barton 2016 Broken Road Viognier (Templeton Gap District)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A delicate nose of ripe strawberry and white flowers show in this pale, salmon pink rosé. There's pleasant roundness on the palate, with ripe peach struck against a crushed stone core.
title: Bellwether 2016 Dry Rosé (Finger Lakes)

description: Floral, this wine offers voluptuously sizable weight and body around a soft texture of lasting acidity. The fruit leans tartly into grapefruit and lime.
title: Benessere 2016 Pinot Grigio (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Smooth and tannic, this wine from Grenache specialist speaks loudly to a core of dried cherry, almost liqueur-like in its richness and density, given savory companionship by thickets of leather.
title: C R Graybehl 2015 Mounts Family Vineyards Grenache (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Moist underbrush, grilled porcini mushroom, toast and oak aromas come to the forefront along with a whiff of game. The aromas carry over to the palate along with raw sour cherry and powdered sage. Austere tannins and firm acidity provide the lean framework.
title: Carpineto 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This has enticing scents of iris, violet and a whiff of sandalwood while the juicy, straightforward palate offers fleshy wild cherry and baking spice notes. Supple tannins provide soft support. Enjoy soon.
title: Casa Emma 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Black plum, tilled soil and cooking spice come together on this while the firm palate offers dried black cherry, star anise and a hint of mocha. Closed-grained tannins offer support.
title: Castello Vicchiomaggio 2014 Agostino Petri  (Chianti Classico)

description: Rich and with fine tannins, this is a solid wine with great structure. It has the juicy fruit typical of the vintage, giving a perfumed wine to drink now.
title: Château de Chatelard 2016 Cuvée les Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: This plump, full-bodied red blend offers loads of luscious black-cherry and plum flavors. Dark herb, earth and leather notes lend rusticity to the midpalate making it an elegant wine for a impressively low price.
title: Château Grande Cassagne 2015 Grenache-Syrah (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Delicate hints of smoke and nuts dissipate into ripe black cherry and strawberry preserve in this supple, full-figured red. It's an easy-drinking wine that's pleasantly plump yet freshly balanced.
title: Château Saint-Louis la Perdrix 2015 L'Envol Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Black raspberry and cherry flavors are sumptuous and ripe on the nose but comparatively restrained on the palate of this wine. It's juicy and easy drinking though with fresh acidity and wispy, delicate tannins. Drink now through 2020.
title: Clef des Murailles 2014 Red (Vacqueyras)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Shy yet pure aromas of citrus, stone fruit and slate carry the nose. The light-bodied palate strikes a balance between ripeness and tension, ripe peach and apricot flavors are shot through with quinine acidity. Just enough acidity propels this along to a medium-long finish.
title: Damiani 2015 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: A bold and fruity wine from the smallest of the cru villages, this has some structure as well as juicy red fruits and bright acidity. The fruitiness makes this lightly textured, generous wine. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de Côtes Rémont 2016  Chénas

description: This is a young, boisterous wine. Its red fruit is still surrounded by tannins and a firm structure, with plenty of acidity. Crisply textured and lively, it will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Maisons Neuves 2016  Chiroubles

description: Old vines give concentration although they don't go far from the rounded fruity style of Brouilly. The result is a wine that is packed with bright berry fruits and also has depth and richness. Drink now.
title: Domaine des Maisons Neuves 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Brouilly)

description: This crisp and structured wine comes from the highest altitude of the Beaujoalis crus. In this vintage, the tannins and structure are an important element although the bright red fruits will develop. Give this wine until mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Chiroubles)

description: Based in Juliénas, but also with vines on the lower slopes in the Beaujolais-Villages appellation, this producer has made a wine that has great fruit and a balance of tannins and acidity. It has weight and is rich and dense, laced with a crisp aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Domaine du Clos du Fief 2016 La Roche  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: The organic fruit that goes into this wine gives it both richness and a pure, clean character that make it immediately appealing. It is a simple wine, all black fruit and with good acidity. Drink this ripe wine now.
title: Domaine Perol 2015 Le Clos Pierres Dorées  (Beaujolais)

description: Bold tannins mark out this wine as needing to age. Its concentrated structure and powerful black fruits are solid, with a firm and dry core. Drink this dark-fruit and dark-chocolate-flavored wine from late 2018.
title: Domaine Raphaël Chopin 2016 Caprice  (Régnié)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Classic Zweigelt aromas of dark cherry, plum and white pepper show some nice depth on the nose. The medium-bodied palate brings a focused intensity of tart black fruit wrapped in a sheen of cedar, vanilla and peppery spice. Soft tannins and hi-def acidity keeps this alive and kicking, while a delicate cocoa-dusted, black-plum skin note closes this out.
title: Shalestone 2015 Zweigelt (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon (45%), Cabernet Franc (30%) and Merlot. Baking spice, licorice, vanilla and dried fruit aromas are followed by full-bodied fruit and spice flavors. It brings appeal, but the fruit seems a bit desiccated. Tasted twice with consistent notes.
title: Substance 2014 Bx Vineyard Collection Klein Vineyard Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: This 100% Sangiovese offers aromas of underbrush, black-skinned berry and tilled soil. The straightforward palate offers blackberry jam, tobacco and mocha alongside closed-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Tenuta degli Dei 2015 Forcole  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 60% Grenache and 40% Syrah shows perky boysenberry, vanilla, crushed flowers and roasted black plum on the nose. Rounded red and purple fruits meet with leather and tannic tension on the sip.
title: Trenza 2013 Mosaico Red (San Luis Obispo County)

description: Light wood aging has enhanced the ripe fruit in this smooth, generous wine. Flavors of honey, almonds and citrus give both richness and minerality. Drink this wine now.
title: Vignerons des Pierres Dorées 2016 Rostre de Bélemnite  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: Not shying away from oak influence, notes of crème brûlée, toasted nuts and some melon at the back lead into a rich palate that's nutty and salty, with some smokey oak characters. Opulent but not flashy, this is a full-bodied white that many will be grateful for.
title: Villa Maria 2016 Private Bin Chardonnay (East Coast)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fresh apples, pear flesh, squeezed lemon and spring blossoms show on the nose of this bottling by musician Zac Brown. There is a strong sizzle of acidity to the sip, where zesty flavors of Key lime spray and Pink Lady apples arise.
title: Z. Alexander Brown 2016 Uncaged Chardonnay (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Sour cherry, coconut, espresso and menthol aromas lead the nose. The palate is full-bodied but doesn't possess a lot of fruit richness, evoking raw cherry, orange zest, licorice and French oak. Taut, astringent tannins grip the finish.
title: Agostino Bosco 2013 La Serra  (Barolo)

description: On the nose, this straddles the fence between citrusy and vegetal. A lean tangy palate plays up crisp minerality over chunkiness, while flavors of green herbs and cactus dominate through the finish.
title: Aresti 2017 Trisquel Gran Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: New York, New York Other, New York, US
description: A ripe mix of red cherry and cranberry has a thin veil of peppery spice and toast on the nose. This Bordeaux-style red blend boasts a round, luscious palate of red plum and tart berries, with ample weight carrying through to the peppery finish.
title: Benmarl 2015 Proprietor's Reserve Red (New York)

description: French oak, coconut and toasted aromas carry over to the tight, taut palate along with vanilla, dried sour cherry, a green note of powdered sage and a hint of roasted coffee bean. Grainy, astringent tannins give it a raspy finish.
title: Ca'Romè 2013 Maria di Brun  (Barbaresco)

description: Oak, coconut and underbrush aromas mingle with espresso on this tightly tannic red. The grainy palate offers licorice, oak-driven spice and a hint of dried cherry but not much fruit richness. Raspy tannins leave an astringent, drying finish. Give the tannins a few more years to unwind then drink to catch the remaining fruit.
title: Ca'Romè 2014 Rio Sordo  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Bright red-cherry and strawberry notes are offset by tones of dried herbs and underbrush. This wine is rounded and full on the palate but delicate in concentration. It finishes with drying, slightly tacky tannins.
title: Château de Campuget 2015 1753 Grenache (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Waxy lemon rind and yellow apples perfume this dry, full-bodied white blend. It's not a deeply concentrated wine but silky on the palate. The finish is bright and brisk. Drink now through 2019.
title: Château de Campuget 2015 Tradition White (Costières de Nîmes)

description: This fruity wine offers crisp lemon and juicy pear and quince flavors. This light, poised wine is ready to drink.
title: Château de Corcelles 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: Scents of biltong, black cherry, plum sauce and potpourri waft from the glass of this savory red. The palate is smooth and easy drinking, with soft tannins and plenty of black fruit flavors at the core. Hints of peppery spice and fruit skin grace the close. Drink now.
title: David Frost 2015 Pinotage (Western Cape)

description: Spicy cocoa aromas are followed on the palate by the crispest acidity and juicy red-berry flavors. Still young with its fruit character, the wine needs to broaden to bring out richness. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Côtes Rémont 2016  Juliénas

description: The winery's ode to its Italian roots, this blends Sangiovese with Malbec, Petite Sirah and Cabernet Sauvignon, creating a hearty fruit-forward endeavor highlighted in cherry, berry, leather and chocolate. Pronounced oak provides a touch of bitterness that contrasts with the fruit.
title: Ferrari-Carano 2015 Siena Red (Sonoma County)

description: This wine is zippy and aromatic in tones of tropical fruit, grapefruit and a hint of bell pepper. The palate shows some weight from the rich fruit flavors, balanced by tingly acidity. It's capped off with a clean, mineral finish.
title: Framingham 2016 Ribbonwood Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Aromas of coffee and cocoa cream are in the fore of this ripe Cabernet Franc. Medium-bodied, yet light in concentration, the palate is carried by juicy cranberry, cherry and sweet oak flavors.
title: Fulkerson 2015 Estate Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: Spicy prune, cooked berry and woodsy aromas open this blend of Carignan, Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot and Cab Franc. Despite showing a black color and coming from a very warm year, this shows a tartaric, bright side that's racy at best and a little out of balance at worst. Toasty blackened plum and blackberry flavors are spicy across an acid-driven finish.
title: Garcia + Schwaderer 2012 Facundo Red (Chile)

description: From several different parcels on the Côte du Py vineyard, this wine is both structured and fruity. Its crisp edge and dry tannic core both point to aging potential. Drink from late 2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: With its fine tannins and dusty texture, this is already delicious. Layers of acidity and a sense of perfumed richness give it full, attractive fruitiness. Drink now.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Château des Déduits  (Fleurie)

description: Richly and dense, this wine has ripe berries, crisp acidity and smooth tannins that are just a background to the juicy, attractive fruit. The wine is totally ready to drink.
title: Jean-Michel Dupré 2016 Terre Noire Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais)

description: This pleasant white opens with pretty scents of white flowers, crisp apple and lemon peel. The palate is surprisingly plush and opulent, with a rich, round mouthfeel and robust flavors of creamed pear and soft melon that lead into a softly spiced finish. Drink now.
title: Linton Park 2015 Black Rhino Chardonnay (Wellington)

description: This wine is all about crisp fruit. It is bright and juicy, full of red cherries with a touch of pepper to add attractive spice. The wine is light, bright and ready to drink.
title: Mommessin 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: An intriguing combo of citrus rind, pear juice, coppery penny and toast aromas precede a palate that is rich but with balanced acidity and a toasty finish. A food friendly tipple.
title: Mt. Beautiful 2015 Pinot Gris (Canterbury)

description: Savory aromas of roasted plum and berry fruits come with a hint of red-apple skins. This feels choppy and grabby, so don't expect a lush smooth mouthfeel. Flavors of raisin, prune and plum are heavy and a bit monotonous on the finish.
title: Oveja Negra 2014 Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Maule Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Whole lime and white peach aromas are at the fore. As promised, it drinks dry with tart Granny Smith apple flavors that provide plenty of intrigue. Enjoy it with food to see it at its best.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 Dry Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Aromas of plum cake, leather, menthol and a whiff of exotic spice slowly take shape. The palate is less expressive and needs time to come around, but eventually offers black cherry extract, star anise and a hint of tobacco. Dusty tannins lead to a drying, somewhat fleeting finish.
title: Piemaggio 2012 Le Fioraie  (Chianti Classico)

description: A classic Chilean Cabernet nose of dark-fruit aromas, wood spice and tar introduces a weighty palate with raw tannins. Savory, barrel-heavy flavors of berry fruits finish with a monotone taste and character of resiny oak.
title: Arboleda 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Aconcagua Valley)

description: Made with 80% Sangiovese, 15% Merlot and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, this has aromas of violet, red-skinned berry and underbrush. The bright palate offers ripe raspberry, red cherry and a sprinkling of white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Barone Ricasoli 2015 Brolio  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Engaging on the nose in racy red cherry and currant, a bit of funk along with smoke and clove offer some intrigue. Grippy, sandy tannins, crisp acidity and a medium body carry similar flavors, with warm spices and toast leading to a cranberry-tannin finish.
title: Benmarl 2015 Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: French oak, spiced plum, coconut and blue flower aromas lead the nose. On the palate, fine-grained tannins underscore oak-driven spice, prune, vanilla and dried black cherry. Drink sooner rather than later to catch the remaining fruit.
title: Bindi Sergardi 2013 Mocenni Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: A blend of 33% Chenin Blanc, 33% Sémillon, 25% Sauvignon Blanc and 9% viognier, this is ripe and forward in fruit tones. Toasted apple, melon rind, orange oil and yellow flowers abound, framed by just enough acidity to counter the palate's opulence. Drink now.
title: Black Elephant 2015 Timothy White (Franschhoek)

Appellation: Los Olivos District, Central Coast, California, US
description: This twist-top bottling is fun and fresh, perfect with a light chill. Aromas of red plums, strawberries, carnations and cola lead into a zippy palate full of plum and more strawberry fruit.
title: Brander 2016 Merlot (Los Olivos District)

description: Moist underbrush, woodland berry, blue flower and dark culinary spice aromas delicately guide the nose. The poised, ethereal palate offers succulent sour cherry, coffee bean and a hint of clove while supple tannins and firm acidity offer lithe support. Drink through 2021.
title: Castello di Monterinaldi 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This structured, firm wine is produced from 65-year-old vines. Tannins and juicy acidity support the red-berry flavors and crisp texture. This fruity wine has the tannins to age further. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château Gaillard 2016  Morgon

Appellation: Sonoma County-Monterey County-Santa Barbara County, California Other, California, US
description: This is a fresh light spicy wine that's well balanced and flavorful without overdoing the body or extract. It blends red cherry, cinnamon, vanilla and strawberry and has good acidity and a touch of tannin to heighten the mouthfeel.
title: Cherry Pie 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County-Monterey County-Santa Barbara County)

description: There's plenty of oak on this full-bodied Bordeaux blend in the form of cedar spice, dusting polish and Dr. Pepper, but it's balanced by juicy blackberries, florals, and the signature Gimblett Gravels stoniness. Lively acidity lifts the powerful, grainy tannins out of broody beast territory. This should age nicely to boot. Drink now–2027.
title: Craggy Range 2015 Te Kahu Gimblett Gravels Red (Hawke's Bay)

description: Hints of vanilla bean and maple bar in the aroma make this full-bodied wine seem more sweet than it is, because it responds on the palate with mouth-filling fruit, moderately firm acidity and tannins. It finishes nicely dry, quite fruity and generous.
title: Dancing Bull 2015 Zinfandel (California)

description: Crisp Anjou pear, white flowers, lime zest and chalk show on the nose of this bottling. There's a great interplay between the high acid and tannic grip on the sip, where ripe Asian pear and red-apple flavors slide into a squeaky, taut finish.
title: Darcie Kent Vineyards 2016 Rava Blackjack Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Monterey)

description: This is a ripe and generous wine from a well-regarded producer. It is rich with red berries and structured with a series of fine tannins and acidity. It offers weight, plenty of fruit and is ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Nugues 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: This wine is ripe, softly structured and ready to drink. Red berres and acidity dominate, smoothed by wood aging in large barrels. It is fruity, with a crisp aftertaste.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016 L'Ancien  (Beaujolais)

description: Produced from the domaine of Roches du Py that this producer purchased in 2004, this wine has much of the same structure and rich tannins as a true Côte du Py. The intense black fruits are poised and will allow the wine to age well. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine Guenaël Jambon 2016 Les Roches du Py  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This family-owned estate, going back to 1700, lies in southern Beaujolais in the golden stones or Pierres Dorées region. The wine is rich and intense with black fruits and impressive texture for a simple Beaujolais. Drink the wine now.
title: Domaine Romy 2016 Vieilles Vignes Les Pierres Dorées  (Beaujolais)

description: Toast, French oak, espresso and forest floor aromas lead the nose. The tight palate is assertively tannic, offering cassis, dried cherry, coconut and vanilla alongside astringent, close-grained tannins that grip the finish.
title: Fattoria di Corsignano 2013 L'Imperatrice Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this medium-bodied red has aromas suggesting underbrush, red plum and a whiff of tobacco leaf. The round palate offers black cherry, clove and a hint of thyme alongside supple tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: Gagliole 2015 Rubiolo  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bright blackberry jam aromas meet with dusty earth, baked bricks and a hint of caramel on the nose of this bottling. The palate is very ripe with blueberry, cassis and baked licorice flavors, very lush and comforting, if a tad sweet.
title: HammerSky 2014 Extraction Special Selection Reserve Petit Verdot (Paso Robles)

description: Aromas of leather, underbrush and a touch of iris take shape on this 90% Sangiovese and 10% Canaiolo blend. The juicy palate doles out black cherry, raspberry jam and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2024.
title: Il Brunone 2012  Chianti Classico

description: This is a soft and round Pinot with no sharp edges. Flower stalks mingle with almost confect cherry and watermelon notes on the nose, while the palate is plush and juicy with a satiny texture, a nice gentle grip of tannins and a raspberry tang to finish things off.
title: Jules Taylor 2016 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

Importer: Vinum Wine Importing and Distributing LLC
description: Aromas of fennel, chopped herb and menthol lift out of the glass. The somewhat lean, racy palate offers dried cranberry, coffee bean and clove framed in firm tannins and vibrant acidity. A touch of licorice softens the tight finish.
title: La Bioca 2014 Pistin  (Barbaresco)

description: Juniper aromas rule the nose on this cool-climate herbaceous Syrah. In the mouth, this is pulling and tannic, with a hard grind of gravel. Black-fruit flavors are narrow and bounce between eucalyptus and chocolate. Those same chocolaty green flavors control the finish.
title: Loma Larga 2012 Unfiltered Syrah (Casablanca Valley)

description: There's plenty of easygoing red and black fruit flavor here, but the classic Mediterranean garrigue—rosemary, lavender and thyme—is central to this bold, full-bodied red. Crisp red-cherry and blackberry flavors are fresh and primary still, marked by vibrant acidity and firm, fine-grained tannins that frame the finish.
title: Marrenon 2013 Sépia Red (Luberon)

description: Candied cherry flavors are brashly ripe and juicy in this simple effort, the structure moderate and the texture oaky and tannic. Meat, black licorice and blackberry jam meld along a density that's hard to shake through the big finish.
title: Muscardini 2013 Gracie Creek Vineyards Syrah (Sonoma Valley)

description: This 85% Sangiovese and 15% Merlot blend opens with aromas of underbrush, red plum and baking spice. On the palate, clove and licorice notes accent a black cherry core while polished tannins provide the framework. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Poggio Torselli 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This dark plush blend of Syrah and Grenache with smaller amounts of Mourvèdre and Tempranillo gets rolling with soft stewed berry aromas and flavors of prune and chocolate. A sweet pastry flavor dominates a short finish. Enjoy this for its ripe chewy lushness.
title: Polkura 2014 GSM + T Red (Marchigue)

description: Rustic notes of moss and freshly tilled soil mingle into fresh cassis and cherry in this plush but savory wine. It's rich and languorous in mouthfeel but bold, tacky tannins lend a dark, bitter tone to the finish.
title: Rémy Ferbras 2015 Red (Rasteau)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Tightly wound aromas of lemon skin meet with hints of toast and pecan wood on the nose of this bottling from Bien Nacido Vineyard. Mandarin pith, lemon rind and toasty croissant flavors show on the subtle palate.
title: Steele 2016 Pinot Blanc (Santa Barbara County)

description: This full-bodied wine packs in ripe dark-fruit flavors of blackberry and boysenberry, and has smoky oaky accents to add some welcome complexity. Adding in 3% Cabernet Sauvignon, 2% Malbec and 2% Merlot accounts for some of that complexity. The wine is concentrated and mouth filling in texture without being too tannic.
title: The Federalist 2015 Zinfandel (Mendocino)

description: This wine, with its firm structure, is dense and concentrated. It offers blackberry fruit, rich tannins and balanced, juicy acidity that leaves a delicious, crisp aftertaste
title: Domaine de Briante 2016 Naturellement  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: A dusting of saline minerality lends freshness to soft yellow apple and pear in this pretty white. It's not a deeply concentrated or complex wine but dry and approachable. The silky finish lingers.
title: Domaine de la Petite Cassagne 2015 White (Costières de Nîmes)

description: This rich and dense wine shows the influence of 60-year-old vines in its depth of flavor. Black cherries and generous, integrated tannins give richness and structure. Drink now.
title: Domaine de la Plaigne 2016  Régnié

description: This ripe and fruity wine has rich yellow and white-fruit flavors that are full and refreshing. Zingy orange zest adds bite to the soft, generous texture. It is ready to drink.
title: Domaine de la Prébende 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: Fleshy black-plum and cherry flavors are accented by vanilla, spice and tobacco in this lush, easygoing red. While not deeply concentrated or complex, its juicy, fruity style and soft tannins make it a friendly choice for casual entertaining.
title: Domaine des Pasquiers 2015  Gigondas

description: Made from 30-year-old vines, this is a smooth, ripe wine. Mangoes and apricots give an exotic character to this densely textured wine. It is rich and ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016 Classic  (Beaujolais Blanc)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of strawberry, apple and citrus provide appeal. The palate is lively and elegant, with a sweet citrus-filled finish.
title: Domaine Ste. Michelle NV Brut Rosé Méthode Champenoise Sparkling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Enticing aromas of ripe stone fruit and red apple pop with intensity on the palate. An acidic vein of crunchy green apple helps maintain balance in this easy-drinking semidry Riesling.
title: Dr. Konstantin Frank 2016 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Aromas of French oak, scorched earth stewed plum and exotic spice lead the nose along with a whiff of nail varnish. The brawny, evolved palate offers coconut, oak-driven spice and steeped prune alongside the heat of evident alcohol and velvety tannins. Drink sooner rather than later to capture remaining fruit and freshness.
title: Elio Filippino 2011 Serra Capelli Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Max Reserva Pinot Noir is one of those consistent performers: it's always very good, if not spectacular. This version is lean and earthy, with a good balance of acidity, dry red-fruit character and varietal correctness. Flavors of spiced tomato, red currant and herbs finish tight and raw.
title: Errazuriz 2015 Max Reserva Pinot Noir (Aconcagua Costa)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Hints of dried herb and sea salt lend a savory tone to fresh lemon, apple and grapefruit in this dry, full-bodied white. It's a restrained but broadly textured wine with an earthy, mineral finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Famille Perrin 2016 Réserve White (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Aromas suggesting exotic spice, plum, violet and balsamic aromas come together on this. The vibrant palate delivers black cherry, blackberry, star anise and a hint of espresso framed in firm acidity and dusty, close-grained tannins.
title: Fattoria di Corsignano 2014  Chianti Classico

description: This soft, creamy wine is full of apple and bright citrus flavors. Good concentration along with a tangy, orange texture give a crisp edge to this already delicious wine. Drink now.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée B  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: The nose is rather shy but eventually reveals leather, underbrush and a menthol note. The tight, straightforward palate offers dried cherry along with hints of baking spice and star anise. Polished tannins provide the framework. Drink 2019–2024.
title: Gabriele Scaglione 2012 Passione di Re  (Barolo)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Woody elderberry, thyme and dried forest aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The dark fruit is somewhat dried on the palate, where oregano and dust flavors round out the sip.
title: Grey Wolf Cellars 2015 Tundra Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: This is a broad, deeply flavored wine, highlighted in dark plum, black cherry and blackberry flavors. Big boned, it offers a softness of texture accounted in clove and toasted oak.
title: Greyscale 2015 Merlot (Oak Knoll District)

description: This is a single-estate wine, ripe with cherry flavors and light on the tannins. The vines, within sight of the landmark of Mount Brouilly, have given a generous wine that is also crisp with acidity. Drink now.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Château de la Perrière  (Brouilly)

description: This is made from 50% Sauvignon Blanc, 25% Chardonnay and 25% Malvasia. The trio work well together, offering jasmine aromas around medium-bodied well-integrated flavors of peach and green apple.
title: Jon Nathaniel 2016 Honest Bucker White (Napa Valley)

description: This is a generous plush-textured wine made from organically grown grapes that combines a fresh fruitiness with more floral, candied, tutti-frutti flavors and just a pinch of sugar to soften the mouthfeel.
title: Korbel 2016 Brut Sparkling (California)

description: This has delicate aromas of blackberry, tobacco and white pepper that follow through to the medium-bodied palate alongside a star anise note. Taut tannins provide tight support. Drink through 2021.
title: Le Bocce 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Ripe berry, dark spice and tobacco aromas mingle in the glass. The straightforward palate shows wild cherry, licorice and a hint of espresso alongside bright acidity and dusty tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Lecci e Brocchi 2014 Il Chiorba Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A blend of 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this offers aromas of cedar, espresso and a touch of cassis. The dense palate shows ripe black plum, roasted coffee bean and vanilla alongside closed-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Mannucci Droandi 2013 Ceppeto Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Made with organically farmed grapes, this has earthy aromas of tilled soil, truffle and a whiff of black-skinned berry. The chewy palate doles out ripe black cherry, licorice and clove alongside smooth tannins. Enjoy through 2022.
title: Mannucci Droandi 2015 Ceppeto  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This 50-50 blend of Santa Maria Valley and Santa Cruz Mountains fruit shows candied lemon rind and Gala apple on the approachable, familiar nose. There is a touch of zip to the palate, where lime cream and salted citrus flavors arise.
title: Mignanelli 2016 Chardonnay (Central Coast)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Generous in ripe black cherry, pine forest, tar and toasted oak, this is a hearty wine, robustly full bodied, with a gravelly aspect to the texture that provides complexity and ample length.
title: Migration 2015 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: This wine offers a wealth of structured tannins that integrate well with its fresh tart red fruit. Soy, baked bread and cedar add nuance and additional flavor.
title: Monticello Vineyards 2013 Estate Grown Merlot (Oak Knoll District)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon. The aromas pop, with notes of raspberry, plum, herb and spice. The palate brings a mixture of fruit and savory flavors, with the tannins bringing a lightly tacky feel. There's much to enjoy.
title: North by Northwest 2015 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: From the granite soils of Saint-Amour in the far north of Beaujolais, this dense wine is rich in both tannins and ripe black fruits. Its structure and firm character promise good aging for this full wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Pascal Berthier 2016 Esprit de Séduction  (Saint-Amour)

description: Bold and tannic, this wine has lots of structure and a firm mouthfeel along with good but tight flavors of black cherry and blackberry. It tastes lean and focused and will pair nicely with rich proteins.
title: Peltier 2015 Black Diamond Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Lodi)

description: This opens with aromas of black currant, cedar and a whiff of green peppercorn. The round palate evokes black plum, star anise and a touch of cinnamon alongside fine-grained tannins.
title: Podere Ciona 2014  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are a bit unusual, with notes of wood spice and raspberry fruit leather, not seeming entirely harmonious. The palate is quite light in style, painting around the edges. It's interesting but it doesn't all come together—not standing on its own.
title: Plumb Cellars 2013 Estate JK Vineyard Sangiovese (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: A hot 2017 with forest fires in many parts of Chile forced early picking, and seemingly this shear high-acid SB with just 12.3% alcohol was part of that. Spunky sharp flavors of briny citrus fruits include pyrazine-based notes of green herbs, while this ends with a high-acid finish.
title: Rafael Tirado Wines 2017 Laberinto Sauvignon Blanc (Maule Valley)

description: This is an oaky, smoky wine, with a full body and firm tannins. It smells like wood smoke and black pepper, tastes like smoked brisket and has an oaky sharpness in the texture.
title: Scotto Family Cellars 2013 Malbec (Lodi)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: The warmth of the vintage shows through in this bottling, which typically exudes tension and focus. This is ripe in stone-fruit aromas and flavors, with shades of savory thyme. The weight of the alcohol is evident on the palate, giving way to a sightly flabby feel, yet a pleasing astringency of grape skins seems to balance everything out come the finish.
title: Sheldrake Point 2016 Dry Estate Bottled Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine is fresh and fruity in flavor but serious in mouthfeel, as firm tannins and considerable acidity give it a lively, crisp sensation on the palate. It has tart red cherry and raspberry flavors and is very focused and pure tasting.
title: Sierra Starr 2015 Rising Starr Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (Sierra Foothills)

description: Dense and full bodied, this wine offers lifted plum and black cherry notes, combined with spicy oak and cola. The tannins are on the chewy, leathery side but the fruit is rich and mouth-filling, with an interesting tangy orange-rind finish. A steak wine if there ever was one. Drink now–2027.
title: Te Mata 2015 Awatea Red (Hawke's Bay)

description: You'll find delicate aromas of baking spice, cassis and underbrush on this blend of 90% Sangiovese, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Merlot. The straightforward palate offers black cherry jam, licorice and a hint of tobacco alongside round tannins.
title: Tiziano 2013  Chianti Classico

description: This wine tastes very lush, fruity and pastry-like, fairly dripping with pineapple and honey flavors, dressed in a rich soft texture with just a touch of good acidity to balance out a perceived sweetness.
title: Vanilla Puddin 2013 Chardonnay (California)

description: This soft, perfumed wine has red-cherry flavors and an attractive light texture. It offers some of the structure associated with the most northern of the Beaujolais crus, but the main thrust is fresh red fruits. Drink now.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2016  Saint-Amour

description: Ripe red fruits dominate in this juicy, easy-going glugger. There's an earthy funk and a meaty savoriness hiding well behind all that plush fruit. The palate is round and satiny while the acidity is on the lower end. Drink now.
title: Villa Maria 2016 Private Bin Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are light, with notes of mocha, herb, plum and pie cherry along with whiffs of tire rubber. Despite the high alcohol level, the palate shows a fairly elegant sense of styling, tapering toward the finish.
title: Wind Rose 2014 Omaggio Barbera (Red Mountain)

description: Ripe cherries and red currants combine with oak-derived flavors of Dr. Pepper, herbs and toast. The palate is on the tart, lean side and the oak is rather prominent, particularly on the finish.
title: Wither Hills 2013 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: A regular Casablanca SB nose with citrus and green aromas introduces a fair and healthy palate. A mix of generic citrus, green herb and bitter flavors tastes salad-like on the finish.
title: Loma Larga 2017 Lomas del Valle Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: There's a mild intensity of cherry and berry aromas on the nose that blend with sweet spice and cedar oil. The medium-bodied palate shows a decent grip of tannins, bolstered by dark cherry, bubble gum spice and specks of pepper. An easy drinking, everyday quaff.
title: 240 Days 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: A foresty earthy nose is based on dark-fruit aromas. This feels round, with good acidity, but there's a hollowness to the palate that makes things not add up. Roasted, savory plum flavors are oaky, while this is mild and short on the finish.
title: Bodega Montsecano 2016 Refugio Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Here's a straightforward red that opens with subdued aromas of mature berry and a whiff of culinary spice. Reflecting the nose, the no-frills palate offers dried cherry and a hint of clove alongside taut, somewhat grainy tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2016 Castelgreve  (Chianti Classico)

description: Earthy cherry coulis and a strong scent of menthol dominate in this value-priced wine—a hard find in this varietal category. Thin on the palate, it's an easygoing quaff, light and fairly nondescript in oak and fruit, dusted in forest pine.
title: Castle Rock 2013 Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: The aromas on this bottling are currently very shy, with hints of black fruit and pepper. The palate is also still emerging from its shell, with hints of tar, black plum and baking spice. The texture is decent, and will allow the flavors to show better in time.
title: Castoro Cellars 2015 Whale Rock Vineyard Reserve Syrah (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Lemon and orange pith aromas meet with toasted brioche and lemongrass on the intriguing nose of this bottling. The palate mixes apples, nuts and spice into a charoseth-like flavor profile, with hints of guava paste and yeasty sourdough rounding out the unique experience.
title: Cottonwood Canyon 2013 Estate Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Dancing Flame is a value-priced line from Luis Felipe Edwards, and this Leyda Sauvignon Blanc delivers punchy aromas that mix citrus notes with green ones. A fresh palate with lime and green-herb flavors loses concentration on the finish.
title: Dancing Flame 2017 Gran Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

description: Based in Odenas, at the foot of Mount Brouilly, Jean-François Pegaz has produced a bright wine. A tannic core gives the wine weight and potential richness, while contrasting acidity adds a crisp, fresh aftertaste. Drink the wine now.
title: Domaine Baron de l'Ecluse 2016  Côte de Brouilly

description: Organic grapes go into this smooth wine with its ripe fruits and soft tannins. Coming from the south of Beaujolais, the wine is soft and has an easy-going warmth. Its fruit and acidity mean it is ready to drink now.
title: Domaine Chasselay 2016 La Platière  (Beaujolais)

description: From vines that average 60 years in age, this concentrated wine brings together ripe, dense blueberry and spice with a dry core of tannins. The fruit is bright and up front, and the wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine de Colette 2016 Coteaux de Colette  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Grown on typical granite soil, this wine is full of red berries and bright cherries that are laced with minerality and crisp acidity. The wine has some concentration and could do with another few months, so drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de La Grume 2016 La Grume  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This soft wine is open, packed with red berry and cherry flavors and delicious to drink now. It has great fruitiness, light spice and pepper, and a round aftertaste just lifted with acidity.
title: Domaine de la Madone 2016 Le Perreon  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: While demure on the nose, ripe red-plum and berry notes seem to expand on the palate of this juicy, fruity wine. Opulent and silky in texture, it's nuanced by hints of vanilla and spice along with fine, sharply edged tannins that brace the finish.
title: Domaine de Verquiere 2016 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This wine has a ripe, creamy, buttery character that makes it smooth and rich. Acidity and crisp green fruits give this rounded wine an attractive lift. It is fruity and ready to drink now.
title: Domaine des Nugues 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: The Beaujolais Blanc style is well expressed here: rich, creamy and light on structure. That gives an early-drinking, fruity wine full of baked apple and pear flavors. It can be enjoyed now.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2016 Roche Noire  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: This opens with aromas of tilled soil, leather and red plum. The straightforward palate offers black cherry, clove and star anise alongside rounded tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Fattoria di Ruppiano 2015 Astorre Noti  (Chianti Classico)

description: This soft style of a wine offers plenty of attractive red-cherry flavors and crisp acidity. The texture is light and open, giving immediate pleasure in a wine that is all about fruit. Drink now.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée B  (Brouilly)

description: French oak, cassis, chopped herb aromas and a balsamic note emerge in the glass. It's taut and full-bodied palate but rather lean in terms of fruit richness, offering prune, licorice, dried sage and espresso while clenching, close-grained tannins leave an astringent finish.
title: Felsina 2013 Rancia Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Soft and varietal in nature, this wine offers dusty plum, leather and black currant and cherry, its structure simple and richly round. There's a hint of black licorice on the lengthy finish.
title: Ferrari-Carano 2015 Merlot (Sonoma County)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a solid reliable wine for the price, with aromas of chamomile, seared lemon peels and butter-poached nectarine. The palate is well rounded with lemon wedge and white peach flavors, presented with decent zip and structure.
title: Firefly Ridge 2016 Chardonnay (Central Coast)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Showing the green, earthy side of the variety, this Merlot opens on aromas of fresh-cut herbs and eucalyptus, with dark cherry, plum, vanilla and cedar providing a dense core. Soft in feel, balanced acidity and building tannins provide a solid structure for the red plum and cranberry spice flavors to run rampant.
title: Fjord 2014 Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: This hilly appellation next door to Moulin-à-Vent is noted for structured wines. With black fruits as well as layers of firm tannins, this wine fits the bill. The ripe, juicy character will give great pleasure from late 2018.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée C  (Chénas)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Clean and focused, crushed stone and citrus notes resound on the nose and palate. Light bodied and dry in style, fleshy peach and tangerine flavors round things out, with a taut vein of lime and dusted minerals maintaining balance.
title: Fox Run 2015 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: País, the first European grape planted in Chile in the 16th century, is on the upswing, and this is a light floral example that's showing the varietal meatiness and soapy sweet aromas the grape is known for. A chewy palate and flavors of red currant, plum and cinnamon finish mild but with grip.
title: Garage Wine Co. 2016 Second Salvo Ferment País (Cauquenes Valley)

description: Chiroubles, with its high-elevation vineyards, produces a light and fruity, almost cool and collected style of wine. This wine fits that bill, with crisp red fruits and light tannins giving an attractive, drinkable character. Enjoy now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine des Pontheux  (Chiroubles)

description: This wine is smooth, juicy and ripe. With broad red fruits and acidity, it has a light touch of tannins that keeps the wine's shape and texture. The wine is fruity, rich and ready to drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine du Riaz  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: Aromas of graphite, toasted hazelnut, oak and exotic spice emerge on this. The firm, linear palate offers sour cherry, orange peel and star anise while austere tannins and brisk acidity offer bracing support. It's a bit lean on fruit richness but the tannins need time to unwind. Drink after 2023.
title: Gomba Boschetti 2013 del Comune di Barolo  (Barolo)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Plush blackberry jam, violet and rich plummy aromas show on this blend of 40% Syrah, 35% Mourvèdre, 21% Counoise and 4% Grenache. It's dense and ever-so-slightly sweet on the sip, with blackberry and red-currant-jam flavors and plenty of candied lilacs.
title: Grey Wolf Cellars 2015 Soulmate Red (Paso Robles)

description: Smoky oak dominates on the nose of this burly wine, a study in black cherry, cola and sanguine undertones of forest, rose and meat. Moderate in acidity, it presents as fully fleshy and ripe, with a soft smooth texture.
title: Gundlach Bundschu 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: This dark-colored wine is made from Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petite Sirah, Cabernet Franc and Syrah, the whole fermented in stainless steel for five months. Tangy cranberry, cherry and orange highlight a medium build of power and moderate acidity.
title: Hoopla 2016 Rosé (Napa Valley)

description: Forward notes of lanolin, lemon oil and citrus peel lead the nose of this pure and direct Riesling. The palate offers a bit more weight of stone-fruit flavor, though in an incredibly fresh, clean and mouthwatering package. Drink now–2020.
title: Jardin 2015 The Real McCoy Riesling (Western Cape)

description: This wine is produced from vines that face the slopes of the hill of Brouilly. Ripe and full of juicy black fruits, it also has a crisp line of acidity. The soft texture is open, encouraging fruitiness and suggesting the wine is now ready to drink.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Château de Bellevue  (Brouilly)

description: Strong but well-applied oak is the lead aroma on a nose that includes scents of popcorn and resin but only modest fruit. A chunky chewy palate blends fruit pulp and resiny oak, while this tastes of oaky nectarine, quince and apricot. A finish with pulpy weight holds onto its barrel-driven essence.
title: Kingston Family 2016 Sabino Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

description: Earthy aromas of tilled soil, truffle, scorched earth and a touch of black-skinned berry lead the way on this blend of 90% Sangiovese, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Merlot. The dense palate offers dried black cherry, black pepper and a touch of mocha alongside firm tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: La Castellina 2013 Squarcialupi Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of mature plum, pressed violet, new leather and ground clove lift out of the glass. The ripe, dense palate shows fleshy wild cherry, blackberry jam, star anise and the heat of evident alcohol. Close-grained tannins offer support.
title: L'Antica Fornace di Ridolfo 2015 Baruffo  (Chianti Classico)

description: This 85% Sangiovese, 10% Merlot and 5% Canaiolo blend conjures up aromas of cedar and black plum. The straightforward palate delivers wild cherry preserves, mocha and vanilla alongside smooth tannins.
title: Le Regge 2014  Chianti Classico

description: This opens with aromas of leather, black-skinned berry and tilled soil. The savory palate offers black cherry, baking spice and white pepper alongside smooth tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Lecci e Brocchi 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Whole-cluster pressed and cofermented with 3% Viognier, this wine is brawny in leather, tobacco and white pepper. Moderately ripe, it offers a slight richness that remains balanced, with a floral hint of lavender on the nose.
title: Lois Rae 2014 Las Madres Vineyard Syrah (Carneros)

description: This 100% Sangiovese offers aromas that recall black plum, tilled soil and tobacco. The dense palate evokes black cherry jam, mocha and licorice alongside tightly drawn tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Machiavelli 2013 Vigna di Fontalle Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Bright aromas of raspberry, macerated cranberry, nougat, vanilla and pencil shavings lead to tart puckering fruit flavors that show intensity and length. There is much to enjoy.
title: Maryhill 2014 Proprietor's Reserve Gunkel Vineyards Barbera (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Waxy apple skins and sun-kissed yellow cherries perfume this dry white wine. Ripe and richly textured, it offers delicate apple and lemon flavors that ripple and roll on the palate. Drink now through 2020.
title: Mas Carlot 2016 White (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Chardonnay may not be the first of the Oyster Bay wines you reach for, but this wine delivers a solid example of what Marlborough can do with the variety. This is on the richer side, with notes of butter, ripe melon, green beans and sea spray, all tucked into a creamy, full-bodied palate that lets its oak influence be known without compromising the fruit.
title: Oyster Bay 2016 Chardonnay (Marlborough)

description: The domination of Merlot in this blend keeps red fruits at the fore, with notes of red currants and raspberries along with licorice, chocolate, and florals. On the palate, savory oak blends nicely with red and black fruits. Still youthful despite its age.
title: Pask 2013 Gimblett Gravels Red (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Blackberry jam, milk chocolate shavings and an asphalt minerality show on the rich and inviting nose of this bottling. The mouthfeel is soft and elegant, with noticeable tannins serving as the backbone, and flavors of bright blackberry and mocha ride a deep lasting acidity.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Pear Valley Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: There's a gamy, earthy thread to this wine, along with notes of tobacco and crushed violets that weave throughout the boysenberry fruit. The palate doles out more black berry flavor, while fine tannins and ample acidity frame the finish. Enjoy through 2018.
title: Piekenierskloof 2015 Grenache (Piekenierskloof)

description: Intensely forested, this wine's fruit is compressed and tightly packed on the palate, with the texture somewhat gravelly in nature. Spicy nutmeg rides alongside cranberry, raspberry, plum and orange flavors, all wrapped in a medium-bodied frame.
title: Portalupi 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Black-fruit aromas are accompanied by rooty notes of cool earth. In the mouth, this is wiry and acidic in a tartaric way. Black-fruit flavors are suggestive of prune and raisin, while this is full but choppy on the finish. This is 61% Merlot, 35% Cabernet Sauvignon and 4% Cab Franc.
title: Rafael Tirado Wines 2011 Laberinto Merlot-Cabernet Sauvignon (Maule Valley)

description: A fruity blend of Sangiovese, 10% Colorino and 10% Canaiolo, this has aromas of red plum, black cherry and a hint of cooking spice. The aromas carry on to the straightforward, easy drinking palate alongside round tannins. Enjoy soon.
title: Coli 2016  Chianti Classico

Designation: Serie Riberas Gran Reserva Ribera del Cachapoal
