description: Minty berry aromas are dark and a touch horsey. In the mouth, this is crunchy and full in body. Herbal blackberry, pepper and chocolate flavors finish with spicy oak and unabsorbed acidity.
title: Concha y Toro 2016 Serie Riberas Gran Reserva Ribera del Cachapoal Carmenère (Peumo)

description: Attractive in floral and citrus notes, the oak is firmly in a supporting role on the nose, yet on the palate it takes the lead. Texturally, this wine is slippery and medium-weighted and slightly clunky in feel.
title: Craggy Range 2015 Kidnappers Vineyard Chardonnay (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Ripe yellow apple and shades of delicate florals dot the nose, leading to a mix of juicy tree fruits on the palate. A mild vein of acidity keeps this feeling fresh, making it a delicious, straightforward wine.
title: Damiani 2016 Davis Vineyard Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: A rich wine with tannins firmly in place, this is structured, with a dry core that contrasts with the juicy black fruits. A wine to age, it should not be drunk before late 2018.
title: Domaine Céline & Nicolas Hirsch 2016  Chénas

description: This wine is all about crisp red-cherry and berry fruits that burst from the glass. It is juicy, perfumed and bright. The dry texture underlines the fruitiness. Drink now.
title: Domaine des Gaudets 2016 Sornay  (Régnié)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: Slightly vegetal notes on opening eventually fade with aeration to reveal prettier tones of blackberry and blueberry. This is a plush, rounded wine with muted black fruit but supple and satisfying on the palate. The finish is moderately long with lingering chalky tannins.
title: Domaine des Pasquiers 2015 Plan de Dieu Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas bring a touch of oxidation that distracts from the notes of cherry, cocoa, vanilla and spice. There are a lot of good things happening on the palate but they can't quite make up the difference.
title: Gamache 2013 Estate Bottled Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas seem unsettled, with notes of vanilla, baking spice and dark fruit that don't seem entirely in harmony. The palate is chock-full of raspberry and barrel-spice flavors, with loads of coconut on the finish. The aromas and flavors seem out of sync.
title: Gamache 2013 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Lots of fresh dark-fruit flavor and a silky texture make this wine easy to sip and enjoy. Touches of spiciness and milk chocolate give it a sweet edge, and the tannins are soft on the palate.
title: Gen5 2014 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: The producer's entry-level Morgon shows many of the appellation's characteristics. Tannins and ripe fruit are all there as well as the balance with acidity and a solid texture. This fruity wine is ready to drink.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Morgon)

description: Strawberry-jam aromas and ripe fruits give this wine a soft, smooth character. It has some tannins although these are incidental to the fruity thrust of the wine. Warm and ripe, it is ready to drink.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Saint-Amour)

description: On the lean and mean side, this wine shows green characters of broccoli, green beans and peas are followed by citrus, passionfruit, ginger and wet stone notes. The palate is searing in acidity but the fruit is well integrated, and the wet stone and white pepper finish is appealing.
title: Grove Mill 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Wairau Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: High-toned aromas of dark cherry and bramble berry lead to a palate of cedar, spice and dark plum. After 13 months in French and Hungarian oak, this wine is weighty and muscular.
title: Hazlitt 1852 Vineyards 2013 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: A wine that goes all out in the juicy, crisp stakes, this is bright with red-currant fruit and plenty of acidity. Old vines in this case means 50 years and there is some concentration as well as good fruitiness. Drink the wine now.
title: Henry Fessy 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Crisp and fruity, this wine is firm, with young tannins and juicy red-cherry flavors. Bright and packed with youthful fruitiness, it needs to be left for a few months. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Les Thibaults  (Brouilly)

description: Subtle spice and earth notes accent core plum, currant and cocoa aromas. This is a full oaky style of Pinot with toasty plum and berry flavors that don't qualify as elegant. Mild heat and oak are players on a chocolaty finish.
title: Luis Felipe Edwards 2015 Marea Pinot Noir (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 80% Sangiovese and 20% Petit Verdot shows punchy aromas of strawberry, hibiscus and light licorice on the nose. The palate combines the red fruit with a pinch of mace and touch of savory herbs, with rising acidity toward the finish.
title: LXV 2015 Crimson Jewel Hearthstone Vineyard Red (Paso Robles)

description: Aromas of toasted oak, raspberry jam and tilled soil lead the nose. The straightforward palate offers dried black cherry, licorice and mocha accompanied by assertive, close-grained tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Machiavelli 2015 Solatìo del Tani  (Chianti Classico)

description: Punchy, reedy aromas of currant come with hints of leather and latex. This is a wiry wine with acidity as its main driver. Salty currant and plum flavors come with some buttery oak, while high residual acidity on the finish makes this shear on the back end.
title: Manos Andinas 2015 Reserva Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Apple and lemon aromas are in the fore of this wine, backed by vanilla oak tones. The palate finds a good balance of acidity and weight, but it's lacking the same appealing fruit offered on the nose. The oak sneaks in to dominate the finish.
title: Marisco Vineyards 2015 The King's Bastard Chardonnay (Wairau Valley)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is 100% varietal and was aged for 20 months in a mixture of American and European oak (40% new). The aromas are bright, with notes of assorted barrel spice, pickling spice, raspberry and dried herb. The palate is medium bodied with a bit of a hole in the middle. Bitters linger on the finish.
title: Maryhill 2014 Vineyard Series McKinley Springs Vineyard Mourvèdre (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: Aromas of French oak, exotic spice, menthol and steeped prune come together. The monolithic palate is evolved, offering vanilla, espresso, dried black cherry and oak driven spice alongside seasoned, not very persistent tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Mauro Sebaste 2011 Ghè Riserva  (Barolo)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: This estate-grown wine shows lively notes of lemon-lime spray on the nose, with undertones of green apple and slate. Light bodied and medium-dry in style, riper notions of candied lemon peel and peach show nice intensity on the palate and linger on the medium-long finish.
title: McGregor 2015 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This is a well-behaved, nicely balanced wine with good cherry and sage flavors, an appetizing texture livened by light tannins and a touch of acidity. It should be versatile with food and easy to pour for a crowd.
title: Michael Sullberg 2015 Lot 91 Reserve Merlot (California)

description: A blend of 95% Sangiovese and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, this offers aromas of bell pepper, black-skinned berry and baking spice. The straightforward palate shows red-currant jam, clove and licorice alongside smooth tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Orsumella 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Full bodied and intensely ripe, this wine is expansive and juicy, with pronounced oak across the palate and fully sketched flavors of baked pear, apple and peach. The structure is moderate and the finish sweet in vanilla.
title: Outlot 2015 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: This wine boasts an attractive combo of honeysuckle, green apple skins and white flowers notes while the palate offers balanced acidity with a citrusy, floral finish.
title: Tablelands 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Martinborough)

description: This wine from one of the estates vinified by this cooperative comes from 50-year-old vines. Made from organic grapes, it's juicy, light and perfumed. Drink the wine now.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2016 Domaine des Schistes  (Morgon)

description: This unctuous, ripe wine has a smooth and creamy texture. Its fruit is open, soft and lacks some definition and crispness. However the wine has weight and a juicy apple and pear aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Vignoble Charmet 2016 Goyette D'Or  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: Aromas of toasted oak, dark spice, grilled herb and coconut lift out of the glass. On the firm palate, assertive, close-grained tannins offset black cherry extract and a hint of star anise. Drink 2020–2027.
title: Bersano 2012 Badarina  (Barolo)

description: Taut and firmly structured, this offers aromas of French oak, espresso, Asian spice and a whiff of moist soil. The firmly structured palate offers raspberry, wild cherry, dried sage and licorice framed in tightly drawn, fine-grained tannins. Vibrant acidity provides balance. It's youthfully assertive so give it a few more years to unwind.
title: Bibbiano 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Despite having spent more than five years in bottle, this Pinot is maintaining a lightly spiced nose with freshness along with herbal plum and currant aromas. A fleshy, chewy palate is mature but still lively. Peppery plum and herbal flavors reflect a cool year, while woody oak notes and medicinal cherry and plum flavors wrap this up.
title: Casa Marin 2011 Lo Abarca Hills Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Antonio)

description: Blackberry and cherry flavors are pristine, almost crunchy, in this straightforward but concentrated Syrah. While brawny and ripe, it's balanced squarely by fresh acidity and savory edges of black pepper and bramble. The finish is tannic and firm.
title: Château Val Joanis 2015 Tradition Syrah (Luberon)

description: This four-acre walled vineyard is owned by the Lebeaupin family. The structure and density of the wine is the product of the old vines, bringing in tannins as well as ripe fruits. Age will soften the tannins. Drink from 2019.
title: Clos de la Brosse 2016  Saint-Amour

description: This structured wine offers dark fruits and a dry core of tannins. It has weight and the potential of rich fruitiness. The concentration from old vines adds to the aging potential. Drink this wine from 2019.
title: Domaine Matray 2016 Cuvée Vieilles Vignes  (Juliénas)

description: New leather, forest floor, woodland berry and a whiff of violet are all classic Sangiovese aromas. It's ripe but bright, offering mature wild cherry, white pepper and a hint of clove alongside compact tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Eredi Enzo Raspi 2015 Bandini Villa Pomona  (Chianti Classico)

description: Earthy aromas of truffle, underbrush, woodland berry, blue flower and a whiff of game lead the nose. The linear, austere palate is on the lean side, offering tart red cherry, star anise and clove framed in taut, close-grained tannins and racy acidity. Give it time to fully unfold and come together. Drink after 2023.
title: Francesco Rinaldi 2013  Barolo

description: This 90% Sangiovese, 5% Canaiolo and 5% Colorino blend has aromas of black-skinned berry, baking spice and leather. The juicy palate offers ripe black cherry, clove and licorice alongside firm, polished tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Geografico 2013 Contessa di Radda Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Clove, allspice and black cherry juice show on the nose of this bottling from the heart of Temecula. Tangy wild berry fruit pops on the sip, where a chalky texture carries vanilla cookie, wild fennel and, toward the finish, and mocha flavors.
title: Gershon Bachus 2013 Auster Syrah (California)

description: Delicious ripe apple and pear aromas meet equally vivid and enjoyable fruit flavors in this medium-bodied, soft and easy-drinking wine. Just a touch of lemony acidity brings a little lift on the palate and keeps the finish nicely fresh.
title: Imagery 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: This is a massive wine, compressed in black fruit, tobacco and cigar box. It has a big-boned nature to its structure and a tight embrace of oak and tannin. It offers plenty of heat around the baked blackberry and currant character.
title: JCB 2014 No. 10 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, underbrush and cedar lead the nose. The firm palate delivers black-currant jam, clove and tobacco alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: La Sala 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A dark color and concentrated aromas of black cherry, cassis, licorice and even some charcoal are good indicators of what's coming—namely a dense palate with tartaric bite and ripe plum and blackberry flavors. A full finish with scratchy tannins tastes of chocolate and oak.
title: Lapostolle 2015 Grand Selection Merlot (Rapel Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Cooked red cherries, sharp cranberries and butter pastries meet with a touch of campfire on the nose of this bottling. There's a tannic firmness to the palate, where sticky tannins carry flavors of tart red fruit and baking spice.
title: Lloyd 2014 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Blueberry and black-cherry flavors are unctuously ripe on this concentrated red, balanced neatly by a fiery dose of black pepper and a cool mineral undertone. Approachable and forward, it finishes with bold, slightly tacky tannins.
title: Marrenon 2015 Grande Toque Red (Luberon)

description: Hints of coffee beans, smoke and caramel entice on the nose of this opulent, fleshy red. Ripe raspberry and preserved-strawberry flavors are lip-smackingly forward yet nuanced by hints of animal and earth. Fine-grained tannins penetrate through a moderately long finish.
title: Notre Dame de Cousignac 2014 Red (Vacqueyras)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very delicate on the nose, this bottling from Blair Pence offers light lemon, fresh nectarine and wet cement aromas. It's very tight and edgy on the sip, with lime flavors and a pinch of baking spice, proving crisp and refreshing.
title: Pence Ranch 2015 Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese, 5% Canaiolo and 5% Merlot, this has aromas of black-skinned berry, leather and a whiff of cedar. The savory palate delivers black cherry, sage and a hint of star anise alongside lithe tannins. Enjoy through 2022.
title: Poggio al Sole 2014  Chianti Classico

description: This high level of good is about where Primus falls year after year. This vintage shows blackberry and spice aromas in front of a tannic, pulling palate. Dark black-fruit flavors are nonspecific, while this has tannic rub along with berry and oak flavors on the finish.
title: Primus 2015 Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Cabernet Sauvignon makes up half of this blend, with the balance Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot and Merlot. The aromas bring wound-up notes of red apple, cherry, herb, graphite, flower and barrel spice. The flavors are smooth and silky in feel, showing a pleasing depth.
title: Tamarack Cellars 2013 Ciel du Cheval Vineyard Reserve Red (Red Mountain)

description: Made with organic Sangiovese, Canaiolo and Merlot grapes, this opens with aromas of red plum, baking spice and a touch of violet. The firm palate delivers tobacco, clove and black cherry alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Terre di Melazzano 2015 ChiAndré  (Chianti Classico)

description: Ripe berry aromas include notes of cassis and raisin. Rarities is a good name for this, because there are few (if any) Grenache-Carignan-Monastrell blends being made in Chile besides this. Old vines and a cool year have combined to create an interesting wine with a medium body but bold acidity. Two years in oak has tamed some of the raciness, leaving sunbaked flavors offset by wiry acidity.
title: Undurraga 2013 T.H. Terroir Hunter Rarities Garnacha-Cariñena-Monastrell Red (Cauquenes Valley)

description: Good balance, medium body and a nice blend of fruit and spice flavors make this wine taste very well put together. It has crisp apple and lemon aromas, deceptively concentrated nutmeg, fig and pear flavors that slowly unfold on the palate, and a good lingering finish.
title: Victor Vineyards 2016 Chardonnay (Lodi)

description: This opens with truffle, black-skinned berry and leather aromas. Made with organically cultivated Sangiovese, the juicy, medium-bodied palate offers dried black cherry, cinnamon and black pepper while polished tannins give it balance and support. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Villa Calcinaia 2014 Vigna Bastignano  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a classic California Chardonnay backbone to this bottling, with aromas of toasted wood, crème brûlée, poached pear and apple custard, yet there are also more delicate hints of chalk and orange blossom as well. The palate combines ripe Pink Lady apples and vanilla spice and caramel richness.
title: Wente 2016 Riva Ranch Chardonnay (Arroyo Seco)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Eucalyptus, Bay leaf and mint figure in prominently to the nose of this wine, an herbaceous expression of the variety that's sinewy in thick blackberry, almost to a syrupy level. From head-trained older vines, it retains that mix of fruit and earth to the powerful end.
title: Wine Guerrilla 2015 Paradise Lane Vineyard Old Vine Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

description: Fleshy and ripe red cherry fruit notes stream from the glass. The palate is equally fruit-driven in an abundance of juicy, almost overripe fruit. There is ample freshness to serve as a counterbalance. The finish has balance and crunchy cherry verve.
title: Winzer Krems 2015 Edition Chremisa Sandgrube 13 Zweigelt (Niederösterreich)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Reserve aromas of black cherry, plum, clove and tarragon-crusted roast pork show on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines pomegranate and rhubarb with subtle star anise spice, proving altogether delicate and easy to drink.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2015 Zoto Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: You'll find aromas of violet, leather and a whiff of game on this approachable red. The sleek palate delivers raspberry, clove and white pepper alongside lively tannins and fresh acidity. Enjoy though 2022.
title: Aiola 2014  Chianti Classico

description: This ripe and richly fruity wine from organically grown grapes is also textured with tannins and cut with acidity. These elements make for a generous wine, full of fruit and with a medium-term future. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Chasselay 2016 L'Héronde  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: Perfumed red fruits and firm tannins give this wine both its immediate attraction and its aging potential. It is dense as well as rich with juicy blackberry fruit freshened with acidity. Drink the wine from 2019.
title: Domaine de la Plaigne 2016  Morgon

description: This wine's shy nose gives little away. The palate comes in with zesty lemon freshness and just a hint of peppery watercress. A light-bodied, refreshing and easy-drinking wine.
title: Dürnberg 2016 Tradition Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: Aromas suggesting black cherry, game and underbrush take the lead while the juicy palate offers raspberry jam, clove and star anise. Fine-grained tannins provide support. Drink through 2023.
title: Fattoria di Lamole 2013 Le Stinche  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: From a selection of clones and vineyard sites, this affordable varietal wine offers forest pine, cardamom and pomegranate, the fruit elusive within its more savory nature.
title: Friedeman 2015 Dichotomy Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This estate, run by the same family for five generations, has produces a structured wine. Its firm tannins and generous black-plum fruits suggest aging potential. Full bodied and rich, the wine should be drunk from 2019.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine du Pourpre  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Made from Bacigalupi Vineyard fruit, which is located on Westside Road, this wine offers a generous bouquet and outlay of dark cherry, baked vanilla, toasted oak and forest notes, its heart savory and tart in additional characteristics of clove, cranberry and pomegranate.
title: Gracianna 2015 Westside Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: San Diego County, South Coast, California, US
description: Quite light in the glass, this Loire-like bottling shows great promise from San Diego County, with very fresh aromas of cranberry, white pepper and green herbs on the nose. Tart yet candied pomegranate fruit shows on the sip, where that peppery character continues, blending crushed thyme and peppercorn with a touch of caramelized herbs on the finish.
title: Hungry Hawk 2015 Cabernet Franc (San Diego County)

description: Cherry aromas dominate the nose on this medium-bodied Pinot with medicinal leanings. A bright youthful palate is more fresh and easy than complex, while this tastes of lightly spiced raspberry, plum and cherry. A juicy appealing finish includes touches of oak and cocoa.
title: Kingston Family 2016 Tobiano Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Here's a 100% Sangiovese that opens with aromas of woodland berry, underbrush and a hint of balsamic herb. The juicy palate delivers ripe black cherry, licorice and black pepper alongside smooth tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Lornano 2013  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of baking spice, vanilla, coconut and cherry are followed by well-proportioned fruit and barrel flavors. Cocoa notes linger on the finish. It hits a lot of high marks, though the variety seems a bit hard to find.
title: Nodland 2014 Malbec (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Whispers of caramel, vanilla and salt entice from nose to finish in this slightly savory, full-bodied white. Apple and pear flavors and soft, lemony acidity are delicate in concentration but it's a luscious, richly textured wine that lingers on the finish.
title: Notre Dame de Cousignac 2014 White (Luberon)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas pop, with appealing notes of marmalade, sugared pineapple and honey. It's sweet and luscious, with abundant apricot and tropical flavors that, despite their sugar, still maintain a sense of elegance and balance.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 Vin de Glacieire Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Soft and elegant on the nose, this bottling presents rounded violets, plump blackberries, broken asphalt and dried dill on the nose. The palate is plush with blueberry and lilac flavors that ride chiseled tannins and benefit from peppercorn and marjoram spices. It is complex and flavorful.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Pear Valley Vineyard Merlot (Paso Robles)

description: Fleshy black-cherry and plum flavors verge on the overripe in this supple, rounded wine. Ripe tannins, caramel and spice accentuate the soft, easy drinking appeal.
title: Pierre Amadieu 2014 Domaine Grand Romane  (Gigondas)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of herb, vanilla, pencil lead, barrel spice and coffee are followed by sleek but well-proportioned fruit flavors. It shows a lovely sense of balance, though the barrel influence plays a large part in the show.
title: Plumb Cellars 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, cedar and tilled earth lead the nose while the chewy palate offers black currant, mocha and baking spice. Assertive but fine-grained tannins lend support. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Savignola Paolina 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: The most plush of Silver Thread's single-vineyard lineup this vintage, this wine shows a wealth of ripe stone fruit, blended with milky, white flowers and crushed stone on the nose. A roundness on the off-dry palate is carried by fleshy yellow apple and tart apricot, balanced by a vein of lemon-lime acidity. A powdered mineral texture lingers through the finish.
title: Silver Thread 2016 Doyle Fournier Vineyard Riesling (Seneca Lake)

description: This is slightly shy and reserved off the bat, but expresses itself more with some time in the glass, yielding scents of melon rind, underripe peach and lightly spiced citrus rind. There's a gorgeous texture to the palate, with a subtle pithiness and grassy astringency that frames the stone-fruit flavors. Drink now–2021.
title: Spioenkop 2015 Riesling (Elgin)

description: A wave red and black cherry comes across as rather fleshy on the nose. The palate is fresh and juicy with all of that fleshy, ripe fruit flooding across the palate. The customary Austrian freshness provides a welcome counterbalance while the tannins are soft and pliable. Drink soon.
title: Steindorfer 2015 Kaiserberg Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: Aromas of scorched earth, toast, espresso and balsamic notes fill the glass. The full-bodied palate offers dried black cherry, raspberry jam, cinnamon and clove framed in firm, close-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Tenuta San Mauro 2013 Canova  (Barbaresco)

description: This wine starts out seemingly semisweet, with a touch of honey, florals and ripe lemon. The palate, however, is dry, with medium weight, zippy acidity, a creamy, slippery texture and a citrusy finish. Sip with roast chicken.
title: The Seeker 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 38% Roussanne, 31% Marsanne and 31% Viognier is a Rhône-style white for Chardonnay lovers, starting with an intriguingly buttery and toast-filled nose that's cut with aromas of squeezed lemon. The palate is bolstered by a citrusy acidity and decent textural grip, finishing on melted butter.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2015 Blend 33 White (Central Coast)

description: This supple red opens with aromas of iris, red-skinned berry and underbrush. The juicy palate delivers red berry, white pepper and a hint of star anise cushioned between rounded tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Valiano 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This is a full wine, juicy with attractive blackberry and rich cherry fruit. Its acidity provides a refreshing contrast to the richness. Drink the wine now.
title: Vignoble Charmet 2016 Moulin la Blanche  (Beaujolais)

description: A tough gritty nose is not the most welcoming, while this holds onto that hardness on the palate, which is raw in feel and stylistically more drawn back than most high-end Chilean Cabernets. This tastes savory, a touch leafy and short on ripe fruit. Overall, it's a dry Maipo red with oak character and toughness. Drink through 2028.
title: Viña Aquitania 2014 Lazuli Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: This Syrah and Merlot blend goes heavy on the Syrah and the dark color. The nose is intense, spicy and concentrated, with creamy mint notes stemming from fruit and oak. A heavy palate is a clunky, while strong flavors of mint, grassy berry and sappy black plum are roasted and slightly herbaceous on the finish. All in all, Neblus is a rare breed of wine.
title: Viña Casablanca 2014 Neblus Red (Casablanca Valley)

description: A deep nose with ample oak is peppery and lightly herbal, with scents of clove and briary black fruits. The palate on this Syrah is dry, reserved and tannic, but fresh and healthy. Herbal spicy plum, berry and tomato flavors are drawing on the finish.
title: Arboleda 2015 Syrah (Aconcagua Valley)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: From equal parts Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, as well as 32% Malbec, this is a slightly reduced expression of earthy black tea, black currant, toasted oak and sizable tannin. Full bodied, it shows the concentration and density of the vintage.
title: Arbor Bench Vineyards 2013 Estate Meritage (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, vanilla and underbrush waft out of the glass. Made entirely with Sangiovese, the concentrated palate doles out ripe black plum, roasted coffee bean and licorice alongside closed-grained tannins and the warmth of evident alcohol. Drink through 2023.
title: Bibbiano 2013 Vigna del Capannino  (Chianti Classico)

description: From one of the single estates vinified by the Bel Air cooperative, this wine has intense red-currant fruit and acidity. It offers a light texture with just enough tannins to give weight and concentration. Drink this wine now.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2016 Domaine des Poutoux  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Pruney raisin and plum aromas and flavors are met by fruit-forward notions of blackberry jam and cherry, providing a generosity of ripe fruit. Spicy cinnamon and dusty tannin accent the full-bodied concentration and simple finish.
title: Wine Guerrilla 2015 Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of sweet herb, cherry and barrel spice are followed by pitch-black-fruit flavors that work around the edges, with a tart citrus filled finish that falls a bit short. The tannins bring a bit of grit.
title: Airfield Estates 2015 Malbec (Yakima Valley)

Appellation: Santa Margarita Ranch, Central Coast, California, US
description: Kirsch, caramel, maraschino cherries and hickory smoke make for a pleasing if somewhat sweet nose. Alpine strawberry and stewed raspberry show on the palate, which is fruity and flavorful if somewhat simple. Caramel pops on the finish.
title: Ancient Peaks 2015 Zinfandel (Santa Margarita Ranch)

description: Light raspberry and cherry aromas are simple and good. This medium-bodied Cabernet is high in acidity and zesty, as if it were harvested before rains hit the 2016 vintage. Flavors of plum, raspberry and tomato finish well.
title: Aresti 2016 Bellavista Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Curicó Valley)

description: Hints of carnation and cocoa powder lead the bouquet of this value-minded Cabernet, supported by fruity tones of red cherry and forest berries. A touch jammy and smooth on the palate, it's balanced by ample tannins and enough acidity to keep the mouthfeel fresh and the finish clean. Drink now.
title: Balance 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Western Cape)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: A pleasant intensity of bramble-berry and red-currant aromas blend with a crushed black pepper tone on the nose. The palate is light and lithe, focused on tart red fruit, flecked with peppery tones. Mild, sandy tannins keep the feel smooth and linger on the cranberry-skin finish.
title: Bedell 2014 Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: The style of this wine is rich, giving structure and ripe tannins as much as red fruits. It is juicy, full of red-berry flavors and has good acidity to keep it all in check. Drink now.
title: Bouchard Aîné et Fils 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: This wine is generous in flavor, a touch soft in texture and has a bit of sweetness to round off any rough edges. It smells jammy and tastes like blackberries drizzled with maple syrup, while any tannins are softened by the ripeness and body.
title: Caricature 2014 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: Aromas suggesting scorched earth, woodshop and grilled herb carry over to the evolved palate along with roasted coffee bean, coconut and a hint of prune. Close-grained tannins provide support. There's not much fruit left so drink sooner rather than later.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2012 Clemente VII Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Delicate scents suggesting dark berry, moist soil and a whiff ground clove waft out of the glass. The fresh, light-bodied palate offers sour cherry and star anise alongside bright acidity and rather loose knit tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: Castello d'Albola 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Aromas of French oak, chopped herb and a whiff of pressed blue flower take shape on this. Reflecting the cool, wet vintage, the fresh, light-bodied palate shows hints of sour cherry and clove alongside loose, not very persistent tannins. Drink soon.
title: Castello della Paneretta 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Aromas of underbrush, grilled porcini, toasted oak and a whiff of cassis lead the nose. The tight, rather lean palate offers star anise, roasted nut, powdered sage, espresso and just a hint of dried berry alongside grainy tannins that leave an astringent finish. Give the tannins a few years to unwind then drink to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Castello di Monsanto 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A ripe, fruity, red-cherry flavored wine, this is attractive already. It has a rich, smoky texture as well as layers of fruit and acidity. With the edge of tannin in the back, the wine could age further, although there is no real need. It's ready to drink now.
title: Château de Lavernette 2016 Le Clos  (Beaujolais-Leynes)

description: While this is good, there are many disparate parts to this Pinot from southerly Malleco. The nose is strained, raw and a bit horsey, while this feels grabby and full on the palate, even creamy. Heavy oak and saucy plum and berry flavors turn chocolaty on the finish. At 15.5%, this is riper than most Chilean PN's.
title: Clos des Fous 2015 Latuffa Pinot Noir (Malleco)

description: Gritty, earthy aromas of plum and cherry lead to a chunky oaky palate that's slightly abrasive and warm. A heavy oak character matches the wine's creamy plum and berry flavors, while a short oaky finish is limited. At 15.1% abv, this is not about finesse.
title: Clos des Fous 2015 Subsollum Pinot Noir (Chile)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Focused aromas of stone fruit, pressed apples and florals carry to a pithy, medium-bodied, off-dry palate, bolstered by ripe peach and tart apricot flavors. A mild vein of acidity keeps the weight in check and helps extend the blossomy, floral finish. For every bottle sold, 20% of the proceeds goes to organizations dedicated to pediatric cardiology.
title: Damiani 2015 Rhysling Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This opens with aromas of underbrush, cured meat and a whiff of violet. The assertive palate offers dried black cherry, tobacco, and star anise alongside firm tannins that leave a rather astringent finish. Drink through 2022.
title: Dievole 2015 Novecento Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Hints of cinnamon and anise lend spice to soft, slightly muted tones of blackberry and cherry here. Full bodied and rich, this is a densely textured wine with yielding acidity and ripe sinewy tannins. Drink now through 2020.
title: Domaine Coudoulis 2015 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This ripe, fruity wine with its red-cherry flavor is open and soft. A crisp, succulent edge adds to the attractiveness, giving a bright, fruity character. Drink now.
title: Domaine de Bel-Air 2016 Les Granits Bleus  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This wine is all bright red berries and crisp acidity. It is a light, tangy and juicy wine from 40-year-old vines on a five-acre parcel. Drink now.
title: Domaine Gaget 2016 Les Galets  (Beaujolais)

description: Ripe cherry fruit and a dense texture give this wine its concentration and spiciness. It is a full wine, rich with red fruits and acidity, and ready to drink now.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2015 Les Extrêmes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This wine is crisp and bright with red-berry flavors and intense acidity. Produced from 40-year-old vines, it has some concentration as well as a tight, mineral aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Domaine Marc Delienne 2016 Avalanche de Printemps  (Fleurie)

description: This is red-berry driven, packed with dense fruits and layered with concentrated tannins. It seems a bit overdone and takes away from the fruitiness of the Gamay. It's definitely for those who like big, powerful wines.
title: Domaine Raphaël Chopin 2016 La Savoye  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Aromas of mocha, coconut and red plum lead the nose. The dense palate delivers raisin, vanilla and toasted oak alongside close-grained tannins that leave an astringent finish.
title: Fattoria Le Fonti 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine is soft, ripe and fruity. With a smooth texture only lightly ruffled by tannins, it is full of red cherries and plums with bright acidity. It is ready to drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Clos des Quatre Vents  (Fleurie)

description: This succulent wine is full of red fruits and dusty tannins. The acidity and fruitiness are balanced by the structure that will allow this wine to mature further. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Fleurie)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Clean notes of peach, citrus and quince on the nose carry to the medium-bodied palate. Fermented in French oak, this shows a pleasant roundness, with a delicate balance of acidity to maintain freshness.
title: Heron Hill 2015 Reserve Macri Vineyard Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

description: Mature black-skinned fruit, dark spice and used leather aromas lift out of the glass. The evolved, straightforward palate shows prune and a hint of clove framed in assertive, dusty tannins that leave a firm finish. Despite the tight tannins drink sooner rather than later to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Il Molino di Grace 2015  Chianti Classico

description: A crisp, attractively perfumed wine, this is packed with red-berry flavors and has a light tannic texture. It has a rich side although the attractive fresh acidity brings this wine to life. Drink now.
title: Jean-Marc Burgaud 2016 Les Vignes de Thulon  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This wine leads with rich and mature notes of toasted apple skin, honeycomb cereal, creamed melon and grilled peach. It's a touch round and soft upon entry, but the palate received a welcome lift from ample acidity and an ensuring sweet-spice finish, reminiscent of spiced orange rind and baked apple. Drink now–2020.
title: Kasteelberg 2015 Chardonnay (Swartland)

description: This hearty red is made from nearly equal parts Cabernet Sauvignon, Malbec and Grenache, with smaller amounts of Petite Sirah and Merlot. Juicy and structured in black and red cherry and currant, it offers sizable tannin weight and a grip of toasted oak, remaining succulent and bright in the glass.
title: Kokomo 2015 Cuvée Red (Sonoma County)

description: Fleshy and full on the palate, this plump red wine offers heaps of juicy, easy black-cherry and berry flavors peppered with spice and mocha. It's a rich, mouthfilling sip but tightens up firmly, with a bold, tannic finish.
title: Laurence Féraud 2013  Gigondas

description: Whiffs of black-cherry preserves dusted in sweet spice introduce this unctuously fruity red. It's a straightforward, juicy wine but boldly concentrated with flavors of sun-kissed plums and berries that linger through a long, softly tannic finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Lavau 2015  Gigondas

description: Dark black color gives way to brooding ripeness and density of black fruit, with a huge presence of oak and tannin. Aspects of leather, black pepper and tomato leaf figure into the mix, saved by a brightness of acidity that helps complement the wine's concentrated tendencies.
title: Madrigal 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Howell Mountain)

description: Punchy blackberry and cherry flavors abound in this straightforward yet delightfully juicy wine. Ripe, yielding tannins and sunny acidity accentuate its approachable, appealing style but elegant touches of mint and spice lend flair to the finish.
title: Maison Denuzière 2015 Domaine des Hunaudieres Red (Luberon)

description: Heady tones of violet, lavender and smoke persist from nose to finish in this opulent, elegant wine. The palate is concentrated and juicy, offering loads of succulent blackberry and plum flavors peppered with spice and bramble. The finish is long and persistent, marked by chalky but firm tannins.
title: Marrenon 2015 Grand Marrenon Red (Luberon)

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: Intensely oaky and robustly built, this young white mellows in the glass to reveal a voluptuous citrus core. It offers vibrancy on the finish that plays well against bold flavors of pear and melon.
title: Materra Cunat Family Vineyards 2016 Chardonnay (Oak Knoll District)

description: This is a big full-bodied wine that packs a bounty of ripe bold fruit flavors into a firm frame of tannins, alcohol and moderate acidity. It has a big impact on the palate with the concentration to back it up. Big proteins will pair nicely with it.
title: Mt. Konocti Winery 2014 Tempranillo (Lake County)

description: Spice, cherries and some tannins are the order of the day in this wine. Tannins are present although not dominant, giving a wine that is more to do with rich berry fruits and black cherries. Still developing, the wine will be better from late 2018.
title: Pardon et Fils 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: Earthy aromas and a smooth texture drive this unorthodox but attention-getting wine, which is the entry-level wine from Phillips Hill and named for the local dialect in Anderson Valley. It has funky reduced aromas at first, then meaty stemmy spicy flavors and a mouth-filling texture that's very smooth.
title: Phillips Hill 2016 Boontling Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: Aromas of exotic spice, underbrush, chopped herb and moist earth lead the nose. The full-bodied palate offers raw sour cherry, star anise, espresso and oak-driven spice alongside assertive, fine-grained tannins and bright acidity. Give the tannins a few more years to come around then drink to capture its subtle fruit flavors.
title: Pio Cesare 2013 Ornato  (Barolo)

description: A blend of 95% Sangiovese and 5% Ancellotta, this opens with aromas of violet, black-skinned berry and cooking spice. The fresh, savory palate offers juicy black cherry, clove, white pepper and a hint of aromatic herb. It's balanced, with fine-grained tannins, lively acidity and good mid-term aging potential. Drink through 2024.
title: Podere Capaccia 2014  Chianti Classico

description: A blend of 80% Sangiovese and 20% Merlot, this offers aromas of bell pepper, moist earth and cooking spice. The firm palate delivers Marasca cherry, white pepper and licorice alongside polished tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Poggio Torselli 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This warm, ripe wine is full of rich black fruit and light tannins. It is dense but has a refreshing light touch of acidity. Savor this wine now for its fruitiness.
title: Richard Rottiers 2016 La Sambinerie  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Yolo County, Central Valley, California, US
description: An abundance of floral and savory flavors drives this full-bodied, dry and mouth-filling wine. It's not fruity per se, but has a good core of white peach along with almond and white-tea notes. This 50-50 blend of Albariño and Verdelho will bring a welcome change of pace to the table.
title: Rock Wall 2016 Alverd White (Yolo County)

description: This perennial value delivers again in a juicy and elegant expression of concentrated black fruit, black olive and tomato leaf, a nice complement of succulent and savory. Stock up.
title: RouteStock 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Made with organic grapes, this opens with aromas of violet, baking spice and a hint of nutmeg. The concentrated palate delivers red plum, mocha and white pepper alongside taut tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: San Fabiano Calcinaia 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Highly aromatic notes of raspberry and wild strawberry immediately charm the nose. These notes carry to a rich flourish across the supple palate. There is warmth countered by lovely freshness and some bite. A wine with immense charm.
title: Schandl 2015 Pinot Noir (Burgenland)

description: Chocolate and mocha set a rich tone on the nose amidst plum and black cherry fruit. The palate falls in with mellow, smooth tannins and a vanilla edge to its lovely cherry fruit. There is freshness, too. A modern, international and fruit-driven style with immediate charm.
title: Schandl 2015 Ried Kreften Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Despite the tough, rain-filled vintage, this is a strong showing from a producer that focuses on red grape varieties. A pristine nose of bramble berries, wild herbs, crushed black pepper and graphite sit over a core of dark cherry. Medium in body, silken in feel and supported by fine-grained tannins, the palate is persistent in tart dark cherry and cranberry, with more graphite and black pepper lingering on the finish.
title: Shalestone 2014 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: Lifted aromas of stone and passion fruit, lime and flowers meld with with an almond butter note on the nose. It's the palate that turns things up a notch with its chalky texture, mouth-watering acidity and more citrus and floral flavors.
title: Te Mata 2015 Cape Crest Sauvignon Blanc (Hawke's Bay)

description: This is a bold wine, showing smoky, toasted oak aromas to bolster ripe pear and apple flavors that are accented by vanilla and cream. It's not something delicate for fresh seafood, but will taste great with a roast chicken or pizza bianco.
title: The Federalist 2016 Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: Aromas of black currant, underbrush and a hint of bay leaf are front and center. The juicy, savory palate delivers black cherry, cassis, clove and green peppercorn alongside chewy tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Villa Calcinaia 2015 Piegaia  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine is two-thirds Evergreen and one-third RAF vineyards, both in the Ancient Lakes appellation and all fermented and aged in stainless steel. It's spot on for the variety, with notes of whole Granny Smith apple and green melon. The palate redolent with sleek fresh apple and pear flavors that power through the finish. It's a standout at this price.
title: Vino 2015 Pinot Grigio (Washington)

description: Aromas of underbrush, toasted oak and dark spice emerge on this firmly structured wine. It's rather austere, evoking dried black cherry, cranberry, star anise and espresso framed in racy acidity. Taut, close-grained tannins grip the finish. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Volpaia 2013 Coltassala  (Chianti Classico)

description: A friendly, ripe and appetizing tone of yellow pear dominates the nose and palate of this wine. It's fluid in its flavors, with a yeasty edge that adds some texture and a touch of saltiness that lasts all the way through the finish.
title: Weingut Frank 2015 Ried Hoher Weg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Generous flavors and full body give this wonderful and affordable wine a warm feel on the palate. Rich black cherry, black tea and cranberry notes offer a lot of complexity. The texture is broad and full, but infused with moderate tannins and subtle acidity that keep it fresh.
title: Angeline 2016 Pinot Noir (California)

description: Jammy berry aromas are a bit heavy and stewed, with a balsamic leaning. That's a fair indication of what's to come: namely a saturated palate with a slightly disjointed mix of blackberry, prune and licorice flavors. A chocolaty flavor takes over on a heavy meandering finish.
title: Antiyal 2015 Pura Fe Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

Appellation: Los Olivos District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dark loamy soil aromas meet with packed violets, red rose petals, crushed gravel and dense blueberry on the nose of this blend of 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot and 20% Petit Verdot. The palate is quite tangy for the grapes in involved, with hints of balsamic reduction sauce, black pepper and underripe black fruit.
title: Baehner Fournier 2014 V3 Red (Los Olivos District)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of flower, peach and jasmine lead to spritzy, off-dry stone fruit flavors. Floral notes are threaded throughout the palate, with light ale notes on the finish that cause some distraction.
title: Kiona 2016 Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Red berry, baking spice and blue flower aromas meld together on this. The bright, rather lean palate offers sour cherry, licorice and orange rind framed in vibrant acidity and taut but not very persistent tannins.
title: Lamole di Lamole 2014 Blue Label  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Engaging aromas of spicy bramble berry and tilled earth start out strong, leading to a medium-bodied, red-fruit-driven palate supported by soft, fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Cedar and pepper spice linger on the finish.
title: Lamoreaux Landing 2014 Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: A heavy blanket of oak dominates a nose with modest melon and apple notes, while this feels big, oaky and short on spine and zest. Previously mentioned heavy oak results in a creamy flavor profile, while wood is in the lead on a weighty finish. Drink now.
title: Lapostolle 2015 Cuvée Alexandre Viñedo Atalayas Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Fresh blossoms mingle amidst earth tones in this freshly composed blend of Grenache Blanc and Viognier. The spry palate offers crisp red-apple and citrus flavors kissed with honeysuckle. The finish is brisk but clean. Drink now through 2020.
title: Lavau 2016 White (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Subtle aromas evoking baking spice, baked plum and a hint of gunpowder emerge on this 85% Sangiovese, 10% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Canaiolo blend. The firm palate offers prune, clove and licorice alongside closed-grained tannins.
title: Le Regge 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Bell pepper and corn tortilla aromas announce a cool-climate wine. Those leanings are confirmed on a tangy palate with the consistency of lime juice. Green-citrus flavors are full of pyrazines, while this feels round on a finish cut by citric acidity.
title: Luis Felipe Edwards 2016 Marea Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (WA), Washington Other, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of 58% Cabernet Sauvignon, 24% Merlot, 9% Cabernet Franc, 5% Malbec and 4% Petit Verdot. The aromas display notes of baking spice, dark fruit and herb. The palate brings a chalky, lightly astringent feel, along with a tart finish.
title: Maryhill 2014 Serendipity Proprietor's Reserve Red (Columbia Gorge (WA))

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: There's a delightfully herbal edge to this wine, with wild strawberry, brambly raspberry and specks of pepper dotting the nose. The palate moves from tart strawberry to creamier, softer red fruit, all while maintaining a balanced zip of acidity.
title: McGregor 2016 Rosé d' Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of vanilla, baking spice and plum are followed by ripe but quite light cherry flavors. The tannins bring a slight sense of astringency that seems at odds with the variety.
title: Mercer 2015 Malbec (Horse Heaven Hills)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are high toned, with notes of wood spice, dried herb and tire rubber, not seeming very varietal. The palate is ripe with plum and barrel flavors that paint around the edges. Mocha notes linger on the finish.
title: Mercer 2015 Merlot (Horse Heaven Hills)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are a bit unusual, with notes of red apple, overripe plum, spice and herb. The palate is lightly styled, with bunched-up cherry flavors that finish short.
title: Mustard Seed 2014 Moving Mountains Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: This is a plush and plummy Pinot full of florals, red and purple fruits, and some cinnamon stalkiness. It's bright and light-bodied, with a slightly savory finish. Chill down and slurp.
title: Oyster Bay 2016 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from the oldest Chenin plantings in the state, the aromas are light, with notes of sliced apple, flower and white peach. It drinks off dry, with sleek well-balanced honeyed-fruit flavors throughout. It's equal parts intrigue and delivery.
title: Pacific Rim 2016 Old Vines Hahn Hill Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Yakima Valley)

description: Dense and full-bodied for an Otago Pinot, this wine shows notes of ripe red and black fruits, chocolate, cola, green herbs and a whiff of smoke. The palate is broody with chewy, savory tannins and an oaky finish.
title: Peregrine 2013 Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

description: Furry berry aromas are a touch oxidized, and overall the nose on this Pinot lacks sizzle and freshness. A full-bodied palate shows little to no acidic lift. Given all that, it's not surprising that this tastes of chunky berry and plum, while hints of clove and wood spice appear on the finish.
title: Ritual 2015 Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: A hint of burnt rubber makes spicy, leafy plum aromas seem gritty. This is full and round on the palate, with modest to low acidity. Cooked plum and black-currant flavors carry over to a soft finish that lacks acidic cut.
title: Tierra Calma 2016 Pinot Noir (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Cayuga Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A heady nose of white blossoms jumps from the glass, with peach juice and tangerine following. The round palate offers more juicy stone fruit tones, with tart apple and citrus providing a line of acidity that leads to a pleasant, blossomy finish. This is well-balanced and perfectly enjoyable as an apéritif.
title: Treleaven 2016 Estate Bottled Dry Riesling (Cayuga Lake)

description: This shows earthy aromas of truffle, tilled soil and a whiff of ripe plum. The bright palate offers raspberry jam, cinnamon and a hint or orange peel alongside round tannins.
title: Villa Calcinaia 2015 Cappone  (Chianti Classico)

description: Savory aromas of carob and herbs outweigh berry aromas on a shallow bouquet. A dense, meaty, almost syrupy palate offers a mix of baked berry, oak and green-herb flavors. Spice, mint and mild green notes are measurable on a plump finish.
title: Viña Chocalan 2014 Reserva Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

description: Peach and passionfruit notes mingle with green bean and white pepper on the nose. The palate is lean with a good lick of acidity and a long, citrusy finish. Enjoy this thirst-quenching drop with a summer salad.
title: Wairau River 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: New York, New York Other, New York, US
description: Dark cherry aromas mix with notes of earth, coffee and coconut on the nose. Medium in weight, but with grippy tannins and bright acidity to offer support, the palate shows more levity in flavors of tart cherry and cranberry, bolstered by earth and bitter spice.
title: Whitecliff Vineyard 2013 Petit Verdot (New York)

description: This medium-bodied wine has a savory earthy style, starting with herb and chicken-stock aromas and leading to lemon and dried herb flavors. It has a good crisp balance that's appealing.
title: Zina Hyde Cunningham 2015 Adeline's Vineyard Chardonnay (Anderson Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Quiet aromas of citrus and stone fruit lead on the nose. A crunchy green apple flavor and quinine acidity keep the palate lean, taut and dry, racing to a mineral-dusted finish.
title: Billsboro 2016 Sawmill Creek Vineyards Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A pleasant intensity of lemon-lime spray and ripe yellow apple carry the nose. Shocks of lime energize the initial sip, with an apple butter flavor pushing through. This wine was fermented and aged in neutral oak barrels, offering a bit of roundness to the palate.
title: Buttonwood Grove 2016 Frances Amelia Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Scorched earth, pressed violet, clove and a whiff of black-skinned berry delicately lead the nose. The palate is rather evolved, offering prune, orange rind and a hint of star anise framed in loose, dusty tannins that leave a drying, somewhat fleeting finish. Drink soon.
title: Cantalici L'Antica Fornace di Ridolfo 2012 Messer Ridolfo Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with subtle aromas of red berry and earth. Mirroring the nose, the no-frills palate offers ripe red berry, clove and a hint of sage alongside dusty tannins. Enjoy through 2019.
title: Carpineto 2010  Chianti Classico

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this has restrained aromas of baking spice, cedar and black plum. The firm palate offers black cherry jam, licorice and mocha alongside close-grained, rather astringent tannins.
title: Casale dello Sparviero 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Camphor, blue flower and spiced plum aromas lead the nose. The palate is rather evolved, offering prune, dried cherry and clove alongside dusty tannins that leave a firm, drying finish.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2012 Castelgreve Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Stewed prune, moist soil, pressed blue flower and dried herb aromas combine with a balsamic note. The dense palate offers dried cherry, orange slice, licorice and espresso framed in close-grained not very persistent tannins that lead to a rather abrupt, drying finish.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2013 Lamole Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of red plum, vanilla and a whiff of baking spice set the tone. The dense palate reflects the nose, showing ripe black cherry, mocha and tobacco alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Bindi Sergardi 2014 Calidonia  (Chianti Classico)

description: This Colombard-based white, with 14% Chenin Blanc, is rich and opulent upfront, offering assertive scents of toasted apple, grilled peach, straw and woodspice that are hit by accents of orange blossom, rose and a drizzle of honey. The palate is round and mouthfilling, with opulent, ripe fruit flavors of lemon custard and apple pie. Drink now–2020.
title: Boschkloof 2015 Patatsblanc White (South Africa)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, this presents with aromas of black currant, green bell pepper and a whiff of cedar. The firm palate delivers blackberry, roasted coffee bean and black pepper alongside closed-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Campo alla Sughera 2014 Adeo  (Bolgheri)

description: Aromas of ripe black cherry, plum, crushed mint and a whiff of baking spice carry over to the to the juicy palate along with a hint of licorice. It's savory and easygoing, with dusty tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Campomaggio 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Bright but wild on the nose, this bottling, which is overseen by actress Drew Barrymore, shows raspberry, thyme, sagebrush, rhubarb and smoke aromas on the inviting nose. The palate offers light berry flavors alongside hickory smoke, proving refreshing if not too complex.
title: Carmel Road 2014 Drew's Blend Pinot Noir (Monterey)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dried red currants and cranberries meet with loamy soils on the nose of this block-designate bottling. Tangy red fruits and a hint of olive show on the palate, where the earthy minerality shines through the energetic acidity.
title: Carmel Road 2014 First Row Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)

description: Made with Sangiovese, 7% Cabernet Franc and 3% merlot, this offers aromas of black-skinned berry, blue flower and a hint of eucalyptus. The firm palate offers black-currant jam, star anise and espresso alongside polished tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Casa Sola 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This easygoing red opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, violet and baking spice. The juicy, medium-bodied palate doles out red cherry, clove and white pepper alongside smooth tannins and fresh acidity. It's made to be enjoyed young so drink soon.
title: Casa Sola 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Firm, fine-grained tannins and hints of anise and dried herb lend savory appeal to this voluptuous red. Ripe black-cherry and plum flavors are succulent and plush in texture but balanced keenly with acidity. The finish is expansive and a touch warming.
title: Château de Nages 2015 Vieilles Vignes Red (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Deep veins of granite and crushed mineral penetrate through this ripe, juicy red full of concentrated blackberry and cassis flavors. This is a fleshy, forward wine but elegantly balanced with fresh acidity and firm, velvet-edged tannins.
title: Château de Valcombe 2015 Syrah-Grenache (Costières de Nîmes)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Hints of granite and moss lend nuance to lush, easy blueberry and blackberry flavors. This is a bold, voluptous wine but elegant, framed with velvet tannins and brisk acidic backbone.
title: Château du Trignon 2015 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Brisk lemon-lime acidity adds a vibrant tone to riper, richer apple, pear and tangerine flavors here. It's a robust, densely textured wine but balanced elegantly. The finish lingers pleasantly on a kiss of vanilla spice.
title: Château Husson 2015 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, underbrush and a whiff of leather come together on this. The concentrated palate offers blackberry jam, tobacco and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Colle Bereto 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Buoyant strikes of honeysuckle, yellow peach and spice abound in this lavishly textured white wine. Soft, sunny acidity and swirls of cream and caramel accentuate its voluptuous style but the finish wraps up neatly on a pleasantly bitter note.
title: Delas Frères 2016 Les Challeys  (Saint-Joseph)

description: A small proportion of this producer's Chénas is aged in wood. The 12 months in wood gives extra richness as well as spice and toast, producing a full, structured wine that's likely to need aging. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Côtes Rémont 2016 Elevé en Fût de Chênes  (Chénas)

description: There are powerful tannins in this structured wine. Happily juicy red-fruit flavors and acidity cut through the tannins. Age the wine over the medium-term and drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de Gry-Sablon 2016 Douby  (Morgon)

description: A two-acre parcel of 70-year-old vines has produced this richly structured wine. With firm tannins and a dense texture, it is likely to age well over the coming few months. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de La Grume 2016 La Grume Grain d'Expression  (Brouilly)

description: From vines within sight of the winery, this wine is rich with generous tannins and bold red-cherry flavors. A good structure supports the bold, open fruitiness and acidity. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine des Maisons Neuves 2016 L'Ecluse  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: This is a ripe, creamy and already delicious wine. Refreshing acidity pairs with a zesty character to provide both minerality and great fruitiness. The wine needs to age a few more months, and will be just right from mid-2018.
title: Domaine du Penlois 2016 du Châtelard  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: You'll find aromas of forest floor, exotic spice, oak, leather and a whiff of game o this concentrated red. The hearty, monolithic palate presents dried cherry, licorice and roasted coffee bean framed in taut, fine-grained tannins. You'll also detect the warmth of evident alcohol. Drink through 2022.
title: Elio Filippino 2007 Serra Capelli 10 Anni Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Enjoy this pretty, light-bodied wine chilling down on a warm day. Bright, ripe strawberry and red cherry notes dominate, with a hint of florals and cracked pepper at the back. This is well-structured by lively acidity and a savory finish.
title: Eradus 2016 Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Awatere Valley)

description: After some time in the glass, this reveals its true colors of lime, flint and wet stone, along with brioche, nuts and toasty oak aromas. The medium-weighted palate comes with prickly acidity and a limey tang, making this an excellent wine to pair with a meal.
title: Esk Valley 2016 Chardonnay (Hawke's Bay)

description: This is a cool, crisp wine from vineyards that face east to the rising sun. Its red-cherry fruitiness and acidity are bright with touches of tannin to give the wine shape. It needs a few months for the fruit to calm down from its intensity. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Fabien Collonge 2016 L'Aurore des Côtes  (Chiroubles)

description: Ripe red berry, crushed aromatic herb and baking spice aromas segue over to the juicy, savory palate along with blackberry and star anise. Supple tannins provide relaxed support.
title: Fattoria San Michele a Torri 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Syrah is the lead grape on this blend that opens with wild berry, animal fur and creamy oak aromas. A narrow concentrated palate doesn't display a lot of structure, while this tastes of ripe plum with brown-sugar sweetness. There's grip but little expansion to the finish. Drink through 2023.
title: Flaherty 2014 Red (Aconcagua Costa)

description: This is a chewy wine, still powered with the typical tannins of a young Morgon. The domaine has vines in the Charmes and Corcelette sub-regions, giving a serious structure as well as great black fruits. Drink from 2019.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine Mont Chavy  (Morgon)

description: There's quite a lot of whole bunch stalkiness in this wine, combined with a good lick of new oak. Aromas of cherry, cinnamon and stems lead the pack along with spiced cedar. The palate offers more juicy red fruits and stalky qualities, and while this has some potential to age, it's also drinking just fine now. Drink now–2025.
title: Giesen 2014 Clayvin Single Vineyard Selection Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Lots of fleshy black and red cherry fruit swishes on nose and palate. It is juicy fruit that firmly stands in the foreground on a fluid palate that has lovely, exciting tinges of white pepper spice. The body is fresh and light, the finish is refreshing and moreish.
title: Höpler 2014 Pannonica Red (Weinland Österreich)

description: Gentle red cherry fruit appears on the nose and swishes across the juicy palate where tannins and freshness provide a frame for all the cherry abundance. This majors on delicious fruit but comes with a lovely grip.
title: Höpler 2015 Pinot Noir (Burgenland)

description: Full of red cherries and succulent black plums, this ripe, soft wine comes from a privately owned estate that is followed by the producer. The wine is ripe, full in the mouth and ready to drink now.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Domaine des Nazins  (Brouilly)

description: Tight and lean, this has restrained aromas of moist forest floor, leather, espresso and balsamic notes. The firm palate offers dried cherry, steeped prune and oak-driven spice alongside raspy tannins that leave an astringent finish.
title: Araldica 2013 Corsini  (Barbaresco)

description: Yeasty vanilla aromas blend with notes of green melon and banana. In the mouth, this is chunky and round, with modest acidity. Yeasty flavors of green banana confirm that this is a mid-level wine.
title: Aresti 2016 Bellavista Reserva Chardonnay (Curicó Valley)

description: A bright pink color and unusually toasty spicy aromas are followed by bright raspberry flavors and a heavy layer of oaky spices, like cinnamon and ginger. It's a rosé with attitude.
title: Ca' Momi NV Heartcraft Sparkling Rosé Sparkling (California)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pomegranate and fennel frond aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate has cranberry and more pomegranate, with a crushed slate and gunmetal minerality.
title: Carmel Road 2014 Panorama Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)

description: This opens with aromas of sun-baked soil, truffle, used leather and a whiff of overripe plum. The earthy aromas carry over to the no-frills palate along with dried cherry and a hint of ground pepper. Dusty, rather fleeting tannins give it an abrupt, astringent finish.
title: Casale dello Sparviero 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This opens with aromas of blackberry jam, exotic spice and a whiff of French oak. The palate is tight and lean, offering prune, star anise and a slightly green note of powdered sage alongside astringent, grainy tannins that leave a dusty finish.
title: Casaloste 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Underbrush, tobacco and stewed prune aromas lead the nose. It's hard and lean in terms of fruit richness, offering raw sour cherry and clove alongside an acidic backbone and bracing, grainy tannins.
title: Ceste 2013  Barolo

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Syrah and Merlot make up the majority of this blend, which is topped off with pinches of other Bordeaux and Rhône varieties. The aromas are inviting, with notes of blueberry pie, herb and smoke. The fruit flavors don't entirely deliver on this promise, with quite-light plum notes that paint around the edges. Vanilla notes linger on the finish.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2015 Indian Wells Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fruity and flowery aromas of black cherry soda, dried strawberry and hibiscus show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is somewhat thin, with flavors of berry jam, anise and a pinch of thyme.
title: Clos La Chance 2015 Reserve Primitivo (Central Coast)

description: This see-through Pinot with a rusty tint smells of tomato leaf, herbs and mixed spices, while fruit aromas are tertiary and not very ripe. A basic palate is low on energy, while this tastes of gritty plum and pepper. A slight bitterness extends onto a dry finish.
title: De Martino 2013 Legado Reserva Pinot Noir (Limarí Valley)

description: Produced from 60-year-old vines, this crisp, juicy wine is bright with acidity and forward red-berry flavors. Light and fruity, it's ready to drink.
title: Domaine Bertrand 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: While this wine has some of the tannic structure of Morgon, its juicy fruits and hints of banana give a much lighter character. Fruitiness drives this crisp wine and will allow it to be drunk from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Gaudets 2016 Sornay  (Morgon)

description: This has a subdued, fleeting nose showing cellar floor, weedy underbrush and used leather. The evolved palate offers mature cherry coffee and a hint of spice around loose, short-lived tannins.
title: Gabriele Scaglione 2013  Barbaresco

description: Punchy citrus aromas are within normal parameters for Leyda SB. A tight citric palate is loaded with pyrazines, causing citrus and lemon-lime flavors to come across as tangy and grassy. A finish with a texture of wet rocks is juicy but green in flavor.
title: Garces Silva 2017 Boya Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: Temecula Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 50% Cabernet Franc and 50% Zinfandel shows pepper, plump purple flowers, candied berries and Concord grape on the nose. There is piquant boysenberry fruit on the sip, as well as candied violets and lilacs, but the wine still tastes very young and needs more resolution.
title: Gershon Bachus 2013 Anteros Red (Temecula Valley)

description: This single-estate wine is perfumed, lightly structured and full of ripe, juicy red fruits. With acidity and spice in equal measure, it is soft, packed with fruitiness and acidity, and ready to drink.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Domaine Pignard  (Beaujolais)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
Designation: Tahija Heart of the Hill Vineyard
description: Aromas of plum, baking spice and Band-Aid lead to a medium-bodied palate with a lightly tacky feel. The tannins bring a sense of astringency and it finishes bitter.
title: Liberty Lake 2013 Tahija Heart of the Hill Vineyard Petite Sirah (Red Mountain)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from one of the state's top vineyards and 100% varietal, this has aromas of cherry, cranberry, vanilla and freshly chopped wood which lead to tart cherry flavors that display a bitter note that distracts. Barrel aged for 20 months in 60% new French oak, the wood influence seems heavy, getting in the way of the finish.
title: Maryhill 2014 Klipsun Vineyard Merlot (Red Mountain)

description: Smoky oak, savory aromas of cured meats and a dark extracted color introduce this blend from the Itata and Maule regions. A heavy tannic palate is rubbery and concentrated in feel, while gritty, roasted blackberry and black plum flavors are peppery and finish with scratchy drying tannins.
title: Pedro Parra y Familia 2015 Pencopolitano Red (Itata Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of green herb, baking spice, nori, licorice and black cherry are followed by tart dark-fruit flavors that seem bunched up. It falls off on the finish, displaying a light medicinal note.
title: Ram 2015 Alder Ridge Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dark plum, mulberry, wet river stones and a touch of caramel show on the nose of this bottling. Candied pomegranate and baked red plum arise on the sip, with strong vanilla and baking spice flavors. The tannins are oddly both thin and chalky.
title: Rolling Plains 2014 Reserve Merlot (Monterey County)

description: Standard plum and berry aromas are clean and get the job done. This is clampy on the palate, with grapy dark-fruit flavors. Solid fruit character and firm tannins on the finish confirm that this is a good everyday Cabernet.
title: Santa Rita 2016 Secret Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Sourced from a vineyard on the bluff of Keuka Lake, this is ultraripe in expression, boasting a mix of stone and tropical fruit, blended with a spicy hop character. The palate is off dry in style, with a lush, slightly overblown feel, that leads to a mildly hot finish.
title: Silver Thread 2016 Gridley Bluff Point Vineyard Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Shades of spicy bramble and strawberry aromas blend with earthier, stemmy tones on the nose of this blend. Light in feel, but with a charming roundness, the palate pleases in boisterous red cherry and raspberry flavors, flecked by peppery spice.
title: Stony Lonesome 2016 Estate Reserve Zweigelt-Cabernet Sauvignon Red (Finger Lakes)

description: Waxy lemon, green beans, melon and wet stone show their faces, if shyly, in this Kiwi Pinot Gris. The palate is slippery in feel, with a tongue-tingling line of prickly acidity and citrus notes throughout.
title: Tablelands 2015 Pinot Gris (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: High-toned aromas of pencil lead, game, dark plum, fruit leather and green herb lead to plentiful dark-fruit flavors. The fruit seems a bit dried out.
title: The Leader 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Plum and berry aromas are solid and clean. This has a fresh snappy linear mouthfeel and plum flavors that hold up to scrutiny. Light notes of chocolate and toast lend some richness to the finish.
title: Undurraga 2016 U Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Fresh cranberry and herbal spice aromas carry to the light- to medium-bodied palate, which boasts a bit of white pepper and violet flavors. Bright acidity and sandy tannins maintain the delicate frame. Drink now.
title: Ventosa 2013 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: The nose, with fiery red-fruit scents, includes a hint of latex. This feels full and slightly heavy on the palate, while lightly burnt berry and plum flavors end in blocky fashion, with a note of chocolate.
title: Veramonte 2015 Pinot Noir (Central Valley)

description: This wine is pure juicy fruit. It bubbles with red cherries, acidity and a crisp shot of acidity. A refreshing, lightly structured wine, it is ready to drink.
title: Vignerons des Pierres Dorées 2016  Beaujolais

description: Soupy noncommittal aromas of grapy black fruits and plastic set up a palate that's showing live-wire acidity but also a full and heavy body. Baked black-fruit flavors are slightly herbal, while this Carignan-led blend of six grapes finishes chewy, dense and sticky, with just enough acidic offset.
title: Garcia + Schwaderer 2015 Bravado Red (Itata Valley)

description: Aromas of espresso, oak, licorice and coconut carry over to the tight knit, medium-bodied palate along with dried black cherry and licorice. Assertive, close-grained tannins clench the finish.
title: Giacosa Fratelli 2014 Vigna Gianmatè  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Cherry, grenadine and rose petal mix on the nose of this vineyard-designate, which is creamy and fully rich on the palate, with a touch of gravel. Vanilla shows through on the grip of oak.
title: Gracianna 2015 Estate Mercedes Riverblock Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Pale gold in color, the nose is a mélange of melon, stone fruit, strawberry leaf and oregano. The palate is full and rich, with an oily texture and flesh, melon fruit followed by a long, slippery finish.
title: Greywacke 2015 Pinot Gris (Marlborough)

description: Flavors of fleshy, preserved red cherry and strawberry verge on overripe here. Soft, plush and fruity, it's an easy-drinking wine with a hint of leather and spice. Although not deeply complex, it's comforting and rich, finishing on fine, furry tannins.
title: Henry de Floret 2014 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)
