description: From 50-year-old vines, this wine is ripe and solid with dark black fruit as well as some firm tannins. It has weight, concentration and a strong minerality from the granite soil of this high-altitude vineyard. Drink from late 2018.
title: Henry Fessy 2016  Chiroubles

description: Melon and fig mark the fruity nature of this wine, which offers a generous helping of oak alongside intense, up-front acidity. Tangerine slices brighten the thick concentration of other fruit and medium-bodied texture.
title: Hoopla 2015 Chardonnay (Yountville)

description: This wine is structured and firm with the classic tannins of Morgon, while red-cherry and banana flavors give a bright texture. The two elements should come together to give an easy, fruit-driven wine. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Domaine de la Chaponne  (Morgon)

description: A blend of 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this opens with subtle aromas of baking spice and black plum. The straightforward palate delivers blackberry jam, white pepper and clove alongside smooth tannins.
title: La Sala 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Tropical and voluptuously full bodied, this wine is oak-laden and ripe in apple, pineapple and mango, with a lemony aftertaste of uplifting acidity.
title: La Storia by Trentadue 2016 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of red plum, cedar and espresso while the firm palate offers dried black cherry, toast and licorice. Closed-grained, rather astringent tannins provide the setting.
title: Le Bocce 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: While wild raspberry and strawberry flavors are plush and plump in this rich, full-bodied red, hints of earth and bramble lend spicy, savory elegance. It's a round and mouthfilling wine that finishes on fine yet persistent tannins.
title: Les Coteaux de Fournès 2014 Les Grès Syrah-Grenache (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: This is a full and rich Sauvy, with loads of cedar shavings and dried straw that frame the yellow apple and lemon fruit. The palate is broad and weighty, with lingering notes of toasty, grilled apple and spiced orange rind that linger on the close.
title: Lismore 2015 Barrel Fermented Sauvignon Blanc (Cape South Coast)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: This wine is delightfully intense on the nose, with aromas of tightly wound currant and dark cherry, amidst hints of pepper, sweet oak and tilled earth. The medium-bodied palate is bolstered by bright acidity and grippy tannins that house dense fruit flavors peppered with savory spice notes. Drink from 2019.
title: McGregor 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

description: Aromas of wild herbs and sage are spicy and earthy, with red-fruit notes. A standard palate belies this wine's high price tag, while lightly herbal plum and currant flavors are minty and suggest a touch of eucalyptus. Moderate oak rises up on a lasting finish.
title: Miguel Torres 2013 Escaleras de Empedrado Pinot Noir (Chile)

description: Dried herb and a twist of lemongrass provide an exotic edge to this light white, which is moderate in acidity and fruity in melon and kiwi. The freshness persists with ease on the finish.
title: Oberon 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Ripe raisiny berry aromas come with clove and nutmeg notes suggestive of spice cake. A plump dense palate is saturated, while this tastes leafy, slightly soapy and like blackberries mixed with herbs. A plump but balanced finish maintains a theme of full-bodied ripeness.
title: Oveja Negra 2014 Single Vineyard Carmenère (Maule Valley)

description: The structure of this northerly Beaujolais cru shows through well in this wine. A core of tannins is boosted by the great swathes of black currant and berry fruits and acidity. The wine is perfumed, ripe and ready to drink.
title: Pardon et Fils 2016 Les PIerres  (Saint-Amour)

description: Subtle aromas of cedar, tobacco and red plum guide the nose while the firm palate offers dried black cherry, clove and black pepper. Assertive, mouth-puckering tannins provide the framework.
title: Podere Capaccia 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: El Pomar District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Plump blackberries meet with roasted meat, rosemary, spearmint, dried rose petals and cherry cola aromas on the plush nose of this bottling. There's strong energy to the sip, with tart black plum skin flavors alongside purple flowers and a growing caramel element on the finish.
title: Pomar Junction 2014 Mourvèdre (El Pomar District)

description: Aromas of wild berry, crushed mint and star anise carry over to the rather lean palate along with raw cranberry, licorice and espresso. Close-grained tannins and bright acidity provide the framework but it finishes rather quickly.
title: Rattalino 2014 Quarantadue 42  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: This bottling explores a richer tropical side of the grape, with aromas of plumeria, guava, coconut oil, passion fruit and fresh tangerines. The palate combines mango and coconut flavors that are cut by zesty Key lime juice.
title: Rendarrio Vineyards 2016 Baja-Ha Chardonnay (Santa Lucia Highlands)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: A perfumed nose of rose, lavender and forest floor gives way to velvety texture and light-bodied notions of red cherry, strawberry, rhubarb and dried herb. The acidity is wrapped in citrus tones.
title: Rolling Plains 2013 Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Made with 80% Sangiovese and 20% Merlot, this presents with aroma of red plum, grilled herb and a whiff of mocha. The straightforward palate offers black cherry, white pepper and a hint of clove accompanied by solid tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Setriolo 2014  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Full, ripe notes of red apple, pear and peach resound on the nose. The medium-bodied palate carries the same expression, with a juicy, round feel counterbalanced by the astringency of apple peels. Enjoy this medium-sweet Riesling alongside a creamy Thai curry.
title: Sheldrake Point 2016 Estate Bottled Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This attractive medium-bodied wine has dried cherry and plum aromas, late-picked fruit flavors and a fairly smooth texture. It has subtle, spicy, tea-like nuances that add complexity.
title: Sierra Starr 2014 Old Clone Zinfandel (Sierra Foothills)

description: Medium gold in hue, this style of nutty, smokey Chard is love it or hate it. Cashews, struck match, stone and tropical fruit are backed by butter from malolactic ferment notes. The palate is equally oak and malo driven but this toasty, grainy-textured wine will nevertheless appeal to many.
title: Stoneleigh Latitude 2015 Chardonnay (Marlborough)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are high toned, with notes of char, plum, butterscotch, vanilla and spice, needing some coaxing to get at them initially. The palate mixes cherry and cranberry flavors that show intensity and length. There are good things going on but they don't all seem to come together at present.
title: Tamarack Cellars 2015 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This 100% Sangiovese offers inviting aromas of black plum and cooking spice. The round, easygoing palate evokes black cherry jam, mocha and a licorice note alongside smooth tannins.
title: Valiano 2013 Poggio Teo  (Chianti Classico)

description: An impressive 120-year-old parcel of vines provides this ripe wine that has concentration and loads of spicy black-cherry fruitiness. The wine is delicious and ready to drink.
title: Vignoble Charmet 2016 La Centenaire  (Beaujolais)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Aged 11 months in used French oak, this wine is citrus driven, with a slight toasty edge. The mild acidity carries short flavors, making this a simple pour.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2015 Barrel Select Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This value wine has an astringency that adds to an overall puckering notion of ripeness and oak. Earthy mushroom and compost underlie red fruit, yeast and cedar.
title: Eagle Glen 2014 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: Earthy notes of carnation, cured game and green peppercorn are pronounced in the bouquet of this wine, with supporting red plum and cherry fruit. The medium-weight palate boasts astringent tannins and fruit-skin flavors that prevail through the grippy close. Drink now.
title: Long Neck 2015 Shiraz (Western Cape)

description: Aromas of licorice, prune and leather try hard to hold up but fade to herbal and soupy. High tartaric acidity throws the palate out of balance, while this tastes of generic plum and prune. A short chewy finish closes this out.
title: Santa Carolina 2016 Reserva Merlot (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Earthy cranberry and an herbaceous underbelly ride along this somewhat thin yet ripe wine, which finishes in a bitter twinge of cardamom and oak.
title: Uptick Vineyards 2014 Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Roasted leafy earthy aromas are less than focused and not entirely fresh. A wide palate is dull-edged, while this tastes woody, earthy and a bit musty, without vivid fruit. Oak-based clove notes ride high on an otherwise bland finish.
title: Calcu 2014 Reserva Especial Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: A neutral, nearly scentless nose sets up a palate with modest acidity but nothing more. Short citrus flavors see oxidized notes poke through on the finish, indicating that this basic wine from a wet vintage is passing its prime.
title: Chilensis 2016 Reserva Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Maule Valley)

description: A three-acre vineyard of 55-year-old vines has produced this bright, crisply fruity wine. It is juicy with considerable acidity as well as plenty of red-cherry flavors. Drink now.
title: Domaine Céline & Nicolas Hirsch 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: Lifted aromas of ginger, florals and ripe citrus are somewhat enticing, but the palate is acidic to the point of sourness, with an odd combo of spritz and fruit weight.
title: Giant Sky NV Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: A very strong and spicy oak aroma leaps from the glass, and the same dramatic smoke and chocolate element dominates the flavors of this full-bodied wine to the point that any fruity aspects are very muted.
title: Jessie's Grove 2012 Vintner's Choice Reserve Zinfandel (Lodi)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Streamlined aromas of citrus, florals and crisp apples on the nose lead to a richer impression of melon and orange on the palate. Mild acidity and little concentration of flavors make for a simple, straightforward pour.
title: Rooster Hill 2016 Traminette (Finger Lakes)

description: Apple cider and white-flower aromas are more briny than fresh and perky. This feels plump and oily due to moderate acidity, while salty white-fruit flavors are chunky on a mostly fruitless finish.
title: William Fèvre 2017 La Misión Reserva Especial Chardonnay (Maipo Valley)

description: A yeasty but light nose with nothing more than a whiff of lime sets up a racy citric palate that's shearing and angular. Tart apple, underripe nectarine and lemon-lime flavors finish long but sharp almost to the point of being sour.
title: Casa Donoso 2017 Gran Reserva Bicentenario Sauvignon Blanc (Maule Valley)

description: Barnyard aromas and massive, drying tannins really stand out in this unusually raw-boned wine. It smells earthy and smoky, tastes meaty and herbaceous, while the texture is full of potent, gripping astringency on first sip that eventually moderates with subsequent tastes.
title: Donkey & Goat 2014 Blakeman Vineyard Merlot (Anderson Valley)

description: Simple fruit and a light texture make for a crisp wine. Red fruits and acidity balance with the light caramel texture and the wine is ready to drink.
title: Ferraud et Fils 2016 Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

description: This is as simple as Beaujolais can get, all berry fruit, crisp acidity and light tannins. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Beau  (Beaujolais)

description: This simple wine offers red berry and banana flavors. With its light structure and soft fruit, it is open and ready to drink.
title: Louis Tête 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: A heavy, sweet-seeming oak coating totally dominates the flavors of this soft-textured low-acid wine. It has baking spices, toasted bread and vanilla, while any fruity component is very subdued.
title: Chasing Lions 2016 Chasing Lions Chardonnay (California)

Designation: Araucano Reserva Made With Organic Grapes
description: Flat aromas of yeast, vanilla and animal cracker cookies lead to a palate that's more amorphous than tight. Bitter flavors of green onion and grassy citrus fruits fail to come around on the finish.
title: François Lurton 2017 Araucano Reserva Made With Organic Grapes Sauvignon Blanc (Lolol Valley)

description: This screw cap wine is made to be simple and affordable and it is, with a reduced sensibility to the big amount of oak on offer and a harshness to the waxy apple flavor.
title: It's a Headsnapper 2015 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: It's not hard to pick out the variety of this wine, with its japaleño jelly and wild strawberry notes. Light in body, with a decent vein of acidity, enjoy soon before the fruit notes drop off.
title: Wicked Water 2016 Sawmill Creek Vineyards Rosé of Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The cranberry and raspberry aromas show volatility that distracts. The flavors are lightly sweet with a tart finish.
title: Catman 2015 Barbera (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a strange wine. The wood aging has taken away the fruit, leaving spice and acidity in its place.
title: Domaine Marc Delienne 2016 Le Petit Ami Rosé (Beaujolais Rosé)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: An odd aroma of peanut skin pervades over muddled cherry tones. The palate is light and delicately structured with soft tannins and shots of cranberry and berries.
title: Glenora 2015 Oak free Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: High-toned aromas of cherry and raspberry are partnered with hints of violets on the nose of this light red. The flavors are fresh, yet fleeting, with a quick finish.
title: Linton Park 2015 Red Rhino Pinotage (Western Cape)

description: Huge oak and an intensity of ripe flavor give this wine a harsh edginess that's hard to dispel. Compost, coconut and a green hint of bay leaf or cedar mix in a disjointed way.
title: Uptick Vineyards 2013 Merlot (Sonoma County)

description: This mostly colorless rosé of Carmenère opens with gritty aromas of orange peel. A tangy palate lacks depth, while this tastes sour and generic, with little virtue to the finish.
title: Apaltagua 2017 Reserva Carmenere Rosé (Maule Valley)

description: An unusual combination of Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc has yielded a yeasty-smelling bubbly with little to no fruit on the nose or palate. This tastes mostly like salad, with the vinaigrette and shallots added in. On the plus side, it feels pretty good.
title: Apaltagua NV Costero Nature Sparkling (San Antonio-Maipo Valley)

description: One whiff tells you this rosé of Malbec and 10% Petit Verdot is all about pyrazines. A high level of citric acid drives a palate that tastes of orange extract and gritty bitterness. Beyond tang there's not much to a sharp finish.
title: Calcu 2017 Reserva Especial Rosé (Colchagua Valley)

description: Rustic gamy aromas of burnt leaves and stewed fruit are sketchy. A raw edgy palate feels tannic and resiny. Stalky berry flavors topped with forced oak end with a mix of wood resin and chocolate notes.
title: Junta 2013 Reserve Momentos Merlot (Curicó Valley)

description: Made with organically grown grapes, this has aromas of baking spice, underbrush and a hint of violet. The medium-bodied palate offers dried black cherry, vanilla and a touch of white pepper alongside polished tannins.
title: Villa Calcinaia 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Aromas of roasted coffee bean, baking spice and a hint of blue flower lead the nose. The medium-bodied palate delivers clove, licorice and red plum alongside close-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Villa Cerna 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: An opaque color and spicy herbal black-fruit and tomato leaf aromas are typical of cool-climate Chilean Syrah. A palate with popping acidity is fresh but abrupt. Snappy berry and plum flavors are saucy and jumpy, while this is herbal and spicy on the finish.
title: Villard 2015 Expresion Reserve Syrah (Casablanca Valley)

Designation: Ecos de Rulo Single Vineyard El Chequén Estate
description: Dry spicy aromas of balsam wood, cherry and berry fruits come in front of a bumpy rough palate with purpose. Roasted spicy berry flavors are oaky and finish with drying tannins and lasting barrel notes.
title: Viña Bisquertt 2014 Ecos de Rulo Single Vineyard El Chequén Estate Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's potential to this as a regional dessert wine. Aromas of honeysuckle, ripe Asian pear and Gala apple lead into a palate of honey glaze, lemongrass and pear syrup. It's a fresh after-dinner sipper.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2016 LoverGirl Late Harvest Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Sangiovese (61%) takes the lead on this blend, followed by Cabernet Sauvignon (29%) and Merlot. The aromas are light, with notes of smoke, herb, plum and cigar box. The palate shows more generosity, with plentiful red fruit and herb flavors backed by firm tannins. The finish comes up a bit short.
title: Airfield Estates 2015 Spitfire Red (Yakima Valley)

description: Ripe blackberry and plum aromas show a hint of asphalt. In the mouth, this is flush, saturated and not holding back. Spicy, lightly herbal berry flavors finish with a warm hint of brown sugar along with tannic rub.
title: Almacruz 2015 Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: Baked earthy berry aromas are herbal and suggest tobacco. In the mouth, this is plump, chunky and full, but also a touch flat. Ripe plum, berry and chocolate flavors come with an oaky, lightly green aftertaste and a chocolaty finish.
title: Almacruz 2015 Single Vineyard Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: High-toned aromas of cherry and red plum mix with cinnamon and clove on the nose. Soft on the palate, with plenty of grip from building tannins, juicy red fruit carries to soft vanilla and earthy moss notes.
title: Anthony Nappa 2014 Reserve Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Earthy aromas include some black olive along with full berry notes. A firm palate with tannic grip tastes mostly of spiced plum and berry fruits, which are honest flavors for Chilean Cabernet. A solid finish fades out with a final note of brined olive.
title: Antiyal 2015 Pura Fe Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

Appellation: North Coast, North Coast, California, US
description: Fruity smelling, smooth and mouth-filling on the palate and full of ripe plums flavors, this full-bodied wine sourced from multiple northern California counties emphasizes richness.
title: Auburn 2016 Pinot Noir (North Coast)

Appellation: Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Bright and vibrant aromas of green apple, lime zest and powdered minerals carry to the light-bodied, dry palate. Tart orchard fruit shot through with lemon-lime acidity makes for a zesty yet balanced feel. Lime zest resounds on the finish.
title: Bellangelo 2015 Dry Riesling (Seneca Lake)

Appellation: Yolo County, Central Valley, California, US
description: This enjoyable wine is complex and intricate in flavor, blending ripe plum and sour cherry notes with black olive, black pepper and bay leaf nuances. It's medium-bodied, smooth in texture and not very tannic.
title: Berryessa Gap 2014 Tempranillo (Yolo County)

description: Savory plum and latex aromas are roasted, spicy and show some burn. This is a dark full-bodied Pinot that's chewy in feel; overall it's more like Syrah than your average Pinot Noir. Oaky, medicinal flavors of cherry and plum are round and beefy prior to a burnt tasting finish.
title: Bodega Montsecano 2016 Montsecano Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Grapy berry aromas are earthy and gamy, with a leathery topping. Cabernet, Carmenère and Syrah is the blend, while the mouthfeel is a touch wan. Plum, cassis, raspberry and tomato flavors are steady and taste lightly baked on the finish.
title: Bodegas Manutara 2013 Evolución Red (Maule Valley)

description: Just-ripe cherry and raspberry fruits open the nose of this restrained Pinot Noir, with touches of mocha and oak that lend depth. The medium-weight palate shows good balance between the ripe fruit flavors, ample acidity and fine tannins, while the finish boasts a kiss of warm, woody spice. Drink now–2020.
title: Boschendal 2015 Appellation Series Pinot Noir (Elgin)

description: Coming from one of the state's coolest vintages, this comes in at an alcohol level rarely seen in modern day reds in the state. The aromas are a bit unusual for this variety, with notes of herb, blue fruit and wood, along with a light floral note. The palate brings sleekly styled fruit flavors backed by tart acidity, tapering toward the finish. Light medicinal notes linger.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2011 Merlot (Washington)

description: The aromas bring generous notes of white peach, lime and quinine. It drinks dry, with plentiful stone fruit and citrus flavors, finishing with floral notes and a touch of bitterness.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2015 Gamache Vineyard Riesling (Washington)

description: A smoky, charred aroma and buttery, heavily toasted flavors clearly define this full-bodied Chardonnay. It's going for the full oak treatment with no apologies. Ripe pear and pineapple lurk underneath the smoke, clove and nutmeg overlay, and a bit of oak tannin combines with good acidity to give a welcome bite to the texture.
title: Butter 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: Moist underbrush, toasted nut, raw oak, dark spice and prune aromas follow through to the assertive palate along with dried cherry, espresso and star anise. A note of powdered sage marks the close. It has firm acidity and austere, close-grained tannins that clench the finish.
title: Castello di Monsanto 2013 Vigna Il Poggio Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This 16th-century castle is an impressive landmark in the region. The wine lives up to the location with its ripe black fruits and generous tannins. The dense structure will allow it to age. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château de la Terrière 2016  Brouilly

description: This wine comes from a vineyard called Siberia because it is so cold and late ripening. It is made without any sulfur added, hence the name Sauvage, wild. The wine is rich with upfront black fruits and some firm tannins as well as juicy acidity. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château de la Terrière 2016 Cuvée Vin Sauvage à Poil  (Régnié)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Dense on the nose in aromas of stewed, spiced berries and brisk pepper. The palate is fresher, with crunchy currant and bright blackberry shot through with streaks of granite. This is ample in weight, with grippy tannins that are held at bay by an unwavering river of acidity.
title: Chateau Lafayette Reneau 2013 Owner's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Contra Costa County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This massively structured wine from a new AVA within California's Contra Costa County is extremely oaky and extremely tannic, so people who love those attributes should love it. The aroma is like cedar and pencil shavings, and the wine tastes very concentrated, spicy and smoky. It has a grippy texture due to a high level of both wood and grape tannins that leave a drying sensation on the palate. Best after 2024.
title: Cinque Capre 2014 Petite Sirah (Contra Costa County)

description: Dry spicy aromas of tomato leaf and oak are fiery more than finessed or fruity. This is another 15% PN from Clos des Fous, and it's round, creamy and a bit low on acidity. Cherry, chocolaty oak and related barrel spice flavors finish full and plump.
title: Clos des Fous 2015 Pucalán Arenaria Pinot Noir (Aconcagua Costa)

description: Owner Dominique Morel makes this wine from a seven-acre parcel. It has red-cherry fruit, juicy acidity and a soft texture. Ripe and fruity, the wine is ready to drink.
title: Domaine de Gry-Sablon 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Fleurie)

Winery: Domaine de la Combe au Loup
description: The sand and granite soil of Regnié gives a soft style of wine. This ripe and fruity wine has a rounded texture and plenty of berry fruits. With attractive acidity and just a dash of tannin, it is ready to drink.
title: Domaine de la Combe au Loup 2016  Régnié

description: A firm layer of tannins give this wine structure and the potential to age. At this young stage, it lies over the juicy red fruits and acidity, although this will change over the next few months. This wine has the potential to be ripe and fruity; wait until mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Gaudets 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Clean and focused on the nose in tangerine, fresh red apple, and pear, these notes carry to the light-bodied palate, with delicate rose petals showing midway. Balanced acidity, a mild, chalky texture and lingering floral finish give this wine some cachet.
title: Dr. Konstantin Frank 2016 Gewürztraminer (Finger Lakes)

description: The rich 2015 vintage has given a ripe wine. With acidity as well as flavors of wood aging and plenty of red-cherry fruits, the wine is juicy and has a balanced tannic base. Drink now.
title: Emmanuel Fellot 2015 Les Cailloux  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: This is an easy-to-like, fruit-forward pour that offers notes of brambly berry and cherry hit by cocoa and cola spice. The palate is juicy and well-balanced, with easygoing tannins and a pleasant kiss of mocha on the close. Drink now.
title: Excelsior 2015 Syrah (Robertson)

description: Smooth and well-balanced, this medium-bodied wine has earthy spicy aromas, flavors that combine meaty, herbal nuances with red and black cherry and a mouthfeel that's relatively light and easy. It's time to drink up this well-aged wine.
title: Fenestra 2011 Tempranillo (Lodi)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Sweet tones of anise and cinnamon glide over a core of red fruit in this wine. The palate shows a mix of crunchy red berries, peppercorn and mint, with decent weight and bright acidity.
title: Fulkerson 2013 Estate Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of blackberry, dark plum, barrel spice and plum lead to super-soft-feeling fruit and barrel flavors that show a pretty styling. It comes up a bit short on the finish.
title: Gamache 2013 Estate Grown Malbec (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: One of Duboeuf's range of single-estate wines, this bottling is full, ripe while also having a light touch of acidity and berry fruits. Tannins are well integrated into this attractive, juicy wine that is ready to drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Château des Vierres  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This wine is soft for a Juliénas, with perfumed juicy, jammy red fruits and attractive acidity. It is rounded and generous, packed with fruit and only a hint of tannin. Drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Juliénas)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Perfumed violet and lavender blossoms accentuate preserved notes of plum and cherry in this full-bodied red. While moderate in concentration, it's rich and silky on the palate. The finish is marked by fine, feathery tannins and a bitter cocoa note.
title: H to H 2016 Homage to Heritage Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: From a two-acre parcel of 50-year-old vines, this is a rich wine. It has the structure of firm tannins, rich cherry fruits and smooth concentration. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Henry Fessy 2016  Saint-Amour

description: The Irish celebrity presenter's second vintage in his foray into winemaking offers a Sauvignon Blanc that is fleshy and gingery, with notes of watermelon, peach, and custard. The palate is fresh and focused, leading to a gingery finish.
title: Invivo 2017 Graham Norton's Own Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Importer: Vinum Wine Importing and Distributing LLC
description: Aromas of coconut, French oak, toast and a whiff of menthol carry over to the linear palate along with vanilla, licorice and dried sour cherry. Assertive, grainy tannins leave and astringent, almost raspy finish.
title: La Bioca 2014 Ronchi  (Barbaresco)

description: Opulent, very ripe fruit flavors fill this wine to the brim, from the aromas of strawberry jam to flavors of blackberry syrup. It's full bodied but light on tannins, so the mouthfeel is lush and soft.
title: Longvale 2015 Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: Waxy lemon skin and pressed-apple notes lend a broad, savory tone to this dry, full-bodied white. It's soft in acidity, with a supple, expansive mouthfeel, but hints of salty mineral and nut skins lend a bracing freshness to the finish.
title: Maison Denuzière 2016 Domaine des Hunaudieres White (Luberon)

description: Notes of moist soil, underbrush, black-skinned berry and an earthy whiff of game slowly take shape on this. The edgy, light-bodied palate is less expressive and rather lean in terms of fruit richness, offering dried cherry, licorice and powdered sage framed in racy acidity and close-grained tannins.
title: Mazzei 2014 Ser Lapo Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with aromas of underbrush, red plum and baking spice. The straightforward palate delivers raspberry jam, star anise and a hint of clove alongside round tannins.
title: Melini 2014 Terrarossa  (Chianti Classico)

description: An opaque color and oaky chocolaty aromas that include rubber and gamy berry fruits lead to a wide palate. Warm, candied plum and cassis flavors are chocolaty and finish with a swipe of oak and tobacco.
title: Montes 2015 Classic Series Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: This is an appealing, opulent wine with a bouquet of watermelon, stone fruit, wild strawberry and white pepper. The palate is full-bodied and viscous in plenty of fruit.
title: Mt. Difficulty 2015 Bannockburn Pinot Gris (Central Otago)

description: Fresh tasting, dry and well balanced, this wine doesn't go overboard but has everything in reasonable balance, including a buttery appley aroma, and good acidity to bolster the fruit flavors and add a little bite to the texture.
title: Murphy-Goode 2015 Chardonnay (California)

description: A dark and hugely full-bodied wine, this blends 54% Cabernet Sauvignon, 19% Malbec, 15% Zinfandel and 12% Merlot. Concentrated blackberry flavor is seasoned in tobacco, clove and cedar, the oak pronounced and tannins tight, as heat vibrates on the finish.
title: Muscardini 2013 Cassata Vineyards Reserve Red (Sonoma Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Rounded, plush and extremely tropical aromas of white peach, cantaloupe and poached pear show on the nose of this bottling. The sip tightens up a bit, then shows coconut oil, tuberose and more poached pear. Cleanly made, but quite ripe.
title: Pear Valley 2016 Pear Valley Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Paso Robles)

description: Aromas of ripe red berry, violet and new leather come together in the glass. The fleeting palate is rather easygoing for Barolo, offering Morello cherry and baking spice alongside fresh acidity and grainy but not very persistent tannins that leave a dusty finish.
title: Piazzo Armando 2013  Barolo

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône Villages, Rhône Valley, France
description: Over-the-top ripeness lends a slightly confected tone to this bold, juicy red. Flavors of candied black cherry and strawberry jam are peppered with spice, vanilla and licorice. It's a flamboyantly fruity, easy wine to enjoy now for its exuberance and youth.
title: Pierre Henri Morel 2014 Signargues Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: Tight-grained tannin wrap around moderate ripeness in this approachable, softly rounded wine. Sanguine, it offers cassis, current, soy and graphite flavors that don't ever overcomplicate.
title: Press Run 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma County)

description: Prickly single-plane aromas of grass and citrus fruits set up a lean tight palate that's pushing lemon-lime and grassy herbal flavors. A lasting finish with acidity and spunk earns this some extra points. Drink now.
title: Prisma 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: Snappy citrus aromas show mild pyrazine-based scents on a typical Casablanca nose. A standard palate is a touch watery, while this tastes of mild citrus fruits and green melon. A briny tasting finish is low on volume and length.
title: Santa Rita 2017 Estate Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Crushed thyme, oregano, anise and dill decorate the roasted blackberry fruit on the nose of this bottling. The palate is juicy with fresh and accessible fruits, though the herb qualities and hint of bell pepper are more prominent.
title: Sean Minor 2016 Four Bears Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Spicy earthy berry aromas carry heft as well as herbal green notes. A jammy concentrated palate tips the scales as big, while this tastes of spicy minty blackberry. On the finish, an herbal minty character is unceasing.
title: Siegel 2015 Special Reserve Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: There's a mix of lemongrass, apricot, yellow apple and dusty mineral aromas on the nose. The palate is juicy and ripe in the same fruit tones, with an addition of blossomy floral notes entering on the midpalate. Semidry in style and with a crisp apple-peel finish, this Riesling shows immediate appeal.
title: Silver Thread 2016 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This tight red offers aromas of toasted oak, vanilla and black plum while the dense palate offers prune, dark chocolate and licorice. It's somewhat austere, with taut tannins.
title: Tenuta di Arceno 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of French oak, coconut, toast and balsamic notes set the tone. The tight palate offers dried cherry, prune and clove framed in astringent, grainy tannins that leave a raspy finish.
title: Tenuta San Mauro 2013  Barbaresco

description: Briny citrus, asparagus and mango aromas cover all ends of the Chilean SB spectrum. A palate with zesty acidity deals green-citrus flavors that carry over to the finish, which is spunky. As to whether this justifies the high price, that's up to you.
title: Tierra Calma 2017 Sauvignon Blanc (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Citrus spray and delicate peach aromas are behind a thin veil of pollen and yeast tones on the nose. The light-bodied palate shows a bit more clarity in its cleaner citrus and stone-fruit flavors, yet ultimately this is a straightforward and accessible semidry Riesling.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2015 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: This unoaked Chardonnay has a mild intensity of underripe peach and citrus on the nose, with a muddling of white flowers. The palate lags a bit with soft acidity, yet ripe peach chugs along to the finish.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2015 Unoaked Cuvée Classique Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Slightly weak aromas of pomegranate-laced fruit punch, herbs and dust show on the nose of this bottling. Strawberry and fennel flavors are evident on the palate.
title: Firefly Ridge 2015 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: Soapy citrus and cantaloupe aromas are rather bland. This is quite high in acidity, while the wine's lean body struggles to support that raciness. Mild stone-fruit and citrus flavors are lightly bitter on a tangy finish.
title: Garces Silva 2017 Boya Rosé (Leyda Valley)

description: Soft although with a crisp edge, this wine offers flavors of red cherry and red currant. Plenty of acidity gives the wine its fresh character. It has a tangy, lightly tannic aftertaste.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Brouilly)

description: Bright, brisk and fruity, this is a light wine. Soft tannins contrast attractively with the red-cherry juiciness. Drink now.
title: Jean-François Coquard 2016 Les Pierres Dorées  (Beaujolais)

description: Largely neutral white-fruit aromas include a hint of citrus. On the palate, this SB feels a bit flat and fleshy, with mild acidity. Standard apple and orange flavors are salty across a basic finish.
title: Lapostolle 2016 Grand Selection Sauvignon Blanc (Rapel Valley)

description: With a straw-gold hue, this is a creamy, full-bodied wine, with an unusual medley of asparagus, tomato stalk, ginger and lemon notes. On the palate, the acidity is nicely balanced but the style is rather brash and odd.
title: Loveblock 2014 Pinot Gris (Marlborough)

description: Hickory-cured meat, sweet smoke, mocha, raspberry compote and berry preserves all dance on the nose of this decadent value wine. The palate offers similar concentration, with fine tannins and a strong dose of blackberry-laced coffee on the close. Drink now.
title: Robertson Winery 2015 Pinotage (Robertson)

description: A raw nose with green-herb and bell pepper aromas offers just enough cohesion and fruit to be decent. In the mouth, this is wiry and racy on first impression, then soft. Leafy herbal berry and prune flavors culminate on a vacuous finish.
title: Tres Palacios 2014 Family Vintage Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

description: This extremely dark and concentrated wine has aromas like smoked ham plus peppery smoky flavors that push the limit of style, along with very obvious tannins that leave a drying impression on the palate.
title: Vinum Cellars 2015 Petite Sirah (Clarksburg)

description: If you're looking for a basic white quaffing wine that's priced to sell but won't wow, this is it. Oily aromas of almond skins come with hints of apple and pear. Modest acidity and nutty, yeasty flavors of salt and bitter melon finish with signs of early oxidation.
title: Dancing Flame 2017 Ojos del Salado Pinot Grigio (Central Valley)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: A very light nose conveys purple fruit, dried herbs and a bit of bell pepper on the nose of this bottling. Pasilla chile shows on the sip alongside boysenberry, thyme and wood spice.
title: Firefly Ridge 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Coast)

description: As simple and fruity as a Beaujolais can be, this is light, bright and full of red cherries. Acidity and tangy texture make the wine totally drinkable now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Flower Label  (Beaujolais)

description: Melony vanilla aromas are flat, while this mature SB feels plump and fleshy, but distant. Wheaty bland flavors of white melon lack pop and vitality, while this tastes and feels bready on a tired finish.
title: Lapostolle 2015 Cuvée Alexandre Viñedo Las Kuras Sauvignon Blanc (Cachapoal Valley)

description: A neutral nose shows a hint of citrus but that's it. This feels more plump and flabby than well cut, but it's not outright flat. Chunky melony citrus flavors end with low acidity. Drink this value-priced quaffer with caution.
title: Luis Felipe Edwards 2017 Loma Negra Rosé (Central Valley)

description: A definitive green nose smells just short of vegetal, while a round, plump palate with good acidity is a high point for this Elqui SB. A vegetal flavor of canned beans doesn't shed the wine's core green character.
title: Mayu 2017 Sauvignon Blanc (Elqui Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Lightly volatile aromas of baking spice, black licorice, cherry and vanilla lead to sweet, pruney fruit and barrel flavors, with the barrel notes riding high on the finish. It seems quite evolved for its age.
title: Rio Vista 2014 River View Grand Reserve Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A rusty color and lean, almost grassy aromas set up a tannic but solid palate. Consistency of fruit flavor is lacking as this tastes like leafy plum and pepper prior to a slightly oxidized finish.
title: Aresti 2014 Bellavista Reserva Pinot Noir (Curicó Valley)

description: Stalky wheaty aromas register as underripe. This Chardonnay feels bland and thin, with yeasty melon flavors that finish without much flavor. Tasted twice with similar findings.
title: Clos des Fous 2015 Locura 1 Terroir de Los Andes Chardonnay (Chile)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Subdued on the nose, slight indications of ripe peach and pollen carry to a citrus-driven palate. This is light in intensity and complexity, with a sweet-tart notion on the finish.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2015 Barrel Select Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

description: This starts off somewhat shy and closed, with muted notes of tight black-plum skin, tree bark and black olive. The medium-weight mouth offers a bit more character, with waves of roasted plum and blackberry that finish with a slightly bitter, charred oak edge. Drink now.
title: Excelsior 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Robertson)

description: Berry fruit aromas are slightly damp and oaky. This feels choppy and chunky, with wiry acidity. Oak and herbal plum flavors finish simply, with a leftover smear of artificial oak.
title: G7 2016 The 7th Generation Estate Bottled Cabernet Sauvignon (Loncomilla Valley)

description: A true-grit nose smells like you're trailing a road crew laying down asphalt. The palate is rather chunky and beefy, with grilled flavors of meat, pepper and burnt leaves. Fresh fruit is just not part of the package, and that's a problem.
title: Garces Silva 2014 Amayna Pinot Noir (Leyda Valley)

description: Waxy white-fruit aromas are mild and simple. On the palate, this blend of 60% Chardonnay and 40% Viognier is wide, oily and grainy in feel. Savory salty green melon and banana flavors are lightly oxidized on the finish.
title: Oveja Negra 2016 Reserva Chardonnay-Viognier (Maule Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Pale watermelon in color, this wine is straight to the point with its melon and raspberry tones against a slight green herb backdrop. The fresh fruit note turns a bit sour on the finish.
title: 240 Days 2016 Rosé (Finger Lakes)

description: Rusty in color, with tomato, herb and raw-currant aromas, this is shear on the palate and raging with acidity. Flavors of hard spices, lamb fat and herbal red plum just make it to the level of good, while this is tangy and sharp on the finish.
title: Apaltagua 2014 Coleccion Limited Edition Pinot Noir (San Antonio)

description: Built to be simple and cheery, at a solid price point, this wine is indeed simple and soft, with flavors of charred oak, soy, black cherry and an intensely herbaceous character.
title: Cannonball 2015 Merlot (Sonoma County)

description: Oily, slightly vegetal aromas are lacking in pop and purity. On the palate, this is similar to the nose: flat with no cut or angles. A mix of grass and melon flavors finishes mildly bitter.
title: Dancing Flame 2017 Chaiten Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: Named after the village in which the producer has its winery, this wine is all fruit. Red berries and hints of banana give a wine that is crisp, laced with acidity and ready to drink.
title: Domaine de Gry-Sablon 2016 Emeringes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Reticent at first, this opens up to dense aromas of blackberry, black cherry, moss and dried leaves. There's good tension and weight to the palate, yet the impression is lighter in feel than the nose suggests. Crunchy red berries and streaks of granite lead to a peppery, dry finish.
title: Inspire Moore 2014 Wisdom Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: This wine is powerful, dense and has a solidly tannic structure. It also offers bold swathes of ripe red cherries and balanced acidity. The wine is almost ready to drink, just wait until mid-2018.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Belles Grives  (Morgon)

description: Still very young, this wine that celebrates the windmill (Moulin-à-Vent) of the appellation is bright purple in color, full of boisterous cherry flavors and shows just the right amount of tannic structure. A small amount of oak aging gives a smooth edge to the wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Au Beau Moulin  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Produced from vines planted on the cool, shallow-granite soil of Chiroubles, this wine is balanced, ripe and has some fine tannins. It is smoothly textured, generous with its acidity and red fruits and ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Le Moulin  (Chiroubles)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: With aeration and time, initial whiffs of smoke and earth give way to fresher herb, lemon and lime. Rounded edges and plump texture accentuate this wine's full-bodied feel but lively citrus notes maintain freshness. The finish is moderately long and a tad warming. Drink now through 2019.
title: La Ferme du Mont 2014 La Truffière White (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a strong density of dark fruits on the nose of this clonal selection, along with muddy clay, crushed slate and fennel pollen. The palate is thick with boysenberry jam, star anise and vanilla, though the strong flavors are competing rather than collaborating.
title: Lafond 2014 Pommard Clone Lafond Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Bright, fresh, fruity aromas and flavors are supported by a good firm texture in this very clean, tangy wine. It has a bracing mouthfeel, plenty of red cherry and raspberry flavors and light to medium body.
title: Lois Rae 2015 Grenache (El Dorado)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Plush, plummy red-cherry and berry flavors mingle into smokier tones of anise and earth in this full-bodied red. It's approachably ripe yet crisply balanced, with a finish marked by firm, velvet-edged tannins.
title: M. Chapoutier 2016 Belleruche Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: The nose on this distinctive Syrah is a mélange of beef teriyaki, aniseed, stewed raspberry and cherry aromas. Oak influence is marked, particularly on the palate, where the juicy red fruit notes and star anise are slightly overwhelmed by a dried bark finish. Nevertheless, this is a savory style many will enjoy.
title: Maimai 2014 Syrah (Hawke's Bay)

description: This opens with scents reminiscent of ripe plum, dark culinary spice, balsamic notes and a whiff of espresso. The austere palate is vibrant but a bit lean in terms of fruit richness, offering dried cherry, licorice and coffee bean framed in bright acidity and assertive, close-grained tannins that grip the finish. Give it a few more years to come around then drink.
title: Ormanni 2013 Borro del Diavolo Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Oozing with blackberry jam aroma and flavor, this medium-bodied wine has a very rich, lightly sweet mouthfeel, tons of blackberry and boysenberry on the palate and a toasty, spicy, lingering finish.
title: OZV 2015 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: This is a balanced, structured wine. With ripe tannins and immediately attractive spice and blackberry fruit, it is a warm wine that also has a more serious, textured side. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Pardon et Fils 2016 Les Mouilles  (Juliénas)

description: From the smallest of the Beaujolais crus, this wine brings out the structured nature from the hilly vineyard sites. The wine is solid and firm, crisp with black cherries and black currant. It should age further, so drink from mid-2018.
title: Pascal Aufranc 2016 En Rémont Vignes de 1939  (Chénas)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Juicy black cherries and plums abound in this plush, velvety red. It's ripe and full bodied yet brightly balanced in acidity. Firm tannins and savory herb tones linger elegantly on the finish. Drink now through 2020.
title: Pierre Amadieu 2015 Roulepierre Red (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Striking tones of roasted coffee, cocoa and toasty oak ride over a dense core of ripe dark cherry and plum. The palate is silken in feel, with a plush, round texture supported by grainy tannins and bright acidity. More oak shows in the flavors, yet it's integrated among bright cherry, dark currant and granite tones which lead to a lingering dark cocoa finish.
title: Raphael 2014 First Label Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Bursting aromas of cranberries and red cherries provide a core for a dusting of vanilla, cocoa and herbal spice tones. Medium in weight and well balanced by sandy tannins and bright acidity, the palate is juicy in red fruit, with shades of granite and pepper offering levity.
title: Red Newt Cellars 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Sierra Pelona Valley, South Coast, California, US
description: This is the strongest showing yet from this region northeast of Los Angeles, with aromas of berry, hickory smoke, slate and rust on the nose. There is a rustic quality to the sip, with fennel seed-laced salami and macerated plum flavors.
title: Reyes 2015 Grenache (Sierra Pelona Valley)

description: Warm and generous, this is a ripe wine from a steep village behind the hill of Brouilly. The slope gives the wine its hellish name because it is so tough to work. This structure wine offers a core of tannins as well as plenty of black fruits and spice. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Robert Perroud 2016 L'Enfer des Balloquets  (Brouilly)

description: A tropical touch of ripe mango hovers on the nose of this wine. The palate also has abundant fruit: ripe citrus with hints of juicy pear, yellow plum and again a notion of mango. This is light-footed yet full-fruited and made for easy drinking.
title: Ruttenstock 2016 Klassik Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Baked blueberry and black cherry meet with black licorice and fresh wisteria florality on the nose of this bottling. The tannic grip is apparent right away on the sip, and a bit strong, but it carries fresh and vibrant black-fruit flavors.
title: Santa Barbara Winery 2014 Curtis Vineyards Petit Verdot (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: A rich malty set of aromas is balsamic in a good way, with ripe notes of cassis making the nose not hard to like. A saturated tough palate is fully extracted, maybe a little more so than ideal. Blackened berry and plum flavors finish toasty and peppery, with the fullest of weight making for a chunky final impression.
title: Siegel 2013 Unique Selection Red (Colchagua Costa)

description: This opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, scorched earth, and cooking spice. The lively palate doles out raspberry, black pepper and licorice alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Tenuta di Nozzole 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine was aged in stainless steel—a rarity for a red in Washington. Aromas of herb, pepper, plum and dark fruit are followed by plump fruit flavors that paint a bit around the edges. There's much to enjoy here, though the middle seems somewhat missing. Put it on the dinner table to see it at its best.
title: Wind Rose 2014 Dolcetto (Yakima Valley)

description: This is a savory, smoky Pinot, reminiscent of charred greens along with red currents and dried flowers. It's medium-bodied, with a soft grip of salty tannins and bright red fruits.
title: Yealands 2016 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Like a home-baked apple pie without the sweetness, this light-bodied wine smells of sliced apples, nutmeg and cinnamon and tastes fruity and lightly spicy. The texture is very smooth and gentle, too.
title: Cabana Wines 2014 Chardonnay (Lodi)

description: Aromas of toast, vanilla and violet are front and center on this 100% Sangiovese. The concentrated palate doles out black cherry jam, star anise and licorice alongside pliant tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Casaloste 2011 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, blue flower and white pepper. The vibrant palate doles out ripe black cherry, star anise and a hint of espresso alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Cecchi 2014 Riserva di Famiglia  (Chianti Classico)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese, 5% Colorino and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, this opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, leather and white pepper. The juicy palate doles out ripe red cherry, clove, star anise and a touch of espresso alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Cecchi 2015 Storia di Famiglia  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine is ripe, full and juicy from 30-year-old vines. It is packed with rich tannins and black-cherry flavors that are generous and full in the mouth. This warm, well-made wine is ready to drink.
title: Château de Lacarelle 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this has subdued aromas of baking spice, meat juice and black-skinned fruit. The firm palate offers black cherry, raspberry jam and clove alongside tightly knit tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Colle Bereto 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This offers subdued aromas evoking red berry and crushed mint that carry over to the somewhat dilute, simple palate along with a hint of star anise. It has firm acidity while dusty, rather fleeting tannins dry out the finish.
title: San Silvestro 2014 Magno  (Barbaresco)

description: Earthy mossy plum and berry aromas are herbal and saucy. This basic Carmenère feels plump and round, but soft. Herbal peppery notes accent generic berry flavors, while this feels heavy and a bit clunky on the finish.
title: Viña Chocalan 2014 Gran Reserva Origen Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

description: This is a very pleasant well-proportioned wine that goes from ripe pear aromas to full expressive peach and pear flavors carried on a lush smooth texture. It's soft on the palate and low in acidity.
title: California Republic 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: Soapy berry aromas are tomatoey and not that stout. This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenère, Malbec and Cab Franc feels wide and a bit flat. Raisiny berry flavors are slightly oxidized, while this is thick but quick on the finish.
title: Casa Donoso 2016 Clos Centenaire Red (Maule Valley)

description: This wine from 40-year-old vines is packed with crisp red fruits. It has acidity, red-currant and red-cherry flavors and only the lightest tannin texture. It is ready to drink.
title: Château de Corcelles 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Coffee and cocoa aromas are front and center, with softer tones of red fruit in the background. Crisp and light in feel, the palate is brisk and refreshing with it's tart red fruit flavors, and peppery spice dotting the finish.
title: Chateau Lafayette Reneau 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

description: Attractive dark-fruit flavors and a smooth texture lend a lot of appeal to this medium-bodied wine despite a reduced, earthy character in the aroma at first, which is not uncommon in screw-capped wines.
title: CK Mondavi 2015 Merlot (California)

description: This opens with fleeting aromas reminiscent of leather, dried herb and a whiff of dark spice. The no-frills palate offers hints of sour cherry and star anise alongside dusty, transient tannins.
title: Collina San Ponzio 2013 Fossati  (Barolo)

description: Reduced cherry aromas are basic, while this feels condensed and solid. Cherry and cassis flavors veer towards medicinal, while this easygoing Cab leaves spice notes on the finish after the fruit has faded.
title: Cuyen 2016 Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Valley)

description: This wine come from the far south of the Beaujolais region, an area known as Pierre Dorées (golden stones) from the color of the buildings. The wine tastes golden in its own way with flavors of yellow plums and light acidity to balance the generous texture. Drink the wine now.
title: Domaine Chasselay 2016 Terroir de Châtillon   (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: This fruity wine has plenty of crisp apple and light apricot flavors. It is tangy, zesty and has refreshing acidity. Simple and open, it is ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016  Beaujolais Blanc

description: This bright, cherry-flavored wine is crisp, with a light texture and plenty of acidity. It is a classic juicy and fruity Beaujolais, ready to drink young.
title: Domaine Dupeuble Père et Fils 2016  Beaujolais

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: A delicate nose of strawberry, raspberry and cola leads to mild red-fruit and black pepper notes on the palate. Light in body, but silken in feel, the tannins are barely perceptible.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2014 Barrel Select Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Shy lemon melds with slate and pollen on the nose of this light-bodied wine. Initially citrus-driven on the palate, riper hints of tree fruits show midpalate, but are slightly flat from a soft vein of acidity.
title: Domaine LeSeurre 2015 Dry Cuvée Classique Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas bring unusual notes of grape cough syrup, smoke, herb and cherry Jolly Rancher. The palate falls flat, painting around the edges.
title: Eleven 2015 Mourvèdre (Yakima Valley)

description: This wine has some depth and richness while preserving the crisp fruit of the vintage. Cherries, red currants and plenty of fruity acidity are the wine's light, easy hallmark. Drink now.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée B  (Beaujolais-Villages)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Baked cherry, pine forest and earthy compost combine in this medium-bodied lightly oaked wine from a vineyard planted to multiple clones of the variety. Native fermentation lets the fruit and spice speak quietly.
title: Friedeman 2015 UV Lancel Creek Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Smoke and streaks of slate, blend with a brooding sanguine note on the nose of this wine. Aged in French oak, there's a acidic pleasant structure to the medium-bodied palate, with racy red currant and shoots of granite balanced by mild tannins. Drink now.
title: Glenora 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: Oaky and extroverted, this full-bodied wine smells lightly smoky and full of baking spices. The flavors resemble sweet cherries, strawberry jam and a touch of maple.
title: Gnarly Head 2015 Merlot (California)

description: Extremely fruity jammy aromas and flavors ride a soft juicy texture in this smooth easy-drinking wine. It's low in tannins and acidity, and has a sweet accent like maple syrup.
title: Hunted 2015 Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: Earthy mushroom and leather greet the nose and palate in this combination of mostly Cabernet Sauvignon and Malbec, with smaller amounts of Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc. Tannic, with a softer landing on the finish, it offers heightened flavors of plum and black cherry.
title: Jon Nathaniel 2014 Bodacious Red (Napa Valley)

description: With earthy aromas, this is a simple, soft wine. It has some black fruits as well as light tannins and an aftertaste with berry and spice flavors. Drink this open wine now.
title: Les Vins Aujoux 2016 Bonne Neige  (Brouilly)

description: This wine is soft, with full cherry flavors and an attractive layer of dusty tannins. Ripe and packed with fruit, it's totally ready to drink now.
title: Louis Tête 2016  Brouilly

description: Mild berry aromas are somewhat flat and rubbery. This medium-bodied Pinot is low on acidity and thus not that bright or vital on the palate. Woody, savory berry flavors are oaky on the finish.
title: Lucatoni 2014 Reserve Pinot Noir (Maule Valley)

description: A boisterous smoky oaky streak dominates this full-bodied wine from the aromas of charred meat and maple syrup to flavors of blackberry syrup and smoked bacon. It's an extreme treatment that not everyone will love, but it stands out in a group of purely fruity wines.
title: Moss Roxx 2014 Ancient Vine Reserve Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: A little shy at first, lime leaf, lemon and peach aromas creep their way out of the glass. The medium-bodied palate offers a slippery texture and a lemony finish.
title: Nobilo 2016 Regional Collection Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: An obvious stamp of oak spices and butter defines this medium to full-bodied wine as it goes from butterscotch aromas to vanilla and eggnog flavors that have a sweet edge. The texture is smooth and rich.
title: One Leaf 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: A dark color and heavy aromas of savory berry fruits, leather and animal are less than elegant. This is more soupy, sticky and ripe than most Itata Cinsaults. Candied red-fruit flavors finish on the hot side due to 14% abv.
title: Pedro Parra y Familia 2015 Imaginador Cinsault (Itata Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of near equal parts Syrah and Sangiovese. Barrel notes are at the fore, with aromas of baking spice and vanilla far out front of the fruit. The palate brings easygoing plum flavors that are concentrated in the middle and light around the edges.
title: Plumb Cellars 2013 Plumb Crazy Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Dried blueberries, thick blackberry jam and gingerbread aromas show on the nose of this older bottling from the Agua Dulce region. The palate is thick and tarry with flavors of charred beef and more blueberries. It's still alive, at least.
title: Reyes 2009 Merlot

description: These vines, planted by the current owner's family in 1940, give a richly concentrated wine. Its dense texture contrasts with generous black fruits and crisp acidity to make a warm, rich wine that is ready to drink.
title: Jean-Michel Dupré 2016 Vieilles Vignes de 1940  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Made with Sangiovese and 5% Canaiolo, this opens with aromas of iris, black-skinned berry and leafy underbrush. The juicy palate delivers black cherry, clove and white pepper alongside brisk acidity and polished tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Le Bocce 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Hugely concentrated in big blackberry and currant fruit, this wine also offers mountainous tannin weight and grip. Pencil shavings, bitter leather and tobacco and a sizable imprint of savory oak demand attention in the glass.
title: Ledson 2013 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Howell Mountain)

description: This wine has the typical soft, round character of Brouilly although a good backdrop of tannins gives it shape. Coming from old vines, it has density as well as plenty of crisp black fruits. Drink this wine now.
title: Les Frères Perroud 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Brouilly)

description: Pretty white blossom and chalky mineral tones lend freshness to soft apple and pear in this full-bodied but elegant white wine. It's soft and rounded on the palate but balanced by delicate acidity and a lingering mineral finish.
title: Marrenon 2015 Doria White (Luberon)

description: Aromas of burnt popcorn, mocha, raspberry sauce and brambly all-fruit preserves lead the nose on this rich and decadent pour. The palate is similarly plush and inviting, with a satiny texture and pillowy tannins that provide subtle framing to the ripe black-fruit flavors. The rich finish entices with notes of berry compote and sweet spice.
title: Neethlingshof 2015 Pinotage (Stellenbosch)

description: Moist earth, game, menthol and dark spice aromas lead the nose. The earthy and spice aromas follow over to the linear palate along with tart sour cherry, orange zest and a hint of coffee bean. Firm acidity and dusty tannins provide the backbone.
title: Podere Ciona 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Dusty floral aromas of raspberry and cherry are what Itata's natural red wines are all about. This blend of Cinsault with 10% País is plush and ripe on the palate. Lightly medicinal flavors of plum, currant, spice and sandalwood finish with a grabby feel.
title: Rogue Vine 2015 Grand Itata Red (Itata Valley)

description: A deep color and potent aromas of blackberry and black currant are the first impressions from this full-bodied, firmly structured and well concentrated wine. They lead to a palate full of deep blackberry flavors, a nicely dry balance and mouth-filling texture.
title: Shooting Star 2015 Merlot (Lake County)

description: This wine is fruity in lime and passionfruit tones, with green bell pepper and a hint of salty minerality. The palate carries a chalky texture and well-balanced acidity, finishing with a zip of lime zest.
title: Sileni 2016 Straits Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Vibrant aromas of underripe peach and apple blossom focus around a core of lemon-lime on the nose. There's a roundness to the dry palate, where ample acidity outlines crunchy green apple and apricot. A clean saline note lingers on the finish.
title: Silver Thread 2016 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas are light on first pour, opening up over time with notes of herb, cherry, wood spice and black licorice. The ripe currant flavors are a bit linear in style, but show polish and appeal, seeming ever so slightly dried out.
title: Substance 2014 Me Vineyard Collection Stoneridge Vineyard Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Candied cherry dominates in this densely concentrated wine, its tannins pronounced. Rose petal aromatics contribute a savory underbrush added to by forest, black tea and cardamom characteristics, as lasting acidity provides freshness.
title: Ancien 2015 Toyon Farm Pinot Noir (Carneros)

Appellation: North Coast, North Coast, California, US
description: Very ripe fruit aromas, a touch of volatility and a bold powerful set of flavors make this full-bodied wine dramatic to taste. It has a firm structure, too, based on good acidity and a considerable layer of fine-grained tannins that easily support all the black cherry and pomegranate flavors.
title: AuburnJames 2013 Cabernet Franc (North Coast)

description: This nearly 100% varietal wine, with a tiny smattering of Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot, is given plenty of time in new oak, a distinction that's noted on the palate. Smoky char and meaty bacon are prominently displayed, as black cherry and blackberry mix along a brawny landscape of ripeness and full body.
title: Ballentine 2015 Pocai Vineyard Estate Grown Cabernet Franc (Calistoga)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Spry citrus and yellow apple aromas lead to a resounding, juicy palate of peach and red apple. Semidry in style, this shows a deft balance of ripeness and acidity, with a light powdery texture on the finish.
title: Barnstormer 2016 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Contra Costa County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a big, sturdy, and firmly textured wine that smells like oak spices, tastes like raspberries and milk chocolate and has a relatively easy mouthfeel for this notoriously tannic varietal.
title: Campos Family Vineyards 2015 Estate Petite Sirah (Contra Costa County)

description: This opens with delicate aromas of pressed blue flower and a woodland berry. The vibrant, easygoing palate offers candied cherry, juicy orange slice and a star anise note set against supple tannins and bright acidity. While it doesn't posses the complexity one expects from a Riserva, it is bright in savory. Drink through 2022.
title: Castellare 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Ripe plum, black-skinned berry and pressed violet aromas take center stage. The no-frills but savory palate offers fleshy Morello cherry and ground clove set against solid tannins. Drink through 2019.
title: Castello di Radda 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Streaks of tobacco, resin and earth offset bright, fresh blackberry and plum in this rich, boldly concentrated wine. Voluminous and a touch warming on the palate, it's framed by sunny acidity and fine-grained but grippy tannins.
title: Château d'Or et de Gueules 2015 Castel Nou Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Aromas of French oak, coconut, scorched earth and underbrush lead the way. Reflecting the nose, the taut palate shows vanilla, oak-driven spice, licorice and candied red cherry framed in assertive, close-grained tannins. A coffee bean note lingers on the firm, drying finish.
title: Ciabot Berton 2012 Roggeri  (Barolo)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this opens with aromas of cassis, baking spice and vanilla. The fruity palate delivers blackberry jam, star anise and a touch of nutmeg set against fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Coli 2013 Montignana Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Designation: Domaine des Hospices Civils de Lyon
description: The vineyard is owned by a charitable foundation that gives money to local hospitals in Lyon. It is a rich wine, generous with both fruit and tannins, giving ripeness as well as crisp acidity. The texture is soft and smooth and the wine is ready to drink.
title: Collin-Bourisset 2016 Domaine des Hospices Civils de Lyon  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Aromas of blue flower, red-skinned berry and a touch of eucalyptus lead the nose. The bright palate offers red cherry, star anise and clove alongside polished tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Dievole 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Softly rich and packed with fruit, this wine is generous and ripe. It has minerality and a light texture of tannins. The wine's dense fruit and acidity are balanced and ready to drink.
title: Domaine de La Grume 2016 La Grume Grain de Sable  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Bouches du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Swirls of caramel, cream and spice perfume this unusual blend of Chasan, Sauvignon Blanc and Gros Manseng grapes. While dry and light in body, it's a striking white wine with flavors of orange cream and burnt sugar amplified by sunny tangerine acidity. Drink now through 2019.
title: Domaine du Mas de Rey 2016 Cœur de Camargue White (Bouches du Rhône)

description: Rich with a dark, firm texture, this is a dense wine. Black fruits, generous tannins and spice are ripe and full. The touch of pepper on the palate hints at the higher alcohol of the wine. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Raphaël Chopin 2016 Le Ronze  (Régnié)

description: Aromas reminiscent of camphor, new leather and exotic spice lead the nose. The tightly wound palate, brooding palate offers dried black cherry and clove set against close-grained tannins. Drink sooner rather than later to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Felsina 2015  Chianti Classico

description: From the Charmes sector of Morgon, this concentrated wine has spiced almond and toast flavors along with warm black cherries. The tannins are still firm giving a dry core. Drink from 2019.
title: Ferraud et Fils 2016 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: Ripe but slightly muted cherry and plum flavors are accented by hints of olive and bramble. This wine is not deeply concentrated on the palate but juicy and fresh, with fine, feathery tannins accentuating its easy style. Drink now through 2020.
title: Gabriel Meffre 2014 Laurus Grenache-Syrah (Vacqueyras)

description: This Beaune-based négociant has made this wine in a Burgundian style, with oak fermentation and aging in vats for up to nine months. The result is a rich wine that will age. Its tannins, spice and rich structure speak to the wood flavors. The fruit balances that, giving richness and concentration. Drink the wine from 2019.
title: Albert Bichot 2016 La Madone  (Fleurie)

description: This robust red is 80% Sangiovese, 15% Merlot and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon. It opens with aromas of black currant, cedar and a hint of violet while the firmly structured palate offers dried black cherry, vanilla, licorice and a touch of mocha. Assertive but polished tannins provide support.
title: Barone Ricasoli 2015 Brolio Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very light in the glass, this bottling from an old vineyard shows candied pomegranate, sage and thyme on the delicate nose. There is solid grip to the sip, with tangy cranberry and a pinch of white pepper. It is a great red wine for a sunny day.
title: Birichino 2016 Saint Georges Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: New leather, dark spice, truffle and fleshy plum aromas take center stage. On the well-structured palate, close-knit, polished tannins support mature black cherry, clove and star anise. Drink through 2020.
title: Cafaggio 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Grass, melon and peach highlight a texture that's creamy and fleshy, with a well-honed balance of lingering acidity. Medium bodied, the wine offers additional flavors of dried herb and nutmeg.
title: Captûre 2016 Les Pionniers Sauvignon Blanc (Alexander Valley)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a great amount of minerality on the nose of this bottling, with crushed slate and muddy soil aromas overcoming the mint herb and red-fruit scents. There's minty lift right away on the sip, with bay leaf, fennel pollen and dusty leather flavors decorating the boysenberry fruit.
title: Carmel Road 2014 North Crest Panorama Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)

description: Aromas of French oak, baked plum, espresso and a whiff of vanilla carry over to the smooth palate together with ripe cherry and a hint of star anise. Fine-grained tannins offer polished support.
title: Castello di Radda 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of forest floor, prune, ground clove, a balsamic note and whiffs of exotic spice are front and center. The tight palate offers dried black cherry, espresso, sage, star anise and oak alongside close-grained tannins that grip the finish.
title: Castello Vicchiomaggio 2013 La Prima Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Whispers of smoked nuts, vanilla and spice highlight savory tones of pressed yellow apple and pear in this richly textured white. Its delicate fruit flavors drink elegantly now but should develop greater densities of earth and mineral with age.
title: Cave de Tain 2015 Terre d'Ivoire  (Saint-Joseph)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: A pleasantly waxy slick enriches flavors of fresh apple, pear and lemon in this ripe, richly textured white. Hints of spice and saline minerality linger elegantly on the finish.
title: Château de Nages 2015 JT White (Costières de Nîmes)

description: From the Chasselay family's négociant business, this single-vineyard wine is lightly structured and full of blackberries. It has a future with its tannins and its mineral edge that need to soften. Drink this balanced wine from late 2018.
title: Claire & Fabien Chasselay 2016 La Carrière  (Chénas)

description: A few swirls of the glass release aromas of truffle, scorched earth, menthol and star anise. On the structured palate, assertive, close-grained tannins weave through dried Morello cherry, licorice and clove. Drink 2022–2030.
title: Costa di Bussia 2012 Bussia Vigna dei Buoi  (Barolo)

description: Whiffs of black-cherry pie and strawberry yogurt lend a primary, fruity feel to this voluptuous red. While soft in acidity and richly textured, it offers fine, firm tannins that lend backbone to the finish.
title: Domaine de la Présidente 2014 Grands Classiques Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Delicate whiffs of mocha and spice accentuate luscious, juicy flavors of ripe blackberry and plum. This wine is plump and plush in mouthfeel but balanced by fresh acidity and a firm, tannic finish.
title: Domaine de Verquiere 2013  Gigondas

description: The wine, always structured, is particularly strong and tannic in this vintage. It has density and plenty of dry tannins that at the moment have a hard edge. This structure will stand the otherwise ripe and fruity wine in good stead as it ages, but it will take time. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016  Juliénas

description: This six-acre parcel has produced a structured but fruity wine. With crisp red berries and a layer of tannins to sustain the fruitiness, it is likely to mature well, with concentration and a rich aftertaste. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaines Laurent Perrachon & Fils 2016 Les Mouilles  (Juliénas)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Clear and focused on the nose, this wine brings crushed stone, lemon-lime and peach blossom aromas into the mix. The clean-cut palate is lithe, with crisp green apple and tart quince riding along a streamlined river of acidity. A delightfully astringent quinine finish keeps this taut and refreshing.
title: Empire Estate 2016 Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Described as a cross between Gewürztraminer and Sémillon created in 1938, this unique white is exotic on the nose in aromatic apple blossom and honeysuckle, its texture dry and salty. It has a strong flavor of fig complemented by green apple.
title: Fogline Vineyards 2016 Spring Hills Vineyard Flora (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Cola, Montmorency cherry juice, star anise and sagebrush aromas show on the nose of this bottling. It's soft, easy and familiar on the palate, with clove spice giving depth to the cooked raspberry and strawberry flavors.
title: Foley 2015 Bar Lazy S Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
