description: Fully ripe aromas of animal hide and dark-berry fruits blend with heavy oak notes that are borderline chemical and like paint. A massive, extracted palate almost goes overboard but remains within the realm of balance. Flavors of clove, burnt oak and blackberry are dense, baked and outright woody on the finish. Drink through 2025.
title: François Lurton 2015 Hacienda Araucano Alka Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Designation: Domaine de Javernière Côte du Py
description: The top wine from this estate comes from the famed Côte du Py vineyard. This wine is densely structured, full of tannins and with great potential. Black fruit is still developing under the dry texture but will surely be there in a few months. Drink from late 2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine de Javernière Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: Rich black-cherry and berry flavors are unctuously ripe, almost syrupy, but offset by sunny acidity and a pleasantly silky mouthfeel. This wine is unabashedly fruity and forward yet maintains a fresh, pretty demeanor and a soft, supple finish.
title: Halos de Jupiter 2015  Gigondas

description: A gentle vanilla note wraps itself around ripe, red cherry on the nose, with a slight touch of smoky mocha. The palate combines these flavors and presents them on a juicy palate held firm by a fine mesh of tannins. This has bite and ample freshness.
title: Heinrich Hartl 2016 Classic Pinot Noir (Thermenregion)

description: Some wood aging has given this single-estate wine its smooth, polished character. Along with having the concentration of old vines, this wine is rich, generous and likely to age well. Drink from late 2018.
title: Henry Fessy 2016 Château des Labourons  (Fleurie)

description: This is a youthful wine, softened by tiny additions of Merlot and Petit Verdot. Peppery black spice, leather and cedar accent compressed black fruit on the palate, bolstered by a sizable tannic structure.
title: Heritance 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Appellation: San Diego County, South Coast, California, US
description: Cassis, roasted chocolate, pepper and buttery pastries show on the nose of this bottling, which is thick and dark in the glass and shows great potential for this grape in Southern California. It's dense on the palate, with soft violet and blackberry flavors, wrapped in a tarry structure with hints of nutmeg and cocoa on the finish.
title: Hungry Hawk 2015 Petite Sirah (San Diego County)

Appellation: San Diego County, South Coast, California, US
description: Cooked cherries, wild chaparral and toasted oak aromas show on this estate bottling from a promising project in San Diego County. It's a classic, down-the-middle Syrah on the palate, where savory herbs of sage and thyme spice up the boysenberry fruit, with vanilla, star anise and mint arising toward the finish.
title: Hungry Hawk 2015 Syrah (San Diego County)

description: Made with 95% Sangiovese and 5% Canaiolo, this has aromas of plum cake, leather, and vanilla. The ripe, savory palate offers juicy blackberry, star anise, white pepper and a touch of almond alongside firm, fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Il Brunone 2011 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This powerful wine offers rich texture and layers of tannin balanced by juicy black fruits and crisp acidity. This wine, from a southeast-facing slope, is ripe, ready to drink from 2019.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Domaine des Billards  (Saint-Amour)

description: This is a classy, food friendly wine from a winemaker of consistent quality. Aromas of peach, melon and guava harmonize with a hint of butter and toast on the nose. The palate delivers understated balance and finesse, simultaneously slippery and grainy in texture, finishing with a guava zing.
title: Jules Taylor 2016 Chardonnay (Marlborough)

Appellation: Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara, Central Coast, California, US
description: A deep, velvety purple color in the glass, this bottling by proprietor Roger Bower and winemaker Adam Henkel shows dark-chocolate-covered black cherries, fresh blackberry, white pepper, violet oil, port reduction sauce and spice sake on the lavish nose. Firm tannins frame the palate, which carries black currant and roasted lean beef flavors through soy and pepper sauce into a finish of caramel and espresso grinds.
title: Crown Point 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: A sultry shade of dark purple, this luxury bottling by the Daou brothers shows freshly smashed black currant, gravelly minerality, black pepper, espresso and shaved chocolate on the nose. The palate is black to the core, from blueberry to dark chocolate to lava rocks, with strong yet polished tannins and wel-balanced acidity. Decant now or drink 2020–2035.
title: Daou 2014 Soul of a Lion Red (Adelaida District)

description: Wonderfully aromatic notions of blood orange zest and ripe Meyer lemon swirl on nose and palate of this wine. Its slender yet concentrated body imbues that citrus tautness with hints of richer fruit—think juicy Mirabelle plum and ripe orange. A lemon note keeps the balance firmly in the tart camp, making for a zesty, intense and clean midpalate and finish. That blood orange aroma lingers magically. Drink 2020–2035.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Kellerberg Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: A faint, flinty touch of reduction still informs this wine's lifted citrus scents. On the palate, a taut, textured structure sends zesty lemon flavors darting across the tongue. This still needs to open up, but all the ingredients for an explosive citrus party are here. It has concentration, freshness, fruit and potential. This may become smoother but will remain beautifully slender. Drink 2020–2035.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Loibenberg Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: Fragrant and elegant, this opens with a textbook Nebbiolo bouquet showing scents of tilled soil, rose, wild red berry, Alpine herb and a whiff of new leather. The taut, vibrant palate evokes sour cherry, juicy strawberry, licorice and clove framed in refined tannins and bright acidity. It's balanced and gorgeous but still young, so give it several more years to evolve into the beauty it's destined to become. Drink 2020–2033.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Asili Riserva  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine takes a light-handed approach, allowing its soul to speak quietly in a blur of perfumed rose petal, lavender and wildflower. The expansive, velvety palate offers highlights of pomegranate, strawberry and freshly squeezed citrus, with pronounced earthy sensations of crushed rock, salt and turned earth.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Allen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine offers incredibly inviting aromas and a fresh, vibrant fruit-driven framework which suggests long-term potential. Youthfully gritty tannins hide beneath a forceful foundation of black raspberry, rose petal and warm baking spice, with a hint of dried herb creeping in. Enjoy 2020–2025.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Rochioli Riverblock Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara, Central Coast, California, US
description: Extremely dense in the glass, this epic bottling offers lavish and penetrating aromas of black currant and elderberry jam on the nose alongside seared beef, chocolate cake and a pinch of violet. Expertly balanced, polished tannins make for a soft mouthfeel yet firm structure, carrying flavors of black fruit, chocolate and grilled figs that lead to an espresso note on the finish.
title: Crown Point 2014 Estate Selection Limited Production Red (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)

description: New leather, camphor, forest berry, dried rose and culinary spice are some of the aromas you'll find on this exceptional red. The firmly structured, poised palate doles out juicy cranberry, crushed raspberry, white pepper and star anise framed in tightly-knit, refined tannins while bright acidity provides energy and balance. Drink 2020–2033.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Montestefano Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: This wine's shy, elusive nose promises hints of ripe citrus and herbal savor. The palate obliges with streamlined, zesty citrus notes and the salty tang of thyme and sage, surrounded by bright lemon freshness. A compact, incisive package of refreshing delight, it dances on tiptoe but knows its every step.
title: Franz Hirtzberger 2016 Rotes Tor Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

Appellation: Diamond Mountain District, Napa, California, US
description: Deliciously crafted and full-bodied ripe, this shows dense layers of blackberry and blueberry crumble succulence and richness, well-etched flavors that are wrapped in soft integrated oak. Juicy and earthy, it offers length and breadth, the tannins roundly structured. Memorable seasonings include chocolate and clove. Enjoy best 2024–2034.
title: Hall 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Diamond Mountain District)

description: Four years after bottling, this vibrant, peppery red is still taut, begging for time to allow its fruit and earthen complexities to meld. Pristine black-cherry and plum flavors penetrate deeply on the palate, shaded by whispers of smoke, violet, tar and leather. It's powerful, complex wine that's yet to see its peak. Hold till 2020 at least, but it's likely to improve for another decade still.
title: Domaine Jamet 2013  Côte Rôtie

description: Perfumed and meaty, this coastal wine offers strong elements of cured bacon and intense hints of smoke. The nose is additionally gorgeous in rose and dried herbes de Provence. Crisp, savory fruit gives it freshness and complexity, a succulent tartness that lingers a long time on the palate.
title: Martinelli 2015 Bella Vigna Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: One of the better Chardonnays tasted in recent years from the Central Coast, this bottling by proprietor Blair Pence is both brisk and warm on the nose, with lemon balm, crushed limestone and yuzu cutting through white peach and butter. The tactile chalkiness on the sip is remarkable, carrying flavors of lime pith, Asian pear, sea salt, shaved lemongrass and lime leaf.
title: Pence 2015 Rosa Estate Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Dark-skinned berry, menthol, new leather, blue flower and baking spice aromas take center stage. On the radiant, full-bodied palate, vibrant acidity lifts red cherry, raspberry compote, star anise, clove and tobacco while youthfully taut, fine-grained tannins provide firm support. Hold on to this for even more complexity once it unwinds and develops. Drink 2023–2038.
title: Attilio Ghisolfi 2013 Bussia  (Barolo)

Appellation: Red Hills Lake County, California, US
description: Combining hyper-concentrated fruit flavors with enticing, sophisticated oak nuances, this wine is a gentle giant. The first thing to notice is the lightly spicy, toasted-baguette aroma, and then the palate floods with saturated, ripe and delicious blackberry and black-cherry flavors, while notes of dark chocolate and black pepper kick in on the finish. This wine ranks with the best yet from Lake County. Best after 2020.
title: Cinnabar 2014 Malbec (Red Hills Lake County)

description: This wine's wet earth, sun-ripened mirabelle plum and vivid citrus aromas spell out the pleasures to come. The palate is still tentative and youthful but sure to blossom into a citrus flavor explosion. Leave this concentrated, promising wine to calm down. A slender, precise and highly aromatic pleasure awaits. Drink 2020–2035.
title: Eichinger 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This wine's pure, ripe Conference pear notes appear on the nose and are given full rein on the palate. They add color and charm to concentrated, savory notions of celery salt and miso. Bright citrus accents make it taut and fresh, depsite that immense concentration. It echoes long on the finish, promising to unfold beautifully with more age. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Franz Hirtzberger 2016 Honivogl Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: Always one of the benchmarks for Sangiovese, the 2013 doesn't disappoint. It boasts alluring scents of blue flower, perfumed berry, exotic spice and a whiff of French oak that flow over to the vibrant, elegant palate along with wild cherry, raspberry compote and licorice. Bright acidity and taut, fine-grained tannins provide an age-worthy framework. Give it time to develop to its maximum potential. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Isole e Olena 2013 Cepparello Sangiovese (Toscana)

description: Green pear and lemon zest characterize the nose of this concentrated, salty wine. Zesty citrus notes give both texture and direction on the taut palate, which shimmers with savory, yeasty, miso-like saltiness. A very delicious, impactful little package that is sure to unfold beautifully. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Pichler-Krutzler 2016 Ried Loibenberg Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A touch of tar, a hint of bitter almond and brooding black cherry fruit mark the nose and spread freely across the palate with dark but delicious intent. This is dense and firm, with drying tannins but no heaviness. There is freshness and intensity to the dark, brooding fruit that will show its true colors with some more age—but all the aromatic promise is already here. Drink 2020–2035.
title: Prieler 2015 Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: Earth and moss scents join the tart apple notes on the nose of this wine. On the palate, a leesy touch of yeast provides a textural backdrop to zesty lemon, tangerine, grapefruit and orange flavors. Its wonderful concentration creates a memorable impression that captivates and refreshes. This is so pure, so fresh and so lasting. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Stadt Krems 2016 Ried Schreck Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This multi-vineyard wine is sourced from hillside sites on the eastern edges of the appellation, including Foss and Calegari vineyards. Perfumed in clove and other baking spice tones, it has beautiful aromatics and ripe, firm tannins. The palate is marked by flavors of plum, rhubarb and cherry clafouti.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Eastside Road Neighbors Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Menthol, pressed rose, woodland berry and crushed herb aromas slowly emerge on this elegant, structured red along with a hint of underbrush. The firm, refined palate delivers Marasca cherry, ground clove and licorice framed in taut, fine-grained tannins. Drink 2022–2034.
title: Gaja 2014 Sorì Tildin  (Barbaresco)

description: A tropical touch of passion fruit plays on the pear-inflected nose of this wine. The concentrated palate, however, is all about lemon freshness and yeasty texture. Soy and miso notions add a salty element while lemon zest and pith notes provide a precise texture. That lemon peel quality dominates with its purity on the long finish. Lovely and compact but very concentrated.
title: Alzinger 2016 Ried Steinertal Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: Layers of mocha, sweet spice and leather integrate seamlessly into luscious black cherry and fig on the palate of this bold, deeply concentrated wine. It's sumptuous, with a kaleidoscope of smoky mineral complexities and a backbone that's peppery and fresh. The long finish is framed by sweet, silken tannins.
title: E. Guigal 2012 Château d'Ampuis  (Côte Rôtie)

description: Juicy, ripe pear notions and bright Mandarin accents swirl enticingly on the nose of this wine. They dominate the streamlined, brisk palate for now, but underneath, the savory, herbal and intriguing saltiness of Grüner Veltliner shimmers. Along with sage, thyme and miso flavors, they make their full, satisfying appearance on the dry finish. This has both charm and depth. Drink 2020–2030.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Steinertal Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: This wine's complex nose of tangy, herbal Seville orange and salty stoniness leads to a tingling, taut and lip-smacking palate. That tautness is shot through with lively lemon freshness that leaves the palate wanting more. But it still needs to fill out. A little patience will be rewarded with even more fruit. Drink 2020–2035.
title: Franz Hirtzberger 2016 Setzberg Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: This is a grainy, herbal red wine, boldly tannic and softly smooth in texture. It takes on mountain airs of sage, clove and tobacco, with a brushy graininess that adds complexity and intrigue. Blackberry, black cherry and currant give a tart fruitiness to the flavor.
title: Turnbull 2015 Estate Grown Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is a robust white, exhibiting an exotic nose of spicy nutmeg and lemongrass. Fully oaked, it presents ripeness and concentration, a sign of its youthful vigor and the density of the vintage. Lemony acidity provides freshness against the bolder landscape of ripe apple and melon.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Allen Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Still a baby, this Hawke's Bay Syrah should develop into a lovely drop. For now, it's dense with drying oak spice, cola, red and black berries, dried herbs and a hint of smoke. The oak is apparent on the palate but there's also plenty of juicy fruit and acidity to keep things in check. Drink 2020–2027.
title: Te Mata 2014 Bullnose Syrah (Hawke's Bay)

description: Made with 90% Sangiovese, 5% Colorino and 5% Merlot, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, leather and a touch of eucalyptus. The firm palate delivers red plum, black cherry, tobacco and clove alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2026.
title: Tenuta di Lilliano 2014 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of violet, red-skinned berry and mint lead the way. The bright palate doles out black cherry, white pepper and a hint of nutmeg alongside velvety tannins and fresh acidity. Enjoy through 2024.
title: Tenuta di Lilliano 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Black-skinned berry, leather and white pepper aromas are at the forefront while the concentrated palate offers ripe black cherry, tobacco and a touch of star anise. Firm, polished tannins provide support. Enjoy through 2024.
title: Tenuta di Nozzole 2013 La Forra Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Candied strawberry fruit meets with the lightest hint of sage on the nose of this bottling, which includes 18% Syrah. Rounded red fruits show on the sip with a pinch of dried garden herbs, set against a structure that's well balanced between acidity and tannins.
title: The Farm Winery 2014 Touchy-Feely Grenache-Syrah (Adelaida District)

description: This wine might be the best value available in California Pinot Noir. It has great varietal character in the aroma and follows through with tempting spice, red fruit and savory nuances on the palate. Black tea, red cherry and clove waft from the glass while cherry and cranberry spread across the palate, braced by moderate tannins and fresh acidity. It's a beautifully balanced medium-bodied wine.
title: Tortoise Creek 2016 Mission Grove Pinot Noir (California)

description: Plonk this on the table at just about any dinner party and it's bound to be enjoyed by all. Be it the appealing nose of red-currant jelly, flowers and savory, stony minerality, or the silky, plush-fruited, light-on-its-feet palate, this easy-drinking wine from one of New Zealand's biggest names offers a lot.
title: Villa Maria 2015 Cellar Selection Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Dried herbs, plums, blueberries, bark and a little dusty oak coalesce in this wine from one of the better known single vineyards in the Awatere Valley. Lean, light and crisp, the palate comes with tart red fruit, more drying herbs and some toasty oak notes on the finish.
title: Villa Maria 2015 Taylors Pass Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

Appellation: San Benito County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Orange blossom, tangerine souffle, light honey and gin-like botanical aromas show on the nose. The palate's concentrated sweetness combines orange zest with lemon cream, making for a nicely complex after-dinner sip.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Vista Verde Vineyard Late Harvest Gewürztraminer (San Benito County)

description: Hints of smoke and mocha clothe the ripe, red cherry fruit on the nose. The mocha notes also color the rounded but still fresh palate with their richness and add a little weight to the midplaate of this fruit-driven wine.
title: Winzerkeller Andau 2015 Selection Zweigelt (Burgenland)

description: Roasted nuts, hot buttered toast with lemon preserves, sea salt and a touch of chalk show on the layered nose of this bottling by winemaker Sabrine Rodems. The flavors are classical and clean, with lemon wedge, cashew, more salt and a hint of butterscotch, all riding atop a strong acidity.
title: Wrath 2015 3 Clone Chardonnay (Monterey)

description: This delicious, easygoing red boasts typical Sangiovese aromas of wild berry, crushed violet and a whiff dark spice. The juicy, enjoyable palate doles out succulent Marasca cherry, fleshy plum and cake spice while supple tannins provide smooth support. Enjoy through 2019.
title: Badia a Coltibuono 2015 RS  (Chianti Classico)

description: Made from biodynamic-certified grapes grown on a coastal-influenced property, this wine is bright in cherry and cardamom spice, with fully realized elements of forest pine, gravel and pomegranate. Fully oaked and somewhat tannic in its youth, it shows vigor and vibrancy as it finishes.
title: Benziger 2015 De Coelo Abore Sacra Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Enticing aromas of fragrant purple flowers, ripe woodland berries and a whiff of pressed powder waft out of the glass. Bright and juicy, the savory palate shows crushed raspberry, fleshy Morello cherry, ground clove and a hint of coffee alongside polished, fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Borgo Scopeto 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Dark berry, pipe tobacco, chopped herb and forest floor aromas carry over to the polished palate along with dried cherry, orange peel and ground clove. Firm, refined tannins provide elegant support. Drink 2020–2027.
title: Borgogno 2012 Cannubi  (Barolo)

description: Aromas of dark-skinned berry, culinary spice and a whiff of French oak waft out of the glass. The taut, linear palate offers cranberry, licorice and coffee bean alongside firm, close-grained tannins that give the finish grip. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Broccardo 2013 Paiagallo  (Barolo)

description: Compact, almost closed aromas of prune, blackberry, tire rubber and clove announce a dark, well-oaked Carmenère. A firm palate of commendable structure is unflinching, while flavors of ripe blackberry, cassis and light herbs carry through to the steady finish.
title: Carmen 2015 Gran Reserva El Peñasco Vineyard Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: You'll find aromas suggesting underbrush, toasted oak, tobacco, new leather and a balsamic note on this firm red. The medium-bodied palate offers dried blackberry, prune, orange zest, clove and espresso framed in gripping, close-grained tannins and bright acidity. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Casa Emma 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Moist underbrush, toasted oak and a whiff of camphor lead the nose. The linear, mid-weight palate shows juicy black cherry, licorice and other oak-driven spices framed in vibrant firm acidity while austere, fine-grained tannins grip the finish. Give it a few more years to mellow and fully come together.
title: Casa Sola 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine is released after 22 months in oak that gave it a delightful perfume and flavors of mint as well as black cherries. A firm tannic structure backed by the ripe fruit will likely allow the wine to age well. Drink from late 2018.
title: Château de Lavernette 2015 Le Clos Cuvée Jadis  (Beaujolais-Leynes)

description: This rich wine, the product of the ripe 2015 vintage, is generous, full and packed with still-developing tannins. Its structure and good acidity are still in place, indicating the wine needs to age further. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château des Gimarets 2015 Tradition  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Smokey and earthy in style, notes of charred meat, blackberry, black olive and dried oregano are all followed by a savory palate with medium tannins. This is a love/hate wine where winemaking prowess overpowers terroirs.
title: Cloudy Bay 2015 Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: These growers, based in Villié-Morgon, have produced a structured wine from arguably the top area of Morgon. It is full of tannins as well as berry fruits. It is not especially dense and the cherry flavors give the wine an attractive crisp texture and aftertaste. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de la Bonne Tonne 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: Producer Cédric Lecareux makes rich wines, typically with some wood aging that enhances the ripe fruits. This wine, produced from organic grapes, is in this mold, juicy with black fruits that are lightly cut by acidity. It should be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Capréoles 2016 Chamodère  (Régnié)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Pristine green apple, lemon and lime are gilded with honeycomb in this medium-to-full-bodied white. Expansive and weighty on the palate, it finises long on a twist of bitter lime pith. Drink now through 2020.
title: E. Guigal 2015 White (Côtes du Rhône)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit for this wine all comes from Riverbend Vineyard and saw just 10% new French oak. Blended with 17% Cabernet Sauvignon, a medley of fresh herbs pop on the aromas, which also show notes of cigar box and whiffs of pencil lead. The palate brings cranberry-tart fruit flavors that offer pleasing depth and stretch out on the finish. It provides a lot of quality and value.
title: Estrin 2014 Merlot (Wahluke Slope)

description: This 100% Sangiovese opens with earthy aromas of tilled soil, baked plum and leather. The dense, solid palate delivers ripe black cherry, licorice and a touch of star anise alongside smooth, seasoned tannins. Enjoy through 2023.
title: Fattoria di Lamole 2011 Le Stinche Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: One of a series of Beaujolais cru wines from this négociant, this bottling comes from the northernmost village. It is firm and structured, layering rich tannins and plenty of spicy blackberry fruits. Drink from late 2018.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée A  (Saint-Amour)

description: One of two wines named after a legendary vine grower in Morgon, this wine has weight and great tannins. Structured, ripe black fruits are generous and developing well. The boisterous fruit needs to calm down a little, so drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Jean-Ernest Descombes  (Morgon)

description: This wine's almost glowingly ripe fruit notes stream from the glass: yellow plum, ripe pear and a touch of melon. That immense juiciness is confirmed on the palate and joined by a yeasty, leesy texture and fresh lemon notes. It persists into a fruit-driven, almost bouncy finish.
title: Hermann Moser 2016 Ried Gebling Reserve Der Löss Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

Designation: Old Vine Family Farmed Sustainably Grown
description: Saturated with dark, almost jammy fruit flavors, this is an extremely flavorful and nicely dry wine that also has great structure. An intensely dark color leads to blackberry and boysenberry aromas, distinctive blackberry and black-pepper flavors and a rich mouthfeel that is well-laced by fine-grained tannins.
title: Artezin 2016 Old Vine Family Farmed Sustainably Grown Zinfandel (Mendocino County)

description: Aromas of toasted oak, tobacco leaf and a balsamic note come together on this. The firmly structured palate is youthfully austere, showing cranberry, sour cherry, dried sage and clove set against taut, fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. It will benefit from more cellar time to unwind and develop complexity. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Badia a Coltibuono 2013 Cultus Boni  (Chianti Classico)

description: A deep gold color and bold buttery aromas set up an expectation for rich flavors, which come in the form of butter, butterscotch, vanilla and cream, with some tasty Anjou pear underneath. The wine was made from biodynamic grapes and matured in French oak barrels, making it full bodied, mouthfilling and indulgent.
title: Bonterra 2015 The Roost Blue Heron Vineyard Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: Forest floor, dried herb, pressed rose and dark spice aromas slowly take shape in the glass. The full-bodied palate offers black cherry steeped in spirits, licorice and clove set against taut, velvety tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Cascina Chicco 2011 Ginestra Riserva  (Barolo)

description: Savory notes of smoke, dried thyme and nuts lend a dark tone to crisp blackberry and cherry flavors. This wine is concentrated and full on the palate but balanced crisply. The finish wraps up with a pleasant bitter cacao note and firm, tacky tannins.
title: Château Husson 2015 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Aromas of cool earth, fall leaves and mossy plum and red-berry fruits lead to a palate with grip and flush tannins. Flavors of mocha and chocolate are mild and don't overcrowd the wine's primary berry character. This is excellent Cabernet for the price; drink through 2022.
title: Clos des Fous 2014 Grillos Cantores Cabernet Sauvignon (Cachapoal Valley)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Thick blackberries, wet slate, sweet anise and a touch of purple flowers show on the rich nose of this reserve wine from a vineyard tucked into a seaside canyon above Cambria. It's quite dry on the tip of sip, with tons of rose petals and lavender, and then the black cherry and vanilla flavors arise. Chalky tannins and refreshing acidity complete the picture.
title: Cutruzzola 2014 Giacomino Reserve Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)

description: This is a concentrated, fleshy coastal wine, weighty in oak and tannin. The vibrancy comes via well-defined layers of cherry, wild strawberry and forest, with wisps of black tea and cardamom knocking on the door.
title: Davies 2015 Piedra Libre Vineyards Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: The 45-year-old vines that go into this wine give it just the right amount of concentration without losing sight of the generous fruit. This wine has structure, with firm, dry tannins as well as rich black-cherry and black-plum-skin flavors that are integrating well. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Nugues 2016  Fleurie

description: Chervil and miso savoriness combine with fresh pear and lemon notes on the nose of this wine, which tingles with white pepper. The palate is rounded and soft but remains juicily slender. There is earthy power here and a deep well of rich fruit that sings with its distinct and beautiful phenolic structure. Drink 2019–2029.
title: Ebner-Ebenauer 2016 Ried Bürsting Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Celery salt and lemon pith scents set the tone in this wine. The palate follows suit with generous pear fruit that swirls against a savory backdrop of miso and yeast. Lemon pith frames all of this with freshness. The finish centers on gentle pear fruit.
title: Hermann Moser 2016 Ried Gebling Reserve Fortissimo Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Spiced plum, pressed violet, exotic spice and saddle leather aromas take center stage. Elegant and structured, the vibrant, assertive palate offers black cherry, tobacco and a hint of licorice while racy acidity and fine-grained tannins provide the backbone. It's youthfully austere and will benefit from more cellaring. Drink 2021–2029.
title: Istine 2014 Levigne Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Designation: Château de Bellevue Climat Les Charmes
description: Partly aged in wood, this wine is richly structured, spicy and packed with dense fruits. Blackberry and black-cherry flavors are sustained by generous tannins and the dry core. The wine will need to age, so drink from 2019.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Château de Bellevue Climat Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: Fresh, primary notes of peach and pear are lifted by lavish orange blossom and honeysuckle perfume. This warming, plush wine is full of vanilla, cream and custard but lemony acidity and delicate spice and smoke tones maintain the finesse.
title: Jean-Luc Colombo 2014 Amour de Dieu  (Condrieu)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This bottling, which includes 5% Viognier, shows aromas of concentrated black currant syrup, vanilla, nutmeg and a bit of bay leaf on the nose. The palate is extremely flavorful, with tons of licorice, blackberry jam, rosemary and thyme. Vanilla rises on the finish.
title: Ken Brown 2014 A Cuvée Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: At first, this unique blend of grapes originally from Bordeaux, the Rhône and Burgundy is oaky and toasty, with jammy berry scents and an overriding note of maple. The palate is saturation city, meaning it's full, chewy and oaky. Flavors of wood resin, vanilla and cedar absorb savory plum and cassis notes both before and on a weighty finish. Drink this broad modern wine now.
title: Lapostolle 2013 Borobo Red (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This well-named project comes from a seaside vineyard that may get the last direct sunlight of the entire Central Coast, and the cool-climate aspects thrive. Aromas of dewy sagebrush, shaved pine and myrrh mix with mulberry and underripe raspberry on the nose. The mouthfeel is full while the flavors are tart with tangy cranberry cherry tomato and Kalamata olive, leaving the palate refreshed.
title: Last Light 2015 Derbyshire Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)

description: The combination of the famed Côte du Py vineyard and old vines is impressive and rich. This is still a structured, dense wine, very firm and with black, ripe and juicy fruit developing alongside the tannins. The wine will be ready to drink in 2019.
title: Laurent Gauthier 2015 Vieilles Vignes Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: Soy-like yeastiness takes turns with ripe, yellow pear scents on the nose of this wine. The palate unites these notions, but the rounded, rich and highly textured mouthfeel wins the day. Some bitter pith appears in the background and makes for a lip-smacking finish.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Spiegel Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: A touch of hazelnut and smoke accompanies the flowing green and yellow apple notes on the nose of this wine. The palate adds a touch of yeasty richness without distorting its svelte translucency in the slightest. The bright green and yellow apple notes are free to swirl against this finely-tuned backdrop. The finish is dry, fresh, yeasty, fruity and long.
title: Netzl 2016 Ried Bärnreiser Weissburgunder (Carnuntum)

description: The Roman emperor Probus is supposed to have brought the Gamay grape to the region, hence the name of this wine. With its rich tannins and generous black fruits, this dense wine is concentrated both with tannins and fruit. It will develop impressively in the months to come. Drink from 2019.
title: Pascal Aufranc 2015 Probus  (Juliénas)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Though not labeled as such, this is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon (78%), Merlot (18%) and Cabernet Franc (8%). Coming from top sites Discovery, Quintessence, Seven Hills, The Benches, Figgins, Klipsun and Champoux, there's a lot of sophistication in the aromas, with notes of dried herb, leaf, graphite, anise, spice and cherry providing appeal. The flavors are intense and full, showing an exquisite sense of balance and intensity. It keeps you coming back for more.
title: Passing Time 2014 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Rich fruit masks the gentle tannins in this opulent wine. Ripe blackberry and juicy black-plum flavors are warm, generous and lightly structured. This full-bodied wine will be ready from mid-2018.
title: Richard Rottiers 2016  Brouilly

Appellation: Santa Cruz Mountains, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a lot of snappy energy to this bottling by Sabrine Rodems, with fresh garden herbs, blackberries, shaved licorice and a dark chocolate touch on the nose, with a lingering, exotic Indian-like spice throughout. Wild sagebrush and pine oil show on the sip, giving depth to the tangy purple fruit. Squeaky tannins rise toward the finish.
title: Scratch 2014 Janaca Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: This wine comes from old vines planted at high elevation. A light-gold color, aromas of toast, peach, pumpkin spice and wet rock are followed by plentiful stone and tropical-fruit flavors speckled with barrel spices, showing superb length on the flavorful finish. It has an absolutely swoon-worthy feel. More please! Best from 2019–2024.
title: Sixto 2014 Roza Hills Chardonnay (Washington)

description: A slight funkiness accompanies the salty yeast notes on the nose of this wine. The funk disappears on the lithe and elegant palate as wonderfully pure notes of green pear and a moreish lick of sage, ivy, moss and fern combine. A lovely and irresistible flavor combination.
title: Weszeli 2015 Purus Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: Ripe dark concentrated aromas of blackberry, prune, charcoal and chocolate are heady. This blend of Cabernet, Carmenère and other red grapes feels solid and tannic. Spicy plum and berry flavors are typically herbal, while chocolate and herb notes carry the finish. Drink through 2020.
title: Vik 2011 La Piu Belle Red (Cachapoal Valley)

description: Up-front oak creates a warm sense of toast and maple to go with herbal leathery berry aromas. A bold lively palate hosts coffee, mocha and herbal plum and berry flavors. On the finish, this is toasty with a long-lasting note of mocha. Drink through 2021.
title: Vik 2012 Milla Cala Red (Cachapoal Valley)

description: An earthy element hugs the ripe yellow pear notes on the nose of this wine. It appears again on the concentrated palate, where it's characterized by pithy citrus and miso-like creaminess. The finish has a spike of bright lemon freshness, which gives direction to the lovely, concentrated palate.
title: Aigner 2016 Ried Obere Sandgrube Privat Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Concentrated aromas of herbal black plum and berries along with graphite are 100% Alto Maipo and ideal for this type of wine, which is a blend based on Syrah. Kuyen has been better than ever in recent vintages; this version is deeply layered and lush, with toasty flavors of black fruits and dark chocolate. Barrel-based toast and spice flavors carry the wine's steady finish. Drink through 2025.
title: Antiyal 2014 Kuyen Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Baked leather rides over flavors of black licorice and pepper in this perfumed, older vine wine. Gritty in texture, it's full bodied and densely packed in tannin, with a streak of acidity that keeps it fresh in underlying strawberry and cherry cola.
title: Howell Mountain Vineyards 2014 Old Vine Zinfandel (Howell Mountain)

description: Made with 93% Sangiovese and 7% Canaiolo, this opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, violet and a whiff of cooking spice. The bright palate offers black raspberry, red cherry and star anise alongside snug tannins and fresh acidity. Enjoy through 2023.
title: I Sodi 2015  Chianti Classico

description: This wine has a firm structure as well as ripe red-berry fruits. The tannins are contained within a floral, rich wine that is still developing. Drink this potentially delicious wine from late 2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Château de Bellevue  (Fleurie)

description: Made from 47% Syrah, 40% Petite Sirah and 13% Cabernet Sauvignon, the nose on this wine at first is shy, but slowly warms to reveal complementary flavors of spicy cedar, pencil shavings, sage and herb. Fully ripe, rich and rewarding in red currant, it has a meaty undertow of varietal character.
title: Jeff Gordon 2012 Joie de Vivre Red (Napa Valley)

description: This is a tasty dry wine with effusive flower and honey aromas and mouth-filling mango and banana flavors, all supported by tangy acidity for good balance. It has interesting pairing options, from tapas to shellfish to cheese.
title: Karine 2013 Laurie's Vineyard Malvasia Bianca (Madera)

description: An opaque color and saucy aromas of tomato, plum, herbs and graphite are typical of cool-climate Chilean Syrah. A wide palate is full and generous but also easy to get through. Herbal blackberry, toast and oaky clove flavors finish spicy, peppery and dense, with some unresolved oak.
title: Kingston Family 2015 Lucero Syrah (Casablanca Valley)

description: A remarkable value for Châteauneuf-du-Pape, this bottling offers ample flavors of sun-kissed black cherry and plum tinged in earth, leather and Mediterranean spice. Savory and supple, it ends with mouthcoating fine-grained tannins.
title: Kirkland Signature 2014 Cuvée de Nalys Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Salty yeast and bruised apples notes create immediate impact on the nose of this wine. The palate brings savoriness and yeasty texture to the fore; its celery salt and miso flavors are bound up with fresh green apple and pear. A big, bold Grüner Veltliner with ample fruit.
title: Kohl 2016 Kohloss Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine opens with a confectionery hint of marshmallow and caramel before showing its more steely underbelly of fresh, focused acidity. Funky apple, nutmeg and lemongrass figure into the medium-bodied mix, a complement of savory and fruit.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 Pratt Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Aromas of exotic spice, French oak and woodland berry meld with whiffs of espresso and menthol on the nose. The dense, full-bodied palate offers dried sage, dried black cherry, coffee and clove framed in tightly-knit, austere tannins. Drink now–2023.
title: Lamole di Lamole 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Truffle, toast, dried mint and plum aromas come together along with a charred note. The taut, chewy palate delivers dried black cherry, cranberry, clove and star anise set against tightly wound, close-grained tannins.
title: Lamole di Lamole 2013 Vigneto di Campolungo Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Black plum and cherry flavors are ripe and rich here, but the more rustic, savory tones of game, leather and moss give this wine its flair. It's flush and full on the palate, with firm, upright tannins and lingering hints of dark herb, nuts and spice that linger on the finish. Enjoy now through 2022.
title: Lavau 2015 Red (Vacqueyras)

description: Aromas of ripe blackberry, new leather and a whiff of tilled soil lead the way. The firm, savory palate offers dried sour cherry, tobacco, clove and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2025.
title: Losi Querciavalle 2012 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This unoaked wine always impresses, offering sweetly floral tones on the nose and a wealth of tropical mango and pineapple on the palate. Steely, with some bristling citrus-driven acidity, it's refreshing and focused, with hints of lemongrass.
title: Marimar Estate 2016 Acero Don Miguel Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: A rare 100% varietal offering, this works well to showcase dusty, pronounced tannin around a richness of baked cherry and strawberry seasoned in white pepper and orange. Tart acidity works alongside floral aromatics of cherry blossom and violet.
title: Meeker 2014 Hoskins Ranch Grenache (Dry Creek Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Rose, hibiscus and pistachio mark the aromatic profile of this hearty, well-made wine, dipping into juicy, succulent and bright layers of blueberry and black cherry. A round rich tannin structure provides a welcome fleshiness, with a backbone of acidity.
title: Merry Edwards 2015 Coopersmith Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Four years after bottling, the vibrance of Viognier's characteristic honeysuckle and tropical fruit are waning here, but a fascinating transformation of earth, nut and spice is taking place. Full bodied and concentrated, the palate offers muted, dustier flavors of dried orange peel and apple shaded with hints of dried herb and bramble.
title: Michel & Stéphane Ogier 2013 Les Vieilles Vignes  (Condrieu)

description: A selection of grapes from different parcels lies behind this wine. With its dense texture contrasted with crisp acidity, it is fruity and has a dry core. Aged on its lees, the wine has a chewy character that will need to soften. Drink from late 2018.
title: Mommessin 2016 Les Grandes Mises  (Saint-Amour)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Blackberry and dark-chocolate flavors push through a firm and tannic texture in this medium to full-bodied wine from a new brand and a French-born winemaker. It's distinctively lean in mouthfeel due to substantive tannin content, but in the aromas and on the finish, an abundance of berries and dark spices comes through.
title: Omen 2015 Zinfandel (Sierra Foothills)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is made in a light nuanced style, and is somewhat shy at first on the nose. Perfumed rose eventually comes through to accent welcoming, bright flavors of orange and red cherry. The oak is tight-grained and supportive.
title: Ousterhout 2015 Bucher Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Oaky black-fruit aromas come with notes of maple and molasses. A dry, tannic palate is braced by lively acidity, while this tastes of tomato sauce, plum and oak. Chewy tannins are at the foundation of a big finish.
title: Oveja Negra 2014 Single Vineyard Carignan (Maule Valley)

description: A hearty blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Malbec and Syrah, given plenty of time in new French oak, this wine is a mix of crayon wax, red and black currant and firm, grasping tannin structure. Complex, it adds secondary characteristics of black cherry and lightly oaked spice as it opens in the glass, full bodied all along.
title: Patland 2014 Proprietary Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Elegant aromas of violet and lilac meet with an intense and pure expression of boysenberry jam on the nose of this blend of 45% Mourvèdre, 34% Syrah and 21% Grenache. The palate is framed by a strong tannic backbone and a plush mouthfeel that carries flavors of black plum, boysenberry and, on the finish, a flash of vanilla.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Inspiration Red (Paso Robles)

description: A small-production wine from a brand-new producer, this is blended with 10% Merlot and 2% Petit Verdot. Simply structured with grace and restrained tannin, it's highlighted in black cherry, vanilla and a hearty streak of cinnamon, presenting full-bodied power from an elegant perspective.
title: Pono 2014 Mana Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Loads of coffee and toasted coconut aromas currently veil the dense, dark fruit that lies beneath. A rich, inky palate of plum and dark cherry shows warming notes of espresso grounds and cocoa nibs. With it's big, broad structure and balanced acidity to boot, this young wine should start to integrate well in a few years' time. Drink from 2020.
title: Raphael 2014 Reserve Petit Verdot (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a dense bottling that combines Petite Sirah and Petit Verdot, so dense that the brooding nature hides many of the aromas and flavors. But patience eventually rewards with black-plum skins, tree bark and earth on the nose, and black currant and chalky tannins on the palate.
title: Sanguis 2014 Acacia Thorns Red (Central Coast)

description: Lovely and appetizing notes of green and yellow pear play on the nose of this wine. The palate adds a bright lemon highlight to these aromas, keeping it sprightly but also juicy and ripe. The finish has a lovely lemony tang.
title: Schwarzböck 2016 Aichleiten Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: A rose petal aroma starts things off in this wine, while the palate provides a delicate rendering of rhubarb, strawberry and forest floor flavors, buoyed by a gravelly texture and balanced ripeness.
title: Sean Minor 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Don Van Staaveren is the winemaker of this well-crafted wine, sourced from Rodger's Creek Vineyard in the Petaluma Gap area. Opulent on the nose, it follows up in richness and robust flavors of toasted oak and apple, finishing awash in butterscotch.
title: Silverpoint Cellars 2015 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: This is a very enticing pour, with vibrant herbal streaks of tobacco, leather and dried fynbos that grace the dark fruit core. The medium-weight palate offers a velvety texture and layers of ripe blackberry and cassis flavors. Earthy hits of turned earth and cigar-box spice grace the lingering finish. Drink now–2021.
title: Spier 2015 Vintage Selection Cabernet Sauvignon (Coastal Region)

description: Rich, heady notes of cola, charred meat, currant, earth and stones are followed by a silky, peppery palate with more currant notes and an earthy core. Perfect for fire side sipping, and at a great price to boot.
title: Sileni 2015 Plateau Reserve Pinot Noir (Hawke's Bay)

Appellation: Red Hills Lake County, California, US
description: This rich and layered wine has deep fruit flavors, a wonderful spiciness, firm structure and a lingering finish. It tastes like black olives, black cherries, cloves and wood smoke and has a wonderful, rounded mouthfeel backed by soft tannins.
title: Sol Rouge 2013 Cabernet Franc (Red Hills Lake County)

description: This is a stunning wine that highlights the beauty and complexity to be found from a South African Pinot Noir. Minty fynbos and forest notes greet the nose upon first whiff, followed by a core of rich cherry and currant tones that are dusted by earth and cola spice. A subtle umami-like, wild-mushroom accent lends additional depth. The medium-weight palate boasts a satiny texture that's full of ripe fruit expression, yet not overdone. The long, evolving finish transitions seamlessly from fleshy berry to cherry skin then earth and bark before landing on a note of lightly toasted oak. Well balanced and complete, this is delicious now but should cellar and develop well through 2026.
title: The Drift 2015 There Are Still Mysteries Pinot Noir (Overberg)

description: This affordable red is high-toned in strawberry and a juicy earthiness, its use of oak and moderate power well-integrated. Smooth and velvety on the palate, it finishes in a bright tease of red licorice.
title: Thirty-Seven Wines 2014 Grenache (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: From a vineyard on the eastern edge of the Sta. Rita Hills, this bottling shows candied boysenberry, mocha, cherry compote, star anise and cinnamon on the nose. The palate is layered with tons of spice, from star anise and clove to licorice, which add depth to the black cherry base. The tannins are soft and polished.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2014 John Sebastiano Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This bottling from a new winery in Ojai shows salty lemons and lush nectarine on the nose alongside a touch of chives and roasted chicken flesh. The palate deftly blends browned butter with crisp citrusy freshness and a strong textural grip, proving refreshing and rich at the same time. The finish is chalky with hints of sea salt caramel and sliced apple.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2015 Bien Nacido Vineyard Reserve Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: This is a complex and compelling wine from one of Hawkes Bay's top producers. Cherry and orange notes mingle with black olive, cracked pepper, smoke and cumin. These lead into a medium body, with balanced acidity and silky tannins. Drink with a wide variety of Middle Eastern cuisine.
title: Trinity Hill 2015 Pinot Noir (Hawke's Bay)

description: This wine's nose is still a little closed, but the palate shows a zesty freshness that features fresh lemon, summer blossom and white currant tartness. It's light in body, with a slightly pithy texture that underlines the refreshing element even more. The finish is tangy and very refreshing.
title: Wieninger 2016 Bisamberg White (Wiener Gemischter Satz)

description: This wine is very aromatic on the nose in notes of dark mulberry and tangy nettle. The palate has the same intensity of fruit and herb—concentrated but not heavy, full-bodied but not rich. This strikes a most lovely balance and convinces with freshness.
title: Artisan Wines DI Franz Scheider 2015 Reserve Zweigelt (Neusiedlersee)

description: Classic Nebbiolo aromas of perfumed red berry, fragrant blue flower, cake spice and a whiff of new leather come together on this. Youthfully tight, the vibrant palate evokes red cherry, cranberry, star anise and pipe tobacco framed in taut, fine-grained tannins. Fresh acidity lends balance and energy. It's still young and tense so give it time to fully develop. Drink 2023–2033.
title: Ascheri 2013 Coste & Bricco  (Barolo)

description: This is a blend of equal parts Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc, barrel-fermented and aged in 40% new French oak for one year. It's fresh and alive in orange blossom aromatics and strong impressions of peach, pear and green apple. A bold streak of minerality gives it a hint of gravel and rock.
title: Ashes & Diamonds 2015 Blanc No. 1 White (Napa Valley)

description: From longtime Kanonkop winemaker Abrie Beeslaar comes this stellar expression of the grape. Ripe, full and powerful, it's wrapped in a plush yet firm and well-balanced package that should mature well through 2026. Upfront oaky notes of cocoa, toasted vanilla and cedar should further integrate into the rich plum, cassis and boysenberry fruit with time. Earthy and herbal tones of tree bark, fallen leaves and licorice root dance in the background.
title: Beeslaar 2015 Pinotage (Stellenbosch)

description: A fruity nose opens the way to piquant, tart acidity in this coastal cool-climate wine. Concentrated extracted flavors of pomegranate and Bing cherry dominate, rounded by a grit of texture and cola spice.
title: Benziger 2015 De Coelo Quintus Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Mokelumne River, Central Valley, California, US
description: This is a fresh, lively and refreshing bottling from old vines, which often offer deep complexity on a lighter frame. Aromas of rose petals, hibiscus, cranberry and wet cement lead into a palate full of crisp red currant, tangy strawberry, and a stunningly rocky minerality. Serve slightly chilled.
title: Birichino 2016 Bechthold Vineyard Old Vines Cinsault (Mokelumne River)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a compelling mix of sweeter fruit and dry earth on the nose of this bottling, with strawberry compote and rusty rock aromas along with a vanilla-laced brioche. The palate loses the ripe fruit a bit, instead showing tighter and fresh flavors of black raspberry with anise, white pepper and sagebrush, proving quite sleek and restrained in style.
title: Black Kite 2015 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: French oak, underbrush, balsamic aromas, coconut and baked plum aromas are front and center on this radiant, structured red. Made entirely with organically farmed Sangiovese, the savory, elegant palate delivers wild cherry, pomegranate, baking spice and a hint of orange zest. Firm, polished tannins and fresh acidity provide age-worthy support and balance. Drink 2019–2029.
title: Castagnoli 2014 Terrazze Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Closely planted 70-year-old vines are behind this richly concentrated wine. Dense tannins and a good structure are allied to the ripe blackberry fruits and acidity. Dark and firm, the wine still needs time. Drink from 2019.
title: Château de Chatelard 2016 Cuvée Terre de Lumière  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Traditional herbal berry and olive aromas are ideal for an old-vines Cauquenes red like this. In the mouth, this Malbec blend is flush and chewy. Blackberry, spice and toast flavors finish with power and an overriding sense of balanced ripeness. Drink through 2021.
title: Clos des Fous 2014 Cauquenina Viñas Viejas de Cauquenes Red (Chile)

description: From the Grand Cras vineyard, this wine is powered by firm tannins and a strongly mineral texture. Its rich red fruits are right there, developing well. Age this powerful wine and drink from 2019.
title: Collin-Bourisset 2016 Les Grands Cras  (Morgon)

description: Ripe red berries, cola, chocolate, florals and a meaty baseline shine through on both the nose and the palate. This is juicy and fruity, with silky smooth tannins, a savory backbone and and an easy-going elegance. Simultaneously glug worthy and serious.
title: Craggy Range 2014 Te Muna Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Martinborough)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This wine is deep in fruit flavors, and has impressive structure in terms of extraction, body and an abundance of fine-grained tannin. It brings soft black cherry and cedar to the aroma, dark chocolate and blueberry to the palate and coats the mouth with a firm but velvety texture. Best after 2020.
title: Darcie Kent Vineyards 2013 Old Vines Merlot (Livermore Valley)

description: Produced from organically grown grapes and with 12 months in wood, this wine is open and rich. A Burgundian character gives it a dense texture and obvious aging potential. With firm tannins as well as great red fruits, the wine will age and should be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Capréoles 2016 Diaclase  (Régnié)

description: This structured wine has oak aging and old vines to thank for its density and ripe concentration. Its power and dark character will let it ripen well. Full of fruit and acidity, it has aging potential. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2015 Vieilles Vignes  (Morgon)

description: This is a rich, full wine, given structure both by wood aging and fruit tannins. Weight, density and good acidity are coming together in an harmonious whole. From 60-year-old vines, it will be ready from late 2018.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016 Piron et Lameloise Quartz  (Chénas)

description: With vineyards in the family since 1877, this producer offers a good range of wines, of which this is an excellent example. It is fruity, firm and seriously structured with juicy black fruit and acidity allied to a solid tannic base. Drink this ageworthy wine from 2019.
title: Domaines Laurent Perrachon & Fils 2016 Roche Bleue  (Juliénas)

description: Complex elegant aromas of sandalwood, spiced candle, blackberry and chocolate set the stage for an alert snappy palate with acidic and tannic clamp. Plenty of oak and related vanilla and spice flavors accent crisp jumpy red-fruit notes prior to a firm long finish. Drink through 2022.
title: Errazuriz 2014 Don Maximiano Founder's Reserve Red (Aconcagua Valley)

description: This opens with aromas of iris, black-skinned berry, truffle and leather. Made with 90% Sangiovese and 10% Canaiolo, the juicy, balanced palate delivers fleshy black cherry, black pepper, pipe tobacco and a touch of balsamic herb alongside polished tannins. Enjoy through 2027.
title: Fattoria La Ripa 2010 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine's intense, creamy, pear-laden nose leads to a textured palate full of yeast and miso-like savoriness. Citrus pith notes and bright lemon freshness only add to the mouthfeel. The finish is lip-smacking and savory.
title: Felsner 2016 Alte Reben Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Smoked pork, dark soil, forest floor, purple flowers and dark-purple fruit show on the nose of this bottling. Rust and dried hibiscus petals are evident on the sip along with potpourri-laced cranberry and carnation flavors. It is quite complex and tasty.
title: Foley 2015 Barrel Select Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Alka typically delivers size and oak, and this vintage shows a thick coating of oak on top of herbal cassis and blackberry scents. A concentrated, lively palate is bright in acidity, while oaky, savory flavors of balsamic berry fruits and chocolate finish baked, spicy, bold and chewy. Drink through 2023.
title: François Lurton 2014 Hacienda Aurcano Alka Organic Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: It's been a long time since Concha y Toro has put out a Don Melchor Cabernet with this much concentration and overall quality. An earthy nose with foresty berry aromas and generous but controlled oak sets up a fully stacked palate that's chewy but not fiercely tannic. Dark toasty chocolaty black-fruit flavors finish with a note of cherry cough drop and moderate acidity that says this is more or less ready now. Drink through 2025.
title: Concha y Toro 2015 Don Melchor Puente Alto Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Puente Alto)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Quite dark in the glass, this bottling shows hearty black fruit, peppercorn and a touch of asphalt. Classic Cabernet Franc flavors show through the richness of the palate, with notes of crushed pepper and loamy soil playing up against a boysenberry backdrop.
title: Daou 2015 Cabernet Franc (Paso Robles)

description: This young wine has great potential. It shows weight and a powerful tannic structure as well as bold, ripe black fruits. Coming from the top sector of Morgon, the wine has concentration and richness. It needs to age, and won't be ready before 2019.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: The pear fruit on the nose of this wine is so heady and pristine it almost comes across as candied. It continues on the palate, where it's joined by a fluid and creamy texture, with hints of citrus pith and salt. An intriguing, elegant and taut package. The finish is long and recalls pure lemon zest.
title: Eder 2016 Raubern Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A touch of yeasty savor appears on the subdued nose of this wine. On the palate, everything seems elevated and poised: there is concentration and texture as well as creamy lemon freshness, deeply savory yeast notes and a salty, herbal tang. This is little pleasure bomb waiting to unfold. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Eichinger 2016 Ried Lamm Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: A sublime set of caramel and honeyed late-harvest aromas couldn't be more inviting. This Torontel is the best dessert wine from Chile. It's intense and pure, with acidic balance. Rich amplified flavors of caramel, toffee and cocoa finish with grace on the finish. This is like a trip to Europe: think Vin Santo or Vendange Tardive.
title: Erasmo 2010 Late Harvest Torontel (Maule Valley)

description: A ripe, tropical pineapple vibe adds richness to the more customary fruit notes of pear in this wine. The palate broadens into rich, savory miso flavors and leesy texture before being countered by lively, pervading lemon freshness. A big, dramatic wine with incisive brightness. Drink 2029–2030.
title: Fischer 2016 Privat Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very pretty and precise on the nose, this bottling, which is quite delicate for the rich-leaning region, shows candied pomegranate, cinnamon spice, hibiscus and wet gravel on the nose. Wild strawberry and rhubarb flavors pop on the sip, alongside watermelon rind and a very rocky and tense minerality. There is tons of tannic and acidic depth.
title: Furthermore 2014 Rosella's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Fresh, almost pristine black cherry beckons from the glass, followed by a gentle whiff of woodsmoke. The medium-bodied palate has the same combination of pristine freshness and gentle oak, showing fresh-faced fruit laced by whisper tannins and delineated by vivid acidity. This is a wonderfully elegant wine with a real force of life inside it. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Gernot and Heike Heinrich 2015 Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: Citrus foliage, conifer resin and pure lemon zest aromas create an enticing nose in this wine. On the linear palate, riper Mandarin and Meyer lemon notions take hold, creating an immensely juicy, zesty sensation. It's refreshing now, but if you can resist drinking it right away, it will develop beautifully. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Hiedler 2016 Ried Gaisberg Reserve Riesling (Kamptal)

description: Sun-ripened Mirabelle plum and bright lemon zest notes make up this wine's juicy but fresh nose. The palate backs the fruit with textured zestiness and wonderfully forward lemon freshness. It's taut, concentrated and streamlined, its pure lemon notes echoing forever. Gorgeous now but with real potential. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Jäger 2016 Ried Achleiten Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: The merest touch of earthy, tarry smoke plays on the closed nose. The body presents a more generous picture of juicy, ripe plum and blackberry fruit, aided by fresh acidity and a finely woven tannic structure. This brims with ripe fruit and is already approachable, but it will develop its alluring aromatics even more.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2015 Holzspur Pinot Noir (Thermenregion)

description: A pure note of lemon blossom paves the way for wet earth, tart green apple, citrus foliage and lemon scents in this wine. The palate is similarly ethereal, presenting tender lemon notes against an earthier background. This is all done with an admirably light touch, which suits this linear, taut, dry Riesling well. It's lovely now but will be far more intriguing with some bottle age. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Jurtschitsch 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Riesling (Kamptal)

Designation: Clos Apalta Limited Release Estate Bottled
description: Right away, aromas of spicy black fruits, balsam wood, clove and earth announce a special wine of extra high quality. In the mouth, this is ripe and concentrated (even for a cool year) and ideally balanced. Dark oaky spicy blackberry flavors finish flush, toasty and loaded. Drink through 2028.
title: Lapostolle 2013 Clos Apalta Limited Release Estate Bottled Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Plum and blackberry notes have a touch of smoke and mocha notes, which mellow on the taut palate. This shows lovely tension between ample fruit and taut freshness, with fine tannins giving a pleasant gauze-like structure. There is warmth and power but both are well contained and held on a tight leash. This shows that well-toned muscle takes you further than mere weight. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Markus Altenburger 2015 Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: An almost maple syrup-like richness comes with the first whiff of cherry fruit on the nose. Similar, more game-like aromatics continue on the concentrated palate that is packed tightly, almost neatly with fruit and fine tannin. This seems compact, with a fine but firm grip. Let it relax for a while, then enjoy its crunchy red-cherry freshness. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Markus Altenburger 2015 Ried Gritschenberg Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: A zesty lemon note pervades this wine's lovely Russet pear peel scents. The palate has an equally lithe and elegant feel. The midpalate texture is like taut and delicate lace. Everything is there, but in filigree. The finish is really lip-smacking and fresh.
title: Markus Huber 2016 Ried Berg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Traisental)

description: The nose wants to give away little but the palate is more forthcoming: red cherry is ripe and crunchy. Firm, structured tannins hold notions of vanilla and pepper while vivid, welcome freshness pervades the entire palate. This is elegant and will come into its own with some bottle age. The ripe but tart cherry leaves a lasting impression on the dry finish. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Netzl 2015 Anna-Christina Red (Carnuntum)

description: Citrus foliage and tangerine zest notes play softly on the nose of this wine. The palate fizzes quietly, like a toned-down lemon sherbet. Everything shows that this is a very young wine with great concentration and potential. The lemon note captures the slender, linear palate and creates a lip-smacking finish. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Pichler-Krutzler 2016 Ried Kellerberg Riesling (Wachau)

description: Sun-warmed hay, gentle pear and ripe lemon notes make for a quietly aromatic nose in this wine. That same harmonised touch is also apparent on the almost-creamy palate. Deep, savory citrus pith and hay tones create an understated backdrop for the lovely, gentle fruit. Very elegant from start to finish.
title: Proidl 2016 Ried Ehrenfels Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Mellow yet ripe russet pear notes are spiked with lemon freshness on the nose of this wine. That wonderful and enticing contrast continues on the palate, where the juicy, rounded ripeness of pear and the brisk streak of lemon gain control alternatingly. It makes for exciting, refreshing and joyful drinking. The finish is brisk, clean, lasting and dry. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Proidl 2016 Ried Hochäcker Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Mango and passion fruit notes put a rich, tropical spin on the nose of this wine. The palate, on the other hand, offers zesty lemon and salty stone flavors and yeasty texture. Altogether, this makes for a complex, refreshing and captivating wine that sings with concentrated fruit and zestiness. The finish rings long with pure, zesty lemon. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Proidl 2016 Ried Pfenningberg Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Made for the long haul from one of Otago's most highly respected winemakers, Rudi Bauer, this is drinking well now, offering up a heady mix of raisiny red currants, florals, soy sauce, mushrooms and tree sap. It's medium to full bodied, with a line of focused acidity amidst dusty tannins and a long, cumin and herbal finish. Drink now–2027.
title: Quartz Reef 2014 Bendigo Estate Hielo y Fuego Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

description: A touch of yeast still mixes with the clean citrus zest notes on the nose of this wine. On the palate, that leesiness cushions the uncompromising pure lemon freshness. It's concentrated in body and dry, vivid and brisk in its flavors, with a stucture that will keep it delightful until 2030, if not longer. Rejoice in the lip-smacking finish.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Ried Kögl Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: This is a new offering from the producer, sparked by winemaker Elias Fernandez's desire to make a Merlot-based blend, rather than a varietal Merlot. It is an estate-grown blend of 56% Merlot, 28% Cabernet Sauvignon and 16% Malbec. Rich and concentrated, it's delightfully robust and vibrant, offering a mix of blackberry, blueberry and caramel chocolate flavors that finish in soft, integrated oak.
title: Shafer 2015 TD-9 Red (Napa Valley)

description: A slight touch of flinty reduction accents this wine's fresh lemon zest notes, hinting at how very young it is. These qualities also pervade the taut, linear palate but have an earthier, textured edge. The finish recalls pure lemon zest and has a clean, lip-smacking finale. Drink 2019–2030.
title: Tegernseerhof 2016 Ried Steinertal Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: Ripe, heady aromas of cassis, raisin and spice cake hold nothing back but aren't overdone. The palate is staunch in feel, with firm but manageable tannins, while this tastes of plum, berry fruits and tomato touched by savory oak. The full yet tight finish offers plenty of balance. Drink through 2025.
title: Undurraga 2014 Altazor Red (Maipo Valley)

description: This wine presents as weighty and broad on entry, a successful exploration of juicy black cherry, toasted oak and cardamom that is both succulent and complex. It develops nuance and balance in the glass, with an exotic floral quality that's hard to resist.
title: Black Kite 2015 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: This is yet another stellar vintage of this unique blend, a mix of Muscadelle, Sémillon, Colombard and Palomino. Floral as a garden brimming in gardenia, it's dry and gravelly, with distinct flavors of quince and melon. Light on its feet, it's well worth seeking.
title: Carlisle 2015 The Derivative White (Sonoma County)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: A blend of 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Syrah, the aromas draw you into the glass, with light notes of savory herb, cassis, pipe tobacco, spice and crushed rock. It brings a flat-out gorgeous sense of texture, covering the palate from end to end with cherry flavors that glide on the finish. The feel is knee-buckling. Best after 2021.
title: Charles Smith 2014 King Coal Stoneridge Vineyard Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A gamy streak plays on the nose as well as a touch of tart, red fruit. The palate also has an earthy undercurrent and a peppery, gamy edge. The fruit shimmers shyly amidst the finely taut tannins, with the promise of luscuous red cherry to come. This will benefit from further integration and will make an intriguing, savory wine.
title: Loimer 2015 Anning Gumpoldskirchen Pinot Noir (Niederösterreich)

description: Aromas of ripe black-skinned berries, scorched earth, exotic spices, menthol and a whiff of oak are just some of the scents you'll find in this vibrant red. The elegant palate delivers mature Marasca cherry, crushed raspberry, orange zest, star anise and a hint of espresso alongside polished, fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Marchesi Antinori 2012 Badia a Passignano Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Soft, velvety, lush and broad, this is a full-figured, darkly brooding wine, steeped in concentrated flavors of strawberry and blackberry. Baked bread, milk chocolate and rose petals accent it well, with supportive, measured oak adding a touch of toast.
title: Merry Edwards 2015 Meredith Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: From a historic site planted in 1905, this impressive wine is defined by well-crafted layers of black pepper, tomato leaf and blackberry. Its sizable length and breadth is laced in cinnamon spice and ample acidity that reins it in from becoming overdone.
title: Mike and Molly Hendry 2015 R.W. Moore Vineyard Zinfandel (Coombsville)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: A super dark color and oodles of dark fruit in both the aroma and the flavor make this a rich and seductive wine. It packs in notes of black cherry, blackberry jam and dark chocolate without obvious oakiness even though winemaker Marco Cappelli aged it 20 months in 50% new oak. The wine has good firm structure from lots of soft tannins, full body and moderate acidity making it very impressive, concentrated and layered.
title: Miraflores 2013 Tempranillo (El Dorado)

description: Just a whiff of black currant accompanies the dark berry fruit and cocoa spice on the nose. The palate adds a touch of tar to these aromas while firm tannins dry the sides of the mouth. The fruit is concentrated but brightened by ample freshness. A dense, punchy wine in an international style. This will mellow with bottle age. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Netzl 2015 Edles Tal Red (Carnuntum)

description: This is still closed and needs both swirling and swishing to awake. Once it opens up a taut, structured red appears with dense, firm but fine tannins corsetting ripe black cherry and blueberry fruit. There is midpalate weight countered by freshness and glimpses of cinnamon and pepper spice. This has impact but is not heavy, it is concentrated but finely woven. Its lovely balance will benefit from bottle age. Drink 2022–2030.
title: Nittnaus Anita und Hans 2015 Comondor Red (Burgenland)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Salted caramel oak is met by pineapple upside down cake in this rich, rewarding and crazily complex white from a well-farmed site. Focused acidity keeps the more voluptuous aspects at bay, settling them into refreshing layers of texture and vibrancy.
title: Patz & Hall 2015 Ritchie Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Big and ripe black fruit is bolstered by sweet thyme, bay leaf and mint on the nose of this powerful bottling by Rachel Silkowski. The wine lands with power and aplomb on the palate, blending star anise, nutmeg and licorice flavors with tangy black plum fruit and a strong acidity to carry it all.
title: Rasi 2016 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: A generous influence from new oak barrels works beautifully on this full-bodied spice-saturated and richly concentrated wine. It has everything from subtle cinnamon and cardamom to ripe and lush black cherry notes to a lingering finish that echoes spices and dark fruit. Fine-grained tannins shape the mouthfeel and add welcome grip.
title: Renwood 2014 Tempranillo (Amador County)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Sea salt, mandarin zest, fresh white spring flowers and lemon balm make for a very appealing and fragrant nose on this vineyard blend by Mikey Giugni and Michael Brughelli. The palate is zesty, fresh and savory, with flavors of salted apple slices, preserved lemons, toast and a hint of roasted wood.
title: Scar of the Sea 2015 Seven Leagues Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: This perennial powerhouse of a wine is a blend of 89% Syrah and 11% Petite Sirah, aged 30 months in 100% new French oak. It offers a hard-hitting intensity of stewed plum, cherry and buttery vanilla, with thick, structured tannins that are firm and integrated. Meat, black licorice and black pepper combine effortlessly and should shine bright after aging. Enjoy from 2029 and beyond.
title: Shafer 2014 Relentless Syrah-Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: Coming from a vineyard planted in 1973 at an elevation of 1,450 feet, the aromas draw you into the glass, with intoxicating notes of cream, ginger, pumpkin spice and chamomile. The palate possesses a creamy texture but also a sense of weightlessness that provides a lot of appeal. The balance is exquisite.
title: Sixto 2014 Moxee Chardonnay (Washington)
