description: Aromatic clouds of citrus foliage, green apple and fresh lemon waft from the glass. The palate is defined by lemon and tart apple flavors but also riper notions of yellow plum. This is fruity, fresh and light on its feet, with a dry, pure and lasting finish.
title: Steininger 2016 Steinhaus Reserve Riesling (Kamptal)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Though a bit lighter than in years past due to the tough vintage, this wine is still gamy and feral as usual, with aromas of creosote, roasted lamb and rosemary layered atop a soft red-currant base. It's light in texture, with bright raspberry compote flavors as well as pine oil and juniper.
title: Stolo 2015 Hillside Reserve Syrah (San Luis Obispo County)

description: This Chardonnay shows great concentration and intensity, yet also poise and elegance. Fruity scents of yellow apple and lemon custard are laced with spicy hits of toasted brioche and pie crust. The palate is rich and smooth in texture, but not overdone, thanks to ample acidity and a streak of orange oil that keeps the ripe flavors in check. It's well-balanced and incredibly harmonious, with a long, evolving finish. Drink now–2022.
title: Storm 2015 Vrede Chardonnay (Hemel en Aarde)

description: A funky, earthy touch of freshly peeled russet pear hovers on the nose of this wine. A slight lemon oil note shines through. The palate has pure, ripe citrus notes of lemon and tangerine and a pleasantly bitter, pithy backdrop that lends texture and structure. The finish is reminiscent of pure lemon. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Tegernseerhof 2016 Ried Loibenberg Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: The nose is till closed but gives a touch of tart red cherry and a hint of game. With some air a floral overtone appears. The palate is gently extracted, unforced and presents beautifully pure notes of tart, red cherry. This has a translucent, pure quality and superfine, graceful tannins. Bright acidity lends finesse. There is nothing heavy or heavy-handed about this. How wonderful. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Tinhof 2015 Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: This mile-deep Syrah opens with spicy berry, black currant and graphite aromas that blare quality and intensity. Purity of fruit is way up the ladder, with touches of cherry, plum, cassis and blackberry all exuding ripeness. Only on a stout finish does this show oak via a chocolate flavor. Drink through 2024.
title: Ventisquero 2013 Pangea Apalta Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is aged in American oak and blended with 12% Petite Sirah. Concentrated blackberry fruit rides a foundation of soft, silky and well-integrated succulence, with touches of leather saddle, black pepper, smoke and brick. While full bodied, it manages to drink light on its feet.
title: Pedroncelli 2015 Mother Clone Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Shy aromas of wet stone and black cherry mark the nose. The palate shows a levity of juicy fruit, yet also a deep well of black cherry richness that needs opening up amidst the shyness and gentle gauze of fine tannins. The long finish seems to promise further development. A lovely, well-judged and rather elegant wine. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Artner 2016 Ried Steinäcker Zweigelt (Carnuntum)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This variety is making waves in Washington and this wine demonstrates why. The aromas are effusive, with notes of dark cherry, dried herb and barrel spice. The palate is filled to the brim with rich fruit flavors that show focus and depth, while the tannins are combed to a fine sheen. It's high on the hedonist scale—a standout offering.
title: B. Leighton 2014 Olsen Brothers Petit Verdot (Yakima Valley)

description: This wine's ripe yellow pear notes are joined by riper notions of yellow plum, with a characteristic salty, herbal tang. The concentrated, fresh palate picks up those notes and puts the yeasty soy-like savor to the fore. Salty, dry freshness rings long into the finish.
title: Bernhard Ott 2016 Ried Rosenberg Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: The sun-ripened yellow pear notes on the nose of this wine still have the faintest touch of flinty reduction. The concentrated salty, yeasty flavors on the palate bring a pithy, lip-smacking note of miso and soy. This is impressive and lasting. Drink 2018–2026.
title: Bernhard Ott 2016 Ried Stein Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: Inviting aromas of wild berry, pressed rose, new leather, culinary spice and a balsamic note lift out of the glass. On the imposing, full-bodied palate, licorice and pipe tobacco notes accent raspberry compote and steeped cherry framed in firm, fine-grained tannins. You'll also notice the warmth of alcohol on the close but it's well supported by chewy fruit. Drink 2020–2031.
title: Brezza 2011 Sarmassa Vigna Bricco Riserva  (Barolo)

description: Both pear and citrus pith inform the shy nose of this wine. The palate expresses more of that pithy citrus, adding streaks of bright lemon and the slightest touch of salty yeast. It's concentrated yet taut and utterly refreshing, with an almost cleansing quality.
title: Buchegger 2016 Ried Vordernberg Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Alluring aromas of wild berry, fragrant purple flower, new leather, dark culinary spice and chopped herb emerge on this classically crafted red. The vibrant, youthfully austere palate offers ripe red cherry, cranberry, tobacco and clove framed in bright acidity and firm, refined tannins. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Cafaggio 2013 Basilica Solatio Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Lavish and ripe, this full-figured red offers loads of plump black-cherry and raspberry flavors. It's surprisingly vibrant on the midpalate, framed by fine, delicate tannins and a bracing hint of charcoal on the finish. Lovely already, it's concentrated enough to improve through 2025.
title: Château de Saint-Cosme 2014  Gigondas

description: Whiffs of black pepper, licorice and vanilla lend a warm, spicy tone to ripe blueberry and black cherry flavors in this bold, richly concentrated wine that's both unctuously fruity and earthy. The finish is warm and supple, marked by fine, persistent tannins.
title: Clos Saint Michel 2015 G-S-M (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Sourced from a parcel of 80-year-old vines, this wine is rich and dense, with a strong mineral texture and ripe black plum and cherry flavors. Drink this powerful, concentrated and structured wine from 2019.
title: Domaine Anita 2016 Très Vieille Vigne Les Caves  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This opens with delicate aromas of black plum and baking spice. The simple palate delivers vanilla, prune and star anise alongside round tannins.
title: Pepi Lignana 2013 Poggio Colombi  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Medicinal berry and plum aromas result in a latex scent. High acidity creates pinching and narrowness on a palate with plum and berry flavors that are more racy and red than dark and ripe. Edgy tannins resurface on a raw finish.
title: Portal del Priorat 2014 Gotes Grenache-Carignan (Montsant)

description: This ultraripe blend of 85% Negroamaro and 15% Malvasia Nera is jammy in black- and red-fruit aromas, with sweet baking spice aromas peppered throughout. The palate feels thick, with black fruit tones under a sheen of cocoa dust and licorice. A bit of acidity helps clean things up come the finish.
title: San Marzano 2013 Talò Red (Salice Salentino)

description: Rich and fruity, this bargain buy shows strawberry and blackberry jam aromas and flavors, speckled with green herb and floral tones. There's some breadth to the palate from the ripe fruit, yet enough tannic grip and acidity to maintain balance.
title: San Marzano 2016 Il Pumo Negroamaro (Salento)

description: Primarily Cabernet Franc, this blend has pleasing aromas of cranberry, plum, lavender, mint and licorice. There is also a charred oak note that carries through onto the palate and overpowers the other flavors. Zippy acidity adds a welcome freshness that cuts though some of the rough tannins. The finish is long and woody.
title: Sunset Hills 2016 Sunset Red (Virginia)

description: Aromas suggesting underbrush and smoke carry over to the simple palate along with raw cranberry and a hint of clove. It's light bodied, with racy acidity and a lean finish.
title: Triacca 2014 Sassella  (Valtellina Superiore)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of stewed prune and toasted notes lead the nose on this brawny red. Searing alcohol dominates the palate and throws it off balance while blackberry extract, licorice and toasted oak notes linger in the background. Grainy tannins give it an astringent finish.
title: Valdipiatta 2015  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Prune and cassis aromas are timid and earthy. This crianza from a cool, rainy year feels choppy, but has substance. Spicy, peppery red-fruit flavors are dry and finish with acidic freshness.
title: Bodegas Faustino 2013 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: Sarsaparilla, cherry and blackberry show medium intensity on the nose. Light in feel, with juicy dark fruit flavors shot through with pomegranate acidity, the palate is supported by medium-grained tannins. A light cherry cola flavor rounds out the finish.
title: Cantele 2014 Riserva Negroamaro (Salice Salentino)

description: Soft aromas of lemon oil, dried hay and crushed stone carry the nose. The palate shows a bit more ripeness in yellow apple and grapefruit flavors, with a mild vein of acidity offering some balance.
title: Cantina Le Grotte 2015 Selva Della Rocca Falanghina (Puglia)

description: Yeasty pear and melon aromas are basic. That could be said for the palate, which is soda-like. Mild citrus and apple flavors dry out on a finish with grassy notes.
title: Casteller NV Brut Sparkling (Cava)

description: With more Cabernet Sauvignon than Merlot, this wine has delicious black-currant flavors as well as a dry edge to the tannins from 12 months in wood. It leaves a bright, fruity aftertaste. Drink from 2021.
title: Château de la Croix 2015  Médoc

description: This wine is ripe, bright with black-currant fruit and crisp acidity. It is simple, easy and fruity. Oak aging gives a smooth character to the soft, warm wine that is ready to drink.
title: Château Mayne de Valence 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Bruised apple peel and very ripe, yellow pear signal ripeness and abundant fruit. While the palate is light it is similarly generous with fruit, helped along by a touch of residual sweetness. This is fruity and friendly, with an off-dry finish. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Roland Schmitt 2016 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Candied flavors like caramel and butterscotch make this full-bodied wine seem sweet, while a lush, soft texture coats the palate.
title: Ivory and Burt 2016 Chardonnay (Lodi)

description: While quiet on the nose, hints of fresh apple and blossoms are a pleasant introduction to this brisk, uncomplicated white blend. Off dry and light in body, it's an easy quaffing wine, with a fresh grapefruit finish.
title: Karl Josef 2016 Piesporter Michelsberg White (Mosel)

description: Jammy red berry aromas and flavors mix with grilled herbs and are grounded by a sanguine vein. Medium in weight and structure, the acidity starts off soft but builds on the midpalate, offering a bit a levity on the finish.
title: La Fiera 2016 Primitivo (Salento)

description: Earthy berry aromas are a bit stalky and flat. This crianza is firm and choppy, but also stewed on the palate. Spicy plum and olive flavors are ordinary if not a touch underripe.
title: Loriñon 2013 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: Oily aromas of orange peel are lively but imprecise. This Verdejo ranks as fruity but simple, with plumpness and citric acidity. Briny citrus flavors give off a sense of grape skins that extend onto the finish.
title: Marqués de Cáceres 2016 Verdejo (Rueda)

description: Reduction clings to the nose but the body is fresh and clean. This is dry and lacks a little fruit and direction but has freshness to redeem itself. The finish is dry and clean. Drink soon.
title: Martin Schaetzel 2016 Cuvée Réserve Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Light aromas of white flower, lemon curd and spice are followed by sleek, light fruit flavors. It's a straightforward, varietally correct wine.
title: Maryhill 2016 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Pungent pineapple aromas, along with a hint of kerosene, indicate that this will not be your standard style of Oregon Pinot Gris. The intense herbaceous character dominates, suggesting some of the stylistic terminology that might accompany a particularly biting Sauvignon Blanc. Some 10% was fermented in concrete egg.
title: Maysara 2016 Arsheen Pinot Gris (McMinnville)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: An entry-level effort, this has thinly-ripened berry flavors, stiff tannins and acids like a squeeze of fresh lemon juice. But it is the aggressive earthy and herbal components that dominate and continue to resonate through the finish.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 Citizen's Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Powdery nectarine and orange-rind aromas comprise the nose on this tangy rosé Cava with a soda-like mouthfeel. Slightly tart apricot and orange flavors show latent bitterness prior to a yeasty-tasting finish that's not that long.
title: Pinord NV M Marrugat Brut Rosé Familiar Sparkling (Spain)

Appellation: Napa County-Sonoma County, Napa-Sonoma, California, US
description: A hearty 12% Cabernet Sauvignon is added to this wine, which is aged in 100% French oak. Reduced, with sizable oak influence, it shows dried herb components wrapped in bittersweet black pepper and leather.
title: Pride Mountain 2014 Merlot (Napa County-Sonoma County)

description: Almost tawny in color, this wine offers aromas of red and black plum, dried fig and prune, with secondary notes of cedar and balsamic. These aromas translate onto the palate where the tannins are abundant, yet silky, and the acidity isn't shy.
title: Rosemont 2015 Cabernet Franc (Virginia)

description: This lightly perfumed wine is also crisp and fruity. Good acidity, citrus fruit and a zesty texture give freshness and a bright character. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Seacampo 2016 Monte Serra Branco White (Dão)

description: Melon and kiwi aromas are a touch green. This wine feels tangy but also plump, while lemon-lime flavors give way to skins-driven bitterness. A bland finish with residual tang is direct in feel.
title: Senda Verde 2016 Godello (Bierzo)

description: Smoky roast beef aromas mingle with peppercorns and black cherries on the foxy nose. The palate shows ripe cherry and cranberry, alongside cedar, pepper and sweet baking spices. The tannins provide an appealing grip, with balanced acidity and good structure. The finish kicks off on peppery spice then softens into raspberries.
title: Shelburne 2015 Marquette (Vermont)

description: This Viognier is bursting with passion fruit, honeydew, peach and elderflower aromas that carry right through to the palate. The mouthfeel is a little flimsy but there are great flavors here.
title: Sunset Hills 2016 Viognier (Virginia)

description: Salty and floral, this wine offers deft layers of green apple, peach and pineapple—its richness subdued and in check—rounded by a bouquet of freshness that persists from start to finish. There's texture and complexity within nuance and restraint.
title: Gary Farrell 2015 Durell Vineyard Chardonnay (Sonoma Valley)

description: Aromatic, ripe red apple and melon notes appear first on the nose but are underpinned by a savory streak of salty yeast. The palate brings the yeastiness to the fore, giving a lovely creamy texture. The finish is fresh but mellow.
title: Heinrich Hartl 2016 Rotgipfler (Thermenregion)

description: Soft and fleshy, this is a nicely made, well-structured wine, dusty in black currant, clove and cedar notes, showing plenty of varietal character. Billowy tannins surround secondary flavors of black cherry and tobacco which add to its muted power and grace.
title: Heritance 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: This wine's abundant yellow pear notes suggest rounded ripeness on the nose. The palate comes in with a creamy, textured mouthfeel and salty, spicy nuances. It's concentrated, round and rich but remains toned and dry. Lovely.
title: Josef Bauer 2016 Ried Rosenberg Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: This structured wine offers complex layers of tannins. Black fruits are very present, still young and developing, making for a wine that is dense, rich and needs time. Drink from 2019.
title: Lucien Lardy 2016 Les Roches  (Fleurie)

description: This structured wine comes from a single parcel in Moulin-à-Vent. Packed with both tannins and black-plum fruits, it is ripe, fruity and crisp. Drink from late 2018.
title: Lucien Lardy 2016 Les Thorins  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Pronounced earth tones on the first whiff dissipate with aeration to reveal a complex array of tropical fruit and floral aromas. Rich and broad on the palate, this wine offers a lavish concoction of sun-kissed tangerine, pineapple and peach flavors kissed with vanilla and spice. The finish is a touch warming but silken and expansive.
title: Michel & Stéphane Ogier 2014 La Combe de Malleval  (Condrieu)

description: A powerful core of wood aging makes this a dry, solid wine that has excellent aging potential. Black cherries and spice contrast with the rich structure. From one of the top sectors of Morgon, the wine will be best drunk from 2019.
title: Mommessin 2016 Les Grandes Mises Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: Largely structured and weighty, this well-made wine is youthful in its grippy tannins and full embrace of huckleberry and black-cherry fruit, the exuberance tempered by complements of black licorice and toasted oak. This will be happy in the cellar; enjoy best 2024–2029.
title: Mt. Brave 2014 Merlot (Mount Veeder)

description: This wine is alluring in aromas of black olive, raw meat, wet earth and stewed raspberry. The palate is satiny in feel, with well-balanced acidity and savory, olivey tannins. Drink now–2020.
title: Mt. Difficulty 2014 Bannockburn Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

description: This Chenin Blanc-based blend, with 12% Clairette, 6% Viognier, 4% Grenache Blanc and 4% Sémillon, opens with medium-intense aromas of apple skin, melon rind, honeysuckle, strawberry hull and orange pith. The full, medium-weight palate boasts ample acidity to complement the rich beeswax, orange oil and pressed flower tones, with a softly pithy texture that lends structure and length to the enduring finish. It's drinkable now, but should benefit from some additional maturity. Drink 2020–2024.
title: Mullineux 2015 Old Vines White (Swartland)

description: While bold in frame and body, this lusciously fruity wine offers structure and balance as well. Plush dark cherry and preserved-strawberry flavors envelope the palate, juxtaposed by hits of spice and leather. The finish is long and warming but lingers pleasantly with fine, persistent tannins and a spicy licorice note. Drink through 2030.
title: Notre Dame de Cousignac 2014 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Full bodied and nicely dry, this well-built wine starts with smoky black-pepper aromas that lead to mouthfilling blackberry and black-cherry flavors supported by firm tannins. It's the kind of wine that will pair beautifully with a meaty or cheesy meal, because it has the texture to match that kind of richness.
title: Paris Valley Road 2014 Zinfandel (California)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a great and extremely fairly priced taste of what the Pence team is up to on the eastern edge of the appellation. Sweet sandalwood, red plum, mulberry, black tea, juniper and a hint of maple show on the nose. The palate combines tart black-plum skins with cherry tomato and dewy garden-herb flavors.
title: Pence Ranch 2016 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: From a single site in Carneros, the Stewart Vineyard, this wine is exuberant in red fruit, with a sly hint of tobacco and tomato leaf. Big ripe tannins wrap around a meaty side show of garrigue and black pepper, finishing in elegant ripeness.
title: Peter Franus 2014 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: The perfume of very ripe, yellow pear and the salty tang of miso are equally strong on the nose of this wine. They continue their contrasting play on the palate, where chervil, pepper and soy savoriness takes over. Pithy citrus and a lots of midpalate texture leave a lasting impression.
title: R&A; Pfaffl 2016 Goldjoch Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: Made predominantly from Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, this bold full-bodied blend is invitingly floral in cherry blossom and coconut flakes, the oak showy and present. Cherry and cassis flavors are rounded and rich on the palate and the body brawny and broadly defined in tannin and oak.
title: Raymond 2014 Salon Privé Red (Napa Valley)

description: This is a lightly spiced 100% varietal wine from a famous site, a showcase of burly black cherry seasoned in cedar, graphite and vanilla. Full bodied, it holds its ripeness well, integrating the weight into a graceful package of understated power.
title: Salvestrin 2013 Three D Dr. Crane Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (St. Helena)

description: Rose petal, new leather, forest floor and balsamic whiffs of menthol take the lead on this youthfully austere red. Linear and vibrant, the full-bodied palate delivers Marasca cherry, orange zest, cinnamon, licorice and an earthy hint of game. Bright acidity and taut tannins provide an age-worthy structure. Fans of classic of Barolo will love this. Drink 2023–2938.
title: Schiavenza 2013 del Comune di Serralunga d'Alba  (Barolo)

description: This wine's wet earth-laden nose is wonderfully framed by fresh lemon notes. The palate has bright, lemony concentration and appetizing freshness. There's something sunny and joyful about this. The finish is zesty and long. Delicious now, it has the potential to age. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Schwarzböck 2016 Aichleiten Riesling (Niederösterreich)

description: This wine's fresh, juicy yellow pear-inflected nose has a touch of ripe cantaloupe and an herbal hint of conifer. The palate continues with intriguing notes of fir honey but gives the starring role to ripe pear and fresh apple notes. It's lithe and fresh, always with that heady hint of honey.
title: Sepp Moser 2016 Ried Schnabel Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This wine's subdued nose just hints at pear and yeast. The palate combines fresh, pure green pear fruit with a slightly textured, sonorous hint of sage and soy-scented yeast. The overall tone is one of wonderfully rich pear fruit.
title: Steininger 2016 Grand Grü Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: A wonderfully tangy note of cress and lime peel creates an unusual but very appetizing nose in this wine. The slender palate adds lemony freshness and a mineral tone reminiscent of wet pebbles. A lovely, clear-cut example of the variety, with gentle but pristine flavors and a refreshing finish. Lovely now, it's sure to round out in the coming years. Drink 2018–2026.
title: Tinhof 2016 Neuburger (Leithaberg)

description: Honeycomb and marzipan accent bright tangerine and peach flavors in this delectably silken sip. It's unctuous and plush in mouthfeel but balanced firmly with sunny citrus acidity and airy plumes of violet and honeysuckle florals.
title: Vidal-Fleury 2014  Condrieu

description: As the last bottling of this vineyard from this producer, this is a fine way to go out. It's a salty, gravelly and complex red, aromatic in rose, lavender and orange blossom. Tart acidity uplifts the weightier aspect of the present tannins, as dried herb, sea spray and black tea season the fruit.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Hirsch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: From the site around the winery on Westside Road—which is planted to heirloom clones—this wine has a refrained, balanced approach to ripeness, showing focused black cherry, black tea and baked rhubarb tones. The moderate structure allows for a soft, layered texture that could use more time in bottle. Enjoy best from 2020–2025.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Williams Selyem Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This wine's nose is dominated by the scent of freshly cut russet pear but also has a streak of citrus pith. That pithiness makes itself felt on the palate, where it's joined by bright, inviting lemon notes and rich, textured, yeasty savor. A wonderfully concentrated wine with an earthy edge.
title: Winzer Krems 2016 Kremser Wachtberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's an impressive density of aromas on this bottling, with black pepper, roasted meat, black currant and tar, yet with a fresh violet kick as well. The concentrated black currant and elderberry flavors are thick on the palate yet cut by tangy black plum skins, salt and pepper and dried herb flavors.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2015 Knucklehead Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: Grown at an estate owned by this Beaune-based producer, this wine is dense and full of black fruits and firm tannins. It is toasty, spicy and ripe, beautifully structured and balanced, and ready to age further. Drink from 2019.
title: Albert Bichot 2016 Domaine de Rochegrés  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This is a solidly medium-bodied style bursting with a complex mix of red and black fruits, warm pavement, green herbs and dusting polish. The palate is well-structured and grainy-textured, with a herbal, graphite finish. Drink now–2024.
title: Amisfield 2014 Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Thick, oaky and exceptionally high in alcohol, there's no subtlety to this wine. The full-bodied fruit flavors bring apple, melon and papaya into play, but it would be difficult to match this to food.
title: Schmidt 2015 Reserve Chardonnay (Applegate Valley)

description: This Chardonnay is reminiscent of a fruit salad, with lush aromas and flavors of lemon, banana, pineapple, honeydew and peach yogurt. Mild acidity keeps everything in check and leads to a short finish.
title: Sunset Hills 2016 Chardonnay (Shenandoah Valley)

description: Spicy aromas of licorice and cedar mingle with vanilla and cassis on the nose. The tannins are somewhat gritty on the palate, with spicy black pepper, cedar and pomegranate flavors. The acidity helps pull this wine together but tannic, oaky flavors overpower with only a suggestion of black fruit on the lingering finish.
title: Sutcliffe 2015 Pinot Noir (Colorado)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Not quite as concentrated as the previous vintage, the fruit flavors of melon and pear are correctly varietal, yet somewhat dilute. The wine was fermented entirely in tank, and there is just a hint of metal flavoring the finish.
title: Sweet Cheeks 2016 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

description: Subdued aromas of nut, dried apricot and overripe nectarine zest all follow over to the straightforward palate along with a hint of yellow apple. A light touch of almond marks the close.
title: Tommasi 2016 Le Fornaci  (Lugana)

description: This opens with muddled aromas suggesting minestrone soup, prune, celery and coffee that carry over to the full-bodied palate along with mature black cherry, baking spice and crushed black olive. Loose-knit, close-grained tannins try to hold it all together.
title: Triacca 2012 San Domenico  (Sforzato di Valtellina)

description: While oak dominant on the nose in aromas of roasted berries, the palate reveals a bit more complexity and balance. Medium-bodied and silken in feel, this shows ripe berry flavors, lifted by shades of violets and crushed granite. There's a slight dip in concentration on the midpalate yet overall this is enjoyable for its soft, approachable feel.
title: Villa Schinosa 2016 Nero di Troia (Puglia)

description: Lush and rich aromas of candied red cherries and vanilla dust show on the nose of this bottling. Rosewater and fruit punch flavors arise on the sip, riding into a vanilla dust finish.
title: Vinum 2016 Pinot Noir (Monterey)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Light aromas of melon, pear and herb lead to similarly faint fruit flavors. It's cleanly made but everything seems very dialed back.
title: Wind Rose 2016 The Keeper Sauvignon Blanc (Yakima Valley)

description: Furry plum and berry aromas are good but fragmented. Racy acidity creates tension and some rawness on the palate, while this wine tastes of tart black plum and black currant. Latent acidity on the finish results in strained freshness.
title: Xiloca 2016 Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: The nose carries aromas of ruby grapefruit alongside honeydew melon. Bright lemon-lime acidity enlivens flavors of key lime and gooseberry on the grassy palate. Hints of bitter lime creep on the finish.
title: Dablon 2016 Riesling (Lake Michigan Shore)

description: Aromas of clementine and juniper berry set the scene for flavors of apricot, white peach, and almond. Offering nice mouthfeel, this wine has a touch of almond blossom on the short finish.
title: Dalton 2016 Reserve Viognier (Galilee)

description: Aromas of honeysuckle and a whiff of tropical fruit lift from the glass while the soft round palate offers yellow peach and a confectionary note. It's simple but well made.
title: Delibori 2016  Lugana

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The grape is rare in Oregon, and most commonly found in Central Europe, where it produces a spicy tannic somewhat rustic red. Here, it's rather thin, in both color and flavor, with dilute strawberry fruit. It's texturally most interesting, and that holds your attention through an otherwise ordinary finish.
title: Johan 2014 Blaufränkisch (Willamette Valley)

description: First produced in 1850, this is widely seen as the original branded red wine in Portugal. The Castelão fruit, then and now, comes from the Setúbal Peninsula. It gives a crisp, juicy and light wine with soft tannins. There is some spice, although the main thrust of this wine is delicious red fruit. Drink now.
title: José Maria da Fonseca 2015 Periquita Original Red (Península de Setúbal)

Appellation: Campo de Borja, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Aromas of clover and yeasty vanilla lack concentration. This feels a bit oily, while nonspecific citrus flavors finish with a note of grape skins and brine. It's acidic and medium to light in body.
title: Legado del Moncayo 2016 Garnacha Blanca (Campo de Borja)

description: Earthy, soupy berry aromas are touched up by forced oak. This wine is tannic, screechy and acidic on the palate, with a swath of creamy oak. Herbal, saucy plum and berry flavors finish raw, pulling and tannic, with an excess of oak flavor.
title: Marco Abella 2015 Loidana Red (Priorat)

description: Jammy red-fruit aromas linger under a blanket of oak-driven coffee in this wine. The palate mirrors the aromas, yet the creeping acidity and medium-grained tannins offer a nice counterbalance.
title: Masseria Altemura 2014 Sasseo Primitivo (Salento)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This seems somewhat disjointed and angular, with tart, almost sour berry and cherry fruit. There are acetic scents and chewy tannins. Though labeled reserve, it's a wine that fails to deliver the more subtle pleasures of the grape. Perhaps this would best be served along with a hearty stew.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A mix of five different Pinot relatives (not clones), this seems a bit overworked. It's flat in the mouth, and the mix of grapes, some white, some red, simply blends into a rather generic tasting white wine. If it resembles anything, it's a Pinot Noir Blanc, and it's already drying out, so drink up.
title: Minimus 2016 #22 Pinot White (Willamette Valley)

description: A particularly good value, this appealing and inexpensive wine adds light and tasty oak and spice accents to a good base of plummy flavors. It feels light and fresh on the palate and has enough concentration for a bit of a lingering finish.
title: Obscured 2015 Red (California)

description: Delicate hints of candied red apple and honeydew extend from nose to palate in this easy-going white blend. Off dry and soft in acidity, it's an approachable everyday sip. Drink now.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Madonna Kabinett White (Rheinhessen)

description: The Arinto in this attractive wine gives it a floral character. Bright acidity and citrus fruits add a crisp lift and go with the hint of oak aging. This refreshing wine can be drunk now.
title: Parras Wines 2016 White (Lisboa)

description: This is hugely tannic, nearly impenetrable wine, hard to break through, a mix of pencil lead, tobacco leaf and dried herb that presents as bittersweet and a touch green.
title: Patland 2014 Terra Del Cuore Estate Grown Malbec (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dried black cherries, baked earth, rust and a hint of feral aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate offers an interesting line of tart red fruit along with dried thyme and a meaty touch.
title: Press Run 2014 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)

description: There's a very strong scent of licorice on the nose of this blend of 54% Syrah and 46% Merlot, along with dark-red fruit and myrrh. Eucalyptus oil flavors arise on the palate, giving intrigue to the black cherry, peppercorn and roasted meat touches.
title: Reyes 2014 Julia's Red (California)

description: The nose is extremely shy but the tangy straightforward palate shows white cherry, Gala apple and a hint of almond. It's linear and crisp, with bright acidity a vivacious perlage.
title: Ricci Curbastro NV Rosé Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: A ripe, fruity blend of local Dão grapes, this unoaked wine bursts with blackberry flavors. Acidity and tannins back up the juicy fruitiness of the wine. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2015 Quinta do Vale Red (Dão)

description: Full bodied and voluptuous, this fleshy red blend offers soft, easy black cherry and berry flavors powdered with cinnamon and cocoa. Soft, yielding tannins amplify its plush, easy style. Drink now.
title: Steam 2016 Reserve Red (Pfalz)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Floral and honey-like aromas lead to very sweet flavors of apricot and peach in this medium-bodied wine. A good choice for friends that only drink sweet, or with your dessert.
title: Terra d'Oro 2016 Moscato (Amador County)

description: Rubbery, medicinal berry aromas smell like Pepto-Bismol. A chunky palate is big and blocky, with no elegance or charm. Candied, grapy flavors finish hard and scratchy.
title: Muruve 2016 Joven  (Toro)

description: A light color and yeasty aromas of sourdough and dusty white fruits are the greeting on this modest white made from Chardonnay, Viura and Moscatel. Flavors of lime are yeasty and finish bitter.
title: Ochoa 2016 Calendas White (Navarra)

description: Stalky aromas are more rough and fruitless than smooth and clean. This brut reserva foams up on the palate and feels clumsy. Briny flavors suggest white pepper but not much fruit, and it's grassy-tasting on the finish.
title: Perelada NV Brut Reserva Sparkling (Cava)

description: Tropical fruit aromas are soft and syrupy. This is chunky and low in acidity, with oily stone-fruit flavors. The finish feels a bit warm and cloying.
title: Vicente Gandia 2016 Hoya de Cadenas Single Estate Chardonnay (Utiel-Requena)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: This Demeter certified blend of Uva di Troia and Montepulciano carries odd aromas of peanut skins, asparagus and saucy, sour red fruit. The palate offers a bit more enjoyment with its more fruit-forward nature, bracing acidity and abrasive tannins carrying to the finish.
title: Giancarlo Ceci 2015 Almagia Rosso Biodinamico Red (Castel del Monte)

description: Strange, yeasty aromas suggest boiled ham and buttercup. This feels full and fleshy, with low acidity. It tastes like it smells: yeasty and much like ham, with little fruit.
title: Batán de Salas 2016 Chardonnay (Somontano)

description: Chunky oak rests heavily atop aromas of stale white fruits and nuts. This is obtuse in feel, with stalky, oaky flavors that are largely devoid of fresh fruit.
title: Bodegas Berceo 2015 Más de Berceo Viura (Navarra)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This blend of Tempranillo, Petite Sirah, Mourvèdre and Syrah is thin and leathery. It's already dried out and not heading in a better direction. Two bottles sampled with the same results. Drink up.
title: Folin Cellars 2015 Passive Aggressive Red (Rogue Valley)

description: This wine is slightly bittersweet and off-putting, with a dullness to the texture.
title: Madrigal 2014 Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: Melony aromas are neutral at best. A pulpy, citric palate holds briny orange flavors that don't show a lot of depth or quality. It falls apart on the finish.
title: Santa Cruz de Alpera 2016 Verdejo (Almansa)

description: Blatant aromas of milk chocolate and candied-berry fruits amount to a soupy, heavy, jammy nose. This Monastrell feels choppy and broken. Saucy berry flavors are loaded with oak and clove notes, while this wine tastes like chocolate and feels hot on the finish.
title: Chopo 2016 Monastrell (Jumilla)

Appellation: Campo de Borja, Northern Spain, Spain
description: This offers light aromas of yeasty white flowers and lemon-lime. The palate is weak, while generic white-fruit flavors fall off quickly and taste oxidized on a bready finish.
title: Santo Cristo 2016 Flor de Añon Verdejo (Campo de Borja)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Not even close to ripe, with not varietal character, this tart, herbal and stemmy wine just doesn't click.
title: Amalie Robert 2012 Top Barrel Syrah (Willamette Valley)

description: Scents of pumpkin, pyrazines and general murkiness lead to a dilute palate. Fruitless flavors of onion and lime finish bitter and with notes of green peppercorn.
title: El Cortijillo 2016 Verdejo (La Mancha)

description: Weathered wood and sawdust aromas are drab. That palate is rounded by flavors of vanilla, oak planks and cherry. Lacking acidity, this finishes flat and oaky.
title: Laurentia 2015 Field Blend Pinot Noir (Grand River Valley)

description: Many years in bottle haven't done much for this briary wine with weedy aromas of field greens and spice. A choppy palate with uncontrolled acidity is less than comfortable, while the finish offers nothing but rawness and tannins.
title: Loriñon 2008 Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Aromas of confected orange and molasses are joined by a rubbery note. The palate is sugary, with flavors of candied orange, lychee and dried pineapple, finishing with peach syrup and latex.
title: Laurentia 2015 Select Reserve Riesling (Grand River Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is extreme in bay leaf and green, herbal flavors, with a touch of oxidation.
title: Trecini 2015 Merlot (Russian River Valley)

description: This funky, skunky Cava smells stale and slightly rotten. A weedy flavor profile offers no help, while this tastes pickled on the finish.
title: Totus Tuus NV Brut Reserva Sparkling (Cava)

Appellation: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla, Central Spain, Spain
description: Aromas suggest pine oil, pineapple and mango syrup. This wine is shapeless, tasting oddly of rose water and flavored tea. The finish is deficient.
title: Mureda 2016 Made with Organic Grapes Chardonnay (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: Intense aromas of potpourri mask cedar and red currant on the nose. Tart cranberry and cedar create a sour palate, while gritty tannins feel disjointed. The finish brings underripe flavors of raspberry and cedar.
title: Dablon 2015 Pinot Noir (Lake Michigan Shore)

description: Funky grapefruit and passion fruit aromas are uncommon and not appealing. This white Grenache is wide but spread thin, with no focal point to the palate. Bitter, weedy citrus flavors don't improve on a finish with a dominant green character.
title: Las Colinas Del Ebro 2016 Garnatxa Blanca (Terra Alta)

description: This isn't just a great wine for what was a notoriously cool, wet vintage, it's quite simply a magnificent wine. Perfumed and loaded with finesse, it opens with scents of woodland berry, iris and baking spice while the vibrant, balanced palate doles out Marasca cherry, dark culinary spice, crushed herb and menthol. Refined tannins and fresh acidity provide the taut framework while a licorice note wraps around the finish. Gorgeous. Drink 2022–2034.
title: Gaja 2014  Barbaresco

description: Intense scents of cherry, star anise and iris float out of the glass. It's firmly structured and loaded with finesse, offering crushed raspberry, Marasca cherry, cinnamon and star anise framed fine-grained tannins. Bright acidity lends balance and energy.
title: Gaja 2014 Costa Russi  (Barbaresco)

description: A hallmark expression of Nebbiolo, Produttori del Barbaresco's 2013 Rabajà Riserva offers aromas of wild red berry, menthol, Alpine herb, saddle leather and dark culinary spice. The concentrated palate boasts enviable finesse and is practically weightless in the mouth, showing crushed black cherry, juicy raspberry, orange zest, clove and licorice alongside firm, velvety tannins. Bright acidity provides impeccable balance. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Rabajà Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Enticing scents of wild berry, rose, baking spice and balsamic aromas come together in the glass. Juicy and full-bodied, the flavorsome palate shows succulent Morello cherry, cranberry, licorice and ground white pepper alongside a backbone of vibrant acidity and firm, polished tannins. It's already exceptional but will benefit with more aging. Drink 2020–2038.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Rio Sordo Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Forest berry, chopped mint, clove and new leather aromas take center stage on this elegant, full-bodied red. The savory, smooth palate doles out succulent red cherry, raspberry compote and licorice while vibrant acidity and tightly knit, refined tannins provide balance and support. It has great energy and is loaded with flavor but will be even better with a few more years aging. Drink 2020–2038.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Pajè Riserva  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Vintner Austin Hope, whose family is intertwined in the rise of Paso Robles, spent years working to release a wine of this caliber. Dark and thick in the glass, aromas of black currant and blueberry meet with roasted coffee, chocolate syrup and baked plum on the wondrous nose. The palate is soft and extremely expressive, with blueberry, black pepper, firm midpalate tannins and a finish that's redolent of cappuccino candies.
title: Austin Hope 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Red berry, blue flower, crushed herb and exotic spice aromas unfold in the glass. Elegant, structured and delicious, the full-bodied palate delivers red cherry and licorice, chopped mint alongside bright acidity and firm, refined tannins. It's impeccably balanced and will age gloriously. Drink 2021 -2043.
title: Paolo Scavino 2013 Bric del Fiasc  (Barolo)

description: Still young but already boasting extraordinary pedigree, this fantastic wine opens with hallmark scents of wild berry, new leather, chopped herb, tilled soil and a whiff of violet. The polished, full-bodied palate delivers juicy wild cherry, crushed raspberry, white pepper and licorice while youthfully assertive, noble tannins and bright acidity provide age-worthy structure. A pipe tobacco note lingers on the finish. Drink 2023–2038.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Montefico Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: This wine, with its light structure and ripe red-berry flavors, is all fruit. It has stalky tannins and plenty of acidity and crispness. It's light, fruity and ready to drink.
title: Parras Wines 2015 Desafinado Unoaked Red (Douro)

description: Cidery apple aromas are yeasty and powdery. This is regular in feel, without much body or depth. Basic flavors of lemon-lime and apple juice are sugary, while this showslightly oxidized flavors on the finish.
title: Poema NV Extra Dry Sparkling (Cava)

description: A fruity blend of Cerceal, Bical and Encruzado, this easy, fruity wine is tangy, full of citrus and orange zest and refreshing. Acidity and crisp flavors are balanced by ripe yellow fruits and an attractive perfumed edge. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2016 Quinta do Vale Branco White (Dão)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Funky aromas of moist underbrush, tilled soil, new leather and game carry over to the rather muddled palate along with black cherry extract, a green note of powdered sage and a hint of clove. Dusty fleeting tannins leave a drying grainy finish.
title: Vecchia Cantina di Montepulciano 2013 Riserva  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: An outlier grape for southern Italy, this Pinot Nero is primary in its ripe cherry and raspberry flavors. It's light, bright and juicy nature make it an easy-drinking selection.
title: Cantine del Borgo Reale 2015 Pinot Nero (Puglia)

description: Aromas of cranberry, cherry pie and baking spice are inviting on the nose. The palate brings more cranberry and cherry, joined by ripe, juicy pomegranate. Gentle tannins slowly emerge while the tart acidity lightens the baked quality of the fruit flavors. Tart fruit tones linger on the finish, with clove and a final grip of tannin.
title: Chankaska 2015 Marquette (Minnesota)

description: This soft, semisweet Dornfelder is a candied swirl of cherry-cola, vanilla and chocolate flavors. It's a plush, easy wine for early consumption.
title: Fritz Windisch 2016 Heimersheimer Rotenfels Dornfelder (Rheinhessen)

description: Toast, cured meat and roasted herb aromas come to the forefront. On the vivacious palate, dried sage and toasted walnut underscore yellow apple while lively acidity and vigorous bubbles provide the aggressive setting.
title: La Montina NV Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This dessert-style wine is quite flavorful and sweet, from the aroma of apple cider and toasted nuts to flavors of golden raisins and honey. It has an almost thick texture and syrupy finish.
title: SIP 2015 Moscato (California)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is a blend of Sauvignon Blanc (61%), Sémillon (23%), Riesling (12%) and Albariño. The aromas are a bit faint, with notes of herb, fig, pear and soda water that don't seem entirely in sync. The palate brings broad, dry flavors that come up short.
title: Three of Cups 2016 White (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This wine has soft tannins and juicy red fruits. It is ripe, with a gentle texture, bright fruitiness and enough tannins to give the fruit their support. Drink this attractive wine now.
title: Vicente Faria Vinhos 2016 Animus Red (Douro)

description: The aromas are oak dominated, with notes of vanilla extract, coconut, dill, spearmint and freshly hewn wood, with the fruit struggling to get to the surface. There's a soft, pleasing feel to the palate but the wood again gets in the way. The fruit is hard to find.
title: Watermill 2014 Estate Vineyard Tempranillo

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Viognier can turn sharp and bitter, as it hints at here. The flavors emphasize green botanicals, lime skin and pith. It's definitely on the less-than-optimal side of ripe for this difficult grape.
title: Whitetail Ridge 2016 Viognier (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This wine seems awkward—there is no other way to put it. Unintegrated, lumbery oak flavors jut out in front of brambly berry fruit. Nothing hangs together and the oak treatment is rough and outsized.
title: Airlie 2014 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Soupy raisin and prune aromas are earthy and sleepy. This feels a lot like prune juice, but with tannins. Prune is also the sole flavor on this overripe but still decent gran reserva from a hot vintage.
title: Burgo Viejo 2009 Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: A good amount of fruit concentration gives this medium-bodied wine likeable aromas and flavors, while a soft smooth texture and a dab of sweetness make it easy on the palate.
title: California Roots 2015 Red (California)

description: At this stage, the wood-aging component of this wine dominates the gentle black fruits. An element of high toast and black-chocolate extraction flavors give bitterness. It seems this wine will always have a hard edge.
title: Château Clos du Moulin 2015  Médoc

description: Dark, jammy fruit aromas are at the core of this otherwise oak-driven wine. Baking spice flavors of vanilla and cocoa powder are prominent and not entirely integrated with the baked fruit character. Medium weight and soft acidity make this an easy-drinking sipper.
title: Concura 2016 Fasi Primitivo (Puglia)

description: There's a great deal of oak covering the red berry aromas on the nose. The medium-bodied palate shows a bit more integration, melding dark cherry and blackberry flavors with baking spice.
title: Layer Cake 2015 Primitivo (Puglia)

description: Ripe red berry and plum aromas are covered in a sheen of vanilla and cocoa in this Negroamaro-dominant wine. Medium in weight, with a soft feel, enjoy this with hard cheeses.
title: Leone de Castris 2016 Maiana Negroamaro (Salice Salentino)

description: Plenty of grapey, jammy aromas and flavors fill this medium-bodied wine to the rim. It is lightly sweet, very fruity, and smooth and soft in texture.
title: Line 39 2016 Excursion Red (California)

description: Delicate apple, pear and mineral flavors give a fruity but lean personality to this light-bodied wine. It makes good, basic refreshment.
title: Lucinda & Millie 2016 Made with Organic Grapes Chardonnay (Mendocino)

description: Intensely savory notes of crushed earth and pollen juxtapose prettier red apple and tangerine. While rustic in style, its light body and subtle tangerine sweetness offers approachable refreshment.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Madonna Liebfraumilch White (Rheinhessen)

description: This 50-50 blend of Sauvignon Blanc and Malvasia is a bit closed on the nose. The palate offers more in its juicy melon and apple flavors, with a hint of florals in the background. This is light in weight and intensity, yet perfect for the price.
title: San Marzano 2016 Il Pumo Sauvignon-Malvasia White (Salento)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: The winery's white wines are extraordinarily high in alcohol, which inevitably blows out any subtleties and varietal character. This is thick, fruity, almost syrupy, with principal flavors of canned peaches.
title: Schmidt 2016 Albariño (Applegate Valley)

Appellation: Southern Oregon, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: The label suggests that this is done in a sweeter style, though it's principally the superripe fruit that you taste. Round and simple, with flavors of peaches, it doesn't deliver the aromatics or the flavor complexity of most Oregon Rieslings.
title: Schmidt 2016 Lot 1 Riesling (Southern Oregon)

Appellation: Columbia Gorge (OR), Oregon Other, Oregon, US
description: Volatile aromas get things off to a difficult start, with a burning sensation and a touch of vinegar. Thin berry fruit and a hint of vanilla wafer come across in the mouth. Two bottles tasted. Drink up.
title: The Pines 1852 2014 Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: Soft scents of white flowers, pear and peach subtly make themselves known on the nose of this direct wine. It's lightweight and approachable, with ripe-fruit and sweet-orange flavors that flesh out the palate. Drink now.
title: Tierhoek 2015 Chenin Blanc (Piekenierskloof)

description: Pleasant white-fruit aromas are simple. This has a fresh but highly basic mouthfeel. Melony flavors come with green pyrazine notes, while this is dilute on the finish.
title: Arrumaco 2016 Verdejo (Spain)

description: This golden oak-aged Airén smells almost entirely of wood grain and vanilla. On the palate, it's resiny to an extreme. Flavors of raw wood and coconut are devoid of fruit, while this tastes like liquid oak on the finish.
title: Bodegas Alejandro Fernandez 2014 Alejairén Airen (La Mancha)

description: A blend of 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, Malvasia Nera and Colorino, this opens with aromas of toast, oak, underbrush and espresso. The concentrated, medium-bodied is dense yet linear, delivering ripe black cherry, crushed blackberry, licorice and coffee bean. Assertive, close-grained tannins grip the finish.
title: Castello di Fonterutoli 2015  Chianti Classico

description: An uncommon find in the appellation, this red is blended with 6% Cabernet Sauvignon and given nearly two years in 60% new French oak. Dusty, it's bold and rustic, with shocks of currant, blackberry and broadly distributed oak, the lasting note a mix of clove and black pepper.
title: Chalk Hill 2014 Chairman's Club Carmenère (Chalk Hill)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: With 8% Petite Sirah, this is a velvety herbaceous wine with earthy undertones that presents as both rustic and refined. Leathery black pepper meets mushroom and cedar as the medium-ripe flavors lead to a juicy finish.
title: Dashe Cellars 2015 Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

Appellation: Arroyo Grande Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a strong hazelnut character on this bottling from an iconic vineyard, with touches of lemon-laced crème brûlée and pink grapefruit zest. The nutty quality translates to pecans on the sip, with orange marmalade, toasted croissant and a slight bit of marshmallow toward the finish.
title: Dolin 2014 Rincon Vineyard Chardonnay (Arroyo Grande Valley)

description: From the northernmost Beaujolais cru, this wine is bold, ripe and fruity. Its tannins and acidity promise good aging and the chance to enhance the fragrant red-cherry fruitiness. This wine will be ready from late 2018.
title: Domaine des Chers 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Saint-Amour)

description: The 55-year-old vines behind this wine have produced a rich wine. Still-dense tannins mark it out as youthful and needing to age further. It will do this well, broadening out the red-berry fruits and increasing the density of the wine. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine Matray 2016 Aux Thèpes  (Saint-Amour)

description: Richly fruity, this wine is dense, structured and impressive. Ripe black fruits and strong tannins will allow it to age. The property was purchased by Dominique Piron from leading restaurateurs, the Lameloise family. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016 Cuvée Piron-Lameloise  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This wine's grassy notes of fresh, green foliage grow richer with air, but thast green, vivid freshness remains on the palate. Lime and lemon freshness pervades the slender palate, giving it verve. The finish is dry and has more grassy intensity.
title: Eder 2016 Ried Silberbichl Smaragd Sauvignon Blanc (Wachau)

description: Compact plum and currant aromas are floral at first then more spicy and foxy as this blend led by Syrah and Carmenère airs out. A tough palate flush with tannins deals spicy, herbal plum flavors with ample chocolate and spice from oak. On the finish, this dries out and tastes peppery. Drink through 2022.
title: Emiliana 2014 Coyam Red (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Intense aromas of ripe peach, tangerine, ginger and white blossoms meld with underscores of slate and smoke in this project from Thomas Pastuszak, wine director at The NoMad. This powerful wine displays rich fruit, floral and spice notes on the palate, with enough acidity to maintain balance. The finish carries on and on in apricot and orchard blossom tones. A perfect match for pork or fowl.
title: Empire Estate 2016 Reserve Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Toasty oak and savory cherry and plum aromas are meaty and calm. On the palate, this is linear and compact, with a beam of acidity that keeps things healthy and plodding ahead. Spicy oak flavors blend nicely with tomato, herbal plum and red currant notes. Drink now through 2024.
title: Erasmo 2011 Reserva de Caliboro Red (Maule Valley)

description: Dry earthy aromas of black fruits are subtle and calm, not loud, oaky or overtly herbal or spicy. A bit of clamp is found on a firm palate, while this Bordeaux blend tastes of spicy, lightly herbal plum and berry fruits. A steady thorough finish feels right. Drink through 2022.
title: Erasmo 2013 Reserva de Caliboro Red (Maule Valley)

description: While this is currently slightly tight and closed, it shows the structure and elements to present a complete and well-balanced wine with a little time. Scents of charred plum skin, dark chocolate, cigar box and roast beef dance in the bouquet, while the bold, firm palate offers flavors of black fruit skin, cassis and char. Drink 2019–2022.
title: Ernie Els 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stellenbosch)

description: The merest hint of passion fruit gives this wine's nose a tropical twist. The palate still has some sprightly fizz, combining earthy, soy-like savory texture with pithy citrus notes. It's slender in body and finishes on a dry, refreshing note.
title: Forstreiter 2016 Schiefer Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This estate is named for the Romans who originally planted vines here. With touches of wood aging giving spice and toast along with rich, juicy black-plum fruits, this is a wine to age. It will be best from 2019.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine de la Vigne Romaine  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This wine has a smoky character that lies over the juicy fruit, adding extra interest and texture to this already structured wine. With solid tannins and rich red-plum and cherry fruit, this wine is still developing. Drink from late 2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine des Rosiers  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This rich, structured wine is full of both ripe tannins and generous fruit. It is dense, as befits a Morgon, while also having a layer of juicy red-berry fruits. The structure and acidity promise aging. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 Domaine des Versauds  (Morgon)

description: Fragrant plum and red cherry notes have an alluring hint of cinnamon. The palate retains these rich, aromatic flavors but presents them on a fresh, slender canvas that shows both fruit and spice. Lovely and highly appetizing.
title: Kohl 2015 Kohloss Zweigelt (Niederösterreich)

description: Earthy mint and blackcurrant leaf aromas sit atop the rich, concentrated black-fruit core of this wine. The medium-weight palate offers bold blackberry, cassis and boysenberry flavors, framed by firm yet well-integrated tannins that provide a crushed-velvet texture. Notes of berry compote, dried fynbos, green peppercorn and roast beef all unfold on the lingering, layered finish. Drink now–2021.
title: KWV 2015 Cathedral Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon (Western Cape)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County-Sonoma County-Monterey County, California, US
description: Aromas of roasted red cherry, sharp black raspberry and herb-tinged smoked pork in mulberry sauce make for a mouthwatering entry to this bottling. The oak is strong on the palate, giving a vanilla overlay to the cranberry and rose petal flavors. A hint of juniper shows on the finish.
title: Landmark 2015 Overlook Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County-Sonoma County-Monterey County)

description: This Syrah-Mourvèdre-Cabernet Sauvignon from the Apalta subzone of the Colchagua Valley deals spicy aromas of road tar and black plum. A tight integrated palate is firm more than luxe, while nicely merged cassis, plum and oak flavors add up to quality. Rich flavors of tobacco and chocolaty oak bolster a long finish. Drink through 2022.
title: Las Niñas 2014 Premium Made With Organic Grapes Red (Apalta)

description: This rich wine is full bodied, structured and, for a bottling from this appellation, packed with tannins. At the same time, the fruit shines through the wine's dry core, giving black plum and cherry flavors as well as acidity. The wine will develop further, so drink from late 2018.
title: Les Frères Perroud 2015 Améthyste  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Oak Knoll District, Napa, California, US
description: Bright, focused acidity flirts with the medium-bodied palate that boasts flavors of lemon and grapefruit. A background of supportive oak never gets in the way, offering complementing tones of nutmeg and vanilla.
title: Materra Cunat Family Vineyards 2015 Southworth Estate Grown Chardonnay (Oak Knoll District)

description: This is a soft oaky wine that builds power and concentration as it opens and expands on the palate, showing brawn and plenty of body. Juicy blackberry is lifted by integrated acidity and welcome jolts of black pepper.
title: Mi Sueño 2014 Syrah (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Hudson River Region, New York Other, New York, US
description: Vibrant on the nose in vivid notes of red apple, peach blossom and slate, the palate carries similar tones along a lime-zest river of acidity. There's nice weight and feel to this wine, with a good concentration of flavors and a delicate astringency to the citrus blossom finish.
title: Millbrook 2016 Castle Hill Vineyard Estate Bottled Riesling (Hudson River Region)

description: Named after the cherry trees (cerisiers) that surround the vineyard, this wine is richly flavored, yes, with cherries as well as crisper red currants. It is spicy, with a touch of pepper as well as some firm tannins. Drink this fruity, young wine from late 2018.
title: Pascal Aufranc 2016 Les Cerisiers  (Juliénas)

description: Grown on volcanic soil on the slopes of Mont Brouilly, this wine has a strong mineral character as well as rich black fruits. It is a concentrated wine with a dry core and balanced acidity. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Robert Perroud 2015 La Fournaise du Pérou  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: From a selection of the estate's best fruit, this wine is richly structured, firmly textured and likely to age well in the medium term. The black fruits are still young, giving a brightness to the wine that perhaps should be allowed to calm down. Drink this ripe wine from late 2018.
title: Robert Perroud 2016 Pollen  (Brouilly)

description: New leather, tobacco and underbrush aromas emerge along with a balsamic whiff of camphor. Firmly structured, the youthfully austere palate evokes tart cherry, red raspberry, star anise and chopped herb framed in taut, fine-grained tannins. It's balanced, with bright acidity. Give it time to fully develop. Drink 2023–2033.
title: Roccheviberti 2013 Rocche di Castiglione  (Barolo)

description: A full-fruit nose with grapy aromas of boysenberry and not much oak character gets this low-alcohol (12.5%) Cabernet off to an unconventional start. Jamminess is maintained on the palate, which feels plump but shy on tannic structure. Easygoing berry-compote flavors settle on blueberry on a soft finish. Only 450 cases were made. Drink through 2020.
title: Santa Carolina 2015 Dolmen Cabernet Sauvignon (Cachapoal Valley)

description: Orange peel and beeswax notes make brief appearances on the apple- and pear-fruited nose of this wine. On the palate, that complexity is borne out by a slightly grippy phenolic texture, making this a very gastronomic, unusual white from one of Austria's rare but indigenous grape varieties. The finish is wonderfully spicy and clean.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2016 Spiegel Zierfandler (Thermenregion)

description: This Chenin Blanc-based blend, with additional portions of Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc and Viognier, is delicate in nature, though complex and layered beneath it's reserved surface. It boasts notes of apple flesh, melon rind, yellow flower, fynbos and beeswax on the nose and mouth, with final impressions of orange pith and lemon zest on the enduing finish. Harmonouos and well-balanced, it offers a pleasant, softly astringent texture that lends structure to the medium-weight palate and length to the close. Drink now–2023.
title: Keermont 2015 Terrasse White (Stellenbosch)

description: Hints of mahogany and vanilla play with cherry and plum fruit on the nose. The palate presents a very rounded, fruit-driven, smooth and concentrated vision of these wood and fruit notes. The mouthfeel is mellow. A red in a very international idiom, yet with a lovely and fresh Austrian accent. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Kirnbauer 2015 Gold Reserve Blaufränkisch (Mittelburgenland)

description: Named after the oak trees close to the vineyard and aged an extra year, this cuvée is always impressive. It has dark, dense tannins as well as powerful black-cherry fruits and a beautifully perfumed aftertaste. Rich and structured, the wine will age well. Drink from 2019.
title: Lucien Lardy 2015 Les Chênes  (Fleurie)

description: A heady whiff of woodsmoke combines with aromatic cherry fruit on the nose. The beautifully taut palate allows freshness to show the purity of the fruit. Right now the oaky smokiness still envelopes the firm fruit, supported by a fine tannic structure. Wait a little and this will blossom. Drink 2020–2030,
title: Malat 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Niederösterreich)

description: Violet, smoke, white pepper and meat come together on an intensely spicy nose. All the hallmarks of Syrah are there: the inky intensity, the heady aroma, the visceral notions of flesh and smoke. The palate intesifies this but remains taut, concentrated and slender. Right now this has teenage excitement and will sweep you along in its exuberance. If you wait, this will calm down into floral elegance. The firm tannic structure, full fruit and freshness give this a long lease of life. 2018–2028.
title: Muhr-Van der Niepoort 2015 Sydhang Syrah (Carnuntum)

description: The tell-tale scent of rose petals is tempered by fresh orange zest aromas on the nose of this wine. An unusual member of the Traminer family, it sings with creamy but textured freshness, centered around a glorious streak of zesty lemon. The flavors linger long and intensely on the dry finish.
title: Neumeister 2016 Ried Steintal Roter Traminer (Vulkanland Steiermark)

description: A tart, tangy hint of ripe grapefruit mixes with the lemon notes on the nose of this wine. The palate brims with juicy, tart apple notes, its slender body both concentrated and vibrant. It's incredibly fruity, tangy and just about dry, with a totally mouthwatering finish.
title: Nigl 2016 Ried Pellingen Privat Riesling (Kremstal)

description: A wonderfully ripe notion of yellow pear plays on the nose of this wine. Its bright, friendly fruitiness is also amply apparent on the creamy palate, where it's joined by a savory element of soy, salt and miso. A little citrus pith adds even more texture to this rich, rounded wine.
title: R&A; Pfaffl 2016 Hommage Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: From a producer making small amounts of wine from Rutherford fruit—much of it estate and farmed by hand—this powerhouse is impressively crafted and memorable, offering smoky oak, graphite and clove. The palate is concentrated in black berry and plum tones, and broadly expansive on the palate, finishing with a welcome succulence.
title: S.R. Tonella 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: This wine's soy-scented miso notes create an immensely savory nose. The palate centers on those yeasty textures and flavors, but a citrusy streak of lemon freshness brightens and highlights them. This is concentrated and intense, with lots of aging potential. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Steininger 2016 Ried Loisium Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: The nose of this wine boasts pear, celery salt and herbal spice notes. The palate delivers a savory tingle of white pepper and salty soy but also adds luminous, fresh, ripe lemon zest for an incisive, savory and dramatic package. The finish is lip-smacking and long. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Tegernseerhof 2016 Bergdistel Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: This bold, beautifully made wine is sizable in tannin while imparting an old-world entry of herbaceousness and classic notions of pencil shavings and cedar. Gritty on the palate, it flirts with black cherry, currant and cassis before offering a rounded lengthy finish of clove and tobacco.
title: Turnbull 2015 Black Label Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: This wine's ripe mirabelle plum and fragrant lemon notes stream from the glass. The palate has the same appetizing contrast between rounded ripeness and fragrant freshness. The lemon tone creates a zesty, lively and textured palate. Very fresh and delicious right now, it's sure to develop. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Wieninger 2016 Ried Rosengartl White (Wiener Gemischter Satz)

Appellation: San Benito County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Rich and roasty on the nose, this excellent Port-style wine shows aromas of cherry cordial, charred fennel, black sage and roast beef. The dense sip offers black cherry, espresso, blueberry jelly and thick chocolate flavors, enhanced by a hint of menthol and Scotch-like elements.
title: Williams Selyem 2011 Vista Verde Vineyard Port (San Benito County)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from one of the state's premier Syrah vineyards, brooding aromas of dried herb, iron, earth and boysenberry lead to a densely saturated palate. There's no doubt it comes from a warm vintage but it still shows pleasing complexities that reveal themselves over time.
title: Avennia 2015 Arnaut Boushey Vineyard Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: Restrained but ripe, this elegant wine offers concentrated black-cherry and plum flavors shaded in tones of granite, licorice and dried herb. It's rich and expansive on the palate, with a warm, comforting heft. Fresh acidity and fine-grained tannins frame a long finish. It's lovely now but likely to show best from 2020.
title: Brotte 2015 Domaine Barville Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This is a seriously structured single-vineyard wine. Rich tannins and black-cherry fruit are broadened by wood aging and concentrated toast and spice flavors. The wine is still young and needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Baron de l'Ecluse 2016 Les Garances  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: The chapel of La Madone is the landmark of Fleurie, set at the top of a hill above vineyards. This wine from a five-acre parcel of 70-year-old vines is concentrated and full of ripe black-cherry fruits. Its weight and intensity will allow the wine to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Gaget 2016 La Madone  (Fleurie)

description: This wine's nose is shy at first but opens to reveal a note of salty yeast. The palate unfolds into a creamy but streamlined expression of salt, chervil, lemon zest and pith. It may need some time to mellow, but its wonderfully fresh streak of zesty citrus promises longevity. Drink 2019–2029.
title: Ebner-Ebenauer 2016 Ried Sauberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: A touch of rose, a notion of honeysuckle and the heady scent of blood orange zest spell aromatic pleasure. The palate falls in with a slender creaminess that corsets all that floral richness, giving shape and direction. Lemon freshness illuminates every nuance, resulting in an extremely elegant wine with a textured, dry and long echoing finish. Mandarine notes continue to dance.
title: Eder 2016 Smaragd Traminer (Wachau)

description: This complex Syrah is from Giesen's top vineyard. Lifted aromas of blackberry jam, roasted meats, black pepper, florals and graphite are followed by a satiny palate of plush, raisiny fruit and silky tannins.
title: Giesen 2012 Clayvin Single Vineyard Selection Syrah (Marlborough)

description: Heady notes of peony mix with woodsmoke and mahogany, plum and black cherry on the nose. The palate is still taut with fine, stilly drying tannin but brims with oak-kissed fruit. This is structured and dense, muscular but harmonious. A big wine with elenace and power which will benefit from bottle age. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Gunter Triebaumer 2015 Oberer Wald Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: The aromatic allure of tiny, dark elderberries captures the nose. They also appear on the palate, embraced by vanilla and polish notes of new oak. The structure is firm but the body is sinuous and marked by vivid freshness. Made in an international style, this will integrate over the coming months to present a sumptuous and elegant Pinot Noir.
title: Heinrich Hartl 2014 Graf Weingartl Pinot Noir (Thermenregion)

description: The first whiff of this wine, with its concentrated hit of pear peel and citrus pith, spells pleasure. The palate adds a softening, enriching touch of creamy yeast as a backdrop to the rich, fruit-driven flavors: pear, fern, sage and Meyer lemon. The finish is lasting and moreish.
title: Hiedler 2016 Ried Kittmannsberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: This wine's enticingly flinty notes of reduction promise a long life ahead. The concentrated palate presents a characteristic and impressive Grüner Veltliner: ripe pear fruit with salty, lemon-tinged yeast and celery salt and miso nuances. This has layers of sophistication and fun.
title: Hirsch 2016 Ried Lamm Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: Iris, violet, perfumed berry, pine forest and aromatic herb aromas come into play on this fragrant red. The fresh, savory palate offers juicy Morello cherry, ripe blackberry, clove and star anise set against precise tannins and fresh acidity. It's well balanced and youthfully taut but will be even better in another few years. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Istine 2015 Vigna Istine  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Cabernet Sauvignon (80%) takes the lead on this wine, which includes 20% Syrah. The aromas announce themselves, with appealing notes of green olive, herb, moist earth, stem, smoked meat and mineral. The mix of fruit and savory flavors delights the senses, showing intensity but also deftness.
title: K Vintners 2014 The Creator Powerline Estate Vineyard Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pioneering winemaker Ken Brown is still making excellent wine 40 years on, including this bottling that's full of deep red cherry, scarlet red rose petals and dark hibiscus on a nose that's veiled by loamy soil and slate. The palate is tightly wound, with crushed herbs, sandy soils, cranberry and fresh purple-flower flavors that sail across the palate into a vanilla-kissed finish.
title: Ken Brown 2014 Rita's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is a bright, acid-driven wine, citrus-laden alongside crisp, tart red fruit and a light-bodied approach to ripeness. The nose is exotic in rose petal and lavender, with streaks of cardamom and cola spice that persist for a long time on the finish.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 Holder Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Alluring scents of fragrant purple flower, perfumed berry, camphor and crushed mint lead the nose. The taut, structured palate delivers juicy Marasca cherry, cranberry and star anise while assertive, fine-grained tannins provide the backbone. It's balanced, with bright acidity. Drink 2021–2034.
title: Ca'Romè 2014 Chiaramanti  (Barbaresco)

description: Aromas of truffle, leather, menthol and mature plum take the lead on this concentrated red. The aromas follow through to the full-bodied, chewy palate along with fleshy black cherry, licorice and dried herb. Close-grained tannins provide the assertive framework. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Castello dei Rampolla 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Round and rich, this is a generous wine. Its tannins are happily embedded in the ripe black fruits that burst from the glass. This is a solid wine, packed with rich and concentrated fruit. Drink now.
title: Château de Juliénas 2015 Cuvée Prestige  (Juliénas)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Focused aromas of black currant and blackberry are edged by thyme, bay leaf, black pepper and Indian spice on the nose of this wine, which comes from vines planted on a limestone hillside. Chalky tannins frame the intense flavors of the sip, including black fruit, dark violets, loamy earth and a gravelly minerality. Hints of vanilla and caramel soften it all.
title: Clos LaChance 2014 Whitestone Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Coast)

description: An enticing and layered expression of the grape, this well-balanced bottling offers hints of cured meat, campfire, asphalt and iris that masterfully augment the brambly red berry and wild raspberry core. Those notes all continue through to the medium-weight palate, framed by a crushed-velvet texture and notes of tilled earth and root spice on the enduing finish. It's hard to resist now, but it should mature well through 2025.
title: David & Nadia 2015 Pinotage (Swartland)

description: Whiffs of orange blossom, vanilla and toast mingle enticingly on this plump but elegantly balanced Viognier. Crisp tangerine and white-peach flavors lend freshness to the midpalate. The finish is long, marked by a swirl of vanilla cream that lingers richly.
title: Delas Frères 2016 La Galopine  (Condrieu)

description: This wine comes from a three-acre parcel of 50-year-old vines. It is beautifully structured with dense tannins as well as deliciously juicy black fruits. The acidity and the firm dry core both point to good aging ability. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Anita 2016 Cuvée P'tit Co Les Brureaux  (Chénas)

description: From the Charme vineyard this wine is firm, dense and packed with tannins. Behind this structure await juicy berry fruits, generous acidity and a solid texture. This is a powerful wine to age, so drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de Colette 2016 Charme  (Morgon)

description: From one of the more structured of the cru villages, this wine is full of tannins as well as black-cherry fruit. It is dense from the concentration of old vines as well as the rich fruitiness and acidity of the vintage. This ripe wine will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine des Chers 2016 Vieiles Vignes  (Juliénas)
