description: This wine is produced from vines grown beside the moulin of Moulin-à-Vent (windmill). It has plenty of structured tannins as well as dense black fruits, making it both rich and ageworthy. It's impressive with its dark plum fruit and dry core. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine du Penlois 2016 Sous l'Aile du Moulin  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Sun-kissed strawberries and red cherries are edged in leather and dried herbs in this buoyant, juicy wine. While rich and silken in texture, it offers freshness on the midpalate and fine, firm tannins that lend vibrance. It drinks well now but should improve through 2025.
title: Domaine du Vieux Lazaret 2013 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This wine is soft, smooth and brightly focused in acidity. The palate offers a sanguine notion, with cassis and meat tones that are highlighted by cinnamon. A persistent freshness prevails on the finish.
title: Experience 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Inviting in warm aromas and flavors of baked cherry cobbler, this medium-bodied wine offers firm focused acidity built around well-developed highlights of cardamom, cinnamon, orange peel and cola. It's lengthy and complex.
title: Furthermore 2014 Gioia Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Aromas of ripe dark-skinned berry, new leather, culinary spice and a whiff of pressed violet lift out of the glass. Chewy and savory, the medium-bodied palate doles out raspberry compote, ripe Marasca cherry, cinnamon and star anise. Polished tannins provide smooth support.
title: Istine 2015 Vigna Cavarchione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bursting with ripe, almost jammy fruit flavors, this exuberant wine from an often-outstanding estate is rich and mouthfilling, full bodied but not too tannic to easily enjoy. Flavors run the gamut from blackberry to Bing cherry to blueberry and espresso.
title: Las Positas 2013 Estate Tempranillo (Livermore Valley)

description: Tender hints of orange peel make this evocative. On the palate, zesty lemon notes highlight the pithiness. It's slender in body, refreshing and ripe but dry. The long finish is supercharged with lemon. Lovely now, it has the potential to age. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Mayer am Pfarrplatz 2016 Nussberg Riesling (Vienna)

description: From a variety of vineyard sources, this 100% varietal wine highlights deft winemaking in its ability to offer plenty of power within a context of vibrancy. Raspberry, cherry and milk chocolate meet savory touches of clove and cinnamon, with plenty of structure within its rounded richness.
title: Mi Sueño 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Designation: Les Grandes Mises Côte du Py
description: One in a series of small-batch production wines from this major producer, this comes from the top vineyard of Côte du Py. The bold tannins and strong mineral texture are typical of this vineyard. Combined with black plum and berry fruits, the wine becomes rich, dense and worthy of aging. Drink from 2019.
title: Mommessin 2016 Les Grandes Mises Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: This fragrant red offers heady scents of ripe dark-skinned berry, pressed violet, eucalyptus and a potpourri of culinary spice. The refined palate is initially understated but then sneaks up on you with ripe wild cherry, cranberry, licorice and clove. It's surprisingly fresh for such a hot, dry vintage and balanced, with firm, refined tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Monte Bernardi 2015 Retromarcia  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine is made from nearly equal parts Malbec and Syrah, grown on Bald Mountain Ranch atop this feral appellation. It succeeds in offering dry complexity around a core of refreshing and lightly spiced raspberry and Rainier cherry. Lengthy, it lasts for days, offering medium-bodied power.
title: Nellcôte 2016 Rosé (Mount Veeder)

description: Classic varietal aromas of young Nebbiolo include woodland berry, freshly picked violet and a whiff of tilled soil fill the glass. The fresh, elegant palate delivers red cherry, strawberry, star anise and a hint of graphite set against vibrant acidity and polished tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2014  Barbaresco

description: The nose presents wonderfully ripe, fleshy and juicy notes of red and black cherry. The full-flavored palate, rich with finely-woven tannin, adds to this impression of fleshy, richness even more but keeps its balance with well-judged, bright crunchy freshness. Hints of cinnamon and plum only add to the generous feel. The grippy but smooth tannins suggest that this will develop. Drink 2020–2025.
title: R&A; Pfaffl 2015 Altenberg Reserve St. Laurent (Niederösterreich)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: An exotic treat, this dry wine overflows with mango and marshmallow aromas, has tasty lemon meringue flavors and a soothing, viscous texture that coats the palate. Winemaker Shauna Rosenblum kept the juice on the skins for four days during fermentation, adding to the wine's concentration, layering and complexity as well as a lingering, honeyed finish.
title: Rock Wall 2016 Del Arroyo Vineyard Malvasia Bianca (Livermore Valley)

description: Quince and grapefruit flavors rise to the top of the palate in this well-made estate wine, aged in a mix of French oak, Acacia and stainless steel. While smooth and round in feel, its acidity has impact, providing stony refreshment around a lingering white peach flavor.
title: S.R. Tonella 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)

description: Elegance mixed with bold fruit is what this overachieving Cabernet is about. Aromas of blackberry, dried black plum, spice and cocoa suggest high quality. A focused palate and textural grip holds cherry and cassis flavors that are ripe and backed by notes of chocolate and coffee. Drink through 2022.
title: Santa Carolina 2015 Reserva de Familia Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

Variety: Cabernet Sauvignon-Cabernet Franc, Bordeaux-style Red Blend
description: Plump fruit, ripe plum, cinnamon and blueberry appear on the nose. These notions unite on a dense, but rather fresh body where the grip of tannin is only revealed on the finish. It is countered by ample fruit and freshness that give a feeling of juicy, smooth roundness. A balanced and harmonious red that stays on the refreshing side.
title: Schandl 2015 Cabernet Cabernet Sauvignon-Cabernet Franc (Burgenland)

description: There is an intriguing, aromatic quality of nettle amidst the ripe, red cherry flavors on the nose. The palate has an inviting, sinuous slenderness, with still slightly grippy but fine tannin. Red cherry ripples across the palate, given precision and vibrancy by lovely, taut freshness. Such an elegant but lively wine, full of joy and measure.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2015 Reserve Zweigelt (Niederösterreich)

description: Made from 62% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Petit Verdot, 15% Cabernet Franc and 8% Merlot, each aged separately in French oak for three years then blended and bottled, the varieties come together effortlessly in the glass. The full-bodied palate offers flavors of leather saddle, spicy vanilla, clove and blueberry. Densely concentrated, it's hearty and complex, dotted on the finish in a cinnamon tone.
title: Silverpoint Cellars 2013 Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is a real standout wine from the Sobon family—established as winemakers in Amador County in 1977. The wine is ripe, full bodied, firmly structured and dry. It starts with whiffs of white and black pepper and aromas, carrying to the juicy palate of blackberry and cedar flavors, with soft tannins offering a mouthcoating feel.
title: Sobon Estate 2015 ReZerve Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: Te Mata's most renowned wine takes some time to open in glass. When it does, its many subtleties start to shine and it shows both its muscularity and its delicacy. Trademark florals lead the pack, closely followed by notes of black-currant and blackberry jam, graphite, tobacco and hot stones. The palate leads with tart fruit but quickly blends with the savory, dusty notes of the oak and chewy, pencil lead tannins. Finishes on the oaky side right now but will age for many years. Drink now–2027.
title: Te Mata 2015 Coleraine Red (Hawke's Bay)

description: Dry and nicely mature, this complex wine is always among California's best bubblies. It combines great balance, tiny bubbles and some very interesting flavors that make it as appealing as a well-cellared white Burgundy at its peak. Hints of toast, butter and almond fill the aroma, and lemon, crisp apple and baking spices fill the palate. It has lively acidity that's softened by a good sense of body.
title: Roederer Estate 2011 L'Ermitage Brut Sparkling (Anderson Valley)

description: This wine's nose is shy, allowing just subtle hints of watercress and lemon zest to escape. The palate reveals the full zesty force of lemon: tart but ripe freshness, a lip-smacking pithy, freshness and a concentrated yet streamlined body. Drink now for utter refreshment or wait for it to mellow. The finish is dry and lasting. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Ried Steiner Kögl Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Gentle, seductive aromatics combine subtle vanilla, peony and ripe, dark cherry on the nose. The palate is full but fresh—svelte one moment, plush the other. There is something seamless in the way the flavors merge, unimpeded by the tannins and their velvety crunch. Highly accomplished, very elegant and quite irresistible. Drink 2020–2035.
title: Tinhof 2015 Gloriette Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Pear and melon meld with briny minerality on the palate of this well-integrated, mildly ripe wine. Lasting acidity supports spicy flavors of nutmeg, allspice and anise. The texture is harmonious and focused.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Drake Estate Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Star anise, pressed rose petal, aromatic herb and camphor are just some of the aromas you'll find on this taut, polished red. The firmly structured, elegant palate delivers sour cherry, pomegranate, licorice and ground clove set against a backbone of fine-grained tannins. It's young but balanced, with bright acidity. Give it time to reach its full potential. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Gaja 2013 Conteisa  (Barolo)

description: Forest floor, Porcini mushroom, eucalyptus, dark spice and toasted aromas curl out of the glass. It's youthfully austere, offering cranberry, sour cherry and licorice framed in assertive, close-grained tannins. It still needs time to come around and fully bloom. Drink 2024–2034.
title: Gaja 2014 Sorì San Lorenzo  (Barbaresco)

description: An earthy touch of wet soil is first to appear in this wine. It's soon replaced by bright lemon zest notes. That lemony freshness stays throughout, brightening the entire palate with fresh, taut, tingling flavor. This is ripe but gloriously tart, finishing on a very pure, lip-smacking and long lemon note. Lovely now, it will evolve. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Alzinger 2016 Ried Steinertal Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: Bold and spicy, this firmly built wine is the first Morning Dew Ranch vineyard-designate from the new owners of the estate. It starts with exotic and enticing aromas of fresh mint, basil and clove, then delivers pure, focused black fruits and a generous dose of new-oak spice on the palate. Healthy, lip-smacking tannins and firm acidity give it great structure, yet there's no hurry to enjoy this wine. Best after 2020.
title: Castello di Amorosa 2016 Morning Dew Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: This wine's unlikely combination of passion fruit, wild thyme, lemon zest and fennel seed aromas is totally convincing. On the palate, that same richness is even more apparent. Savory herb and salt notes seem counter a richer, oilier citrus aspect, making for high drama and lots of flavor. Sip, sit back and savor the long, citrus-inflected finish.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Liebenberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: This wine's tender yellow apple notes have an edge of orange and grapefruit zest. Tingling green apple freshness pervades the palate, along with lemon zest notes and a flowing, generous juiciness that almost lets you forget how pithy the flavors actually are. Very streamlined, linear and dry, with a lip-smakcing finish and lots of future promise. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Geyerhof 2016 Ried Kirchensteig Riesling (Kremstal)

description: A hedonistic touch of juicy peach swings along with the lemon notes on the nose of this wine. That lovely creamy peachiness also makes for a happy flavor tandem with lemon on the taut, concentrated palate, adding texture and echo. The finish returns to pure, lasting lemon. What a joy. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Jäger 2016 Ried Steinriegl Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: Reveling in the tannins of a young Côte du Py, this wine is rich, structured and dense. The tannins give the dry edge this wine needs to age. Intense with ripe black fruits, this is a powerful wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Marc Burgaud 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: This wine's nutty, rounded richness and hint of woodsmoke glint in the glass. Its intense citrus freshness, which recalls lemon and orange oil, is tempered by a slight smoky edge. Rotgipfler is one of Austria's rarer indigenous grapes, and this one is particularly unusual, intense and concentrated. It's a party in a glass that's lovely now but will develop. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2016 Satzing Rotgipfler (Thermenregion)

description: This wine's fresh green apple notes vie with green Conference pear scents while a flinty touch of reduction promises longevity. The palate shimmers with miso, sage and bright, pure lemon notes, which emphasize darker tones of savory yeast. The combination is totally moreish and effective.
title: Jurtschitsch 2016 Ried Schenkenbichl Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Snipes Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Consistently one of the winery's top bottlings, aromas of blackberry, black plum, dried herb, anise and spice are followed by supremely rich, tightly wound black fruit and crème de cassis flavors that show a firm grainy handshake of tannins and a warm crazy-long finish. It's all about intensity of expression but what intensity it brings.
title: K Vintners 2014 Cattle King Upland Vineyard Syrah (Snipes Mountain)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from a vineyard in the Rocks District, aromas of kirsch, black currant, tobacco, spice and mineral are followed by saturated, rich black fruit and smoked meat flavors. It packs a powerful delicious punch.
title: K Vintners 2014 Ovide Jack's Vineyard Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This new exciting vineyard is located in the foothills of the Blue Mountains and is filled with cobblestone soils. The aromas explode with notes of raspberry, green and black olive brine, green stem, smoked meat, mineral and earth. The palate is rich and focused, with intense earth, fruit and savory flavors that lead to an extended finish. Abundant floral notes are speckled throughout. It's a stunner.
title: K Vintners 2014 Powerline Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: The yeasty smell of lees is still present on the nose of this wine, and its lemony fruit struggles to come through. On the palate, however, bright, brisk and pure lemon is in full force. Fruitiness and bitter pith are interwoven, creating a textured mouthfeel of fresh and tart flavors. This still needs to knit together but promises to be a long-lived, concentrated Riesling. Drink now until 2030 or longer.
title: Lesehof Stagård 2016 Steiner Hund Riesling (Kremstal)

description: The funkiness of lees and fermentation dominate this wine's aroma. On the palate, fresh green and red apple notes shine through, along with a vibrant, pure streak of lemon freshness. It's concentrated yet slender, needing time to show its lemony poise. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Lesehof Stagård 2016 Steiner Schreck Riesling (Kremstal)

description: This is a warm welcoming wine, resolved in tannin and well-integrated overall. Black licorice, tobacco and clove add to the savory depth of dark, brooding character as an unexpected waft of violet perfume lurks beneath the full-bodied ripeness and concentration.
title: Madrigal 2013 Final Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Made from grapes sourced from several sites across the appellation, this shows deft winemaking skill in its velvety, gritty texture and beautiful aromas and flavors of cured meat, rose petal and Asian five spice. Bright acidity lifts the tones of cranberry, rhubarb and mushroom earthiness, with a touch of crushed stone.
title: Merry Edwards 2015 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Soft vanilla tones overlay fresh, bright passion fruit and rich vanilla notes in this wine, but something more intense lies beneath. The palate reveals great concentration, freshness and intensity, with passion fruit, cream and lemon flavors. Despite the creamy descriptors this is resolutely svelte—a real marvel of what mature vines can do. The finish is dry, intense and very long. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Neumeister 2013 Stradener Alte Reben Sauvignon Blanc (Vulkanland Steiermark)

description: The nose is shy but with air it gives way to ripe but tart plum and cherry fruit. On the palate this broadens out into the aromatic hit of Morello and Amarena, kept in tight check by a fine web of gossamer tannins and fresh, bright acidity. This is dense but not extracted, toned but not bulky. This very honestly presented red comes across as totally elegant and wonderfully understated. Impressive but certainly not showy. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Nittnaus Anita und Hans 2015 Altenberg Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: Wild berry, camphor, moist earth and violet aromas unfold in the glass. The structured, refined palate delivers juicy Marasca cherry, crushed raspberry, orange slice and white pepper alongside polished tannins and bright acidity. A soothing licorice note lingers on the finish. Drink through 2028.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Ovello Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: An aromatic cloud of orange and lemon zest rises from the glass. With air, the citrus notions become more heady and intense. They perform the same trick on the palate, shimmering in an aromatic array of bergamot, lemon, tangerine and orange. There's a vein of freshness, unforced concentration and the promise of a long future ahead. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This wine's ripe, yellow pear aromas have a savory touch of miso. On the palate, a pleasantly bitter note of citrus pith takes over, adding freshness and texture to complement the savory flavors that give it so much intrigue. It would be simply impossible to not take another sip. The finish is clean, taut and moreish.
title: Tegernseerhof 2016 Ried Höhereck Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: This wine's pure notes of ripe green pear send enticing signals. The palate adds zesty lemon and floral woodruff touches, creating a green cloud of aroma that fills nose and palate. It's slender and taut in body, its salty midpalate texture beautifully pervaded by citrus brightness. The finish is moreish and totally lip-smacking.
title: Alzinger 2016 Ried Loibenberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A heady, tropical touch of passion fruit clings to the more forward citrus notes on the nose of this wine. Lemon and tangerine notes play on the svelte, linear palate. This is totally refreshing and enlivening, with an almost electric lemon verve. Lovely now; sure to evolve. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Alzinger 2016 Ried Loibenberg Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: That salty-sweet aspect of maple syrup makes a brief appearance on the nose before plum, nutmeg and vanilla take over. The body is smooth with elastic but present tannins, the fruit is dense and plush while ample freshness gives both harmony and crunch. It is just on the finish that some alcoholic heat becomes apparent in this very approachable but sophisticated, international style. This is big and bold but brimming with fine fruit. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Artner 2015 Kirchweingarten Blaufränkisch (Carnuntum)

description: The Hominis Fides represents some of the estate's oldest Grenache vines, with an average age of 90 years. Irresistibly ripe, rich and mouthfilling, this is a powerhouse of black-plum and cherry flavors refined by layers of pencil shavings, cedar and spice. It's bold and lavishly textured yet has freshness and a fine, filigreed tannin structure. Drink now through 2030.
title: Château de Saint-Cosme 2014 Hominis Fides  (Gigondas)

description: Tropical fruit and sucking-candy aromas are inoffensive and lively. This varietal Merseguera is texturally pulpy but also zippy due to high acidity and only 12% abv. Lime and lychee flavors finish quickly.
title: De Moya 2016 Tibó Meseguera (Valencia)

description: Heavily charred aromas suggest a steak or hamburger left on the grill for too long. In the mouth, this is compact and boxed in by rock-hard tannins. Like the nose, this young Toro tastes burnt and grilled. Flavors of black coffee and clove dominate an oak-driven finish.
title: Jorge Ordóñez & Co. 2016 Triton Old Vines  (Toro)

Appellation: Southern Oregon, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: A light straw color, with a hint of tawny skin, this is a blocky, simple and fruity style, high in alcohol with flavors of peaches. There isn't much to it beyond that one-dimensional fruit, and it finishes short and quick.
title: Schmidt 2016 Pinot Gris (Southern Oregon)

description: Raisiny, saucy aromas are slightly volatile. A wide, saturated palate comes up short on focus and acidity, while a mix of raisin and green-herb flavors lands on a heavy finish.
title: Albada 2014 Viñas Viejas Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: A strong, smoky aroma and equally obvious oak flavors dominate this very full-bodied and fairly smooth-textured wine that was aged in whiskey barrels. It's a one-note song, recommended for people who really like that note.
title: Apothic 2015 Inferno Red (California)

description: Stalky cherry and berry aromas announce a lean, tart palate with a texture influenced by grape skins. Plum and currant flavors are thin, and this is earthy, tangy and low on ripeness on the finish.
title: Bodega Classica 2013 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: A nose with mild citrus and banana aromas is indicative of the fleshy but basic wine that follows. Dry flavors of peach pit are slightly bitter, and it's dull on the finish. This wine marks Cuatro Rayas's 40th year.
title: Cuatro Rayas 2016 Cuarenta Vendimias Verdejo (Rueda)

description: Nectarine and orange aromas are basic and yeasty. A peachy, pulpy palate with flavors of orange and bitter grape skins loses fruit rapidly on the finish.
title: Egeo 2016 Verdejo (Rueda)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is somewhat dull in texture and elusive in fruit, offering a strong sense of oak and sulfur.
title: Merriam 2014 Bacigalupi Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: The nose of this wine is melony and chunky, with a touch of oak. The palate feels solid enough but still a bit mushy, while dry, creamy flavors of tropical fruits are diffuse in front of a finish that's low in verve.
title: Torres 2014 Milmanda Chardonnay (Conca de Barberà)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: This wine is already past its prime. The blend of Müller-Thurgau, Viognier and Moscato is interesting, but the fruit is fading into a simple, lightly peachy syrup, with a hint of plastic. Drink now.
title: Whitetail Ridge 2014 Valo White (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León, Northern Spain, Spain
description: Briny, wayward aromas of grape skins and yeast are funky and unfocused. This Verdejo has a pulpy, soft mouthfeel and flat white-fruit flavors that turn bitter on the finish.
title: Belondrade 2016 Quinta Apolonia Verdejo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León)

description: This dense and rich wine is dominated by dark-chocolate flavors of excessive extraction and bitterness. The fruit is hard to find in this wine that will probably remain tough.
title: Château Doyac 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: With aromas of petrol and spearmint, this wine has flavors of lemon-lime and eucalyptus that do not feel completely integrated. Citrus and minty flavors fight for dominance on the palate, with cooling herb flavors overpowering the finish.
title: Dalton 2016 Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Galilee)

description: A dull gold tint and steamrolling barrel aromas announce an oak-heavy wine that's tight, resiny and high in acid. Woody flavors of salted apricot echo on the finish.
title: Dominio de la Abadesa 2016 Verdejo (Rueda)

description: This medium-bodied straightforward wine has good fruit flavors and a few hints of black pepper and tobacco to keep it interesting. The texture is smooth and only a light touch of tannin shows through.
title: Eden Ridge 2013 Barrel Select Red (California)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: As most Methven Pinots are estate grown, it's not entirely clear what distinguishes this from the others. What it does show, rather agressively, are aromas of leather. Those same flavors render the wine thin, astringent and bitter, amplifying the drying tannins.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 Estate Cuvée Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Designation: Porta Regia Colección Privada Old Vines
description: Rough, rubbery aromas suggest road tar. This Monastrell feels clumsy and hollow. Raw, slightly weedy berry flavors are green and minty on a chewy finish.
title: Sierra Norte 2016 Porta Regia Colección Privada Old Vines Monastrell (Jumilla)

description: A serious commitment to wood aging has given this wine a dark-chocolate and extracted set of flavors. This masks the wine, which seems almost austere. It will likely remain tough.
title: Château Tour Castillon 2015  Médoc

Appellation: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla, Central Spain, Spain
description: This Sauvignon Blanc has little to no varietal character. The nose is quiet, with just a hint of peach. A round palate shows almost no acidity, while this tastes peachy and yeasty prior to a pulpy finish.
title: Felix Solis 2016 Flirty Bird Sauvignon Blanc (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: This wine has respectable but generic fruit flavors, a soft texture and a touch of sugar to round off its edges. It is light to medium bodied and tastes low in acidity.
title: Firefly Ridge 2015 The Red Flash Red (California)

description: This wine is severe in acidity, with a somewhat jarring lack of fruit. Butterscotch and melted butter develop in the glass for a lingering richness.
title: Iron Hub 2015 Small Lot Chardonnay (Sonoma Valley)

description: Pushy oak and heavy clove aromas seem excessive for this level of Garnacha. A rough, grating palate leads to oak-heavy flavors of clove and blackberry, while a persistent flavor of oak-driven clove controls the finish.
title: Marqués de Montañana 2016 Selección Especial Garnacha (Calatayud)

Appellation: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla, Central Spain, Spain
description: Prickly citrus aromas include notes of white flowers. Yeasty grapefruit and lime flavors are bland and short, while this ends flat, yeasty and lightly oxidized.
title: Paso a Paso 2016 Verdejo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: This Tinta de Toro smells like a cherry Lifesaver. In the mouth, there's not much body or substance. Candied blackberry and cassis flavors are medicinal and jammy on the finish.
title: Bodegas Fariña 2016 Dama de Toro  (Toro)

description: Jammy, baked aromas are raisiny and slightly volatile. This blend of 85% Monastrell and Syrah is heavy and soupy, with dull edges. Herbal prune and raisin flavors are raw and green on the finish.
title: Monterebro 2016 Joven Red (Jumilla)

Winery: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
description: This pale-colored wine, a blend of Tinta Francisca and Tinta Roriz, was aged in wood for eight months. That was probably too long, for the fruit has been sucked into the wood, leaving a trail of acidity behind.
title: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo 2016 Rosé (Douro)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Made entirely from Merlot, this sweet pink wine might pass for a simple white Zinfandel. The sugary sweetness comes with light flavors of cherry Kool-Aid.
title: Schmidt NV Pink Rosé (Applegate Valley)

description: Stalky aromas of citrus peel are stale and mostly fruitless. A full, flat palate indicates a wine that has lost its youthful freshness, offering bitter apricot flavors and a finish that's low on acidity.
title: Astrales 2011 Godello (Valdeorras)

description: The nose of this Marquette is all spice and jammy dark berries, with whiffs of Luxardo cherries. The rounded palate shows warm, baked cranberry, blackberry, blueberry sauce and cedar. Soft tannins lend structure and quiet acidity teeters on the edge of flabby, finishing on cassis and a woody tang.
title: Chankaska 2015 Estate Marquette (Minnesota)

description: Made in a rich style, this wine is fully structured and full of black fruit flavors. Grapes from a densely planted old-vine vineyard have added concentration. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016  Fleurie

description: Swirls of char, smoke, earth and game mingle into bright tones of black cassis and plum in this alluring, savory wine. A quiet seductress rather than a powerhouse Côte-Rôtie, this soft-spoken yet deeply complex bottling should improve through 2030.
title: Domaine Jean-Michel Gerin 2013 Champin Le Seigneur  (Côte Rôtie)

description: This memorable wine blends 84% Grenache from the Dry Stack Vineyard in Bennett Valley with 16% Mourvèdre from the Kopf Vineyard. It's exotic and approachable, offering a rich taste of cherry liqueur and tart pomegranate with a background of soft oak and earthy baking spice.
title: Donelan 2014 Cuvée Moriah Red (Sonoma County)

description: A burnished gold color and rich honeyed aromas open this dessert Torontel with Old World shadings. A balanced palate suggests lateharvest Pinot Gris from Alsace, while flavors of honey, caramel and toffee finish with a pecan note. Drink through 2028.
title: Erasmo 2012 Late Harvest Torontel (Maule Valley)

description: The nose is still closed and shy, just giving off hints of cherry. The palate is also taut and tightly coiled. Fine, taut tannins are still drying and firm, the fruit is still enclosed with brisk freshness. There is a dense core of fruit that still needs to unfold. This is powerful but remains resolutely elegant. Give this time to come into its own. Drink 2022–2030.
title: Esterházy 2015 Ried Föllig Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

description: Beautifully aromatic and pure notions of red cherry and tart blueberry become immediately apparent on the nose. They also swish across the palate where they reverberate with alcoholic warmth and a ripe, taut and concentrated density that is countered by equally impressive freshness. The long finish pull these elements together into a potent, powerful but smooth red. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Esterházy 2015 Ried Schildten Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Very ripe and concentrated, this full-bodied wine has powerful fruit flavors, a big impact on the palate and very firm tannins to hold it together. The color is very deep, the aromas are like ripe blackberries dusted with cinnamon and cedar, and the flavors go deep and long, lingering on the finish.
title: Everhart Cellars 2014 Dorado Canyon Vineyard Malbec (El Dorado)

description: This is an envelope-pushing wine that's distinctive and untamed in the way that it combines earthy, savory and mineral aromas with complex, meaty and fruity flavors that have wonderful depth on the palate and length on the finish. The balance is spot-on due to firm acidity and lean tannins, supporting the tangle of unusual and enjoyable tastes. Zero in this case means no added yeast, no new oak and no fining or filtration.
title: Foursight 2015 Charles Vineyard Zero Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: This wine's intriguing nose offers creamy apricot and zesty green Seville orange notes. The interplay is less dramatic on the fluid, dry and fresh palate, where zestiness definitely has the upper hand. It's ripe and rounded. Long live Riesling contradictions, especially when they are so delicious and persistent.
title: Hermann Moser 2016 Ried Gebling Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: This wine's nose gives a tropical aspect of pineapple, a green hint of moss and a citrusy dash of lime. The slender, almost slight palate comes in with juicy, fluid freshness that surrounds a vein of lemon flavor. The dry finish has a cleansing quality.
title: Hirsch 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Riesling (Kamptal)

description: One of three bottlings from this producer that showcases different aspects of Morgon, this wine is full, seriously structured and has the potential of great fruitiness. That will take time; for now the wine is a series of rich tannins that are very mineral in texture. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Marc Burgaud 2016 Grands Cras  (Morgon)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from The Rocks District, the aromas pop, with notes of flower, raspberry, moist earth, wet gravel, green stem, smoked meat, peat and musk. The palate shimmers with intensity but still shows a lot of restraint, displaying an assortment of fruit and savory flavors that persist on the finish.
title: K Vintners 2014 Rock Garden Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: The merest hint of tar rises from the glass. The palate needs a lot of swishing to open up but allows glimpses of black cherry and white pepper, of ripeness and cinnamon spice, of precision and freshness. The tannic frame is fine but still closed and very crunchy. Allow this to mellow a little, then rejoice in the balance provided by the vivid freshness. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Kirnbauer 2015 Das Phantom Red (Burgenland)

description: This lusty Cabernet is earthy, jammy and warm on the nose. In the mouth, this is broad and massive, with an undercurrent of firm tannins that provide structure. Deep dark flavors of blackberry, plum and fig finish with strength on a fruit-packed finish. Drink through 2025.
title: Lapostolle 2014 Cuvée Alexandre Viñedo Apalta Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: This famed slope in Morgon has produced yet another powerful, structured wine. This one, with dark black fruit, rich tannins and plush texture, is dense and delivers great ripe fruitiness. With its tannins and acidity it will age. Drink from 2019.
title: Lucien Lardy 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

Appellation: Edna Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Springtime fresh on the nose, this bottling by Mike Callahan, who is the assistant winemaker at Chamisal, shows guava, crushed chalk, Key lime skin and ripe melon on the nose. The palate is zippy with penetrating acidity, offering more lime as well as Asian pear and Gravenstein apple flavors.
title: Maidenstoen 2016 Oliver's Vineyard Riesling (Edna Valley)

Appellation: Diamond Mountain District, Napa, California, US
description: From one of the warmer mountain ranges in the Napa Valley, this wine exhibits plenty of elegance and classic restraint, though it offers plenty of simultaneous power. Cedar, dried herb, red currant and black pepper make up the range of seasonings, with a touch of crayon wax and bitter leather riding along a substantial grip of tannin. Cellar and enjoy best from 2023–2033.
title: Mueller 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Diamond Mountain District)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: A salty air of quince and tart lemon give life and freshness to this focused and expansive white, from a site long farmed by the Martinelli family in the heart of the appellation. Green apple and nutmeg add secondary characteristics, as toasted oak appears on the lengthy finish.
title: Patz & Hall 2015 Zio Tony Ranch Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Sumptuous notes of ripe, red cherry almost veer into ripe plum territory and have a spicy, mellow hint of vanilla and cinnamon. The palate replays these flavors, complete with finely woven tannins that seem to boost the aromatic cinnamon notes. A rich wine but without superfluous fat. Lovely, velvety and sensuous in a modern, international style.
title: R&A; Pfaffl 2015 Burggarten Reserve Zweigelt (Niederösterreich)

description: This is a well-made, powerful wine. Elegant, integrated and effusive in oak, it has pretty apple blossom aromas and boldly layered flavors of green apple, crème brûlée and lemon meringue. The finish is lush in a honeycomb tone. This will do well in the cellar; enjoy 2020–2025.
title: Robert Mondavi 2015 Reserve Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: This wine's shy nose gives very little away, apart from a promise of citrus. The palate evokes layers of salty and tangy miso, brightened by mouthwateringly fresh lemon notes that accentuate the saltiness all the way through the finish. This needs time to unfold. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Von Stein Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This wine is a blend of fruit from Frenchman Hills (33%), Moxee (28%), Roza Hills (27%) and Evergreen vineyards. The aromas show a lot of subtlety, with notes of candied ginger, lemon curd, spice, peach, cream, toast, wet stone and pineapple, revealing more on each swirl. The palate is rich but refined, showing precision and detail along with a real sense of freshness. The finish impresses. Beautiful stuff.
title: Sixto 2014 Uncovered Chardonnay (Washington)

description: A creamy whiff of fresh, juicy yellow pear creates a most appetizing nose in this wine. That pure, friendly fruit dominates the palate, illuminating a background of spice and herb notes that recall dried sage and fennel seed. Lemon freshness frames everything and makes for a lovely, full-flavored mouthful.
title: Stadt Krems 2016 Ried Wachtberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: The nose of this wine appears as a creamy cloud of green and yellow pear. The palate continues in a similar vein, its fruity, friendly, appetizing fruit congregated around a central spine of bright lemon freshness. A deeper core of savory yeast notes sleeps just beneath the surface; give it time to wake up. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Stift Göttweig 2016 Ried Gottschelle Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Redcurrant, blue flower, cedar and a hint of pine forest are just some of the aromas you'll find on this fragrant red. A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, the bright, savory palate doles out juicy raspberry, red cherry, tobacco and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins while a licorice note graces the close. Enjoy through 2027.
title: Tenuta San Guido 2015 Guidalberto Red (Toscana)

Appellation: Yorkville Highlands, North Coast, California, US
description: Theodora Lee is making brilliant wines from her estate property in Mendocino County. Great concentration and a monumental structure help this wine stand out, even in a field crowded with those qualities. It tastes a little quiet and tight now, and needs time to mellow so that more complexity and richness comes out. Still, it already shows intriguing layers of black pepper, black fig, blueberry and graphite. Best after 2025.
title: Theopolis 2015 Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Yorkville Highlands)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: A stellar example of freshness from a richer region, this bottling offers buoyant and snappy raspberry fruit on the nose alongside dewy forest floor, tangy juniper, soft rose petals and a hint of nutmeg. Fresh and invigorating on the palate, the sip shows tart raspberry, crushed thyme, green peppercorn and tapenade flavors.
title: Theopolis 2016 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Hints of coffee, earth, smoke and spice highlight cool, crisp blackberry and cherry flavors in this wine. It's fragrant and fresh, with a vibrant core of acidity and firm tannins that feel taut and thrilling. While beautiful already, its concentrated fruit and mineral complexities should meld from 2021 and continue to improve through 2030.
title: Vidal-Fleury 2013 Brune et Blonde de Vidal-Fleury  (Côte Rôtie)

description: From a challenging vintage at times, a tiny amount of this subdued wine was made. The nose offers a sense of peatiness, like a good Scotch. Tart orange and grapefruit define an essential crispness to the fruit, as crushed rock and rose petal play along. The tannins and oak are soft and seamlessly interwoven.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Coastlands Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: A faint note of lemon zest plays on the tender nose of this wine. On the palate, lemon takes over, accented by a richer edge of pineapple. It's zesty, brisk and concentrated, providing spine and spark, with a clean, citrus-focused and moreish finish. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Winzer Krems 2016 Ried Pfaffenberg Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Rich in fruit and oak flavors, this medium to full-bodied wine has cinnamon and cedar aromas, ripe black cherry and red-cherry flavors, a firm and rather tannic texture and an oaky spicy finish. A sense of warmth and sweetness makes it hard to resist.
title: Dutton-Goldfield 2015 Angel Camp Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: A creamy note of yellow apple fruit constitutes this wine's nose. The palate follows through with creamy, rounded but still precise freshness. This is a slender, textured Chardonnay with bright, lemon-like acidity and a leesy texture.
title: Esterházy 2016 Chardonnay (Leithaberg)

description: One of a range of wines produced by négociant Florent Descombe, this properly structured wine is rich in black fruits as well as tannins. Coming from the impressive Côte du Py slope, it has power and concentration as well as layers of fruit. Age this wine longer and drink from late 2018.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée M Côte du Py  (Morgon)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: The inviting nose on this bottling combines rich black cherry, zesty raspberry, allspice and dried ginger aromas. The palate delivers big fruit flavors of baked boysenberries and ripe cranberries, which are balanced by a bit of tartness.
title: Foley 2015 JA Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: This wine is more full bodied and rich than first glance would suggest. It has a rather light red mahogany color. Generous strawberry and red-cherry flavors on a mouth-filling texture show light tannins and relatively soft acidity but plenty of viscosity. It was made from 100% Pommard clone vines and aged in 60% new French oak.
title: Foursight 2015 Charles Vineyard Clone 05 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Smooth on entry, with a gravelly texture that develops in the glass, this wine welcomes with a strong perfume of rose petal and strawberry. High-toned, it integrates healthy portions of oak into the savory fruit, with a persistence of acidity on the finish.
title: Furthermore 2014 Gloria Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Blended with 14% Merlot, aromas of char, smoke, ground green herb, spice box, graphite and dark cherry lead to plentiful, saturated dark fruit and barrel flavors with a pleasing mouthfeel. It hits a lot of high notes.
title: Gård 2014 Lawrence Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Named after the grower who owned the estate for many years (his daughter now runs it), this wine is dense, structured and complex. It has a strong mineral element as well as generous tannins and ripe berry fruits. This complex wine will age well. Drink from 2019.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Jean-Ernest Descombes Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: This wine's wonderfully heady citrus pith and pear notes hit the nose. The palate joins with a rounded mouthfeel and more savory flavors; this is all about textured miso and salty sage. A spike of lemon brightness leads to a clean finish.
title: Geyerhof 2016 Ried Steinleithn Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This blends in 13% Petit Verdot and 6% Syrah, strong, powerful varieties that contribute power and dark color. Blackberry, chocolate and coconut ride a rich wavelength of soft mellowed tannin and fig flavor, finishing in a bite of graham cracker.
title: Geyser Peak 2013 Devil's Inkstand Winemaker's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: This wine, with its floral aroma, is rounded, potentially rich and has layers of firm tannins. It comes from a blend of two of the sectors of Juliénas, a combination that brings out great fruit and a fine, dusty structure. Drink this wine with its good potential from late 2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Domaine de la Vieille Eglise  (Juliénas)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: From a great site, this tiny production wine is dark gold in color and oaky on the nose. Baked caramel apple, lemon chiffon and nutmeg all play a role—bold flavors that highlight this well-made effort that offers a throughput of bright acidity.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 Chenoweth Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Fresh lime-spritzer aromas meet with whiffs of smoke and wood ash on the nose of this bottling. The palate is extremely zippy, with sea salt and lemon-zest flavors on the forefront and more complex Brie rind, cashew cream and dried apple toward the finish.
title: Lumen 2015 Sierra Madre Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Aromatic and attractive, this wine spends a little time on its skins, causing it to burst with aromas of flowers, peaches, lime leaf and a bit of honey. The skins also contribute to the satiny texture, balanced by crisp acidity and finishing with a limey, saline tang.
title: Manu 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Aromas of tilled soil, plum cake, exotic spice and a whiff of toasted oak come to the forefront of the nose. Elegantly structured, the taut, linear palate doles out wild cherry, cranberry, licorice, crushed herb and clove set against vibrant acidity and fine-grained tannins. Drink from 2019–2024.
title: Marchesi Antinori 2014 Marchese Antinori Riserva Tenuta Tignanello  (Chianti Classico)

description: This lush, plush and almost syrupy wine has oodles of blackberry and chocolate aromas, a full body and ripe fruit flavors. A subtle layer of oak spice intermingles with all that fruit while soft tannins make it feel broad but not astringent on the palate.
title: Michael David 2014 Inkblot Cabernet Franc (Lodi)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is an extremely smooth, mellow, complex and interesting wine. From the first whiff of cedar and cinnamon in the aroma, to intricate flavors of raspberry, black tea and clove, it brings a lot of nuances to the palate and wraps them in a soft blanket of mild acidity and light tannins.
title: Miller Wine Works 2014 Naked Vine Vineyard Grenache (El Dorado)

description: This is a full-bodied, well-balanced and mouth-filling wine that offers ripe berry and cherry flavors enhanced by subtle baking spices. It has a good framework of fine-grained tannins and supportive acidity to keep it lively on the palate.
title: Papapietro Perry 2014 Charles Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: Parducci has been making great-value Petite Sirah for decades and here's another super example. This is an unusually complex wine for the varietal, offering hints of violet, anise and black pepper, along with rich blackberry aromas and flavors. The mouthfeel is big and tannic, but not overly so, and the rich flavors melt over the palate and linger a long time on the finish.
title: Parducci 2014 Small Lot Petite Sirah (Mendocino County)

description: Cofermented with just under 5% Viognier, this varietal wine was aged in neutral French oak, the better to let its earthy core speak loudest. Mushroom, pine forest and black pepper highlight a broad brawny plate that's densely packed and moderately structured.
title: Pride Mountain 2014 Syrah (Sonoma County)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Sourced from a particularly well-drained block in the Lahoma Vineyards—located on the western shore of Seneca Lake—this is an incredibly pristine dry Riesling, with aromas of racy lime, ripe green pear and crushed stone. The palate offers an intriguing balance between weight and tension, showing ripe tree fruit braced by a vein of lime zest and powdered minerals, ending on an ultraclean, quinine finish.
title: Red Newt Cellars 2014 The Knoll Lahoma Vineyards Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Ripe boysenberry, blackberry and cherry tones are accented by hints of game, licorice root and lemon oil in this well-balanced and accessible Pinotage. The palate is smooth and plush, with well-integrated tannins and enough acidity to balance the opulent fruit flavors.
title: Southern Right 2015 Pinotage (Hemel en Aarde)

description: A rich overlay of cocoa and roasted coffee bean sit atop ripe, brambly notes of blackberry, boysenberry and black cherry on the nose and mouth of this decadent, immediately likeable bottling. The plush mouthfeel offers silky tannins, with ample acidity and hits of toasted oak and fresh mint leaves that add depth and lift through the close.
title: The Drift 2015 Gift Horse Barbera (Overberg)

description: This is a proprietary estate-grown blend of Syrah, Grenache, Mourvèdre and Zinfandel, aged in French oak (45% new). Savory flavors of earth, black licorice, tobacco, meat and leather are lifted on the palate by fresh blueberry and blackberry tones, dusted in chocolate shavings.
title: Westwood 2015 Legend Estate Proprietary Red (Sonoma County)

description: The smell of freshly peeled ripe golden pears shimmers on the nose of this wine, alongside yeasty richness. The palate puts more stress on the rich, textured seam of yeast, which has a satisfying, sonorous miso note.
title: Weszeli 2015 Ried Schenkenbichl Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Templeton Gap District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Tangy plum skins and mulberry sauce meet with salt and pepper and roasted duck aromas on the nose of this blend of 50% Grenache, 35% Syrah and 15% Mourvèdre. The palate is full of brambly fruit, lifted by smoke and sagebrush.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2015 Wanderlust Red (Templeton Gap District)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Lovely aromas of exotic red cherries, sultry mace, spicy cinnamon and white sage show on the nose of this fresh and crisp bottling. There is ample peppery spice to the palate and a strong tannic tension, with flavors of Earl Grey tea, cranberry and pomegranate. Cherry compote shows on the finish.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2015 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of ripe black-skinned berry, underbrush and leather. The juicy palate doles out ripe black cherry, clove, licorice and white pepper alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2025.
title: Cecchi 2015 Valore di Famiglia Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This is a great taste of the freshness of Sonoma without the sticker shock, showing freshly pressed red berries, vanilla and wet slate on the nose. It's light in density on the sip, so the flavors of cranberry, carnation, wet earth and brown spice sail effortlessly across the palate.
title: Alma de Cattleya 2016 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)

description: Dense dark aromas of road tar, eucalyptus, blackberry, tea and barrel spice are Chilean through and through. Aresti's 65th anniversary blend is dry, oaky and intense on the palate. Woody creamy berry flavors are robust as this finishes oaky, spicy and fleshy.
title: Aresti 2012 Codigo de Familia Red (Curicó Valley)

description: A shy, restrained nose hints at red cherry, red plum and cherry juice. The palate, on the other hand, brims with aroamtic cherries: black and red, ripe and luscious but always fresh. It is the slender freshness and appealing texture of fine tannin that makes this both elegant and moreish. Wonderfully concentrated but somehow understated despite its juiciness.
title: Netzl 2015 Ried Haidacker Zweigelt (Carnuntum)

description: This wine goes to the limit with ripeness and full body, showing an especially dark color, very ripe and effusive fruit flavors like macerated cherries and blackberry jam and a mouth-filling texture packed with lots of expansive tannins. It's hard to resist its exuberance and concentration.
title: Patland 2015 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: This wine is ripe, structured, full of fruit and rich. It also is elegant, with a spacious texture and smooth fruit full of black-plum flavors. Drink this wine now.
title: Patrick Tranchand 2015 Poncié  (Fleurie)

description: This wine's beautiful red and yellow apple fruit has a zesty edge of lemon. On the palate, a more textured element reminiscent of apple peel and lemon pith adds body. Freshness pervades the wonderfully elegant and earthy flavors. The finish is bright, dry and fresh with lemon. Lovely now, it's sure to develop. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Prieler 2016 Pinot Blanc (Leithaberg)

description: A touch of wet earth colors the lemony notes on the nose of this wine. On the palate, the lemon becomes even more intense and concentrated. It's a ripe, fruity kind of freshness that recalls Meyer lemon and leaves the palate envigorated with mouthwatering but spiky richness.
title: Proidl 2016 Ried Ehrenfels Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Aged in large wooden barrels called foudres, this wine is smoothly textured as well as generous in its fruitiness. Great red fruits are surrounded by young tannins and touches of wood aging. With its rich texture, the wine will need time. Drink from late 2018.
title: Richard Rottiers 2016 Foudres  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This is a beautiful Pinot from one of New Zealand's most acclaimed biodynamic producers. Plummy fruit, florals, dried leaves, white pepper and a hint of cinnamon lead into a light- to medium-bodied palate that is crisp and elegant yet powerful too, with grainy, almost chewy tannins, a warm stone character, and a long, tangy finish. Still youthful, this should go for another 7–9 years. Drink through 2027.
title: Rippon 2013 Mature Vine Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

description: A touch of ripe melon colors the more usual pear notes on the nose of this wine. Here, they're pure and pristine. That same fruity pear perfume characterizes the creamy palate, along with a yeasty texture and pithy citrus richness. A highly elegant, svelte style, with friendly, delicious fruit.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Ried Lindberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: A touch of soy, a spark of lemon and friendly ripe pear notes combine on the nose of this wine. That harmonized interplay continues on the concentrated palate, where lemony brightness and yeasty, soy-like richness play off each other to great effect. The finish is taut and refreshing.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Ried Wachtberg Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This opens with a strong herbal impression, redolent of fresh fynbos, tilled earth, frankincense and violet, which mix harmoniously with the wild strawberry and boysenberry fruit core. The medium-weight palate is bright and juicy, with a crushed-satin texture thanks to ample acidity and structured yet fine tannins. The pure finish leads with ripe fruit flavors that transition seamlessly to notes of sweet spice and finally a fresh, salty sea-spray accent. It's easy to like now, but it should hold well through 2028.
title: Savage 2015 The Girl Next Door Syrah (Cape Peninsula)

description: Of the vineyard designated wines, this one comes from the youngest vines, planted in 1998. The aromas of peach, pear, mineral, pineapple upside down cake and spice are subtle and refined. The palate is sleek yet silky and extremely flavorful, full of stone-fruit flavors that lead to a finish that won't quit. It's all about delicacy and restraint, hitting a lot of high notes.
title: Sixto 2014 Frenchman Hills Chardonnay (Washington)

description: A touch of russet apple peel plays with the lemon notes on the nose of this wine, suggesting riper, juicier fruit that is made manifest on the palate. Full-fruited, juicy red apple, yellow pear and ripe Meyer lemon notes abound and dance aromatically on the palate. It all comes together on a pithy vein of citrus freshness. This is exuberant and joyful and will evolve if you can actually resist drinking it. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Stift Göttweig 2016 Ried Silberbichl Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Fresh, bright hints of Golden Delicious apples meet zesty lemon notes and a wet pebble stoniness on the nose of this wine. With air, the fruit grows more insistent. The palate is beautifully svelte and lets the apple and ripe pear fruit shine. Lemony freshness lends precision. There is concentration and just a little heat on the dry finish.
title: Tinhof 2016 Weissburgunder (Leithaberg)

description: This Syrah-based blend is ripe, flush and sets a new high-water mark for Viña Maipo, part of the Concha y Toro group. On the palate, this is jammy, deep and complete, with layering. Flavors of toasty oak, graphite, wood spice and blackberry end with oak-aided coffee, mocha and chocolate notes. Drink this concentrated Syrah-Cabernet blend through 2022.
title: Viña Maipo 2012 Alto Tajamar Red (Buin)

description: Exotic spice, toast, crushed mint, French oak and black-skinned berry aromas follow over to the linear, elegant palate along with wild cherry and licorice. It's youthfully austere but well balanced, with fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Give it time to unwind and fully develop. Drink 2019–2033.
title: Volpaia 2013 Il Puro Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: A mere whisper of rose flashes on the nose before herbal, yeasty fruitiness takes over. This field blend is intriguing and layered, made from several varieties. The palate has a salty edge and a rich mouthfeel that veers from creamy to pithy and back again. It lets its yeasty pear, apple and citrus flavors ripple across the tongue. Lovely now but sure to evolve. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Wieninger 2016 Ried Ulm-Nussberg White (Wiener Gemischter Satz)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: A richly ripe and rewarding white, this wine opens in hearty notions of butter, quince and spicy peach. Velvety smooth, it has a decadence that persists and impresses, its use of oak integrated and complementary to the wine's gravelly elements.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Lewis MacGregor Estate Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: From a vineyard known better for Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, this lovely aromatic white is well crafted and fruity, dry with a creaminess to the texture that's beguiling without becoming too much. Lemon, quince and apricot flavors provide a bright, lingering fleshiness that will work deliciously at the table. It spent six months in neutral French oak.
title: Ancien 2016 Sangiacomo Vineyard Pinot Gris (Carneros)

description: This wine's fresh, fruity nose is most inviting, recalling freshly cut Conference pear and the lovely tang of miso and yeast. The palate is evenly balanced between yeasty, salty savoriness and the yellow and green pear notes, creating a lively and full-flavored white with a long, dry finish.
title: Bernhard Ott 2016 Ried Spiegel Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: There's a lot of wild thyme, dusty creek bed and rainy cement on the nose of this bottling, with only the slightest hint of cranberry fruit. The palate's tension is tremendous, which is common for the appellation, with crushed sandstone and rugged earthiness cloaking the cran-hibiscus flavors. It's lightly bodied but firm with upright tannins.
title: Birichino 2014 Antle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chalone)

description: A pure streak of freshly cut green pear on the nose promises a bight, lively wine. The palate is indeed bright and slender, dominated by that pure, lithe, lifted pear note. It's fruit-driven but elegant and understated. The finish is both lemon-fresh and smooth.
title: Buchegger 2016 Ried Moosburgerin Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Aromas of truffle, mocha, scorched earth, toast and baked plum slowly take shape in the glass. Succulent and smooth, the generous, polished palate doles out fleshy black cherry, raspberry compote, cinnamon, crushed mint and licorice framed in velvety tannins. It's already delicious and accessible but still offers good mid-term aging. Drink through 2023.
title: Casa Emma 2011 Fiorella Lepri Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine that comes from three different parcels has an open style, ripe with berries and rich flavors. The style is rich and concentrated, with a solid core of tannins. This wine could well age, and it will be best from 2019.
title: Château du Moulin-à-Vent 2016 Couvent des Thorins  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Earthen notes of freshly tilled soil and garrigue lend depth to plump strawberry and blackberry in this bold yet nuanced wine. It's sunny and pliant on the palate, but fine, feathery tannins linger persistently, along with piquant hints of anise and leather. It drinks well already but should hold nicely through 2027.
title: Château Maucoil 2014 Privilège Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: All the pent-up power inherent in a Morgon awaits in this wine. With dense tannins and concentration from the 85-year-old vines, this still-structured wine is packed with ripe black fruits. The juicy acidity gives a crisp edge, although the wine will need to age until 2019.
title: Domaine Anita 2016 Château-Gaillard  (Morgon)

description: Les Capitans is one of the top sectors of Saint-Amour, producing wines that are perfumed and have great fruit. This wine also has some firm tannins that add to the richness. It is likely to age well and, with its juicy acidity, will be best from late 2018.
title: Domaine du Clos du Fief 2016 Les Capitans  (Saint-Amour)

description: Produced from vines that are more than 50 years old, this wine is concentrated and dense. It also has the great style and elegance that is the hallmark of Fleurie. Black fruits are cut with good acidity, giving a juicy character to balance the ripe tannins. This beautiful wine will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016  Fleurie

description: A youthful aroma of peardrop is foremost on the nose, but on the palate, a zesty, pithy texture takes over. It's accompanied by vivid, lively lemon freshness that keeps it slender and streamlined, ringing with purity and verve. The finish is moreish and lip-smacking.
title: Domäne Wachau 2016 Ried Achleiten Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A tropical hit of pineapple precedes the lemony notes on the nose of this wine. The palate is utterly pure and fresh, with slender and poised lemon notes that carry fluidly and mouthwateringly into the long finish. There is something salty, lip-smacking and very refreshing about this. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Domäne Wachau 2016 Ried Achleiten Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: The merest hint of passion fruit gives a tropical twist to the lemony nose of this wine. The palate comes in with fresh juiciness, combining ripe yellow and tart green apple notes. There's an intense and deeply fruity aroma that sweeps from ripe pear and crunchy apple to ripe orange and zesty lemon. Lovely, pure and sure to evolve. Drink 2028–2028.
title: Ebner-Ebenauer 2016 Alte Reben Riesling (Niederösterreich)

description: This wine is called the heart of Gamay. The name could be a play on the appellation of Saint-Amour or a description of the intense Gamay fruits in this rich wine. Either way, it is dense, full of cherry flavors and with a firm structure that will help it age further. Drink from 2019.
title: Patrick Tranchand 2016 Coeur de Gamay  (Saint-Amour)

description: Beautifully pure and aromatic notes of green pear, lemon peel and green spring herbs like tarragon and chervil play on the nose of this wine. The palate shows similar fruity, fresh and lively charm. Very refreshing.
title: Ruttenstock 2016 Ried Reipersberg Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: Very ripe, Port-like flavors and a big but soft presence on the palate give a bold personality to this full-bodied wine. It's deep and concentrated in berry and raisin flavors that are layered and delicious, squeezing dark chocolate and mint between them.
title: Shooting Star 2015 Zinfandel (Mendocino County)

description: This opens with a gorgeous, highly aromatic nose reminiscent of purple flower fields partnered with ripe raspberry, blueberry and blackberry. The fruit-forward palate offers more berry goodness, with a velvety tannic structure and enough acidity to enliven the finish. Drink now–2019.
title: The Foundry 2015 Grenache Noir (Stellenbosch)

description: From the sector known as Les Capitans on the edge of the village, this wine is produced from vines up to 120 years old. Like other wines from this producer, it offers a mint and herbal character as well as ripe fruit. The wood aging and acidity contribute the richness of the aftertaste. Drink from late 2018.
title: Trenel Fils 2016 Les Capitans  (Juliénas)

description: Juicy, round and silky, this is an approachable, relatively light-bodied wine that doesn't shirk on underlying brawn. Moderate structure maintains a fresh, focused likability as slightly spicy oak hits hardest and most pleasantly on the finish.
title: Turnbull 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Dusty cocoa and charred coconut are front and center on the nose of this decadent pour, supported by notes of jammy berry preserves and violets. The tannins lend ample structure, yet are fine and well-integrated into the ripe fruit flavors. Final notes of green peppercorn and tobacco leaf lend a pleasant herbal and earthy send-off to the sip. Drink now–2021.
title: Van Loveren 2015 Christina The Heritage Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Western Cape)

description: This Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah-led blend opens with blackened oak, vanilla and blackberry aromas. A deep strong palate is not shy with tannins, while this tastes a bit woody, with herbal, spicy black-fruit notes. A dense peppery finish is texturally solid.
title: Viña Chocalan 2013 Vitrum Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this bright red opens with aromas of red-skinned berry, violet and eucalyptus. The savory, easygoing palate doles out tart cherry, star anise with a hint of citrus zest alongside fresh acidity and limber tannins. Enjoy through 2019.
title: Bibbiano 2015  Chianti Classico

Appellation: Yolo County, Central Valley, California, US
description: Great structure and vivid fruit flavors make a winning combination in this full-bodied and firmly tannic wine. Powerful but well polished, it has blackberry and dark chocolate flavors, a solid, grippy texture and is nicely dry.
title: Black's Station 2015 Malbec (Yolo County)

description: Crushed mint, dried red berry, underbrush and new leather aromas lift out of the glass. The lithe, straightforward palate offers dried cherry, grilled herb and a white pepper note set against polished tannins. Vibrant acidity lends balance.
title: Cafaggio 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This 100% Sangiovese shows varietal aromas of underbrush, black-skinned berry and a whiff of leather. The tight, savory palate delivers tobacco, white pepper and Morello cherry set against fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2025.
title: Capannelle 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This vintage of a widely available bottling is a solid everyday sipper for the price, with aromas of star anise, fresh red cherry and snappy hibiscus punch. The palate is fresh and vibrant with raspberry fruit, thyme and white pepper dust, with a warm baking-spice note on the finish.
title: Carmel Road 2015 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)

description: Mixed berry and briary aromas flatten out with airing. This is 85% Romano and 15% Carignan, and it's tight and drawing on the palate, with moderate weight and bright acidity. Popping plum and berry flavors are mildly salty, while this is medium-long on a middleweight finish. Drink through 2021.
title: Casa Donoso 2015 Sucesor Romano Red (Chile)

description: Aromas of new leather, menthol and dark culinary spice lift out of the glass. The linear, refined palate offers red cherry, clove and a hint of licorice framed in fine-grained, polished tannins and vibrant acidity. Drink through 2022.
title: Castello d'Albola 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Made from 65-year-old vines in a five-acre parcel, this wine is rich with black fruits and crisp acidity. With its structure and dense texture, this wine needs to age for a few months. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Château Gaillard 2016  Beaujolais-Villages

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Orange blossoms and citrus peels waft from this lavish, full-bodied blend of Roussanne and Grenache Blanc. It's silky and rich in mouthfeel but crisp white grapefruit and lemon flavors lend freshness to the palate. The finish lingers with hints of white peach and lime pith. Drink now through 2020.
title: Chateu des Coccinelles 2016 Roussanne-Grenache Blanc (Côtes du Rhône)

description: This limited-production wine from old vines is aged in wood, giving it a rich character by adding hints of toast while also enhancing the fruit. It's a ripe wine that will likely age. Drink now for its young, fruity character or age until late 2018.
title: Domaine Chasselay 2016 Eparcieux  (Beaujolais Blanc)

description: Bernard and Thomas Collonge have produced a rich and smooth wine from this three-acre vineyard. It shows the round texture typical of the appellation although with the addition of some fine tannins. The ripe berry fruits are well balanced with the structure. Drink now.
title: Domaine de Colonat 2016 Clos Reisser  (Brouilly)

description: Named after the location of the domaine's winery, this wine is richly structured and full of spice as well as fruit flavors. Dense and concentrated with black-cherry fruit, it has a firm, dry core that will soften. Drink this wine from 2019.
title: Domaine de Grand Garant 2016 Le Vivier  (Fleurie)

description: Whiffs of violets, freshly tilled earth and strawberries abound on the nose. This is a fleshy, voluptuous wine but vibrant black-cherry and berry flavors keep the palate fresh. Fine, ripe tannins accentuate a soft, delicate finish.
title: Domaine des Sénéchaux 2013 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This structured wine is firm and has an intense mineral texture. Rich black-cherry fruits are balanced with intense youthful acidity that needs to soften. This fruity, structured wine is one to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Guenaël Jambon 2016 Les Charmes Cuvée Nature  (Morgon)

description: Seductive in black raspberry, this wine has sizable walls of tannin to burst through and is hugely concentrated in cherry cola and an herbaceous streak. Interlaced acidity saves the day and keeps it relatively fresh in the glass.
title: Enkidu 2014 Bedrock Vineyard Zinfandel (Sonoma Valley)

description: Cinsault is emerging as a serious player in Chile after decades of neglect, with wines like Soothsayers Ferment helping the category. A fresh fruity nose is backed by a grabby palate that deals saucy plum and wild berry flavors. A drawing rubbery finish tastes savory and roasted.
title: Garage Wine Co. 2015 The Soothsayers Ferment Cinsault (Chile)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Forested tree pine, soy and cardamom greet the nose in this intriguing, inviting wine, which is brooding in baked cherry and concentrated strawberry flavors. Velvety and luscious, it's made in a fuller, lusher style that works within its balance of supportive acidity.
title: Geyser Peak 2014 Pluto's Fury Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This is an unusually drinkable Petite Sirah, considering the varietal's reputation for massive tannins. It has lots of jammy fruit flavors, firm but not drying tannins, and a sense of richness that counters the tannin for a soothing mouthfeel.
title: Graziano 2013 Petite Sirah (Mendocino County)

description: Hazelnut and lemon appear in complete synthesis on the nose and palate of this wine. The body reveals more fleshy, lemon-tinged pear fruit and relies on its leesy, nutty and slightly toasty background for structure and texture. This is a big wine that manages to remain toned and fresh.
title: Hannes Reeh 2016 Rohstoff Chardonnay (Burgenland)

description: Chénas, the small appellation next to Moulin-à-Vent, shares something of the same structure. Coming from steep hillside slopes, this wine has a mineral character as well as solid tannins. Its black fruits are still developing, so drink this wine from mid-2018.
title: Henry Fessy 2016  Chénas

description: Very bright, fresh and youthful fruit flashes invitingly in the glass. A second sniff reveals how juicy the black and red cherry notes are. The palate presents this even more clearly: abundant, ripe but balanced fruit brims and plays. Tannins remain in the background while freshness corrals the fruit. This is smoth, fresh and entirely fruit-driven.
title: Höpler 2014 Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: The nose of this wine gives an immediate breezy, zesty impression before a faint hazelnut note appears. On the palate, fruit is in the foregound. Green apple plays with ripe yellow pear and leaves a lovely, light-footed yet textured mouthfeel with a refreshing finish.
title: Höpler 2016 Kirchberg Grüner Veltliner (Burgenland)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
Designation: It Was One of Those Rare LA Days Where There Happened to Be a Thunder Storm.
description: Rachel Silkowski works for winemaker Brian Loring, and his ripe and rich style is certainly apparent in this bottling with an incredibly long name. Dried alpine strawberry meets with mace and allspice on the nose, while the palate packs tarragon-laced boysenberry flavors alongside shaved licorice and allspice.
title: Rasi 2016 It Was One of Those Rare LA Days Where There Happened to Be a Thunder Storm. Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Truffle, moist soil, ground clove leather and balsamic aromas emerge on this along with an earthy whiff of game. The smooth, ripe palate doles out fleshy black cherry, prune, licorice and cured meat alongside polished, velvety tannins. It's already hitting its ideal drinking window so drink sooner rather than later.
title: Rattalino 2010 Quarantacinque 45 Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Whiffs of leather and barnyard lend earthen complexities to bright blackberry and cherry on this elegant wine. Medium bodied and taut, it finishes long, with a bite of bitter nut skin and bold yet integrated tannins.
title: Rémy Ferbras 2015 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Full bodied and full flavored, this complex wine smells like black olive, wood smoke and tobacco, tastes like blackberry and dried herbs and feels broad and firmly tannic on the palate. It's built to age well, and should show even more nuances after 2020.
title: Shannon 2014 Home Ranch Reserve Tempranillo (Lake County)

description: Smoky mocha hints precede notes of aromatic black cherry. The smooth and lush ripeness of the fruit only becomes apparent on the rich, almost luscious palate. The body is smooth and rounded, pliable and plush. The tannins are present but soft, it is ripe black cherry fruit that reigns supreme. Lovely freshness on the finish makes for a bright, long aftertaste.
title: Steindorfer 2015 Reserve Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: Made with 70% Cabernet Sauvignon and 30% Sangiovese, this boasts inviting aromas of blue flower, cedar and red-skinned berry. The bright palate doles out juicy Marasca cherry, red raspberry, a hint of mocha and star anise alongside silky, polished tannins.
title: Tenuta San Guido 2015 Le Difese Red (Toscana)

description: Dense vanilla and baking spice aromas decorate the dried plum and purple-flower aromas on the nose of this single-vineyard expression from the eastern mountains above the Salinas Valley. Black plum sauce, peppercorns and dark chocolate flavors show on the sip, which is quite heavy and dense but expertly structured to stand the test of time.
title: Thacher 2014 Solo Concert Coast View Vineyard Syrah (Monterey)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of grapes from Rita's Crown and Radian vineyards delivers a unique nose of wet wood, lemon verbena, macadamia nut and nectarine cream. The mouthfeel is zippy and the backbone is quite chalky, carrying flavors of poached Asian pear, sea salt and lemon zest, with touches of pecan and browned butter.
title: Velvet Bee 2013 Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: This Maipo Cabernet is spicy and dry on the nose as opposed to bursting with ripeness. A dry tightly wound palate defines the word firm, while slightly rustic Old World flavors of mixed spices, dried red fruits and oak finish with structure. This is the type of Cab that will benefit from further aging. Drink through 2026.
title: Viña Aquitania 2015 Lazuli Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Earthy black-fruit aromas open wild and give this blend of Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Syrah some teeth. A fleshy fully stacked palate shows structure and tight tannins. Spicy blackberry flavors benefit from integrated oak, while chocolate and residual spice flavors make for a dark finish. Drink through 2022.
title: Vistalago 2014 Mezcla Tinta Red (Maule Valley)

description: Highly appetizing notes of Mirabelle and fresh green pear dominate the nose of this wine. The palate is lithe and slender, with a citrus focus, a yeasty, savory midpalate texture and a moreish, salty finish.
title: Weixelbaum 2016 Wahre Werte Gaisberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: Strong spice aromas and ripe, bold fruit flavors make this a powerful, extroverted wine with a lot of flavor impact. It seems to represent a style shift at Williams Selyem. Nutmeg, ginger and clove leap from the glass and laser-focused black-cherry flavors fill the palate, supported by firm tannins and acidity. Best after 2021.
