title: Williams Selyem 2015 Ferrington Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

Appellation: Templeton Gap District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Black currant, elderberry, charred beef, dried violets and cedar show on the nose of this dark, complex bottling. The energized sip delivers tart black plum before offering more elegant and woody flavors of sandalwood and myrrh, with leathery tannins and savory meat flavors throughout.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Templeton Gap District)

Designation: The Fuder Single Vineyard Selection Matthews Lane
description: Over one million cuttings from the Matthews Lane Vineyard have been supplied to nurseries—and therefore wineries—around Marlborough, so in theory, there's a small piece of it in many of the wines from the region. It's special, then, to taste Giesen's single-vineyard version, aged in 1000 liter German fuders. Trademark regional characters of jalepeño, green beans and grass marry with lemon balm, pressed yellow flowers and butter, all ensconced in an oily texture, slippery acidity and a grassy finish.
title: Giesen 2014 The Fuder Single Vineyard Selection Matthews Lane Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Earthy notes of mushroom, white pepper, dried leaves and buttered cannellini beans lead over aromas of lemon and stone fruit. The palate boasts mouth-watering acidity yet not without weight and texture, thanks to time maturing on lees.
title: Hillersden 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Paso Robles isn't known for Pinot Noir, although this historic vineyard has been growing it successfully, and with plenty of aging ability, since 1964. Aromas of light berries, tarragon and sandalwood lead into a palate full of mint, fennel pollen, mulberry and dried plum, altogether exhibiting a li hing mui sort of character.
title: Adelaida 2015 HMR Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Adelaida District)

description: Aromas of iris, tilled soil, new leather, chopped mint and ground clove take shape in the glass. The vibrant, austere palate delivers Marasca cherry, cranberry, espresso and a toasted note while a hint of tobacco marks the close. Close-grained tannins provide assertive support. Give this time to fully develop. Drink 2021–2031.
title: Badia a Coltibuono 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine hails from two top vineyards. A full eight-years-old and coming from a cool vintage, the aromas are light, with notes of cedar, cherry and herb, dotted with wood spice. The palate brings quite tart fruit flavors, accented by racy acidity and supported by firm, tightly wound tannins. With acid like this, it absolutely requires some food alongside it to be properly enjoyed. Steak should do.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2010 Reserve Champoux and Loess Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)

Appellation: Arroyo Seco, Central Coast, California, US
description: Soft red rose petals, cranberry, wet slate and dust show on the nose of this bottling, one of the first new-block designates released by this producer. The palate's plush texture carries strong soil flavors of crushed gravel and turned earth, which give depth to the background of ripe raspberry and pomegranate fruit.
title: Carmel Road 2014 South Crest Panorama Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas leap up out of the glass, with exquisite notes of plum, spice and cherry cream. On the palate, it's rich with soft fruit flavors, showing ripeness but still maintaining its balance, with the neutral oak aging bringing an aching sense of purity. Candied-cherry notes linger on the finish. It's a whole lot of wine for the money, with a big yum factor.
title: Casa Smith 2015 Cervo Northridge Vineyard Barbera (Wahluke Slope)

description: Herbal notes of menthol, garrigue and blackcurrant leaf are upfront on the bouquet of this wine, supported by red forest-berry fruits. Medium in weight and body, it offers fine yet present tannins that frame the red currant, strawberry and rhubarb fruit. Hints of sous bois and spicy oak dance on the earthy finish. Drink now–2023.
title: Chamonix 2015 Feldspar Pinot Noir (Franschhoek)

description: The aromas erupt from the glass, with notes of potpourri, orange peel, moist earth, stem and garden herb. The palate shows a pleasing mix of earth and savory flavors that deliver a lot of elegance and intertwine effortlessly through the finish. The variety seems a bit hard to find but the expression is quite intriguing.
title: Charles Smith 2014 Broncho Stoneridge Vineyard Malbec (Washington)

description: Firm and with great poise, this ripe wine has benefitted from the ripe vintage. It is succulent, balanced between poised tannins and the black-cherry fruits. This rich wine is ready to drink.
title: Château de Juliénas 2015 Cuvée Tradition  (Juliénas)

description: This estate has been vastly improved since it was bought by the Richard family whose coffees are familiar in many French cafés. Richly aromatic and juicy, this wine is full, packed with fruit and acidity, and already delicious to drink.
title: Château des Tours 2016  Brouilly

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Plumes of honeysuckle, citrus peel and spice perfume this vibrant varietal Marsanne. It's medium to full in body but feels spry and fresh, boasting crisp pink grapefruit and guava flavors. Drink now through 2020 to enjoy its youthful appeal.
title: Château du Trignon 2016 Marsanne (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Truffle, new leather, pressed blue flower, menthol and a whiff of game lead the way. The aromas carry over to the ripe, full-bodied palate along with fleshy black cherry, steeped prune, cinnamon and star anise. Buffed tannins provide the framework. It's already accessible but will maintain well for the next several years. Drink through 2023.
title: Curto Marco 2011 Arborina Riserva  (Barolo)

description: This lovely wine has a baked aspect to the fruit that makes it warm and welcoming. It's otherwise edgy in high-toned characteristics of rhubarb, cranberry, orange and forest floor, with bolts of cardamom throughout. Complex, the oak is prominent yet integrated.
title: Davies 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Malibu-Newton Canyon, South Coast, California, US
description: Elliott Dolin's wines continue to make the strongest case for the potential of Malibu viticulture. This bottling shows rounded aromas of lemon rind, white peach, tangerine and a touch of butter. There is great energy and grip on the sip, where nectarine flavors meet with sea salt and sandalwood.
title: Dolin 2015 Chardonnay (Malibu-Newton Canyon)

description: Aged for a short period in large wooden barrels, this wine comes from organically grown grapes and has a smooth texture. It emphasizes the pure, clean fruit and brings out the tannins for aging potential. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de la Bonne Tonne 2016 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: This is a ripe, densely structured wine with the typical perfumed character of the appellation. Ripe berry fruits and layers of acidity lift the wine and give it a crisp texture. Produced from 75-year-old vines, it will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine Matray 2016 Cuvée Les Paquelets  (Juliénas)

description: Invigorating freshness sets this impeccably balanced Viognier apart in a flight of peers. Crisp yellow peach, mango and orange flavors are pristine and cutting, accented by subtle vanilla, spice and marshmallow. It's primary and fruity without restraint, yet elegantly composed in heft and body.
title: Château de Saint-Cosme 2014  Condrieu

description: An impenetrable, teeth-staining purple, the 2014 Le Sol is calling out for a cozy sweater and a fireplace. Intense woodsy aromas of cedar, pine needles, blackberry jam, medicinal herbs and smoky undertones lead into a palate that's herbal, juicy-fruited and framed by powerful, chewy tannins. The oak is on show at the moment but will settle with time. Drink now–2027.
title: Craggy Range 2015 Le Sol Gimblett Gravels Syrah (Hawke's Bay)

description: Heady layers of anise, vanilla and dark chocolate lend a seductive tone to this opulent red. Fleshy black-plum and blackberry flavors are mouthcoating and plump but offset neatly by brisk acidity and sharper edges of pepper and spice. Fine-grained but persistent tannins mark a long finish.
title: Domaine de la Royère 2015 Quercus Red (Luberon)

description: Generous and broad, this plush wine offers lavish layers of strawberry and red-cherry preserves nuanced by more savory hints of earth, nuts and garrigue. It's rich and fleshy in texture with fine, soft tannins that extend the finish.
title: Domaine de Nalys 2014 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Big and bold, this is a rich wine, packed with both fruit and a dense mineral texture. The wine is ripe, full of blackberry flavors and good acidity that will allow it to develop. This has structure, tannins and will age well. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Guenaël Jambon 2016 Côte du Py Réserve  (Morgon)

description: Rich and structured, this wine offers tense tannins balanced by an underlay of ripe black fruits and acidity, although these will take time to come through. Full of ripe tannins, it needs time. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2016 Corcelette  (Morgon)

description: Layers of vanilla, spice and anise are prominent from nose to finish, but this lavish red also offers deep reserves of succulent black fruit, bramble and earth. It's a bold, rather opulent wine framed by ripe, densely structured tannins and fresh acidity. It should continue to improve through 2025.
title: Domaine les Evigneaux 2015 Red (Rasteau)

description: A hint of quince and apple peel clings to this wine's lemony aromas. The palate centers on lithe, high-toned lemon freshness. It's slender and bright, brimming with lemon zest and flesh in equal measure. The clean finish also leaves a lasting lemon note.
title: Eder 2016 Federspiel Riesling (Wachau)

description: Fragrant, ripe apricot scents hover above pear and herb base notes on the nose of this wine. On the concentrated palate, they add smooth fluidity to the yeasty, salty, herb-tinged texture. Pure lemon freshness adds incisive, welcome and beautiful brightness, making this both slender and utterly delicious.
title: Eder 2016 Ried Süssenberg Federspiel Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Sagebrush, French toast and raspberry-syrup aromas make for a dynamic and delicious nose on this bottling. Then chaparral spice meets with salt, pepper, bay leaf and juniper on the palate, spicing up the ripe dark-red fruits.
title: Foley 2015 T Anchor Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: A floral note of white blossom hovers above this wine's lemon and hazelnut notes. The creamy palate provides a lemon flavor that is both bright and rich at the same time. A lovely wine that will improve with time. Drink 2019–2026.
title: Gunter Triebaumer 2016 Ried Geyerumriss Chardonnay (Burgenland)

description: This blends in 11% Petit Verdot, 7% Merlot, 5% Malbec and 1% Cabernet Franc, the whole aged nearly two years in 90% new French oak. Brawny in leather saddle, clove, dark chocolate and a substantial backbone of dense tannin, it's also rounded and fleshy in black cherry and cassis, with fresh, focused acidity.
title: Hertelendy 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A flinty touch of reduction plays among the zesty, aromatic orange notes on the nose. The palate is fluid and light, with an earthy background but ultimately lemon flavors set the tone on the slight, light body. The finish is dry and full of citrus notes.
title: Hirsch 2016 Ried Gaisberg Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This leads with an impression of yeasty, funky richness. That lovely funkiness then turns to great concentration on the palate as leesy, salty savory notes combine with celery, sage and fern flavors. A pure lemon note is a lovely and fresh accent on the long, salty finish.
title: Hirsch 2016 Ried Renner Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: This is a big flavor-packed wine made from some of Lodi's oldest vines. It's quite deep in color, very fruity in aroma, full in body and with nice depth to the ripe blackberry and boysenberry flavors. Accents of black pepper and clove emerge with each sip, while the wine has enough tannin and more than enough body to give a good sense of heft.
title: Jessie's Grove 2013 Royal Tee Ancient Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: A brightness of cherry and cranberry shines in this medium-bodied well-integrated wine, that has a complexity of texture that offers a slight grittiness. Cardamom, clove and white pepper successfully season the fruit.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 VDK Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)

description: A 100% varietal wine from the Entav clone, intended to showcase a Loire Valley-style, this does indeed possess grace and charm, with a well-developed sense of dried herb and violet. Soft and expansive in texture, it features integrated tannins within a strong sense of French oak, allowing plenty of room for expansive raspberry flavors to burst throughout.
title: Lang & Reed 2014 Two-Fourteen Cabernet Franc (Napa Valley)

description: The scent of ripe, tangy red apple peel sets the appetizing tone for this wine made from an indigenous grape. The palate has a slender but textured, pithy feel, made lively by lemony freshness. An incredibly gastronomic white for those wanting something unusual. The finish is wonderfully dry and tangy with citrus notes.
title: Leth 2016 Ried Scheiben Roter Veltliner (Wagram)

description: Freshly cut yellow pear peel aromas hover above a rich yeasty note on the nose of this wine. The palate counters this with an almost fizzy note of lemon freshness that accentuates both its core of pear fruit and the celery and sage notes of the salty yeast. Very refreshing and moreish.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Loiserberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: Attractive scents of brambly berry and red plum set the stage on the nose of this bright, spicy wine. A pleasant herbaceous, stalky note coupled with a peppery lift lend depth and complexity, while final flavors of church incense, cola spice and tilled soil unfold on the finish. The satiny mouthfeel offers plush tannins and an earthy, mineral texture that provides good grip to the close. Drink now–2026.
title: Mullineux 2015 Granite Syrah (Swartland)

description: This wine's fresh, green Conference pear notes immediately hit the nose. The slender, dry, streamlined palate adds a streak of fresh, bright lemon. Spicy yeast, celery salt and miso notes appear on the taut, refreshing finish. Lovely.
title: Nigl 2016 Alte Reben Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This wine was aged longer than most in oak barrels, and has wonderful subtleties that make it compelling to sip, savor and sip again. It starts with a light-gold color, then offers tangy citrus aromas that are lighter and leaner than the flavors to follow, which bring ripe pear and subtle baking spices. The body is full and the texture feels smooth, but with a good touch of crisp acidity.
title: Paul Dolan 2015 Chardonnay (Mendocino County)

description: Great structure and a brilliant sense of restraint make this vividly flavored and medium-bodied wine a keeper—and nearly as good as the winery's vineyard designates. A couple of years in a cellar or cool closet will enhance its red cherry, tea leaf and rhubarb flavors while softening the rather lean texture just a bit. The wine is elegant, appetizing and will be best after 2020.
title: Phillips Hill 2015 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is a standout wine that tastes ripe and full, but also has good balance, a polished texture and a wonderful sense of layering and finesse. Blueberry, blackberry and dark chocolate waft from the glass and equally generous flavors coat the palate, propelled by a velvety layer of fine-grained tannins and full body.
title: Portalupi 2015 Shake Ridge Ranch Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: Vivacious and peppery, this delicately framed Côte-Rôtie offers crisp black-cherry and berry flavors accented by savory truffle, leather and spice. Fine, rigid tannins and penetrating black-fruit flavors still need time to integrate. Hold till 2020.
title: Rémi Niero 2013 Eminence  (Côte Rôtie)

description: Derived from the location of the vineyard in Saburin, this wine's name also reminds us that the Romans were here. The wine is well structured and rich. With 12 months in wood, it conveys spice as well as generous fruit, warm tannins and final acidity. Drink this wine now.
title: Robert Perroud 2015 Romain En Saburin  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Black currant, plum skins and roasted pork aromas show on the nose of this Syrah that's enhanced by Grenache, Petite Sirah, Viognier and Roussanne. Char siu flavors meet with lilacs, violets, charred beef and roasted plums on the palate.
title: Sanguis 2014 Misfit Syrah (Central Coast)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Veteran winemaker Bruce McGuire packs telltale freshness in every bottling, and this reserve is no different, with buoyant aromas of black currant, crushed violets and black licorice. The palate's snappy blackberry flavors are cut by hints of anise, bay leaf and dill, with plenty of purple-flower lift.
title: Santa Barbara Winery 2014 Reserve Syrah (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Utterly fresh hints of green Conference pear play on the nose of this wine. They also dominate the palate at first, before a salty, moreish touch of yeastiness leaves its textured imprint. All is united by bright, lemony freshness that has a savory tang.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2016 Ried Lamm Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: An atypical variety for such a southern region, this Pinot Grigio shows some semblance to its brethren in the north, with apple, citrus and blossom tones, yet its missing all of the vibrancy and verve from a lack of acidity. Nevertheless, it's a perfectly clean and simple drink.
title: Cantine del Borgo Reale 2016 Pinot Grigio (Puglia)

description: A dark color and damp aromas of raisin lack freshness. Overall, this wine is stewed and oaky, with a heavy palate that's showing salty oak-driven berry and prune flavors. It's missing in balance and finishes with persistent saltiness.
title: Casar de Burbia 2014 Casar Mencía (Bierzo)

description: Reticent on the nose in aromas of struck flint, citrus and pollen, these notes carry to a astringent palate of apple and citrus. Medium in weight, there's just enough mouthwatering acidity to maintain freshness.
title: Castello Monaci 2016 Acante Fiano (Salento)

description: Aromas of dried apricot and walnut carry over to the straightforward sparkling palate along with acacia honey. It's racy and assertive, with vivacious, rather steely bubbles.
title: Contadi Castaldi 2011 Soul Satèn  (Franciacorta)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Apparently a reserve-level offering, this has the same overripe raisined character as the regular 2015 Pinot. The fruit may have been harvested late, or in a rain window—whatever the cause, this is well past optimal ripeness, drying out, and should be consumed in the near term.
title: Cória 2014 Don Simón Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Foxy berry aromas are fruity and jumpy. This wine is grapy on the palate, and more flat than lively. Jammy black-fruit flavors are herbal, while mint and peppery notes drive the finish.
title: Cruz de Piedra 2016 Garnacha (Calatayud)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of soda water, peach and melon lead to broad, dry, slightly viscous fruit flavors. It's an unusual interpretation of this variety.
title: Eleven 2016 Sugarloaf Vineyard Pinot Grigio (Yakima Valley)

description: Rubbery dark-fruit aromas come with cheesy oak scents. This basic Toro has a sticky, saucy mouthfeel bolstered by tartaric acidity. Short, oaky berry flavors are chocolaty, while the finish offers mostly residual oak notes.
title: Gil Luna 2016 Sin Complejos  (Toro)

description: Medium cherry in color, this wine offers aromas of blackberry, cassis and smoked meat. Flavors of black cherry, flint and game never quite coalesce, dropping off into a soft sage-tinged finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2016 Gilgal Pinot Noir (Galilee)

description: This wine offers candied citrus aromas and flavors, with a chalky fruitiness reminiscent of crushed vitamins on the palate. It has mild acidity but is a little flimsy in feel. The finish is long and sour.
title: Gray Ghost 2016 Seyval Blanc (Virginia)

description: This opens with earthy aromas of black-skinned fruit, tilled soil and a hint of crushed black olive. The aromas carry over to the extremely simple palate alongside round tannins.
title: Rocca di Frassinello 2015 Poggio alla Guardia  (Maremma Toscana)

Appellation: Applegate Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Viognier above 16% alcohol is really pushing the boundaries of ripeness, and it results in a rather flat-tasting wine with one-dimensional fruit flavors and nothing else. Peaches and melon, with a hint of citrus rind come through, but how do you match this to food?
title: Schmidt 2016 Viognier (Applegate Valley)

description: Made in Montana with grapes from Yakima Valley, this wine shows aromas of tangerine, white peach and honeysuckle. The palate is juicy in ripe lime, fresh apricot and blossom tones, with enough acidity for balance. The finish is brief and peachy.
title: Ten Spoon Vineyard + Winery 2016 Sweet Talk Riesling (America)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Barrel fermented for 10 months, this wine, now three years past its vintage, remains awkward and oaky. Flavors of wood and butter dominate, with light generic fruit well in the background.
title: Airlie 2014 Estate Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)

description: Ultraripe cherry and blackberry aromas are covered in a sheen of dark chocolate on the nose of this Negroamaro, Primitivo and Malvasia Nero blend. The medium-bodied palate turns more jammy in baked strawberry and cherry flavors, with grainy tannins and just enough acidity for balance.
title: Anarkos 2015 Rosso Red (Puglia)

Appellation: Campo de Borja, Northern Spain, Spain
description: A largely neutral nose hints at papaya and melon. This Viura is lean in feel, with lemony acidity. Generic nectarine and melon flavors flatten out on a finish that's showing early-onset oxidation. Drink right away for best results.
title: Aragus 2016 Blanco Viura (Campo de Borja)

description: Baked berry and sweet spice aromas dominate the nose, while toasted oak tones arise on the palate. Medium in weight and light in concentration, crisp acidity keeps this refreshing, but short.
title: Cantele 2015 Primitivo (Salento)

description: A dark purple color and aromas of blackberry, creamy oak and pencil lead to a raw, pulling palate that's thick but also high in tartaric acidity. Grainy oak and mocha flavors make a louder statement than does the fruit.
title: Casa L'Angel 2016 Cabernet Sauvignon (Valencia)

description: Toasty and woody, this wine is currently dominated by the wood aging, leaving little space for the juicy red fruits in the background. It will soften and should be less aggressive from 2019.
title: Château le Monge 2015  Haut-Médoc

description: Stalky aromas of earthy plum and burnt leaves are not fully ripe. A choppy, gritty palate holds saucy flavors of salted plum, tomato and herbs. A short finish with herbal notes is appropriate. The blend holds Garnacha, Carignan and Syrah.
title: Clos de Nit 2014 Crianza Red (Montsant)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Scents of wet hay and overripe raisined fruit don't mislead: those are also the flavors. Some tarragon and citrus is in view, but overall it's disjointed and seems already well on the way to being over the hill.
title: Cória 2015 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Mild aromas of green apple and lemon have a grassy undercurrent, yet the palate is surprisingly lush in flavors of canned peach and mandarin. The finish is rubbery, with notes of lime pith and orange-blossom honey.
title: Dablon 2016 Sweet Riesling (Lake Michigan Shore)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Aromas of ripe black-skinned fruit, violet and a whiff of wet leather lead the nose. The palate starts off with fleshy black cherry and a hint of clove then but quickly falls apart thanks to loose, fleeting tannins.
title: Duca di Saragnano 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Made entirely with organically farmed Sangiovese, this has delicate aromas suggesting wild berry and blue flower. The fresh, simple palate offers hints of Marasca cherry and raspberry alongside bright acidity.
title: Fattoria di Magliano 2016 Illario Rosato (Maremma Toscana)

description: Pale lemon and thyme aromas precede a very buttery flavor in this split-personality wine. It is medium bodied, crisp at first sip but turns butterscotchy on the palate.
title: Fetzer 2016 Sundial Chardonnay (California)

description: Flowery, yeasty aromas are also soapy and buttery, which makes sense given that this is overly mature and was brought up in oak barrels. Moderate acidity means balance is fleeting, and the wine tastes of stale stone fruits and yeast.
title: La Val 2012 Fermentado en Barrica Albariño (Rías Baixas)

Appellation: Eola-Amity Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Despite the landmark vintage, this wine seems thin, under-ripe, and rife with green stem and herb flavors. The tannins are substantial, hard and bitter, effectively blocking out the finish.
title: Methven Family Vineyards 2014 House Block Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: The Noir is an equal blend of co-fermented Gamay Noir, Pinot Noir and Trousseau Noir. An interesting experiment, but it emerges with a bitterness around barely ripe flavors of rhubarb and pink berry.
title: Minimus 2016 #23 Noir Red (Willamette Valley)

description: Raw, herbal, oaky aromas include notes of Navajo sage stick and oak-driven clove. This wine is 85% Monastrell with Syrah, and it feels grabby, tannic and oaky. Creamy oak flavors are soupy, while this wine is plump, chewy and tastes of forced oak on the finish.
title: Monterebro 2016 Barrica Red (Jumilla)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is half Syrah with the rest equal parts Pinot Noir and Tempranillo. Aromas are locked up on first pour, only revealing high-toned notes of barrel spice. Over time, notes of candied raspberry, black licorice and dried cherry start to emerge. The palate brings plump red- and black-fruit flavors. Cranberry notes linger on the finish.
title: Oak Knoll NV Toasted Cow Vintners Blend 6th Edition Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a sturdy, grippy and youthful 100% varietal wine, from vineyards planted in mountainous locations. Bright focused acidity lifts dense flavors of black cherry and sage that are wrapped in spicy, clove-driven oak. Enjoy best from 2024–2034.
title: Hall 2014 Jack's Masterpiece Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This is an inaugural release, blended with 7% Merlot and aged 22 months in French oak, 65% new. From vines grown predominantly in the northern end of the valley (Calistoga and St. Helena), it features a grainy texture of bold dark cherry that's granular and focused on the palate. Tobacco and leather add to the savory nature of the billowy tannin power. Enjoy best 2020–2030.
title: Hall 2014 The North End Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: There is a savory, gamy touch to the red cherry aroma on the nose. The palate counters this with crunchy, vivid cherry flavors, with a fine tannic grip offering a wild but alluring quality. A very individual but beautiful expression of this grape. Drink 2019–2030.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2015 Holzspur St. Laurent (Thermenregion)

description: Blended with 10% Petit Verdot, this age-worthy beauty is impressively oaked and tannic in its youth, with a concentration of densely packed red and black currants. Coconut shavings and vanilla appear from the background alongside black pepper and cinnamon. This should drink best from 2024–2034.
title: La Jota Vineyard 2014 Merlot (Howell Mountain)

description: A touch of lemon, a whisper of pineapple. This combines ripeness and freshness on a palate that's concentrated yet slender and taut. Glimpses of much riper fruit appear; orange, apple and russet pear shimmer and wait. You should wait a little, too, for this elegant, fluid and slender Riesling. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Lesehof Stagård 2016 Gaisberg Riesling (Kremstal)

description: This wine's fresh citrus foliage, lemon zest and fragrant conifer notes make for a highly aromatic opening. It's dominated by a bright lemon freshness that pervades every aspect of the concentrated but finely structured palate. A taut, delicious package that's lemon-charged to the hilt. Lovely now, it will only get better. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Markus Huber 2016 Ried Berg Reserve Riesling (Traisental)

description: Aromatic, ripe blueberry notes entice the nose. The merest hint of licorice and tar tingles around the edges. A crushed, juicy berry charm also pervades the palate which dances along with such ease, such abandon, such joy, entirely on tiptoe, twirling, smiling and charming its way to a long finish. There is so much vigor and easy charm, a fresh-faced, unforced and disarmingly honest wine. Yet there is nothing facile. Lovely. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Muhr-Van der Niepoort 2015 Liebkind Blaufränkisch (Carnuntum)

description: A seductive and well-integrated veil of earthy spice—cinnamon stick, fruit cake, bark, black tea leaf—sits atop a ripe core of red berry and cherry fruits. Beneath that is a strong gravelly, flinty minerality that pervades the palate and expresses strongest on the finish. Medium weight, the tannins are firm yet fine, resulting in a rich, velvety texture. A beautiful and complex expression, this should continue to develop and unfold additional layers of flavor through 2031.
title: Mullineux 2015 Schist Roundstone Syrah (Swartland)

description: The sonorous and soft vanilla notes of oak provide a beautiful backdrop for all the blueberry aromas that rise from the glass. The palate adds some black cherry notions to the ripe blueberry but remains more slender and fresh than the nose would suggest. Its full, mellow fruit is held in a fine gauze of tannins on a fresh, vivid body. Full marks for elegance. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Nittnaus Anita und Hans 2015 Pannobile Red (Burgenland)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dark and dense aromas of blackberry and black currant meet with tar, black pepper, tapenade and plush violet on the nose of this fascinating wine, which treads the line between piquantly savory and richly ripe. The palate shows sour black plums, crushed peppercorns and seared lamb flavors against a dense black-fruit background.
title: Pence 2015 Sebastiano Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Charming, just-ripe fruit flavors and a silky texture make this wine elegant and polished. It has a lighter color than average, but that doesn't make the generous black cherry and rose petal flavors any less gorgeous, or the medium-body and good acidity any less appetizing. It's a beauty from one of Anderson Valley best sites.
title: Phillips Hill 2015 Cerise Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: You'll find aromas of star anise, freshly tilled soil, chopped herb and balsamic notes on this full-bodied red. The smooth, concentrated palate delivers juicy black cherry, raspberry compost, pipe tobacco and licorice accompanied by firm, refined tannins and bright acidity.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Muncagota Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Scents of menthol, new leather, dark culinary spice, cured meat and forest floor lead the nose. The smooth, full-bodied palate shows juicy Morello cherry, pomegranate, licorice and clove balanced by vibrant acidity and tense, refined tannins. An almost salty note closes the finish. This a classic Barbaresco with years ahead of it. Drink 2021–2038.
title: Produttori del Barbaresco 2013 Pora Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: After some time in the glass the 2014 Bric Turot reveals complex aromas of wild berry, dark spice, blue flower and balsamic notes. The elegantly structured, energized palate is even more expressive than the nose, delivering ripe Marasca cherry, raspberry compote, cinnamon and star anise. It has terrific tension, thanks to vibrant acidity and firm, polished tannins. Open a few hours ahead of time. Drink 2019–2029.
title: Prunotto 2014 Bric Turot  (Barbaresco)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
Designation: Block 11 Mount Eden Clone Bien Nacido Vineyard
description: Toast, crushed slate, crisp jicama, sliced Asian pear and squeezed tangerine show on the nose of this block-designated bottling from the historic vineyard. There is a salty kick to the sip, where the rounded and full mouthfeel delivers flavors of hot toast, lemon marmalade, the lightest touch of marshmallow and an iodine-laced finish. Incredible depth overall.
title: Scar of the Sea 2015 Block 11 Mount Eden Clone Bien Nacido Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: A hint of passion fruit here, a dash of lemon there. This wine's nose is not willing to give too much away. The palate has generous apple and citrus freshness and also rings with richer, more tropical notes, such as mango. It's vibrant, dry and fresh but still needs to unfold fully. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Stift Göttweig 2016 Ried Pfaffenberg Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

Appellation: San Benito County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This 35th anniversary vintage bottling is as excellent as ever, with aromas of black cherry, boysenberry, wild thyme and sagebrush on the nose. The palate treads the same balance between dark fruit and chaparral spice, with baked red cherries, nutmeg and star anise flavors.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Vista Verde Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Benito County)

description: This new producer offers the first vintage of this wine. Made by Steve Matthiasson in a classically restrained style, it was given plenty of room to develop varietal character, with 25% Merlot adding softness. It shines in a texture of dense black raspberry dotted in black pepper and pencil—its herbaceous character in balance. An underlying freshness keeps it compelling and bright.
title: Ashes & Diamonds 2014 No. 1 Cabernet Franc (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Horse Heaven Hills, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aged in neutral French oak, this wine puts a premium on purity of expression. The aromas are distinctive in intriguing notes of crushed flower, blackberry and sage, along with whiffs of mountain berry and meat. The flavors show depth and intensity, with a swoon-worthy feel and a finish that sails.
title: Avennia 2015 Le Corbeau Discovery Vineyard Syrah (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: This delicious white combines 87% Sauvignon Blanc, 8.5% Viognier and 4.5% Sémillon. Together, they are as compelling as a garden in jasmine and gardenia aromas. Smooth and sublime on the palate, it offers a succulence of peach and pear within a refreshing core of medium-bodied fleshiness.
title: Blue Rock 2016 Baby Blue Blanc White (Sonoma Coast)

description: Amalfi lemon notes—just hinted at on the nose—get their full expression on the streamlined palate of this wine. It's focused, clean, slender and pithy, oozing freshness and charm in equal measure. The finish is clean, brisk, dry and lasting. Drink through 2030.
title: Buchegger 2016 Ried Moosburgerin Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Pressed iris, red-skinned berry, underbrush and a whiff of rosemary lead the nose on this 100% Sangiovese. The supple, juicy palate delivers black cherry, white pepper, leather and star anise alongside velvety, polished tannins. It's already accessible but will also age well for the next several years or more. Drink through 2025.
title: Capannelle 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dense black currant, lanolin, leather and vanilla aromas are lifted by sagebrush and dried mint on the nose of this single-vineyard expression from the famous vineyard. The palate is jam-packed with herbs, from oregano and thyme to juniper, with blackberry rising in the midpalate and spearmint and more vanilla carrying through the finish.
title: Carlisle 2015 Rosella's Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: This small-production wine, produced from parcels of old vines, is impressive for its richness and density. With flavors from wood aging adding spice as well as giving extra structural dimension, the wine is set for good aging. Do not drink before 2019.
title: Château Thivin 2016 Cuvée Zaccharie  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: From one of the top districts of Morgon, this wine is well structured and offers rich fruits. A good mix of spice and black fruit is supported by tannins. It is a rich, juicy wine with an impressive future. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016 Corcelette  (Morgon)

description: The headiness of freshly cut tangerine zest fills nose of this wine with perfume. The palate continues in the same citrus-flavored vein, bringing more tangerine but also piercingly fresh lemon. This is linear, slender and totally refreshing. Pure lemon marks the long, moreish finish. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Eder 2016 Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: A tropical touch of passion fruit clings to the otherwise earthy, savory nose of this wine. The palate plays on that savoriness, displaying salty, herb-tinged miso notes in a streamlined, bright and lemony package. It's taut but packs a punch.
title: Forstreiter 2016 Alte Reben Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: A whiff of passion fruit adds tropical allure to the ripe pear fruit on the nose of this wine. It's tempered by rich yeastiness, which continues on the palate, softening the texture. A spike of lemon freshness brightens everything in this impressive, full-bodied wine.
title: Hiedler 2016 Ried Schenkenbichl Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: At this point the nose does not give away much, merely a hint of tar. The palate, also rather closed at this point, promises plum notes colored with a maple syrup spice. There is concentration and a fine tannic weave alongside bright freshness. This has fruit, structure and balance and was made with a knowing, light touch but needs time to unfold. It carries its weight well and will blossom. Drink 2022–2032.
title: Jalits 2015 Steinberg Red (Burgenland)

description: A rather shy nose gives nothing away apart from a slight hint of earth. The palate is also subdued in flavor, but presents a firm structure of fine tannin and a spine of bright freshness, balancing out alcoholic warmth. This needs to develop and unfold, all the harmony is already there, the fruit just needs to come out of its shell. Drink 2022–2030.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2015 Grillenhügel Pinot Noir (Thermenregion)

description: This wine is hugely structured and dense, displaying the real heart of Moulin-à-Vent in its richness. Black plum and spice from wood aging give this wine a ripe character and aging potential. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine Anita 2016 Coeur de Vigneronne  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Fragrant ripe apricot notions hover above the pear and herb base notes of the nose of this wine. On the concentrated palate, they add smooth fluidity to the yeasty, salty, herb-tinged texture. Pure lemon freshness adds welcome and beautiful brightness, making it slender, precise and utterly delicious.
title: Eder 2016 Ried Süssenberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: The appetizing brightness of freshly cut Conference pear engages upon first sniff. The palate then brings a yeasty riff that goes hand-in-hand with luminous lemon flavors, presenting a harmonious balance of lasting savory, creamy and fruity flavors. This shows immense poise and purity.
title: Eichinger 2016 Ried Gaisberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot and 25% Petit Verdot is all about richness, starting with aromas of vanilla, caramel, milk chocolate, licorice and black-currant crème brûlée. Soft and plush on the palate, the butterscotch, candied blueberry and black plum-syrup flavors are laid across slices of hot spice cake and lifted by mulling spices.
title: HammerSky 2014 The Party Estate Grown Red (Paso Robles)

description: This is a Merlot-driven blend at 82%, with 12% Cabernet Sauvignon, 4% Malbec and 2% Petit Verdot. The Merlot is from an older site on Howell Mountain. Spicy nutmeg seasons a soft, rounded palate of integrated tannin and full-bodied but balanced weight, the fruit flavor crisp in cherry, raspberry and cassis, just a touch of butterscotch revealed at the wine's end.
title: Hertelendy 2014 Signature Mountain Red (Napa Valley)

description: Quintessential Sangiovese aromas of pressed violet, wild berry, new leather and dark culinary spice pave the way on this delicious wine. Tight and structured, the elegant palate delivers juicy red cherry, licorice and truffle. Bright acidity and assertive, refined tannins provide energy and compelling tension. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Istine 2015 Vigna Casanova dell'Aia  (Chianti Classico)

description: Freshly cut green and ripe yellow pear notes appear enticingly on the nose of this wine. The palate takes these along and adds zesty citrus brightness and a spicy herbal tang of sage and celery salt. It's taut, fresh, concentrated and muscular, without any fat.
title: Jäger 2016 Ried Achleiten Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A touch of mahogany and bitter almond colors the tight, dark fruit on the nose, which translates as black cherry on the taut, immensely concentrated palate. Fine, drying tannin, warming alcohol and bright acidity highlight the fruit flavors, yet right now there still is a bitter edge of tannin that needs to mellow. The stuffing is there to go the distance—this is a big and muscular wine. Drink 2022–2032.
title: Jalits 2015 Szapary Reserve Blaufränkisch (Eisenberg)

description: This wine suggests fresh greenery on the nose: sage and fern are the foremost aromas. The palate brims with an intense savoriness recalling miso, salt and soy. It's intense, concentrated and compact and totally designed for the long run. Drink 2019–2030.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Käferberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: This wine's tart, spiky glints of passion fruit tease the nose amidst all its lemony freshness. With a little air, the passion fruit note grows smoother and fuller. The palate glows in this fruitiness and complements it with creamy richness. It's lovely and seductive now but sure to get even better. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Neumeister 2015 Ried Moarfeitll Sauvignon Blanc (Vulkanland Steiermark)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Playing to the tangier side of the grape, this bottling offers dewy sagebrush, barely ripe red currant, white pepper and a hint of tapenade on the nose. The flavors are more rounded with tart cranberry and tangy red-plum skins, enhanced by thyme, dill, green peppercorn and exotic coriander spice. It is very well structured.
title: Pence 2015 Fugio Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: A beautifully evocative red cherry aroma perfumes the nose, joined by a gentle oak tone. The palate has a graceful, fine structure of subtle but firm tannins that frame the rich, ripe red cherry flavor. There still is bite and freshness, making the generous weight appear all the more alluring. A touch of cinnamon makes this feel even more sumptuous. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Steindorfer 2015 Reserve St. Laurent (Burgenland)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a delicious, dark and suave blend of 32% Syrah, 22% Petite Sirah, 20% Mourvèdre, 20% Cabernet Sauvignon and 6% Viognier. Roasty aromas of cocoa and coffee mesh with elegant and rich black plum on the nose. The palate is wrapped in polished tannins, full of both power and finesse, with black currant, espresso, loamy earth, pepper and chaparral herb flavors.
title: Adelaida 2015 Twenty Three Twenty Red (Adelaida District)

description: From the Haynes Vineyard, this is a beautifully floral wine on the nose, dripping in honeysuckle. Green apple and Bosc pear are deeply interwoven into the softly structured fabric of its balanced ripeness, as ginger and nutmeg highlights provide depth. The acidity is lasting and well mannered through the finish.
title: Ancien 2015 Musque Chardonnay (Coombsville)

Appellation: Santa Cruz Mountains, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a fascinating and lovable rustic quality to this single-vineyard bottling, which is slightly cloudy in the glass and shows aromas of red cherry, hickory smoke, dust and a feral gaminess. That foxy element is prevalent on the sip, with hints of animal hide and dried chaparral set against pomegranate and wild strawberry flavors.
title: Birichino 2014 Lilo Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: A touch of leesy freshness clings to the lemon notes on the nose of this wine, revealing how very young it still is. The palate has a textured mouthfeel that owes as much to yeast as it does to lemon zestiness. With its great concentration, it still needs time to unfurl the full force of its citrus power. The finish is incisive, long and lemony. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Buchegger 2016 Ried Vordernberg Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Earth, yeast and saltiness color the lemony freshness of this wine's aroma. They also give a funky twirl to the palate, which shines with tart but ripe red apple and orange flavors. Underneath is a bright vein of lemon zest. It pulls the focus into the long finish, which tingles long after you have swallowed. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Geyerhof 2016 Ried Goldberg Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Lovely notes of black cherry on the nose have a pleasant edge of bitter almond. The palate is dense with that same aromatic cherry and has both ample power and concentration to make this a rather muscular, potent wine. The tannins are fine and have a soft but distinct grip, with a warming, long finish. Drink 2022–2032.
title: Jalits 2015 Diabas Reserve Blaufränkisch (Eisenberg)

description: Fragrant green notes inform the nose of this wine: there are moss and sage, citrus foliage and fern scents. That herbal richness also plays on the slender, fresh and streamlined palate. Citrus freshness emanates from the textured flavors, softened slightly by salty yeast notes. An impressive, concentrated wine with lots of potential. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Jurtschitsch 2016 Ried Käferberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: This wine's clean, ripe yellow pear notes stream from the glass, accentuated even more by bright, lemony freshness. This fruity combo continues throughout the slender, lithe palate, which reveals intriguing celery salt and fennel seed notes as you slowly savor. It is made all the more precise by fresh, lemon-like acidity. Gorgeous now but sure to develop. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Malat 2016 Ried Höhlgraben Alte Reben Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Baking spice and baked plum highlight a juicy, velvety explosion of sultry fruit in this well-made, full-bodied wine. Toasted oak wraps around hints of pine forest and mushroom, with a layering of dried herb and a tart rhubarb-kissed finish.
title: Merry Edwards 2015 Flax Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Salt rock, caramel and quince combine effortlessly in this medium-bodied wine with a wealth of elegance and structure. The soft texture provides a framework for its balanced complexity and length of baking spice.
title: Patz & Hall 2015 Bootlegger's Hill Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Made from organically grown grapes, this wine comes from vines that are between 40 and 80 years old. It has structure, tannins and concentration as well as richness, powerful blackberry flavors and a balance with acidity. The wine will age, so drink from 2019.
title: Richard Rottiers 2016  Moulin-à-Vent

description: The nose of this wine is still subdued. Hints of wet earth and pure lemon notes can barely be discerned. With a few swirls of the glass, tender hints of blood orange zest pervade nose and palate, alongside spiky, bright lemon notes. The body is slender and taut but brims with gorgeous citrus aromas. Lovely now but sure to last. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2016 Ried Gaisberg Riesling (Kamptal)

description: A deep color and thick aromas of blackberry and black fig lead to opulent mouth-filling dark-fruit flavors and a velvety substantive texture. This wine ranks with Turley and Carlisle Petite Sirahs at the top of the quality pyramid in California. It has all the expected tannin along with oodles of fruity, smoky richness to stand up to it. Best through 2028.
title: Sirius 2013 Eaglepoint Ranch Petite Sirah (Mendocino)

description: This stunning white is sourced from a handful of sources, including the producer's estate, but also Hyde Vineyard in Carneros, Stagecoach on Atlas Peak, and several sites on Sonoma Mountain. It offers a demure nose of honeysuckle and peach before allowing room for lemon, quince and dried herb to come forth on the creamy palate, finishing bright and fresh in acidity.
title: Spottswoode 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa-Sonoma)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Almost black in the glass, this rich and dense bottling impresses immediately with aromas of black currant, caramel, spearmint and wet slate. It's soft and velvety on the palate and loaded with flavors of roasted coffee, shaved chocolate and black plum pudding. Tannins uphold the weight perfectly.
title: Adelaida 2015 Anna's Estate Vineyard Syrah (Adelaida District)

description: This is the first release of this wine from this new producer based in Napa. Made by Steve Matthiasson, it is a bleed of Cabernet Franc grapes grown in Carneros, Oak Knoll and Yountville that's both exuberant and expressive in candied cherry and tart orange. Barrel-aged in steel barrels, it offers fully realized acidity in a tightly wound package of pure quenchability.
title: Ashes & Diamonds 2015 No. 1 Rosé (Napa Valley)

description: Fresh celery and dried fennel seed notes play against a backdrop of pear-fruited freshness on the nose of this wine. These qualities are repeated on the light, translucent and refreshing palate. There is something quite disarming about so much flavor in such a lithe body. Simply lovely.
title: Bründlmayer 2016 Langenloiser Alte Reben Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: From a prime vineyard along Westside Road, this is a restrained take on a warm site and concentrated vintage, with beautiful floral aromas at its heart. Gravenstein apple and stone fruit play well against each other, alternating in tartness and succulent fleshy flavor. The acidity is buoyant and focused, offering a bit of precision to the palate.
title: Gary Farrell 2015 Bacigalupi Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Santa Margarita Ranch, Central Coast, California, US
description: The price-to-flavor ratio on this bottling is astounding, as this blend of 55% Syrah, 27% Zinfandel, 13% Petite Sirah and 5% Petite Verdot offers all that Paso power at half the price. Intense black currant, blackberry pie, beef jerky and caramel aromas lead onto a palate full of chalky tannins and balanced acidity. The roasted blackberry flavors would pair perfectly with a beef stew.
title: Ancient Peaks 2015 Renegade Red (Santa Margarita Ranch)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bright black raspberry aromas meet with shaved licorice, fresh fennel, tarragon and purple rose petals on the nose of this single vineyard expression. The sip dries up considerably at first, with loamy, minty flavors, but they open toward strawberry, sumac and candied red cherry. Cinnamon and menthol spice fire up the finish.
title: Black Kite 2015 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Aromatic notes of ripe red berries and much more tart Morello cherries play on the nose, joined by a faint hint of woodsmoke. The palate is beautifully translucent in pure cherry fruit, with a graceful structure of very fine tannins. Both ripeness and extraction are beautifully judged here. Lovely already but sure to evolve. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Bründlmayer 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Niederösterreich)

description: Hazelnut-like oak notes still dominate the nose of this wine. The palate counters them with friendly mirabelle plum fruit and ample lemon freshness. This needs time to integrate and unfold but has all the necessary balance and stuffing to do just that. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Bründlmayer 2016 Ried Lamm Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: After skipping a vintage, the producer offers this impressively powerful expression of the variety from a great site. Compressed plum and strawberry compote flavors are rich and rounded, nuanced in juicy acidity. A lasting bite of oak and tannin permeate on the long finish, accented in white pepper.
title: Carlisle 2015 Rossi Ranch Grenache (Sonoma Valley)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: Candied blackberry, black cherry, plum paste, mint and dark chocolate show on the nose of this dense built-to-last bottling by Mike Officer. The palate is thick and the tannins are still a bit stiff, but the intense marjoram, oregano, thyme and elderberry jam flavors will grow even more fascinating with time. Drink from 2019 through 2035.
title: Carlisle 2015 Sierra Mar Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Sébastien Gutty is proud owner of this three-acre parcel of 90-year-old vines. They have given a wine that reflects both richness and concentration. It is densely packed with dark tannins, black fruits and a shot of acidity. This is a wine to drink from 2019.
title: Château Gaillard 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Morgon)

Appellation: San Luis Obispo County, Central Coast, California, US
description: The nose on this bottling is both juicy and serious, with cherry, pomegranate, dewy morning sagebrush, muddy sand and star anise aromas. There is solid textural tension to the sip, where red currant and lavender flavors mesh. Strong acidity soars into the finish.
title: Cutruzzola 2014 Gloria Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)

description: Layers of spice, vanilla and toast are integrated from nose to palate in this elegant Condrieu. It's an unctuous, textured sip marked by silken waves of orange water, blossom and cream. The acidity is soft but lemony, lending freshness to a salty, savory hazelnut finish.
title: Delas Frères 2016 Clos Boucher  (Condrieu)

description: This is a big, rich wine, full of tannins as well as intense fruit. Concentration and dense texture are just part of the fruitiness and structure that give this wine its potential. Still developing, the wine is likely to show at its best from 2019.
title: Domaine des Chers 2016 Vieiles Vignes  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Fermented with natural yeasts and with little added sulphur, this wine concentrates on its fruit character. Fine tannins, rich black fruits and a dense texture all contribute to its distinctiveness. With its structure and acidity it does need to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016  Morgon

description: This large, 61-acre, vineyard forms part of the Piron properties. The wine has great concentration and a dense ripe texture that comes from low-yielding, 50-year-old vines. Let this wine fill out even more. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaines Dominique Piron 2016 La Chanaise  (Morgon)

description: This wine has that lovely mix of citrus zest and pith on both the nose and palate. The two vie for attention, giving both freshness and texture. The flavors are highlighted by lemon but also informed by greener, savory hints of ivy, celery and fennel. White pepper makes an intriguing appearance on the dry finish.
title: Domäne Wachau 2016 Smaragd Terrassen Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

Appellation: Marin County, North Coast, California, US
description: Delicious fruitiness and full body are a winning combination in this luxuriously oaky, very ripe-tasting and impressive wine from Marin County—not exactly a household name for wine, but it should be. The black cherry and stewed-plum flavors are so rich they seem sweet, but a good structure of acidity and firm tannins keeps the wine balanced.
title: Dutton-Goldfield 2015 Azaya Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Marin County)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: There is extreme florality throughout this bottling, starting with wisteria and dark carnations on the bright and fresh nose, alongside black raspberry fruit. The flowers are a bit more subdued on the sip, yet violet, lilac and rosewater flavors persist, decorating the suave strawberry and rhubarb background.
title: Gainey 2015 Limited Selection Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Russet pear peel and citrus pith notes hovers above the savory, yeasty nose of this wine. The palate then gives full rein to wonderully rounded, salty soy notes, which ring with citrus freshness and ripe pear notes through the midpalate. Beautifully balanced, concentrated and very appetizing.
title: Geyerhof 2016 Ried Gaisberg Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Fragrant and refined, this radiant red opens with enticing scents of wild berry, aromatic herb, dark spice, menthol and whiffs of tilled soil and pressed blue flower. Ethereally elegant and boasting great tension, the palate delivers succulent red cherry, raspberry, clove and a hint of licorice while vibrant acidity and polished, fine-grained tannins lend balance and support. Drink through 2024.
title: Isole e Olena 2014  Chianti Classico

description: Even in old-vine Beaujolais terms, vines planted in 1935 are rare. These come from a small parcel within the Grand Cras sector of Morgon and produce a richly concentrated wine. With firm tannins, ripe black fruits and balanced acidity, this dense wine needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Michel Dupré 2016 1935 Vieilles Vignes  (Morgon)

description: An aromatic streak of wild cherry flashes up on the nose and completely takes over on the smooth palate. This is structured by finely woven tannins, corsetting the beautifully judged fruit. At the perfect juncture of freshness and ripeness, this brims with sensuous, red cherry fruit.
title: Johanneshof Reinisch 2015 Frauenfeld St. Laurent (Thermenregion)

description: A creamy, subdued ripe pear aroma is the first impression this wine gives. The palate counters with ample freshness and a wonderfully textured mouthfeel that combines cream, fruit, celery salt and citrus pith notes. All the concentration is cushioned with generous fruit and invigorating freshness.
title: Josef Bauer 2016 Ried Spiegel Alte Reben Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: A flinty touch of reduction colors the pear and yeast notes on the nose of this wine. The palate shows all the characteristic savory richness of yeast, but here it seems to sparkle with lightness and brisk, lemony freshness, finishing on savory notes of sage and fennel. Although slender, it's full of flavor.
title: Jurtschitsch 2016 Ried Loiserberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: The aromas are sweet and sappy, with notes of raspberry, herb, pipe tobacco and musk. The palate shows more restraint than the aromas might suggest, bringing a suave sense that carries through a finish that just won't quit. It's equal parts intrigue and delivery, bringing a lot of richness.
title: K Vintners 2014 El Jefe Stoneridge Vineyard Tempranillo (Washington)

description: This is a 100% varietal wine, juicy and broad on the palate with mouth-filling tannins that show its youthful vigor. Cherry and raspberry liqueur flavors run deep, wrapped in sizable oak and well-integrated accents of cedar and dried herb. This stands to be cellared; enjoy best 2024 through 2029.
title: La Jota Vineyard 2014 Cabernet Franc (Howell Mountain)

description: Savory tones of tobacco leaf, dried lavender and thyme lend a dark, sultry appeal to rich blackberry and plum here. This wine is fleshy on the palate yet firmly balanced with fine, taut tannins and a lingering, brambly finish. Graceful and nuanced, it should drink at prime through 2030.
title: Laurence Féraud 2014 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: A touch of reduction still lets this come across as brooding. But lemon zest and dried hay flower flavors come to the fore with a little air. The concentrated midpalate boasts zestiness and a yeasty texture. This will deliver joy, poise and refreshment with its pervasive lemon notes. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Loiserberg Riesling (Kamptal)

description: If you know Matetic Syrah, then this will be like an old friend coming back for a visit. The nose is earthy and dense, with the winery's patented mix of heady herbal fruit and eucalyptus aromas. A chewy palate spreads its wings with grace and strength, while this tastes of toasty oak, medicinal black fruits and minty herbs. Drink through 2022.
title: Matetic 2012 Syrah (San Antonio)

description: Plenty of up-front oak makes for a nose with char and smoke on top of perfumed black fruit. A profoundly deep palate is fairly tannic and firm. Flavors of black plum, dark chocolate, herbs and pepper finish more dry than jammy, with tea and wood spice notes. Drink through 2025.
title: Montes 2013 Folly Syrah (Apalta)

description: Pure notes of crushed blueberry have a wonderfully tart edge, almost like wild little huckleberries. The same, pure and intense fruit spreads across the textured body where fine and ever so slightly rustic tannins crunch pleasurably. This wine shows the juicy, almost voluptuous ripeness of 2015 without ever losing freshness or tone. This should come with some sort of pleasure warning. Simply delicious. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Muhr-Van der Niepoort 2015 Samt & Seide Blaufränkisch (Carnuntum)

description: This Syrah has an old-world soul, with pronounced earthy scents of game, tobacco leaf, chicory root and loads of white pepper that grace notes of black cherry and raspberry fruit. The velvety palate boasts smooth yet enduring tannins, while flavors of charred animal hide, cherry skin and forest floor carry through to the long, evolving finish, ending with a soft sanguine accent. While it's enticing now, it should mature gracefully through 2028.
title: Mullineux 2015 Iron Syrah (Swartland)

description: This wine combines so much of what we've come to love about New Zealand Pinot. It's light and bright but serious too, with plenty of site expression. Raspberry and blueberry compote, violets, baking spices and sun-baked stones all dance together on the nose. There's plush fruit on the palate but a savoriness too, framed by sinewy tannins and finishing on a stoney, mineral note.
title: Nautilus 2015 Southern Valleys Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Rich and benefitting from the ripe fruit of the vintage, this is a generous wine. Bold red and black cherry fruits are broad and open with soft tannins and good acidity. The wine is smooth and ready to drink.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2015 Grand-Pré  (Fleurie)

description: This wine is ripe, soft and generous. With red fruits and only light tannins it is cushioned and rounded. Happily the acidity and the crisp texture counter the richness. Drink this friendly wine now.
title: Domaine Lathuilière Gravallon 2016 Pisse Vieille  (Brouilly)

description: A 100% varietal wine from the producer's own vineyards, this is a full-bodied take on the variety that drinks crisp and clean, shining in extracted red and black cherry that become succulent in the glass. Broadly expansive tannins support rather than distract.
title: Ehlers Estate 2015 Cabernet Franc (St. Helena)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is a meaty, brawny wine that's well-conceived and velvety smooth in texture. It ticks all the boxes on the variety, imparting well-defined layers of leather, black pepper, blackberry and gunpowder, lasting long on the finish in a trickle of spice.
title: Eric Kent 2014 Atoosa's Vineyard Syrah (Russian River Valley)

description: Brawny and vibrant in acidity, this well-made full-bodied wine is a study in softened tannin and texture, with an oomph of weight and breadth. Raspberry and black cherry shine alongside black pepper and cinnamon with a lasting tease of brick and leather.
title: Eric Kent 2014 Kalen's Big Boy Blend Syrah (Sonoma County)

description: Pristine blackberry and raspberry are flecked with spice and earth in this plump yet freshly composed wine. A hint of rubber accentuates earthy, savory tones on the midpalate but these turn to prettier dried herb and lavender on the finish.
title: Famille Quiot 2013 Les Combes d'Arnevel Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This wine is rich and ripe with red fruits. Its structure and dry tannins suggest a wine that will age. Red berries, acidity and a dry core point to a well-structured wine to drink from mid-2018.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée C  (Chiroubles)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Brawny, this is an inviting version of the variety, well-done by this historic producer. Baked plum is met by dusty, soft and rounded tannin structure, the oak supportive and happy in the background. The light spiciness adds a sense of richness. This is an ideal pizza wine.
title: Foppiano 2014 Grant Station Carignane (Russian River Valley)

description: Firm and with a dry core, this wine has great concentration and rich tannins. Ripe fruit lies under this solid exterior, waiting to come through in the coming months. The wine will show great richness as it matures. Drink from 2019.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine de Roche Noire  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Concentrated black cherry and black olive aromas have an earthy edge in this full-bodied wine, while the flavors are dry and potent black fruits with touches of dark chocolate. It's a well-structured wine that will improve through at least 2021.
title: Geyser Peak 2014 Merlot (California)

description: Blue flower, baked plum and pipe tobacco aromas slowly take shape. The palate offers dried cherry, prune, star anise and crushed sage while firm acidity and assertive, close-grained tannins provide the austere backbone. Give this a few more years for the tannins to unwind. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Il Molino di Grace 2013 Il Margone Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Balsamic aromas of camphor, eucalyptus and dried herb mingle with whiffs of blue flower and ripe plum. The taut, juicy palate delivers fleshy Marasca cherry, black raspberry and white pepper alongside lithe tannins. Drink through 2021.
title: Istine 2015  Chianti Classico

description: The vineyards on the steep hill leading down from the chapel of La Madone have given a structured wine. Weight and richness underly firm tannins. Concentrated and potentially full of fruit, the wine needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Claude Debeaune 2016 La Madone  (Fleurie)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Heady aromas of rose and jasmine meld with rich tangerine and red apple notes. Off-dry on the palate, melon and florals meet with plenty of acidic cut from grapefruit zest, leading to a candied lemon and ginger finish. Weight, power and balance make this an excellent offering from winemaker August Diemel.
title: Keuka Spring 2016 Gewürztraminer (Finger Lakes)

description: Elegant and plush, this wine is both light and rich at the same time, as it combines a fine mousse of bubbles with good fruit and spice flavors and a creamy texture. Everything is well-blended and harmonious as well as concentrated enough to have a long and lightly ripe finish.
title: Korbel NV Blanc de Noirs Sparkling (California)

description: Notes of forest floor, saddle and garrigue lend a rustic feel to this wine. Black-cherry and strawberry flavors are more muted than fresh, but nuanced and savory. It's moderately concentrated but lingers nicely, guided by a fringe of fine, upright tannins.
title: La Ferme du Mont 2013 Jugunda  (Gigondas)

description: Rose garden and lavender aromas make for an inviting entry into this mountain-grown wine, as stiff, bristling acidity keeps it bright and lively in the glass. The texture is broadly soft and seductive, developing a silkiness on the finish.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 Van der Kamp Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Good fruit concentration and firm structure make a fine combination in this full-boded mouth-filling wine. It has a compelling dense chewy texture that supports lots of ripe black cherry and blackberry flavors with firm tannins.
title: Le Casque 2014 Cabernet Franc (Sierra Foothills)

description: A bold, bombastic, 100% varietal wine made from mountain fruit, this red was aged 30 months in all French oak, 52% of it new. It imparts burly notions of oak and tannin alongside graphite, cedar and sage highlights. Very ripe, it expands on the palate in blackberry and black pepper, never letting go of its power.
title: Ledson 2013 Stagecoach Vineyard Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Prickly, lightly herbal berry aromas are spicy, traditional and a touch rough. This is full in body but not overly packed with tannins. Flavors of herbal berry fruits and chocolate finish spicy, oaky and chocolaty, all of which are normal for a Colchagua red blend like this. Drink through 2023.
title: Luis Felipe Edwards 2013 Doña Bernarda Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Ripe smooth aromas of lightly herbal black fruits are foresty and chocolaty. In the mouth, this Shiraz blend is fleshy but also tannic. Baked flavors of spiced black fruits spread out on a finish that's more bulky than it is finessed.
title: Luis Felipe Edwards 2014 LFE 900 Single Vineyard Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Veins of waxy orange and lemon peels freshen this full-bodied but lively Viognier. Vanilla bean and tangerine flavors are rich and silky on the palate accented by fragrant swirls of honeysuckle. The finish is a tad warming but lingers pleasantly.
title: Maison Denuzière 2014 Aphrodite  (Condrieu)

description: Dark earthy rubbery aromas of spicy black fruits set up a saturated grabby palate with firm tannins. Spicy but ripe blackberry flavors finish with dark oaky flavors of coffee and toast. For a wine that's been in the bottle for over four years, this is in a comfortable state. Drink through 2020.
title: Maquis 2011 Lien Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Heady aromas of ripe red berry, fragrant purple flower, truffle and dried aromatic herb take the lead. The smooth, savory palate doles out juicy wild cherry, black raspberry and baking spice. It's extremely enjoyable, with supple tannins.
title: Marchesi Antinori 2015 Pèppoli  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of exotic spice, pressed blue flower, menthol and an earthy whiff of forest floor slowly take shape. The tight, youthfully austere palate offers dried cherry, star anise, espresso and thyme while assertive, fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity provide firm support. Best after 2020.
title: Mazzei 2013 Ser Lapo Riserva Privata  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Spicy bramble berry aromas take on a slight brandied character, bathed in anise and savory dried meat tones. The plush, silken palate boasts some additional savory streaks, with shots of cranberry acidity keeping the weight in check. Soft tannins offer just enough support to carry this wine to the lingering rich, cherry finish.
title: McCall 2010 Corchaug Estate Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: There's an overt richness to this wine, with bold aromas and flavors of muddled berries and cherries that are hit by cocoa powder and Dr. Pepper. The medium-weight palate boasts a surprisingly firm and structured texture, with the lingering grip countering the ample ripe fruit. Drink now–2023.
title: Meerlust 2015 Pinot Noir (Stellenbosch)

Appellation: Hudson River Region, New York Other, New York, US
description: Diffused aromas of citrus, sea spray and peach blossom envelop the nose, with apple peel, tart peach and jasmine marking the flavors. The medium-bodied palate is silken in feel, yet balanced by quinine acidity.
title: Millbrook 2016 Proprietor's Special Reserve Dry Riesling (Hudson River Region)

description: This wine starts off a bit tight and closed, dominated by cocoa nib and espresso bean notes that are coupled with char and earthy, rooty accents. The palate is similarly tightly wound, with black fruit flavors that are eager to harmonize with the overt oaky tones. It's all there, it just needs some time to align; drink 2020–2023.
title: Neethlingshof 2015 The Owl Post Pinotage (Stellenbosch)

description: Like a pear tart, this wine from a new small producer is full of baking spices, butter and fruit from the first whiff to the lingering, rich finish. While dry, it's full bodied, very lush and viscous in texture, and the yummy flavors are bold without going to extremes.
title: Oro Bello 2016 Chardonnay (California)

description: Velvety in concentrated black-cherry and strawberry flavors, this age-worthy wonder also displays floral intensity and persistent, supportive tannins. Bright acidity is layered throughout, adding a sense of energy to the robust nature of its brambly undercurrent of dry-farmed fruit. Enjoy 2020–2025.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Precious Mountain Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: From a cool site above Occidental, this shows smoky peppercorn, high-toned strawberry and raspberry notes—the fruit expansive and succulent throughout. Focused acidity provides a persistent energy and freshness, with a measured sense of oak.
title: Dutton-Goldfield 2015 Fox Den Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)

description: This leads with a ripe, tropical whiff of pineapple that's subsumed into a more savory notion of celery-salt and miso. The concentrated palate similarly juxtaposes salty miso with bright lemon notes. In the end, the saltiness triumphs and leaves you wanting more. A compact but very concentrated wine that's full of flavor and has a lip-smacking finish.
title: F X Pichler 2016 M Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A lovely touch of freshly grated lemon rind appears on the nose of this wine, with the slighest hint of green Seville orange. The palate comes in with pristine green apple freshness, highlighted by lively, zesty lemon notes. It's zingy and taut with citrus freshness, attaining a riper Mandarin notion as it lingers. How enlivening and how lip-smacking. Drink 2018–2035.
title: Franz Hirtzberger 2016 Singerriedel Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: What a beautiful wine from the famed site still farmed by the Rochioli family. This is fresh, fruity and layered in citrus and Gravenstein apple, with interweaving acidity through flavors of lemon and lime. It has a stony quality that speaks to underlying minerality and a jolt of briny, salty air.
title: Gary Farrell 2015 Rochioli Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Softened by 6% Merlot, this is a classically elegant wine, juicy in cranberry, cassis and teases of coffee bean, the tannins broadly etched and brawny in their youth. Integrated oak provides a spiciness of toffee and black licorice, as the aromas develop floral notions of violet as the wine opens in the glass. Enjoy best 2024–2034.
title: Hall 2014 Ellie's Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Subdued at first, this wine awakens with a little air, as notions of citrus foliage, lemon zest and Mandarin emerge. The palate presents these flavors with purity and verve, adding earthy depth against which the lemon freshness shines. It's taut, concentrated and lovely now but has the stuffing to go the distance. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Steinmassl Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This wine's pure notes of ripe Amalfi lemon awake the senses. They unfurl on the palate, revealing their full, zesty, linear force, accented by tart red apple and riper orange zest notes. Freshness pervades and highlights the concentration of the streamlined palate, leaving you with a clean, lasting and absolutely addictive finish. Drink 2020–2030 at least.
title: Malat 2016 Ried Silberbichl Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Highly aromatic notions of cinnamon-dusted blueberry and plum rise from the glass. The richness of that scent is countered on the palate by ample freshness which gives clean delineation to the fruit flavors and precision to the body. Right now this is taut but flavors run deep. There seems to be a floral core of peony and berry that still needs to unfold—elegance and depth are to come. Drink 2022–2035.
title: Muhr-Van der Niepoort 2015 Ried Spitzerberg Blaufränkisch (Carnuntum)

description: Flavor complexity and a lot of finesse in the texture give just about everything a Pinot Noir can give. This wine, from a tiny, high-elevation vineyard, has enticing aromas of mint, clove, bay leaf and red cherry followed by vibrant flavors of red cherry and black tea (that are satisfying but don't overpower the firm texture), fine tannins and welcome acidity. Best after 2020.
title: Phillips Hill 2015 Valenti Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)

description: Freshly cut green Conference pear notes hover above more elusive crushed wild thyme aromas in this wine. There is a touch of wet pebble, too. The palate is concentrated but streamlined. An earthy hue of textured miso adds a little richness to the otherwise zesty, lemony, fresh and enduring flavors. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Pichler-Krutzler 2016 Ried Kellerberg Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: Evocative, tender hints of lemon balm and verbena play on the nose of this wine, alongside ripe lemon and orange peel. The palate has a steely concentration of pure lemon freshness. It's linear, fluid, concentrated and dry. The finish is incisive and lip-smackingly fresh. Drink now–2030.
title: Sepp Moser 2016 Ried Gebling Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)
