description: Stony, flinty reduction still clouds the nose of this wine. Underneath, the promise of citrus is evident. On the palate, that citrus freshness is clothed in juicy swirls of ripe apricot and yellow apple. A sonorous take on Riesling that's intriguing and charming at once. Wait for this to come together. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Bründlmayer 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Alte Reben Reserve Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This wine is currently a big, bold beast, with serious concentration and tremendous depth that suggests this wine's considerable future. Deep aromas and flavors of ripe black cherry, plum and raspberry all abound on the nose and mouth, with a equally intense overlay of whole baking spice, campfire, black tea and pepper. The mouthfeel is bold yet balanced, with a texture like crushed velvet and a long, evolving finish that transitions masterfully from dark fruit flesh to charred cherry and baked plum, and then final notes of espresso bean, black forest cake and tilled earth. Expect even more complexity and layers of flavor to develop with age. Built for long-term cellaring, drink 2023–2033.
title: De Toren 2015 The Black Lion Shiraz (Coastal Region)

description: Pure, tender and evocative notes of fresh orange and Mandarin zest characterize the nose of this wine. That zesty freshness veers from Mandarin to Meyer lemon on the taut, fresh almost fizzing palate. Still young, it shows utter promise, with precise flavors and great concentration. Irresistible for its citrus notions now but certain to develop. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Eichinger 2016 Ried Gaisberg Riesling (Kamptal)

description: A 100% varietal wine, this is brawny in tannin and big in oak, yet completely beguiling in an underlying delicacy of refined power. Chalky texture that traffics in tobacco and leather overlays elusive fruit and persistent earthiness, a mix of blueberry and blackberry bramble and mountain sage.
title: Hall 2014 Eighteen Seventy-Three Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This wine must have spent very little time in bottle; it still smells of the leesy, fermentative richness of the cellar. But never fear: this newborn is loaded with lemon concentration and uncompromising briskness. Electric freshness is its central purpose, and it will provide sophisticated refreshment for years to come. Drink 2020–2030 at least.
title: Lesehof Stagård 2016 Pfaffenberg Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Beautifully pure and life-like apple notes stream from the glass, depicting at once tart crunch and ripe juiciness. That lovely fruit carries all the way across the palate, supported by a streak of zesty lemon and a pithy, tingling texture that tapers into an arrow of freshness. Its trajectory is long, and while lovely now, it will get even better. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Malat 2016 Ried Steinbühel Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Incredibly pure notes of ripe Mandarin peel and juicy tangerine encapsulate the nose of this wine. A full blaze of ripe citrus freshness is then unleashed on concentrated but taut palate. Ripe tangerine, Mandarin and Meyer lemon flavors are expressed with aromatic and heady intensity, performing a high-wire act with bright, stony acidity that keeps it from feeling fleshy or fat. Wow. Lovely now, sure to evolve. Drink 2018–2035.
title: Pichler-Krutzler 2016 Ried Steiner Pfaffenberg Alte Reben Riesling (Niederösterreich)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Deep and dark on the nose, this savory bottling, which is enhanced by bits of Viognier and Grenache, shows chocolate, seared beef, black currant, black pepper, rosemary and lamb aromas. It's soft and thick on the palate, where baked black-cherry flavors are spiced by star anise, coffee and cola, with energetic acidity popping on the finish.
title: Sanguis 2013 Pilgrim Syrah (Central Coast)

description: A smoky touch of reduction still hovers on the nose of this wine. Underneath, apple freshness with an earthy, leesy touch and lots of lemon notes spreads across the linear palate. This needs time to unfurl but will do so beautifully. The finish is dry and long and has a wonderfully pure, almost transcendent lemon note. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Bründlmayer 2016 Ried Heiligenstein Lyra Reserve Riesling (Kamptal)

description: Pristine, aromatic Mandarin notes color the underlying notions of green and yellow pear in this wine. The palate has that same aromatic Mandarin ripeness, along with textured, concentrated yeast flavors that shimmer with soy saltiness on the palate. The body is taut and streamlined; it promises to be very long-lived. Citrus aromas tingle long into the finish.Drink 2019–2030.
title: Domäne Wachau 2016 Ried Kellerberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: Beautifully pure green pear notes are joined by ripe yellow plum aromatic touches on the nose of this wine. These fruit notions persist, despite the palate being all about texture and salty, lemony concentration. Still tightly curled, it needs time to unfold and is totally worth waiting for. Drink 2020–2030.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Kellerberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A note of flinty reduction still clings to the pear and yeast notes on the nose of this wine. Apart from citrus zest, the palate almost dispenses with fruit, delivering instead a celery-salted, yeasty, tingling texture. It crackles on the tongue with its freshness and savor and leaves an immensely fresh, long aftertaste. Drink 2020–2030.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Loibenberg Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: A touch of citrus foliage and the merest hint of passion fruit color this wine's lemon aromas before taking over the entire palate. Its svelte, linear and concentrated body fizzes with lemon sherbet-like flavor. Let it calm down and reveal all its lemon verve. Drink 2020–2035.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Ried Steinertal Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: An urgent, almost piercing note of green Seville orange zest and conifer leads the nose of this wine. It intensifies on the palate, where it's edged with stony saltiness and a touch of dark, bitter fir honey. A taut, concentrated wine that shimmers with rich layers of flavor, this was built to last. Give it some time, and your patience will be rewarded. Drink 2020–2035.
title: F X Pichler 2016 Unendlich Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: Coming from the Royal Slope, which no doubt will deservedly have its own appellation before long, enchanting aromas of ground rock, green olive, green stem, crushed flower and dark fruit lead to a panoply of fruit and savory flavors that shake the senses to the core, with smoked meat notes persisting on the long finish. It's an intense experience but maintains a sense of balance that is heart stopping. Drink from 2020-2027.
title: K Vintners 2014 Royal City Stoneridge Vineyard Syrah (Washington)

description: This wine's gloriously heady notes of lime and lemon zest hit the pleasure center of the brain upon first whiff. That theme of bold, zesty citrus continues with abandon on the taut, linear and concentrated palate. It leaves a lip-smacking, refreshing finish that's loaded with pithiness. If you want refreshment and verve, this is the answer. Lovely now, sure to develop. Drink 2018–2030.
title: Loimer 2016 Ried Seeberg Riesling (Kamptal)

description: This wine is memorable and impressive—a ripe exploration of earthy goodness that's rich and velvety smooth. Spicy red fruit seduces amidst a well-developed core of complex orange peel and cardamom highlights, the oak well-integrated and supportive.
title: Martinelli 2014 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: This organically farmed ranch on the eastern edge of the Sta. Rita Hills is producing amazing wines, including this bottling, which combines boysenberry, forest moss, gamy meats and a mud-pit earthiness on the nose. Focused and intense flavors of earth, ripe elderberry, sagebrush and a hint of truffle show on the sip, where the tannins are polished but structurally sound. Drink now–2030.
title: Pence 2015 Unum Estate Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Very rich and ripe, but also very firm and well built, this full-bodied wine has all the elements for great balance: robust and dry fruit flavors, good acidity, firm but not harsh tannins and appropriately strong alcohol. It's great to drink now with something rich and fatty, but will be best after 2020.
title: Vinum Cellars 2013 The Scrapper Cabernet Franc (El Dorado)

description: Smooth and delicious, this opens with inviting scents of raspberry jam, cake spice, violet and a whiff of forest floor. The savory, juicy palate doles out ripe Marasca cherry, truffle and star anise framed in polished tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Volpaia 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Lots of spicy oak aromas and vivid, ripe, concentrated fruit animate this medium- to full-bodied wine that nevertheless has great acidity and firm tannins to support all that showy flavor. It smells like cinnamon and cherry tart, and tastes full of black cherry, sour cherry and cranberry. Best after 2021.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Burt Williams' Morning Dew Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: Earthy foresty aromas of cool damp earth and olive are as prominent as this blend's berry scents. A ripe smooth saturated palate reflects a warm vintage, while this deals herbal notes of tea and pepper along with berry and plum fruit. Mint and spice flavors play leading roles on a savory well-oaked finish. Drink through 2021.
title: Vik 2012 Red (Cachapoal Valley)

description: This kitchen-sink blend, which includes nine different varieties, is superbly pretty right from the start, with tremendous floral scents that flood the bouquet—honeysuckle, jasmine, apple blossom and fresh fynbos. Supporting fruit flavors of pink apple and white melon rind ground the clean and direct palate. Vibrant and pure, it boasts stony minerality and notes of freshly dried grass that prevail on the finish. It's enticing now, but it should also continue to mature well through 2022.
title: A.A. Badenhorst Family Wines 2014 White (Coastal Region)

description: Intense aromas of fragrant purple flower, crushed berry, menthol and cake spice steal the spotlight while the tight, savory palate doles out juicy black cherry, crushed raspberry, licorice and tobacco. Taut, fine-grained tannins provide the framework. Drink through 2023.
title: Badia a Coltibuono 2015  Chianti Classico

description: Earthy aromas of forest floor, grilled herb and a balsamic note come together in the glass. The firm, tightly wound palate shows dried cherry, pomegranate, clove and cinnamon while taut, fine-grained tannins provide support. It's vibrant, with bright acidity. Drink after 2023.
title: Batasiolo 2013 Brunate  (Barolo)

description: Quietly floral, this wine has small percentages of Syrah and Alicante Bouschet. Red licorice and a thread of white pepper play along a thick big-boned entry that softens into a silky wonder as it develops. It highlights the savory aspects of this beloved Rhône variety.
title: Benovia 2014 Grenache (Sonoma)

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 55% Grenache, 25% Syrah, 16% Mourvèdre and 4% Cinsaut starts of with a reserved nose that slowly offers aromas of roasted cranberries, tangy black plum, dried lilacs and crushed asphalt. The palate shows a dried fruit elegance, with blackberry leather, loamy soils, minty herbs and roasted meats.
title: Bonny Doon 2013 Le Cigare Volant Reserve Red (Central Coast)

Appellation: Costières de Nîmes, Rhône Valley, France
description: Violet, bramble and thyme lend depth to deep black-cherry and plum flavors in this lavish Carignan-dominant red. Made from vines averaging 80 years in age, it's richly concentrated and supple. Fresh acidity, firm tannins and hints of earth and spice extend an elegant but bold finish.
title: Château d'Or et de Gueules 2015 Qu'es aQuo Carignan (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Rippling, ripe fruit counteracts deep mineral tones in this bold, brooding wine. Black plum and berry notes are spiced with anise and vanilla, but also shades of dried sage, thyme and earth. It's a concentrated red with a slightly drying, emery-board finish.
title: Château Maucoil 2015 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: From the granite and schist soil of the most famous of the named sectors of Morgon, this wine has power and density. It also has a perfumed character both from the light wood aging and from the juicy red fruits. Tannins sustain the fruit and promise aging. Drink the wine from 2019.
title: Domaine Gaget 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: An earthy note tones down the lemon brightness on the nose of this wine. On the palate, the roles reverse: lemon brightness stirs up earthy hints of yeast, miso and soy. That citrusy freshness carries it through to a lively, dry and lip-smacking finish.
title: Eder 2016 Federspiel Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

description: Aromas of new leather, forest floor, pressed rose and an earthy whiff of truffle segue over to the juicy palate along with ripe Marasca cherry and black raspberry. Firm, velvety tannins provide the backbone while a hint of licorice backs up the finish. Drink 2020–2027.
title: Elio Filippino 2012 del Comune di Castiglione Falletto  (Barolo)

description: Notes of red and black cherries have a touch of tar and earth on the nose. On the palate the fruit is fresh and crisp, ripe but still wonderfully crunchy, just like a fine web of tannins that crunch like starched tulle. There is ample freshness, good concentration and a finely struck balance. Highly appetizing, neither light nor heavy, just right.
title: Esterházy 2015 Blaufränkisch (Leithaberg)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Cranberry, hibiscus and rosewater converge on the soft, plush nose of this bottling, which also shows hints of star anise and baked goods. It's a light and playful wine on the sip, where juicy raspberries meet with gravelly minerality. This is light, fresh and full of long, simmering acidity.
title: Foley 2015 Rancho Santa Rosa Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Underbrush, truffle, iris, baking spice and aromatic herb aromas all come seamlessly together on this alluring wine. The tense, savory palate delivers ripe black cherry, red berry, star anise and ground clove. Tightly knit, fine-grained tannins and bright acidity offer balance and support. Drink 2020–2029.
title: Fontanabianca 2014 Bordini  (Barbaresco)

description: This is brooding on the nose with the allure of an elderberry aroma. The same note dominates the palate which has a smooth, sensuous body, still held tight by brisk, fine tannin and lively acidity. There is almost stealthy, surprising grip. With its ample fruit this is already enticing, but structure and freshness promise more pleasure to come. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Heinrich Hartl 2014 Reserve Pinot Noir (Thermenregion)

description: A two-acre parcel of 84-year-old vines forms a great starting point for this intense wine. With considerable acidity as well as dark tannins, the black-currant fruit is still firmly embedded in the wine's dark core. So wait, and drink it from 2019.
title: Jean-Marc Burgaud 2016 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: A blend of 43% Grenache, 24% Mourvédre, 20% Syrah, and 13% Roussanne, the aromas show a lot of subtlety, with notes of raspberry, garrigue, stem, pepper and moist earth. The palate brings a compelling sense of texture that is absolutely swoon-worthy, running just a touch warm.
title: K Vintners 2014 Charlotte Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: On first pour, this wine is not ready to reveal its charms, with locked-up notes of plum, herb, coffee, licorice, smoke, flower and spice. The fruit flavors are rich yet sophisticated, gliding across the palate to a lingering finish.
title: K Vintners 2014 Phil Lane Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Baked red currants and cherries are lifted by vanilla, dewy sagebrush and hint of peppercorns on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate is bright and energized with tart red fruits, but it's approachably rich and spiced with jasmine and Earl Grey tea flavors.
title: Ken Brown 2014 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Berry, plum, cola and mineral aromas amount to an inviting whole. A solid yet racy palate delivers punches and jabs of herbal blackberry, plum and spice flavors. On the finish, this blend of Syrah, Carmenère, Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon is generous with oak, coffee and herbal fruit flavors. Drink through 2022.
title: Lapostolle 2014 Red (Rapel Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Mourvèdre and Syrah each make up 40% of this blend, with the balance Grenache. The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of garrigue, black pepper and blackberry. The palate is dense and ripe, pulling no punches but delivering a whole lot of hedonistic appeal. The value can't be beat.
title: Les Trouvés 2015 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A fresh, tropical whiff of passion fruit clings to the pear notes on the nose of this wine. The palate, on the other hand, is far more savory, with its textured, yeasty mouthfeel and sage and celery salt fragrance. It's soft but full-flavored.
title: Leth 2016 Ried Scheiben Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: Gritty in oak, this coastal appellation wine is bright and herbal, offering crisp, vibrant overtones of cranberry, pomegranate, strawberry and rose. A streak of fresh mint and lavender adds floral elements that contribute to its lovely profile of delicate elegance, supported by mellowed oak.
title: Merry Edwards 2015 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Blended with 14% Cabernet Sauvignon, then aged 18 months in 64% new French oak, this is a lovely example of the variety: velvety, with smoky undertones of oak. Milk chocolate, raspberry and wild truffle combine well on the medium-bodied palate, the finish bright and focused in acidity.
title: Mt. Brave 2014 Cabernet Franc (Mount Veeder)

description: This wine's wonderfully pithy, pear-fruited nose hints at once at freshness and depth. The palate honors this with pristine, ripe pear notes and lovely, fragrant lemony depth. Everthing about this brims with pear and Mandarin fruit, concluding in a refreshing citrusy finish.
title: Nigl 2016 Ried Pellingen Privat Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Jammy blackberry, prune, tobacco and oak-grain aromas lead to a flush, chewy and balanced palate, with just enough natural acidity to make things nice. Blackberry and toasty oak flavors include notes of mocha and exotic spices, while this is rich, dense and balanced across a full-bodied finish. Drink through 2021.
title: Oveja Negra 2014 The Lost Barrel Red (Maule Valley)

description: Subtle, nuanced aromas of freshly dried grass, yellow flower and beeswax mingle with notes of honeydew rind, apple skin and Asian pear on the nose of this delicate and superbly balanced wine. The palate boasts a bit more forward-fruit and concentration, with loads of baked apple, honeycomb and toasted nut flavors. A salted butter character and a hint of savory umami unfold on the enduring finish, lending lift and intrigue. Drink now–2023.
title: Paserene 2015 Chardonnay (Elgin)

description: A tiny production wine from a backbone site, used in many of the producer's other wines, this is brash in coconut and black currant, the tannins grainy and firm, given additional heft by the ripeness and generous use of oak. Velvety smooth, it's densely packed in black pepper and clove.
title: Oakville East 2014 Harter Family Double H Ranch Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: Mint and violet notes accent a dense black-cherry core in this full-bodied, juicy wine. Light touches of baking spices and dried herbs add complexity and play into a layering effect on the palate.
title: Paris Valley Road 2014 Merlot (California)

description: While this wine is tough and closed up at this stage, it has good potential. The weight shows it has plenty of ripe fruit while wood aging has added spice. The wine needs time, so drink from 2019.
title: Patrick Tranchand 2015 Roche Grès  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Aromas of wild berry, tilled soil, new leather, camphor and a whiff of violet take center stage on the nose. Bright and succulent, the vibrant, graceful palate doles out juicy red cherry, crushed raspberry and baking spice. Taut, refined tannins and racy acidity lend balance and tension. Fans of elegance will love its ethereal finesse. Drink 2019–2026.
title: Prunotto 2014  Barbaresco

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: With a waxy nose of peach and apricot—both dried and fresh—this is a dry, light-bodied wine that dances on the palate in moderately high acidity. Fermented entirely in stainless steel, it remains softly textured and vibrant from start to finish.
title: Ron Rubin 2016 Pinot Gris (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Livermore Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a beautifully balanced wine with medium body and nicely firm tannins supporting fairly rich black fruits and herbal accents. It is sleek and agile rather than big and rich, and its appetizing texture should make it really versatile at lunch or dinner.
title: Steven Kent 2014 BDX Collection Cabernet Franc (Livermore Valley)

Appellation: Santa Maria Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Earthy aromas of pepper spice, roasted meat crust and red cherry sauce show on the nose of this bottling from a new property in Ojai. The flavors are clean yet pleasantly rustic, full of cooked herbs, dust and rounded cranberry. It's light in body, soft in texture and easy to drink.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2015 Bien Nacido Vineyard Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: While this displays dark aromas of burnt oak, road tar and blackberry, the palate is not heavy or overly hard driving. Plum and berry flavors are loaded with wood spice and nutmeg accents, while this tastes chocolaty on a bold finish. Drink this exemplary Carignan through 2025.
title: Undurraga 2014 T.H. Terroir Hunter Carignan (Maule Valley)

description: Menthol, mature black-skinned fruit, clove, pressed violet and balsamic aromas take shape in the glass. The linear, elegantly structured palate is youthfully austere, offering tart red cherry, cranberry and licorice alongside assertive, fine-grained tannins and firm acidity. Give this time to unwind and fully develop. Drink 2020–2033.
title: Vignamaggio 2013 Mona Lisa Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: This blend of 55% Grenache Blanc and 45% Viognier is one of the zippiest white wines you can find. It's restrained on the nose with honeydew, yellow grapefruit peel and wet slate aromas, and then the wound-up acidic power explodes on the sip, where tart apple and pear flavors linger behind the sizzle.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2016 Force de Fleurs White (Paso Robles)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Attractive and focused raspberry and black-cherry flavors fill out this medium-bodied wine. The aromas are fruity, with touches of clove and cinnamon, while the flavors are clean, pure and delicious. The wine has just enough tannin to firm up the texture, along with good acidity for balance. Drink now or hold through 2022.
title: Bella Grace 2015 Old Vine Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: Licorice, olive, cherry cough drop and cassis aromas lay the framework for a flush chewy wine that includes descending amounts of Cabernet Sauvignon, Malbec, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot. Toasty flavors of blackberry, coffee and mocha are peppery, spicy and dry on a solid finish. Drink through 2022.
title: Laura Hartwig 2014 Edición de Familia Red (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is thick and sinewy, ripe and soft in texture. Spicy clove, pepper and cinnamon highlight a richness of strawberry puree and mocha chocolate flavor, the oak subdued in the background.
title: Bailarin 2015 Love Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a very young but already fully enjoyable twist-top bottling from a unique site in the rugged mountains north of the Santa Maria Valley. Fresh aromas of blackberry and black currant meet with rosemary and tarragon on the nose while the easy-to-quaff palate shows flavors of freshly plucked boysenberries, oregano and marjoram. The high alcohol is completely hidden.
title: Brander 2016 Colson Canyon Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: Aromas of French oak, exotic spice and black-skinned berry take center stage. Fresh and linear, the palate offers cassis, cranberry and crushed raspberry framed in fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Drink through 2023.
title: Casa Emma 2013 Vignalaparco  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Swirls of orange blossom and vanilla introduce this richly textured Roussanne. Soft, honeyed flavors of tangerine and pear are broad and expansive on the palate, accented by hints of earth and sunny lemon acidity. Drink now through 2021.
title: Château du Trignon 2016 Roussanne (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Leathery berry aromas are dense and earthy, while not showing any of the green character that sometimes plagues Carmenère. A flush mouthfeel is solid and fruity, with berry and plum flavors that are roasted and meaty. Savory notes on the finish suggest a pairing with barbecue.
title: Concha y Toro 2015 Marques de Casa Concha Carmenère (Peumo)

description: A bright bouquet of red currants, strawberries, back pepper, olives and medicinal bitters show this Pinot to be approachable when young, particularly by Martinborough standards. The palate is silky, savory, earthy and red-fruited, finishing salty yet refreshing.
title: Discovery Point 2014 Pinot Noir (Martinborough)

description: This ripe, soft wine is rich with red-cherry fruit and a generous texture from old vines. Spice, fruitiness and a hint of tannins contribute to a wine that is delicious and rounded, ready to drink now.
title: Domaine Baron de l'Ecluse 2016 Vieilles Vignes  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: A perfumed wine, this is rich in tannins and generous, ripe black fruits. Aged nine months in wood, it has a hint of caramel as well as spice that adds extra dimension. It is juicy, ripe and needs to age further. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Bel-Air 2016 Les Burdelines  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Densely tannic, this rich wine is dark and structured. The black fruits have great weight and a generous nature. The acidity, typical of the vintage, indicates the wine needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de la Bouronière 2016  Fleurie

Winery: Domaine de la Combe au Loup
description: The granite soil of Morgon is very much in evidence in this firm, mineral-textured wine. With dark tannins and the potential of rich fruit, the wine is structured and needs time to mature further. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de la Combe au Loup 2016  Morgon

description: This ripe, succulent wine, from the producer's home vineyard, is softly textured and richly fruity. Dense texture gives it a full-bodied feel as well a dry edge. This wine needs to age further. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de Roche Guillon 2016  Fleurie

description: The richness of Grenache is offset elegantly with Syrah's edgy, peppery finesse in this elegant blend. Black cherry and strawberry preserves are plump and ripe but balanced neatly with zesty acidity and bristling undertones of savory herb and chalky tannins.
title: Domaine de Verquiere 2015 Red (Rasteau)

description: Lancié is one of the villages of Beaujolais-Villages, next door to Morgon and sometimes seeing itself as a Beaujolais cru in waiting. This wine certainly has structure and aging potential along with plenty of red fruits and acidity. These elements suggest that the wine will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine du Penlois 2016 Lancié  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: Aged in large wooden 158- and 800-gallon barrels, this wine is rich with spice and toast flavors over the soft tannins. It has great black-fruit flavors, acidity to balance and a ripe aftertaste. The wine is smooth and can be enjoyed now but will be better from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Marc Delienne 2016 Greta Carbo  (Fleurie)

description: Underbrush, new leather, game, pipe tobacco and menthol aromas lift out of the glass. The full-bodied palate offers dried black cherry, licorice, a hint of mint and the same gamy note as the nose alongside assertive, close-grained tannins. Drink through 2022.
title: Elio Filippino 2012 San Cristoforo  (Barbaresco)

description: Aromas of dark culinary spice, mature berry and a whiff of new leather emerge in the glass. The tightly wound, tense palate offers wild cherry, licorice and clove framed in taut, fine-grained tannins. Give this another year or two at least to fully develop. Drink 2019–2025.
title: Felsina 2015 Berardenga Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This is a surprisingly bright and herbal Pinotage, with an intense fynbos streak to the bouquet and a distinct, almost mentholated character that lifts the ripe plum, cherry and berry fruit. Framing tannins lend structure to the medium-weight palate, while notes of lightly charred black fruit and espresso unfold on the spicy close.
title: Fram 2015 Pinotage (Citrusdal Mountain)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: A shy nose belies a simplicity of green apple and fennel in this full-bodied fleshy wine that satisfies in freshness and complexity. Toasted oak marks the lengthy finish.
title: Fritz 2015 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: A slight reduction on the nose gives way to a brawny full-bodied expression of leather, black pepper and meat—the fruit elusive at first. Soft and sinewy, it shines on the finish in more discreet elements of cassis and red currant.
title: Hoopes 2014 Maya's Block Merlot (Howell Mountain)

description: Given the appellation (named after a Roman soldier), there has to be a wine called after great lovers in history. This wine with its good structure will indeed be lovely, or certainly deliciously fruity. Hints of black cherries and acidity suggest it will be ready from late 2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Les Grandes Amours  (Saint-Amour)

description: Notes of pressed apple, nuts and candlewax lend a rustic feel to this full-bodied white blend. While broad and savory, there's an enticing slick in texture as well as zesty hints of lemon and orange peel that linger brightly on the finish.
title: La Ferme du Mont 2014 Vendage White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This wine has a crispness on entry that develops a fleshy texture and medium-bodied weight on the midpalate. Subdued notions of apple, pear and pineapple ply a vibrant layering of stony acidity, with a finish of richer honeycomb.
title: La Pitchoune 2014 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: This wine's subdued nose hints at lemon, pear and yeast. The palate puts those three elements in an almost fizzing but concentrated package, where lemon and green pear notes enwrap a yeasty core. Citrusy freshness is the lasting impression.
title: Laurenz V. 2016 Friendly Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: Pristine black plum and cherry notes are edged with fresh herbs and lavender on the nose of this pretty red. Yielding acidity lends a soft, plump feel on the palate but it's sumptous and supple, finishing on fine-grained, persistent tannins.
title: Les Collines 2014 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

Appellation: Santa Clara Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Tightly wound aromas of crisp Asian pear, pomelo pith and talcum show on the refreshing nose of this bottling. The palate is extremely grippy, fresh and texturally compelling, with racy flavors of grapefruit pith, Key lime rind and a hint of melon.
title: Lion Ranch 2016 Viognier (Santa Clara Valley)

description: The sector known as Combiaty lies in the southern part of the Brouilly appellation. This wine made from 50-year-old vines is soft and ripe in the classic style of Brouilly. Crisp acidity gives a red-cherry-flavored lift. Drink this wine now.
title: Manoir du Carra 2016 Terre de Combiaty  (Brouilly)

description: Delicate whiffs of apple, pear and lemon gain concentration and strength on the palate of this richly composed white. It's a languid, supple wine, almost creamy in mouthfeel, but balanced keenly by delicate acidity and a refreshingly salty, smoked-nut finish.
title: Marrenon 2016 Grand Marrenon White (Luberon)

description: Roasted gamy aromas are spicy and common for cool-climate Syrah, which makes up 60% of this wine along with Malbec and Cabernet Franc. A full and balanced palate is where it needs to be, while gamy spicy black-fruit flavors are mildly herbal and finish with noticeable oak.
title: Matetic 2013 Corralillo Winemaker's Red (San Antonio)

description: Herbal berry aromas are crisp and toasty. In the mouth, this blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Carmenère is jammy and compact. Cherry, blackberry and chocolate flavors are just deep and complex enough to overcome a finish that breaks up quickly.
title: MontGras 2014 Antu Cabernet Sauvignon-Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine smells just like blackberry jam, and tastes wonderfully rich, fruity and full. It has a very deep purple-black color, expressive dark fruit aromas and ripe, almost-sweet, flavors that spread across the palate and linger on the finish.
title: Naggiar 2016 Estate Grown Malbec (Sierra Foothills)

Designation: Seven Sinners The Ransom Old Vines
description: Very seductive and spicy, this full-bodied wine has enticing aromas of baking spices and toasted oak, with lavish flavors of chocolate and blackberry jam that seem to coat the tongue. The ripe feel seems to soften the thick layer of tannin, so it's approachable now.
title: Nine North Wine Company 2015 Seven Sinners The Ransom Old Vines Petite Sirah (California)

description: Currently, notes of mocha and chocolate-covered cherry lead the nose of this wine, but there's earthy nuance and restrained red-fruit notes underneath that suggest this wine could benefit from a bit more time for full expression. The structured tannins provide a crushed-velvet texture that retains good grip through the close, with cherry- and plum-skin flavors that carry on through the lingering spicy finish. Try in late 2018 and through 2022.
title: Paul Cluver 2015 Estate Pinot Noir (Elgin)

Designation: Le Pas de L'Aigle Terroir d'Exception
description: Voluptuous and warming, this full-bodied red offers lavish heaps of jammy blackberry and raspberry flavors. It's a bold, intensely juicy wine, nuanced with hints of cocoa, cinnamon and toast. Fine, feathery tannins cushion the finish.
title: Pierre Amadieu 2013 Le Pas de L'Aigle Terroir d'Exception  (Gigondas)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this has classic varietal aromas of red cherry, leather and a touch of violet. The savory palate offers ripe raspberry, licorice and clove while polished tannins and fresh acidity provide balance and structure. Enjoy through 2025.
title: Poggio al Sole 2014 Casasilia Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, underbrush and grilled herb while the soft, round palate offers up fleshy black cherry, licorice and a hint of star anise. Supple tannins and fresh acidity provide support. Drink through 2023.
title: Poggio Torselli 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Spring Mountain District, Napa, California, US
description: This is a thick, herbal red wine that's tannic in bittersweet tobacco, tomato leaf, black pepper and gunpowder elements, rounded by ripe rich blackberry fruit.
title: Raymond 2014 District Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Spring Mountain District)

description: Ripe yet muted black-cherry and plum notes are cloaked in layers of smoked game, earth and forest floor in this intensely savory red. Medium to full in body, it's sumptuous and rich in mouthfeel yet upright and elegantly composed. Drink now through 2025.
title: Secret des Princes 2013 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Incredibly pure, pristine aromas of lime zest, crushed stone and white blossoms waft from the glass. The palate is light bodied but boasts firm lime-driven flavors around tart apricot and mineral tones. A pillowy, marshmallow finish lingers on the tongue.
title: Sheldrake Point 2016 Wild Ferment Dry Estate Bottled Riesling (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
Designation: Cs Vineyard Collection Powerline Estate Vineyard
description: This is 100% varietal, coming from the winery's young estate vineyard. The aromas are bright, with notes of dried herb, cherry liqueur, anise, black tea and whole bell pepper, seeming ever so slightly dried out. The flavors are plump but still light on their feet, supported by surprisingly soft tannins for the variety.
title: Substance 2014 Cs Vineyard Collection Powerline Estate Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Red and yellow apple aromas meld with smoke and slate in this Riesling made by Roman Roth. There's impeccable clarity to the palate in its tart peach, lime zest and additional crisp-apple notes. Concentrated, balanced and delightful to sip, flavors of smoke, slate and citrus linger on the finish.
title: The Grapes of Roth 2016 Virgin Berry Dry Riesling (Long Island)

description: This wine is both herbaceous and densely thick in chocolate and raspberry liqueur richness, concentrated in flavor and soft in integrated tannin. The oak presents as somewhat charred and toasted, but plays well ultimately with others.
title: Titus 2014 Cabernet Franc (Napa Valley)

description: This is a taut sinewy wine, generous in oak, with a bite of powerful ripeness. Cherry cola, vanilla and biscuit add richness to the flavor profile, with streaks of black pepper and cinnamon chiming in.
title: Titus 2014 Zinfandel (Napa Valley)

description: A blend of 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this presents with aromas of underbrush, black-skinned berry and forest floor. The juicy palate doles out black cherry, clove, tobacco and a touch of cedar alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2023.
title: Valiano 2013 6.38 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: A very earthy tone recalling bruised apple and yeast dominates the nose of this wine. The creamy, soft and rounded palate combines these fruity and savory elements. Citrus pithiness lends texture to the warming finish.
title: Weixelbaum 2015 Anno Dazumal Grüner Veltliner (Weinland Österreich)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This full-bodied wine has more concentration than average, adding accents of black pepper and smoke to the varietal's famously fruity flavors. The texture is nicely firm, having support from moderate tannins, so it invites a pairing with substantial proteins.
title: Wilderotter 2014 Primitivo (Amador County)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This is made from a field-blended vineyard planted in 1995. Ripe, juicy and dusty, it's a well-integrated wine that holds its high level of succulence and concentration well, showing a sweetness of vanilla oak that's nicely offset by black pepper and leather accents.
title: Wine Guerrilla 2015 Conte Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

description: This wine's yeasty nose tinged with celery salt and lemon zestiness is very appetizing. The palate continues with this very attractive fruity-savory combination, adding a lovely creaminess against which the tangy herbal and lemon notes shine.
title: Winzer Krems 2016 Edition Chremisa Sandgrube 13 Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Hints of smoke and licorice lend spice to this unctuous, slightly warming red. Black-plum and blueberry flavors are fleshy and ripe but offset by ripe, rigid tannins and fresh acidity.
title: Alain Jaume et Fils 2015 Les Valats Red (Rasteau)

description: Heady scents of pressed violet, tilled soil, perfumed berry, new leather and a balsamic note of camphor flow out of the glass. Vibrant and linear, the medium-bodied palate evokes juicy Marasca cherry, ripe raspberry, licorice and crushed mint framed in taut, refined tannins. Bright acidity lends balance. Drink through 2026.
title: Fontanabianca 2014  Barbaresco

Designation: Hacienda Araucano Clos de Lolol Single Vineyard
description: Cool black-fruit aromas are solid, a bit oaky and show nothing that's unnecessary. A fully extracted palate with moderately hard tannins is where you'll find herbal black-fruit flavors along with tobacco and spice notes. A dry finish is firm and peppery. Drink through 2023.
title: François Lurton 2014 Hacienda Araucano Clos de Lolol Single Vineyard Red (Lolol Valley)

description: Among the winery's larger offerings, this wine doesn't hold back on quality, offering tight-grained tannin expression around rounded flavors of plum compote, blackberry bramble, cedar and clove. Bright, focused acidity tempers the ripeness and underlying richness, making for a welcome drinking experience.
title: Hall 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This blends 65% Cabernet Sauvignon with 35% Merlot, the two playing well against each other to provide a broad soft expanse of decadent flavor. Cherry, plum, vanilla and well-integrated oak make it round and opulent, with a fleshiness that persists through a lengthy finish.
title: Hall 2014 Craig's Red (Napa Valley)

description: Chocolate mocha caramel flavors contribute a deep sense of richness in they approachable, well-rounded wine, a testament to the appellation's dusty nature and embrace of black cherry.
title: Heritance 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: Friendly ripe yellow pear notes perfume the nose of this wine. That generous fruitiness continues on the rounded, pear-driven and juicy palate. The body is cushioned with all of this fruit but remains streamlined and bright thanks to vivid lemon notes.
title: Hermann Moser 2016 Ried Gebling Reserve HannaH Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

Appellation: Pine Mountain-Cloverdale Peak, Sonoma, California, US
description: This wine is pretty in honeysuckle and honeycomb aromas, featuring beautifully resolved acidity in a dry, welcoming context. Spicy lemon is bracing before it mellows, allowing room for green apple and pear.
title: Imagery 2016 Upper Ridge Estate Riesling (Pine Mountain-Cloverdale Peak)

description: This dramatic and inky-colored wine has tight aromas of pencil lead, blackberry and mint followed by extremely concentrated flavors like berries, black cherries and black pepper, but with lighter floral accents, too. The structure is huge due to very firm tannins and robust alcohol, so the wine needs time to mellow. Best after 2022.
title: Jessie's Grove 2013 Petite Sirah (Lodi)

description: The nose of this wine just hints at lemon, but the palate is brimming with fruit. Its lemon sherbet and yellow plum notes are boosted by a dollop of residual sweetness and countered by a zesty freshness that helps it finish dry. A lovely, fruity and very refreshing wine.
title: Josef Bauer 2016 Ried Losling Reserve Riesling (Wagram)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very fresh and crisp on the sip, this bottling shows lively lime flavors, steely minerality, citrus blossoms and pomelo pith, and is reminiscent of a crisp summer morning. It's tightly wound on the palate, where vibrant lime and orange-peel flavors hang from a chalky texture, with Asian pear, Meyer lemon, tangerine and petrol toward the finish.
title: Lafond 2015 SRH Riesling (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Firm tannins mark this wine out for a good future. Its structure and generous fruits are slowly developing, promising richness as it matures. A dense wine packed with both fruit and tannins, it will be ready from 2019.
title: Laurent Gauthier 2016 Grand Cras Vieilles Vignes  (Morgon)

description: Candied orange peel and dried apricot are accented by heady notes of vanilla, cream and spice in this opulent wine. Three years after bottling, it reveals spicy potpourri notes of maturation, but plenty of fresh tangerine and peach remain. Rich and rounded, it's a lavish wine with a long, slightly warming finish.
title: Lavau 2014  Condrieu

description: A tropical suggestion of passion fruit plays with the pear notes on the nose of this zesty Viennese field blend. Freshness and texture are key, imbuing the slender body with zesty, aromatic tangerine-laden depth This is enlivening, aromatic and bold, with a refreshing dry finish.
title: Mayer am Pfarrplatz 2016 Nussberg White (Wiener Gemischter Satz)

description: Sourced primarily from Stagecoach Vineyard on Atlas Peak and blended with a small amount of Oakville Vineyard grapes, this is a blend of 57% Cabernet Sauvignon and 22% Cabernet Franc, with small percentages of Merlot, Petit Verdot and Malbec. Big, tannic and overpowering in its youth, it reveals hints of cedar, pencil shavings and sage, with a rich black-cherry core. Enjoy 2023–2033.
title: Miner 2013 The Oracle Red (Napa Valley)

description: This wine comes from the grand estate of Pierreux with its handsome château. Rich and fruity, it has a spicy, vanilla character from aging in wooden vats. With its ripe black-plum and damson flavors, the wine is smooth and generous, ready to drink now.
title: Mommessin 2016 Château de Pierreux  (Brouilly)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This medium-bodied wine has an appetizing lip-smacking texture and a rather taut balance that could take on a wide variety of food pairings. It's all fresh berry and cherry flavors, snappy acidity and firm tannins.
title: Naggiar 2015 Estate Grown Grenache (Sierra Foothills)

description: A blend of 95% Sangiovese and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, this opens with aromas of iris, red plum and baking spice. The juicy, fragrant palate doles out ripe black cherry, white pepper, star anise and tobacco while fine-grained, polished tannins provide support. Enjoy through 2028.
title: Panzanello 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: If you want to try noninterventional wine from Itata, Rogue Vine is a good place to start. This blend of Malbec, Carignan and Syrah opens with leathery blueberry aromas graced by mocha. A smacking palate is tannic and firm but not mean. Intense cassis, tobacco and spice flavors finish flush and saturated.
title: Rogue Vine 2014 Super Itata Red (Itata Valley)

description: The scent of freshly cut Conference pear peels combines with crisp, green apple notes in this wine. The palate shows the characteristic, yeastiness that shimmers with saltiness, but juicy pear fruit plays the starring role. The finish is totally moreish.
title: Steininger 2016 Ried Kittmannsberg Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from one of the state's top vineyards, this wine is a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon (78%), Cabernet Franc (10%), 7% Petit Verdot and Merlot. The aromas are tightly wound on first pour, with pleasing notes of red and black fruit, along with plum, mocha, herb, flower and spice. The flavors are pitch black, soft and silky in feel with highly structured, ever so slightly dry tannins that smooth out with some time open. This one needs additional time. Best after 2021.
title: Tamarack Cellars 2013 Sagemoor Vineyard Reserve Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, new leather and eucalyptus. The concentrated palate offers ripe black cherry, blackberry jam, tobacco and licorice alongside brooding tannins. Drink 2018–2027.
title: Tenuta di Lilliano 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This is big yet approachable wine with many years ahead of it. There's mocha, black cherry, soy sauce and violet tones wrapped in structured, mouth-filling tannins, plenty of acidity and an umami finish. Drink now–2032.
title: Trinity Hill 2014 Homage Syrah (Hawke's Bay)

description: A dark color and leathery, spicy, musky aromas lead to a medium to full palate with boldness and balance. Spiced cherry and black-plum flavors are lasting on a finish in which moderately complex fruit and spice flavors fade away with grace.
title: Undurraga 2014 T.H.Terroir Hunter Pinot Noir (Leyda Valley)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Very good concentration and a sleek texture make this medium to full-bodied wine from the Sierra Foothills easy to appreciate. It has layers of ripe black fruits, nuances of toasted oak and cedar, and fine-grained tannins that provide good backbone.
title: Uphill Vineyards 2013 Estate Grown Tempranillo (Amador County)

description: Rawness and aromas of tree bark, tomato and spice form the greeting on this Limarí Syrah that spanks the palate with scratchy gravelly tannins and acidity. Flavors of red plum and currant are earthy, while some oak character is found on a rugged finish. Drink through 2024.
title: Valdivieso 2013 Caballo Loco Grand Cru Limarí Syrah (Limarí Valley)

description: Beaujolais holds many old vineyards, but this one sets a record at 114 years. This wine is firm and concentrated, with wood aging giving a minty character as well as a dry core, . Low-yielding vines bring richness and a solid texture that will repay another few months aging. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Yohan Lardy 2015 Vieilles Vignes de 1903  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: All coming from Hedges Estate Vineyard and aged in 100% new French oak, this wine has had a full five years to develop and it's made the best of it. Aromas of spice box, dark fruit, vanilla and high-toned herb are followed by luscious black-fruit flavors that stretch out on the finish. It's unabashed in its appeal with a lot of life in front of it.
title: Barons 2012 Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The fruit for this wine all comes from Hedges Estate Vineyard. Brooding aromas of soil, dark fruit, cigar, herb and spice box lead to palate-coating dark-fruit flavors—well balanced fruit flavors. Firm, lightly chalky tannins provide support. Give it some time in the cellar to develop. Best after 2020.
title: Barons 2013 Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

description: The aromas bring notes of cigar box, mortared herbs and cherry. The palate is tart, fresh and acid driven, with well-balanced fruit flavors that stretch out on the finish. It should still have a good life in front of it.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)

description: An aroma like white pepper and damp earth leads to brawny fruit flavors in this full-bodied, concentrated and complex wine made from a grape variety genetically identical to Zinfandel, but often with slightly different flavors. It has a good firm texture, attractive balance for a wine of its size, and a lingering finish.
title: Brutocao 2013 Contento Vineyard Estate Bottled Primitivo (Mendocino)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: A mix of five different Pinot relatives (not clones), this seems a bit overworked. It's flat in the mouth, and the mix of grapes, some white, some red, simply blends into a rather generic tasting white wine. If it resembles anything, it's a Pinot Noir Blanc, and it's already drying out, so drink up.
title: Minimus 2016 #22 Pinot White (Willamette Valley)

description: A particularly good value, this appealing and inexpensive wine adds light and tasty oak and spice accents to a good base of plummy flavors. It feels light and fresh on the palate and has enough concentration for a bit of a lingering finish.
title: Obscured 2015 Red (California)

description: Delicate hints of candied red apple and honeydew extend from nose to palate in this easy-going white blend. Off dry and soft in acidity, it's an approachable everyday sip. Drink now.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Madonna Kabinett White (Rheinhessen)

description: The Arinto in this attractive wine gives it a floral character. Bright acidity and citrus fruits add a crisp lift and go with the hint of oak aging. This refreshing wine can be drunk now.
title: Parras Wines 2016 White (Lisboa)

description: This is hugely tannic, nearly impenetrable wine, hard to break through, a mix of pencil lead, tobacco leaf and dried herb that presents as bittersweet and a touch green.
title: Patland 2014 Terra Del Cuore Estate Grown Malbec (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Monterey County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dried black cherries, baked earth, rust and a hint of feral aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate offers an interesting line of tart red fruit along with dried thyme and a meaty touch.
title: Press Run 2014 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)

description: There's a very strong scent of licorice on the nose of this blend of 54% Syrah and 46% Merlot, along with dark-red fruit and myrrh. Eucalyptus oil flavors arise on the palate, giving intrigue to the black cherry, peppercorn and roasted meat touches.
title: Reyes 2014 Julia's Red (California)

description: The nose is extremely shy but the tangy straightforward palate shows white cherry, Gala apple and a hint of almond. It's linear and crisp, with bright acidity a vivacious perlage.
title: Ricci Curbastro NV Rosé Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: A ripe, fruity blend of local Dão grapes, this unoaked wine bursts with blackberry flavors. Acidity and tannins back up the juicy fruitiness of the wine. Drink now.
title: Seacampo 2015 Quinta do Vale Red (Dão)

description: Full bodied and voluptuous, this fleshy red blend offers soft, easy black cherry and berry flavors powdered with cinnamon and cocoa. Soft, yielding tannins amplify its plush, easy style. Drink now.
title: Steam 2016 Reserve Red (Pfalz)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Floral and honey-like aromas lead to very sweet flavors of apricot and peach in this medium-bodied wine. A good choice for friends that only drink sweet, or with your dessert.
title: Terra d'Oro 2016 Moscato (Amador County)

Appellation: Yakima Valley, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine is 50% Cabernet Sauvignon along with 25% Cabernet Franc and 25% Malbec. Slightly elevated volatiles are followed by aromas of cherry, cinnamon, green herb, licorice and baking spice. Flavors are light and elegant in style.
title: Twin Cedars 2014 Bronze Star Red (Yakima Valley)

description: Dusty aromas of white fruits are equaled by barrel-based vanilla scents. A round, easy palate gives off a powdery sense, while this barrel-fermented Viura-Tempranillo blend tastes yeasty and of green banana. Latent acidity on the finish creates mild sourness.
title: Viña Otano 2016 Blanco Fermentado en Barrica White (Rioja)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas of watermelon Jolly Rancher, peach and flower are followed by broad honeysuckle and stone-fruit flavors. It doesn't all come together.
title: Watermill 2015 Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a serviceable red, built to be enjoyed now, approachable in its mix of doughy leather, black cherry and structured tannin.
title: Whitehall Lane 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Snappy aromas of tangerine lead to a chunky palate with thickness as well as cutting acidity. This tastes of dried melon and bitterness, while on the finish it's chunky, with distant flavors of green melon.
title: Altavins 2016 Ilercavònia Garnacha Blanca (Terra Alta)

description: The nose on this 85% Vermentino and 15% Viognier blend reveals delicate aromas of candied citrus and almond. The palate is straightforward, offering white peach and bitter almond notes.
title: Bruni 2016 Plinio White (Maremma Toscana)

description: Raisiny aromas indicate that this Syrah is a bit overripe and lazy. Its big, bulky palate is weighty but unfocused, with a jammy berry flavor profile backed by a plump finish.
title: Cellers Unió 2015 Perlat Syrah (Montsant)

description: Ripe cherry notes on the nose have a conifer edge. The palate has ample freshness but also seems to lack a little focus. This is full-fruited and fresh. Drink soon.
title: Domaine Fernand Engel 2016 Tradition Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: A blend in equal parts of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, this opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, cedar and cooking spice. The lively palate offers cherry jam, tangerine and mocha alongside supple tannins.
title: Fattoria San Felo 2015 Balla La Vecchia  (Maremma Toscana)

description: This wine has a persistent bitter note that's part earthy, part savory and a touch extreme. Rhubarb, rose, black tea and cardamom all figure in, but don't get along all together well.
title: Flowers 2015 Camp Meeting Ridge Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Brandied cherry juice and crushed herbs have a slightly saucy feel in this Rosato from the thin-skinned Bombino Nero. There's nice weight to the palate, which boasts flavors of strawberry jam and additional herbs, alongside soft acidity in the background.
title: Giancarlo Ceci 2016 Parchitello Bombino Nero Rosato (Castel del Monte)

Appellation: Rogue Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Though considerably higher in finished alcohol than the previous vintage, this still carries an impression of underripe fruit. It's tart, thin and tannic, lightly cloaked in baking spices from barrel aging.
title: Grizzly Peak 2014 Cabernet Franc (Rogue Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine comes off as a bit green out of the gate, with aromas of mortared fresh herb along with blueberry, woodspice and vanilla. Flavors are soft and light, concentrated in the middle but missing at the edges. The tannins bring a lightly gritty feel.
title: Maryhill 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A mild nose offers bready aromas. The plump, foamy palate delivers short, briny flavors of generic citrus fruits in front of a clean finish with no complexity. Enjoy this in casual situations, or blended with fruit juice.
title: Maset del Lleó NV Castell de Sant Pau Sparkling (Cava)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Lightly earthy, this offers pleasant berry fruit, though it comes across as more generic than specific to the variety. There's a light touch of toast also, perhaps from the barrel aging. Drink up over the next couple of years.
title: Melrose 2015 Tempranillo (Umpqua Valley)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Dull aromatics open the way to thick dense red fruit, oak and a thickness of tannin in this estate-driven red which drinks heavy on the palate. Cedar and pencil shavings accent it well.
title: Merriam 2013 Windacre Vineyard Estate Merlot (Russian River Valley)

description: Heady aromas of canned peach, kumquat and pollen convey the awaiting playful sweetness. The confected palate shows flavors of ripe yellow peach, candied mandarin and quince. This wine is plush with just enough acidity to keep it from becoming cloying, while flavors of Satsuma and honeydew melon carry through the medium-short finish.
title: Messina Hof NV Angel Late Harvest Reisling Riesling (Texas)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Used leather, underbrush scorched earth and toasted aromas slowly appear on this evolved wine. The mature palate shows prune and the heat of evident alcohol. It's seen better days for sure. Drink sooner rather than later before it dries out completely.
title: Montemercurio 2011 Mssaggero  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Made with 90% Sangiovese and 10% Merlot, this has aromas suggesting moist forest floor, truffle, cassis and a whiff of graphite. The palate is on the lean side, offering suggestions of blackberry, orange rind, game and star anise alongside dusty, rather fleeting tannins and firm acidity.
title: Nottola 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Lifted aromas of lemon and grapefruit combine with toasted nuts, green flower stems and a salty, sea spray quality. The acidity in this wine at the moment is searing and texturally it's a little lean and mean, but this fumé-style Sauvignon should be an interesting one to watch develop in bottle over the next few years.
title: Spy Valley 2015 Envoy Johnson Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from a site in the Rocks District, this wine is lighter in color and brings initially subdued aromas of cassis, herb, medicine cabinet, plum, cherry and spice. The palate is soft in feel, putting a premium on elegance of expression, with the tannins quite supple. It's a very pretty expression of the variety.
title: Substance 2014 Cs Vineyard Collection Jack's Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Listed at a low alcohol level for a hot vintage and vineyard, this is still and 100% varietal. Its black licorice, sugared plum and exotic spice aromas show a penetrating purity. The palate is all about delicacy and elegance, which it brings in abundance. It's a very pretty expression of this variety, maybe too much so for some tastes, without the variety's typical tannic heft. But for those looking for a sophisticated style of Cabernet, look no further.
title: Substance 2014 Cs Vineyard Collection Stoneridge Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this offers aromas of cooking spice, leather and a whiff of violet. The firm palate delivers ripe black cherry, clove and licorice set against closed-grained tannins. Drink through 2025.
title: Terra di Seta 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Black olive, truffle and a nuanced streak of graphite shine in this softly textured, focused and well-made wine. Cedar and dried herb complement quietly in the background, as integrated oak supports without distraction.
title: Trentadue 2014 La Storia Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma County)

description: To the eye, this Cabernet Sauvignon-led blend is light, with some rustiness. An earthy nose is funky and reduced at first, then more clear. A reduced palate with gritty tannins shows flavors of cooked berries, raisin and fig. Those same very ripe flavors persist on a warm finish.
title: Valdivieso 2013 Caballo Loco Grand Cru Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Nice depth and breadth come together in this richly packed, fruity and full-bodied wine. There's a hint of freshly baked bread in the aroma along with ripe plum and blackberry, while an almost jammy richness spreads across the palate and lingers on the finish.
title: XYZin 2014 Old Vine Zinfandel (California)

description: A flinty note of reduction hovers above the ripe, fleshy aromas of red and black cherries. The palate drives home the ripe and abundant cherry fruit on a concentrated, ripe but still fresh palate. The finish has ample fruit but also levity.
title: Zantho 2015 Reserve Zweigelt (Burgenland)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very light in the glass, this twist-top bottling offers tight and bright aromas of white rose petals, citrus peel and cold steel on the nose. The palate is much more exciting, with lime zest, baked nectarine, lemongrass and a hint of marshmallow, all set against a chalky structure.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2015 Zoto Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: High-toned red fruit is complemented by citrus-squeezed vibrancy in this full-bodied red, that presents as fleshy on the palate. A grit of oak adds power to the lengthy spicy finish.
title: David Bruce 2015 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: A blend of 85% Sangiovese, 10% Merlot and 5% Colorino, this has aromas suggesting cassis, leather and a touch of cedar. The juicy palate doles out black cherry, clove and tobacco set against fine-grained tannins. Enjoy through 2025.
title: Aiola 2013 Cancello Rosso Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of black-skinned berry, mint and cooking spice abound on this 90% Sangiovese and 10% Colorino blend. The medium-bodied, juicy palate offers ripe black cherry, crushed raspberry, licorice and clove alongside polished tannins. Enjoy through 2022.
title: Barone Ricasoli 2015 Brolio Bettino  (Chianti Classico)

description: Red-skinned berry, dark spice and balsamic aromas are front and center on this structured red. The firm, savory palate offers dried black cherry, licorice and grilled herb alongside fine-grained tannins. Drink through 2024.
title: Barone Ricasoli 2015 Rocca Guicciarda Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Shenandoah Valley (CA), Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Gripping in texture and nicely saturated with ripe plum and smoked meat flavors, this is a compelling, interesting and complex wine. It shows light oaky, toasted accents on top of vivid red cherry and black-cherry flavors, while firm tannins and good acidity keep it appetizing.
title: Bella Grace 2014 Grenache Noir (Shenandoah Valley (CA))

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Dark but friendly fruit flavors seem to saturate this medium-bodied wine, showing admirable concentration on the palate and good length on the aftertaste. Blueberry, boysenberry and mild spices give it lots to like. The wine is 100% varietal and has a firm texture without obvious tannins.
title: Bella Grace 2015 Estate Zinfandel (Amador County)

Appellation: Ballard Canyon, Central Coast, California, US
Designation: Old Fashioned Superstition Tierra Alta Vineyard
description: A bit lighter in color than the typical Syrah, this wine, which comes in a squat wide bottle, shows strawberry, roasted fennel and a savory smoke quality on the nose. There's a bountiful burst of acidity at the tip of the sip, followed by a spice rack of flavors that give depth to the light, slightly sour red-currant fruit. It's very tangy on the finish.
title: Bevela 2014 Old Fashioned Superstition Tierra Alta Vineyard Syrah (Ballard Canyon)

description: French oak, espresso, toast, coconut, underbrush and spiced plum aromas carry over to the assertive palate along with cranberry, star anise and dried sage. Flexed, close-grained tannins and vibrant acidity provide firm support.
title: Borgo Salcetino 2013 Lucarello Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This wine comes from a number of top sites, including Bacchus, Champoux, Loess and Seven Hills. Pleasing aromas of graphite, spice, fresh herb, whole green pepper and earth lead to light fresh-feeling fruit and savory flavors. It shows a lovely sense of balance. This was a cool, particularly challenging vintage for the state but this wine succeeds on many levels.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2011 Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)

description: Underbrush, cassis and star anise aroma follow over to the firm palate together with dried Morello cherry, espresso, powdered sage and a hint of tobacco. Tightly stretched, close-grained tannins leave an assertive, slightly astringent finish. Give the tannins a few years to unclench then drink up.
title: Cafaggio 2013 San Martino Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Heavy oak makes for a rooty-smelling wine with aromas of clove and Old Spice fragrance. A firm drying palate delivers an intense oaky attack, while this tastes of forest berries, clove and wood spice. A powerful finish ensures that this will last a while. Drink through 2024.
title: Calcu 2014 Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: Mature black-skinned berry, exotic spice, baked plum and menthol aromas lead the nose while the ripe palate offers dried black cherry, prune, clove, toast and a hint of dried sage. Close-grained tannins provide firm support while a blast of orange zest brightens the finish.
title: Castelli del Grevepesa 2012 Castello di Bibbione Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Vibrant blackberry aromas leap from the glass on this bottling, which also offers anise, violet and dried meat aromas. The palate combines smashed black currants with black pepper and violets, wrapped together by serious tannins.
title: Castle Rock 2015 Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: While plush with ripe blueberry and cherry flavors, this full-bodied, warming red has tinges of freshly tilled earth, garrigue and spice. It's not concentrated but easy drinking and fresh. The finish is moderately long, ending in slightly tacky but fine-grained tannins.
title: Château Beauchêne 2014 Cuvée de la Serrière Grenache-Syrah (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: The name of this cuvée refers to the natural yeast used in fermentation. Bottled without added sulfur, the wine is soft with a direct line of juicy acidity. It is a complex, structured wine with some firm tannins. Drink this wine from 2019.
title: Château de Javernand 2016 Indigène  (Chiroubles)

description: The subtle nose of this 100% Sangiovese suggests aromas of tilled earth, baking spice and black-skinned berry. The linear palate offers black cherry, licorice, clove and a hint of ripe raspberry alongside firm, fine-grained tannins. Enjoy though 2025.
title: Colle Bereto 2013 Il Convento Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

Designation: Moulin-à-Vent des Hospices Civils de Romanèche-Thorins
description: This bright purple wine, still very young, is sold to raise funds for a local hospital. It is rich and packed with firm tannins as well as succulent black fruit. This powerful wine needs to age further, so drink from 2019.
title: Collin-Bourisset 2015 Moulin-à-Vent des Hospices Civils de Romanèche-Thorins  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Almost as pale as water in color, this is a fascinating wine. It starts with aromas of sweet peach blossom, candied tangerine rinds, honeysuckle and white rocks. There is a flash of sweetness on the zippy palate, but it quickly dissipates into a floral Asian pear-laced flavor profile. It is a perfect pairing for sushi.
title: Di Bruno 2016 Vermentino (Santa Barbara County)

description: Oak aging has given this otherwise intensely fruity wine extra richness. It adds spice to the red-berry fruits and bright acidity. Concentration from the 65-year-old vines intensifies tannin and enhances the fruitiness of this wine. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Colonat 2016 Les Charmes Tradition  (Morgon)

description: Waxy lemon peels and dusty mineral notes lend a savory feel to this medium-bodied white. Red apple and pear notes are more muted than fresh, but highlighted by fresh lemon acidity and lingering salinity on the finish.
title: Domaine de Nalys 2015 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This is a delicious wine, full of structured fruit. Acidity and bright cherry flavors are given shape and density by the concentration of old vines and the dry core of tannin. Drink this balanced wine now.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016  Côte de Brouilly

description: A dense nose with prune, mint and green-herb aromas is soft, round and loses spunk as it breathes. A wide chunky palate is full but inexact, while this tastes oaky and savory, with spice, berry and tobacco flavors. Heat and chewy tannins propel the finish on this Apalta Carmenère. Drink through 2020.
title: Santa Rita 2013 Pehuén Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

description: There's currently an upfront oaky presence to this wine, with bold notes of toasted vanilla, char and sweet spice that sit atop a brambly, ripe dark-fruit core. Flavors of cherry pie, baked plum and boysenberry abound on the medium-weight palate, framed by structured tannins that result in a texture like crushed velvet. Notes of pepper, game and leather unfold on the evolving finish. Drink now–2022.
title: Spier 2015 Vintage Selection Pinotage (Coastal Region)

description: The nose brims with fruit. Fleshy, almost overripe red cherry sets the tone on the nose that carries through to the palate. The body is juicy but has some grip while the cherry fruit attains crunch, a touch of white pepper spice and more freshness than the nose would suggest. The finish is vivid and moreish.
title: Steindorfer 2015 Apetlon Rouge Red (Burgenland)

description: Sophisticated and full bodied, this generous style of wine wraps complex and subtle oak spices around a warm, generous core of ripe pear and apple notes. Wonderful vanilla bean and nutmeg nuances linger in the creamy texture and echo on the finish.
title: Tangley Oaks 2015 Lot 12 Chardonnay (Mendocino)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very chalky aromas of lime skins, lemon rinds and a touch of Gala apple flesh makes for an inviting nose to this estate bottling. The palate picks up the region's telltale sea salt flavors, which give savory depth to the seared lemon rind flavors. The acidity and texture are both firm.
title: Zotovich Cellars 2015 Estate Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

Designation: The Fuder Single Vineyard Selection Dillons Point
description: More subtle in style compared to Giesen's other single-vineyard offering, Matthews Lane, this wine offers stone fruit, lemon tea, snow pea and wild mint aromas. The opulent palate boasts tongue-tingling acidity and a citrus zing amidst a round texture.
title: Giesen 2014 The Fuder Single Vineyard Selection Dillons Point Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Burly red fruit meets charred oak and a soft supple texture in this medium-bodied wine that's made entirely from estate-grown grapes. A pretty undertone of violet provides floral flourish as white pepper teases on the lengthy, earthy finish.
title: Ampelos 2014 Gamma Ampelos Vineyard Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Substantial fruit flavors and good structure highlight this medium- to full-bodied, concentrated wine. It has a straightforward, no-nonsense approach and offers great value. Enticing black cherry and raspberry flavors are supported by fine-grained tannins and crisp acidity. The fruit notes are pure, clean and linger nicely on the finish.
title: Angeline 2016 Reserve Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)

description: Hailing from one of the coolest vintages the state has ever seen, the aromas bring brooding notes of cherry cola, raspberry, dried leaf, wood and cigar box. The flavors are light, tart and focused, maintaining a sense of freshness. It's a lovely offering from this challenging year.
title: Boudreaux Cellars 2011 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)

Appellation: Contra Costa County, Central Coast, California, US
description: This is a traditional dry, full-bodied and well-balanced Zin that's made to go with a hearty meal. Ripe but not sweet boysenberry flavors are shaded with mineral, wood smoke and light baking spice accents, while firm tannins lend grip to the mouthfeel.
title: Campos Family Vineyards 2015 Estate Zinfandel (Contra Costa County)

description: Truffle, tilled earth, exotic spice and woodland berry aromas emerge in the glass along with a toasted note. It's racy and firmly structured, offering tart red cherry, cranberry, coffee bean and clove set against tightly drawn, close-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Castello d'Albola 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: This estate is the most famous and influential in Côte de Brouilly. This wine, from 50-year-old vines, is rich and structured while also having delicious ripe berry and black-cherry fruits. It is likely to age well over the next few months, being ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Château Thivin 2016  Côte de Brouilly

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Quite dark in the glass, this bottling shows deep blackberry and black currant aromas alongside baked goods and a touch of violets. There is impressive density to the palate, where black plum flavors blend with lavender and baking spice on the finish. The tannins are still quite firm.
title: Clos LaChance 2015 Estate Vineyard Proprietary Red (Central Coast)

description: This wine displays the typical mineral texture associated with this vineyard of granite and schist soils. The texture is given further strength by tannins that suggest the wine needs to age. There is plenty of black fruit as well, so this wine will be best from 2019.
title: Collin-Bourisset 2016 Côte du Py  (Morgon)

description: This Pinotage-based blend, with 15% Shiraz and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, is dark and brooding in the glass. Initial aromas of rich, ripe black berries and cherry sets the stage for the bold, concentrated palate, with notes of char, espresso, licorice root and earthy spice throughout. Medium-plus in weight, with robust and gripping tannins, this firmly structured pour shows great power and length. Drink 2020–2025.
title: David Frost 2015 Par Excellence Cape Blend Red (Western Cape)

description: This is the fourth harvest from owners Virginie and Grégory Barbet and they've made a fine job of it. The wine, with its rich black fruits and concentrated tannins has the structure typical of this appellation. The juicy fruits go along with tannins to give a wine that should age. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de la Pirolette 2016  Saint-Amour

description: This wine from a 2.4-acre vineyard is firm and structured as befits a bottling from this appellation. Rich smoky tannins and fruitiness are balanced and lifted by the juicy red-fruit acidity. Drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Roche Guillon 2016  Moulin-à-Vent

description: This dense, well-structured wine has bold tannins as well as rich cherry and blackberry fruits. The structure and the fruit are still young and up front, needing to calm down before the wine will be ready to drink. Wait until late 2018.
title: Domaine des Maisons Neuves 2016 Les Bois-Combes  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: This is a ripe and juicy wine. Tannins support the generous layers of red-berry and cherry fruits and acidity. The wine has a succulent quality, spicy and fruity at the end. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine des Marrans 2016  Fleurie
