description: A blend from several small parcels, this wine has richness and structure. It is dense, packed with firm tannins as well as juicy black-cherry flavors. This firm wine will age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaines Laurent Perrachon & Fils 2016 Terre Roses  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Lovely scents of forest berry, new leather, Mediterranean herb and a whiff of pipe tobacco take shape in the glass. It's racy and elegant, offering crushed strawberry, red cherry and star anise set against a backbone of firm acidity and taut, refined tannins. Drink 2020–2029.
title: Eredi Enzo Raspi 2014 Bandini Villa Pomona  (Chianti Classico)

description: Low yields from a cool vintage make for a delicate nuanced experience in the glass in this perfumed velvety wine. Plum and cherry flavors are crisp and tart, with the acidity persistent. White pepper and ocean spray contribute context and complexity.
title: Flanagan 2015 Platt Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: This wine has a puckering sensibility of tannin on entry but settles in the glass to show a fresh core of strawberry, black cherry, cedar and soy. The oak is buttery and spicy, good at playing a supporting role to the density of mountain-grown high-toned fruit.
title: Heitz 2013 Ink Grade Vineyard Zinfandel (Napa Valley)

Designation: Xavier et Nicolas Barbet Vieilles Vignes
description: This is a ripe and smooth wine. With firm tannins cushioned by rich black fruits, it is spicy, smoky and will age. The wine, from the personal property of the Barbet family that also runs négociant Jean Loron, will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 Xavier et Nicolas Barbet Vieilles Vignes  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Black cherry and elderberry create a brooding impression of fruit. The palate adds freshness and spicy charm. There is a lovely contrast between plump juiciness and vivid tartness that adds a little drama to the palate. The tannins give way to a fine crunch on this medium, fruit-driven body. The finish is dry and moreish.
title: Josef Bauer 2015 Bella Viktoria Red (Wagram)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Floral aromatics are the star of the show in this full-bodied, densely packed wine, blended with 2% each of Grenache, Petite Sirah, Malbec, Zinfandel and Carignane. Tart in acidity, it's juicy and bright, with a persistence of black raspberry.
title: Longboard 2014 Dakine Vineyard Syrah (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Amador County, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine, from a vineyard first planted in the 1860s, has good balance an a distinctive flavor profile, as meaty earthy nuances vie for attention with delicious plummy flavors and a broad, rather soft texture. Full body gives the wine a little grip on the palate in lieu of heavy tannins.
title: Noceto 2013 OGP The Original Grandpère Vineyard Zinfandel (Amador County)

Appellation: Côtes du Rhône, Rhône Valley, France
description: Waxy blossoms, lemon peels and gooseberries perfume this zesty, dry white. Fresh grapefruit and lemon flavors are cutting and pristine, lingering on a revitalizing mineral tone. Drink now through 2020.
title: Ogier 2016 Artesis White (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Earthy aromas of sunbaked soil, new leather, overripe plum and a whiff of game come together on this. The chewy, savory palate doles out fleshy black cherry, crushed raspberry, cinnamon and star anise framed in lithe tannins. It's already hit its ideal drinking window so enjoy soon.
title: Querceto di Castellina 2015 L'Aura  (Chianti Classico)

description: Passion fruit-like tropical notes dominate the nose of this wine, but the palate predominantly expresses lemon, herbs and salt. Its citrusy freshness tingles on the textured midpalate, opening into richer soy, miso and preserved lemon notes. The body is taut and fresh and the finish long.
title: Fischer 2016 Ried Frauenweingärten Smaragd Grüner Veltliner (Wachau)

Appellation: Santa Lucia Highlands, Central Coast, California, US
description: This winery is packaging the freshness and restraint that's possible in this region, with this bottling offering aromas of pomegranate, red currant and star anise on the nose. The palate is vibrant with red fruit, yet it is the bay leaf, wild mint and fennel pollen flavors that impress most and carry deep into the finish.
title: Furthermore 2014 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: From the coastal ridges of the appellation, this lovely wine is tart in strawberry, pomegranate and orange, with a beautiful floral element that lifts the pronounced acidity and darker, more savory forest-floor tones. Spicy and complex, it hits the palate with an intriguing twist of sea spray.
title: Gary Farrell 2015 Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)

description: The nose is both sonorous and toned with vanilla hints meeting tart, dark fruit. The palate plays a similar game of soft, mellow oak meeting concentrated black and red fruit which is ripe but still has bite. Hints of tar and cinnamon shimmer darkly, while fine tannins, warm alcohol and bright acidity make the mouth water. This is quite a mouthful and will benefit from mellowing. These muscles are all still tightly flexed. Wait for this to relax and drink 2022–2032.
title: Gunter Triebaumer 2015 Plachen Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: Oak notes of roast chestnut and mocha dominate the nose for now and signal a potent wine. The palate is dense with dark plum and black cherry. The fruit is concentrated and the tannins are fine but firm. A streak of fine, pure acidity pulls these elements together and brightens and lightens this brooding wine. The genie in this bottle is still asleep but will show itself with elegance and restrained power with some bottle age. Drink 2020–2028.
title: Gunter Triebaumer 2015 Reserve Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

Appellation: Paso Robles Willow Creek District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Snappy boysenberry meets with vanilla, roasted chocolate and purple flowers on the nose of this bottling, which is made by a Texas-based producer. There is great peppery spice to the sip, where dried herbs and roast beef give depth to the black-currant flavors. Lively acidity and polished tannins complete the picture.
title: Heath Vineyards 2014 Absolution Red (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)

description: A tart kind of ripeness recalling zesty orange and crisp yellow apple creates immediate appetite on the nose of this wine. The palate follows through with fizzing lemon and lime zestiness, great concentration and a rich, leesy texture. The finish is dry, lipsmacking and wonderfully refreshing.
title: Hiedler 2016 Ried Kogelberg Reserve Riesling (Kamptal)

description: Blueberry and conifer, a slight whiff of woodsmoke and plum. These are the lovely aromas that pervade nose and palate of this taut, finely structured red. The tannins crunch while the fruit has a really ripe aspect that only becomes apparent with some of the alcoholic warmth on the palate. Everything is so well integrated but still tight. This needs a little bottle age to come into itself. Something to look forward to. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Jalits 2015 Fasching Reserve Blaufränkisch (Eisenberg)

description: A seriously structured wine from this fine vintage will surely have good aging potential. This wine is ripe, dense with tannins and rich with blackberry fruits. It makes a powerful statement about the longevity of Morgon wines. Drink from 2019.
title: Jean-Michel Dupré 2015 Le Griottier  (Morgon)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: This wine was grown at a mere 1.2 tons per acre and spent 20 months in the barrel aging on lees in 60% new puncheons. The aromas are brooding on first pour, with notes of blackberry, mineral, fresh herb, potpourri and barrel spice. The palate is incredibly dense and rich, with a lick of tannins on the warm finish. It's a big wine, made in a muscular powerful style.
title: K Vintners 2014 Klein Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from the Rocks District, this wine is a blend of 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Syrah. The aromas are brooding on first pour, opening up over time, with notes of blackberry, kirsch, high-toned barrel spice, herb and scorched earth. The flavors are concentrated and rich but still bring a sense of deftness, lingering on the finish. It has a long life in front of it.
title: K Vintners 2014 Roma River Rock Vineyard Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Ballard Canyon, Central Coast, California, US
description: Dense blackberries, vanilla, star anise and ripe elderberries show on the nose of this clonal selection by Grenache guru Mikael Sigouin. Thick black plum and black-currant-sauce flavors combine with soy and violet on the palate, where a bright acidity penetrates deep into the finish.
title: Kaena 2014 Clone 362 Tierra Alta Vineyard Grenache (Ballard Canyon)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This is a great find from a high-performing estate in the Sierra Foothills. Wonderful ripe fruit and chocolate flavors combine in a seductive texture that seems to melt in the mouth. Bursting with black cherries and blackberries, this full-bodied wine offers a plush mouthfeel from softly layered tannins and relatively low acidity, so it's delicious to drink.
title: Lava Cap 2014 Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (El Dorado)

description: A creamy, earthy note joins pear fruit scents on the nose of this wine. The palate bears out the earthy note completely, adding sonorous, textured, yeasty richness. This is countered by lemony freshness, which carries through a fine, long finish.
title: Malat 2016 Ried Gottschelle Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: An herbal hint of crushed mint and thyme hovers above bright lemon scents in this wine. The palate is fluid and swishes across the tongue like a caress. The finish lingers forever with a dreamy, pure lemon note. Simply lovely.
title: Markus Huber 2016 Ried Rothenbarth Reserve Riesling (Traisental)

description: Ripe red apple and orange zest richness create a fresh, appetizing aroma in this wine. The palate comes in with even more freshness, adding green apple tartness and lively, translucent lemon notes, with a pithy, zesty texture. A very original, refreshing and moreish wine quite outside its varietal idiom, it delivers loads of impact and flavor for such moderate alcohol. Bravo.
title: Meinklang 2016 Graupert Pinot Gris (Weinland Österreich)

description: All the joy of ripe but tart apple is expressed here, supported by lemon freshness. This is as fruity as it is fresh, and the contrast between these two poles creates delicious excitement. An incredibly lively, energetic wine with a dry, zesty and moreish finish.
title: Nigl 2016 Ried Goldberg Riesling (Kremstal)

description: From the Stewart Vineyard in Carneros, planted with the Musqué clone, this white is spicy and sublimely textured, with a persistence of mineral mystique and balanced ripeness. Its well-crafted fruitiness is a mix of white peach and lime.
title: Peter Franus 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Wonderful complexity and an appealing earthiness help this mouth-watering wine stand out. It has earthy, forest-floor aromas and bold but not overtly ripe flavors like rhubarb and sour cherry while the texture is nervy and alive with good acidity and moderate tannins. It's worth a search for something this unique.
title: Phillips Hill 2015 Oppenlander Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino)

description: Aromas of wild blackberry, cedar, violet and a hint of mocha lead the way on this 100% Sangiovese. The polished, balanced palate doles out ripe black cherry, cinnamon, vanilla and tobacco alongside fine-grained tannins that give the finish grip. Enjoy through 2028.
title: Querceto di Castellina 2013 Sei  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: This complex vineyard-designate is spicy and earthy in equal measure, the burst of white pepper most memorable. Dried plum, rhubarb and strawberry compote add to the story, with a fresh core of acidity piercing through the strong tannin.
title: Quivira 2015 Wine Creek Ranch Grenache (Dry Creek Valley)

description: This wine's tart apple, grapefruit and touch of mango spell fruity intensity on the nose. The palate follows with total juiciness, amplifying the tropical aspects even more. There's concentration and a pithy, warming core. A full-fleshed Riesling with exuberance and freshness.
title: Salomon-Undhof 2016 Ried Pfaffenberg Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Despite this wine's shy nose, it loses no time in presenting ripe, rounded and aromatic pear fruit on the palate, which is made more precise by bright lemon freshness. That zestiness pervades the concentrated palate and underlines the juiciness of its yellow pear fruit. The finish is dry and clean, with lasting freshness. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Stadt Krems 2016 Ried Grillenparz Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's an impressive density to the aromas of this blend of 39% Cabernet Sauvignon, 37% Syrah, 15% Petit Verdot and 9% Malbec, with ample amounts of black plum, lilac and damp forest wood. The palate's hearty structure carries ample purple fruit as well as dark chocolate, thyme marjoram and rainbow peppercorn flavors. Quite thick with tannins.
title: The Farm Winery 2014 The Big Game Red (Adelaida District)

description: Inviting aromas of wild berry, violet and sandalwood are front and center. Made with organically farmed Sangiovese, it's bright, savory and linear, offering Morello cherry, black raspberry and baking spice framed in vibrant acidity and refined tannins. A fantastic showing and unusually expressive for the cool, wet vintage.
title: Volpaia 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: A shy touch of pear, green apple and passion fruit hovers above the yeasty nose of this wine. On the palate, lemon zest-like freshness takes over and spotlights the textured, savory and salty yeast that fills it out. That pure lemon tone seemingly lasts forever on the moreish finish.
title: Weixelbaum 2016 Ried Wechselberg Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Sourced from older vines, this vineyard-designate wine is seductive on the nose in a rose petal aroma. Restrained in oak, the palate is structured and vibrant in flavors of dark cherry, raspberry, black licorice and chocolate.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Bacigalupi Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: Yorkville Highlands, North Coast, California, US
description: Strong cinnamon and clove aromas lead to bold, ripe black-cherry and cassis flavors in this full-bodied, well-structured and ultimately nuanced wine. It has lots of grip on the palate from firm tannins and bright acidity, while the spicy oak flavors last all the way to the finish.
title: Williams Selyem 2015 Weir Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yorkville Highlands)

description: A blend of 48% Roussanne, 27% Chenin Blanc, 22% Chardonnay and 3% Verdelho, herbal aromas of dried fynbos, hay and pressed yellow flowers are partnered with rich notes of yellow apple, citrus oil and beeswax. The medium-weight palate offers great acidity that enlivens the ripe fruit flavors, while a saline impression provides further lift and lingers long on the close. Drink now–2021.
title: Alheit 2015 Hemelrand Vine Garden White (Hemel en Aarde)

description: There's no reason to drink Cabernet all the time when delicious, well-balanced and firm-textured California Tempranillos like this are becoming more and more common. From a specialist in Iberian grape varieties, the wine has lightly spicy aromas, nicely layered red- and black-cherry and wood-smoke flavors that fill the mouth and linger on the finish.
title: Bokisch 2014 Tempranillo (Lodi)

description: Rich in green apple and fig, this wine offers medium-bodied weight and breadth and grainy acidity. From the famous site planted to densely concentrated berries, it's fleshy and textured, with plenty of complexity from spice and oak.
title: Patz & Hall 2015 Hyde Vineyard Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: A big wine that's unabashedly oaky, this one sports a delicious, spicy, toasty, and lightly smoky character from the first whiff through the densely packed flavors and then continues to linger on the finish. The texture is dense, soothingly tannic, and coats the tongue and sides of the mouth with spiciness as well as ripe berry and new leather accents.
title: Peltier 2015 Estate Grown Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pence is one of the few producers growing and making Gamay in California, and they're setting quite a standard. Aromas of red cherry, cola, hibiscus, pomegranate, fennel and cracked pepper lead into a lightly bodied palate full of airy berry fruit, juniper and licorice spice. Quite refreshing and just complex enough.
title: Pence 2016 PTG Gamay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Whiffs of violet and red raspberry perfume this charming, vibrantly fruity wine. While lavish and full bodied, it's balanced neatly with tart blackberry acidity and finely textured tannins. It's lovely already but likely to maintain well through 2025.
title: Pierre Amadieu 2014 Romane Machotte  (Gigondas)

description: There's a surprising amount of depth and complexity to this wine, given it's price tag. Initial minty, earthy aromas give way to a core of crème de cassis, blackberry and black plum fruit. The medium-weight palate is plush and creamy, with concentrated black-fruit flavors that are framed by a solid tannic structure. Hints of char, tobacco and cigar-box spice unfold on the finish. It should hold well through 2022, but it's an absolute pleasure to drink now.
title: Rainbow's End 2015 Estate Wine Cabernet Sauvignon (Stellenbosch)

description: Vanilla, honeysuckle and grapefruit are shaded in savory shades of earth, pollen and nuts in this creamy, full-bodied Viognier. It's a hefty, concentrated wine with soft acidity but hints of lemon and orange peel brighten a lingering finish.
title: Rémi Niero 2014 Chéry  (Condrieu)

description: In 1966 when this wine was first made, the best of the large barrels was No 5, and the name has stuck. It is a rich wine, full of firm tannins with the potential of fine fruit to follow. With its dark structure and acidity it needs to age a few months more. Drink this rich wine from late 2018.
title: Robert Perroud 2016 Foudre No 5  (Côte de Brouilly)

description: The nose gives very little away. While the palate also still needs to unfold and blossom, it shows all of the elements needed for successful development. Ample freshness and a fine tannic grip showcase the beautifully pure red berry fruit. A beautifully understated and elegant wine, drink 2019–2028.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Niederösterreich)

description: This wine's combination of ivy leaf and golden pear scents makes for a highly seductive nose. On the palate, bright, ripe lemon takes center stage. It lends an appetizing accent of freshness that complements the yeasty texture of the concentrated midpalate.
title: Schloss Gobelsburg 2016 Ried Renner Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: Very elegant aromas of black plum, black cherry and black currant meet with bay leaf and mint on the deep nose of this blend of 61% Syrah and 39% Grenache. It's soft on the palate, with a cohesive flavor profile that pairs deep berry flavors with pepper and dried herbs. The Syrah is well tamed, proving mellow and soft overall.
title: The Farm Winery 2014 Primal Scream Syrah-Grenache (Adelaida District)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Pale pink in color, this is a pitch-perfect rosé, with aromas of watermelon, pink grapefruit, rose petal and lemon rind. There is great zip to the palate and a chalky texture, carrying fresh but tight red-fruit flavors. It will please New World and Old World fans alike.
title: Topa Mountain Winery 2016 Rosé of Grenache (Santa Barbara County)

description: One sniff gives this away as real-deal Carmenère, meaning it's spicy, foxy and fruity, but also herbal and piquant. A deep, firm palate is well cushioned and in balance. This pours on dark flavors of peppery blackberry prior to a toasty finish. If any wine captures the essence of Peumo Carmenère, this is it. Drink through 2022.
title: Undurraga 2015 T.H. Terroir Hunter Carmenère (Peumo)

description: Jumpy berry aromas include notes of juniper and oaky maple. Once this settles, what you have is a fully extracted Syrah with a rich but balanced palate. Spiced plum and wild berry flavors are powerful but show restraint, while this shows savory flavors of dark fruits, olive and spice on the finish.
title: Viña Maipo 2012 Limited Edition Handpicked Syrah (Maipo Valley)

Appellation: Adelaida District, Central Coast, California, US
description: From a historic vineyard, this Chardonnay is quite Burgundian in style, showing much potential for this grape in Paso Robles. Reserved aromas of lemon rind, vanilla shavings and cashew are quite fresh, while the palate drives the middle road, with tight grapefruit rind and mandarin pith flavors on one side and plumeria and guava on the other. Chalky texture throughout.
title: Adelaida 2016 HMR Estate Vineyard Chardonnay (Adelaida District)

description: Floral aromas of violet petal blend with berry and cassis notes to form a fruity pleasant nose. This blend of 60% Carmenère and 40% Malbec features tight tannins that create a downward pull on the palate. Baked plum and spice flavors are fairly straightforward on a lasting finish. Drink through 2023.
title: Casa Donoso 2014 Grand Domaine Red (Maule Valley)

Appellation: Wahluke Slope, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Coming from a vineyard that is increasingly establishing its prominence, fruit-forward aromas of plum, boysenberry, dried herb, spice and moist earth are followed by saturated sumptuous blue and red-fruit flavors. It's undeniable in its appeal at a price that can't be beat.
title: Casa Smith 2015 Porcospino Northridge Vineyard Primitivo (Wahluke Slope)

description: Subtle aromas of exotic spice, crushed herb and wild berry slowly unfold in the glass. The austere, medium-bodied palate offers sour cherry, pomegranate and star anise set against bright acidity and tightly knit, close-grained tannins. It needs time to unwind and fully develop. Drink 2020–2033.
title: Castello di Radda 2013 Gran Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Planted on the granite soil typical of the region, the vines behind this wine give a textured, mineral character to the black fruits. That makes this a rich wine, although one that needs to be aged. Drink this concentrated wine from late 2018.
title: Château de Chatelard 2016 Cuvée les Vieux Granits  (Fleurie)

description: Deep tannins mark out this structured wine. A dense texture dominates the young black fruit. This will change, because the fruit is as powerful as the tannins. The wine will age well; drink from 2019.
title: Château de la Terrière 2016 Cuvée de la Lure  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Fresh lemon, grapefruit and apple flavors are embellished with decadent swirls of vanilla, spice and cream in this lavish white blend. It's silky smooth, almost creamy, in texture, but balanced neatly by sunny tangerine acidity and hints of dusty minerals.
title: Château Maucoil 2016 Trésor des Papes White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Harvested at night to keep freshness, this wine comes from 35-year-old vines. It has a fruity character, bright with red-cherry acidity and a rounded, smooth texture. The edge of tannins and intense acidity make it a food wine. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Domaine Anita 2016 Reine de Nuit  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Schist rocks in the Les Charmes vineyard give this wine its rich mineral character. Along with that are tannins both from the fruit and the wood aging, making this a rich wine that needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de Bel-Air 2016 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: Owner Jacky Gauthier has produced a rich wine from low-yielding vines. The tannins and dark, firm texture are both rich and well placed to support the black-plum and berry fruits. This ripe wine will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Domaine de Colette 2016 Le Mont  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Winery: Domaine de la Voûte des Crozes
description: Old vines and 10 months in wood have given this wine both concentration and great elegance. Ripe berry fruits have been smoothed by hints of toast, balanced acidity and ripe tannins. Drink this wine from mid-2018.
title: Domaine de la Voûte des Crozes 2016  Côte de Brouilly

description: Earthy, gamy notes accentuate muted tones of strawberry leather and dried plum in this rustic but elegant wine. Ripe and rich on the palate, it extends long through a delicately tannic, cocoa-dusted finish.
title: Domaine de Nalys 2013 Réserve Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Ripe black fruits and generous tannins give this wine richness and dense texture. It is aged for seven months in wood, adding a caramel and toast character to the generous fruit. The wine is packed with fruit and ready to drink.
title: Domaine des Capréoles 2016 Sous la Croix  (Régnié)

description: Aged in large wooden barrels, this wine is dense, with a firm texture and juicy, crisp black-currant fruit. Bright acidity gives a lively aftertaste, although the structure remains firm and the wine needs to age. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2016  Moulin-à-Vent

description: The Tête family is well known in Beaujolais. Here they have produced a richly textured wine with just the right amount of tannin and acidity to highlight the generous red fruits. Drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Domaine du Clos du Fief 2016 Tradition  (Juliénas)

description: This wine is on the edge, complex in white pepper and cool-climate characteristics of dried herb and leather. Brawny and full-bodied, it's also bright in underlying acidity, with plenty of elusive power.
title: Flanagan 2014 Syrah (Bennett Valley)

description: Négociant Florent Descombe has produced a range of cru Beaujolais, including this one. Sourced from vines on the slope of Moulin-à-Vent, it is firm and packed with tannins. There is no compromise in the rich structure that permeates the ripe black fruit. Drink from 2019.
title: Florent Descombe 2016 Cuvée M  (Moulin-à-Vent)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: This is part of the winery's Winemaker Series, a limited production wine that was barrel-aged for 17 months in two-thirds new French oak. It remains stiff and quite astringent, with vanilla being the principal flavor. In fact, there is so much vanilla that it more or less covers most of the fruit.
title: Sweet Cheeks 2015 Barrel Select Kody's Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Fruit for this wine comes from Block 66 of Double Canyon Vineyard in the Horse Heaven Hills. The aromas aren't entirely harmonious, with notes of dark, slightly overripe-seeming fruit along with black pepper and licorice, showing some volatility. The black-raspberry flavors are light and compact. It doesn't come together.
title: William Marie 2016 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: Despite the excellent vintage this is just barely ripe, with green notes among generic red fruits. Tannins are stiff, and a hint of butter runs through the finish. Nothing really jells.
title: Airlie 2014 Vintner's Blend Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This blend of 79% Shiraz and 21% Mourvèdre is dense and concentrated, with deep notes of all-fruit preserves, raspberry sauce and licorice. The palate offers more decadence in waves of fudgy brownie and candied cherry, while the thick and chewy tannins lend some serious grip and a firm texture. Drink now.
title: Black Pearl 2015 The Mischief Maker Shiraz-Mourvèdre (Coastal Region)

Designation: Cueva del Monge Fermentado en Barrica
description: Mild fruit aromas are crowded by peanut and burnt-wood notes. This Viura-led blend is full and round on the palate, with forceful flavors of nutty stone fruits, sea brine and wood resin. A lean, acidic palate is better in feel than weak flavors.
title: Bodegas Vínícola Real 2014 Cueva del Monge Fermentado en Barrica White (Rioja)

description: This wine is straightforward in its ripe melon and citrus aromas, which carry to the lightly concentrated palate. There's something enjoyable about its simple, fruit-driven nature that makes it an easy wine to sip on.
title: Cantele 2016 Chardonnay (Salento)

description: Aromas reminiscent of a struck match and a whiff of medicinal herb lead the nose. The palate is on the lean side, offering yellow-apple skin, lemon zest and a taste of bitter walnut alongside continuous lively bubbles.
title: Castel Faglia NV Brut  (Franciacorta)

description: This wine comes from a small 12-acre vineyard. Black-currant fruits and firm tannins keep the wine structured while maintaining a crisp character. The wine is simple, fruity and will be ready to drink from 2020.
title: Château Myon de l'Enclos 2015  Moulis-en-Médoc

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Predominately Uva di Troia, with additions of Aglianico and Montepulciano, this blend shows aromas of peanut skins over crushed bramble berries and violets. The palate is light in feel, tart berries are zesty and bolstered by drying, grippy tannins. Racy acidity cuts this short but makes it a food-friendly wine.
title: Giancarlo Ceci 2015 Parco Grande Red (Castel del Monte)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Malbec and Petit Verdot, this has aromas that bring notes of black licorice and cherry, along with light oxidative notes. It's a shame, as good things are happening on the palate.
title: J & J NV 4 Boys 4th Release Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

Appellation: Central Coast, Central Coast, California, US
description: Slightly reductive aromas of wet clay and sour olive meet with dark cranberry fruit and camphor on the nose of this bottling. The palate is tart with raspberry and orange rind and gains complexity from the forest moss and wet wood flavors.
title: Layer Cake 2016 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: Savory aromas of baked raspberry, cranberry and vanilla are somewhat foxy. Flavors of raspberry and pomegranate are nearing overripe, with layers of pepper and grippy tannins that offer some much needed structure. The finish is all tannin and spice.
title: Markko 2012 Select Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Ohio)

Appellation: Umpqua Valley, Southern Oregon, Oregon, US
description: Merlot seems to struggle a bit in Oregon, lacking the volume of other West Coast versions. Quite tart with simple berry flavors, this wine shows no real flesh, but goes directly from a sappy start into a tangy finish.
title: Melrose 2015 Merlot (Umpqua Valley)

description: Subtle aromas of cherry cola are basic. This Rioja is medium in body, with slightly scratchy tannins and acidity. Earthy plum and berry flavors finish with a hint of toasty oak and a sense of dryness.
title: Pagos del Rey 2013 Castillo de Albai Crianza  (Rioja)

description: While the nose is earthen and mineral, the palate of this light-bodied blend of Silvaner and Riesling offers pleasant lemon and green melon refreshment. It's a juicy, uncomplicated sip, with subtle sweetness and a brisk finish. Drink now.
title: Peter Mertes 2016 Platinum Dream Stone Liebfraumilch White (Rheinhessen)

description: A good blast of black cherry and cedar in the aroma is followed by rich, grapey flavors on a moderately firm texture. It is softened by a hint of sweetness on the finish.
title: Rare 2014 Rare Black Red (California)

description: Red- and black-plum aromas dominate the nose, with layers of coffee, kirsch and geranium in the background. A coffee tone starts off on the palate, moving to cranberry, grape and plum flavors. Zippy acidity matches the full body to create a nice balance.
title: Sunset Hills 2015 Shenandoah Springs Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Shenandoah Valley)

Appellation: Dundee Hills, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: It's not apparent what's going on with this wine, which tastes disjointed and aggressively stemmy. Its fruit is thin and generic, plain red berries, with a whiff of compost. But the impact of stems (whole clusters?) is what dominates. Two bottles tasted with equal results.
title: The Four Graces 2015 Reserve Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

Appellation: Red Mountain, Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: Aromas are light and dominated by barrel notes, including vanilla and coconut. The black-fruit flavors are sweet and rich, showing quite a bit of warmth. It doesn't all add up.
title: Twin Cedars 2014 Syrah (Red Mountain)

Appellation: Willamette Valley, Willamette Valley, Oregon, US
description: At just 10% alcohol and 22 g/l residual sugar, you might expect a fruity wine with a fair amount of sweetness, which indeed is what you get. Simple fruit flavors of apple and melon come with a slightly granular sugary finish.
title: Airlie 2015 Müller-Thurgau (Willamette Valley)

description: Lemon peel aromas take on a slightly savory cheese-rind like tone on the nose. The palate offers richness from oak, displaying toasted nut flavors over lemon and apple. The oak seems a bit disjointed, throwing the fruit tones off balance.
title: Cantele 2015 Teresamanara Chardonnay (Salento)

Appellation: Primitivo di Manduria, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Saucy plum and cherry syrup aromas turn brighter and cleaner on the medium-bodied palate. A bit of sweet spice kicks in on the midpalate, but ultimately this is primary, soft in feel and straightforward.
title: Felline 2015  Primitivo di Manduria

Appellation: Castel del Monte, Southern Italy, Italy
description: Canned peas taking center stage over grapefruit and white flowers in an interesting mix of aromas. There's decent weight to the palate, with bright acidity to boot, yet the flavors are subdued and focus on the citrus and herbal elements.
title: Giancarlo Ceci 2016 Panascio Bombino Bianco (Castel del Monte)

description: This wine shows mealy apple, margarine and nut aromas on the nose. The flavors are similar yet the mouthfeel is easy-going and pleasing.
title: Keswick Vineyards 2016 Signature Series Viognier (Monticello)

description: Sweet-tart red cherry and berry flavors are plump and juicy in this fresh, easy drinking Dornfelder. It's not a complicated wine but balanced in sweetness and acidity. Drink now.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2016 Dornfelder (Rheinhessen)

description: This Merlot shows aromas and flavors of bright cherry, red plum and baking spices. The fruit comes off as tart on the palate but the oak spices are appealing. Not much tannic expression but a nice acidic lift keeps the finish going strong.
title: Rosemont 2015 Merlot (Virginia)

description: Wild-berry aromas are muted and a shade green on the nose. Cassis, wild raspberry and tart cranberry flavors are at odds with cedar, cinnamon and oak influences. Lacking focus, crushed velvet tannins attempt to pull everything together, riding into a tangy finish.
title: Shelburne 2015 Crimson Sails Dry Marquette (Vermont)

description: Violet, blueberry and cedar aromas ride over a rubbery tone. Mild acidity struggles to keep jammy pomegranate and cranberry flavors in check, while structured tannins and white-pepper flavors tingle on the tongue.
title: Shelburne 2015 Untamed Marquette (Vermont)

Appellation: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy
description: Subdued aromas of underbrush, moist soil, chopped herb and a whiff of red berry appear in the glass. The simple light-bodied palate shows suggestions of sour cherry and white pepper alongside loose fleeting tannins. Drink now.
title: Vecchia Cantina di Montepulciano 2014  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano

description: Spiced fruit cake aromas dominate the nose and carry to the medium-bodied, approachable palate. This is simple and straightforward with everything in balance—perfect for everyday enjoyment.
title: Cantina Le Grotte 2015 Selva Della Rocca Nero di Troia (Puglia)

description: This wine is brooding in aromas of dark fruit, earth, cola, chocolate, dried herbs and a whiff of smoke that carry to a toasty, medium-bodied palate, leading to a medicinal, herbal and oaky finish. The structure and fine tannins of this wine suggest it will age into something quite lovely. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Ata Rangi 2015 Pinot Noir (Martinborough)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Thick and hearty aromas of elderberry and blueberry jam meet with lilacs and black pepper on the rich and spicy nose of this blend of 50% Syrah and 50% Teroldego. The sip is dense and attention-grabbing, with strong blackberry and boysenberry flavors alongside loamy soil and chalky tannins.
title: Bevela 2014 Unforeseen Alchemy Red (Santa Barbara County)

description: This full-bodied wine has a big structure, strong oak, spice and red fruit flavors all wrapped in fine-grained tannins. The color is relatively light, but the aromas are full of toasted oak and nutmeg while the flavors have vivid cranberry and cherry. The wine should mellow with age, and will be best after 2020.
title: Brassfield 2015 Grenache (High Valley)

description: This is extremely racy wine that starts with clean chalky aromas of apple skins and Meyer lemon pith will pair excellently with fresh raw shellfish. The tightly wound palate clings to the tongue with a limestone-like grip, delivering flavors of Asian pear flesh, yuzu zest, oyster shell and tons of wet gravel.
title: Cambiata 2016 Albariño (Monterey)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Healthy doses of black pepper and smoked meat in both the aromas and the flavors give this wine a pretty forceful personality, and it has good fruit to back them up. It smells like cracked pepper and cedar, tastes spicy, meaty, quite firm in texture and brings out raspberry and red cherry on the finish.
title: Cedarville Vineyard 2014 Estate Grenache (El Dorado)

description: Named after the rocky soil of the vineyard, this wine is suitably firm and structured. A mineral texture gives an edgy character and enhances the firm tannins. The wine, rich in fruit potential, will age well. Drink from 2019.
title: Château de Chatelard 2016 Cuvée les Roches  (Morgon)

description: This bold, ripe wine is packed with rich fruit and tannins. It is produced by direct descendants of the Loron family of négociants who still run the estate. With its dense texture and rich concentration, it will be ready to drink from late 2018.
title: Château de Fleurie 2016  Fleurie

description: This brother-and-sister team have produced a structured wine as part of their négociant business (they also have a family domaine). Packed with red-cherry fruits and acidity, this wine is young but promises a good future. The crisp texture and the tannins will keep the wine developing until 2019.
title: Claire & Fabien Chasselay 2016  Moulin-à-Vent

description: Peppery, this old-vine wine is well integrated and thickly restrained, with a lip-smacking taste of blackberry cobbler and dark chocolate. Gritty tannins give it texture and ample weight, while a freshness of acidity persists.
title: Dashe Cellars 2015 Todd Brothers Ranch Old Vines Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)

description: Fresh lemon acidity invigorates this glossy, unctuous white wine. Full bodied and fleshy on the palate, it offers soft red apple, grapefruit and hazelnut flavors accented by a hint of dried herb.
title: Domaine de Nalys 2015 Eicelènci White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: A kiss of honey extends from nose to finish, accenting fresh apple and grapefruit flavors in this plush, full-bodied white. It's brawny and expansive on the palate, but sunny lemon acidity cuts through the fat, adding freshness to a lingering finish.
title: Domaine du Vieux Lazaret 2014 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This wine is big, rich and bold. Its black fruits and tight acidity indicate the aging potential of this wine. Add in the firm tannins and mineral texture, and it will take time to mature. Drink this dense wine from 2019
title: Domaine Guenaël Jambon 2016 Côte du Py Cuvée Nature  (Morgon)

description: Producer Marc Delienne, fresh from Paris, has made an impression with his three cuvées of Fleurie. Aged in wood, this rich, concentrated wine has density and great power. Tannins and dark fruits promise aging potential. Drink this wine from late 2018.
title: Domaine Marc Delienne 2016 Abbaye Road  (Fleurie)

description: Dusty plum, tobacco and dark chocolate play against a soft texture in this varietal wine with a Merlot-like sensibility. Black and red currant and cherry mix well on the fruity palate, seasoned in appropriate measures of cedar, black pepper and clove.
title: Duckhorn 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Ripe, juicy cassis and blackberry notes seduce from nose to finish here. It's a brawny, rather lavish wine, both in body and fruit, but nuanced too, accentuated by hints of garrigue, leather and earth. Richly concentrated and long on the finish, it's framed neatly by fine, persistent tannins.
title: E. Guigal 2013  Gigondas

description: Sanguine and smoky, this wine has a welcome herbal component that keeps it fresh and complex in the glass. Thick and densely concentrated in blackberry and plum, it persists in an underlying vibrancy that helps balance and complement its more luxurious tease of dark chocolate.
title: Eric Kent 2014 Las Madres Vineyard Syrah (Carneros)

description: This classically styled Cabernet Sauvignon-driven blend shows power and structure in equal measure, with a backbone of integrated oak. Raspberry, black cherry and cassis flavors are succulently displayed along a carpet of thick concentrated richness.
title: Ferrari-Carano 2013 Tresor Red (Sonoma County)

Appellation: Columbia Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas show nuance, with notes of toast, assorted dried herb, cherry and plum. The fruit flavors are seamless in feel, showing concentration and depth. It's thoroughly enchanting.
title: Gård 2014 Lawrence Vineyards Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: With its Burgundy-colored tile roof and porticoed facade, this is a showpiece estate in Juliénas. The wine it produces is dense, with solid, complex tannins. Ripe red fruits plus acidity and a core of dryness will allow it to age. Drink from late 2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Château des Capitans Estate Bottled  (Juliénas)

description: Fully concentrated in dusty black fruit and tobacco, this blends 79% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Petit Verdot, 3% Merlot, 2% Malbec and 1% Cabernet Franc. Mellowed in terms of ripeness and body weight, it offers sizable tannins that provide structure along the midpalate.
title: Geyser Peak 2013 Reserve Alexandre Winemaker's Reserve Meritage (Alexander Valley)

Appellation: Walla Walla Valley (WA), Columbia Valley, Washington, US
description: The aromas explode with notes of honeysuckle, ripe peach, apricot and creamsicle. The flavors are supremely rich but also show a beautiful sense of delicacy along with a pronounced finish. It's full bore but delicious.
title: K Vintners 2015 Powerline Vineyard Viognier (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Ripe black-fruit aromas are nicely toasted and subtle. On the palate, this is chewy and concentrated but not too heavy. Darkchocolaty oaky blackberry flavors are just spicy and feral enough to let you know this is Syrah. As a whole, this is smooth and ready to drink.
title: Lagar de Bezana 2014 Edición Limitada Syrah (Cachapoal Valley)

Appellation: Sta. Rita Hills, Central Coast, California, US
description: There's a potent smoky character on the nose of this bottling, with hickory and oak aromas wrapped around the strawberry and plum flavors. The palate is fresher in a note of red plum, yet smoke, salt, peppercorn and oregano tones pervade. It's savory without being rich, showing a strong backbone of acidity.
title: Landmark 2015 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Appetizing notes of green and yellow pear and even a touch of yellow plum play on the nose of this wine. The palate has that varietal, textured backdrop, but pear, plum and fresh, ripe lemon notes take center stage. It's concentrated and fruity, with a citrusy finish.
title: Laurenz V. 2016 Charming Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

Appellation: Santa Barbara County, Central Coast, California, US
description: Light in the glass, this bottling by Winemaker Lane Tanner offers plum, red cherry, baked earth, shredded slate and a hint of vanilla-laced croissant on the nose. The palate shows energetic acidity and a grippy texture, with underripe strawberry, plum skin and green-peppercorn tones.
title: Lumen 2015 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Cherry syrup and a roasted meat element are revealed the nose of this bottling from a winery with a popular tasting room in downtown Paso Robles. Vanilla and baked good flavors show right away on the palate, where chalky tannins and rich cherry fruits arise mid-sip to ride a bountiful acidity and touch of pepper spice into the finish.
title: LXV 2015 Bootjack Ranch Reserve Cabernet Franc (Paso Robles)

description: Zesty freshness and lemon brightness characterize the nose and palate of this slender, sinuous wine. Herbal savor tinges the edges while ripe pear fruit comes to the fore. Lovely, lively and wonderfully taut.
title: Markus Huber 2016 Ried Alte Setzen Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Traisental)

description: Aromas of French oak, vanilla, exotic spice and espresso follow over to the medium-bodied, linear palate along with cranberry, licorice, chopped mint and coconut. Racy acidity and austere, fine-grained tannins provide balance and support. Drink 2019–2028.
title: Mazzei 2013 Castello Fonterutoli Gran  Selezione  (Chianti Classico)

description: Aromas of pressed blue flower, perfumed berry, dried herb and exotic spice are in the forefront. The vibrant, linear palate delivers dried black cherry, crushed raspberry, licorice and a hint of coffee set against taut, fine-grained tannins that leave a firm finish. It's balanced, with fresh acidity. Drink 2021–2033.
title: Negretti 2013 Mirau  (Barolo)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: There are loads of sagebrush, thyme, peppercorn and dried oregano aromas on this blend of 50% Grenache, 35% Syrah and 15% Mourvèdre, all set against a baked boysenberry frame. The mouthfeel is easygoing and airy, with flavors of tart berry, plum skins and more garden herbs. A peppery spice extends through the finish.
title: Nelle 2015 Maniacal G-S-M (Paso Robles)

description: Violets and raspberries perfume this soft, pretty wine full of sun-kissed berry and cherry flavors. It's not complex, but irresistibly plush and fruity. Drink now through 2021.
title: Gabriel Meffre 2014 Laurus  (Gigondas)

description: Lavishly ripe blueberry and blackberry flavors penetrate through this robust yet freshly balanced red. It's a juicy, easy-drinking wine but pristine in fruit with hints of nuts and spice. The finish is softly tannic, almost powdery.
title: Gabriel Meffre 2015 Sainte-Catherine  (Gigondas)

Appellation: Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Graham cracker and vanilla wafer aromas entice on the nose. This is a ripe, dense wine which boasts a sinewy backdrop of leather among a broad expanse of rich fruit flavors. A cinnamon note lingers on the finish.
title: Gallo Signature Series 2015 Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Named after the general who was so involved in American independence, this ripe and generous wine is warm with red-berry fruits and attractive acidity. It is smooth, with hints of vanilla and tangy black fruits at the end. Drink now.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine de Lafayette  (Brouilly)

description: This is a firm, structured wine produced at one of the many estates of Duboeuf. Rich black-cherry fruit contrasts with the dry core of the wine. Ripe and juicy, it will be ready to drink from mid-2018.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2016 Domaine des Quartre Vents  (Fleurie)

description: Black cherry, smoke and a lick of mocha signal richness right away. The palate continues in the same vein, imbuing dark, ripe fruit with the richness of chocolate and mocha. Inherent freshness keeps this balanced. A modern, international style with an Austrian accent.
title: Hannes Reeh 2015 Rohstoff Zweigelt (Burgenland)

Appellation: Finger Lakes, Finger Lakes, New York, US
description: Inviting, dense aromas of dark cherry and cranberry meet soft, sweet spice accents on the nose of this single-vineyard wine. Medium in weight, with ample roundness and structure, crunchy red-fruit flavors are bolstered by a peppered mesquite tone.
title: Heron Hill 2015 Ingle Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

Appellation: El Dorado, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: Crisp and dry, this light-bodied wine has vivid acidity and fresh Bosc pear and white peach flavors along with a lightly astringent texture that's very appetizing. It will be just the thing as an apéritif with toasted almonds and olives. Blended from Grenache Blanc, Roussanne, Picpoul and Viognier.
title: Holly's Hill 2016 Patriarche Blanc White (El Dorado)

description: Violet, black-skinned berry and vanilla scents are front and center while the concentrated, polished palate doles out juicy red cherry, cinnamon, mocha and a drop of vanilla. Fine-grained tannins provide the lithe framework. Enjoy through 2025.
title: I Sodi 2014 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Shenandoah Valley (CA), Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This soft-textured, mouth-filling wine has woodsy, fallen leaf aromas, gentle flavors that combine savory and fruity accents and mild acidity and tannins. It's medium to full-bodied and soothing to sip.
title: Iron Hub 2014 Estate Bottled Grenache (Shenandoah Valley (CA))

description: An 18th-century family-owned property is the source of this crisp, textured, ripe wine. With plenty of tannins to give it structure and perfumed black fruits to convey the juicy character, it is in good shape to age a little longer. Drink from mid-2018.
title: Jean Loron 2016 La Victorine  (Saint-Amour)

description: This blend of Carmenère, Syrah, Cab Sauvignon and Petit Verdot smells of forced oak and mint on top of standard berry scents. The palate is full and oaky but not heavy. Ripe yet racy flavors of jammy berry fruits are woody on a finish that's tannic and rubbing.
title: Junta 2011 Escalera Red (Curicó Valley)

description: This wine boasts a fruity nose of tropical and citrus notes along with white flowers and ginger spice. The acid is balanced by some fruit weight and a chalky texture, while a copper penny tang keeps things interesting.
title: Kelly's Crossing 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Plum and raspberry aromas are on the spot and inviting, with a touch of smoky darkness. A firm, slightly clamping palate is lively and extended, while this tastes of savory, saucy plum and berry fruits. A note of ocean salt pokes through on a solid finish.
title: Kingston Family 2016 Alazan Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Very fresh, primary notes of green pear and apple swirl on the nose of this wine. The palate adds a savory element of chervil, celery salt and yeast to this refreshing, moreish package.
title: Kohl 2016 Kittl Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Full of yummy, ripe plum, boysenberry and black-pepper flavors, this full-bodied and smooth-textured wine is a great value. It's very mouth filling, soothing and delicious and has a deep color, lush and fruity aromas and soft tannins on the palate.
title: LangeTwins 2014 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: Crisp blackberry and cherry flavors are bright, but this complex wine is more about their interplay with earthier tones of smoke, game and mushroom. Rich and full on the palate yet balanced firmly with tannins and acidity, it's a stately wine with a lingering, slightly astringent chestnut finish.
title: Lavau 2015 Red (Rasteau)

description: From a vineyard recently replanted and coming into its own, this offers dusty weavings of tobacco, clove, tar and leather, as burly and brawny as a humidor. Big-boned and ripe, the tannins are seamless on the palate and soft on the finish.
title: Ledson 2013 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this opens with aromas of underbrush, cedar and black-skinned berry. The chewy palate delivers blackberry jam, licorice, black pepper and coffee alongside fine-grained tannins. Enjoy now through 2024.
title: Lornano 2013 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

Appellation: Santa Ynez Valley, Central Coast, California, US
description: Bolstered by a splash of co-fermented Syrah, this wine's soft and inviting nose shows black cherries, smoke, bay leaf and crushed rose petals. This bottling contains more hickory smoke seasoning than past vintages, which surrounded the red cherry and purple flower flavors. It's a great pairing for a barbecue.
title: Lucas & Lewellen 2014 Cote Del Sol Cabernet Sauvignon (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: This is a ripe and juicy young wine. With soft tannins and bright red fruits, it is crisp while also structured. It will age further to give a generous, full-throated wine. Drink from late 2018.
title: Manoir du Carra 2016 Les Bottières  (Juliénas)

description: A slight hint of ripe, red cherry appears on the nose. The palate has a gentle, red-fruited freshness and a juicy generosity. A slight frame of tannins provides a gentle structure in this honest, fresh-faced and highly appetizing Pinot Noir.
title: Mayer am Pfarrplatz 2015 Nussberg Pinot Noir (Vienna)

Appellation: Russian River Valley, Sonoma, California, US
description: Sublime in texture, all smooth and velvety contours, this fruit-forward wine has a medium-bodied frame rich and ripe in white peach and vanilla. The peach turns to guava and mango as it develops additional fleshy creaminess in the glass.
title: Merry Edwards 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)

Appellation: North Fork of Long Island, Long Island, New York, US
description: Immediately appealing on the nose, this reserve offers a wealth of aromas in brandied cherry, anise, clove and savory dried meat tones. The palate works to further integrate these notes into flavors of juicy cherry, rich plum and shots of cranberry. Sultry in feel, with integrated, bolstering tannins, drink this wine through 2020 to enjoy its luscious primary flavors and developing sanguine tones.
title: Osprey's Dominion 2012 Reserve Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

Appellation: Paso Robles, Central Coast, California, US
description: Smoked meat, dried mint and roasted black plums combine for a complex sense of elegance on the nose of this bottling. The flavors are cohesive yet varied, with black currant, violet and roasted beef, upheld by tannins that are soft yet steady, with a growing presence toward the finish.
title: Pear Valley 2014 Pear Valley Vineyard Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: This John Legend-led partnership is a blend of 55% Petite Sirah, 39% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon and 1% Petit Verdot. Bright, focused red cherry and cassis meets soft rounded tannin that contributes to a seamless offering of velvety texture.
title: Raymond 2014 LVE Legend Vineyard Exclusive Red (Napa Valley)

Appellation: Sutter County, North Coast, California, US
description: This is a great change-of-pace wine, rather full in body and rich in texture, but with zesty grapefruit and rose petal aromas and buttery almond-like flavors that are more savory than simply fruity. As if Italian native Fiano wasn't a rare enough grape variety, the winemaker blended in 15% of Coda di Volpe, an even lesser known Italian variety.
title: Rock Wall 2016 Sicilia Vineyard Fiano (Sutter County)

description: This wine's fresh, bright and zesty lime and green pear scents have a lovely touch of nettle. The palate has the sprightly ease of these fresh flavors but also a lovely, grounding element of yeast that adds texture.
title: Schwarzböck 2016 Ried Sätzen Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

Appellation: Sierra Foothills, Sierra Foothills, California, US
description: This wine is nicely balanced considering how big it is in terms of tannin and body. The aromas ooze blueberry syrup and blackberry jam while the flavors are equally rich. The texture is quite firm and tannic but it feels right and will help the wine age well. Best after 2022.
title: Sierra Starr 2015 Super Nova Petite Sirah (Sierra Foothills)

description: A touch of smoky, spicy oak flavor gives this ripe and berry-soaked wine a nice twist of complexity. It has strawberry and boysenberry aromas, and full, almost jammy, flavors riding on a smooth texture that shows soft tannins.
title: Sobon Estate 2016 Zinfandel (Fiddletown)
